content.section_title: FAQ: Does the Indian Red Cross Society or its regional branches qualify as an admissible implementing agency capable of applying for this financial assistance?
content.text: Yes, the Indian Red Cross Society or any of its branches are explicitly listed as eligible organizational types for receiving financial assistance under the Integrated Programme for Senior Citizens.

title: AYUSH Health and Wellness Centre - Ayushman Bharat Yojana
topic: AYUSH Health and Wellness Centre - Ayushman Bharat Yojana

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to provide accessible, affordable, and holistic healthcare services based on Ayurveda, Yoga, Unani and Homeopathy systems to people of all age groups.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The “AYUSH Health and Wellness Centre - Ayushman Bharat Yojana” is implemented by the Ayush Department, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to provide accessible, affordable, and holistic healthcare services based on Ayurveda, Yoga, Unani and Homeopathy systems to people of all age groups.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
A total of 300 AYUSH Health and Wellness Centres are operating in the state. At these centres, medicines are distributed according to the specific disease, and yoga and naturopathy treatments are provided as per the condition.

- Counselling based on nature testing and seasonal routines- screening and diagnosis of non-communicable diseases such as diabetes, hypertension, oral cancer, breast cancer, and cervical cancer, are conducted.
- Medicinal plants are also distributed.
- OPD charges have been fixed at ₹1.00 per person in rural areas and ₹2.00 per person in urban areas.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
- The beneficiary must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
- The beneficiary may belong to any age group.
- The beneficiary must be registered at a government-recognized Ayurvedic, Unani, or Homeopathic dispensary or hospital.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text: The beneficiary needs to register at a government-recognized Ayurvedic, Unani, or Homeopathic dispensary or hospital.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Registration Proof at the Government Ayurvedic/Unani/Homeopathic Dispensary or Hospital
- Identity Proof
- Any other document as required

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible to benefit from this scheme?
content.text: Any resident of Uttarakhand, irrespective of age, is eligible to avail services under the AYUSH Health and Wellness Centre scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the eligibility condition for registration?
content.text: The beneficiary must be registered at a government-recognized Ayurvedic, Unani, or Homeopathic dispensary or hospital in Uttarakhand.

content.section_title: FAQ: How many AYUSH Health and Wellness Centres are operating in Uttarakhand?
content.text: A total of 300 AYUSH Health and Wellness Centres are currently operating across the state.

content.section_title: FAQ: What type of healthcare services are provided under this scheme?
content.text: The centres offer Ayurvedic, Unani, and Homeopathic treatments, yoga and naturopathy sessions, disease-specific medicines, counselling, and preventive health services.

content.section_title: FAQ: What kind of diseases are screened or diagnosed at these centres?
content.text: Screening and diagnosis for non-communicable diseases such as diabetes, hypertension, oral cancer, breast cancer, and cervical cancer are carried out.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are counselling services available?
content.text: Yes.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can a person register under this scheme?
content.text: A person can register by visiting the nearest government-recognized Ayurvedic, Unani, or Homeopathic dispensary or hospital and enrolling as a beneficiary.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to focus on early stimulation and responsive parenting in the best interest of each child from 0-3 years. The scheme envisages promoting parent-led, home-based, joyful child-friendly learning by creating an early stimulating environment at the household level.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
The scheme “Aama Kuni Pilaa” has been launched by the Department of Women & Child Development, Government of Odisha. It aims to focus on early stimulation and responsive parenting in the best interest of each child from 0-3 years. As the name "Aama Kuni Pilaa” suggests, the unconditional care and affection of parents, grandparents and caregivers for the children is the driving force for the conceptualisation of this unique initiative. Early experiences with mother, father and grandparents shape lifelong learning and development of children, because of rapid brain development. 80% of the brain develops by three years, and stimulating experiences with caregivers strengthen the neural connections, promoting the holistic development of the child.
> **Objectives:**

The scheme envisages promoting parent-led, home-based, joyful child-friendly learning by creating an early stimulating environment at the household level. It aims at:

- Building capacities by engaging parents and caregivers on responsive parenting messages using different platforms.
- Skill building of ICDS officials/ caregivers on importance of early years, play and learning activities for children.
- Sensitizing the parents, care givers and the entire community on importance of early years. Involvement of father & male care givers through Men-streaming.
- Engaging young children with low cost contextual and child friendly toys by the parents & caregivers.
- Facilitating love, talk, touch & play through toys, lullabies, stories and interactive talk and activity with children below three years by the Anganwadi worker.
- Equity outreach through appropriate parent & caregiver friendly IPC, IEC/ SBCС materials.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
***The key components of the scheme are as listed below:***
**Sl. No.** **Components** **Unit Cost** **Specifications** **Usage** **Point of Distribution** **Procurement by**1Kuni Calendar for children (0-3 years)₹1.53/- per calendar/month/childSize: 11.5" x 17"    Paper: 220 gsm art paper      Print: Multi colour single sideTo be used at household level by the families of children (0 - 3 year)One Calendar for each family having children (0 - 3 year)District2Aama Kuni Pilaa Toy Kit₹340/- (per AWC)[As per Catalogue](https://wcd.odisha.gov.in/sites/default/files/2025-06/21.%2026179_ECCE-Aama%20Kuni%20Pilaa.pdf.pdf)AWC level To be supplied to each AWCDistrict3Mo Bikash Patra (Child Assessment Card)NA-To be used at household level by the families of children (0- 3 year)One Card for each family having children (0-3 years)AWC4Aama Kuni Pilaa Flip BookNA-AWC level To be supplied to each AWC.District5Sukhada Lalana Palana handbookNA-AWC level To be given to AWCs.District**Note: The components mentioned above have been planned for 5 years. However, for current financial year i.e. 2023-24, funds shall be transferred for Activity-1 i.e Kuni Calendar which is to be printed and distributed for three months i.e. for January, February & March, 2024 and Activity-2 i.e. Aama Kuni Pilaa Toy Kit.**
***A. Kuni Calendar:***
This is a calendar with pictorial demonstration of age and development specific stimulating activities facilitating different development components such as psycho-social, physical & motor, language, creative & aesthetic aspects. The Calendar is categorized for three age groups i.e. for less than one year, 1-2 years & 2-3. It shall be distributed each month by the AWW to the concerned parents during Ankur Diwas to ensure timely accessibility. The AWW would sensitise the parents and caregivers so that they can engage with their children and monitor its cognitive and physical development.
***B. Toy Kit:***
Funds for preparing toys at Anganwadi centres shall be released under Aama Kuni Pilaa scheme. DSWOs, shall share the guidelines and funds to CDPOs, who shall then transfer the same to the AWWs to prepare low cost and child friendly toys for under three children, in coordination with parents and community. The AWW would undertake due discussions with Matru Committee/ Jaanch committee/ parents to create awareness amongst them about the scheme and its benefits and also to keep them involved. A copy of the toy catalogue attached at [Annexure-A](https://wcd.odisha.gov.in/sites/default/files/2025-06/21.%2026179_ECCE-Aama%20Kuni%20Pilaa.pdf.pdf) to this guideline may be referred to while developing the Toy Kit guidelines at District level. Operation modalities and budget break up for toy preparation is attached at [Annexure-B](https://wcd.odisha.gov.in/sites/default/files/2025-06/21.%2026179_ECCE-Aama%20Kuni%20Pilaa.pdf.pdf) for your reference.
***C. Training:***
State level training on Aama Kuni Pilaa shall be organized at HETC, Bhubaneswar wherein all Als, retired officials such as CDPOs/ DSWOs and Freelancers shall be trained. Thereafter, the trained State Level resource personnel shall conduct physical training at district level. District level trainings for the CDPOs, LSs & AWWs shall be organized at AWW and Helper Training Centres of the District.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The beneficiary must be a child aged 0 to 3 years, residing in Odisha.
1. All children in the 0–3 years age group who are enrolled in the Poshan Tracker shall be considered as the targeted beneficiaries under this scheme.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
The applicant/beneficiary must contact the nearest Anganwadi Centre or approach the Anganwadi Worker, ICDS officials, or the Child Development Project Officer (CDPO) to avail the benefits under the scheme.
***Notes:***

- Families with children aged 0–3 years are identified through Anganwadi Centres by the Anganwadi Workers.
- The Anganwadi Worker conducts regular interactions with parents and caregivers to guide them on early stimulation and learning activities for children.
- The Kuni Calendar is distributed every month during Ankur Diwas to the concerned parents to ensure timely access to age-specific stimulation activities.
- Toy Kits are prepared at Anganwadi Centres using funds released through the District Social Welfare Officer to the Child Development Project Officer and then to the Anganwadi Worker.
- ICDS officials and Anganwadi Workers receive State-level and District-level training before carrying out community-based activities under the scheme.
- Continuous monitoring is carried out by Supervisors and Child Development Project Officers at the Sector, Block, District and State levels.
- The Anganwadi Worker maintains proper registers to record the stock of Toy Kits, Kuni Calendars and other materials supplied under the scheme.
- Good-quality photographs of activities, toy preparation, home visits and parent engagement sessions are taken and shared for documentation.
- Case studies are prepared every year and disseminated to highlight successful practices and outcomes.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Birth certificate of child
- Any other documents (as per the requirement)
Note: The families with children aged 0-3 years are identified directly through Anganwadi Centres, and all activities (Kuni Calendar distribution, toy-kit preparation, home visits, and stimulation sessions) are conducted through the ICDS system.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can benefit from this scheme?
content.text: Families with children aged 0–3 years, identified through their local Anganwadi Centre, are the main beneficiaries.

content.section_title: FAQ: How are children enrolled in this programme?
content.text: Children are identified via Anganwadi Workers who interact with parents and caregivers and include eligible children listed at the Anganwadi Centre.

content.section_title: FAQ: What does the scheme provide for children?
content.text: It provides a monthly pictorial “Kuni Calendar” and a low-cost, child-friendly Toy Kit to support age-appropriate learning and play.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the “Kuni Calendar”?
content.text: It’s a monthly calendar with pictorial guidance on stimulating activities tailored to a child’s age group (0–1, 1–2, and 2–3 years) covering development areas like motor, language, psychosocial, and creative skills.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is in the Toy Kit?
content.text: The Toy Kit contains low-cost, safe, and appropriate toys made at the Anganwadi Centre with guidance, to help children engage in play-based learning.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who makes the toys in the Toy Kit?
content.text: AWWs (Anganwadi Workers) coordinate with parents, the Matru Committee, and the community to prepare the toys locally.

content.section_title: FAQ: Do fathers or male caregivers take part in the scheme?
content.text: Yes, the scheme emphasizes “men-streaming,” encouraging fathers and male caregivers to participate in child stimulation and toy-making.

content.section_title: FAQ: If my child is 2.5 years old, can we still benefit?
content.text: Yes, children in the 0–3 years age group are included, and they receive the relevant calendar and Toy Kit materials.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is “Ankur Diwas”?
content.text: It is the monthly day when the Anganwadi Worker meets parents and caregivers to give them the Kuni Calendar and share guidance on its use.

title: Ayurswasthya Yojana: AYUSH for Sports Medicine
topic: Ayurswasthya Yojana: AYUSH for Sports Medicine

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme-component aims to promote AYUSH interventions for the treatment and rehabilitation of sports injuries and to enhance health and fitness among sports persons.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The scheme-component "AYUSH for Sports Medicine" under the "Ayurswasthya Yojana" by the Ministry of Ayush, Government of India, aims to provide AYUSH-based treatment and rehabilitation services to sports persons. AYUSH Sports medicine is a unique concept where, the treatment will be provided on sports injuries and promote the health and fitness of sports persons through AYUSH intervention. Fit India Movement is a nation-wide movement in India to encourage people to remain healthy and fit by including physical activities and sports in their daily lives.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
> **Financial Assistance:**

- Maximum grant of ₹1,00,00,000/- for one project for 18 months.
- The funds will be released in two installments of 60% and 40% of the total amount sanctioned. The second installment will be released after receiving expenditure of at least 75% of the released amount and after acceptance of UC thereof.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
- The AYUSH teaching Institutions and AYUSH hospitals can apply for the scheme.
- The Institutions/ Organisations should be having collaboration with only recognized National and International sports bodies such as the Sports Authority of India, All India Football Federation, Indian Olympic Association, The Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI), International Cricket Council (ICC), etc.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
> **Registration Process:**

**Step 1:** Visit the [Ayush NGO Portal](https://ngo.ayush.gov.in/register).
**Step 2:** Fill in all the mandatory details and upload the relevant documents.
**Step 3:** Verify the captcha and click on "Register". If required, click "Cancel" to discard changes.
> **Login Process:**

**Step 1:** Visit the [Ayush NGO Portal](https://ngo.ayush.gov.in/login).
**Step 2:** Enter the registered email id, password.
**Step 3:** Type the captcha and click on "Login".

- User Manual: [Click here](https://ngo.ayush.gov.in/uploads/assets/Frontend_User_Manual_v-1.2.pdf).  - Contact Us: [Click here](https://ngo.ayush.gov.in/contact-us).

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
- Eligible organizations should apply in the prescribed format ([Annexure- A](https://ayush.gov.in/resources/pdf/schemes/Ayurswasthya.pdf)) along with a detailed proposal (two copies) to:
> **Director (Schemes),**

> **Ministry of Ayurveda, Yoga & Naturopathy, Unani, Siddha and Homoeopathy,**

> **AYUSH Bhawan, B-Block, GPO Complex, INA, New Delhi - 110023.**

content.text:
Documents:- Application as per Annexure – A.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the "AYUSH for Sports Medicine" component under the "Ayurswasthya Yojana"?
content.text: The AYUSH for Sports Medicine component is a scheme initiative that supports AYUSH-based interventions for prevention, treatment, and rehabilitation of sports-related injuries and for promoting fitness among sports persons.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the main objectives of the "AYUSH for Sports Medicine" component?
content.text: The objectives of this component are to promote AYUSH interventions for health and fitness of sports persons and to support AYUSH therapeutic interventions for treatment and rehabilitation of sports-related injuries.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can apply for funding under this component?
content.text: AYUSH teaching institutions and AYUSH hospitals with collaboration with recognized national or international sports bodies are eligible to apply for the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What type of collaborations are required for eligibility?
content.text: The applicant institution must have collaboration with recognized sports bodies such as Sports Authority of India, All India Football Federation, Indian Olympic Association, BCCI, ICC, or other recognized national or international sports organizations.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the minimum beneficiary coverage under one project?
content.text: Each approved project should benefit a minimum of 200 sports persons.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the maximum financial assistance provided under this component?
content.text: The scheme provides financial assistance up to ₹1.00 crore for one project with a duration of up to 18 months.

content.section_title: FAQ: How is the grant amount released to the implementing institution?
content.text: The grant amount is released in two installments of 60% and 40% of the total sanctioned amount.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the conditions for release of the second installment?
content.text: The second installment is released after at least 75% utilization of the first installment and submission and acceptance of the Utilization Certificate.

content.section_title: FAQ: What types of interventions can be supported under this component?
content.text: The component supports AYUSH interventions for sports injury treatment, rehabilitation, fitness promotion, and preventive health measures for sports persons.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is the "Fit India" Movement linked to this component?
content.text: This component aligns with the "Fit India" Movement and supports national efforts to promote physical activity, sports, and healthy lifestyles through AYUSH interventions.

title: Ayurswasthya Yojana: Up-gradation of Facilities to Centre of Excellence (CoE)
topic: Ayurswasthya Yojana: Up-gradation of Facilities to Centre of Excellence (CoE)

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme-component aims to support the establishment and up-gradation of reputed institutions to Centres of Excellence for strengthening AYUSH education, research, innovation, and advanced healthcare services.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
The scheme-component "AYUSH and Public Health" under the "Ayurswasthya Yojana" by the Ministry of Ayush, Government of India, provides financial assistance to reputed AYUSH and allied institutions to develop advanced facilities, research capacity, and specialized healthcare services. The scheme promotes innovation, education technology, and international collaboration in AYUSH systems. It also seeks to enhance infrastructure and professional competencies to promote AYUSH at national and international levels.
> **Main activities under the scheme:**

- Up-gradation of facilities and infrastructure in AYUSH hospitals.
- Development of research and development activities in AYUSH medicines and therapeutics.
- Provision of specialized AYUSH healthcare services to patients.
- Professional competency development of AYUSH professionals.
- Development and sharing of AYUSH activity data with the Ministry of AYUSH.
- Implementation of specialized AYUSH projects and innovations.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
- **Financial Assistance:** Financial assistance of up to ₹10,00,00,000/- for a period of 3 years is provided to eligible institutions for establishing and upgrading Centres of Excellence.  - **Release of Installment-Based Fund:** Funds are released in 3 installments of 40%, 40%, and 20% of the total sanctioned amount, ensuring phased and performance-linked financial support.  - **Performance-Linked Disbursement:** The second and third installments are released only after expenditure of at least 75% of the released amount and submission and acceptance of the Utilization Certificate and satisfactory performance report.  - **Construction Cost Limit:** Expenditure on the construction of buildings is capped at 30% of the approved project budget.
> **Note:** Purchase of land /vehicle, **except ambulance** and foreign travel, is not allowed from the grant.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
> **Eligible Organisation:**

- Ayush teaching institutions recognised under the [IMCC Act, 1970](https://www.indiacode.nic.in/bitstream/123456789/19303/1/a1970-48_0.pdf), and the [HCC Act, 1973](https://www.indiacode.nic.in/bitstream/123456789/19294/1/homoeopathy_central_council_act%2C_1973.pdf),- Other Central/ State Govt. institutes,
- Government-aided Institutions,
- Undertaking/ Universities/ Research Councils/ Autonomous Organisations,
- Non–profit Non-Governmental organisations engaged in one or more specialisations in AYUSH,
- Reputed tertiary care hospitals with at least 250 beds aiming to establish AYUSH tertiary healthcare and research facilities.
- Non-AYUSH reputed research and educational institutes willing to undertake AYUSH projects.

> **Eligibility Criteria:**

- The organisation should furnish the necessary evidence for its potential in having achieved a level of eminence in the respective field at state level, and further potential to become a Centre of Excellence at the National level, as per the illustrative list placed at Annexure – D of the scheme guidelines.
- The organisation should be registered under the Societies Registration Act / Trust / Companies Act or any other Act.
- The applicant should have a competent core staff in the field in which the Centre of Excellence is proposed.
- The applicant should have a credible and transparent governing body and management committee consisting of reputed persons.
- Applicant shall have to make information available regarding any assistance if ever taken from the Government, along with the details of Utilization Certificate (UC) status of the previous grant availed.
- The institute should have a valid NABH/NAAC or other relevant accreditation/certification.

content.section_title: FAQ: 1. What is the objective of the "Centre of Excellence (CoE)" component?
content.text: The objective of the CoE component is to support the establishment and up-gradation of reputed institutions to strengthen AYUSH education, research, innovation, and advanced healthcare services at national and international levels.

content.section_title: FAQ: Which organizations are eligible to apply for the CoE component?
content.text: Eligible organizations include recognized AYUSH teaching institutions, Central or State Government institutes, Government-aided institutions, universities, research councils, autonomous organizations, non-profit organizations, reputed tertiary care hospitals, and reputed non-AYUSH research and educational institutes willing to undertake AYUSH projects.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the minimum experience required for AYUSH institutions to apply?
content.text: The applicant AYUSH institution should have a minimum track record of 5 years of meritorious work in the AYUSH sector in the proposed area of Centre of Excellence activity.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the maximum financial assistance available under this component?
content.text: The maximum financial assistance available under this component is ₹10,00,00,000 for a period of 3 years for one approved project.

content.section_title: FAQ: How are funds released to the approved institutions?
content.text: Funds are released in 3 installments of 40%, 40%, and 20% of the total sanctioned amount, subject to satisfactory performance and submission of Utilization Certificates.

content.section_title: FAQ: What conditions apply for the release of the second and third installments?
content.text: The second and third installments are released after the institution has utilized at least 75% of the released funds and submitted satisfactory achievement-cum-performance reports and accepted Utilization Certificates.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is construction of buildings allowed under the CoE grant?
content.text: Construction of buildings is allowed under the approved budget, but the expenditure on construction is capped at 30% of the total approved project cost.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can vehicles be purchased under the CoE grant?
content.text: Vehicles cannot be purchased under the CoE grant, except for ambulances which are permitted for healthcare service delivery.

content.section_title: FAQ: What facilities must be provided to Economically Weaker Sections (EWS)?
content.text: The grantee institution must ensure that at least 25% of the facilities created using Government grants are provided free of cost or at concessional rates to Economically Weaker Sections for at least 10 years after the release of the last installment.

content.section_title: FAQ: What responsibilities do hospitals have under the CoE component?
content.text: Hospitals are required to maintain IPD and OPD data, provide services to EWS patients, maintain grievance redressal systems, share treatment outcomes with the Ministry of AYUSH, ensure bed occupancy above 50%, and collaborate with national and international reputed organizations.

content.section_title: FAQ: What training and academic activities are expected from CoE institutions?
content.text: CoE institutions are expected to conduct national and international seminars, provide training to scholars, and develop AYUSH resource centers for education and capacity building after completion of the project.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are purchase of land and foreign travel allowed under the CoE grant?
content.text: Purchase of land and foreign travel are not allowed under the CoE grant, and only expenditures permitted under the approved project budget can be incurred.

title: Ayurswasthya Yojana: AYUSH and Public Health
topic: Ayurswasthya Yojana: AYUSH and Public Health

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme-component aims to promote AYUSH-based interventions to improve community health outcomes by supporting preventive, promotive, curative, and rehabilitative healthcare services.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The scheme-component "AYUSH and Public Health" under the "Ayurswasthya Yojana" by the Ministry of Ayush, Government of India, aims to promote AYUSH interventions through funding healthcare projects for public health issues affecting communities. It also aims to support for implementing Sustainable Development Goal-2 (SDG2) and Sustainable Development Goal -3 (SDG 3) through integrating AYUSH system.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
> **Financial Assistance:**

- Maximum grant of ₹1,50,00,000/- for a project duration of 3 years.
- Funds are released in installments of 40%, 40%, and 20%.

> **Note:** The grant received under the scheme should be utilized as under:-

- Not more than 30% of the total grant should be spent on Establishment / Project Management.
- Not LESS than 50% of the total grant should be spent on Medicines, laboratory testing or other investigations.
- Remaining 20% of the total grant may be spent on AYUSH awareness, minor Equipment etc., for the project.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
- The applicant organization must have credible project management expertise and core staff.
- Applicant / organization (Other than AYUSH teaching institute registered under [IMCC Act, 1970](https://www.indiacode.nic.in/bitstream/123456789/19303/1/a1970-48_0.pdf) and [HCC Act 1973](https://www.indiacode.nic.in/bitstream/123456789/19294/1/homoeopathy_central_council_act%2C_1973.pdf)) should have track record of minimum 5 years of working in the field of community health. The organization should furnish the necessary evidence for implementing such programmes in the past.  - Applicant / organization shall have to make information available regarding any assistance taken from any State/Central Agency in the last 5 years.
- The organizations (other than the AYUSH teaching institutes recognized under [IMCC Act, 1970](https://www.indiacode.nic.in/bitstream/123456789/19303/1/a1970-48_0.pdf) and [HCC Act, 1973](https://www.indiacode.nic.in/bitstream/123456789/19294/1/homoeopathy_central_council_act%2C_1973.pdf) and the Central/ State Govt. institutes) must submit their application in the Proforma given at Annexure – C, by the State Government officer of the respective State / UT concerned. Recommendations of the State / UT should clearly mention the following points:-

1. About the credibility of the organization and capability of applicant organization in
1. Implementing such public health initiatives project,
1. Past experience,
1. Relevance and usefulness of the project.

- The State Government has to forward the proposal in the prescribed Proforma within 60 days of submission of the proposal by the applicant organization to the State. In case, no response is received from State Government, it shall be presumed that State Government has no specific comments against the applicant organization and Ministry of AYUSH will consider the proposal accordingly.

> **Eligible Organisation:**

- Directorates of Health/AYUSH of States / UTs.
- Government Institutes dealing with public health.
- Non-profit/Voluntary organizations working in the area of public health for at least five years and having a proven track record and a capable team comprising of public health specialists.
- Government aided Institutions/ Undertaking/ Universities/ Research Councils, Autonomous Organizations and Non – Profit Non-Governmental organizations engaged in one or more of the above fields listed in the introduction to the scheme.
- Any reputed organization or stakeholders dealing with sports activities in India (only for proposals on AYUSH Sports medicine).
- Collaboration of United Nations and International Organizations will be encouraged.

content.section_title: Exclusions
content.text: - Any institution that has obtained funding from any other Central Government Ministry/ Department for the same purpose in the last five years shall not be eligible for funding under this scheme.

**Step 1:** Visit the [Ayush NGO Portal](https://ngo.ayush.gov.in/register).
**Step 2:** Fill in all the mandatory details and upload the relevant documents.

- Detailed Project Proposal as per Scheme Guidelines.
- State Certificate (Annexure C).
- Major Achievements of the Organisation in Last 5 Years (Annual Reports).
- Details of the infrastructure available in the Organisation Along with Equipment and Vehicles.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the "AYUSH and Public Health" component under "Ayurswasthya Yojana"?
content.text: The "AYUSH and Public Health" component is a Central Sector Grant-in-Aid initiative that supports AYUSH-based interventions to improve community health outcomes.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the main objective of this component?
content.text: The main objective of this component is to demonstrate the effectiveness of AYUSH interventions in public health and integrate them into national health programmes.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can apply for funding under this component?
content.text: Eligible applicants include State Directorates of Health or AYUSH, Government institutions, universities, research councils, and non-profit organizations with relevant experience.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the minimum experience required for non-profit organizations?
content.text: A non-profit organization should have at least 5 years of experience in public health or AYUSH-related activities to be eligible.

content.section_title: FAQ: What types of health issues can be covered under this component?
content.text: Projects can cover communicable diseases, non-communicable diseases, maternal and child health, geriatric care, mental health, disaster management, and other public health concerns.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the minimum population coverage requirement?
content.text: The proposal should generally cover at least one block and benefit a minimum of 1,000 persons, unless relaxed by the competent authority in special areas.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the maximum financial assistance provided under this component?
content.text: The scheme provides financial assistance up to ₹1,50,00,000/- for a project duration of up to 3 years.

content.section_title: FAQ: How is the grant amount released to the selected organization?
content.text: The grant amount is released in three installments of 40%, 40%, and 20%, subject to submission of required reports and utilization certificates.

content.section_title: FAQ: What conditions must be met for release of subsequent installments?
content.text: Subsequent installments are released after at least 75% utilization of the previous installment and submission of Utilization Certificates and progress reports.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can an organization receive funding if it has received similar funding earlier?
content.text: The organization should not have received funding for the same purpose from another Central Government Ministry in the last 5 years.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are collaborations with other organizations allowed?
content.text: Collaboration with national and international organizations is encouraged to strengthen project implementation and outcomes.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the responsibilities of the selected implementing organization?
content.text: The implementing organization must execute the project as approved, maintain records, submit reports, and ensure proper utilization of funds.

content.section_title: FAQ: What happens if the funds are misused or conditions are violated?
content.text: In case of misuse or deviation from scheme conditions, the Ministry can recover the grant amount with 12% interest and take appropriate legal action.

title: Awareness Programme on Child/Human Trafficking, Family Planning, Legal Awareness, Health etc.
topic: Awareness Programme on Child/Human Trafficking, Family Planning, Legal Awareness, Health etc.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to generate awareness among the Tea Tribes and Adivasi community regarding child/human trafficking, family planning, legal awareness, and health. It generates awareness benefits for the Tea Tribes and Adivasi community through Non-Governmental Organizations proposals.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The scheme "Awareness Programme on Child/Human Trafficking, Family Planning, Legal Awareness, Health etc." was launched by the Tea Tribes and Adivasi Welfare Department, Government of Assam. The scheme aims to generate awareness among the Tea Tribes and Adivasi community on critical social issues. The scheme provides a platform to generate awareness regarding child/human trafficking, family planning, legal awareness, and health issues such as the reduction of Maternal Mortality Rate, Infant Mortality Rate, and Alcoholism, along with maintaining hygiene. The applications for this scheme are accepted by inviting proposals through Expression of Interest (EOI).

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
- The benefit is to generate awareness among the Tea Tribes and Adivasi community on issues like child/human trafficking, family planning, legal awareness, and health (reduction of Maternal Mortality Rate, Infant Mortality Rate, Alcoholism, and maintenance of hygiene).
- This awareness generation aims towards bringing effective social and behavioural change amongst the community.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
- The applicant must be a Non-Governmental Organization.
- The applicant must have valid registration under Societies Act, XXI of 1860.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Step 1:** The implementation of the scheme is initiated by issuing proposals inviting Non-Governmental Organizations (NGOs).
**Step 2:** Eligible NGOs must submit their proposals for the implementation of the scheme by responding to the Expression of Interest (EOI).
**Where to Apply**
The details of the scheme guidelines will be available at the official website ([**http://ttwd.assam.gov.in**](http://ttwd.assam.gov.in)).

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Track Record Of Having Experience In Providing Awareness Programmes Which Includes Child/Human Trafficking, Family Planning, Reducing Child Labour, Improvement Of Health, Prioritizing Girls Education, Reduction Of Maternal Mortality Rate, Infant Mortality Rate, Alcoholism, Maintenance Of Hygiene Etc. (With Documentary Evidence Such As Audit Report, Photographic Evidence, Etc.).
- Details Regarding The Availability Of Well Trained Staff, Infrastructure, And The Required Managerial Expertise To Organize And Carry Out The Activities Under The Scheme (With Proper Details).
- Extent Of Experience In The Number Of Districts (Please Indicate The Number/District).
- Years Of Experience By The Non-Governmental Organization (With Documentary Evidence).
- Documentation Regarding Any Financial Assistance Received From Government (If Yes, Provide Documentary Evidence).
- Total Population Covered Till Date (Indicate The Number Of Population).
- Any Action Plan By The Non-Governmental Organization Including The Proposed Number Of Camps And Geographical Area To Be Covered Under The Selected District.

content.section_title: FAQ: What specific legal requirement must our organization meet concerning our official registration status before submitting a proposal for implementation?
content.text: Non-Governmental Organizations applying for this scheme must possess valid registration under the Societies Act.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the mandatory nature of the entity that must apply for this particular program designed for social awareness generation?
content.text: The applicant must strictly be a Non-Governmental Organization possessing valid registration under the relevant Societies Act.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any preferential treatment or weightage provided during the selection process based on the operational location of our Non-Governmental Organization?
content.text: Yes, preference will be extended to Non-Governmental Organizations that are based within the Tea Garden areas.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the rationale behind offering an advantage or prioritization to Non-Governmental Organizations situated specifically in Tea Garden locations?
content.text: Preference is given to Tea Garden based Non-Governmental Organizations because they are presumed to be well aware of the specific environment and dynamics of the garden area.

content.section_title: FAQ: What specific range of social and health issues should our organization have a prior track record of experience in conducting awareness programs for?
content.text: Your organization should have experience in awareness programs covering child/human trafficking, family planning, reducing child labour, and improvement of health, among others.

content.section_title: FAQ: What types of supplementary documentation should be included in the submission package to prove the organization’s previous experience and track record?
content.text: You must provide documentary evidence of your track record, which should include an Audit Report, Photographic Evidence, and any other relevant proof.

content.section_title: FAQ: Does the proposal need to detail the internal capacity of the organization regarding personnel and logistical setup necessary for managing the scheme activities?
content.text: Yes, the proposal must provide proper details regarding the availability of well-trained staff, adequate infrastructure, and the required managerial expertise necessary to carry out the activities under the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: How should the Non-Governmental Organization quantify or elaborate on the geographical scope of its past awareness work during the application submission?
content.text: Applicants must clearly specify the extent of their experience by indicating the number and specific names of the Districts where activities have been previously conducted.

content.section_title: FAQ: What form of confirmation or proof is required to certify the total number of years the Non-Governmental Organization has been operational?
content.text: The Non-Governmental Organization needs to submit documentary evidence demonstrating its total years of experience in the relevant field.

content.section_title: FAQ: If our organization has previously received monetary support from government bodies, is documentation of such financial assistance required for this application?
content.text: Yes, you must provide documentary evidence detailing any financial assistance that your Non-Governmental Organization has received from the Government.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is it necessary to provide an accurate estimate of the number of individuals or communities that the organization has successfully served or impacted thus far?
content.text: Yes, the organization must indicate the number of the total population covered by its awareness activities till the date of application.

content.section_title: FAQ: Should the proposal outline future operational strategies, specifically detailing the intended outreach efforts for the selected district?
content.text: Yes, the Non-Governmental Organization must include an action plan detailing the proposed number of camps and the specific Geographical area intended to be covered under the selected district.

content.section_title: FAQ: Through what official mechanism or process does the implementing agency invite organizations to submit their proposals for this awareness scheme?
content.text: Implementation of this specific scheme is carried out by issuing proposals that invite Non-Governmental Organizations to respond through an Expression of Interest (EOI).

content.section_title: FAQ: Are there particular health metrics, such as mortality rates or substance abuse, on which the organization should demonstrate a history of conducting impactful awareness programs?
content.text: Yes, required experience includes programs aimed at the reduction of Maternal Mortality Rate, Infant Mortality Rate, Alcoholism, and maintaining hygiene.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: Under this scheme, all babies born to BPL mothers in the State (approximately 3,00,000 births per annum) will be covered for neonatal care by partnering pediatricians, including care in their Neonatal Intensive Care Unit (level 2 ) at no cost to the beneficiary.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
1. The Bal Sakha Yojana is a community-based program that aims to provide support to children in need of care and protection. The scheme is implemented by the Department of Women and Child Development in collaboration with the NHM and the State Child Protection Society.
1. A network of trained volunteers called "Bal Sakhis" are deployed in villages and urban areas to identify and report cases of child abuse, neglect, and exploitation.
1. The Bal Sakhis also provide support to families in need of assistance to ensure the well-being of their children. The scheme also provides financial assistance to children in need of care and protection.
1. The scheme is targeted towards vulnerable children in the age group of 0-18 years, especially those from socially and economically disadvantaged backgrounds. The Bal Sakha Yojana aims to ensure the overall development and well-being of children by providing them with necessary support and protection.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
1. Under this scheme, expert care will be provided to newborn babies to ensure child health.
1. The paediatrician attends all eligible newborns at the place of birth and has to ensure their survival by providing early neonatal care including immunizations at birth, nutrition advice.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text: - All newborns up to 30 days old belonging to Below Poverty Line (BPL) families and Scheduled Caste categories, who do not pay income tax, will be eligible for benefits.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
1. Women can contact primary social workers ASHA present in all village and urban area
1. Anganwadi centres
1. Women can contact to District Development officer or Chief District Health Officer if she faces any obstacles while availing the of benefits.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Aadhaar card
- BPL card/Ration Card
- Scheduled caste certificate, if applicable
- Authorization by a locally recognized authority
- Application form is available from the hospital itself
- Income certificate
- Any other documents as required

content.section_title: FAQ: What kind of care does the Bal Sakha Yojana provide to newborn babies?
content.text: The Bal Sakha Yojana provides expert care to newborn babies to ensure child health. This includes early neonatal care, immunizations at birth, and nutrition advice.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who attends to eligible newborns under the Bal Sakha Yojana?
content.text: Under the scheme, a paediatrician attends all eligible newborns at the place of birth and ensures their survival by providing the necessary care and support.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the objective of the Bal Sakha Yojana in terms of child health?
content.text: The primary objective of the Bal Sakha Yojana is to ensure the health and wellbeing of newborn babies, particularly those from vulnerable sections of society, by providing expert care and support.

content.section_title: FAQ: Does the Bal Sakha Yojana cover all newborn babies?
content.text: No, only eligible newborn babies are covered under the Bal Sakha Yojana. The specific eligibility criteria may vary depending on the details and objectives of the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: How does the Bal Sakha Yojana help in improving child health?
content.text: By providing expert care and support to newborn babies, the Bal Sakha Yojana can help in improving child health and reducing infant mortality rates. The early neonatal care, immunizations, and nutrition advice provided under the scheme can help in ensuring the healthy growth and development of newborn babies.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible to apply for the Bal Sakha Yojana?
content.text: The eligibility criteria for the Bal Sakha Yojana may vary, but generally, children who are orphans, abandoned, or destitute are eligible to apply.

content.section_title: FAQ: What kind of healthcare institutions are eligible for the Bal Sakha Yojana?
content.text: Children born in private hospitals or government healthcare institutions that are running the Chiranjeevi Yojana are eligible for the Bal Sakha Yojana.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are there any income-based eligibility criteria for the Bal Sakha Yojana?
content.text: Yes, all newborns up to 30 days old belonging to Below Poverty Line (BPL) families and Scheduled Caste categories, who do not pay income tax, will be eligible for benefits.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can children born at home apply for the Bal Sakha Yojana?
content.text: Yes, children born at home, subcenters, or PHCs are also eligible for the Bal Sakha Yojana

content.section_title: FAQ: How can women apply for the Bal Sakha Yojana?
content.text: Women can contact the primary social worker ASHA present in all village and urban areas or visit the Anganwadi centres to apply for the Bal Sakha Yojana.

content.section_title: FAQ: What should a woman do if she faces any obstacles while availing the benefits of the Bal Sakha Yojana?
content.text: Women can contact the District Development Officer or Chief District Health Officer if they face any obstacles while availing the benefits of the Bal Sakha Yojana. These authorities can provide guidance and assistance to ensure that women can access the benefits they are entitled to under the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What type of ration card is required to apply for the Bal Sakha Yojana?
content.text: Women are required to have a BPL (Below Poverty Line) ration card to apply for the Bal Sakha Yojana.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is an authorization by a local recognized authority, and is it necessary for the application process?
content.text: An authorization by a locally recognized authority is a document that certifies that the applicant is eligible for the scheme. It is necessary for the application process of the Bal Sakha Yojana

content.section_title: FAQ: Where can women find the application form for the Bal Sakha Yojana?
content.text: The application form is available from the hospital itself. Women can ask the hospital staff for the application form.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to reduce severe malnutrition and child mortality by providing free institutional care, diet, and medical treatment to severely malnourished children below 5 years of age.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The “Bal Shakti Yojana” was launched by the Department of Public Health and Medical Education, Government of Madhya Pradesh. It aims to reduce child mortality caused by severe malnutrition among children below 5 years of age. The scheme provides free medical care, nutrition rehabilitation, diet, and transport through 315 Nutrition Rehabilitation Centres (NRCs) across the state.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
- Free institutional management and treatment for severely malnourished and sick children at Nutrition Rehabilitation Centres (NRCs).
- Free balanced diet
- Free medical check-up, medicines, and diagnostic services for enrolled children.
- Free transport facility.
- Counselling and training sessions.
- Nutritional awareness.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The child should be a resident of Madhya Pradesh.
1. The applicant should be a child below 5 years of age.
1. The child must be severely malnourished or sick and meet at least one of the following criteria:

- Mid-Upper Arm Circumference (MUAC) less than 11.5 cm.
- Weight-for-height Z-score less than -3 SD (standard deviation).
- Bilateral pitting oedema (swelling on both feet).

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
- The child is identified by health workers (ANM/ASHA/AWW) during community surveys or health check-ups.
- Eligible children are referred to the nearest Nutrition Rehabilitation Centre (NRC).
- On arrival, the child undergoes medical examination and anthropometric assessment to confirm eligibility.
- Upon meeting admission criteria, the child and mother/caregiver are admitted free of cost.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text: No documents are required to avail the benefits of this scheme. The child is identified by health workers (ANM/ASHA/AWW) during community surveys or health check-ups.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who are the beneficiaries?
content.text: Severely malnourished and sick children under 5 years of age.

content.section_title: FAQ: What facilities are provided under the scheme?
content.text: Free diet, medicines, medical care, transport, and counselling for the mother.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the criteria for admission to NRC?
content.text: MUAC less than 11.5 cm, weight-for-height below -3 SD, or presence of bilateral oedema.

content.section_title: FAQ: How many NRCs are currently operational in Madhya Pradesh?
content.text: A total of 315 NRCs are functional across the state.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is the service completely free?
content.text: Yes, all services including treatment, stay, and food are provided free of cost.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can refer a child to the NRC?
content.text: Health workers such as ASHA, ANM, or a government doctor.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are urban children eligible for the scheme?
content.text: Yes, the scheme covers both urban and rural areas.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: This initiative aims to ensure the survival, healthy growth, and overall well-being of children aged 0–5 years by providing accessible, comprehensive and quality healthcare services.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The “Bal Swasthya Karyakram” is an initiative under the National Health Mission (NHM) implemented by the Department of Medical Health and Family Welfare, Government of Uttarakhand. This initiative aims to ensure the survival, healthy growth, and overall well-being of children aged 0–5 years by providing accessible, comprehensive, and quality healthcare services across the state.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: Under this Programme, medical services are provided to children aged 0–5 years through Special Newborn Care Units (SNCUs), Newborn Stabilization Units (NBSUs), and Nutritional Rehabilitation Centres (NRCs). Additionally, ASHA workers, Anganwadi workers, and ANMs distribute Vitamin A and IFA syrup free of cost to children aged 0–5 years.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text: 1. The beneficiary must be a child between 0 and 5 years of age from Uttarakhand.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text: SNCU, NBSU, and NBCC are operational in the district hospitals, sub-district hospitals, primary health centers, and community health centers across the state. Services can be availed by registering at these centers.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
1. Birth certificate
2. Other documents as required

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible to benefit from the scheme?
content.text: Children between the ages of 0 to 5 years who are residents of Uttarakhand are eligible to avail services under this scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What type of healthcare services are provided under the programme?
content.text: The programme provides medical care, growth monitoring, nutritional supplementation, immunization support, etc. through health centers across the state.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where are SNCU, NBSU, and NBCC facilities available?
content.text: These facilities are operational in district hospitals, sub-district hospitals, primary health centers, and community health centers across Uttarakhand.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can beneficiaries avail services under the Programme?
content.text: Parents or guardians can avail services by registering their child at the nearest district hospital, sub-district hospital, PHC, or CHC where these units are operational.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the role of ASHA, Anganwadi Workers, and ANMs in this programme?
content.text: They play a key role in community outreach, distributing Vitamin A and IFA syrup to children aged 0–5 months, creating awareness, and ensuring regular health check-ups.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: Aswasakiranam Scheme envisages assisting the caregivers of physically and mentally disabled bedridden patients, who are their family members or relatives. All those who have been providing caregiving service to patients since 02/08/10 will be paid monthly assistance with retrospective effect.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
**Detailed**
Kerala is a state well known for its efficient and effective health care system. Despite these advancements, much headway could not be made in-home care. Most of the lowest income group always opt for homely treatment as they cannot afford the charges levied by the well-known hospitals. They are bedridden causing a heavy social burden on their family members who are forced to stay back at home to look after these bedridden patients. While the upper-income group can possibly afford to engage paid caregivers or home nurses for looking after such bedridden family members, the lower-income group usually take it upon themselves to care for their bedridden brethren. These family members in fact function as informal caregivers, without due recognition or economic gain.

**Coverage Of Diseases**
●	All bed-ridden patients who need a full-time caregiver.
●	Mentally challenged (Autism, Cerebral Palsy, Mental retardation, Mental illness).
●	100 % blind, bed-ridden cancer patients, old-aged bed-ridden.
●	Brittle bone disease.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
**Eligibility**

1. One who is totally bedridden and at home with a debilitating disability (physical, functional or mental).
1. One can not take care of himself/herself for any day-to-day activity without the support of another person.
1. One who belongs to a family, Below Poverty Line as per the BPL list and as per the ration card.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
List of the required documents
- Medical certificate attested by Govt./ Vayomithram / NRHM doctors.
- Attested copy of BPL ration card / BPL certificate issued by Panchayat or Municipality or Corporation / Income certificate issued by village officer as proof for patient income.
- Attested copy of Aadhar card/confirmation slip received.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is "ASWASAKIRANAM" Scheme ?
content.text: Aswasakiranam Scheme envisages assisting the caregivers of physically and mentally disabled bedridden patients, who are their family members or relatives.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any implementing date ?
content.text: Yes, All those who have been providing caregiving service to patients since 02/08/10 will be paid monthly assistance with retrospective effect.

content.section_title: FAQ: What the Disease are cover under the Aswasakiranam ?
content.text:
Coverage Of Diseases
*	All bed-ridden patients who need a full-time caregiver.
*	Mentally challenged (Autism, Cerebral Palsy, Mental retardation, Mental illness).
*	100 % blind, bed-ridden cancer patients, old-aged bed-ridden.
*	Brittle bone disease.

content.section_title: FAQ: What type of assistance will be provided under the Aswasakiranam ?
content.text: This scheme financial assistance of Rs 600 per month is provided to those caregivers who look after their physically and mentally bedridden patients.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the eligibility ?
content.text:
1. One who is totally bedridden and at home with a debilitating disability (physical, functional or mental).
2. One can not take care of himself/herself for any day-to-day activity without the support of another person.
3. One who belongs to a family, Below Poverty Line as per the BPL list and as per the ration card.

content.section_title: FAQ: How to get the application form ?
content.text: The application can be downloaded online or from the nearest Anganwadi or ICDS office.

content.section_title: FAQ: What type of document need to be submit with application form ?
content.text:
1. Medical certificate attested by Govt./ Vayomithram / NRHM doctors.
2. Attested copy of BPL ration card / BPL certificate issued by Panchayat or Municipality or Corporation / Income certificate issued by village officer as proof of patient income.
3. Attested copy of Aadhar card/confirmation slip received.

title: Atal Mission For Rejuvenation And Urban Transformation
topic: Atal Mission For Rejuvenation And Urban Transformation

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: Providing basic services (e.g. water supply, sewerage, urban transport) to households and building amenities in cities which will improve the quality of life for all, especially the poor and the disadvantaged is a national priority.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
The purpose of the Atal Mission for Rejuvenation and Urban Transformation (AMRUT) is to

1. Ensure that every household has access to a tap with the assured supply of water and a sewerage connection.
1. Increase the amenity value of cities by developing greenery and well maintained open spaces (e.g. parks) and
1. Reduce pollution by switching to public transport or constructing facilities for non-motorized transport (e.g. walking and cycling).

All these outcomes are valued by citizens, particularly women, and indicators and standards have been prescribed by the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs (MoHUA ) in the form of Service Level Benchmarks (SLBs).

#### Thrust Areas
The Mission will focus on the following Thrust Areas:

1. Water Supply
1. Sewerage and septage management
1. Storm Water Drainage to reduce flooding
1. Non-motorized Urban Transport
1. Green space/parks

#### Coverage
**Five hundred cities** have been selected under AMRUT. The category of cities that have been selected under AMRUT is given below:

1. All Cities and Towns with a population of over one lakh with notified Municipalities as per Census 2011, including Cantonment Boards (Civilian areas),
1. All Capital Cities/Towns of States/ UTs, not covered in above ,
1. All  Cities/  Towns  classified  as  Heritage  Cities  by  MoHUA  under  the  HRIDAY Scheme,
1. Thirteen Cities and Towns on the stem of the main rivers with a population above 75,000 and less than 1 lakh, and
1. Ten Cities from hill states, islands and tourist destinations (not more than one from each State).

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
This is an open scheme and there is no eligibility required for the scheme.

Five hundred cities have been selected under AMRUT.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text: There are no documents required for the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is my city listed in the 500 cities selected for AMRUT Mission?
content.text:
You can check the list of cities here.
http://amrut.gov.in/content/citiescovered_map.php

content.section_title: FAQ: Can the ULB hire consultant for the SLIP preparation?
content.text: The MoHUA has simplified the process for SLIP preparation and is also hand -holding the cities. It is for States/ ULBs to decide on hiring the services of a consultant.

content.section_title: FAQ: 2. Is there any limit to the project cost for each sector that the ULBs will propose for SLIP?
content.text: No, but the states/ ULBs has to give consideration to the National Priorities of the Mission and the Guidelines. It is universal coverage water and sewerage (Para 5.1, 6.5 and 6.6).

content.section_title: FAQ: How ULB Will Share 30% In AMRUT? It Is Very Difficult Of ULBs Which Are Depended On States.
content.text: The contribution of State Govt is minimum 20%. There is no upper limit on State share in SAAP. The States / ULBs may decide in accordance with para 7.4 and 7.5 of guidelines.

content.section_title: FAQ: What shall be the general order of prioritization of projects in water and sewerage sector?
content.text: Universal coverage of water supply shall be given top priority. Next will be universal coverage of sewerage connection to all households.

content.section_title: FAQ: How should replacement of pipes be decided in identifying the activities?
content.text: Replacement of pipe line shall be considered only if it leads to NRW reduction in case of water supply and improvement in collection efficiency in case of sewerage.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is separate budget for city mission management units and state mission management shall be provided?
content.text: The AMRUT Mission focuses on 500 target cities/ULBs. 10% of the annual budget allocation shall be kept apart and given to the States/UTs every year as incentive for achievement of Reforms. The reforms achievement will be a result of the joint efforts of Mission ULBs/State Government. The Guidelines of the Mission provide that the incentive award will only be used in Mission Cities on admissible components of the AMRUT.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can the incentive for Reforms be extended to ULBs other than AMRUT cities?
content.text: The AMRUT Mission focuses on 500 target cities/ULBs. 10% of the annual budget allocation shall be kept apart and given to the States/UTs every year as incentive for achievement of Reforms. The reforms achievement will be a result of the joint efforts of Mission ULBs/State Government. The Guidelines of the Mission provide that the incentive award will only be used in Mission Cities on admissible components of the AMRUT.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can the Capacity Building be extended to ULBs other than AMRUT cities?
content.text: The Capacity Building component aims at training of 45000 personnel from the Mission Cities. Presently only personnel from the Mission Cities will be preferred.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: Gujarat Chief Minister Shri Vijay Rupani here today announced the Atal ‘Screening of Newborn for Enhancement of Health’ (SNEH) Yojna to be started in the state from the former Prime Minister Atal Behari Vajpayee’s birthday on December 25, 2016.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: **Detailed** Atal Sneh Yojana in Gujarat is the scheme that has launched on the 25th of December 2016 on the occasion of former Prime Minister Atal Bihari Vajpayee’s ninety-second birthday. The scheme is launched by the Chief Minister of Gujarat, Mr. Vijay Rupani. The scheme will look forward to a healthy child after their birth. It was decided at the Gandhinagar Civil Hospital and it will look upon the children up to two months after their birth. After focusing on the health of the children and covering some major diseases the government has now shifted its focus to core issues. Basically, the Atal Sneh Yojana will replace the decades-old Annual School Program which was being carried out by the health and education departments. For this new scheme, newborn children will be screened specifically to look for health defects and improve them. This is being launched wide across Gujarat. Under this scheme, the newborn kids will be screened and given treatment immediately if there is any illness found. It will be carried out for two long months. **Objectives** 1. Through this scheme, children can be provided with better health. Better health will lead to avoiding malnutrition, disorders, and diseases. 1. Around seven percent of newly born babies every year are suffering from certain disorders and that has created a serious issue in the international platform. So by preventing such trouble, the country can be in a better position in international health conferences. 1. With such schemes, the government is also looking for a stronger young generation and this young generation would lead to having a stronger nation. **some of the dire diseases kids suffer from** 1. The children suffer from some very grave diseases such as complicated birth defects such as Down’s syndrome, congenital deafness, club foot, neural tube defect, etc. 1. These are not all. There are some diseases, which are there present in the dormant state within the genes. By checking the kids at their tender age, the doctors will be able to better treat them. 1. The Chief Minister of the state launched that all of the diseases that are detected at the maternity homes by the government will be taken care of by the government.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
**Eligibility**

1. All Newborn children are eligible.
1. Age should be up to two months after their birth.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
List of the required documents
- Birth Certificate.
- Parents/Guardian Aadhar card
- Other relevant documents related to health issues.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the Atal Sneh Yojna
content.text: The government is looking for a stronger young generation and this young generation would lead to having a stronger nation.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the benefits
content.text: Children Can Be Provided With Better Health. Better Health Will Lead To Avoiding Malnutrition, Disorders, And Diseases.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the age Criteria of newborn child ?
content.text: The child should be up to two months after their birth.

content.section_title: FAQ: How to get the benefits ?
content.text:
1. Visit the nearest Anganwadi Centre or District Child Protection Officer office and obtain the application form.
2. Fill out the application form with all the required details and attach the necessary documents such as birth certificate, income certificate, and Aadhar card.
3. Submit the application form along with the documents to the Anganwadi Centre or District Child Protection Officer's office.
4. The application will be verified by the officials and the eligible applicants will be selected for the scheme.
5. The selected applicants will be provided with the benefits under the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Any list of required documents
content.text:
1. Birth Certificate.
2. Parents/Guardian Aadhar card.
3. Other relevant documents related to health issues.
