content.section_title: Overview
content.text: Biju Swasthya Kalyan Yojana (BSKY) has been launched by the Department of Health and Family Welfare, Government of Odisha as a path-breaking program to provide universal health coverage, with special emphasis on the health protection of vulnerable families and women.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
**Biju Swasthya Kalyan Yojana (BSKY)** is a scheme under the Department of Health and Family Welfare, Government of Odisha aims to provide universal health coverage, with special emphasis on the health protection of vulnerable families and women. Its primary objective is to provide financial protection and improve access to healthcare services for the people of Odisha, particularly targeting economically disadvantaged families. To achieve its objectives, the BSKY has two components:-

1. State Government will bear full cost of all health services delivered to all patients (irrespective of income, status or residence) in all State Government health care facilities starting from Sub center level to District Head Quarter and Government Medical College Hospital and Blood Bank level.
1. State Government will bear the cost of healthcare provided in empaneled private hospitals for over 96.5 lakh economically vulnerable families in the State, amounting to Annual Health coverage of Rs. 5 lakh per family and additional Rs. 5lakh for the women members of the family after exhaust of initial limit.

At all empaneled private hospitals families having BSKY Smart Health Card can avail cashless treatment at any empaneled private hospital under BSKY within or outside the State. Card holder families can avail facilities such as registration, consultation, medical tests, pathologies, treatment, IPD and follow-up consultation under BSKY at any empaneled private hospitals, for which State Government will bear the cost upto the annual coverage amount. The State Government will collect direct Feedback from beneficiaries and Hospitals in respect of this scheme. The citizens can share their feedback on **14545.**  Grievance Redressal Helpline Number is **155369** (6 AM to 10 PM) & 104 (24 x 7).

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
Families having BSKY Smart Health Card/ NFSA/ SFSS card can avail of cashless IPD and Day care treatment for the procedures for which the hospital is empaneled under BSKY within or outside the State up to the annual amount covered.  The scheme provides cashless health coverage of ₹5,00,000 per annum per family and an additional ₹5,00,000 for women members of the family after exhaustion of the initial limit at empaneled private hospitals within and outside Odisha.

- **PACKAGES**

The updated package list under BSKY, as modified from time to time is available on the website www.bsky.odisha.gov.in. About 3% of the total packages are reserved for Government health institutions. About 10% of the packages are under the referral category, which can be availed only on referral by the appropriate authority.
**1. Reserved Packages**
Treatment under these packages is only available in Govt. healthcare facilities. Treatment for minor ailments, and diseases under National Health Programs such as Management of superficial burns, minor injuries, Malaria, Typhoid fever, Gastroenteritis, Diarrhea, Dysentery, Tuberculosis, Urinary Tract Infection, Heat stroke, and surgeries for hydrocele, Abscess drainage, Lipoma and cyst excision and procedures like Dilatation and Curettage and MTP are reserved for Government facilities.
**2. Referral Package**
The updated list of referral packages is available on the website www.bsky.odisha.gov.in.
**3. Open Packages**
Any beneficiary can directly walk into any empaneled private hospital and avail of the treatment under this category of packages if he is found suitable for that package by the treating doctor of the empaneled hospital.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
All BSKY Smart Health Card/ National Food Security Act (NFSA)/ State Food Security Scheme (SFSS) card holders are eligible.
**For the referral of the patient to the empaneled private hospital for treatment:**

1. The referral will be done if the particular package/ procedure cannot be provided by a Government facility in the district.
1. Cases will be referred if there is a long waiting time to undertake the procedure at a Government facility in the district.
1. Where there is an emergency case that requires immediate referral.
1. Referral process under BSKY is decentralized up to the Sub-Divisional Hospital level. The following authorities have been designated to issue referrals to beneficiaries under BSKY.

- **District Headquarter Hospital:** CDM & PHO/ District Medical Officer-cum Superintendent & concerned Specialist.  - **Sub-divisional Hospital:** Superintendent & concerned Specialist.  - **All Govt. Medical College & Hospitals:** Superintendent & HoDs of concerned Department of the Medical College & Hospitals

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
1. There is no direct enrolment process under Biju Swasthya Kalyan Yojana.
1. All families covered under NFSA / SFSS within Odisha are eligible beneficiaries under this scheme.

> **For BSKY Smart Health Cards**

1. BSKY Smart Health Cards are distributed by the Government to each family.
1. The beneficiary can also collect her/his BSKY Smart Health Card at designated Mo Seva Kendra by producing her/his valid NFSA/ SFSS Card.

Note: For latest development about this scheme, beneficiary may visit the official website at: [**https://bsky.odisha.gov.in/bsky/home**](https://bsky.odisha.gov.in/bsky/home)

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
For Cashless IPD and Day Care Treatment- BSKY Smart Health Card / National Food Security Act (NFSA) Card / State Food Security Scheme (SFSS) Card
For getting ₹5,00,000 health coverage per annum per family and ₹10,00,000 health coverage per annum for women members of the family- Biju Krushak Kalyan Yojana (BKKY) Card, or Below Poverty Line (BPL) Card, or Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY) Card
NOTE: For low-income families who do not have these cards, a valid income certificate (annual income below ₹ 50,000/- in rural areas and below ₹ 60,000/- in urban areas) will be required to be produced at the Empaneled Hospital.

content.section_title: FAQ: What Is Biju Swasthya Kalyan Yojana?
content.text:
"Every life is precious..." has always been the guiding principle of the Hon'ble Chief Minister Naveen Patnaik. The vision of the Hon'ble Chief Minister has been to provide assurance of quality health care to all the citizens of the State, especially the vulnerable sections. With this objective, Biju Swasthya Kalyan Yojana has been launched as a pathbreaking program to provide universal health coverage, with special emphasis on the health protection of economically vulnerable families. To achieve its objectives, the BSKY has two components:
A. State Government will bear the full cost of all health services delivered to all patients
(irrespective of income, status, or residence) in all State Government health care facilities starting from Sub center level to District Head Quarter and Government Medical College Hospital and Blood Bank level.
B. State Government will bear the cost of healthcare provided in impaneled private hospitals for over 9,65,00,000 economically vulnerable families in the State, amounting to Annual Health coverage of [?] 5,00,000 per family and an additional [?] 5,00,000 for the women members of the family after exhaustion of the initial limit.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who Is Eligible?
content.text:
A. All families are eligible for healthcare services at Government Healthcare Institutions to District Head Quarter Hospitals and Government Medical College Hospitals and the Blood Bank level, for which the state Govt will bear the total cost.
B. For additional healthcare facilities beyond the District Headquarters hospital level, through annual health coverage of [?] 5,00,000 per annum per family and an additional Rs. 5lakh for the women members of the family after exhausting of initial limit, all BSKY Smart Card holders and NFSA/ SFSA card holders are eligible to avail of the special health coverage, for which State Government will bear the cost.

content.section_title: FAQ: What Are The Benefits Of The Biju Swasthya Kalyan Yojana Scheme?
content.text:
A. All health services charges are borne by the State Government, including those for drugs, diagnostics, dialysis, Cancer chemotherapy, OT, I.C.U, in-patient admission blood bank services, etc., in all government health institutions up to Government Medical College Hospital level, for all persons.
B. Families having BSKY Smart Health Card can avail of cashless treatment at any impaneled private hospital under BSKY within or outside the State. Cardholder families can help with facilities such as registration, consultation, medical tests, pathologies, treatment, IPD, and follow-up consultation under BSKY at any impaneled private hospital. State Government will bear the cost up to the annual coverage amount.

content.section_title: FAQ: In Which Hospital Can The Beneficiaries Get Free Treatment?
content.text:
A. Under universal health coverage cost of health care in all Public Health Care Institutions from Sub-centre to District Head Quarters Hospitals and Government Medical Colleges hospitals and Blood Bank are entirely borne by State Government.
B. BSKY Smart Card holders can avail treatment at any impaneled private hospitals within or outside of the State for which the State Government will bear the cost up to the annual coverage amount.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is There Any Enrolment Process?
content.text: No, there is no direct enrolment under BSKY. However, families covered underNFSA / SFSA within Odisha is auto-enrolled under this scheme. Any family who did not collect her/his BSKY Smart Health Card during mass distribution can collect a new/duplicate card from Mo Seva Kendra by producing her/his valid NFSA/ SFSA Card.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who Is There To Facilitate At The Point Of Service?
content.text: Swasthya Mitras are deployed at each help desk of impaneled private hospitals to facilitate the beneficiaries.

content.section_title: FAQ: During Emergency Can I Avail Cashless Treatment At Any Impaneled Hospitals Without Carrying A Card?
content.text: Yes, all BSKY Smart Card holders are eligible to avail of cashless treatment at impaneled private hospitals even during emergencies. BSKY beneficiaries are allowed to submit the BSKY Smart Card as eligibility at the BSKY helpdesk counter within 72 hours of hospitalization.

content.section_title: FAQ: How Many Members In My Family Are Eligible To Avail Of Cashless Treatment Under BSKY At Impaneled Private Hospitals?
content.text: All the registered family members under NFSA/ SFSA who are issued with BSKY Smart Cards are eligible to avail [?] 5,00,000 for family and an additional [?] 5,00,000 for the female members after exhaustion of an initial [?] 5,00,000 at any impaneled private hospitals under BSKY.

content.section_title: FAQ: How To Get Card Duplicate BSKY Card If Original Card Lost/ Damaged?
content.text: If a beneficiary either loses/ damages his / her card then the same can be obtained from the nearest Mo Seva Kendra by producing his/her NFSA/ SFSA card.

content.section_title: FAQ: How To Get Card Related Information And Also Rectify Errors In The Card Such As Adding Or Deleting A Member From Card?
content.text: Card-related information can be accessed from the web-portal "bsky.odisha.gov.in" or from Mo Seva Kendra's service point. Any error relating to the card can be rectified at Mo Seva Kendra after producing NFSA/ SFSA card. Before requesting any change relating to information available in the BSKY Card, a beneficiary has to first get his/her details modified in the NFSA/ SFSA card.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where To Get The List Of Empanelled Hospitals For Availing Health-Related Services Using BSKY Smart Health Card?
content.text: The list of empanelled hospitals is available in the web portal "bsky.odisha.gov.in" in the link at empanelled hospitals list. A user can get the details of empanelled hospitals both within or outside the state from the said web portal.

content.section_title: FAQ: How Do We Calculate The Annual Coverage Amount For Male And Female Members Of The Family ?
content.text:
All BSKY smart card holder's health-related ailments expenses are borne by The government of Odisha limited to [?] 5,00,000 per family (an additional [?] 5,00,000 for female members of the family after exhaustion of the initial limit) per annum starting from 1st September 2021 at an impaneled private hospital within or outside of the State. For example: If in a family there are two members one male member and another women member then,
A. If a male member falls sick and exhausts [?] 3,00,000 from the BSKY Smart Health card and then the women member falls ill, she can avail of up to [?] 7,00,000 under the card.
B. If a male member doesn't fall sick then women member falls sick she can avail of up to [?] 10,00,000 under the card.
C. If a women member falls sick and exhausts [?] 3,00,000 from BSKY smart health card, then if male members fall sick, they can avail of [?] 2,00,000 from the card.

content.section_title: FAQ: What Is The Helpline Number For BSKY?
content.text: Any person can raise any query or complaint regarding Biju Swasthya Kalyan Yojana with toll-free number 104 which is available 24X7. Further, scheme details and a list of impaneled hospitals can also be seen on the websites: health.odisha.gov.in, bsky.odisha.gov.in, and www.nhmorissa.gov.in.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is There A Feedback Mechanism In The BSKY?
content.text:
Hon'ble Chief Minister always directs the administration to ensure the dignity of the citizens while availing government program benefits. With this vision, the Mo Sarkar feedback system has been launched to directly get feedback from the beneficiaries and the stakeholders.
In BSKY also, the State Government will take direct feedback from the beneficiaries and the Hospitals regarding the implementation of the program. The citizens can also provide feedback on the Mo Sarkar Call center no 14545.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The Government of Kerala launched the “Cancer Suraksha Scheme” on 1st November 2008 for children below the age of 18 who suffer from cancer and belong to such families which are too poor to afford treatment. This scheme was launched under Kerala Social Security Mission.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
The Government of Kerala launched the “Cancer Suraksha Scheme” on 1st November 2008 for children below the age of 18 who suffer from cancer and belong to such families which are too poor to afford treatment. This scheme was launched under Kerala Social Security Mission. Those patients found eligible would be registered under the scheme and a Patient Card would be issued. Registered patients would be provided treatment free of cost at the designated hospitals. On further verification, if it is found that any registered patient is ineligible for assistance, the assistance would be stopped forthwith.
Cancer, the killer disease, was once considered incurable. However, advances in medical science have helped conquer the disease to a very large extent. It is scientifically proven that pediatric cases of cancer are largely curable if treated early. Recognizing the urgent need to extend financial support to cancer victims from families, who are too poor to afford treatment, the Government of Kerala launched this scheme.
To provide cashless transactions funds have already been deposited with the superintendents of the hospitals. The patients who are eligible for assistance would receive the benefit from these hospitals directly without approaching the Mission.
**Hospitals Covered under the Scheme:**

1. Regional Cancer Centre, Thiruvananthapuram
1. Government Medical College Thiruvananthapuram
1. Sree AvitamThirunal (SAT) Hospital, Thiruvananthapuram
1. Government Medical College Alapuzha
1. Government Medical College Kottayam
1. Government Medical College Thrissur
1. Government Medical College Kozhikode
1. Co-operative Medical College Pariyaram, Kannur
1. Malabar Cancer Centre, Thalassery, Kannur
1. Government General Hospital, Ernakulam
1. Institute of Maternal and Child Health, Kozhikode
1. Govt. Medical College Hospital, Ernakulam

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
1. Under this scheme, children under 18 years would be given free treatment for cancer through designated Government hospitals in the state.
1. The entire cost of treatment, including the cost of investigations, would be borne by the hospitals through the funds provided by the Mission.
1. The ceiling of expenditure per child would be initially ₹50,000/-.

**Note:** The hospital can incur additional expenditure, based on the report of the committee comprising the Oncologist/treating doctor, the Head of the Radiology Department, and the Superintendent, subject to ratification by the Mission.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Kerala State and scheme is applicable only to patients from Kerala.
1. Cancer patients up to the age of 18 years in the Below Poverty Line (BPL) groups are eligible to be included in this scheme.
1. Cancer patients should belong to families which are too poor to afford treatment.
1. To be included in this scheme a confirmed diagnosis of cancer is a must.
1. Those patients who are yet to complete 18 years of age and need treatment are eligible for the benefit for one more year.

content.section_title: Exclusions
content.text:
1. Patients over 18 years old are not eligible to apply under the scheme.
1. Patients already receiving free treatment in government hospitals are not eligible.
1. Patients who are not domiciled in Kerala state.
1. Those patients who avail of the pay ward facility and are eligible for medical reimbursement from other sources or have medical insurance are not eligible for this scheme.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
To avail of the benefits of the scheme, the applicant may contact the following authorities:
1.	Scheme counselor who is appointed at selected hospitals/covered hospitals under this scheme.
2.	Concerned District Social Justice Officer.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
1.	Copy of Aadhaar/ Proof of Identity of patient
2.	Proof of age of the patient
3.	Copy of BPL card
4.	Copy of Ration card
5.	Residence certificate
6.	Passport-size photograph
7.	Proof/report confirming the diagnosis of cancer
8.	Registered Patient Card under the scheme

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the “Cancer Suraksha Scheme”?
content.text: The Government of Kerala launched the "Cancer Suraksha Scheme" on 1st November 2008 for children below the age of 18 who suffer from cancer and belong to such families which are too poor to afford treatment

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the objective of the scheme?
content.text: Those patients found eligible would be registered under the scheme and a Patient Card would be issued. Registered patients would be provided treatment free of cost at the designated hospitals. On further verification, if it is found that any registered patient is ineligible for assistance, the assistance would be stopped forthwith.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible to get the benefits under the scheme?
content.text: Cancer patients up to the age of 18 years in the Below Poverty Line (BPL) groups are eligible to be included in this scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is this scheme only for Cancer patients?
content.text: Yes, this scheme is only for Cancer patients.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is this scheme only for the applicant belonging to Below Poverty Line (BPL) category?
content.text: Yes, the applicants should belong to Below Poverty Line (BPL) category.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is this scheme only for a resident of Kerala?
content.text: Yes, the applicant should be a resident of Kerala State and the scheme is applicable only to patients from Kerala.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the age limit to apply under the scheme?
content.text: The age limit of the applicant has to be 18 years or below.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is a patient receiving free treatment in a government hospital eligible to apply under the scheme?
content.text: No, the patient already receiving free treatment in government hospitals is not eligible.

content.section_title: FAQ: Does the patient who has medical insurance eligible to apply under the scheme?
content.text: No, those patients who avail of the pay ward facility and are eligible for medical reimbursement from other sources or have medical insurance are not eligible for this scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the benefit of the scheme?
content.text: Under this scheme, children under 18 years would be given free treatment for cancer through designated Government hospitals in the state.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can the hospital incur additional expenditure?
content.text: Yes, the hospital can incur additional expenditure, based on the report of the committee comprising the Oncologist/treating doctor, the Head of the Radiology Department, and the Superintendent, subject to ratification by the Mission.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can an applicant avail of the benefits of the scheme?
content.text: To avail of the benefits of the scheme, the applicant may contact the Scheme counselor who is appointed at selected hospitals/covered hospitals under this scheme or the concerned District Social Justice Officer.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to ensure early detection & timely treatment of cancer by providing accessible & affordable healthcare services within the state. Under this initiative, free screening for common cancers such as oral, breast, & cervical cancer is being conducted across all districts of Uttarakhand.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The “Cancer Day Care Centre” is implemented by the Department of Medical Health and Family Welfare, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to ensure early detection and timely treatment of cancer by providing accessible and affordable healthcare services within the state.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: Free screening for common cancers (oral, breast, and CA cervix) is being conducted across all districts of Uttarakhand to detect cancer at an early stage and start treatment.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The beneficiary must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
1. The beneficiary must be a cancer patient.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text: Cancer Day Care Centres are being established in all 13 districts of Uttarakhand to provide chemotherapy, pain management, and other daycare services to cancer patients. Currently, Cancer Day Care Units have been set up in 10 hill districts of the state. Patients can avail of these facilities by visiting their respective district hospitals.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
1. Identity proof
2. Medical reports
3. Any other documents as required

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can benefit from this scheme?
content.text: All cancer patients residing in Uttarakhand are eligible to benefit from the services provided under this scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What services are provided under theses Centres?
content.text: The centers provides chemotherapy, pain management, follow-up care, and other daycare services for cancer patients.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are cancer screenings available in these centers?
content.text: Yes, free screening for common cancers such as oral, breast, and cervical (CA cervix) cancers is being conducted in all districts of Uttarakhand.

content.section_title: FAQ: How many Cancer Day Care Centres are planned in Uttarakhand?
content.text: Cancer Day Care Centres are being established in all 13 districts of the state.

content.section_title: FAQ: How many Cancer Day Care Units have been established so far?
content.text: Currently, Cancer Day Care Units have been established in 10 hill districts of Uttarakhand.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where can patients access these facilities?
content.text: Patients can visit their respective district hospitals to avail of the services provided under the Cancer Day Care Centre.

title: Andaman & Nicobar Islands Scheme for Health Insurance
topic: Andaman & Nicobar Islands Scheme for Health Insurance

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The Health Insurance (ANISHI) shall provide cashless treatment upto Rupees 5 Lakhs per patients per illness which include indoor and outdoor treatments.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The scheme “Andaman & Nicobar Islands Scheme for Health Insurance (ANISHI)” was launched by the Health Department, Union Territory of Andaman and Nicobar Islands on 26.01.2015. The scheme provides cashless medical treatment benefit upto ₹5.00 Lakhs per patient per illness to the people belonging to Below Poverty Level/Priority House Hold, Pensioners of A&N Administration who have settled in these Islands after retirement with their dependent family member and Permanent Resident whose family annual income is below ₹3.00 Lakhs. Many of these patients come from economically disadvantage backgrounds and are unable to bear the cost of hospital expense in mainland hospital. In order to provide assistance to such patients the Administration decided to lodge its own health Insurance Scheme which shall provide better health care to the persons.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
1. The Health Insurance (ANISHI) shall provide cashless treatment upto ₹5 Lakhs per patients per illness which include indoor and outdoor treatments.
1. Free travel expenses for patients with one attendant shall be reimbursed (Bunk class fare).
1. Critically ill patients, if referred under the scheme to travel by flight, will be entitled to reimbursement of the air ticket for the patient and one attendant.
1. In the very rare case of a beneficiary falling sick during a personal visit to the mainland and who requires emergency treatment, benefits under the scheme can be availed of but the travel expenses will not be allowed.

**Note:** In case the beneficiary undergoes treatment or on his own gets admitted in empanelled/non-empanelled hospital without completing codal formalities at State Nodal Cell (ANISHI), the reimbursement of hospital expense for such cases ‘will not be allowed’ under the scheme.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
The persons/patients of the following categories are eligible to avail the benefits under the scheme:

1. The person who belongs to the BPL category settled in the A&N Islands can avail of the benefits under the scheme.
1. The Government pensioners of the A&N Administration who have settled in these Islands after retirement with their dependent family members can avail of the benefits under the scheme.
1. The permanent resident of the Andaman & Nicobar Islands whose family annual income is below ₹3.00 Lakhs can avail of the benefits under the scheme.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Step 01:** The eligible person(s)/patient can avail of the cashless benefit in any empanelled hospital notified by the Administration from time to time ‘by completing the codal formalities at Nodal Cell (ANISHI)’.
**Step 02:** The patient is to be referred by the concerned specialist of GB Pant Hospital, Port Blair thereafter, he/she has to approach the Nodal Cell (ANISHI) located at the DHS Office for the cashless benefit of the scheme.
**Step 03:** The state Nodal Cell processes the case within 02 days and after obtaining approval of the Chairman ANISHI, orders are issued to the empanelled hospital to provide cashless benefit to treat the referred patient under the ANISHI Scheme.
**Note 01:** In case the beneficiary is on his personal visit to the mainland and had fallen sick requiring emergency treatment and gets admitted to one of the empanelled hospitals under the scheme, the Medical Superintendent /Director of the concerned empanelled hospital in the mainland will have to first inform the Nodal Officer (ANISHI) about the particulars of the patient and his/her clinical condition.
**Note 02:** The State Nodal Cell after verification of records convey the approval to treat the patient as per the guidelines of the scheme, however, the patient/relatives have to submit the documents as the case may be.
**Note 03: I**n such cases, the travel expense to the patient /attendant will not be reimbursed. If the patient gets admitted in a ‘hospital which is not empanelled’ under the scheme, then the benefit will be extended only if the patient is shifted to the nearest empanelled hospital under the scheme.
([Application Form](https://dhs.andaman.gov.in/DHSForms/Health%20Insurance.pdf))

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
BPL Patient:- Islander Card/Voter ID Card
- BPL Ration Card
Pensioners:- Pension Payment Order
- Ration Card
- Islander Card/Voter ID Card
Permanent Resident Category Patients:- Islander Card/Voter ID Card
- Ration Card

content.section_title: FAQ: What is ANISHI?
content.text: ANISHI stands for Andaman & Nicobar Islands Scheme for Health Insurance. It was launched by the Health Department, Union Territory of Andaman and Nicobar Islands on January 26, 2015.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can avail benefits under the scheme?
content.text: Individuals belonging to Below Poverty Level (BPL), Government pensioners of A&N Administration who settled in the islands after retirement with their dependent family members, and permanent residents with a family annual income below [?]3.00 Lakhs are eligible.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the benefits offered by the scheme?
content.text: The scheme provides cashless medical treatment up to [?]5 Lakhs per patient per illness, including both indoor and outdoor treatments. It also covers travel expenses for patients and one attendant, with provisions for flight reimbursement in critical cases.

content.section_title: FAQ: How does the reimbursement process work for travel expenses?
content.text: Patients and one attendant are entitled to reimbursement of travel expenses (bunk class fare) if they need to travel for medical treatment. In critical cases requiring air travel, the air ticket expenses for the patient and one attendant are reimbursed.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can patients avail treatment outside of the Andaman & Nicobar Islands under the scheme?
content.text: Yes.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the steps involved in the application process for ANISHI?
content.text:
Step 1: Eligible patients complete codal formalities at the Nodal Cell (ANISHI).
Step 2: Patients are referred by specialists at GB Pant Hospital, Port Blair.
Step 3: The State Nodal Cell processes the case and issues orders to empanelled hospitals for cashless treatment.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are there any exceptions to the reimbursement policy for hospital expenses?
content.text: Yes, reimbursement of hospital expenses will not be allowed if the patient undergoes treatment without completing codal formalities at the State Nodal Cell (ANISHI).

content.section_title: FAQ: Can individuals who are not permanent residents of Andaman & Nicobar Islands avail the scheme benefits?
content.text: No.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can beneficiaries choose any hospital for treatment under the scheme?
content.text: Beneficiaries can avail treatment only at empanelled hospitals notified by the Administration. Treatment at non-empanelled hospitals may not be covered under the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: How long does it take for the application to be processed under the scheme?
content.text: The State Nodal Cell processes cases within two days, subject to verification and approval from the Chairman ANISHI.

content.section_title: FAQ: What if a beneficiary falls sick during a personal visit to the mainland?
content.text: In such cases, the beneficiary can avail benefits under ANISHI for emergency treatment, but travel expenses will not be reimbursed.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The "Annual Medical Assistance Scheme" by the Bihar Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Labour Resources Department, Government of Bihar, provides a yearly financial assistance of ₹3,000/- to the construction workers who are registered with the Board.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The "Annual Medical Assistance Scheme" by the Bihar Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Labour Resources Department, Government of Bihar, provides a yearly financial assistance of ₹3,000/- to the construction workers who are registered with the Board.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: Financial Assistance of ₹3,000/- per annum.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
- The applicant should be a worker.
- The worker should be engaged in building or construction work.
- The worker should be registered with the "Bihar Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board".
- The worker's membership with the Welfare Board must be active at the time of application.
- The worker should have at least one year of membership with the Board.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
> **Registration**

**Step 1:** Visit the [Official Website](https://bocw.bihar.gov.in/Website/LabourReg.aspx) of the Bihar Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, and click "[Apply for New Registration](https://bocw.bihar.gov.in/Registration/AddLabourRegistrationWeb.aspx)".
**Step 4:** On the next page, provide your details including your Aadhaar Number. Then click "Authenticate".
**Step 5:** An online form will appear. In the form, fill in all the mandatory fields. The fields marked with an asterisk (*) are mandatory.
**Step 6:** Click "Submit". Proceed to pay the application fee online.
**Step 7:** **** Upon successful payment, you will receive your construction worker registration number.
> **Application**

**Step 1:** **** Visit the [Official Website ](https://bocw.bihar.gov.in/Website/SchemeApply.aspx)of the Bihar Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
**Step 2:** Click on "[Scheme Application](https://bocw.bihar.gov.in/Website/SchemeApply.aspx)" and select "[Apply for Scheme](https://bocw.bihar.gov.in/Registration/SchemeTaggingWeb.aspx)".
**Step 3:** **** Enter your registration number and click "Show" to view your details.
**Step 4:** **** Select the scheme you intend to apply for, enter all mandatory information and upload the necessary documents in the specified format and size.
**Step 6:** **** Click on "Submit Application" to complete the process.
> **Application Tracking**

Applicants can track their application status online on the [official website ](https://bocw.bihar.gov.in/Registration/ApplicationStatus.aspx)of the Board by providing their registration number.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Identity Proof i.e. Aadhaar Card, Voter Card
- Bank Account Details
- Proof of Age
- Identity Card of the Registered Worker
- Proof of Membership
- Any Other Documents as Required

content.section_title: FAQ: What type of work should the applicant be engaged in to qualify for the financial assistance scheme?
content.text: The applicant should be engaged in building or construction work to be eligible for the financial assistance.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is it mandatory for the applicant to be registered with the Bihar Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board?
content.text: Yes, the applicant must be registered with the Bihar Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board to qualify for the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: How long should the applicant’s membership with the Welfare Board be active to apply for the scheme?
content.text: The applicant's membership with the Welfare Board must be active for at least one year at the time of application.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the annual financial assistance provided under this scheme?
content.text: The scheme provides an annual financial assistance of [?]3,000/- to eligible construction workers.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can applicants track the status of their application online?
content.text: Yes, applicants can track their application status online by providing their registration number on the official website.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are bank account details required for the application process?
content.text: Yes, the applicant must provide their bank account details as part of the application process.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can the applicant apply for the scheme without an Aadhaar card?
content.text: No, the applicant must provide their Aadhaar number during the registration process to authenticate their identity.

content.section_title: FAQ: How do I know which fields are mandatory when filling out the online registration form for construction workers?
content.text: In the online registration form, all fields marked with an asterisk (*) symbol are mandatory and must be filled out. Make sure to complete all these required fields before proceeding.

content.section_title: FAQ: What essential documents should I keep ready before starting my application process?
content.text: You should have your Aadhaar Card or Voter Card for identity proof, bank account details, proof of age, registered worker identity card, proof of membership, and any other specifically requested documents.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can I monitor the progress of my medical assistance application after submission?
content.text: You can track your application status at any time by visiting the official website of the Board and entering your registration number in the application tracking system.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme “Aravanaippu Illam" was launched by the Department of Social Welfare, Government of Puducherry. The objective of this scheme is to take care of persons who are lacking basic needs & proper care in society, and to maintain them in a safe living condition.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The scheme “Aravanaippu Illam" was launched by the Department of Social Welfare, Government of Puducherry. The objective of this scheme is to take care of persons who are lacking basic needs & proper care in society, and to maintain them in a safe living condition.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
- Free Shelter and Food.
- Free Supply of 4 Sets of Dresses and Other Daily Living Needs.
- Free Medical Treatment (Referred to Nearby PHC).
- Training in Korai & Coir Mat Weaving.
- Labour Charges of ₹ 20/- Per Mat for the Work of Mat Weaving as and When Carried Out.
- ₹ 5/- Per Day as Daily Pocket Money.
- Recreation Like Radio, TV, Library, Besides Local Trips to Places of Interest and Temples.
- Annual Tour to Places of Interest.
- Annual Day, Conducted Every Year in Order to Exhibit Their Talents and for Prize Distribution.

**NOTE:** Contact the authorities below in case of delay in disbursal of benefits:
Puducherry: The Director, Directorate of Social Welfare, Saradhambal Nagar, Ellaipillaichavadhy, Puducherry.
Karaikal: The Collector, Karaikal.
Mahe/Yanam: The Regional Administrator, Mahe/Yanam.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant should be lacking basic needs and proper care in society.
1. The applicant should be 20 years or above in age.
1. The applicant should not be suffering from communicable disease.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Step 1:** The interested applicant should visit (during office hours) the [Office/ Sub-Office](https://socwelfare.py.gov.in/sub--office-social-welfare-department) of the Department of Social Welfare, and request the hard copy of the prescribed format of the application form from the staff exclusively entrusted to issue and collect filled-in applications.
**Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste the passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
**Step 3:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the authority given below -

- **Puducherry:** The Deputy Director, Differently Abled Section, Directorate of Social Welfare. - **Karaikal:** The Assistant Director, Social Welfare Department (Sub Office).- **Mahe / Yanam:** The Welfare Officer (i\c), Social Welfare Department (Sub Office).
**Step 4:** Request a receipt or acknowledgement from the concerned authority to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Aadhaar Card.
- Voter ID.
- Address Proof.
- Ration Card.
- Age Proof.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the primary objective of the "Aravanaippu Illam" scheme?
content.text: The scheme aims to provide care for individuals lacking basic needs, ensuring a safe living condition in society.

content.section_title: FAQ: What benefits are offered under the scheme?
content.text: Benefits include free shelter, food, medical treatment, dresses, daily living needs, and training in mat weaving.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the labor charge for mat weaving under the scheme?
content.text: Labour charges for mat weaving amount to [?]20/- per mat, providing beneficiaries with an opportunity for skill-based income.

content.section_title: FAQ: What recreational activities are provided to the beneficiaries?
content.text: Recreational activities include access to radio, TV, library, local trips, an annual tour, and an annual day for talent exhibition.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can one address delays in benefit disbursal?
content.text: In case of delays, contact the respective authorities in Puducherry, Karaikal, Mahe, or Yanam as specified in the guidelines.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the eligibility criterion for applicants?
content.text: Applicants must lack basic needs, be 20 years or above, and not suffer from any communicable disease.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where should the application be submitted, and who are the designated authorities?
content.text: Submit the application to the Field Officer in Puducherry, Assistant Director in Karaikal, or Welfare Officer in Mahe/Yanam.

content.section_title: FAQ: What documents are required for the application process?
content.text: Necessary documents include Aadhaar card, voter ID, address proof, ration card, and age proof.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the application process for medical treatment under the scheme?
content.text: Medical treatment is accessible through nearby Primary Health Centres (PHC), as referred by the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any provision for skill development in the scheme?
content.text: Yes, beneficiaries receive training in Korai & Coir Mat Weaving, promoting skill enhancement.

content.section_title: FAQ: How is the annual day significant for beneficiaries?
content.text: The annual day serves as a platform for beneficiaries to exhibit their talents and receive prize distributions.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the sources or references available for detailed information on the scheme?
content.text: Detailed guidelines can be found at https://socwelfare.py.gov.in/aravanaippu-illam-puducherry.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can grievances be addressed related to the "Aravanaippu Illam" scheme?
content.text: Grievances can be redressed through the portal https://pgportal.gov.in/ as specified in the sources.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where can citizens find the contact information for the Social Welfare Department?
content.text: Contact details for the department are available at https://socwelfare.py.gov.in/contact-us.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are there any age restrictions for applicants under the scheme?
content.text: Yes, the applicants should be 20 years or above to be eligible for benefits under the "Aravanaippu Illam" scheme.

title: Asangathit Karmakaar Mrtyu Evan Divyaang Sahaayata Yojana
topic: Asangathit Karmakaar Mrtyu Evan Divyaang Sahaayata Yojana

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: Chhattisgarh State Government started a scheme "Asangathit Karmakaar Mrtyu Evan Divyaang Sahaayata Yojana " for unorganized worker on 09 December 2019.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: In 2019, Chhattisgarh State Government started a scheme "Asangathit Karmakaar Mrtyu Evan Divyaang Sahaayata Yojana" for unorganized worker. With this scheme, family will able to get the financials benefits after the death or disability of the unorganized worker.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: **Situation** **Financial Help**On Death₹ 1,00,000/-Permanent Disability₹ 50,000/-

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. Unorganized workers must be between the ages of 18 and 60 are eligible.
1. Unorganized workers must be registered as beneficiaries under Section 10 (3) of the Act.
1. Assistance will not be provided in the case of death due to suicide, drug or substance abuse, or death due to fighting with each other with the intent to commit a crime.
1. Benefits will be given only on the death of the registered worker and not on the death or accident of a family member.
1. Beneficiaries who have benefited from the Financial Assistance and Rehabilitation Assistance Scheme for unorganized workers suffering from silicosis run by the Board will not be eligible.

content.text: Labor Department of Chhattisgarh State started this scheme "Asangathit Karmakar Gambhir Bimari Chikitsa Sahayata Yojana" for unorganized worker on 28 April 2012.
