- Applications from litterateurs, poets, and authors must be recommended by the relevant literary academy.
- All applications will be reviewed by the Rajasthan Journalist Welfare Fund Committee. Upon verifying eligibility and other necessary details, the Committee will issue appropriate orders, which will be implemented by the Member Secretary.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Identity Proof
- Medical Reports and Prescription (If applicable)
- Proof of age
- Income Certificate
- Bank Passbook Copy
- Any Other Document if Required

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the Journalist Financial Assistance Facility?
content.text: A financial aid scheme for journalists/litterateurs in Rajasthan.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who manages the scheme?
content.text: Managed by DIPR under a committee formed by the state government.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible?
content.text: Accredited/non-accredited journalists, litterateurs.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is financial help available for health treatment?
content.text: Yes, for serious diseases like cancer, stroke, kidney failure, etc.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the maximum one-time financial aid?
content.text: ₹1,00,000; ₹2,00,000 in special cases.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can the committee deny aid?
content.text: Yes, if the applicant eligibility is not met.

content.section_title: FAQ: How do I apply?
content.text: Submit an offline application to DIPR, Jaipur.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can the amount be increased?
content.text: Yes, in special cases up to ₹2,00,000.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the contact office for queries?
content.text: Directorate, Department of Information & Public Relations, Rajasthan, Jaipur.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who reviews the application?
content.text: All applications will be reviewed by the Rajasthan Journalist Welfare Fund Committee. Upon verifying eligibility and other necessary details, the Committee will issue appropriate orders, which will be implemented by the Member Secretary.

title: Financial Assistance Scheme for Dependents of Deceased Journalists and Litterateurs - Rajasthan
topic: Financial Assistance Scheme for Dependents of Deceased Journalists and Litterateurs - Rajasthan

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to provide financial support to the dependents of deceased journalists and litterateurs in situations such as medical emergencies, unemployment, or when there is no other source of income.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
The "Financial Assistance Scheme for Dependents of Deceased Journalists and Litterateurs" scheme was launched by the Department of Information & Public Relations (DIPR), Government of Rajasthan. The scheme aims to provide financial support to the dependents of deceased journalists and litterateurs in situations such as medical emergencies, unemployment, or when there is no other source of income. Eligible dependents under this scheme include an unemployed son below the age of 28 years, an unmarried daughter, the wife, and the parents of the deceased, provided they do not have any other means of financial support.
> **Scheme Implementation**

The scheme is implemented by a committee constituted by the state government and managed under the Rajasthan Journalist and Litterateur Welfare Fund. The objective is to promote social security and ensure the well-being of media personnel and literary contributors in Rajasthan.
> **Under the scheme, illness covers:**

Cancer, leprosy, valve replacement, angioplasty, bypass surgery, kidney transplant, brain stroke (brain and neurological diseases), epileptic seizures, severe injury in an accident, severe infectious diseases - brain infection, lung infection/pneumonia, severe infection in other organs of the body, general surgery - (hernia, appendix, stones in kidney and gall bladder), pregnancy (cesarean) and serious gynecological diseases, diseases related to excessive bleeding, paralysis/palsy, asthma, Alzheimer's, COVID-19, thyroid, etc.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
> **Monetary Assistance for Medical Treatment:**

- Up to ₹1,00,000/- for treatment of critical diseases (e.g., cancer, bypass surgery, kidney transplant, brain stroke, etc.).
- In special cases, the committee may approve up to ₹2,00,000/-.

> **Monthly Financial Assistance:**

- ₹1,000/- to ₹2,500/- per month for a maximum of one year.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant should be a dependent of a deceased journalist or litterateur.
1. The deceased journalist or litterateur should have been a resident of Rajasthan.
1. The applicant should fall under one of the following categories:

- Widow of the deceased journalist or litterateur with no other source of income.
- Unemployed son below 28 years of age.
- Unmarried daughter.
- Father or mother of the deceased, provided they have no other source of income.

**Note:** Financial assistance will be given to only one person at a time.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
> **Application Process**

**Step 1:** The interested applicant should visit (during office hours) the Director, Directorate of Information & Public Relations, Rajasthan Government, Jaipur and request a hard copy of the prescribed format of the application form from the staff exclusively entrusted to issue and collect filled-in applications.
**Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attested, if required).
**Step 3:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the Director, Directorate of Information & Public Relations, Rajasthan Government, Jaipur.
> **Post Application Process:**

- All applications will be reviewed by the Rajasthan Journalist Welfare Fund Committee.
- Upon verifying eligibility and other necessary details, the Committee will issue appropriate orders, which will be implemented by the Member Secretary.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Identity Proof
- Medical Reports and Prescription (If applicable)
- Proof of income
- Bank Passbook Copy
- Any Other Document if Required

content.section_title: FAQ: Who manages the scheme?
content.text: Managed by DIPR under a committee formed by the state government.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible?
content.text: Spouse, unmarried daughters, unemployed sons, and aged parents of the journalists and litterateurs.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is financial help available for health treatment?
content.text: Yes, for serious diseases like cancer, stroke, kidney failure, etc.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the maximum one-time financial aid?
content.text: ₹1,00,000; ₹2,00,000 in special cases.

content.section_title: FAQ: What diseases are covered?
content.text: Cancer, bypass surgery, COVID-19, paralysis, asthma, and many more.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can I get monthly aid?
content.text: Yes, ₹1,000 to ₹2,500 per month for a year.

content.section_title: FAQ: How do I apply?
content.text: Submit an offline application to DIPR, Jaipur.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is income proof required?
content.text: Yes, especially for dependents.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the contact office for queries?
content.text: Directorate, Department of Information & Public Relations, Rajasthan, Jaipur.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to provide assistance to the youths of the Tea Tribes and Adivasi Community for training in job-assured technical and professional Diploma and Degree courses. It supports training for courses like ANM, GNM, and Para-Medical, with a minimum duration of one year.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The scheme "Financial Assistance for ANM/GNM/Technical Courses" was launched by the Tea Tribes Welfare Department, Government of Assam. The scheme aims to provide assistance to the youths of the Tea Tribes and Adivasi Community for training purposes. The scheme provides financial assistance for Auxiliary Nurse Midwife (ANM), General Nursing and Midwifery (GNM), Para-Medical, and other job-assured technical and professional Diploma and Degree courses. The courses must have a minimum duration of one year and be conducted in either Government or Non-Government Institutes. The applications for this scheme are accepted online.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
- Financial Assistance to the youths of the Tea Tribes and Adivasi Community for training of Auxiliary Nurse Midwife (ANM)/General Nursing and Midwifery (GNM)/Para-Medical and other job assured technical and professional Diploma and Degree courses.
- The training must have a minimum duration of one year and can be conducted in both Government and Non-Government Institutes.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
- The applicant must be a permanent resident of Assam.
- The applicant must belong to the Tea Tribes and Adivasi community.
- The applicant's family income must not exceed ₹5,00,000/- per annum.

- The applicant must currently be undergoing the course in a Government or Government Recognized Institute.
- The applicant must be undergoing professional, job-oriented technical Diploma/Degree courses.
- The applicant must be enrolled in a course with a minimum duration of one year.

content.section_title: Exclusions
content.text:
- Students who have passed out shall not be eligible for financial assistance.
- Students who have abandoned their courses shall not be eligible for financial assistance.
- The applicant eligible under this scheme will not be eligible for other schemes in the same year, except for the "Simon Sing Horo (Special Post-Matric) Scholarship".

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
##### ***Step 1:*** ***Portal Access and Scheme Selection***
**A. Navigating to the Portal:** The applicant must first open the official Tea Tribes Welfare Department URL, www.ttwd.assam.gov.in, and then click the "Online Application" link to be redirected to the [SIRISH application portal](https://sirishassam.in/).
**B. Initial Login or Registration:** New users must register themselves on the online portal, while existing users will log in using the username and password created during prior registration.
**C. Selecting the Scheme:** Once the applicant is logged into the Dashboard, they must select the appropriate scholarship scheme from the dropdown menu available.
##### ***Step 2:** **** *** **Entering Personal Details**
**A. Inputting Mandatory Data:** Enter "Personal Details" where all required information in the application form must be accurately provided.
**B. Specifying Location Details:** Location data, including District, Assembly Constituency, and Sub-Division, must be selected, along with other essential address details like Village/Town.
**C. Saving Personal Information:** After filling in all necessary fields in this step, click on the "Save Form Details" button to proceed further with the application.
##### ***Step 3:** **** *** **Document Upload**
**A. Uploading Required Files:** Under the "Upload Documents" tab, upload all required supporting documents in the specified image formats (e.g., JPG/JPEG).
**B. Document Formatting Check:** All uploaded documents must adhere to specified maximum file sizes (e.g., photo maximum 50 KB, other documents generally 100 KB) before they can be saved.
##### ***Step 4:** **** *** **Updating Bank Details**
**A. Providing Account Information:** Enter the Bank Account Number and the name of the bank under the "Update Bank Details" tab.
**B. Entering Branch Identification:** Details essential for electronic transfer, specifically the Bank Branch Name and the corresponding IFSC Code, must be accurately provided.
**C. Securing Financial Data:** Once all bank details have been correctly entered, click on the "Save Bank Details" button to confirm the information.
##### ***Step 5:** **** *** **Preview and Final Submission**
**A. Application Review:** "Preview Application" displays all details entered in the form for a thorough and final check.
**B. Final Confirmation:** After cross-checking all information and uploaded documents for accuracy, the applicant must confirm the application by clicking the "Proceed with Submit Application" button.
**C. Tracking Status:** Upon successful submission, an application number will be visible on the screen, allowing the applicant to view the status under the "APPLICATION STATUS" tab.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Passport Size Photo (Maximum 50kb In JPEG/JPG Format).
- Signature (Maximum 20kb In JPEG/JPG Format).
- Date Of Birth Certificate (Maximum 100kb In JPEG/JPG Format).
- Original Copy Of Mark-sheet Of Last Examination Passed (Maximum 100kb In JPEG/JPG Format).
- Caste Certificate (Maximum 100kb In JPEG/JPG Format).
- Undertaking On Annual Income As Per Attached Format (Maximum 100kb In JPEG/JPG Format).
- Admission/Fee Receipt Of The Course Undergoing (Maximum 100kb In JPEG/JPG Format).
- First Page Of Bank Passbook (Maximum 100kb In JPEG/JPG Format).

content.section_title: FAQ: Am I eligible to apply if I am pursuing my Auxiliary Nurse Midwifery, General Nursing and Midwifery, or Technical Course at a private institute that is recognized by the government?
content.text: Yes, the applicant must currently be undergoing the courses in a Government or Government Recognized Institutes to be eligible for the assistance. Furthermore, the assistance is provided for training in both Government and Non-Government Institutes.

content.section_title: FAQ: Do the courses related to Auxiliary Nurse Midwifery, General Nursing and Midwifery, or Para-Medical training specifically qualify for this assistance?
content.text: Yes, the scheme is designed to provide assistance to youths of the Tea Tribes and Adivasi Community for training of Auxiliary Nurse Midwifery (ANM), General Nursing and Midwifery (GNM), Para-Medical, and other job assured technical and professional Diploma and Degree courses.

content.section_title: FAQ: If I cannot provide the admission fee receipt, is there an alternative document I can submit to verify my enrollment status?
content.text: Yes, an applicant must produce either the admission fee receipt of the course undergoing or a recommendation from the Head of the Institution certifying that he or she is undergoing the course.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there a maximum limit placed on the annual income of my family or guardian that determines my financial eligibility?
content.text: Yes, the annual family income must not exceed ₹5,00,000/- (Rupees Five Lakh) per annum. An undertaking on the annual family income must be submitted by the Parent or Guardian.

content.section_title: FAQ: What specific categories of academic programs qualify for this financial support, besides nursing and midwifery courses?
content.text: The scheme covers professional and job-oriented technical Diploma and Degree courses. These courses must also have a minimum duration of 1 (one) year.

content.section_title: FAQ: If I have already completed or quit the ANM, GNM, or Technical course recently, can I still submit an application for financial assistance?
content.text: No, students who have passed out or who have abandoned their courses shall not be eligible for financial assistance under this scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Do I need to demonstrate that I have permanent residency within the State of Assam to fulfill the basic geographical requirement?
content.text: Yes, the applicant must be a permanent resident of Assam to qualify for this scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is it mandatory to be an active, enrolled student in the course during the application period to qualify for this benefit?
content.text: Yes, only students who are currently and regularly enrolled in the courses shall be eligible to apply for the financial assistance.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the required community background needed for an applicant to successfully meet the demographic eligibility criteria?
content.text: The applicant must belong to the Tea Tribes and Adivasi Community.

content.section_title: FAQ: Which specific document is required to certify my community status, and who must issue this official document?
content.text: The applicant must produce a valid Caste certificate. This certificate must be duly certified or issued by the District Commissioner of the concerned district or his or her designated officer.

content.section_title: FAQ: If I receive financial support through this program, am I permitted to simultaneously avail benefits from any other government scheme?
content.text: The applicant eligible under this scheme will not be eligible for other schemes in the same year, except for the "Simon Sing Horo (Special Post-Matric) Scholarship," if eligible.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The Employee Health Scheme (EHS) is a government-run health scheme in Andhra Pradesh, providing cashless treatment at network hospitals for government employees, pensioners, and their families under the Dr. YSR Aarogyasri Healthcare Trust.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
The Dr YSR Aarogyasri Health Care Trust, under the auspices of the Government of Andhra Pradesh, has implemented the Employee Health Scheme (EHS) with the primary objective of providing quality healthcare to all State Government Employees. The scheme covers government employees, pensioners and their dependent family members, who are eligible for cashless treatment at empanelled hospitals or Network Hospitals (NWH).

The EHS has replaced the erstwhile medical reimbursement process and offers new features such as post-hospitalisation medical care and treatment of chronic diseases. This scheme is applicable to both serving and retired employees of the state government of Andhra Pradesh. It is aimed at providing comprehensive healthcare services to the beneficiaries and ensuring their well-being by offering high-quality medical services.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. Serving employees:

- All regular State Government employees. A State Government employee will have the meaning as defined under Fundamental Rules.
- Provincialised employees of local bodies.

2. Retired employees:

- All Service Pensioners
- Family pensioners
- Re-employed service pensioners

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Aadhaar Card: Scan your Aadhaar Card and ensure the photo and the Aadhaar Number are clearly visible.
- Photograph: A scan of a 45mm x 35mm ICAO compliant passport size colour photograph of 200Kb size.
- Disability Certificates (if any)
- Spouse’s Pensioner ID/Employee ID: A scanned copy of the spouse In case working in the State Government or is a Service Pensioner.
- Date of Birth Certificate: A scan of the DOB Certificate of dependent family members less than 5 years of age.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the financial coverage offered by the Andhra Pradesh government under EHS?
content.text: The financial coverage offered by EHS is up to Rs. 2 lakhs per episode of illness for state government employees, pensioners, and their dependent family members.

content.section_title: FAQ: How do I check the status of my EHS application?
content.text: You can check the status of your EHS application online through the official website. Follow the step-by-step guide provided on the website to check the status.

content.section_title: FAQ: How do I find my EHS ID?
content.text: You can find your EHS ID by contacting the 104-Seva Kendra by dialing 104, or by contacting the respective Sub Treasury Office (STO)/Assistant Program Officer (APO).

content.section_title: FAQ: How do I check the medical reimbursement status for pensioners in Andhra Pradesh?
content.text: Pensioners can check the status of their medical reimbursement by following the same process as state government employees

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the contribution ratio of the State Government versus the employee towards EHS?
content.text: The contribution ratio for EHS is 60:40, with the State Government contributing 60% and the employee contributing 40%.

content.section_title: FAQ: How much is the monthly contribution for EHS for different pay grades?
content.text: The monthly contribution for Slab A (Pay Grade I to IV) and Slab B (Pay Grade V to XVII) is Rs. 90, while the monthly contribution for Slab C (Pay Grade XVIII to XXXII) is Rs. 120

content.section_title: FAQ: Do I need to apply for EHS coverage after hospitalization?
content.text: No, you do not need to apply for EHS coverage after hospitalization. The network hospital takes care of the process within 10 days of discharge.

title: District Central Cooperative Banks and through its Branches: For Maternity Loan through Self Help Groups
topic: District Central Cooperative Banks and through its Branches: For Maternity Loan through Self Help Groups

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme offers financial aid to pregnant women via Self Help Groups. The loan is capped at ₹2,000/- with an interest rate of 11%, subject to changes.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The "District Central Cooperative Banks and through its Branches: For Maternity Loan through Self Help Groups" scheme launched by the Co-operation, Food and Consumer Protection Department, Tamil Nadu, offers financial support through Self Help Groups to pregnant women for maternity-related expenses. The loan is capped at ₹2,000/- with an interest rate of 11%, subject to changes.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: - **Access to Loan:** ₹2,000/-.  - **Interest Rate:** 11% (subject to change).

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
- The beneficiary should be a woman.
- The beneficiary should be pregnant.
- The beneficiary should be a resident of Tamil Nadu.
- Loans should be applicable only for Maternity.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Step 1:** The interested applicant should collect the application form from the District Central Cooperative Banks or its branches.
**Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste the passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
**Step 3:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the concerned authority..
**Step 4:** Request a receipt or acknowledgment from the concerned authority to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, a unique identification number (if applicable).

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Identity proof (Aadhaar, PAN card, etc.).
- Address proof.
- Proof of maternity.
- Any other documents (if required).

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the objective of this scheme?
content.text: The scheme offers financial aid to pregnant women via Self Help Groups. The loan is capped at [?]2,000/- with an interest rate of 11%, subject to changes.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can apply for the maternity loan?
content.text: Pregnant women are eligible for the maternity loan.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the maximum loan amount available?
content.text: The maximum loan amount available under the scheme is [?]2,000/-.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the interest rate on the loan?
content.text: The interest rate for the loan is currently set at 11%, but it is subject to change.

content.section_title: FAQ: What documents are required to apply?
content.text: Applicants must provide proof of pregnancy, identification documents, and any other documents if required..

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there an income limit to apply?
content.text: There is no specific income requirement to qualify for the maternity loan.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the process for applying?
content.text: Interested individuals must visit the nearest District Central Cooperative Bank branch, submit the application form along with the required documents.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is the loan amount fixed?
content.text: Yes, the loan amount is fixed at a maximum of [?]2,000/-.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can the loan be used for other purposes?
content.text: No, the loan is specifically intended for maternity-related expenses only.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can individuals contact for more information?
content.text: Applicants can contact the General Manager or Special Officer at their nearest cooperative bank branch for more information.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: "Divyangjan Scholarship Scheme" provides financial assistance for the education and training of differently-abled children, who are regular students in a school or college. The application needs to be submitted to the District Office, Panchayat and Social Welfare / Janpad Panchayat Office.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: "Divyangjan Scholarship Scheme" by the Social Welfare Department, Government of Chhattisgarh provides financial assistance for the education and training of differently-abled children, who are regular students in a school or college. The application needs to be submitted in prescribed format to the District Office, Panchayat and Social Welfare / Janpad Panchayat Office through the Head of the Institution.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
- ₹150/- per month for the Students of 1st to 5th Standard
- ₹170/- per month for the Students of 6th to 8th Standard
- ₹190/- per month for the Students of 9th to 12th Standard

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
- The applicant should be a resident of Chhattisgarh.
- The applicant should be a Student.
- The applicant should be differently-abled.
- The applicant should have a disability of 40% or above.
- The applicant should be a regular student in a college/technical school.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Step 1:** The interested applicant should take print of the [prescribed format](https://sw.cg.gov.in/sites/default/files/divyangscholarshipscheme.pdf) of the application form available on the official website of the Social Welfare Department, Government of Chhattisgarh.
**Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
**Step 3:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents, within the prescribed period (if any), to the **District Office of Social Welfare / Janpad  Panchayat Office** through the Head of the institution.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Disability Certificate
- Residence Proof (Aadhaar Card, Ration Card, or any government-issued ID).
- Income Certificate
- Bonafide Certificate from the school, college, or technical institution.
- Previous Academic Marksheet
- Bank Account Details

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the monthly financial benefits provided to students under this scheme?
content.text: Students receive [?]150/- per month for 1st to 5th standard, [?]170/- per month for 6th to 8th standard, and [?]190/- per month for 9th to 12th standard.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the minimum disability percentage required to qualify for this scheme?
content.text: Applicants must have a disability of 40% or above to be eligible for the financial assistance.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can students from any state apply for this scholarship scheme?
content.text: No, only students who are residents of Chhattisgarh are eligible to apply for this scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What type of students are eligible to apply for this financial assistance?
content.text: Regular students enrolled in a school, college, or technical institution are eligible to apply.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any specific format for submitting the application form?
content.text: Yes, applicants must use the prescribed format available on the official website of the Social Welfare Department, Government of Chhattisgarh.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where should the completed application form be submitted?
content.text: The application form must be submitted to the District Office, Panchayat, or Social Welfare/Janpad Panchayat Office through the Head of the Institution.

content.section_title: FAQ: What documents are required to apply for this financial assistance?
content.text: Applicants need a domicile certificate, disability certificate, proof of enrolment, and marksheets or passing certificates.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is a domicile certificate mandatory for applying to this scheme?
content.text: Yes, applicants must provide a domicile certificate to prove their residency in Chhattisgarh.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can a student with less than 40% disability apply for this scheme?
content.text: No, only students with a disability of 40% or above are eligible to apply.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can students from technical schools apply for this financial assistance?
content.text: Yes, regular students enrolled in technical schools are eligible to apply.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any age limit specified for applicants under this scheme?
content.text: No specific age limit is mentioned; however, applicants must be regular students in a school, college, or technical institution.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the process to obtain the application form for this scheme?
content.text: Applicants can download and print the prescribed application form from the official website of the Social Welfare Department, Government of Chhattisgarh.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to provide fortified, flavored, double toned pasteurized milk to students, pregnant women and lactating women. The scheme is implemented by the Anganwadi Centers and also by the Child Development Project Officers.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The scheme "Doodh Sanjeevani Yojana" was launched by the Department of Education, Government of Gujarat. The scheme aims to provide fortified, flavored, double toned pasteurized milk to students, pregnant women and lactating women. The scheme is implemented by the Anganwadi Centers and also by the Child Development Project Officers.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
> ***For the Students (Education Department)***

◦ The scheme aims to provide nutritious food to the students.
◦ The students get 200 milliliters of flavoured milk per day.
◦ The milk provided is fortified with added 3% fat, 24 grams carbohydrate, 7 grams protein, Vitamin A 500 International Units, and Vitamin D 40 International Units.
◦ This provision is served five days in a week.
> ***For Women & their Child (Women & Child Development Department)***

◦ Children in the age group of 6 months to 6 years registered at Anganwadi centers receive 100 milliliters of fortified, flavored, double toned pasteurized milk, five days a week.
◦ Pregnant and lactating mothers registered at Anganwadi centers receive 200
milliliters of fortified, flavored, double toned pasteurized milk, twice in a week.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
For the Students
◦ The student must be studying in an elementary school.
◦ The student must be studying in a Balvatika or in Standard 1st to 8th.
◦ The student must be a studying in a Government, Grant-in-Aid, or Primary School located in one of the 28 talukas of the developing tribal areas of Gujarat.

For Women & their Child
◦ The applicant must be a child registered at an Anganwadi center between 6 months and 6 years of age.
◦ The applicant must be a pregnant mother/lactating mother registered at an Anganwadi center.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
1.   The intended beneficiaries receive the fortified, flavored milk directly at the premises (school or Anganwadi center) upon meeting eligibility criteria, and therefore typically do not need to apply separately for this scheme.
2.   For scheme implementation related details,

- The students should inquire about the process at their primary school.
- Pregnant Mothers, or lactating mothers should inquire at the Anganwadi Center.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text: The benefits (fortified, flavored milk) are provided directly at the premises (schools or Anganwadi centers) to the individuals meeting the eligibility criteria, therefore any formal submission of supporting documents is not required.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can children attending government or grant-in-aid schools in specific tribal regions of Gujarat receive fortified milk as part of a nutritional support initiative?
content.text: Yes. Students enrolled in government, grant-in-aid, or primary schools located in 28 talukas of developing tribal areas receive 200 milliliters of flavored fortified milk daily, five days per week.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the frequency of milk distribution for expecting and nursing mothers who are registered beneficiaries at childcare centers?
content.text: Pregnant and lactating mothers registered at Anganwadi centers receive 200 milliliters of fortified, flavored, double toned pasteurized milk twice weekly.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are young children registered at Anganwadi centers between six months and six years eligible to receive nutritional milk supplements through this initiative?
content.text: Yes. Children aged 6 months to 6 years who are registered at Anganwadi centers receive 100 milliliters of fortified, flavored, double toned pasteurized milk five days per week.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there a requirement for beneficiaries to submit formal documentation to avail the fortified milk provided at educational institutions or childcare centers?
content.text: No. Since benefits are provided directly at premises to individuals meeting eligibility criteria, formal submission of supporting documents by beneficiaries is generally not required.

content.section_title: FAQ: Which nutritional components are added to the milk provided to elementary school students under this nutritional support program?
content.text: The milk is fortified with 3% added fat, 24 grams carbohydrate, 7 grams protein, 500 International Units of Vitamin A, and 40 International Units of Vitamin D.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the weekly frequency of milk distribution for elementary school students enrolled in schools within designated tribal development areas?
content.text: Students receive 200 milliliters of flavored fortified milk per day, distributed five days per week during school days.

content.section_title: FAQ: Do pregnant mothers need to be formally registered at specific childcare centers to qualify for receiving nutritional milk supplements under this initiative?
content.text: Yes. Pregnant mothers must be registered at Anganwadi centers to receive 200 milliliters of fortified, flavored, double toned pasteurized milk twice per week.

content.section_title: FAQ: What quantity of fortified milk do young children registered at Anganwadi centers receive per serving under this nutritional support scheme?
content.text: Children in the age group of 6 months to 6 years receive 100 milliliters of fortified, flavored, double toned pasteurized milk per serving.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there a separate application process that students or parents must complete to enroll their children in this milk distribution program?
content.text: No. Intended beneficiaries generally receive the fortified milk directly at premises upon meeting eligibility criteria and typically do not need to apply separately for the initiative.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are lactating mothers who are registered at Anganwadi centers eligible to receive fortified milk as part of this nutritional wellness program?
content.text: Yes. Lactating mothers registered at Anganwadi centers are eligible to receive 200 milliliters of fortified, flavored, double toned pasteurized milk twice weekly.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the daily serving size of fortified milk provided to elementary school students studying in government or aided schools?
content.text: Elementary school students receive 200 milliliters of flavored fortified milk per day during the distribution days at their respective schools.

content.section_title: FAQ: Which educational institutions in Gujarat are covered under this milk distribution initiative for providing nutritional support to students?
content.text: Government, grant-in-aid, and primary schools located in 28 talukas of the developing tribal areas of Gujarat are covered under this initiative.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to enhance the nutritional health of children aged 6 months to 6 years and pregnant and lactating mothers by providing fortified flavored milk in Anganwadi centers across 20 districts in the state.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The "Doodh Sanjivani Scheme" launched by the Women and Child Development Department, Gujarat, is a welfare initiative designed to improve the nutritional status of children and women in tribal and underdeveloped communities. The scheme benefits children aged 6 months to 6 years and pregnant and lactating mothers registered at Anganwadi centers. It covers 138 tribal and developing communities in 20 districts and is implemented through Anganwadi centers.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: - **Nutritional Support for Children:** 100 ml of fortified flavored milk is provided 5 days a week to children aged 6 months to 6 years.  - **Nutritional Support for Mothers:** 200 ml of fortified flavored milk is provided 2 days a week to pregnant and lactating mothers.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
- The applicant must be a child aged 6 months to 6 years or a pregnant or lactating mother.
- The applicant must be registered at an Anganwadi center.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Step 1:** Visit the nearest Anganwadi center.
**Step 2:** Register yourself or your child with the Anganwadi center.
**Step 3:** Provide the required documents.
**Step 4:** Avail of the benefits during scheduled distribution days.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Birth Proof for child.
- Identity proof (e.g., Aadhaar card, ration card).
- Pregnancy proof for pregnant mothers (e.g., medical certificate).
- Any other document (if required).

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the main objective of the "Doodh Sanjivani Scheme"?
content.text: The scheme aims to improve the nutritional status of children and mothers in tribal and underdeveloped communities.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible for the scheme?
content.text: Children aged 6 months to 6 years and pregnant or lactating mothers registered at Anganwadi centers are eligible for the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What type of milk is provided under the scheme?
content.text: Fortified flavored milk is provided under the scheme to enhance nutritional intake.

content.section_title: FAQ: How much milk do children receive under the scheme?
content.text: Children aged 6 months to 6 years receive 100 ml of fortified flavored milk 5 days a week.

content.section_title: FAQ: How much milk do pregnant and lactating mothers receive?
content.text: Pregnant and lactating mothers receive 200 ml of fortified flavored milk 2 days a week.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any cost to avail of the benefits of the scheme?
content.text: No, the milk is provided free of cost to all eligible beneficiaries.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where can beneficiaries access the benefits of the scheme?
content.text: Benefits can be accessed at Anganwadi centers in the participating districts.

content.section_title: FAQ: Does the scheme have an income limit for eligibility?
content.text: No, there is no income limit for eligibility under the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can beneficiaries register for the scheme?
content.text: Beneficiaries must register themselves or their children at their nearest Anganwadi center.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can children who are not registered at an Anganwadi center benefit from the scheme?
content.text: No, only children registered at Anganwadi centers are eligible for the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: In how many districts is the Doodh Sanjivani Scheme implemented?
content.text: The scheme is implemented in 20 districts of the state, covering 138 tribal and developing communities.

content.section_title: FAQ: What should beneficiaries do if they need more information about the scheme?
content.text: Beneficiaries can contact their nearest Anganwadi center or the Child Development Scheme Officer for further information.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme “Dr. Ambedkar Medical Aid Scheme” has been revised and implemented by the Directorate of Welfare of Scheduled Caste & Backward Classes, Government of Haryana in 2009. Under this scheme, the state government provides medical treatment facilities to patients of the SC/ST communities.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
The scheme “Dr. Ambedkar Medical Aid Scheme” has been revised and implemented by the Directorate of Welfare of Scheduled Caste & Backward Classes, Government of Haryana in 2009. The scheme is meant to provide medical treatment facilities to patients suffering from serious ailments requiring surgery of the Kidney, Heart, Liver, Cancer, and Brain or any other life-threatening diseases including Knee surgery and Spinal surgery to Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe persons whose annual family income is less than ₹ 1,00,000/- per annum and will be implemented through the following Hospitals:
•	All India Institute of Medical Sciences, New Delhi.
•	Sanjay Gandhi Post Graduate Institute, Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh.
•	Patna Medical College Hospital, Patna, Bihar.
•	Jabalpur Hospital and Research Centre, Jabalpur, Madhya Pradesh.
•	B. Barua Cancer Institute, Guwahati, Assam.
•	Birla Heart Foundation, Kolkata, West Bengal.
•	Kalinga Hospital Ltd. Chandrashekharpur, Bhubaneswar, Orissa.
•	Tata Cancer Research Institute, Mumbai, Maharashtra.
•	Nizam Institute of Medical Sciences, Hyderabad, Andhra Pradesh.
•	The Voluntary Health Services, Chennai.
•	All CGHS-approved Hospitals as revised from time to time by the Ministry of Health & Family Welfare, Government of India.
•	All State Government Medical Colleges attached Hospitals even if not included under CGHS Scheme.
•	All State Hospitals.
•	All Hospitals recognized by State Government.
•	All Hospitals are fully funded by either the Central Government or the State Governments.
•	All Government Hospitals in District Headquarters/ major towns where surgery or treatment facility for Kidney, Heart, Liver Cancer, and Brain or any other life-threatening disease including knee surgery and spinal surgery is available.
•	In exceptional cases where the Chairperson is personally convinced of the genuineness and justification for the need to cover any Hospital outside the approved list, can be approached for eligible treatment.

Disbursement:
1.	75% of the estimated cost of the treatment will be directly released to the concerned Hospital, with a maximum ceiling limit of ₹ 1,00,000/- in each case, in the form of a crossed cheque/ DD.
2.	The 50% of the total admissible medical aid will be paid, in advance, directly to the Hospital before surgery as the first installment.
3.	The remaining 50% amount will be released to the concerned Hospital after the surgery, on submission of the final bills duly certified by the Medical Superintendent of the said Hospital.
4.	Further, medical aid from the foundation and other sources should not exceed the total estimated cost of the treatment. A certificate in this regard should be obtained from the Medical Superintendent of the concerned Hospital.
5.	The Estimated Cost certificate to be submitted along with the application should contain the date fixed for the surgical operation.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
- Under this scheme medical treatment facilities provide to the patients suffering from serious ailments of Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe persons whose annual family income is less than ₹ 1,00,000/- p.a.
- A patient having serious ailments requiring surgery of the Kidney, Heart, Liver, Cancer, Knee, Spinal and Brain, or any other life-threatening diseases may be given treatment.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant shall belong to Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe Community.
1. Annual family income shall not exceed ₹ 1,00,000/- per annum.
1. Those who are suffering from major ailments which need surgery such as kidney, heart, liver, cancer, brain or any other life-threatening disease including knee surgery and spinal surgery.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
Application Link: [http://haryanascbc.gov.in/sites/default/files/documents/application_form_for_medical_aid.pdf](http://haryanascbc.gov.in/sites/default/files/documents/application_form_for_medical_aid.pdf)
**Step 01:** The applicant shall apply for medical aid in the prescribed application form, duly certified by the Medical Superintendent of the concerned Hospital. The application format is given overleaf.
**Step 02:** The application must be submitted along with the caste certificate, income certificate, ration card, and estimated cost of the treatment duly certified by the Medical Superintendent of the Hospital.
**Step 03:** The application shall be recommended and forwarded by the local sitting Member of Parliament (Lok Sabha or Rajya Sabha) or by the District Magistrate & Collector / Deputy Commissioner of the concerned District or the Secretary in charge of Health & Social Welfare Departments of the State/UT.
**Step 04:** The duly filled-in form should reach the Director, Dr. Ambedkar Foundation, 15, Janpath, New Delhi, at least 15 days before the date of surgery. All the applications received will be processed by Dr. Ambedkar Foundation.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Original Estimated Cost certificate duly signed by the Medical Superintendent of the concerned hospital.
- Original or attested photocopies of the income certificate, caste certificate, and ration card of the patient.
- The application should be recommended and forwarded either by a local sitting Member of Parliament (Lok Sabha or Rajya Sabha) or by the District Magistrate & Collectors/Deputy Commissioner of the concerned District or the Secretary in-charge of Health & Social Welfare Departments of State/UT.
- Medical aid from the Foundation and other sources should not exceed the total estimated cost of the treatment. A certificate in this regard should be obtained from the Medical Superintendent of the concerned Hospital.
- The Estimated Cost Certificate, accompanied with the application, should contain the date fixed for the surgical operation as far as possible.
- An attested photograph of the patient should be affixed on the Application Form.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the scope of this scheme?
content.text: Under this scheme, the state government provides medical treatment facilities to patients suffering from serious ailments to Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe persons whose annual family income is less than [?] 1,00,000/- per annum.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the types of medical treatments provided under this scheme?
content.text: Patients having serious ailments requiring surgery of the Kidney, Heart, Liver, Cancer, Knee, Spinal, and Brain, or any other life-threatening diseases may be provided treatment under this scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: How much amount of the treatment will be directly released to the concerned Hospital?
content.text: The 75% of the estimated cost of the treatment will be directly released to the concerned Hospital, with a maximum ceiling limit of [?]1,00,000/- in each case, in the form of a crossed cheque / DD.

content.section_title: FAQ: How much amount of the total admissible medical aid will be paid, in advance to the concerned Hospital?
content.text: The 50% of the total admissible medical aid will be paid, in advance, directly to the Hospital before surgery as the first installment. The remaining 50% amount will be released to the concerned Hospital after the surgery, on submission of the final bills duly certified by the Medical Superintendent of the said Hospital.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is this scheme for all type of patient?
content.text: No, this scheme is only for the patient of Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe communities.

content.section_title: FAQ: What should be the family income to avail the benefit of this scheme?
content.text: The annual family income should be less than [?] 1,00,000/- per annum.

content.section_title: FAQ: How I can apply for the medical aid?
content.text: The applicant shall apply for medical aid in the prescribed application form, duly certified by the Medical Superintendent of the concerned Hospital.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can I apply only through offline mode for medical aid?
content.text: Yes, the duly filled-in form should reach the Director, Dr. Ambedkar Foundation, 15, Janpath, New Delhi, at least 15 days before the date of surgery.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who will process the application?
content.text: All the applications received will be processed by Dr. Ambedkar Foundation.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The DULARI Scheme was implemented by the Directorate of Health Services in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands to provide incentives for newborn baby girls and to the girl children who pass Classes VIII, X, and XII.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The “Dulari Scheme” was launched by the Health Department, Union Territory of Andaman and Nicobar Islands on 26th January, 2013 to give incentives to newborn baby girls and girl children on passing VIII, X & XII standards. As per the present scheme, the girl child born on or after 26.01.2013 will be eligible to get the benefit under the scheme for birth incentives and the girl children who are below 13 years of age not registered under the Planned family scheme (old scheme) will also get the benefit of this scheme.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
As per the scheme, the following benefits are given to the girl child whose parents are domicile of Andaman & Nicobar Islands:

**Sl. No.** **Descriptions** **Amount of Assistance**1Benefits at Birth- Institution deliver₹8,000/-2Benefits at Birth- Home delivery₹5,000/-3Benefits on passing VIII Standard₹5,000/-4Benefits on passing X Standard₹10,000/-5Benefits on passing XII Standard₹10,000/-6Benefits on getting married at the age of 21 years₹5,000/-7Benefits on getting married after age of 25 years₹10,000/-

> **Note**- The incentive amount will be fixed in a bank account and must not be withdrawn before the girl attains 18 years of age, unless permitted by the A&N Administration.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant must be a domicile of the Union Territory of Andaman & Nicobar Islands.
1. The applicant must be a woman or a girl child to avail benefits under the scheme.
1. The applicant must be born on or after 01.10.2013 to receive full benefits.
1. Girl children between 0–13 years, born before 01.10.2013 and not covered under the Planned Family Scheme, should be eligible only for educational incentives after passing Class VIII.

content.section_title: Exclusions
content.text:
> **Benefits will cease if:**

1. The applicant or her parents provide false or concealed information.
1. The girl gets married before attaining 21 years of age.
1. The girl drops out of school (as further education-based incentives will not be granted).
1. In case of the girl’s death before reaching the eligible disbursal age, the Directorate of Health Services may permit premature closure of the deposit, and the applicable amount may be released to the parents.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
> **Registration on Portal:**

**Step 01:** Visit [official Portal.](https://serviceonline.gov.in/login.do)
**Step 02:** On the home page, click on ‘Register’ and fill in all the mandatory details.
**Step 03:** Click on the ‘Submit’ button and validate your Email ID & Mobile Number using OTP received.
**Step 04:** After successful validation, the applicant will be registered.

> **Registration for Dulari:**

**Step 01:** Applicant can login on [official portal ](https://serviceonline.gov.in/login.do)through their email ID that is used while registering
**Step 02:** Enter Password & Captcha
**Step 03:** Click on ‘Login’
**Step 04:** After successful login, click on ‘Apply for Services’ and then ‘View all Available Services’
**Step 05:** Now, search for ‘Dulari’ and click on ‘Dulari Registration’.
**Step 06:** Fill in all the mandatory details, preview the form, and click on ‘Submit’.
**Step 07:** After successful registration, the applicant will receive a registration ID.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Identity proof of parent (s) i.e. Aadhaar Card
- Passport-size photograph of parent (s)
- Birth Certificate of child
- Local certificate/domicile certificate/permanent resident certificate
- Ration Card of Family
- Address proof of the parent(s)
- Mark sheet of Class XIII/X/XII, if applicable
- Bonafide certificate from the school or school leaving certificate, if applicable
- Marriage registration certificate, if applicable
- Bank account details/Copy of passbook
- Any other document as required

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the Dulari Scheme?
content.text: The 'Dulari Scheme' is a government initiative launched by the Health Department of the Union Territory of Andaman and Nicobar Islands on January 26, 2013, aimed at providing various benefits to women and their girl children.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible for benefits under the Scheme?
content.text: Parents domiciled in Andaman & Nicobar Islands with girl children born on or after January 26, 2013, are eligible for benefits. Additionally, girl children between 0-13 years born before October 1, 2013, and not covered under the previous Planned Family Scheme are also eligible.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the benefits provided under the Scheme?
content.text: The scheme provides benefits at birth, educational milestones (VIII, X, and XII standards), and upon marriage at the ages of 21 and 25.

content.section_title: FAQ: How much are the benefits for institutional delivery?
content.text: For institutional delivery, the benefit is [?]8,000/-.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the benefit amount for home delivery?
content.text: For home delivery, the benefit is [?]5,000/-.

content.section_title: FAQ: How much is the benefit upon passing the VIII standard?
content.text: The benefit upon passing VIII standard is [?]5,000/-.

content.section_title: FAQ: What benefits are provided upon passing X and XII standards?
content.text: Upon passing X standard, the benefit is [?]10,000/-, and upon passing XII standard, it is also [?]10,000/-.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are there benefits provided for marriage under the Scheme?
content.text: Yes, there are benefits upon marriage. [?]5,000/- is given upon marriage at the age of 21, and [?]10,000/- is given if married after the age of 25.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the eligibility criteria for availing benefits under the Scheme?
content.text: Eligibility includes having parents domiciled in Andaman & Nicobar Islands and being born on or after January 26, 2013.

content.section_title: FAQ: What leads to the cessation of benefits under the Dulari Scheme?
content.text: Benefits will cease if false information is provided, if the child marries before the age of 21, if the child becomes a school dropout, or if the child passes away before reaching the age for benefit disbursal.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any penalty for providing false information?
content.text: Yes, benefits will cease if false or misleading information is provided by the parent or beneficiary.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can I apply for benefits under the Scheme?
content.text: The eligible applicant can register and apply under the scheme through the online Portal: https://serviceonline.gov.in/

content.text: Chhattisgarh State Government started this scheme "Durghatana Mein Chikitsa Sahaayata Yojana" for labor workers.

content.text: "Chhattisgarh Durghatana Mein Chikitsa Sahaayata Yojana" has been started by the Chhattisgarh Government and manage/run by the Chhattisgarh, Labor Department.
