title: Chief Minister's Comprehensive Health Insurance Scheme
topic: Chief Minister's Comprehensive Health Insurance Scheme

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: Chief Minister Comprehensive Health Insurance Scheme  provides quality health care to the eligible person through empanelled government and private hospitals to reduce the financial hardship to the enrolled families and move towards universal health coverage by effectively linking with public

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
The Chief Minister Comprehensive Health Insurance Scheme, formerly known as Kalaingar Kaappittu Thittam, was launched on 23rd July 2009, with the objective of providing quality healthcare to eligible individuals through empanelled government and private hospitals, thereby reducing financial hardship for enrolled families and moving towards universal health coverage by effectively linking with the public health system.

From January 2022 to 2027, the scheme is being implemented through United India Insurance Company. As of January 2022, the scheme covers around 1.37 crore families. A total of 1090 procedures, 8 follow-up procedures, and 52 diagnostic procedures are covered under CMCHIS in 800 government and 900 private empanelled hospitals.

The scheme aims to provide cashless hospitalization for specific ailments/procedures and provides coverage up to Rs. 5,00,000/- per family, per year on a floater basis for the ailments and procedures covered under the scheme. The CMCHIS is a significant step towards ensuring that vulnerable sections of society receive adequate healthcare without suffering financial distress, while simultaneously promoting access to quality medical care.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
The Chief Minister’s Comprehensive Health Insurance Scheme is intended for the residents of Tamil Nadu who meet the specified eligibility criteria. To qualify for benefits under the scheme, the applicant's name must be present in the family card, and their family's annual income should be less than Rs.1,20,000/- per annum. This eligibility can be confirmed by producing necessary documents, such as the family card, Aadhar card, and income certificate issued by the VAO/Revenue Authorities, along with a self-declaration from the head of the family.

Under this scheme, the definition of family includes the eligible member, their legal spouse, their children, and their dependent parents. By ensuring that these individuals meet the necessary criteria, they can access the benefits of the scheme and receive quality healthcare services without financial burden.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Income certificate from the Village Administrative Officer/Revenue authorities
- Ration Card (both original and photocopy)
- Self-declaration from the head of the family
- Proof of identity
- Address proof
- Aadhar card
- PAN card (optional)

content.section_title: FAQ: Which Family Members Are Eligible To Enrol Under CMCHIS?
content.text: The following members of a family with an annual income of less than 1.2 lakhs rupees can enrol under the scheme:>Head of the family>Legal spouse>Children of the eligible person>Dependant parents

content.section_title: FAQ: Can I file a claim online?
content.text: Yes, you can file a claim online on the official website of the Tamil Nadu Chief Minister's by entering the User ID and Password provided to you.

content.section_title: FAQ: Till what age are children of the beneficiary included in the policy cover?
content.text: The children of the eligible individual are covered under this policy until they reach the age of 25 years, get employed or get married, whichever comes first.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are reimbursement claims allowed under CMCHIS Health Insurance?
content.text: No, only cashless claims can be availed at an empanelled hospital. This is to reduce the financial burden on the beneficiary of the health insurance plan.

content.section_title: FAQ: What if the head of the family is not present at the time of enrollment?
content.text: If the head of the family is not present at the time of enrollment, the spouse is considered as the head. The enrollment process is still carried out for all family members present.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are dental treatments included in CMCHIS?
content.text: Yes, various types of dental treatments are covered under CMCHIS. Dental procedures come under outpatient treatments

content.section_title: FAQ: Is abdominal hysterectomy listed in CMCHIS?
content.text: Yes, a beneficiary of Amma Health Insurance plan can undergo abdominal hysterectomy for both benign and malignant conditions. These costs will be covered under the plan.

title: Chief Minister’s Health Insurance Scheme - Nagaland
topic: Chief Minister’s Health Insurance Scheme - Nagaland

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: Launched on 16th October 2022, the scheme "Chief Minister Health Insurance Scheme (CMHIS)" is a Public Health Insurance Scheme by the Health and Family Welfare Department, Government of Nagaland. This scheme provides cashless treatment up to ₹20 lakhs for employees and pensioners and ₹5 lakhs.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
Launched on 16th October 2022, the scheme "Chief Minister Health Insurance Scheme (CMHIS)" is a Public Health Insurance Scheme by the Health and Family Welfare Department, Government of Nagaland. This scheme provides cashless treatment up to ₹20 lakhs for employees and pensioners and ₹5 lakhs for the general category, covering inpatient and specific daycare procedures in empanelled hospitals across India.
The applications are accepted through Primary Health Centres (PHCs), Community Health Centres (CHCs), District and Sub-division Treasury Offices, outreach camps, and designated private hospitals.
The scheme is being implemented by the Department of Health and Family Welfare, Government of Nagaland.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
All beneficiaries of the scheme are entitled to free and cashless hospitalization care for various ailments including pre-existing conditions/diseases, which can be availed from any empanelled hospital across the country. The treatment will include both surgical and medical procedures and limited day-care packages. However, OPD services and standalone diagnostics investigations will not be covered.

1. CMHIS (GEN) beneficiaries will be entitled to more than 1950 medical and surgical packages across 27 major clinical specialties as well as entitlement to General Ward, similar to AB PM-JAY.
1. CMHIS (EP) beneficiaries will be entitled similar to that available for central government employees under the Central Government Health Scheme (CGHS) as well as differential room entitlement as per their employee grade.

**Annual Risk Cover/ Sum Insured per family:**

1. For CMHIS (GEN) beneficiary families, the Sum Insured is ₹ 5,00,000/- (Rupees Five Lakhs Only) per family per annum on a family floater basis, similar to AB PM-JAY.
1. For CMHIS (EP) beneficiary families, the Sum Insured is ₹ 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakhs Only) per family per annum on a family floater basis.
1. Further, for Government employees, any additional expenses beyond the sum insured shall be reimbursed on a case-to-case basis by the State government on recommendation of the State Medical Board.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
**Eligibility**
The Chief Minister Health Insurance Scheme is available to all permanent and indigenous residents of Nagaland. They are divided into two categories:
**1. For CMHIS (EP):**
- The applicant should be a government employee, pensioner, or dependent family member.
- The applicant should possess valid supporting documents such as the PIMS Code, PPO book, Aadhaar card, and certificates for dependents.
**2. For CMHIS (GC):**
- The applicant should be an Indigenous Inhabitant or Permanent Resident of Nagaland not covered under AB PM-JAY.
- The applicant should possess an Indigenous Inhabitant Certificate (IIC) or Permanent Resident Certificate (PRC) issued after 2016, along with an Aadhaar card and valid mobile number.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
**Step 1:** Visit the Official Website of the [Chief Minister’s Health Insurance Scheme (CMHIS) Nagaland](https://cmhis.nagaland.gov.in/).
**Step 2:** Click on the Register. Read out the [R[egistration](https://cmhis.nagaland.gov.in/register) instructions carefully. ](https://cmhis.nagaland.gov.in/register)
**Step 3:** [Fill in the application form with all the required details, including personal details, family details, income details, and medical history.](https://cmhis.nagaland.gov.in/register)
**Step 4:** Submit the completed application form along with the necessary documents to the nearest Common Service Center (CSC) in your area or the empanelled hospital.
**Step 5:** The CSC or the empanelled hospital will verify the application form and documents and upload the details to the online portal.
**Step 6:** The insurance provider will verify the application and approve or reject the application based on the eligibility criteria.

> Note: For any query call us at  14555 or mail at  nagaland.nhpm@gmail.com.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
List of the required documents
- Mandatory KYC Document Required for CMHIS (GEN) category:
(a): For General Population :
- Valid Mobile Phone number.
- Indigenous Inhabitant Certificate (IIC) or Permanent Residential Certificate (PRC) issued by the competent authority of the respective district Administration after 2016.
- NB : Ration Card holders must get their eCards issued using their ration card in the BIS portal directly without registering in the CMHIS Portal (Ration Card data is already integrated with NHA BIS. Operators to go directly to the BIS URL).

(b): For employees without PIMS number:
- Valid Mobile Phone number
- Govt issued Photo ID card
- Engagement/ Appointment Order.
- Proof of continuity of being engaged (Letter from the controlling officer)
- Filled and downloaded online registration form with household member details (FORM), countersigned by Village council Chairman/Ward Chairman.

- Mandatory KYC Document Required under CMHIS (EP) category:
(a): For Pensioners:
- Valid Mobile Phone number.
- First Three pages of the Pensioner’s PPO book indicating PPO number, last pay scale or designation of the post held or last pay is drawn prior to retirement.
(b): For Government employees:
- Valid Mobile Phone number
- PIMS code.
(c): For dependent family members:
- Document proving dependency for each dependent member to be added:
- Proof of Relationship/DOB- Birth Certificate/Aadhaar/Class X Admit Card/voter‘s ID/Pancard or any other Govt. issued ID indicating the relationship with any one of the parents and Date of Birth.
- Disability Certificate in case of PwD

content.section_title: FAQ: What is CMHIS?
content.text: The Chief Minister Health Insurance Scheme (CMHIS) Nagaland was initiated with the aim to alleviate financial hardships due to hospitalization expenses and to prevent inaccessibility to medical care on account of unaffordability, by providing free and cashless benefits for treatment of various ailments, to every citizen of the State.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who are Eligible under CMHIS (GEN) Category:
content.text:
1.     CMHIS (GEN) category includes all Indigenous and/or Permanent residents of the State who are not beneficiaries under the AB PM-JAY or any other public funded health insurance scheme of the government and who are not covered under CMHIS (EP) category.

2.     CMHIS (GEN) category also includes non-dependant family members of CMHIS (EP) categories, employees without PIMS number, contractual, adhoc, fixed pay employees of the State government and contractual employees under various CSS, and their household members.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who are Eligible under CMHIS (EP) Category:
content.text: CMHIS (EP) category includes State government employees who are eligible for monthly Medical Allowance (MA) and or reimbursement of medical expenses under Medical Re-imbursement scheme viz (1). Regular employees, (2). Pensioners, (3). Serving Legislators/ Ex- Legislators, (4). Employees of State Public Sector Undertakings, Corporations and Autonomous Bodies, (5). Workcharge employees (on Scale Pay), and their dependent family members.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the definition of Family under CMHIS(EP):
content.text: The term 'family' means and includes- "Husband or wife as the case may be and dependant family members."

content.section_title: FAQ: Who are not to be treated as members of a household?
content.text:
1 Temporary visitors and guests whose total period of stay is less than 6 months in the past one year.

2.     Members who have permanently migrated or left the household on marriage, employment etc. even if they occasionally visit the HH.

3.     A resident employee, or domestic servant or a paying guest taking common meal and living with the household.

4.     Those who are not considered normal member.

NB:

a.      AB PM-JAY Beneficiaries including Building and other Construction Workers will not be covered under CMHIS (GEN) category.

b.     There is no financial /economic dependency criteria for households under the AB PM-JAY/ CMHIS (General) Category.

content.section_title: FAQ: How much is the Annual Risk Cover/ Sum Insured per family:
content.text:
1.     For CMHIS (GEN) beneficiary families, the Sum Insured is Rs. 5,00,000/- (Rupees Five Lakhs Only) per family per annum on a family floater basis, similar to AB PM-JAY.

2.     For CMHIS (EP) beneficiary families, the Sum Insured is Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakhs Only) per family per annum on a family floater basis.

Further, for Government employees, any additional expenses beyond the sum insured shall be reimbursed on a case-to-case basis by the State government on recommendation of the State Medical Board

content.section_title: FAQ: Are all medical treatments covered under the CMHIS scheme?
content.text: No, certain medical treatments and procedures, such as cosmetic surgeries, dental treatments, and infertility treatments, are not covered under the CMHIS scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can I apply for the CMHIS scheme if I already have health insurance from another provider?
content.text: Yes, you can still apply for the CMHIS scheme if you already have health insurance from another provider. However, you cannot claim benefits from both insurance policies simultaneously for the same treatment

content.section_title: FAQ: Can I include all my family members under the CMHIS scheme?
content.text: Yes, you can include your spouse and up to three dependent children under the CMHIS scheme. However, parents, siblings, and other relatives are not covered under the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can I check my eligibility for the CMHIS scheme?
content.text: You can check your eligibility for the CMHIS scheme by visiting the official website of the Health and Family Welfare Department of Nagaland, contacting the empaneled hospitals and healthcare providers who are implementing the scheme, or visiting the nearest Common Service Center (CSC) in your area.

content.section_title: FAQ: What documents are required to apply for the Chief Minister's Health Insurance Scheme?
content.text: You will need to provide proof of identity, proof of address, and proof of income to apply for the Chief Minister's Health Insurance Scheme. You may also be required to provide additional documents, such as medical certificates or hospital bills.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the coverage provided under the Chief Minister's Health Insurance Scheme?
content.text: The Chief Minister's Health Insurance Scheme provides coverage for medical treatment up to Rs. 5 lakh per family per year. The coverage includes hospitalization expenses, medical consultation fees, laboratory tests, and other medical expenses incurred during the treatment.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The Chief Minister’s free diagnostic services is envisaged to ensure the availability of a minimum set of diagnostic services at government-run health institutions. Under the program, CT scan, X-Ray, and laboratory services are provided free of cost to all irrespective of APL and BPL citizens.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
Quality diagnosis is vital for medical practitioners to make effective decisions about treatment. Access and availability of quality Radiology and Laboratory Services have been key challenges for the health system. The out-of-pocket expenditure of patients on diagnostic tests is increasing and forms the second major segment of such expenditure after medicine. Hence, the provisioning of quality diagnostic services (laboratory and radiological services) in public health facilities is critical for ensuring evidence-based care and reducing expenditure.

To address the above issues, Chief Minister's Free Diagnostics Services Programme is envisaged to ensure the availability of a minimum set of diagnostic services at government health institutions. Under Chief Minister's Free Diagnostic Services Programme CT Scan, X-Ray and Laboratory services are provided in the State of Assam free of cost to all patients in Govt. Health Institutions are irrespective of APL/BPL status.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
1.	To provide essential diagnostic services free of cost to all patients visiting government hospitals. No user charges will be levied on the patient.
2.	Ensure the availability of a minimum set of diagnostics appropriate to the level of care.
3.	Reduce high out-of-pocket expenditure incurred by patients for diagnostics.
4.	Use of appropriate diagnostics to screen patients for a set of chronic conditions so as to enable secondary prevention measures.
5.	Improve overall quality of healthcare and patient experience as a result of the availability of comprehensive healthcare in public health facilities.
6.	Free CT scan services, X-Ray services, and Laboratory services are being implemented in District Hospital, PHCs, CHCs, etc in PPP mode

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1.	The applicant must be a resident of Assam.
2.	The scheme is applicable to individuals of all age groups.
3.	There is no specific income criteria, and the scheme is open to all socio-economic backgrounds.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
1.	Visit the nearest government-authorized diagnostic center or hospital.
2.	Fill out the application form for the scheme.
3.	Submit the necessary documents along with the application form.
4.	Upon verification, the diagnostic tests will be conducted free of cost.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
1.	Proof of residence in Assam (e.g., Aadhaar card, voter ID, or residence certificate).
2.	Medical prescription or recommendation from a registered medical practitioner.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who Is Eligible To Avail The Benefits Of The CM Free Diagnostic Scheme?
content.text: Any resident of Assam can avail of the benefits irrespective of their income level.

content.section_title: FAQ: What Types Of Diagnostic Tests Are Covered Under This Scheme?
content.text: The scheme covers a wide range of diagnostic tests, including blood tests, imaging scans, and specialized tests recommended by doctors.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are There Any Income Criteria To Qualify For This Scheme?
content.text: No, there are no specific income criteria. The scheme is open to individuals from all socio-economic backgrounds.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is There Any Limit On The Number Of Diagnostic Tests One Can Avail Under The Scheme?
content.text: There is no specific limit on the number of tests. Individuals can avail themselves of the necessary diagnostic tests as per medical recommendations.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are Private Diagnostic Centers Covered Under This Scheme?
content.text: No, only government-authorized diagnostic centers and hospitals participate in the CM Free Diagnostic Scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is There Any Registration Fee Or Cost Involved In Applying For The Scheme?
content.text: No, there is no registration fee or cost involved. The diagnostic tests are provided free of cost.

content.section_title: FAQ: How Can I Find The Nearest Government-Authorized Diagnostic Center?
content.text: You can inquire at government hospitals or visit the official website of the scheme for a list of authorized diagnostic centers.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can I Avail The Scheme Benefits For My Family Members As Well?
content.text: Yes, the scheme benefits can be availed by all eligible family members.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is There Any Time Limit To Avail The Free Diagnostic Services After The Application?
content.text: The diagnostic services can be availed within a reasonable time frame after the application is submitted.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is The CM Free Diagnostic Scheme Applicable Only To Specific Medical Conditions?
content.text: No, the scheme is applicable for all types of diagnostic tests, irrespective of the medical condition, as per the recommendation of a medical professional.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: "Chikitsa Sahayata Yojana" is a Medical Assistance scheme by the Department of Labour, Employment, Training & Skill Development Govt of Jharkhand. The scheme provides medical assistance to all registered workers who are admitted to the hospital for five or more working days.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: "Chikitsa Sahayata Yojana" is a Medical Assistance scheme by the Department of Labour, Employment, Training & Skill Development Govt of Jharkhand. This is a 100% State Sponsored Scheme. Only the residents/domicile of the state of Jharkhand can apply to this scheme. Applications for this scheme are accepted online through the official website of the department. The scheme provides medical assistance to all registered workers who are admitted to the hospital for five or more working days.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant should be a domicile in the state of Jharkhand.
1. The applicant should be a Worker registered with the 'Jharkhand Building and Other Construction Worker Welfare Board (JBOCWW Board)'.
1. The applicant should be between 18-60 years of age.
1. The applicant should be engaged in construction work such as masons, carpenters, electricians, porters, painters, etc.
1. The applicant should be admitted to the hospital for five or more working days.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Aadhaar Card
- Residential Certificate / Domicile Certificate
- Proof of Identity
- eSHRAM Card
- Passport Sized Photograph
- Proof of Registration as a Construction Worker
- Details of the Bank Account
- Income Certificate
- Proof of the Medical Condition
- Proof of Admission into the Hospital

content.section_title: FAQ: Which Department Manages This Scheme?
content.text: This scheme is managed by the Department of Labour, Employment, Training & Skill Development, Govt. of Jharkhand.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is This Scheme State Sponsored Or Centrally Sponsored?
content.text: This scheme is a 100% State Sponsored Scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where Can I Find The Link To The Scheme Guidelines?
content.text: The Scheme Guidelines can be found at this link - https://shramadhan.jharkhand.gov.in/schemmeDetailsShow.action

content.section_title: FAQ: Can Women Also Apply To This Scheme?
content.text: Yes, this scheme is open to applicants from all genders.

content.section_title: FAQ: Does This Scheme Accept Offline Applications?
content.text: No, this scheme only accepts online applications. In order to apply, please register yourself on the Official website of Comprehensive Labour Management System for Factory Establishment / Worker Registration / Inspection / Management & Grievance Redressal; Department of Labour, Employment, Training & Skill Development; Govt. of Jharkhand.

content.section_title: FAQ: What Is The URL Of The Website Of Comprehensive Labour Management System For Factory Establishment/Worker Registration/Inspection/Management & Grievance Redressal?
content.text: The URL of the website of Comprehensive Labour Management System for Factory Establishment / Worker Registration / Inspection / Management & Grievance Redressal is https://shramadhan.jharkhand.gov.in/home.

content.section_title: FAQ: What Details Are Required To Register On The Portal?
content.text: The following details are required to register on the portal - First Name, Last Name, Email, Mobile, Username and Password.

content.section_title: FAQ: What Details Are Needed To Be Provided In The "Basic Details" Section Of The Application Form?
content.text: The following details are needed to be provided in the "Basic Details" section of the Application Form: BOC Registration Number, Applicant's Name, Applicant's Father's Name / Husband's Name, Applicant's Date of Birth, Applicant's Aadhaar Number, District, Sex.

content.section_title: FAQ: How Do I Know If A Field Is Mandatory?
content.text: The mandatory fields are marked with a "Red Asterisk" (*) at the end.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is There Any Application Fee?
content.text: No. The entire application process is completely free of cost.

content.section_title: FAQ: How Do I Track The Status Of My Application?
content.text: To track the status of your application, follow the stepwise process given below: Step 1: Once submitted, the application goes for 3-level approval: Clerk > Labour Superintendent > DLC. Step 2: Go to Services > BOC Scheme Benefit > Application Status. Step 3: On the next page, fill in your Application ID, and click "Search". The status of your application will be displayed in a tabular format: Application ID, Applicant Name, Scheme Applied, Application Status, Remarks, Date, and Time. Step 4: Once the DLC approves the application, the "Application Status" changes to "Approved". The Applicant can take print of this page by clicking "PRINT" and submit the same at the Labour Office to avail of the scheme benefit.

content.section_title: FAQ: How Many Schemes Can Be Selected At A Time From The List Of Schemes In The Application Form?
content.text: The application form allows only one scheme to be selected per application.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is There A Percentage Of Slots Reserved For Female Applicants?
content.text: No, there is no reservation of slots based on the gender of the applicant.

content.section_title: FAQ: By When Can The Applicant Receive The Scheme Benefits?
content.text: The benefits are due within three months of registration.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can A Person Who Is Domiciled In Madhya Pradesh Also Apply To This Scheme?
content.text: No, this scheme is open to applicants who are domiciled in the state of Jharkhand.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is It Mandatory For The Applicant To Be A Registered Construction Worker (Nirman Shramik) In Order To Be Eligible To Apply?
content.text: Yes, in order to be eligible to apply, the applicant should be a Registered Construction Worker (Nirman Shramik).

content.section_title: FAQ: Where Can I Find The Link To The User Manual Of The Application Process?
content.text: The User Manual of the Application Process can be found at this link - https://shramadhan.jharkhand.gov.in/ftp/WebAdmin/documents/BOC_SCHEME_BENEFIT_APPLICANT_SIDE.pdf

content.section_title: FAQ: What Is The Full Form Of JBOCWWB?
content.text: Jharkhand Building and Other Construction Worker Welfare Board (JBOCWW Board).

content.section_title: FAQ: What Shall Be The Rate Of Payment Of Wages?
content.text: Payment of minimum wages at the prescribed rate for unskilled category workers.

content.section_title: FAQ: For How Many Days Will The Payment Be Paid?
content.text: Payment is equivalent to a maximum of 40 working days.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: "Chikitsa Pratipoorti Yojana" is a scheme by the Department of Labour, Employment, Training & Skill Development Govt of Jharkhand. The scheme provides financial assistance to the Registered Workers for the treatment of serious illnesses (cancer, heart disease, kidney disease, etc.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: "Chikitsa Pratipoorti Yojana" is a scheme by the Department of Labour, Employment, Training & Skill Development Govt of Jharkhand. This is a 100% State Sponsored Scheme. Only the residents/domicile of the state of Jharkhand can apply to this scheme. Applications for this scheme are accepted online through the official website of the department. The scheme provides financial assistance to the Registered Workers for the treatment of serious illnesses such as cancer, heart disease (including surgery), kidney disease (including surgery), incurable mental disease (including surgery), AIDS, etc. The scheme's benefits are applicable once per disease per beneficiary.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant should be a domicile in the state of Jharkhand.
1. The applicant should be a Worker registered with the 'Jharkhand Building and Other Construction Worker Welfare Board (JBOCWW Board)'.
1. The applicant should be 18 years or above in age.
1. The applicant should be engaged in construction work such as masons, carpenters, electricians, porters, painters, etc.
1. The scheme's benefits are applicable once per disease per beneficiary.
1. The applicant should be suffering from one of the following serious illnesses: Cancer, heart disease (including surgery), kidney disease (including surgery), incurable mental disease (including surgery), AIDS, total hip replacement, spinal surgery, major vascular disease, bone marrow transplant, liver transplant, hepatoma, advanced cirrhosis of the liver, retinal detachment, proliferative diabetic retinopathy, reflux disease, reflux disease.

**NOTE:** The list of diseases and the maximum limit of medical expenditure and modifications/modifications made in the list of listed hospitals by the Department of Health, Medical, Education and Family Welfare from time to time and the modifications/modifications made in the list of listed hospitals will be considered applicable for the purpose of this scheme.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Aadhaar Card
- Residential Certificate / Domicile Certificate
- Proof of Identity
- eSHRAM Card
- Passport Sized Photograph
- Proof of Registration as a Construction Worker
- Details of the Bank Account
- Income Certificate
- Proof of the Medical Condition

content.text: This scheme is a 100% State Sponsored Scheme.

title: Children With Special Needs Under Goa Sarva Shiksha Abhivan
topic: Children With Special Needs Under Goa Sarva Shiksha Abhivan

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The "Children With Special Needs (CWSN)" scheme aims to provide comprehensive educational support and resources tailored to the needs of children with disabilities. It is designed to ensure that every child, regardless of their disability type (intellectual, physical, etc.), receives the necessary a

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The scheme "Children With Special Needs (CWSN)" by the Directorate of Education, Government of Goa aims to provide comprehensive educational support and resources tailored to the needs of differently-abled children. It is designed to ensure that every child, regardless of their disability type (intellectual, physical, etc.), receives the necessary assistance to facilitate their education and overall development. This includes provisions for medical assessments, the establishment of resource rooms, home-based education, the provision of teaching-learning materials (TLM), and specialized training for educators and volunteers.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: Grant is released for activities for CWSN, medical assessments, resource rooms, home-based education, TLM, training of educators, volunteer educators, assistive aids, and corrective surgeries.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text: The child must either be differently-abled or require special needs.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
The applicant (parent, heads of school, and others) is required to submit a plain application detailing the specific individual needs of the child with disabilities along with any available medical certificates.
**NOTE:** The applicants can contact the designated educational authorities or visit official government websites providing details about the CWSN scheme.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Aadhaar Card.
- Medical Certificates (if available).
- Other supporting documents, as specified in the scheme guidelines.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the objective of the CWSN scheme?
content.text: The scheme aims to cater to the special educational needs of children with disabilities, ensuring they receive comprehensive support for their education and overall development.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible to apply for the CWSN scheme?
content.text: Parents/guardians of children with disabilities, heads of schools where CWSN are enrolled, and other stakeholders directly involved in their care and education can apply.

content.section_title: FAQ: What types of disabilities are covered under the scheme?
content.text: The scheme covers various disabilities including intellectual disabilities, physical disabilities, and others as specified in the eligibility criteria.

content.section_title: FAQ: What types of assistance are provided under the scheme?
content.text: The scheme provides cash grants for activities related to CWSN such as medical assessments, resource rooms, home-based education, training of educators, and provision of assistive aids.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can I apply for the CWSN scheme?
content.text: Applicants need to submit a plain application detailing the specific individual needs of the child with disabilities along with any available medical certificates.

content.section_title: FAQ: What documents are required to apply for the scheme?
content.text: Applicants typically need to submit a detailed application, medical certificates (if available), and other supporting documents as specified by the scheme guidelines.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who oversees the implementation and monitoring of the CWSN scheme?
content.text: The implementation and monitoring of the scheme are overseen by relevant educational authorities or government bodies responsible for special education and disability support.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are there specific guidelines for utilizing the grants provided under the scheme?
content.text: Yes, grants provided under the scheme are to be used strictly for activities specified in the guidelines, aimed at improving the educational and developmental outcomes of CWSN.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where can I get more information about the CWSN scheme?
content.text: For more information, applicants can contact the designated educational authorities or visit official government websites providing details about the CWSN scheme.

title: Compensation for Livestock Loss Caused by Wild Animals
topic: Compensation for Livestock Loss Caused by Wild Animals

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to provide financial relief to livestock owners for losses due to wild animal attacks.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The “Compensation for Livestock Loss Caused by Wild Animals” scheme is launched by the Forest Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh. The scheme aims to provide financial relief to individuals whose livestock is injured or killed by wild animals. The scheme applies to both urban and rural areas and covers a variety of livestock including cows, buffaloes, camels, goats, sheep, donkeys, mules, and pigs.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
**Compensation Amounts:**

- Dairy animals (cow, buffalo, camel): ₹30,000
- Sheep/Goat: ₹3,000
- Non-dairy animals (bull, buffalo, camel, horse): ₹25,000
- Young animals (calf of cow/buffalo/horse/camel), Donkey/Mule: ₹16,000
- Pig: ₹3,000

**Injury Compensation:**

- 50% of the amount payable for injury under revenue circular provisions.

**Mode of Disbursement:**

- Compensation is paid directly to the beneficiary via e-payment.

**Expected Time of Disbursement:**

- Compensation is provided within 30 working days of claim verification.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Madhya Pradesh.
1. The applicant should be the owner of the livestock injured or killed by wild animals.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
**Registration**
**Step 1:** Visit the Official Website of "[Madhya Pradesh Lok Sewa Guarantee Portal](https://mpedistrict.gov.in/MPL/CitizenRegistration.aspx)".
**Step 2:** On the homepage, click "Citizen Registration". You will be taken to the registration page.
**Step 3:** On the registration page, fill in all the mandatory fields (name, mobile number, gender, and captcha) and click "Verify Mobile Number."
**Step 4:** A verification code will be sent to your mobile number. Enter the code and complete the registration.
**Application**
**Step 1:** Visit the Official Website of "Madhya Pradesh Lok Sewa Guarantee Portal".
**Step 2:** Enter your User ID, Password, and Captcha Code, and click "Login."
**Step 3:** Click on "Apply Online Service" and select the scheme you intend to apply for.
**Step 4:** In the scheme's online application form, fill in all the mandatory fields and upload all the mandatory documents in the specified format and size (fields marked with * are mandatory), and click "Submit".
**Note 1:** The incident must be reported within 48 hours to the nearest Forest Range Officer, either verbally or in writing.
**Note 2:** The dead or injured animal should remain at the site for verification by the forest authority.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
> **Application Process:**

**Step 1:** The interested applicant should visit (during office hours) the nearest Forest Range Office and request a hard copy of the prescribed format of the application form from the staff exclusively entrusted to issue and collect filled-in applications.
**Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
**Step 3:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the Forest Range Office.
**Step 4:** Request a receipt or acknowledgment from the Forest Range Office to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).
> **Post-Application Process:**

**Step 1:** The Forest Range Officer verifies the incident and prepares a recommendation.
**Step 2:** Approved claims are processed for e-payment to the beneficiary within 30 working days.
> **Appeal Process:** If the application is rejected

**Step 1:** First appeal can be made within 30 days to the Divisional/Assistant Conservator of Forests.
**Step 2:** Second appeal within 60 days to the Conservator of Forests.
**Note 1:** The incident must be reported within 48 hours to the nearest Forest Range Officer, either verbally or in writing.
**Note 2:** The dead or injured animal should remain at the site for verification by the forest authority.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Identity Proof
- Resident Proof
- Bank Account Details
- Death Certificate
- Treatment Bills
- Other Necessary Documents, if required

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible to apply?
content.text: Any livestock owner in Madhya Pradesh whose animals are killed or injured by wild animals.

content.section_title: FAQ: Which animals are covered?
content.text: Cow, buffalo, camel, bull, horse, sheep, goat, donkey, mule, and pig.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the compensation for injury to livestock?
content.text: 50% of the applicable compensation amount under revenue circular provisions.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the application fee?
content.text: The application is free of cost.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where should the application be submitted?
content.text: At the nearest Forest Range Office or online at mpedistrict.gov.in

content.section_title: FAQ: Within what time should the incident be reported?
content.text: The incident be reported within 48 hours of occurrence.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the expected time for payment?
content.text: Payment within 30 working days of verification.

content.section_title: FAQ: How is the compensation disbursed?
content.text: Directly to the beneficiary via e-payment.

content.section_title: FAQ: What if my application is rejected?
content.text: A first appeal can be filed within 30 days, and a second appeal within 60 days.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are urban livestock owners eligible?
content.text: Yes, both urban and rural livestock owners are eligible.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can I move the dead animal before verification?
content.text: No, the dead animal must remain at the site for verification by the Forest Officer.

title: Dental Care Scheme- Haryana Labour Welfare Board
topic: Dental Care Scheme- Haryana Labour Welfare Board

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: Under this Scheme, workers & their dependents are entitled to receive financial assistance of upto ₹4,000/- or the actual expenditure, whichever is less, for dental care. Furthermore, assistance of upto ₹10,000/- or the actual expenditure, whichever is less, is provided for fixation of full denture.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The scheme “Dental Care Scheme” is implemented by the Haryana Labour Welfare Board (HLWB), Labour Department, Government of Haryana. Under this scheme, financial assistance of ₹4,000/- or the actual cost of dental care, whichever is less, is provided for dental care to the workers employed in the industrial and commercial establishments in the state of Haryana. Furthermore, financial assistance of ₹10,000/- or the actual cost of the jaw, whichever is less, is provided to the workers for getting full denture fitted.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
1. Under this scheme, workers and their dependents are entitled to receive financial assistance of up to ₹4,000/- or the actual expenditure, whichever is less, for dental care.
1. Financial assistance of up to ₹10,000/- or the actual amount of expenditure, whichever is less, is provided for fixation of full dentures to workers and their dependents.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant should be a worker employed in an industrial or commercial establishment in Haryana.
1. The worker should be registered with the Haryana Labour Welfare Board.
1. The monthly income/salary of the worker should not exceed ₹25,000/-.
1. The service period of the worker is fixed for one year.
1. The application should be submitted within three months of the date of the dentist's prescription or bill for dental care or jaw implants.
1. The beneficiary should not have availed of the benefit of this scheme earlier.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
> **Registration of a Worker Under the Haryana Labour Welfare Board:**

**Step 01:** The applicant should visit the Official Website of the Labour Department Haryana. [https://hrylabour.gov.in/](https://hrylabour.gov.in/)
**Step 02**: On the home page, click on the “[Welfare Board Beneficiary Login](https://hrylabour.gov.in/login/welfare)”, a new window page will be opened. If you don't have login details then click on “Click Here” to register.
**Step 03:** Applicant has to read all the instructions and then “Tick” the acknowledgment, then click on the “Submit” button.
**Step 04:** Verification using Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP)- Family ID:
a) Forget/not having Family ID: If you do not have PPP ID or forget the PPP ID, then you have to select that radio button and you are redirected to the [PPP Portal](https://meraparivar.haryana.gov.in/) for getting their PPP ID.
b) Having a Family ID: If you have a family ID, then only you can move to the next step for registration. To proceed further, follow the given steps:
• Select the “I have family ID” radio button.
• Enter Parivar Pehchan Patra number - Family ID.
• Click on “Fetch Family Details” which displays the family members registered under the given Family ID.
• Select a family member for registration.
• Enter the OTP that is being sent to the selected family member for verification.
• Click on “Click to Verify”
**Step 05:** A new page will be opened with basis information details of the applicant. Applicant need to verify all the pre-filled details.
**Step 06:** After verification, the applicant has to fill all the details and red* marked fields are mandatory. Applicant has to upload his/her photo and then click on the ‘Submit’ button to submit the application.
**Step 07:** Successfully Registered page will appear like below image and SMS/ email will be send to applicant.
**Step 08:** Login into the account: After the final submission of the registration form, applicant can login into his/her account using their username and password.
**Note:** An applicant can also apply for registration, if he/she doesn’t have family ID, through their Aadhaar Number.
> **Apply for scheme benefits:**

**Step 01:** Visit the [Official Website](https://hrylabour.gov.in/) of the Labour Department Haryana.
**Step 02:** On the home page, click on "[Welfare Board Beneficiary Login](https://hrylabour.gov.in/login/welfare)".
**Step 03:** Enter your Username, Password, Captcha, and click on the submit button.
**Step 04:** In the opened window, click on "Schemes", and a list of schemes will appear on the screen.
**Step 05:** Now, select the scheme and read its details.
**Step 06:** Fill in all the required details in the online application form and submit it.
> **Application Tracking:**

Workers can track their application status online through the official website of the Labour Department Haryana. Relevant updates will be sent to the registered mobile number.

> **Apply for scheme benefits through the Antyodaya-SARAL Portal:**

**Registration Process on Antyodaya-SARAL Portal:**
**Step 01:** To avail of the benefits of the scheme, the applicant needs to visit Official Portal -Antyodaya-SARAL Portal: [https://saralharyana.gov.in/](https://saralharyana.gov.in/)
**Step 02:** If the applicant is not registered on the portal, then he/she gets registered on the portal.
**Step 03:** For registration, click on “New User” under ‘Sign In Here’ and fill in all the mandatory details i.e. Full Name, Email ID, Mobile Number, Password & State. Click on ‘Submit’.
**Step 04:** Verify your mobile number and email ID through the received OTP and complete the registration process.
> **Login to Apply for the Scheme:**

**Step 01:** Now, to avail the benefits of the scheme, visit the Official Portal -[Antyodaya-SARAL Portal](https://saralharyana.gov.in/) and login through the email ID that is used while registering.
**Step 02:** Enter Password & Captcha and click on ‘Login’.
**Step 03:** After successful login, click on ‘Apply for Services’ and then ‘View all Available Services’
**Step 04:** Now, search for the ‘Dental Care Scheme- Haryana Labour Welfare Board’ and click on the scheme to proceed to fill out the application form.
**Step 05:** Enter Parivar Pehchan Patra number - Family ID and Click on “Click here to Fetch Family Data” which displays the family members registered under the given Family ID.
**Step 06:** Select the beneficiary/applicant name and enter the OTP that is being sent to the selected family member for verification. Click on “Click to Verify”
**Step 07:** Fill in all the mandatory details and upload all the mandatory documents.
**Step 08:** Preview the application form and click on ‘Submit’.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
Documents required for registration under the Haryana Labour Welfare Board:- Aadhaar Card
- Passport-size photograph
- Parivar Pehchan Patra (Family ID)
- Bank account details
- Proof of residence
- Ration Card
- Caste Certificate (If applicable)
- Proof of age
- Disability certificate (If applicable)
- Any other documents as required
Documents required for Scheme Benefits:- ID proof of the worker issued by the organization
- Employer Certificate
- Employee Salary Slip (Previous Month)
- Affidavit/Undertaking (Self)
- Dentist's prescription and medicine purchase bill
- Ration Card/ESI Card/ Parivar Pehchan Patra (Family ID)
- Bank account details
- Any other documents as required
Note: For a full jaw implant, the dentist's prescription and expense bill from the dentist will have to be clearly written and the bill will have to be signed and stamped by the dentist and uploaded.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the Dental Care Scheme?
content.text: The scheme has been initiated by the Haryana Labour Welfare Board to provide financial assistance for dental care to workers employed in industrial and commercial establishments in the state.

content.section_title: FAQ: What kind of financial assistance does the scheme offer?
content.text: The scheme provides financial assistance of up to [?]4,000/- or the actual cost of dental care, whichever is less, and up to [?]10,000/- or the actual cost of full denture fitting, whichever is less.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible to benefit from this scheme?
content.text: Workers employed in industrial or commercial establishments in Haryana who are registered with the Haryana Labour Welfare Board, with a monthly income not exceeding [?]25,000, and who have not previously availed of the scheme are eligible.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the time frame for applying for assistance under this scheme?
content.text: Applications should be submitted within three months from the date of the dentist's prescription or bill for dental care or jaw implants.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any specific tenure of service required to qualify for this scheme?
content.text: Yes, the worker should have a fixed service period of at least one year.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can a worker register with the Haryana Labour Welfare Board?
content.text: Workers can register through the official website of the Labour Department Haryana. https://hrylabour.gov.in/

content.section_title: FAQ: Where can I apply for the benefits of this scheme?
content.text: You can apply for the scheme benefits through the Antyodaya-SARAL Portal, accessible at https://saralharyana.gov.in/.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can family members of the worker also avail of these benefits?
content.text: Yes, dependents of the worker are also entitled to receive financial assistance under this scheme.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme has been implemented to ensure that construction workers and their families get primary medical treatment at their doorsteps, prevent serious diseases and maintain their health at places including construction sites, labour colonies and Kadianaka.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The “Dhanvantari Arogya Rath Yojana” was launched by the Gujarat Building and Other Construction Worker’s Welfare Board (GBOCWWB), Labour, Skill Development & Employment Department, Government of Gujarat. The scheme has been implemented to ensure that construction workers and their families get primary medical treatment at their doorsteps, prevent serious diseases, and maintain their health at places including construction sites, labour colonies, and Kadianaka. The scheme is implemented by EMRI Green Health Services Gujarat (GVK-108) through making a tripartite MoU with the Health Department and EMRI Green Health Services Gujarat.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: - Under this scheme, treatment of primary injuries including common diseases like fever, diarrhea, vomiting, skin diseases and laboratory tests like urine, blood, blood sugar, malaria, and various diagnoses including primary care of women and children of their family and primary care of pregnant women during pregnancy done on the spot. Along with this, they are also given free medicines on the spot.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text: - The benefits of this scheme can be availed by construction workers registered with the Gujarat Building and Other Construction Worker’s Welfare Board and their family members.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
•	First aid is provided by the Dhanvantari Arogya Rath at Kadianaka & construction sites.
•	The registered construction workers and their family members can avail the benefits by visiting there.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text: - e-Nirman Card

content.section_title: FAQ: Who implements the scheme?
content.text: The scheme is implemented by EMRI Green Health Services Gujarat (GVK-108) through a tripartite MoU with the Health Department and EMRI Green Health Services Gujarat.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the objectives of the scheme?
content.text: The scheme aims to ensure that construction workers and their families receive primary medical treatment, prevent serious diseases, and maintain their health at various locations including construction sites, labour colonies, and Kadianaka.

content.section_title: FAQ: What benefits are provided under the scheme?
content.text: Treatment for primary injuries, common diseases like fever, diarrheas, vomiting, skin diseases, laboratory tests such as urine, blood, blood sugar, malaria, primary care for women and children, and primary care for pregnant women during pregnancy are provided on the spot. Free medicines are also given.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible for the scheme?
content.text: Construction workers registered with the Gujarat Building and Other Construction Worker's Welfare Board and their family members are eligible for the scheme benefits.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can construction workers and their families avail of the benefits?
content.text: They can avail of the benefits by visiting the Dhanvantari Arogya Rath stationed at Kadianaka and construction sites.

content.section_title: FAQ: What documents are required to avail of the benefits?
content.text: E-Nirman Card

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any cost associated with availing the benefits?
content.text: No, the medical treatment and medicines provided under the scheme are free of cost for registered construction workers and their families.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can non-registered construction workers avail of the scheme benefits?
content.text: No, only construction workers registered with the welfare board are eligible for the benefits of the Dhanvantari Arogya Rath Yojana.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme "Disability Pension," introduced by the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (HPBOCWWB), Government of Himachal Pradesh, provides a monthly pension of ₹500/- to registered beneficiaries in case of disability caused by accidents or diseases.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The scheme "Disability Pension," introduced by the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (HPBOCWWB), Government of Himachal Pradesh, provides a monthly pension of ₹500/- to registered beneficiaries in case of disability caused by accidents or diseases. Additionally, a lump sum of ₹50,000/- is granted if the disability is more than 50%., and ₹25,000/- is granted if the disability is more than 50%.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
- A pension of ₹500/- month.
- Additional ₹50,000/- in case the disability is more than 50%.
- Additional ₹25,000/- in case the disability is less than 50%.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
***For Registration***

1. The applicant should be a resident of Himachal Pradesh.
1. The applicant should be a Building or Construction Worker.
1. The age of the applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
1. The applicant should have worked in building or construction activities for at least 90 days in the preceding 12 months.

_*The registration should be renewed annually._
**For Application**

1. The worker should be engaged in building or construction work.
1. The worker should be registered under the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (HPBOCWWB).
1. The worker should have an active membership with the Board.
1. The worker should be a Person with Disability (PwD).
1. The disability should have been caused due to an accident or an illness.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
**Registration**
**Step 1:** **** Visit the [official website](https://bocw.hp.nic.in/index.aspx) of the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
**Step 2:** **** In the "Menu Ribbon", click "Worker Services > Registration > [New](https://bocw.hp.nic.in/PublicPages/AddBeneficiary.aspx)".
**Step 3:** **** Navigate to the registration form. In the form, fill in all the mandatory fields (marked with an asterisk *). Carefully review all the information provided. Make any necessary corrections.
**Step 5:** Click "Save" to submit your application for registration.
*The [Registration Status](https://bocw.hp.nic.in/PublicPages/checkstatus.aspx) can be checked by providing your Beneficiary ID and your Date of Birth.
**Application**
**Step 1:** **** Visit the [official website](https://bocw.hp.nic.in/index.aspx) of the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
**Step 2:** **** In the "Menu Ribbon", click "Worker Services > [Apply for Benefits](https://bocw.hp.nic.in/PublicPages/ApplyForSchemes.aspx)". On the next screen, provide your Beneficiary ID, and Date Of Birth, and click "Search". A list of schemes will be displayed. Click on the scheme you intend to apply for. You will be taken to the online application form of the scheme.
**Step 3:** **** In the form, fill in all the mandatory fields and upload all the mandatory documents in the specified format and size. Carefully review all the information provided and the documents uploaded. Make any necessary corrections.
**Step 4:** Finally, click "Submit" to complete the process.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Registration**
**Step 1:** Take the print of the [prescribed format](https://bocw.hp.nic.in/pdf/formno27_A1b.pdf) of the registration form.
**Step 2:** **** In the form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste a passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attested, if required).
**Step 3:** **** Submit the duly filled and signed registration form to the Labour Officer.
_* A one-time registration fee must be paid at the office, and it will be valid for three years._
_* After three years, the worker must visit the office to pay a renewal fee and submit proof of 90 days of work each year to maintain eligibility for benefits._
**Application**
**Step 1:** Take the print of the [prescribed format](https://bocw.hp.nic.in/pdf/pension3539_A1b.pdf) of the application form.
**Step 2:** **** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste a passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attested, if required).
**Step 3:** **** Submit the duly filled and signed application form to the Labour Officer.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Identity Proof i.e. Aadhaar Card, Voter Card
- Bank Account Details/ Copy of Bank Passbook
- Age Proof
- Labour Card
- Disability Certificate
- Medical Bill, countersigned by the Doctor (if applicable)

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the Disability Pension Scheme?
content.text: The scheme provides financial assistance to registered construction workers who become disabled due to an accident or illness.

content.section_title: FAQ: How much monthly pension is provided under the scheme?
content.text: A monthly pension of [?]500 is provided to eligible disabled workers.

content.section_title: FAQ: What financial assistance is given for permanent disability?
content.text:
[?]50,000 for more than 50% disability.
[?]25,000 for less than 50% disability.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can a worker apply for both the lump sum and pension?
content.text: Yes, if eligible, a worker can receive both the one-time financial assistance and the monthly pension.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can a worker register for HPBOCWWB?
content.text: Workers can register by visiting the Labour Welfare Office or through self-registration on the official portal.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the age limit for registration with HPBOCWWB?
content.text: Workers must be between 18 and 60 years old.

content.section_title: FAQ: How much work experience is needed for HPBOCWWB registration?
content.text: The worker must have completed at least 90 days of construction work.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can a worker apply if they have a pre-existing disability?
content.text: No, the scheme covers only disabilities caused by accidents or illnesses after registration.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can a worker apply online for disability benefits?
content.text: Yes, applications can be submitted online through the HPBOCWWB portal.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can a retired worker apply for the scheme?
content.text: No, only active registered workers are eligible.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where can workers get more information?
content.text: Workers can visit the nearest Labour Welfare Office or the HPBOCWWB official website.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The "Disability Pension and Ex-Gratia'' by the BOCW Welfare Board, Department of Labour, Karnataka, is a welfare scheme that provides a monthly pension to beneficiaries partially disabled due to worksite accidents or diseases, with an additional ex-gratia payment based on disability percentage.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The "Disability Pension and Ex-Gratia'' by the BOCW Welfare Board, Department of Labour, Karnataka, is a welfare scheme for the registered workers of the board. Under this Scheme, financial support shall be provided to registered construction workers in the form of pension who are partially disabled due to accidents or diseases incurred at their worksite. Beneficiaries may receive an ex-gratia payment depending on the extent of their disability. The pension is discontinued upon the beneficiary reaching sixty (60) years of age.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: - **Monthly Disability Pension:** ₹2,000/-.  - **Additional Ex-gratia Payment:** ₹2,00,000/- based on the percentage of disability.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
> ***For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:***

- The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker (Unorganised Worker).
- The applicant should be a minimum of 18 years of age.
- The applicant should have engaged in building and construction work for at least 90 days in a given year.

> **For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:**

- The applicant should be a registered construction worker Under the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of Karnataka.
- The applicant should be partially disabled due to worksite accidents or diseases.
- The applicant should not have availed “Accident Benefits” for the same disability.
- The applicant must be below sixty (60) years of age.

**Note:** The Disability Pension granted shall be discontinued once the beneficiary attains the age of sixty (60) years.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
> ***Registration Process as a Building/ Construction Worker:***

**Step 1:** Visit the official [Website ](https://kbocwwb.karnataka.gov.in/login)of Karnataka Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (KBOCWWB) and click on “Register”.
**Step 2:** Select “Register as New Construction Worker” then enter your mobile number as per the Aadhaar Registered and verify with the received OTP.
**Step 3:** From the dashboard, click on “Registration”.
**Step 4:** Fill in all the mandatory details and upload the required documents. Click on “Final Submit” to complete the registration process.
> **Application Process of the Welfare Scheme:**

**Step 1:** Visit the official [Website ](https://kbocwwb.karnataka.gov.in/login)of Karnataka Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (KBOCWWB) and click on “Login”.
***Step 2:*** Enter your registered mobile number and the received OTP.
**Step 3:** From the dashboard, click on “Schemes” (After the user Registration is approved, the user can access the schemes page).
**Step 4:** Select the concerned scheme. Fill in all the mandatory details and upload the required documents.
**Step 5:**  Review the details and check the declaration box. Now click on “Submit” to complete the application process.
> **Renewal Process:**

**Step 1:** Visit the official [Website ](https://kbocwwb.karnataka.gov.in/login)of Karnataka Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (KBOCWWB) and click on “Login”.
Step 2: Enter your registered mobile number and the received OTP.
**Step 3:** From the dashboard, click on “Renewal”. Fill in mandatory details and upload the required documents.
**Step 4:** Review the details and click on “Submit” to complete the renewal process.
> **Note:**

- **Renewal:** Every construction worker must renew their registration with the Karnataka Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board every 3 years. If not renewed within this period, a 1-year grace period is granted. After that, the registration becomes invalid.  - **Timeline:** Application for this assistance can be submitted within 6 months from the date of issue of disability ID card by the competent authority.
> **Helpdesk:**

Karnataka Building And Other Construction Workers Welfare Board,
**Address:** Karmika Bhavan, Dairy Circle, Bannerghatta Road, Bangalore-29.
**Office No:** 080-29753078
**E-mail:** labour.commissioner42@gmail.com

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:- Employment Certificate/90 Days Work Certificate (Form V(A)/V(B)/V(C)/V(D)).
- Aadhar Card (Self-Attested).
- Ration Card(non-mandatory).
- Age Proof (Voter ID Card/Aadhar Card).
For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:- Beneficiary/original Identity card (Issued by the board).
- Photocopy of beneficiary's bank passbook.
- Living Certificate (every year).
- Ration Card.
- Employer Certificate.
- Medical Report.
- ID card issued by the department for the empowerment of differently abled and Senior citizens.
- Photocopy of the disabled beneficiary.
For the Renewal Process:- Employment Certificate/90 Days Work Certificate (Form V(A)/V(B)/V(C)/V(D)).
Note: In case the death of Beneficiary Nominee should provide a death certificate to the Board.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can register as a beneficiary under the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of Karnataka?
content.text: Any construction worker whose age is minimum 18 years of age and has worked in any construction work for a minimum of 90 days in a year, are eligible to register as a beneficiary under the Board.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there a minimum service requirement to register as a beneficiary?
content.text: Yes, the building worker must have completed 90 days of service as a building worker in the preceding 12 months.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can apply for the welfare scheme?
content.text: Any registered worker of the Board can apply for the welfare scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the mandatory documents for the registration?
content.text: The documents required for registration are: Employment Certificate (Form V(A)/V(B)/V(C)/V(D)), Aadhar Card (Self-Attested), Ration Card(non-mandatory), Age Proof (one of them Example: Voter ID Card, Aadhar Card).

content.section_title: FAQ: How often must a construction worker renew their registration with the Karnataka Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board?
content.text: A construction worker must renew their registration every 3 years.

content.section_title: FAQ: What happens if a worker doesn't renew their registration within the 3-year period?
content.text: If a worker doesn't renew their registration within the 3-year period, they are granted a 1-year grace period to complete the renewal.

content.section_title: FAQ: What happens if a worker doesn't renew their registration during the grace period?
content.text: If the worker fails to renew their registration during the 1-year grace period, their registration will become invalid.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any application fee required for the registration under the Karnataka Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board?
content.text: No, as per the guidelines, there is no fee required for the registration.

content.section_title: FAQ: How to enroll as a beneficiary under the Karnataka Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board?
content.text: Applicants can visit the official Website of Karnataka Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (KBOCWWB) to enroll themselves as a beneficiary under the board: https://kbocwwb.karnataka.gov.in/login

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible for the “Disability Pension and Ex-Gratia” scheme?
content.text: Applicants who are partially disabled due to accidents or diseases incurred at their worksite, are eligible for this scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the amount provided under this scheme?
content.text: The "Disability Pension and Ex-Gratia" Scheme provides a monthly pension of [?]2,000/- and an additional ex-gratia payment of up to [?]2,00,000/- based on the percentage of disability.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can a beneficiary receive “Disability Pension and Ex-Gratia” if they have already availed “Accident Benefits”?
content.text: No, if a beneficiary has already availed Accident Benefits for the same disability, they are not eligible for the "Disability Pension and Ex-Gratia".

content.section_title: FAQ: How is the pension paid to beneficiaries?
content.text: The pension is paid through the beneficiary's bank account or any other payment method authorized by the Board.

content.section_title: FAQ: What happens to the pension when a beneficiary turns sixty (60) years?
content.text: The Disability Pension is discontinued once the beneficiary reaches the age of sixty (60) years.

content.section_title: FAQ: What should be done in the event of the beneficiary’s death?
content.text: In the event of a beneficiary's death, the bank or legal dependent must inform the Board or authorized Officer along with the death certificate to close the pension account.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the process if an application for Disability Pension is rejected?
content.text: If an application for Disability Pension is rejected, the applicant will be informed and will have the opportunity to be heard before a final decision is made.

content.section_title: FAQ: How to apply for the assistance under this scheme?
content.text: Applicants should visit the official Website of Karnataka Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (JKBOCWWB) to avail the assistance under this scheme: https://kbocwwb.karnataka.gov.in/login

title: Distress Relief Fund For The Differently Abled (Medical Treatment)
topic: Distress Relief Fund For The Differently Abled (Medical Treatment)

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme “Distress Relief Fund for the Differently Abled (Medical Treatment)” was launched by the Department of Social Justice, Government of Kerala.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
The scheme “Distress Relief Fund for the Differently Abled (Medical Treatment)” was launched by the Department of Social Justice, Government of Kerala. As per [G.O.(MS) 4/95 SWD](http://sjd.kerala.gov.in/DOCUMENTS/Order_new/GOs/11544.pdf) dated 24-01-1995, the Government of Kerala sanctioned the Distress Relief Fund for disabled persons. An amount of Rupees One crore has been deposited in Treasury as a corpus fund. The interest accrued from the fund is being utilized for giving financial assistance to disabled persons for the following purposes:
1.	For medical treatment, including surgery.
2.	To persons who become handicapped due to accidents.
3.	For any other purpose which is not covered by the existing schemes for the handicapped.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: 1. Under the scheme, financial assistance of ₹5,000/- per person in a year will be given to the disabled person or who has become handicapped for their medical treatment, including surgery.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant should be a native and resident of Kerala State.
1. The disabled person or who has become handicapped due to an accident is eligible to apply under the scheme for their medical treatment, including surgery.
1. Those differently-abled persons whose annual income does not exceed (₹20,000/- in Rural areas and ₹22,375/- in Urban areas) shall be eligible for financial assistance.
1. Physically handicapped persons shall include the following disabilities/categories:

a. Blind
b. Deaf and Dumb
c. Orthopedically Handicapped
d. Mentally Challenged
e. Persons in the Below Poverty Line (BPL) category having 40 % disability or more.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
**Registration Process:**
**Step 01**: To avail of the benefits of the scheme, the applicant needs to visit [SUNEETHI- Online Service Application Portal](https://suneethi.sjd.kerala.gov.in)
**Step 02:** On the home page, click on “One time Registration”
**Step 03:** Fill in the details like Name, Email ID & Mobile Number and click on ‘Register’.
**Step 04:** The applicant needs to enter the verification code received on the mobile number and click on ‘Verify’.
**Step 05:** Now, a form will open to create the new profile, fill in all the mandatory details and click on ‘Register’.
**Login/Application Process:**
**Step 01:** Once the registration process is completed, the applicant should visit [SUNEETHI- Online Service Application Portal](https://suneethi.sjd.kerala.gov.in) and click on “Citizen Login”.
**Step 02:** Enter the user name i.e. Mobile Number and Password and click on “Sign in”.
**Step 03:** Now, the applicant needs to update all their details i.e. Personal Information, Contact Details, Identity Cards & Bank Details by clicking on the 'Edit' button.
**Step 04:** Once the applicant updated their profile successfully, the schemes will appear on the left side of the screen.
**Step 05:** Select the scheme and click on the “Apply Now” button.
**Step 06:** Fill in all the mandatory details in the application form and click on the ‘Submit’ button.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Step 01:** To avail of the benefits of the scheme, the applicant needs to visit the [official website](http://sjd.kerala.gov.in/scheme-info.php?scheme_id=NzRzVjh1cVIjdnk=) of the Social Justice Department, Government of Kerala to download the [application form](http://sjd.kerala.gov.in/DOCUMENTS/Downloadables/Application%20Forms/11543.pdf).
**Step 02:** The applicants need to fill out the application form completely.
**Step 03:** The duly filled application forms along with the documents prescribed can be submitted to the concerned District Social Justice Officer (DSJO).
**Step 04:** The DSJO shall forward the applications to the Secretary to Government- Social Justice Department in the Secretariat with recommendations duly certifying the eligibility after a personal inquiry in the individual cases with a copy to the Director of Social Justice.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Identity proof i.e. Aadhaar Card, Voter ID Card
- Mobile number
- Email id
- Passport size photo
- Doctor's certificate that treatment is necessary
- Copy of Income Certificate (Original)
- Bank account details- Copy of bank passbook
- Other documents, if any

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the “Distress Relief Fund for the Differently Abled (Medical Treatment)” Scheme?
content.text: Under the scheme, the Government of Kerala has sanctioned the Distress Relief Fund for disabled persons. An amount of Rupees One crore has been deposited in Treasury as a corpus fund and interest accrued from the fund is being utilized for giving financial assistance to disabled persons.

content.section_title: FAQ: Which department has launched this scheme?
content.text: Social Justice Department, Government of Kerala

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible to get the benefits under the scheme?
content.text: The disabled person or who has become handicapped due to an accident is eligible to apply under the scheme for their medical treatment, including surgery.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is this scheme only for a resident of Kerala State?
content.text: Yes, The applicant should be a native and resident of Kerala State.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any criterion for annual family income?
content.text: Yes, the annual income of the applicant should not exceed ([?]20,000/- in Rural areas and [?]22,375/- in Urban areas).

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the benefit of the scheme?
content.text: Under the scheme, the maximum amount of financial assistance per person a year has been fixed at [?]5,000/- for medical treatment, including surgery.

content.section_title: FAQ: For what purpose will financial assistance be given to disabled persons?
content.text: The financial assistance shall be given to disabled persons for the purposes such as (a) For medical treatment, including surgery. (b) To persons who become handicapped due to accidents (c) For any other purpose which is not covered by the existing schemes for the handicapped.

content.section_title: FAQ: Which type of disabilities/categories of disabled persons shall be included under the scheme?
content.text:
The disabled persons shall include the following disabilities/categories:
a. Blind
b. Deaf and Dumb
c. Orthopedically Handicapped
d. Mentally Challenged

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the criterion for disabled persons belonging to the BPL category?
content.text: Disabled persons in the Below Poverty Line (BPL) category having 40 % disability or more are eligible to apply under the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can an applicant apply under the scheme?
content.text: To avail of the benefits of the scheme, the applicant should visit the Official Portal- SUNEETHI- Online Service Application Portal.  https://suneethi.sjd.kerala.gov.in

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to provide the general public with round-the-clock (24×7×365) access to reliable health information and medical advice through the toll-free number 104.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
