content.text: The scheme “Integrated Toll Free 104 Helpline” is an initiative under the National Health Mission (NHM), implemented by the Department of Medical Health and Family Welfare, Government of Uttarakhand. It aims to provide the general public with round-the-clock (24×7×365) access to reliable health information and medical advice through the toll-free number 104.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: Under the National Health Mission (NHM), the Integrated Toll-Free 104 Helpline is being operated 24×7×365day. Through this helpline, health-related advice and information can be obtained.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text: 1. The general public of Uttarakhand is eligible to avail the benefits.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text: The related information can be obtained by calling the helpline number 104.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text: No documents are required to avail the benefits.

content.section_title: FAQ: Under which mission is the helpline launched?
content.text: It is launched under the National Health Mission (NHM).

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the main objective of the 104 Helpline?
content.text: The main objective is to offer round-the-clock health information, medical guidance, and support to the general public.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can benefit?
content.text: The general public of Uttarakhand can avail the benefits of this service.

content.section_title: FAQ: What kind of information can be obtained through 104?
content.text: Health-related information & medical advice can be obtained.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is the helpline available 24 hours?
content.text: Yes, it is available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, 365 days a year.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: This health initiative aimed at improving maternal and infant health by increasing institutional deliveries, reducing infant and maternal mortality rates, and promoting immunization in Bihar.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: Launched as part of efforts towards women's empowerment, "Janani Bal Suraksha Yojana" has significantly improved health outcomes for pregnant women and nursing mothers in Bihar. The scheme, through the efforts of trained health workers like ASHA and MAMTA, has contributed to a remarkable rise in institutional deliveries and reductions in both infant and maternal mortality rates.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: - **Increased Institutional Deliveries:** The percentage of institutional deliveries has risen from 4% in 2006-07 to 47% now, ensuring safer childbirths.  - **Reduction in Infant Mortality:** Infant mortality rate has decreased from 61 per thousand births in 2005 to 35 per thousand births, contributing to healthier infants.  - **Higher Immunization Coverage:** Regular immunization rates have increased from 18.6% in 2005 to 84% in 2018-19, surpassing the national average.  - **Decrease in Maternal Mortality:** Maternal mortality rate has reduced from 312 in 2005 to 165 in 2018-19, promoting safer childbirth for mothers.  - **Improved Life Expectancy:** The average life expectancy of women in Bihar has increased from 61.6 years in 2006 to 68.5 years in 2016, enhancing overall women's health.  - **Empowerment of Women:** The scheme supports women’s health and also includes provisions for police recruitment, including 35% reservation for women in police positions.  - **Support for Marginalized Communities:** Additional benefits for women from Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes to ensure inclusivity and equality in access to healthcare.  - **Improved Healthcare Access:** Trained health workers like ASHA and MAMTA provide guidance, ensuring pregnant women and nursing mothers receive timely and appropriate care.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
- The applicant must be a pregnant woman or nursing mother residing in Bihar.
- The applicant should be willing to undergo institutional delivery and follow the health protocols set by the health department.
- The applicant should be a member of a marginalized community to receive additional benefits under the scheme.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Step 1:** Visit the nearest health center or hospital.
**Step 2:** Meet with an ASHA or MAMTA worker for guidance and support.
**Step 3:** Submit the necessary documents for verification.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Proof of pregnancy (e.g., ultrasound or medical certificate).
- Identification proof (Aadhaar card, voter ID, etc.).
- Proof of residence (e.g., ration card, voter ID).
- Medical documents from a registered healthcare provider.
- Any other documents (if required).

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the Janani Bal Suraksha Yojana?
content.text: The Janani Bal Suraksha Yojana is a health initiative designed to improve maternal and infant health in Bihar by promoting institutional deliveries and reducing mortality rates.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible for the scheme?
content.text: Pregnant women and nursing mothers residing in Bihar who are willing to undergo institutional delivery and follow the health protocols are eligible for the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the key benefits of this scheme?
content.text: The scheme offers benefits such as increased institutional deliveries, reduced infant and maternal mortality, improved immunization rates, and enhanced life expectancy for women in Bihar.

content.section_title: FAQ: How has the infant mortality rate improved due to this scheme?
content.text: The infant mortality rate has decreased from 61 per thousand births in 2005 to 35 per thousand births, reflecting the success of the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can one apply for the Janani Bal Suraksha Yojana?
content.text: Interested applicants can visit their nearest health center or hospital to apply for the scheme and receive guidance from ASHA or MAMTA workers.

content.section_title: FAQ: What documents are required to apply?
content.text: Applicants need to provide identification proof, proof of pregnancy, and proof of residence, among other necessary documents.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the current rate of institutional deliveries in Bihar under this scheme?
content.text: Institutional deliveries have increased to 47% in Bihar, a significant improvement from 4% in 2006-07.

content.section_title: FAQ: How does this scheme reduce maternal mortality?
content.text: The scheme contributes to reducing maternal mortality by promoting institutional deliveries and providing medical care through trained health workers, leading to better outcomes for mothers.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can a woman from any community apply for this scheme?
content.text: Yes, any pregnant woman or nursing mother in Bihar is eligible to apply. However, additional benefits may be available to women from marginalized communities.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the role of ASHA and MAMTA workers in the scheme?
content.text: ASHA and MAMTA workers provide guidance, support, and health services to pregnant women and nursing mothers to ensure safe deliveries and post-natal care.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to provide free and safe delivery and newborn care services to BPL families by offering free treatment to pregnant women in government and accredited private hospitals.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The scheme "Janani Sahayogi Yojana" is launched by the Department of Public Health and Medical Education, Government of Madhya Pradesh. It aims to provide safe motherhood and child healthcare services to women belonging to Below Poverty Line (BPL) families by offering free medical care, including normal delivery, cesarean section, abortion, and newborn care, in government and accredited private hospitals to ensure maternal and infant health.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
- Free delivery services (both normal and cesarean) for pregnant women belonging to BPL families.
- Free maternal and newborn healthcare, including antenatal, delivery, postnatal, and neonatal services.
- Free abortion and emergency obstetric care when medically required.
- Free medicines, diagnostics, and hospitalization at government or accredited private hospitals.
- Linkage with Janani Suraksha Yojana (JSY) for eligible women to receive additional incentives.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Madhya Pradesh.
1. The applicant must be a pregnant woman.
1. The applicant must belong to a Below Poverty Line (BPL) family.
1. The applicant must present a valid BPL Health Card such as Blue, Yellow, or Antyodaya Card.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text: - The applicant should visit a government or accredited private hospital participating in the Janani Sahayogi Yojana.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- BPL (Below Poverty Line) Card or Antyodaya, Blue, or Yellow Health Card
- Any Other Document if Required

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible for this scheme?
content.text: Pregnant women from Below Poverty Line (BPL) families residing in Madhya Pradesh.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is the scheme available in rural areas?
content.text: Yes, it covers both urban and rural regions.

content.section_title: FAQ: What healthcare services are provided?
content.text: Free delivery (normal/cesarean), abortion, diagnostics, medicines, and newborn care.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are private hospitals included?
content.text: Yes, only accredited private hospitals recognized under the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What documents are required?
content.text: BPL or Antyodaya Health Card, Aadhaar Card, and hospital registration proof.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any application fee?
content.text: No, all services are free.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can non-BPL families apply?
content.text: No, only BPL-registered families are eligible.

title: Janani Shishu Suraksha Karyakram Madhya Pradesh
topic: Janani Shishu Suraksha Karyakram Madhya Pradesh

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to provide free healthcare services to pregnant women and infants up to one year of age, including free treatment, medicines, diet, and transport facilities in government hospitals.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The "Janani Shishu Suraksha Karyakram" scheme is launched by the Department of Public Health and Medical Education, Government of Madhya Pradesh. The scheme aims to ensure free and cashless healthcare services to pregnant women and infants up to one year of age in government health institutions by providing free medicines, diagnostics, consumables, blood transfusions, diet, and transport services during pregnancy, delivery, and postnatal care.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
- Free and cashless delivery
- Free treatment for sick newborns and infants up to one year of age.
- Free medicines, consumables, and diagnostic services for both mothers and infants.
- Free provision of blood and blood transfusion services, if required.
- Free diet during hospitalization
- Free transport facility

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Madhya Pradesh.
1. The applicant must be a pregnant woman.
1. The applicant’s child (newborn or infant) should be up to one year of age to receive benefits.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text: - The beneficiary should visit the nearest government health institution (District Hospital, Community Health Center, or Primary Health Center) for antenatal or delivery services.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text: - Documents are required as per the department's guidelines for the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible under this scheme?
content.text: All pregnant women and infants up to one year of age availing services in government health institutions.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is the scheme available in rural areas?
content.text: Yes, it covers both urban and rural areas.

content.section_title: FAQ: What kind of healthcare services are provided?
content.text: Free delivery, medicines, diagnostics, diet, blood transfusion, and transport services.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are cesarean deliveries covered under this scheme?
content.text: Yes, cesarean deliveries are fully covered without any charge.

content.section_title: FAQ: Does the scheme cover newborn healthcare?
content.text: Yes, it covers treatment for sick newborns and infants up to one year of age.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any application fee or registration charge?
content.text: No, all services under this scheme are completely free.

title: Healthy Lifestyle for School Children through Ayurvidya-AYUSH
topic: Healthy Lifestyle for School Children through Ayurvidya-AYUSH

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to promote and develop a healthy lifestyle among school children through the principles and practices of AYUSH. Under this initiative, free AYUSH-based health services and medicines are provided to children registered in government schools.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The “Healthy Lifestyle for School Children through Ayurvidya-AYUSH” is implemented by the Ayush Department, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to promote and develop a healthy lifestyle among school children through the principles and practices of AYUSH.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: - Promoting a healthy lifestyle among school children through AYUSH and providing free distribution of medicines based on their specific health conditions. This facility is provided free of cost to the students.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text: 1. The beneficiary must be a student enrolled in a government school in Uttarakhand.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text: School children may contact the concerned authorities of their respective government schools to avail the benefits under this scheme.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text: Documents are to be submitted as per the directions of the respective schools.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible to benefit from this scheme?
content.text: All children enrolled in government schools across Uttarakhand are eligible to benefit from this scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What does the scheme provide to students?
content.text: The scheme provides free AYUSH-based health check-ups, medicines, and guidance on healthy lifestyle practices.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any cost involved for the students?
content.text: No, all the services and medicines under this scheme are provided completely free of cost.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where can students avail the benefits of this scheme?
content.text: Students can avail the benefits directly in their respective government schools.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are private school students eligible for the scheme?
content.text: No, the scheme is applicable only to students studying in government schools of Uttarakhand.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: A health scheme by Ministry of Health & Family Welfare for financially poor patients to defray a part of the expenditure on Hospitalization/treatment in Government Hospitals,for life threatening diseases covered under Rashtriya Arogya Nidhi (RAN), in cases where free medical facilities aren't there.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: A health scheme by Ministry of Health & Family Welfare for financially poor patients to defray a part of the expenditure on Hospitalization/treatment in Government Hospitals,for life threatening diseases covered under Rashtriya Arogya Nidhi (RAN), in cases where free medical facilities are not available. Only those having annual family income up to Rs.1.25,000/- and below are eligible for financial assistance from Health Minister’s Discretionary Grant (HMDG). Under this scheme, Financial assistance is provided towards treatment in Government hospitals including super specialty Government hospitals / institutes.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant must not be an employee of either Central or State Govt.
1. The applicant must have been Hospitalized / Undergoing Treatment in a Government Hospital (including super specialty Government hospitals / institutes).
1. The Total Annual Family Income of the applicant must not exceed ₹1,00,000/- per annum.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Application form in prescribed proforma duly signed by the treating doctor and countersigned by the Medical Superintendent of the Government hospital/institute.
- Quantum of financial assistance granted to the patient is- (a) Rs.75,000/-, if the estimated cost of treatment is up to Rs.1,25,000/-(b) Rs.1,00,000/- if the estimated cost of treatment is above Rs.1,25,000/- and up to Rs.1,75,000/- and (c) Rs.1,25,000/- if the estimated cost of treatment is above Rs.1,75,000/-.
- A Certificate in original from the BDO/ Tehsildar/Collector /SDM (quoting full name, designation, telephone number and complete official address of the certificate issuing authority) regarding occupation and monthly/yearly income of the applicant and his/her family members from all sources.
- Copy of the ration card.

content.section_title: FAQ: I am admitted in a government hospital and the expenditure on my treatment is expected to be beyond ₹50,000/-. What is the maximum amount of financial assistance I can expect through this grant?
content.text: Financial Assistance of [?]50,000/- if the estimated cost of treatment is up to [?]1,00,000/- .

content.section_title: FAQ: Is the assistance provided as a lump sum or on monthly basis?
content.text: The assistance is provided as per the requirement of the treatment and can be monthly or lumpsum.

content.section_title: FAQ: What would be the mode of payment of the financial assistance?
content.text: The assistance will either be paid in cash or paid online.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are there any exclusions to the eligible beneficiaries?
content.text:
Yes,  the following are not eligible to avail benefits under HMDG:
Prolonged treatment involving recurring expenditure is not permissible.
diseases of common nature where treatment is not expensive.
T.B. cases
Cases for treatment in private hospital.
Central as well as State Government employees.

content.section_title: FAQ: How do I know if a field in the application form is mandatory?
content.text: The mandatory fields have a red asterisk (*) mark at the end.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can I submit the application form online?
content.text: No, the application needs to submitted offline.

content.section_title: FAQ: What do I write in “Itemwise breakup of expenditure” field. Where do I get it from?
content.text: The Itemwise breakup of expenditure can be requested from the hospital staff.

content.section_title: FAQ: In the application, can I request for treatment in private hospital?
content.text: No, cases for treatment in private hospital are not entertained under this grant.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to provide comprehensive care, support, and treatment to patients suffering from Thalassemia and Hemophilia in Uttarakhand.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The scheme “Hemoglobinopathy” is an initiative under the National Health Mission (NHM) implemented by the Department of Medical Health and Family Welfare, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to provide comprehensive care, support, and treatment to patients suffering from Thalassemia and Hemophilia in Uttarakhand.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: Under this scheme, free blood filters, chelators, and blood are provided to patients suffering from Thalassemia. Free haemophilia factors (life-saving medicines) are provided to patients suffering from Haemophilia.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
- The applicant must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
- The applicant must be suffering from Thalassemia or Haemophilia.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
- Patients suffering from Thalassemia must get themselves registered by visiting the DEIC Centres established at Sub-District Hospital Roorkee, Base Hospital Almora, Base Hospital Nainital, and District Hospital Rudrapur (Udham Singh Nagar). For registration, patients are required to provide their Aadhaar card, mobile number, and a medical certificate confirming the disease issued by a qualified doctor. After registration, patients can avail of free blood filters, chelators, and blood transfusion facilities.
- Patients suffering from Haemophilia can obtain free haemophilia factors from their nearest government medical unit after registering with the Haemophilia Society by submitting a doctor’s diagnostic prescription and Aadhaar card. They can also complete their registration by contacting the 104 Helpline (toll-free) or directly through the Haemophilia Society.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Aadhaar card
- Mobile number
- Medical certificate confirming the disease issued by a qualified doctor

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible to benefit under this scheme?
content.text: All residents of Uttarakhand who are diagnosed with Thalassemia or Hemophilia are eligible to avail benefits under this scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What facilities are provided to Thalassemia patients?
content.text: Thalassemia patients receive free blood filters, chelators, and blood transfusions under this scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What facilities are provided to Hemophilia patients?
content.text: Hemophilia patients are provided with free haemophilia factors (life-saving medicines) from designated government medical units.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is registration mandatory to avail of the benefits?
content.text: Yes, registration is mandatory for both Thalassemia and Hemophilia patients to receive free treatment under the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where can Thalassemia patients register?
content.text:
Thalassemia patients can register at the DEIC Centres located in:

- Sub-District Hospital, Roorkee
- Base Hospital, Almora
- Base Hospital, Nainital
- District Hospital, Rudrapur (Udham Singh Nagar)

title: Hita-dhikaari Ke Samanye Athava Durghatana Me Mrityu Ya Ghayal Hone Ki Dasha Me Sahayata Yojana
topic: Hita-dhikaari Ke Samanye Athava Durghatana Me Mrityu Ya Ghayal Hone Ki Dasha Me Sahayata Yojana

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: By the labor department of Rajasthan started "Hita-Dhikaari Ke Samanye Athava Durghatana Me Mrityu Ya Ghayal Hone Ki Dasha Me Sahayata Yojana" for the construction workers.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The Beneficiary's Death or Injury Assistance Scheme, established by the Labor department of Rajasthan State, is a compassionate government program designed to offer a helping hand to families of beneficiaries who have experienced the loss of a loved one or the sudden hardship of an injury, regardless of whether the cause was natural or accidental. Recognizing the significant emotional and financial strain that such events can inflict, this initiative aims to provide a measure of financial security during these difficult times, allowing families to focus on healing and rebuilding their lives.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
1. In case of death due to accident : ₹5,00,000/-
1. In case of permanent total disability due to accident : ₹3,00,000/-

- Permanent total disability means disabling of two eyes or both hands or both legs due to accident.

1. In case of partial permanent disability due to accident : ₹1,00,000/-

- Permanent partial disability means disabling of one hand or one leg.

1. In case of serious injury in an accident : Up to ₹20,000/-

-  (Serious injury in an accident means the beneficiary being admitted as an in-patient in the hospital for at least 5 days. The determination of serious injury will be decided based on the certificate issued by the Medical Board .)

1. In case of minor injuries in an accident : Up to ₹5,000/-

- Minor injuries means hospitalization as an inpatient in a hospital for less than 5 days

1. In case of normal death of a construction worker : ₹75,000/-

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. Construction workers must be between 18 to 60 years of age.
1. Construction workers must be registered with the labor board.
1. Construction worker must be depositing their contributions regularly.

content.section_title: Exclusions
content.text:
- Death due to suicide or consumption of drugs or substances or
- In case of death or injury due to fighting with each other in violation of the law for committing a crime.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
> **Registration**

**Step-1:** Applicant have to visit the [official portal](https://sso.rajasthan.gov.in/signin).
**Step-2:** Click on the option “[Register](https://sso.rajasthan.gov.in/register)”.
**Step-3:** Then you will be redirected to the SSO registration page. The registration page will appear with the following options.

-   Citizen

**Step-4:** Choose the either one option from  the Jan Aadhaar Or Google to process further.

-  Jan Aadhaar : Enter the Jan Aadhaar number, click on the ‘Next’ button,

- Select your name, the name of the head of the family and all the other members and
- Click on the ‘Send OTP’ button. Enter the ‘OTP’ and
- Click on the ‘Verify OTP’ button to Complete the registration.

- Google : Enter the Gmail ID, click on the ‘Next’ button,

- Enter the password.
- A new link appear on screen, now click on new SSO link.
- SSO id will appear on screen, now create the password.
- Enter Mobile number, click on registration.

> **Apply**

**Step-1:** Applicant have to visit the [official portal](https://sso.rajasthan.gov.in/signin).
**Step-2:** After login, dashboard will open.
**Step-3:** Click on “LDMS” option.
**Step-4:** In LDMS, select "Welfare Schemes" from the side menu and select BOCW Welfare Board.
**Step-5:** Now click on "Apply for Scheme" on screen.
**Step-6:** In this new page, will get a list of names of all the schemes of Rajasthan Shramik Card, out of which click on the name of "Yojana"
**Step-7:** Fill out the all relevant detail.
**Step-8:** Submit.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Copy of discharge ticket from hospital in case of accident.
- Copy of mortuary report or FIR in case of accidental death.
- Death certificate issued by the registered authority in case of death.
- Certificate from the employer/employer of the place where the worker worked.
- Copy of beneficiary registration identity card or card.
- Copy of Bhamshah family card or Bhamshah nomination.
- Copy of Aadhar Card.
- Copy of first page of bank account passbook.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the objective of the scheme?
content.text: providing financial assistance in case of natural or accidental death or injury of the beneficiary by the Building Labor Welfare Department.

content.section_title: FAQ: What benefits are provided under the scheme?
content.text: Financial Assistance will be provided.

content.section_title: FAQ: May Other State persons also apply for this scheme?
content.text: No, the scheme is currently limited to Rajasthan.

content.section_title: FAQ: What amount of benefits is provided in cases of death?"
content.text:
1. In case of death due to accident : [?]5,00,000/-
2. In case of normal death of a construction worker : [?]75,000/-

content.section_title: FAQ: What amount of benefits is provided in case of permanent disability ?
content.text: In case of permanent total disability due to accident : [?]3,00,000/-

content.section_title: FAQ: What amount of benefits is provided in cases of partial permanent disability?
content.text: In case of partial permanent disability due to accident : [?]1,00,000/-

content.section_title: FAQ: What amount of benefits is provided In case of minor injuries?
content.text: In case of minor injuries in an accident : Up to [?]5,000/-

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the eligible age to get the scheme benefits?
content.text: Construction workers must be between 18 to 60 years of age.

content.section_title: FAQ: What documents are required ?
content.text:
1. Copy of discharge ticket from hospital in case of accident.
2. Copy of mortuary report or FIR in case of accidental death.
3. Death certificate issued by the registered authority in case of death.
4. Certificate from the employer/employer of the place where the worker worked.
5. Copy of beneficiary registration identity card or card.
6. Copy of Bhamshah family card or Bhamshah nomination.
7. Copy of Aadhar Card.
8. Copy of first page of bank account passbook.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is official portal to apply online ?
content.text:
Follow the below URL
Link : https://sso.rajasthan.gov.in/signin

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme "Home for Mentally Challenged Children" by the Department of Social Welfare, Government of Puducherry, aims to provide care for children facing mental challenges, i.e. low Intelligence Quotient (IQ). The scheme offers free boarding and lodging, uniform sets, daily living needs, etc.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The scheme "Home for Mentally Challenged Children" by the Department of Social Welfare, Government of Puducherry, aims to provide care for children facing mental challenges, i.e. low Intelligence Quotient (IQ). The scheme offers free boarding and lodging, uniform sets, daily living needs, and medical check-ups for the inmates. Additionally, it includes educational and recreational activities to enhance their overall well-being.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
- Free boarding and lodging for all the inmates.
- Free supply of 4 uniform sets to the inmates per year.
- Free supply of all daily living needs for the inmates.
- Free medical check-ups, including paediatric and psychiatric services, for all the inmates.
- Annual tours and local picnic trips to places of interest for the inmates.
- Annual Day for prize distribution to winners in sports events and cultural programmes.
- Non-formal education to assist and develop inmates in their day-to-day activities.
- Training in habits of discipline, cleanliness, good health, drawing, painting, yoga, and other extracurricular activities.

**NOTE:** Contact the authorities below in case of delay in disbursal of benefits:

- **Puducherry:** The Director, Directorate of Social Welfare, Saradhambal Nagar, Ellaipillaichavadhy, Puducherry. - **Karaikal:** The Collector, Karaikal.- **Mahe/Yanam:** The Regional Administrator, Mahe/Yanam.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
The applicant should qualify as ANY ONE of the following:

- Boys in the age group of 7 to 17 years.
- Mild Mental Retardedness with an IQ level between 50-70.
- Moderate Mental Retardedness with an IQ level between 35-49.
- Severe Mental Retardedness with an IQ level between 20-34.
- Profound Mental Retardedness with an IQ level under 20.
- Unspecified Mental Retardedness with mental efficiency and sub-normality.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Step 1:** The interested applicant should visit (during office hours) the [Office/ Sub-Office](https://socwelfare.py.gov.in/sub--office-social-welfare-department) of the Department of Social Welfare, and request the hard copy of the prescribed format of the application form from the staff exclusively entrusted to issue and collect filled-in applications.
OR
The interested applicant should take print of the prescribed format of the application form.
**Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste the passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
**Step 3:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the authority given below -

- **Puducherry:** The Deputy Director, Differently Abled Section, Directorate of Social Welfare. - **Karaikal:** The Assistant Director, Social Welfare Department (Sub Office).- **Mahe / Yanam:** The Welfare Officer (i\c), Social Welfare Department (Sub Office).
**Step 4:** Request a receipt or acknowledgement from the concerned authority to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Aadhaar Card.
- Voter ID.
- Address Proof.
- Ration Card.
- Age Proof.
- Disability Certificate.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the significance of the Annual Day in the scheme?
content.text: The Annual Day serves as a platform for prize distribution to winners in sports events and cultural programmes, fostering a sense of achievement and recognition among the inmates.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can you elaborate on the eligibility criteria for moderate mental retardation?
content.text: Moderate mental retardation eligibility requires an IQ level between 35-49, ensuring that those falling within this range receive tailored support under the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the key educational components of the scheme?
content.text: The scheme includes non-formal education aimed at assisting and developing inmates in their daily activities, ensuring a well-rounded approach to their educational needs.

content.section_title: FAQ: How does the scheme contribute to the recreational aspect of the inmates' lives?
content.text: Annual tours and local picnic trips are organized to places of interest, providing recreational opportunities and fostering a positive environment for the inmates.

content.section_title: FAQ: Besides medical services, what other forms of assistance are provided to the inmates?
content.text: In addition to medical care, the scheme offers non-formal education to assist and develop inmates in their day-to-day activities, fostering their overall growth.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the frequency of uniform distribution under the scheme?
content.text: Inmates receive four uniform sets annually, ensuring they have an adequate and regular supply for their daily needs.

content.section_title: FAQ: What age group does the scheme target?
content.text: The scheme is designed for boys aged 7 to 17 years who face varying levels of mental challenges, providing tailored support for their specific needs.

content.section_title: FAQ: How does the scheme support inmates with unspecified mental retardation?
content.text: The scheme caters to those with unspecified mental retardation by addressing mental efficiency and sub-normality, ensuring inclusive care for a diverse range of challenges.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are there any cultural events included in the scheme?
content.text: Yes, the scheme organizes an Annual Day for prize distribution to winners in sports events and cultural programmes, enhancing the cultural aspect of the inmates' lives.

content.section_title: FAQ: What activities contribute to the overall well-being of the inmates?
content.text: Apart from education, the scheme includes training in discipline, cleanliness, good health habits, as well as activities such as drawing, painting, and yoga for the inmates' holistic development.

content.section_title: FAQ: What services are provided during the medical check-ups for the inmates?
content.text: The medical check-ups encompass paediatric and psychiatric services to ensure comprehensive healthcare for mentally challenged children.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the specific benefits covered under the scheme?
content.text: The benefits include free boarding, 4 sets of uniforms annually, daily living essentials, medical check-ups, annual tours, local trips, and non-formal education for overall development.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme “Home for Visually challenged Person" was launched by the Department of Social Welfare, Government of Puducherry. The objective of this scheme is to take care of the male persons who are visually impaired and who lack care and protection in society and are deprived of basic needs.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The scheme “Home for Visually challenged Person" was launched by the Department of Social Welfare, Government of Puducherry. The objective of this scheme is to take care of the male persons who are visually impaired and who lack care and protection in society and are deprived of basic needs like food, shelter, clothes.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
1. Free Food and Shelter.
1. Free Supply of Daily Living Needs.
1. Pocket Money of ₹ 5/-.
1. Vocational Training Such as Korai & Coir Mat Weaving, According to Their Interest.
1. Labour Charges of ₹ 20 /- for Mat.
1. Recreations Such as TV, Radio, Musical Instrument, Besides Local Trips to Places of Interest and Temples.
1. Annual Tour to Places of Interest.

**NOTE:** Contact the authorities below in case of delay in disbursal of benefits:
Puducherry: The Director, Directorate of Social Welfare, Saradhambal Nagar, Ellaipillaichavadhy, Puducherry.
Karaikal: The Collector, Karaikal.
Mahe/Yanam: The Regional Administrator, Mahe/Yanam.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant should have a total absence of sight.
1. The applicant should have a visual acuity not exceeding 6/60 or 20/200 in the better eye with correcting lenses.
1. The applicant should have a limitation of field of vision sub standing an angle of 20 degree.
1. The applicant should be above 20 years of age.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Step 1:** The interested applicant should visit (during office hours) the [Office/ Sub-Office](https://socwelfare.py.gov.in/sub--office-social-welfare-department) of the Department of Social Welfare, and request the hard copy of the prescribed format of the application form from the staff exclusively entrusted to issue and collect filled-in applications.
**Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste the passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
**Step 3:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the authority given below -

- **Puducherry:** The Deputy Director, Differently Abled Section, Directorate of Social Welfare. - **Karaikal:** The Assistant Director, Social Welfare Department (Sub Office).- **Mahe / Yanam:** The Welfare Officer (i\c), Social Welfare Department (Sub Office).
**Step 4:** Request a receipt or acknowledgement from the concerned authority to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Aadhaar Card.
- Voter ID.
- Address Proof.
- Ration Card.
- Age Proof.
- Disability Certificate.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the primary objective of the "Home for Visually Challenged Person" scheme?
content.text: The scheme aims to provide care and protection to visually impaired male individuals who lack basic necessities.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the key benefits offered under this scheme?
content.text: Beneficiaries receive free food and shelter, daily living needs, [?]5 pocket money, and vocational training in activities like Korai & Coir Mat Weaving.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the vocational training provided, and how does it align with the beneficiaries' interests?
content.text: Beneficiaries undergo vocational training, including Korai & Coir Mat Weaving, tailored to their individual interests.

content.section_title: FAQ: How are the labor charges for mat weaving addressed under the scheme?
content.text: The scheme provides a labor charge of [?]20 for the production of mats by the visually impaired individuals.

content.section_title: FAQ: Besides basic needs, what recreational activities are offered to beneficiaries?
content.text: Recreational activities include access to TV, radio, musical instruments, local trips, and an annual tour to places of interest.

content.section_title: FAQ: What should an applicant do in case there is a delay in receiving the scheme benefits?
content.text: In case of delays, applicants can contact designated authorities based on their location in Puducherry, Karaikal, Mahe, or Yanam.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the eligibility criteria regarding the absence of sight for applicants?
content.text: The primary eligibility criterion is the total absence of sight for individuals seeking benefits under the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the acceptable visual acuity for applicants under this scheme?
content.text: Applicants should have a visual acuity not exceeding 6/60 or 20/200 in the better eye with correcting lenses.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the specified limitation for the field of vision for an applicant to be eligible?
content.text: Eligible applicants should have a limitation of field of vision, substantiated by an angle of 20 degrees.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there an age requirement for individuals to apply for this scheme?
content.text: Yes, applicants should be above 20 years of age to qualify for the "Home for Visually Challenged Person" scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What geographical areas are covered under the scheme's contact points for delayed benefits?
content.text: The contact points for delayed benefits include Puducherry, Karaikal, Mahe, and Yanam, each with designated authorities.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are female visually impaired individuals eligible for this scheme?
content.text: The scheme specifically focuses on visually impaired male individuals for care and support.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can beneficiaries choose the vocational training based on their personal preferences?
content.text: Yes, vocational training is customized according to the individual interests of visually impaired beneficiaries.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there a provision for annual outings for the beneficiaries?
content.text: Yes, the scheme includes an annual tour to places of interest as part of the recreational activities offered.

content.section_title: FAQ: How does the scheme contribute to the overall well-being of visually impaired individuals?
content.text: The scheme addresses the holistic well-being by providing not just basic needs but also vocational training, recreational activities, and social engagement for the beneficiaries.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: This scheme was launched by the Telangana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, LET&F (Labour) Department Telangana financial assistance is provided to construction workers who suffer temporary disabilities due to accidents or diseases & hospitalized for five or more days.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The "Hospitalisation Relief Scheme" was launched by the Telangana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, LET&F (Labour) Department Telangana. The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to construction workers who suffer temporary disabilities due to accidents or diseases and are hospitalized for five or more days. The relief may be extended up to three months for severe or terminal conditions.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: - **The relief amount is ₹300/- per day, subject to a maximum of ₹4,500/- per month.**

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The worker must be a registered building and other construction worker with the Telangana Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
1. The worker should be hospitalized for at least five days due to an accident or disease.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Step-1:** The interested applicant visits the [official website](https://tbocwwb.telangana.gov.in/index.html) and clicks on "[Downloads](https://tbocwwb.telangana.gov.in/new_downloads.html)" at the top of menu options.
**Step-2:** Now click on the download button corresponding to the scheme name.
**Step-3**: In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
**Step-4:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the concerned Assistant Labour Officer.
**Step-5:** Request a receipt or acknowledgment from the concerned authority to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Passport-size Photograph.
- Registration Card under the BOCW Act (Attested copy).
- Renewal Challan Copy.
- Admission Card of Hospital.
- Doctor Certificate/Medical Certificate (issued not below the rank of Assistant Civil Surgeon of the Government Hospital/Primary Health Centre).
- Advance Stamped Receipt.
- 1st page of Bank Passbook (Attested copy).

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible for this scheme?
content.text: The scheme is applicable to registered building and other construction workers who suffer temporary disabilities due to accidents or diseases requiring hospitalization.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the amount of relief provided under this scheme?
content.text: The relief amount is [?]300 per day, subject to a maximum of [?]4,500 per month.

content.section_title: FAQ: How long can the relief amount be provided?
content.text: The relief can be provided for up to three months if the worker's disability continues due to severe or terminal conditions.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is this scheme applicable for all types of hospitalization?
content.text: Yes, as long as the hospitalization is due to an accident or disease and lasts for five or more days.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the effective date for the enhanced relief amount?
content.text: The enhanced relief amount is applicable from 01.05.2017

content.section_title: FAQ: How is the relief amount disbursed?
content.text: The relief amount is disbursed directly to the worker's bank account through online transfer.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any cost associated with applying for this scheme?
content.text: No, there is no cost to apply for this scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are there any income criteria for the scheme?
content.text: No, the scheme does not have any income criteria.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can this scheme be combined with other compensation schemes?
content.text: Yes, this relief is in addition to any other compensation or relief schemes the worker may be entitled to.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can I contact for assistance with the application process?
content.text: You can contact the local Assistant Labour Officer for assistance.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: This initiative aims to protect every child from preventable, life-threatening diseases by providing free and timely vaccination as per the National Immunization Schedule.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The “Immunization Programme” is an initiative under the National Health Mission (NHM) implemented by the Department of Medical Health and Family Welfare, Government of Uttarakhand. This initiative aims to protect every child from preventable, life-threatening diseases by providing free and timely vaccination as per the National Immunization Schedule.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
To protect children from various life-threatening diseases, free vaccinations are provided under the Universal Immunization Programme (UIP) as per the prescribed schedule below:
***National Immunization Schedule:***
**Vaccine** **Dose** **Vaccination Age**BCG1 doseAt birth, Upto one yearOPV5 dosesAt birth, 6 week, 10 week, 14 week & 16-23 monthsFIPV3 doses6 week, 14 week, & 9 monthHepatitis-B1 doseAt birthPentavalent3 doses6 week, 10 week, 14 weekRVV3 doses 6 week, 10 week, 14 weekPCV3 doses6 week, 10 week, 14 weekMR2 doses9 month & 16-23 monthJE(In endemic districts only)2 doses9 month & 16-23 monthDPT2 doses16-23 month & 5-6 yearTd2 doses10 year, 16 year

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text: 1. All children of Uttarakhand aged 0–16 years who require vaccination are eligible under the scheme.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
The vaccination facility is available to the entire community free of cost at government health institutions, including health centres, sub-centres, post-natal centres, and urban health centres. All children aged 0–16 years can avail of these benefits by registering for vaccination.
For registration, the child must be taken to the concerned government hospital, and the birth certificate or card issued by the ANM is required.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Child’s Birth Certificate
- Card issued by the ANM

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible under this scheme?
content.text: All children aged 0–16 years of Uttarakhand who require vaccination are eligible under the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is the vaccination under this programme free of cost?
content.text: Yes, all vaccinations are provided free of cost at government health facilities.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where can parents get their children vaccinated?
content.text: Vaccinations are available at government health centres, sub-centres, post-natal centres, and urban health centres across Uttarakhand.

content.section_title: FAQ: What documents are required for registration?
content.text: Parents need to present the child’s birth certificate or the card issued by the ANM (Auxiliary Nurse Midwife) for registration.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can a child be registered for vaccination?
content.text: The child must be taken to the nearest government health facility, where registration is done using the required documents.

content.section_title: FAQ: Which vaccines are included under the programme?
content.text: The programme includes vaccines for BCG, OPV, FIPV, Hepatitis-B, Pentavalent, RVV, PCV, MR, JE (in endemic districts), DPT, and Td.

title: Incentive Scheme for Establishment of New Super Specialty Hospital in Private Sector
topic: Incentive Scheme for Establishment of New Super Specialty Hospital in Private Sector

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme offers financial incentives (capital subsidy, interest subvention, and electricity duty reimbursement) to private/NGO entities establishing new super specialty hospitals (50+ beds) in Gujarat, targeting underserved areas.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: Launched on 13th December 2016, the "Incentive Scheme for Establishment of New Super Specialty Hospital in Private Sector" by the Health & Family Welfare Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to address the shortage of high-end medical facilities in remote areas by incentivizing private and non-governmental organizations to establish super specialty hospitals. The scheme provides 10% capital subsidy on medical equipment loans (up to ₹10 crore), 4% interest subvention (max ₹40 crore loan) for 7 years, and reimbursement of electricity duty for 5 years. Eligible entities include registered doctors, firms, trusts, and corporations.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
> **Capital Subsidy**

- 10% of bank loans for medical equipment (max ₹10 crore).
- Disbursed post-hospital operationalization.

> **Interest Subvention**

- 4% interest relief on loans up to ₹40 crore for 7 years.
- Paid in 6 annual installments after a 1-year grace period.

> **Electricity Duty Reimbursement**

100% reimbursement for 5 years (quarterly claims).
> **Employment Generation**

Mandates hiring qualified medical/paramedical staff.
> **Disbursement**

- **Capital Subsidy:** After Equipment Installation and Hospital Launch.- **Interest Subvention:** Annual Installments Post 1-Year Grace Period.- **Electricity Reimbursement:** Quarterly Claims.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
> **The Following Entities Are Eligible**

- Registered Doctors (Gujarat Medical Council).
- Partnership Firms, Companies, Trusts, Societies, Cooperatives.

> **Hospital Requirements**

- 50+ Total Beds,including 10+ Super Specialty Beds.
- Covers Specialties Like Cardiology, Neurology, Oncology, etc. (List Expandable by Government).

> **Loan Conditions**

- Only Loans for Construction/Equipment (Excludes Land/Working Capital).

content.section_title: Exclusions
content.text: Excludes Government/Semi-Government Bodies.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Step 1:** The interested applicant should take a printout of the [prescribed format](https://gujhealth.gujarat.gov.in/images/pdf/Application-forms-for-Establishment-of-New-Super-Specialty-Hospital-new.pdf) of the application form.
**Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
**Step 3:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents, within the prescribed period (if any), to - ***Commissioner, Health & Medical Education, Dr. Jivraj Mehta Bhavan, Gandhinagar***. Pay ₹20,000/- scrutiny fee via Demand Draft.
**Step 4:** Request a receipt or acknowledgement from the concerned authority to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date of submission.

**Approval & Verification**

- State-Level Committee scrutinizes applications.
- Technical Group conducts physical inspections.

**Compliance**

- Submit quarterly utilization reports.
- Maintain services for 7 years (penalty: repayment if violated).

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- ₹20,000/- Scrutiny Fee (Demand Draft).
- Bank Loan Sanction Letter (for Construction/equipment).
- Property Documents (if Applicable).
- Deed of Undertaking (₹100/- Stamp Paper) Committing to:
- 7-year Operational Continuity.
- Compliance With Staffing and Service Norms.
- Electricity Bills (for Duty Reimbursement).

content.section_title: FAQ: Which specific medical specialties are covered under this super specialty hospital scheme's definition?
content.text:
**Benefits include:**
100% reimbursement of electricity duty.
Quarterly reimbursement payments.
Duration of 5 years from hospital commencement.
Separate notification for claim procedures.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the minimum infrastructure requirements that a hospital must meet to qualify as a super specialty facility under this scheme?
content.text:
**To qualify, hospitals must have:**

- Minimum 10 dedicated beds for super specialty care.
- Total bed strength of at least 50 beds.
- Proper infrastructure for diagnosis and treatment in at least one recognized super specialty.

content.section_title: FAQ: What types of financial assistance are available under this scheme for establishing new super specialty hospitals?
content.text:
**The scheme offers three forms of financial support:**

- 10% capital subsidy on bank loans for medical equipment (max ₹10 crore).
- 4% interest subvention on authorized loans (max ₹40 crore loan) for 7 years.
- Full reimbursement of electricity duty for 5 years.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the key eligibility criteria for private healthcare providers to avail benefits under this scheme?
content.text:
**Eligible applicants must be:**

- Doctors registered with Gujarat Medical Council.
- Registered partnership firms/institutions.
- Companies, charitable trusts, societies or cooperative institutions.
- Entities establishing completely new facilities (existing hospitals are ineligible).

content.section_title: FAQ: Which expenses are covered and excluded under the bank loan assistance component of this scheme?
content.text:
**Covered expenses:**

- Construction loans for new hospital buildings.
- Loans for medical equipment purchase.

**Excluded expenses:**

- Land acquisition costs.
- Working capital.
- Furniture/air conditioners.
- Maintenance/repair costs.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the mandatory operational commitments that beneficiaries must adhere to under this scheme?
content.text:
**Key requirements include:**

- Maintaining super specialty services for minimum 7 years.
- Not relocating purchased medical equipment.
- Hiring qualified medical/paramedical staff.
- Joining the MA/MA Vatsalya Scheme.
- Complying with all healthcare regulations.

content.section_title: FAQ: How does the interest subvention component of this scheme work for eligible hospitals?
content.text:
**The scheme provides:**

- 4% interest relief on approved bank loans.
- Maximum loan ceiling of ₹40 crore.
- Subvention paid annually for 7 years (first payment after 1 year of operation).
- Total of 7 installment payments.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the application process and timeline for availing benefits under this super specialty hospital scheme?
content.text:
**Applicants must:**

- Submit proposal with ₹20,000 scrutiny fee via demand draft.
- Get registration approval from Commissioner of Health.
- Begin hospital operations before 31/3/2022.
- Post-commencement, apply for financial assistance.
- Undergo technical committee verification.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the key conditions regarding electricity duty reimbursement under this scheme?
content.text:
**Benefits include:**

- 100% reimbursement of electricity duty.
- Quarterly reimbursement payments.
- Duration of 5 years from hospital commencement.
- Separate notification for claim procedures.

content.section_title: FAQ: What legal obligations and undertakings must beneficiaries comply with under this scheme?
content.text:
**Mandatory requirements:**

- Submit ₹100 stamp paper undertaking
- Maintain hospital for minimum 7 years
- Not avail similar state government subsidies
- Return all assistance if conditions are violated
- Allow periodic inspections by authorities

content.section_title: FAQ: How does the scheme prevent double-dipping or misuse of government incentives?
content.text:
**Safeguards include:**

- One-time assistance only.
- Exclusion if similar benefits received.
- Bar on equipment subsidy duplication.
- Mandatory 7-year operational commitment.
- Recovery provisions for violations.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the composition and role of the State Level Committee that oversees this scheme?
content.text:
**The committee comprises:**

- Health Commissioner (Chairman).
- Finance Department representative.
- Medical education experts.
- Hospital administrators.
- Technical subject experts.
- Roles include proposal scrutiny, assistance sanctioning, and monitoring compliance.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: A scheme by Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) for the welfare of Overseas Indians; assisting them in distress situations. ICWF has also been a critical support in emergency evacuation of Indian nationals from conflict zones, countries affected by natural disasters and other challenging situations.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
A scheme by Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) for the welfare of Overseas Indians; assisting them in distress situations.

ICWF has also been a critical support in emergency evacuation of Indian nationals from conflict zones, countries affected by natural disasters and other challenging situations. In view of its immense utility, ICWF stands extended to all Indian Missions and Posts abroad.

The ICWF guidelines have been revised further to make them more broad-based and expand the scope of welfare measures that can be extended through the Fund. They are expected to provide Indian Missions and Posts abroad greater flexibility in swiftly addressing to requests for assistance by Overseas Indian nationals.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text: 1. The applicant must be an **Overseas Indian.**1. The applicant must be in a **distress situation (as defined under scheme benefits).**

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
For filing Compensation Claims for Indian Nationals who died Abroad:
(varies from nation to nation)

- Death Certificate
- Bank details of the deceased
- Insurance Number of the deceased
- Marriage Certificate (spouse) and Birth Certificate (of children)
- Original Passport of the deceased along with a photocopy
- Power of attorney from next-of-kin in the name of Embassy or any individual residing in Austria whom the deceased’s family would like to appoint.
- Legal Heir Certificate, to be obtained by the next-of-kin through the concerned Taluk/Thasildar or through the District Civil Court of the concerned area in India.
- Any other document that seem relevant depending upon the death case, such as police report (from the local police), medical report (from the hospital)
- NOC from other Heirs (in case of multiple heirs)

Source - https://www.mea.gov.in/images/pdf/compendium.pdf

content.section_title: FAQ: Is legal assistance also provided to the Overseas Indian students who are in distress?
content.text: Yes, Overseas Indian students who are in distress are eligible for support under this scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: In what cases is an Overseas Indian woman eligible to receive legal assistance?
content.text: Legal/financial assistance is provided to Indian women abandoned/ cheated / abused by their Overseas Indian/PIO or foreign spouses (up to seven years after their marriage.

content.section_title: FAQ: Under what circumstances does the scheme provide support for transportation of Mortal Remains of the deceased Overseas Indian?
content.text: Such support is provided in such cases where the employer, sponsor or insurance company is unable or unwilling to do so as per the contract and the family is unable to meet the cost.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are OCI card holders eligible for financial assistance under ICWF?
content.text: No, the "Overseas Citizens of India (OCI) Card Holders" are not eligible for individual financial assistance from ICWF.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is a person from Indian origin but distressed in a foreign country eligible for assistance?
content.text: No, Persons of Indian Origin aren't covered under ICWF.

content.section_title: FAQ: Does ICWF also provides support in emergency evacuation of  Indian nationals?
content.text: Yes, ICWF has been a critical support in emergency evacuation of Indian nationals in conflict zones in Libya, Iraq, Yemen, South Sudan and other challenging situations.

content.section_title: FAQ: Whom should I approach to file the compensation claim for my husband?
content.text: You need to contact the "First Point of Contact" of the nation in which your husband died.

title: Indira Gandhi National Disability Pension Scheme (Jammu and Kashmir)
topic: Indira Gandhi National Disability Pension Scheme (Jammu and Kashmir)

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: Launched on 15th August 1995, the "Indira Gandhi National Disability Pension Scheme"is a pension scheme by the Department Of Social Welfare  Jammu and Kashmir. In this scheme, a pension of ₹1000/- is provided to differently-abled individuals from 'Below Poverty Line'.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: Launched on 15th August 1995, the scheme "Indira Gandhi National Disability Pension Scheme (IGNDPS)" is a pension scheme by the Social Welfare Department, Union Territory of Jammu and Kashmir. The scheme provides ₹1,000/- per month to BPL individuals aged 18 to 79 who have severe or multiple disabilities. Launched under the National Social Assistance Programme (NSAP), the scheme aims to ensure social justice by providing financial support to the most vulnerable sections of society, fulfilling the Directive Principles of State Policy.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
**Amount of Pension:** The beneficiary will receive a monthly pension of ₹1000/-.
**Mode of Disbursement:** The pension is disbursed to the beneficiary either through bank transfer or money order.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant should be a resident of the Union Territory of Jammu and Kashmir.
1. The applicant should be a person with a disability.
1. The applicant's age should be between 18 and over 80 years.
1. The applicant should belong to a Below Poverty Line (BPL) family.
1. The applicant should not be receiving pension benefits under any other social security scheme.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Step 1:** The applicant should visit the Tehsil Social Welfare Officer (TSWO) in the concerned area. during office hours and request a hard copy of the[ prescribed application form](https://cdn.s3waas.gov.in/s3f4b9ec30ad9f68f89b29639786cb62ef/uploads/2018/01/2018112294.pdf) from the concerned authority.
**Step 2:** Fill in all the mandatory fields in the application form. Paste a passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all necessary documents (self-attested, if required).
**Step 3:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents, within the prescribed period (if any), to the Tehsil Social Welfare Officer (TSWO).
**Step 4:** Request a receipt or acknowledgement from the concerned authority to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).

**Post Application Processes**
**Step 1:** The TSWO verifies the submitted forms and forwards them to the District Social Welfare Officer (DSWO) for review.
**Step 2:** The District Level Committee reviews the application and sanctions the pension.

**NOTE 1:** Upon submission of the completed application form and the necessary documents, make sure to take a photocopy of the filled application form and the acknowledgement receipt for your future reference.
**NOTE 2:** Ensure that the application is submitted within the prescribed period, if any.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Aadhaar Card
- Passport-size Photograph
- Identity Proof i.e. Voter ID Card or Driving License
- Disability Certificate
- Proof of Age
- Below Poverty Line (BPL) Card/ Ration Card
- Caste Certificate (if applicable)
- Address Proof
- Bank Passbook/Bank Account Details
- Any other documents as required

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the primary objective of the IGNDPS?
content.text: The primary objective is to provide financial assistance to individuals with disabilities living below the poverty line, ensuring their social and economic welfare.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible to apply for the pension under the scheme?
content.text: Applicants must be residents of Jammu and Kashmir, aged between 18 and 79, with disabilities, and belong to Below Poverty Line (BPL) families.

content.section_title: FAQ: How does one submit an application for the IGNDPS?
content.text: Applicants must fill out the required forms and submit them to the concerned Tehsil Social Welfare Officer.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are there any specific documents required for the application?
content.text: Yes, applicants typically need to provide proof of residency, disability certificate, and evidence of BPL status.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can individuals over 79 years old apply for the pension?
content.text: Yes, different pension provisions apply for individuals aged 80 and above.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is the pension amount fixed or subject to change?
content.text: The current pension amount is fixed at [?]1000 per month, but it may be reviewed and updated by the government.

content.section_title: FAQ: How is the pension disbursed to beneficiaries?
content.text: The pension amount is directly transferred to beneficiaries through bank accounts or money orders.

content.section_title: FAQ: What impact does the scheme aim to have on beneficiaries?
content.text: The scheme aims to alleviate financial hardship and improve the quality of life for individuals with disabilities.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can beneficiaries seek assistance if they face issues with their applications?
content.text: Beneficiaries can reach out to the Tehsil Social Welfare Officer or the local Social Welfare Department for support and guidance regarding their applications.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The Integrated Child Development Service (ICDS) Scheme providing for supplementary nutrition, immunization and pre-school education to the children is a popular flagship programme of the government. Launched in 1975, it is one of the world’s largest programmes providing for an integrated package of

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
Launched on 2nd October, 1975, the Integrated Child Development Services (ICDS) Scheme is one of the flagship programmes of the Government of India and represents one of the world’s largest and unique programmes for early childhood care and development. It is the foremost symbol of country’s commitment to its children and nursing mothers, as a response to the challenge of providing pre-school non-formal education on one hand and breaking the vicious cycle of malnutrition, morbidity, reduced learning capacity and mortality on the other. The beneficiaries under the Scheme are children in the age group of 0-6 years, pregnant women, and lactating mothers.

To reduce mortality morbidity and malnutrition among the children in U.T of Chandigarh, the ICDS project was started on 1.8.1979 with 24 anganwadi centres. At present there are 3 ICDS Projects comprising of 450 anganwadi centres covering an approx. population of over 5.50 lac in peripheral areas, villages, colonies, and slum areas.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
- Supplementary Nutrition
- Immunization
- Health Check-up
- Referral Services
- Pre-School Education
- Nutrition & Health Education

Three of the six services viz. immunization, health check-up and referral services are related to health and are provided through National Health Mission and Public Health Infrastructure. The services are offered at Anganwadi Centers through Anganwadi Workers (AWWs) and Anganwadi Helpers (AWHS) at grassroots level.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
- Children in the age group of 0-6 years.
- Pregnant women.
- Lactating mothers.
- Women from age group 14-18 years of UT Chandigarh.
-  The Scheme is universal for all categories of beneficiaries and in coverage.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text: The citizen need not apply to the scheme. Visit the nearest Anganwadi center.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text: - Aadhar Card

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the schemes under ICDS?
content.text:
Supplementary Nutrition (SNP)
Health & Nutrition Check-Up
Immunization
Non-Formal Education for Children in Pre-School
Health and Nutrition Education
Referral services

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the objectives of ICDS scheme?
FAQ: Who will be the beneficiary of ICDS  schemes?
content.text:
Pregnant and lactating women.
Children under 6 years of age.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: In Nagaland, the ICDS programme was launched in the year 1975 in the Jalukie block of Peren district with 50 Anganwadi centres. The programme has since grown and at present, there are 60 (sixty) ICDS projects in 11 (Eleven) districts of the State.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
In Nagaland, the ICDS program was launched in the year 1975 in the Jalukie block of Peren district with 50 Anganwadi centers. The program has since grown and at present, there are 60 (sixty) ICDS projects in 17 (Seventeen) districts of the State. Of this 2 (two) are Urban projects, one is rural project and the rest are Tribal projects. All the 6(six) services under ICDS such as Supplementary nutrition, Health checkup, Non formal pre-school education, Immunization, Nutrition & Health education and Referral services are being provided through a Network of 3980 Anganwadi center’s in the State.
Presently 60 (sixty) ICDS projects are operating in the state covering 2.11 Lakh beneficiaries under 3980 Anganwadi Centers where all the 6 (six) services are provided to children in the age group of 0-6, pregnant women and lactating mothers and adolescent girls.
Poshan Tracker management application provides a 360-degree view of the activities of the Anganwadi Centre (AWC), service deliveries of Anganwadi Workers (AWWs) and complete beneficiary management for pregnant women, lactating mothers, children and adolescent girls. The POSHAN Tracker App is managed by National e Governance Division (NeGD) through a State Coordinator placed at the Social Welfare Directorate.
**Objectives**

1. To improve the nutritional and health status of children in the age-group 0-6 years;
1. To lay the foundation for proper psychological, physical and social development of the child.
1. To reduce the incidence of mortality, morbidity, malnutrition and school dropout;
1. To achieve effective co-ordination of policy and implementation amongst the various departments to promote child development; and
1. To enhance the capability of the mother to look after the normal health and nutritional needs of the child through proper nutrition and health education.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
**The ICDS Scheme offers a package of six services,**

1. Supplementary nutrition.
1. Immunization.
1. Health check-up.
1. Referral services.
1. Pre-school non-formal education and
1. Nutrition & Health Education.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
**Eligible beneficiaries under this scheme include:**

1. Children up to 6 years of age
1. Pregnant women
1. Lactating mothers
1. Adolescent girls (14–18 years)

content.section_title: Exclusions
content.text: > **The children above the age of 6 years are not eligible.**

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text: > **Applicants need to visit the nearest Anganwadi centres to avail the benefits of the scheme.**

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Aadhaar Card
- Passport Sized Photograph
- Proof of Age / DOB
- Proof of Address.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the benefit of this scheme?
content.text: It improves the nutritional and health status of children in the age group 0-6 years.

content.section_title: FAQ: What does the benefit the child avail from this scheme?
content.text: To lay the foundation for proper psychological, physical and social development of the child.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the aim of this scheme?
content.text: To reduce instances of mortality, malnutrition and school dropouts among Indian children.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the benefit avail to mother from this scheme?
content.text: To provide health and nutritional information and education to mothers of young children to enhance the child-rearing capabilities of mothers in the country of India.

content.section_title: FAQ: How does it works for mothers?
content.text: It provides nutritional food to the mothers of young children & also at the time of pregnancy period.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can avail this scheme?
content.text: The children up to the age of 6 years.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the primary selection of the Beneficiaries?
content.text: Pregnant women and Lactating mothers.

content.section_title: FAQ: How this service are provided
content.text: These services are provided from Anganwadi centres established mainly in rural areas and staffed with frontline workers.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The "General Surgery Scheme'' by the BOCW Welfare Board, Department of Labour, Punjab, is a welfare scheme that provides financial assistance for general surgery of the beneficiary and his family members.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The "General Surgery Scheme'' by the BOCW Welfare Board, Department of Labour, Punjab, is a welfare scheme for the registered workers of the board. Under this scheme, the Board shall provide financial assistance for general surgery of the beneficiary and his family members.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: - **Financial Coverage:** ₹50,000/- or actual expenses, whichever is less.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
> ***For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:***

- The applicant should be a resident of Punjab.
- The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker (Unorganised Worker).
- The applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
- The applicant should have engaged in building and construction work for at least 90 days in a given year.
- The applicant should have a monthly income of ₹15,000/- or below.
- The applicant should not be a member of any other Welfare Fund established under any law.
- The applicant should not have membership of the Employees' Provident Fund (EPF)/ National Pension System (NPS)/ Employees' State Insurance Corporation (ESIC).
- The applicant should not be an income tax payer.

> ***For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:***

- The applicant should be a registered member Under the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of Punjab or should be a family member of the worker.
- The member should be depositing his contribution to the Board regularly.
- The applicant should have undergone general surgery for himself or any member of his family.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online - via CSC)
content.text:
> **Registration Process as a Building/ Construction Worker:**

- For new registration of workers please approach the nearby Sewa Kendra or Registering officers/ Labour Inspector.

> ***Application Process of the Welfare Scheme:***

- To apply for benefits of the concerned welfare scheme, please approach to nearby Sewa Kendra or Registering Officers/ Labour Inspector of your area.

> ***Note:***

- **Registration with the Board:** A construction worker becomes a member of the board by filling out Application Form No 28 along with depositing registration fees of ₹ 25/- only (once in life) and ₹ 10/- per month as contribution fees. A worker at a time can get himself registered for a minimum period of 1 year & maximum period of 5 years. The registered worker is called 'Beneficiary' of the Board.
> **Important Links:**
