content.text: Eligible workers (Shramyogis) must have their Labour Welfare Fund regularly paid by their organization/unit for the last one year. Additionally, male Shram Yogis above 45 years and female Shram Yogis above 35 years working in the organized sector are eligible.

content.section_title: FAQ: How often can a worker avail of the benefit?
content.text: Benefits will be provided once a year to the eligible laborers.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is the Gujarat Labour Welfare Board liable for any side effects during or after the physical examination?
content.text: No, the Gujarat Labour Welfare Board shall not be responsible for any side effects during or after the physical examination.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the timeline for completing the medical examination after receiving in-principle approval?
content.text: The medical examination must be done within 30 days of receiving in-principle approval, with mutual agreement between the company and the hospital.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are the fees paid under this scheme refundable?
content.text: No, fees paid under this scheme will not be refunded.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can one apply for the scheme benefit?
content.text: Applicants can apply through the Sanman Portal: https://sanman.gujarat.gov.in/

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: Launched on 13th August 2013, the "Funeral Assistance Scheme" by the Department of Social Justice and Empowerment, Govt. Madhya Pradesh provides ₹3,000/- for the last rites of destitute or unclaimed bodies.  The scheme also supports families registered under labour schemes.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: Launched on 13th August 2013, the "Funeral Assistance Scheme" by the Department of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of Madhya Pradesh, provides ₹3,000/- for the last rites of destitute individuals and unclaimed bodies. The scheme also supports families registered under labour schemes, especially those in the unorganized sector, who struggle to afford last rites due to financial constraints. The scheme helps ease this burden by providing essential funds for performing the final rites.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: A financial assistance of ₹3,000/- is provided.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant must be a native of Madhya Pradesh.
1. The deceased should have been a member of a family registered under the labour cadre schemes operated by the Government of Madhya Pradesh.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Step 1:** The applicant should visit the Gram Panchayat or Public Service Center during office hours and request a hard copy of the prescribed application form from the concerned authority.
**Step 2:** Fill in all the mandatory fields in the application form. Paste a passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all necessary documents (self-attested, if required).
**Step 3:** After applying, the applicant will receive an acknowledgement receipt from the office, confirming that the application has been received.
**Step 4:** The submitted documents will be verified by the District Panchayat/ Gram Panchayat/ Urban Body or Ward Office depending on to whom the application was submitted.
**Step 5:** Upon submission of the completed application form and the necessary documents, make sure to take a photocopy of the filled application form and the acknowledgement receipt for your future reference.

**Post Application Processes**
Once the documents are verified and found to be correct, the application will be approved, and the applicant’s name will be added to the pension proposal for that month.

**NOTE:** If any discrepancies or missing information are found, the application will be rejected. In such cases, the applicant will receive a written notice specifying the reason for rejection, and the rejection will be duly recorded in the system.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Certificate issued by the place of cremation/resting place/graveyard etc. of the deceased.
- Death certificate.
- FIR in case of death due to accident, quarrel etc.
- FIR and Panchnama in case of unclaimed dead body.
- Doctor's certificate in case of death in hospital.
- Address Proof
- Bank Passbook/Bank Account Details
- Any other documents as required

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the Funeral Assistance Scheme?
content.text: The Funeral Assistance Scheme provides financial support to families facing difficulties in arranging the last rites of a deceased family member.

content.section_title: FAQ: How much assistance is provided under the scheme?
content.text: An amount of [?]3,000 is provided to support the funeral expenses.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can apply for funeral assistance?
content.text: The application must be submitted by the family of the deceased person to the Sarpanch of the Gram Panchayat or the Zonal Officer of the Municipal Corporation.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where should I submit the application for funeral assistance?
content.text: Applications should be submitted to the Sarpanch (in rural areas) or Zonal Officer (in urban areas) of the respective Gram Panchayat or Municipal Corporation.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there a specific age limit for the deceased to apply for assistance?
content.text: There is no specific age limit for the deceased, but the deceased must be a member of a family that is registered under the Madhya Pradesh labor schemes.

content.section_title: FAQ: How is the application processed?
content.text: After submission, the application is reviewed by the Sarpanch or Zonal Officer, who will send the proposal for reimbursement to the District Panchayat (in rural areas) or Municipal Corporation Commissioner (in urban areas).

content.section_title: FAQ: How long does it take to receive the funeral assistance?
content.text: Once the proposal is approved, the assistance amount is disbursed directly to the beneficiary. The timeline depends on the approval process, but it is generally processed quickly.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can I apply for this scheme if I live in an urban area?
content.text: Yes, the scheme is available in both rural and urban areas of Madhya Pradesh. The process in urban areas is handled by the Municipal Corporation.

content.section_title: FAQ: What happens if the application is rejected?
content.text: If the application is rejected, the applicant will be informed, and the reason for rejection will be provided.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is the Funeral Assistance Scheme available to all families?
content.text: No, the scheme is specifically for families living below the poverty line and those registered under the labor schemes of the Madhya Pradesh government.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The "Funeral Support Scheme" under the Labour and Employment Department, Himachal Pradesh, provides ₹20,000 to the nominees or dependents of registered beneficiaries to support funeral expenses.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The "Funeral Support Scheme," launched by the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (HPBOCWWB) under the Government of Himachal Pradesh, provides financial assistance of ₹20,000/- (twenty thousand) to the nominees or dependents of a registered beneficiary in the event of the worker's death.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: ₹20,000/- is provided for a registered worker's death.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant should be a nominee/legal heir of the deceased worker.
1. The deceased worker should have been registered with the "Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board".
1. The membership of the deceased worker with the Welfare Board must be active.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Step 1:** The interested applicant should visit the Labor Welfare Office (during office hours) and request a hard copy of the prescribed [application form](https://bocw.hp.nic.in/pdf/formno37_A1b.pdf) from the concerned authority.
**Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste a passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attested, if required).
**Step 3:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with a wage slip or appointment letter from the employer, verifying 90 days of construction work. If unavailable, a certificate from a local authority (such as a councilor, executive officer, or Panchayat Secretary) can be submitted to verify employment.
**Step 4:** After submitting the documents and payment, the worker will receive an acknowledgment receipt confirming their registration.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
**Scheme Application Process:**
**Step 1:** The applicant must visit the [official website](https://bocw.hp.nic.in/index.aspx) of the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
**Step 2:** Click on the option "[Apply for Benefits.](https://bocw.hp.nic.in/PublicPages/ApplyForSchemes.aspx)"
**Step 3:** Enter your beneficiary details and date of birth, then click "Search" to view your information.
**Step 4:** Select the scheme you wish to apply for to avail of the benefits.
**Step 5:**  Enter all required information and upload the necessary documents as prompted.
**Step 6:** Finally, click on "Submit Application" to complete the process.

**Application Tracking:**
Applicants can track their application status online through the[ official website](https://bocw.hp.nic.in/index.aspx) of the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board. Relevant updates will be sent to the registered mobile number.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Identity proof i.e. Aadhaar Card, Voter Card
- Passport-size photograph
- Undertaking
- A copy Work Slip
- Proof of residence
- Ration Card
- Bank account details/copy of bank passbook
- Caste Certificate
- Age proof
- Labour Card
- Death Certificate
- Identity proof of the nominee/legal heir
- Any other documents as required

content.section_title: FAQ: What does the scheme provide?
content.text: [?]20,000 for funeral expenses.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible for the benefit?
content.text: Nominees or dependents of registered workers.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the purpose of the scheme?
content.text: To support funeral expenses of registered workers.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is the benefit only for accidental deaths?
content.text: No, it applies to all cases of death.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can claim the benefit?
content.text: The registered worker's nominees or dependents.

content.section_title: FAQ: What documents are needed to apply?
content.text: ID proof, death certificate, and nominee verification.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can I apply for the benefit?
content.text: Submit the required documents to the Labour and Employment Department.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is this scheme available to unregistered workers?
content.text: No, only registered workers are eligible.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who manages the scheme?
content.text: The Labour and Employment Department, Himachal Pradesh.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The "Gambhir Bimari Sahayata Yojana" is a scheme by the government of Uttar Pradesh, India, to provide financial assistance to registered construction workers for the  treatment from the serious Illness

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
The Uttar Pradesh Gambhir Bimari Sahayata Yojana is a government-funded scheme that provides financial assistance to such construction workers who are not covered under "Ayushman Bharat Jan Arogya Yojana" and "Pradhan Mantri Jan Aarogya Yojana" in the UP Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.

Benefits will be provided to the family member of the construction worker in case of illness, for treatment in any government hospital or autonomous hospital of the Government of India or Uttar Pradesh Government or such hospitals which have been empanelled by the National Health Authority of Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana or SACHIS (State Agency For Comprehensive Health Insurance and Integrated Services) functioning at the state level.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
1. Full reimbursement of an amount equivalent to the benefit payable under Ayushman Bharat Scheme on treatment in government/autonomous hospitals or SACHIS empaneled hospitals.
1. In case of medical/surgical treatment, advance amount can also be paid to the hospital after the hospital gives the estimate for the treatment.
1. No maximum amount is fixed.
1. The scheme covers the cost of treatment for a variety of serious illnesses, including:

- Heart surgery.
- Kidney transplant.
- Liver transplant.
- Brain surgery.
- Changing the knees of the legs.
- Cancer treatment.
- HIV AIDS disease.
- Eye surgery.
- Stone Surgery.
- Appendix surgery.
- Hydrocele surgery.
- Surgery for breast cancer in women.
- Surgery for cervical cancer.

1. Also cover the diseases which comes under Ayushman Bharat Pradhan-mantri Jan-arogya Yojana and Mukhya-mantri Jan-arogya Yojana.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. Applicant must be registered workers under labor department.
1. Beneficiary can not get the similar type of government scheme benefits like Pradhan Mantri Jan-Arogya Yojana and Chief Minister Jan-Arogya Yojana.
1. Only below family members are covered

- Self.
- Depended parents.
- Spouse (Husband/Wife).
- Daughter.
- Son (only if below 21 years of age)

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
> **How to get register and apply for the scheme**

1. Applicant must visit the below offices

- Nearest labor office.
- Tehsildar of the concerned tehsil.
- Block Development Officer of the concerned development block.

1. Get the application form and fill out the completely.
1. Attach required documents in the prescribed format.
1. Submit the application form in the same office.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
> **How to get register**

1. Applicant must visit the [official web portal.](https://www.uplmis.in/Guest/frm_createlogin_forlabs.aspx)1. Provide the required details

- Enter Aadhaar Number.
- Choose Circle.
- Choose District.
- Mobile Number.

1. Submit the registration request.

> **How to submit the application**

1. Applicant must visit the official [web portal](https://www.uplmis.in/Guest/frm_CreateLogin.aspx?type=2).1. Provide the required details

- Choose Circle.
- Choose Scheme name.
- Enter Registered Aadhaar Number.
- Enter Registered Mobile Number.

1. Click on "Open Application form".
1. Provide the required details in application form.

- Applicant/Worker details
- Child details
- Bank Account Details
- Submit the required documents

1. Submit the application request.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Copy of construction worker registration card.
- Copy of Aadhar Card.
- Copy of bank passbook for account number, branch name and IFSC code.
- Disease related Details.
- Doctor's certificate on format.
- Medicines original bill.
- Certificate of being an unmarried daughter or a daughter below 21 years of age.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is this scheme about ?
content.text: The "Gambhir Bimari Sahayata Yojana" is a scheme by the government of Uttar Pradesh, India, to provide financial assistance to registered construction workers for the treatment from the serious Illness

content.section_title: FAQ: What benefits are provide under the scheme?
content.text: Financial assistance for series illness treatment.

content.section_title: FAQ: Which illness are cover under the scheme ?
content.text: All the diseases which comes under Ayushman Bharat Pradhan-mantri Jan-arogya Yojana and Mukhya-mantri Jan-arogya Yojana.

content.section_title: FAQ: How many family members are cover under the scheme ?
content.text:
Family member cover under the scheme.
1. Depended parents.
2. Husband/Wife.
3. Daughter.
4. Son.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any age restriction for the family members?
content.text: There is no restriction for the members except the son. Benefits will be provided to the son if the age below 21 years.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the procedure to get the scheme benefits ?
content.text:
1. Firstly applicant get register his/her self with labor department.
2. Submit the application form with required documents to labor department.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is registration link ?
content.text: URL : https://www.uplmis.in/Guest/frm_createlogin_forlabs.aspx

content.section_title: FAQ: What is application link ?
content.text: URL :https://www.uplmis.in/Guest/frm_CreateLogin.aspx?type=2

content.section_title: FAQ: What Essential Documents are required ?
content.text:
1. Copy of construction worker registration card.
2. Copy of Aadhar Card.
3. Copy of bank passbook for account number, branch name and IFSC code.
4. Disease related Details.
5. Doctor's certificate on format.
6. Medicines original bill.
7. Certificate of being an unmarried daughter or a daughter below 21 years of age.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: State Government is granting financial assistance in the form of Grant-in-Aid to a person having no employment under the Government, Public Sector undertaking, or under any local bodies may qualify to receive the grand suffering from fell disease.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
The Grant-in-Aid (TB) Scheme of the Health and Family Welfare Department, Mizoram, was introduced to provide financial assistance to indigent persons suffering from serious diseases. In order to regulate the grant of such assistance, the State Government framed a set of rules known as the Mizoram Health & Family Welfare (Grant-in-Aid to Indigent Scheduled Tribes and Scheduled Castes Patients Suffering from Serious Diseases) Rules, 1997, which came into force from the date of their publication in the Mizoram Gazette.

Under these rules, Scheduled Tribes and Scheduled Castes persons who are permanent residents of the State of Mizoram, whose family members are not employed under the Government, Public Sector Undertakings, or local bodies, and whose total family income from all sources does not exceed ₹2,000 per month, are eligible to receive the Grant-in-Aid, provided they are suffering from serious diseases.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
1. The grant-in-aid shall be given in cash or by means of a Bank Draft.
1. The grant-in-aid shall be made subject to the availability of found and cannot be claimed as a matter of right.
1. The amount of grant-in-aid shall be limited to the actual cost of treatment and fare as per the cheapest route available subject to the maximum of ₹ 2500/- per head.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. Scheduled Tribes and Scheduled Castes persons permanently residing within the State of Mizoram
1. None of whose family members are Government employees and whose family income from all sources does not exceed ₹2000/- per month and who suffering from fell diseases are eligible to receive the grant.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
1. The application shall be forwarded by the Medical Officer i/c of the locality.
1. The authenticity of the residence of the person shall have to be certified by the Village Council President and Block Development Officer concerned and the Medical Board shall examine the patient and issue certificate according to.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Caste Certificate
- Aadhaar card
- Income proof
- Candidates' bank account details

content.section_title: FAQ: What is maximum amount will be given to patient?
content.text:
The amount of grant-in-aid shall be limited to the actual cost of treatment and fare
as per the cheapest route available subject to the maximum of Rs.2500/- per head.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are benefits available for Government employees?
content.text: No, benefits are not available for government employees.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible to take benefits of this scheme?
content.text: Beneficiaries belonging to Scheduled Tribes and Scheduled Castes and none of family member  should be in Government service.

content.section_title: FAQ: What should be income limit?
content.text: Income limit of family should not exceed 2000/-Rs

content.section_title: FAQ: What is mode of Application?
content.text: You can apply offline.

content.section_title: FAQ: To Whom The Application Shall Be Forwarded?
content.text: The Application must be forwarded to medical officer.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who Will Examine The Patient Condition And Approve Application?
content.text: Block Development officer and Medical board will examine and approve the application of the patient.

title: Grants In Aid To SC/ST Patients Suffering From Cancer And Other Malignant Diseases
topic: Grants In Aid To SC/ST Patients Suffering From Cancer And Other Malignant Diseases

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The objective of the scheme is to provide financial assistance in the form of one-time grants to patients undergoing medical treatment in connection with T.B., Cancer and other malignant diseases. This scheme provides one-time financial assistance to patients belonging to the Tea Tribes community.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
Special provisions have been enshrined in the Constitution for the welfare of SC / ST people in every sphere of their life. There are a number of SC / ST people suffering from cancer and other malignant diseases who are unable to carry forward the treatment of their diseases due to the economic hardships faced by them. The government has decided to bring some help to such patients by making available some financial assistance in the form of grants in Aid to SC/ST patients suffering from cancer and other malignant diseases.

SC / ST patients who are suffering from cancer and other malignant diseases are unable to afford advanced treatment due to their Socioeconomic condition prevalent in the community. So financial assistance in the form of grants in Aid is proposed to be provided to such patients through a scheme namely "Grants in Aid to SC / ST patients suffering from cancer and other malignant diseases". Under the scheme, such SC / ST patients will be given a one-time, fixed financial assistance of Rs. 50,000/- (Rupees Fifty thousand) only.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1)	The applicant must belong to an SC / ST community of Assam.
2)	Person suffering from cancer or other malignant diseases may apply.
3)	No person shall be eligible to claim this financial aid more than once.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
1)	Caste Certificate in prescribed Performa of applicant suffering from Cancer or other malignant diseases issued by the concerned authority.
2)	Medical certificate of the applicant and relevant documents.
3)	Passport size Photograph of the applicant
4)	Bank Account No. along with IFSC of the applicant.(copy of Front Page of Bank Pass Book )

content.section_title: FAQ: What Is The Main Aim Of This Scheme?
content.text: The aim of the scheme is to provide financial assistance to those poor scheduled caste patient who are suffering from TB/Cancer and other malignant diseases and unable to bear the cost of medicines for their treatment.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who Can Apply For This Scheme?
content.text: The applicant must belong to a SC / ST community of Assam.

content.section_title: FAQ: How Many Times A Person Can Claim For This Financial Aid?
content.text: No person shall be eligible to claim this financial aid more than once.

content.section_title: FAQ: To Whom The Preference Is Given While Selecting The Beneficiaries?
content.text: While selecting beneficiaries preference is given to Widows / aged / unmarried persons / physically handicapped / BPL families.

content.section_title: FAQ: From Where Applicants Get To Know About The Application?
content.text: Applicants can visit the concerned department and get an Application form offline. Applications can be made online as and when the Official Website of the Directorate of Welfare of Scheduled Castes, Assam, / Directorate of Welfare of Plain Tribe and Backward Classes, Assam starts to be fully functional.

title: Grants To Patients Suffering From Tuberculosis/Cancer And Other Major Diseases
topic: Grants To Patients Suffering From Tuberculosis/Cancer And Other Major Diseases

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance for medical treatment to patients suffering from Tuberculosis, Cancer, and other major diseases. It provides a one-time financial grant to patients of the Tea Tribes and Adivasi community.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The scheme "Grants To Patients Suffering From Tuberculosis/Cancer And Other Major Diseases" was launched by the Tea Tribes Welfare Department, Government of Assam. The scheme aims to provide financial assistance in the form of a one-time grant to patients belonging to the Tea Tribes and Adivasi community who are undergoing medical treatment. The applicant must be a permanent resident of Assam belonging to the Tea Tribes and Adivasi Community. The applications for this scheme are accepted online.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: Financial assistance (one-time grant) to patients undergoing medical treatment.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
- The applicant must be a permanent resident of Assam.
- The applicant must belong to the Tea Tribes and Adivasi community.
- The applicant's family income must not exceed ₹5,00,000/- per annum.
- The applicant should not be a Government Service holder.

- The applicant must be a patient undergoing medical treatment for specified diseases such as Eye Diseases, Tuberculosis, Eye Cataract, Accident, Auto Immune Diseases, Liver Diseases, Nerve Disorder, Cancer, Kidney Diseases, Dialysis, Major Operations, Bone Diseases like Rheumatoid Arthritis, Bone Fractures, Elephantiasis, Heart Diseases, and Strokes.
- The applicant must not hold any position under Government/Semi Government Authorities/Boards.
- The applicant must not be a member of Panchayati Raj Institutions.

_*Preference should be given to widow/aged/unmarried women/physically handicapped/Below Poverty Line (BPL) families._

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
##### ***Step 1:*** ***Portal Access and Scheme Selection***
**A. Navigating to the Portal:** The applicant must first open the official Tea Tribes Welfare Department URL, www.ttwd.assam.gov.in, and then click the "Online Application" link to be redirected to the [SIRISH application portal](https://sirishassam.in/).
**B. Initial Login or Registration:** New users must register themselves on the online portal, while existing users will log in using the username and password created during prior registration.
**C. Selecting the Scheme:** Once the applicant is logged into the Dashboard, they must select the appropriate scholarship scheme from the dropdown menu available.
##### ***Step 2:** **** *** **Entering Personal Details**
**A. Inputting Mandatory Data:** Enter "Personal Details" where all required information in the application form must be accurately provided.
**B. Specifying Location Details:** Location data, including District, Assembly Constituency, and Sub-Division, must be selected, along with other essential address details like Village/Town.
**C. Saving Personal Information:** After filling in all necessary fields in this step, click on the "Save Form Details" button to proceed further with the application.
##### ***Step 3:** **** *** **Document Upload**
**A. Uploading Required Files:** Under the "Upload Documents" tab, upload all required supporting documents in the specified image formats (e.g., JPG/JPEG).
**B. Document Formatting Check:** All uploaded documents must adhere to specified maximum file sizes (e.g., photo maximum 50 KB, other documents generally 100 KB) before they can be saved.
##### ***Step 4:** **** *** **Updating Bank Details**
**A. Providing Account Information:** Enter the Bank Account Number and the name of the bank under the "Update Bank Details" tab.
**B. Entering Branch Identification:** Details essential for electronic transfer, specifically the Bank Branch Name and the corresponding IFSC Code, must be accurately provided.
**C. Securing Financial Data:** Once all bank details have been correctly entered, click on the "Save Bank Details" button to confirm the information.
##### ***Step 5:** **** *** **Preview and Final Submission**
**A. Application Review:** "Preview Application" displays all details entered in the form for a thorough and final check.
**B. Final Confirmation:** After cross-checking all information and uploaded documents for accuracy, the applicant must confirm the application by clicking the "Proceed with Submit Application" button.
**C. Tracking Status:** Upon successful submission, an application number will be visible on the screen, allowing the applicant to view the status under the "APPLICATION STATUS" tab.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Passport Size Photo (JPG/JPEG Format Maximum 50kb).
- Signature (JPG/JPEG Format Maximum 20kb).
- Aadhaar Card (JPG/JPEG Format Maximum 100kb).
- Self Declaration Undertaking On Annual Income (JPG/JPEG Format Maximum 100kb).
- Original Copy Of Caste Certificate (JPG/JPEG Format Maximum 100kb).
- First Page Of Applicant’s Bank Passbook (JPG/JPEG Format Maximum 100kb).
- Medical Reports Specifying The Nature Of Disease The Person Is Suffering From Issued By A Registered Medical Practitioner/Doctor Under Whom The Patient Is Undergoing Treatment.
- Recommendation From Local Member Of Legislative Assembly/Member Of Parliament Chairman/Sub-Divisional Beneficiary Selection Committee (Preferable) (JPG/JPEG Format Maximum 100kb).
- Certificate From The Hospital/Nursing Home Where Undergoing Treatment (PDF Maximum 1MB). *This document serves as the Medical Certificate from the Government Doctor not below the rank of Sub-Divisional Medical Officer or from the Doctor of the Nursing Home/Hospital wherein the patient is undergoing treatment, confirming the disease of the patient.

content.section_title: FAQ: Must applicants be permanent residents of Assam and belong to the specified Tea Tribes and Adivasi Community to qualify for this particular medical support scheme?
content.text: The candidate must be a permanent resident of Assam and must belong to the Tea Tribes and Adivasi Community.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are applicants required to secure a medical certificate specifically from a government doctor of a high rank, such as a Sub-Divisional Medical Officer, or can a private hospital doctor provide certification?
content.text: The medical certificate must be from a Government Doctor, not below the rank of Sub-Divisional Medical Officer (SDMO), or from the Doctor of the Nursing Home/Hospital where the patient is undergoing treatment, confirming the disease of the patient.

content.section_title: FAQ: What kind of medical reports should be submitted to the Directorate, and who must issue these documents to ensure their validity?
content.text: Medical reports must specify the nature of the disease the person is suffering from and must be issued by a registered Medical Practitioner/Doctor under whom the patient is undergoing treatment.

content.section_title: FAQ: If an individual is currently receiving treatment for Tuberculosis or has been diagnosed with Cancer, does their condition make them eligible for this grant?
content.text: Yes, patients undergoing medical treatment for Tuberculosis (T.B.) or Cancer are among those eligible for this one-time grant.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is it a mandatory requirement for applicants to provide a recommendation letter issued by their local Member of Legislative Assembly or Member of Parliament as part of the application process?
content.text: Yes, a recommendation letter from the local Member of Legislative Assembly (MLA)/Member of Parliament (MP) on the disease and the need for financial assistance should also be submitted.

content.section_title: FAQ: If I require treatment for conditions affecting my renal system, such as chronic Kidney Diseases or ongoing Dialysis treatments, does this scheme cover those costs?
content.text: Yes, financial assistance is provided for medical treatment connected with Kidney Diseases and Dialysis.

content.section_title: FAQ: For patients seeking assistance for serious cardiovascular conditions, does this scheme cover treatments related to Heart Diseases or medical events like Strokes?
content.text: Yes, financial assistance is provided for medical treatment connected with Heart Diseases and Strokes.

content.section_title: FAQ: Does this assistance program only offer a single payment, meaning I can only receive the financial support one time across all years of the scheme's operation?
content.text: Yes, the grant is provided in the form of a one-time grant, meaning one applicant is eligible for the grant only once under this scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: If an applicant is already employed and holds a position within a Government or Semi Government Authority, are they still permitted to apply for this grant?
content.text: No, the applicant should not hold any position under Government/Semi Government Authorities/Boards, nor be a member of Panchayati Raj Institutions, or a member of the Selection Committee, etc.

content.section_title: FAQ: What specific preference is given during the beneficiary selection process for vulnerable groups such as aged individuals or families categorized as Below Poverty Line?
content.text: Preference is given while selecting beneficiaries who are widow, aged, unmarried women, physically handicapped, or belong to Below Poverty Line (BPL) families.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the maximum annual family income limit that applicants must adhere to when submitting their applications for this financial assistance program?
content.text: The applicant must submit an undertaking that the annual family income does not exceed ₹5,00,000/- (Rupees five lakh) per annum.

content.section_title: FAQ: Does the financial assistance extend to serious Bone Diseases such as Rheumatoid Arthritis or treatments for complex injuries like Bone Fractures or Elephantiasis?
content.text: Yes, financial assistance is provided for medical treatment connected with Bone Diseases like Rheumatoid Arthritis, Bone Fractures, and Elephantiasis.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are patients requiring medical intervention for conditions like Eye Diseases or requiring surgery for an Eye Cataract covered under the provisions of this scheme?
content.text: Yes, patients undergoing medical treatment connected with Eye Diseases and Eye Cataract are covered under this scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: In addition to general medical documentation, must an applicant also provide a valid Caste certificate issued and certified by the District Commissioner or a designated officer?
content.text: Yes, the applicant must produce a valid Caste certificate which is duly certified or issued by the District Commissioner of the concerned district or his/her designated officer.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: Registered construction workers having continuous membership of the Board can avail an amount of ₹5,000/- once in a year for his/ her annual health checkup in any Hospital.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The “Health Check-up” benefit scheme was started by the Assam Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (A.B.O.C.W.W.B), Labour Welfare Department, Government of Assam. Under this scheme, financial assistance will be provided annually to registered construction workers with continuous Board membership for their annual health check-up at any hospital.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: 1. Under this scheme, registered construction workers can avail an amount of ₹5,000/- once in a year for his/her annual health check-up in any Hospital.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Assam.
1. The applicant should be engaged in any building & other construction work.
1. The construction worker should be registered with the Assam Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
1. The registered construction workers having continuous membership of the Board are eligible to apply under the scheme.
1. The registered construction workers can avail benefits once in a year for his/her annual health check-up in any Hospital.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
> **Registration Process under Assam Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board:**

**Step 01:** The eligible building & other construction workers can submit an online application through the official website: [https://abocwwb.assam.gov.in/](https://abocwwb.assam.gov.in/)
**Step 02:** On the home page, click on ‘[Registration Page for New Worker](https://abocwwb.assam.gov.in/index.php/health-cheackup-form)’ and a registration page will open. Fill in all the mandatory details and upload all the required documents.
**Step 03:** After filling out the form, a payment option shall then prompt to allow the applicant to make the payment using UPI-based modes/Cards/net banking or even Challan.
> **Post-Registration Process:**

**Step 01:** The registering officer shall be notified of the applications successfully submitted and the Registering officer shall scrutinize the applications.
**Step 02:** The Registering Officer shall arrange for a scrutiny committee meeting every 15 days and place all applications received.
**Step 03:** The committee may recommend for approval, reject, or query the application. In case of queries, an SMS shall be sent to the applicant with a brief on the query. The applicant may re-upload the requisite documents as per the query raised by the Registering officer.
> **Notification to the worker through SMS; ID generation:**

- Applications if approved shall be notified through SMS and ID card shall be generated automatically for approved applications.
- The applicant shall be notified for each stage such as queries, rejection, and allied.

> **Application Process to apply under the scheme:**

**Step 01:** Only active registered construction workers may login in through ID No./registered phone number and apply through the portal. [https://abocwwb.assam.gov.in/](https://abocwwb.assam.gov.in/)
**Step 02:** On the home page of the portal, click on ‘[Login Now](https://abocwwb.assam.gov.in/index.php/Base/userLogin)’ and enter the Mobile Number or User ID. OTP received on the registered mobile number should be entered to complete the login.
**Step 03:** The individual console of the applicant gets opened with various functions such as detail updating, Fees due, and benefits eligible.
**Step 04:** Click on ‘Benefit’ and select the applicable scheme under the benefit and the portal redirects to an online application form specific to the scheme.
**Step 05:** The applicant may fill up the application with all mandatory fields and upload the necessary documents.
**Step 06:** The applicant submits the application form and a tracking/reference number is generated as a future reference for the applicant.
> **Post-Application Process:**

**Step 01:** Successfully submitted applications pop up in the concerned Officers console. The officer may assign a subordinate to make verifications through the portal.
**Step 02:** Subsequent to preliminary verification, the application is placed in the scrutiny committee for detailed checks.
**Step 03:** Minutes of the Scrutiny Committee meeting get uploaded and Forwarded to the H.O.
**Step 04:** Applications/ Pops up in the Officer’s Console of Head Office. Officer assigns to the Assistant concerned and allotted with the District concerned.
**Step 05:** The assistant processes the application through PFMS. Account details get auto-verified in PFMS.
**Step 06:** On successful PFMS verification, Print Advice is generated through PFMS.
**Step 07:** Approving authority approves for disbursement of the benefit through PFMS.
([User Manual](https://labourcommissioner.assam.gov.in/sites/default/files/swf_utility_folder/departments/coi_labour_uneecopscloud_com_oid_14/portlet/level_2/sop_bocw_online_mode_for_uploading.pdf))

content.section_title: Application Process (Online - via CSC)
content.text:
- Workers may approach the CSCs (VLE) for requisite assistance for registration application and allied. - CSCs (VLEs) shall be assistors to the applicant.
- Applicants can approach the nearest VLE for required assistance.

**Note 01:** Services of CSCs engaged for assistance to Registered construction workers for registration and renewal only shall be free for the applicants. No fee/gratitude/rebates must be paid to the VLEs by Construction workers towards the services rendered.
**Note 02:** VLEs may charge only for ID card printing if workers desire to acquire a printed Card. The rate for the same shall be notified by the Board and displayed at CSCs.
**Note 03:** In case VLEs assistances are utilized, VLEs shall ensure recording reference/tracking numbers at their end and also providing the same to the worker.
**Note 04:** A worker can directly check for the status of an application or make telephonic inquiries to the concerned VLE by providing a tracking ID. VLEs shall ensure telephone numbers are displayed in centers at all times.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
Documents required for Registration under A.B.O.C.W.W.B:- Passport size photograph (Applicant and Nominee)
- Proof of Age/DOB
- Copy of Aadhaar Card
- Copy of Voter ID Card
- Bank passbook first page
- Experience Certificate/Employers Certificate (90 days working record to check eligibility)
- Completely filled Nomination Form
- Completely filled Registration Form
- Any other relevant documents
Documents required for Grant of Scheme:- ABOCWWB ID Card
- Self-Attested Copy of ID Card
- Copy of Payslip of Account Pay book Showing Monthly Contribution Deposited
- Copy of Bank Account Passbook
- Any other relevant documents

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the Health Check-up Benefit Scheme by A.B.O.C.W.W.B?
content.text: The Health Check-up Benefit Scheme is an initiative by the Assam Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (A.B.O.C.W.W.B), under the Labour Welfare Department, Government of Assam. It provides financial assistance to registered construction workers for their annual health check-up.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible for the scheme?
content.text: Registered construction workers with continuous membership in the A.B.O.C.W.W.B are eligible for the Health Check-up Benefit Scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the benefits under this scheme?
content.text: Eligible workers can avail an amount of [?]5,000/- once a year for their annual health check-up at any hospital.

content.section_title: FAQ: How often can a registered construction worker claim the benefit?
content.text: The benefit can be claimed annually, allowing registered workers to undergo a health check-up once every year.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can the financial assistance be utilized only at specific hospitals?
content.text: No, the financial assistance can be utilized at any hospital for the annual health check-up of the registered construction worker.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the residency requirements for workers?
content.text: Workers must be permanent residents of Assam to be eligible for the Scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can a worker apply for the scheme if they are registered with another welfare board in a different state?
content.text: No, applicants must be registered specifically with the Assam Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board to be eligible for the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: How to apply under the scheme?
content.text: The eligible beneficiaries may apply through the portal. https://abocwwb.assam.gov.in/

title: Health Benefits for Pregnant Mothers and Newborns through Supraja-AYUSH
topic: Health Benefits for Pregnant Mothers and Newborns through Supraja-AYUSH

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to promote the health and well-being of pregnant women and newborns by providing Ayurvedic-based healthcare services and treatment.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The “Health Benefits for Pregnant Mothers and Newborns through Supraja-AYUSH” is implemented by the Ayush Department, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to promote the health and well-being of pregnant women and newborns by providing Ayurvedic-based healthcare services and treatment.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
- Health benefits are provided to pregnant mothers and newborns through Ayurvedic treatment.
- Medicines are dispensed as per the individual’s medical condition.
- An OPD fee of ₹10/- per person is charged for availing the services.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text: 1. The beneficiary must be a pregnant woman or a newborn belonging to Uttarakhand.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text: The beneficiaries can visit and register for treatment at any of the three Ayurvedic colleges (Ayurvedic University Campus, Rishikul, and Gurukul) in Uttarakhand to avail the benefits under the scheme.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Identity Proof
- Other documents as required

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible to avail benefits under this scheme?
content.text: Pregnant women and newborns who are residents of Uttarakhand are eligible to avail the benefits under this scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where can beneficiaries avail treatment under this scheme?
content.text: Beneficiaries can visit and register for treatment at any of the three Ayurvedic Colleges in Uttarakhand (Ayurvedic University Campus, Rishikul, and Gurukul).

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any fee for availing treatment under the scheme?
content.text: Yes, a nominal OPD fee of ₹10 per person is charged for availing the services.

content.section_title: FAQ: What type of treatment is provided under this scheme?
content.text: The scheme offers Ayurvedic-based diagnosis, treatment, and care for pregnant women and newborns.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are medicines provided under the scheme?
content.text: Yes. Ayurvedic medicines are dispensed to beneficiaries based on their specific medical condition.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The Journalist's Medical Welfare Scheme was first implemented in 2012 by the Government of Assam. Under this scheme, one-time financial assistance is provided to the accredited and recognized journalists of Assam for their medical treatment.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
In consideration of the long pending demands of the journalists in the State for their welfare etc. the State Govt. has considered to introduce a new scheme viz. “Journalist Medical Welfare Scheme” for financial assistance to the Accredited Journalist as identified by the Director of Information & Public Relations (DIPR) Assam under the Rule of the Assam Press Correspondents (Accredited/ Recognitions) Rules, 1989 for Medical treatment of such journalists who suffer from:-

1.	 Complicated ailments needing long-term, highly expensive treatment/involving the transplant of organs/complicated surgeries/long-term medical treatments, e.g. cardiological/ nephrological/ oncological/ neurological/ gastroenterogical/ endoerinological etc. in nature.
2.	Complicated surgical treatments arising out of injury caused by accidents etc. including the cost artificial replacements of etc., as and where required.
3.	Incurable chronic ailments requiring life-saving devices/treatment etc.
4.	Any other serious health-related problems that may be Medically Diagnosed to be of life-threatening nature. For this purpose the State Govt. have introduced a new Head of Accounts in the Annual Budget Estimates from the financial year 2009-10 and the fund provided under this Head of Account shall be administered by a committee to be constituted by the Information & Public Relations Department with the following members and the D.D.O. powers shall be delegated to the DIPR, Assam.

Under this scheme, one-time financial assistance is provided to the accredited and recognized journalists of Assam for their medical treatment. This scheme covers the medical requirements of the journalist as well as their dependent family members. This financial assistance is extended out of the interest accrued from the corpus fund of the scheme.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant should be a citizen of India.
1. The applicant should be an ordinary resident of Assam.
1. The applicant must be a journalist who is formally accredited / recognised by the DIPR and fulfil all the norms required for the said accreditation/ recognition.

**General Note:** Grant of financial assistance to any working journalist is not a matter of right. Assistance would be extended depending on the Committees satisfaction as to the eligibility/ merits of the cases and the availability of the financial resources for the purpose. The Committee reserves the right to reject or accept any application without assigning any reasons thereof.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Attested or self-attested copy of Identity Card (DIPR),
- Identity Card of Present Organization,
- Bank details like Passbook (Front page) & Cancelled Cheque.
- Medical documents like Admission Certificates, Discharge Certificates, Prescriptions, Bills/Vouchers, Clinical Reports, etc.
- A copy of his/her PAN Card/Aadhar card.
- Other certificates and evidence regarding the current financial situation

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the benefit of this scheme?
content.text: Under this Scheme, one-time financial assistance is to be provided to the Accredited and Recognized Journalist of Assam for treatment.

content.section_title: FAQ: What does this scheme also covers?
content.text: The Scheme also covers dependent family members of the journalist.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is recognized as an Accredited Journalist?
content.text: Accredited Journalist as identified by the Director of Information & Public Relations (DIPR) Assam under the Rule of the Assam Press Correspondents (Accredited/ Recognitions) Rules, 1989.

content.section_title: FAQ: How is the financial assistance extended?
content.text: The financial assistance is extended out of the interest accrued from the Corpus fund.

content.section_title: FAQ: How does the Committee decide upon the fund to be made available for an applicant?
content.text: The Committee may decide upon the fund to be made available per applicant depending upon the nature and severity of the ailments/ disease.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can apply for this scheme?
content.text: A journalist of Assam who is formally accredited/recognized by the DIPR and fulfill all the norms required for the said accreditation/ recognition.

content.section_title: FAQ: How will payment be made to the beneficiaries under this scheme?
content.text: Payment to the beneficiaries will be made out of the interest income earned against the corpus fund. Interest income will be credited to Savings /A/C to be maintained for the purpose.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is this scheme only for a journalist of Assam?
content.text: Yes, a journalist, who is formally accredited/recognized by the DIPR and fulfills all the norms required for the said accreditation/recognition, and dependent family members of the journalist, is eligible for the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: How will payment be decided to the beneficiary under this scheme?
content.text: The payments will be decided by the Committee and shall be made in the form of Cheques to be issued by the DIPR as the fund for the purpose shall be maintained in the Savings Account.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can I apply under this scheme?
content.text: Application for grant of financial assistance shall be submitted to the Convener of the Committee (Director, DIPR) in the prescribed form.

content.section_title: FAQ: How is the decision taken in cases of urgency/emergency?
content.text: In cases of urgency/emergency, decisions may be taken with the approval of the Chairperson and reported to the next meeting of the Committee for its approval.

content.section_title: FAQ: How shall the financial assistance be provided?
content.text: Financial assistance shall be provided on the date of issue of the order sanctioning the assistance unless otherwise specified therein.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: KCR Nutrition Kits Scheme is a welfare initiative launched by the Government of Telangana, India, aimed at providing nutritious food to pregnant. The primary objective of the scheme is to improve the nutritional status of pregnant women.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
Telangana Government launched yet another pioneering initiative ‘KCR Nutrition Kits’, which is aimed at reducing Anemia and improving hemoglobin levels in pregnant women. Each kit contains a kg of Nutritional Mix Powder, a kg of Dates (Khajoor), three bottles of Iron Syrup, 500 grams of Ghee, and a Cup. The kits were distributed to pregnant women in nine districts of Telangana where the prevalence of anemia was high. The programme was launched by Health Minister T Harish Rao at Kamareddy on 21st December 2022.

**Objective:** The primary objective of the scheme is to improve the nutritional status of pregnant and lactating women and young children in Telangana.

**Beneficiaries:** The scheme aims to cover all pregnant in Telangana. The beneficiaries will receive a kit containing essential food items every month.

The scheme is implemented by the Department of Women Development and Child Welfare, Government of Telangana.

Overall, the KCR Nutrition Kits Scheme is an important initiative aimed at improving the nutritional status of pregnant in Telangana. The scheme provides essential food items that can help in meeting the nutritional requirements of these vulnerable groups.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
The KCR Nutrition Kits Scheme in Telangana provides several benefits to the beneficiaries. Here are some of the key benefits of the scheme:
**•	To provide quality nutrition throughout pregnancy and post-delivery.**
**•	To reduce Anaemia and improve hemoglobin levels in pregnant women.**
**•	To reduce Maternal Mortality Rate and Infant Mortality Rate**

***Improved Nutritional Status:*** The primary objective of the scheme is to improve the nutritional status of pregnant women in Telangana. The nutrition kit provided to the beneficiaries contains essential food items that can help in meeting their daily nutritional requirements.

***Reduced Malnutrition:*** Malnutrition is a major problem among pregnant in Telangana. The KCR Nutrition Kits Scheme can help in reducing malnutrition by providing nutritious food to the beneficiaries every month.

***Improved Health:*** Good nutrition is essential for good health. The KCR Nutrition Kits Scheme can help in improving the health of pregnant women in Telangana by providing them with essential nutrients.

***Increased Awareness:*** The scheme can also help in increasing awareness about the importance of good nutrition among the beneficiaries. The Anganwadi workers can educate the beneficiaries about the importance of good nutrition and how to make the most of the food items provided in the kit.

***Empowerment of Women:*** The scheme can also help in empowering women by providing them with the necessary resources to take care of their health and the health of their children. The nutrition kit provided under the scheme can help in reducing the financial burden on women and enable them to make better decisions about their health and nutrition.

Overall, the KCR Nutrition Kits Scheme in Telangana has several benefits for the beneficiaries. It can help in improving their nutritional status, reducing malnutrition, improving health, increasing awareness, and empowering women.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
Pregnant Women: Pregnant women are eligible to receive the nutrition kit under the scheme. The scheme aims to provide support to pregnant women to ensure that they have access to essential nutrients required for a healthy pregnancy.

Age: Minimum 18
Gender: Female
Residence Type: Both

**Resident of Telangana:** The scheme is applicable only to the residents of Telangana. Non-residents of the state are not eligible to receive the nutrition kit.

**Economically Weaker Sections:** The scheme is designed to provide support to the economically weaker sections of society. Families with a monthly income of less than Rs. 10,000 are eligible to receive the nutrition kit.

**Identification by Anganwadi Worker:** The beneficiaries of the scheme are identified by the Anganwadi workers. The workers conduct surveys and identify the eligible beneficiaries based on the eligibility criteria mentioned above.

content.section_title: Exclusions
content.text:
•	If beneficiaries have more than two live children.
•	If the beneficiary has taken treatment from non-government hospitals (eg: private hospitals)
•	If the beneficiary’s Aadhaar card does not belong to the Telangana state

**Non-residents of Telangana:** The scheme is applicable only to the residents of Telangana. Non-residents of the state are not eligible to receive the nutrition kit.

**Families with higher income:** The scheme is designed to provide support to the economically weaker sections of society. Families with higher incomes may not be eligible to receive the nutrition kit.

**Families with malnourished children:** Families with malnourished children are not excluded from the scheme, but they may require additional support beyond the nutrition kit. The government may provide additional support to such families through other schemes.

**Families with disabilities:** The scheme is designed to support pregnant women. Families with disabled members may require additional support beyond the nutrition kit. The government may provide additional support to such families through other schemes.

Overall, the KCR Nutrition Kit Scheme in Telangana has certain exclusions to ensure that the resources are utilized efficiently and effectively to provide support to the most vulnerable sections of society.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text: Identification may be done by Anganwadi Workers: The beneficiaries of the scheme are identified by the Anganwadi workers. The workers conduct surveys and identify the eligible beneficiaries based on the eligibility criteria mentioned.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text: 1.	Aadhaar Card

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the KCR Nutrition Kit Scheme?
content.text: The KCR Nutrition Kit Scheme is a welfare initiative launched by the Government of Telangana in India to provide nutritional support to pregnant women, who belong to economically weaker sections of society.

content.section_title: FAQ: What does the nutrition kit contain?
content.text: Each kit contains a kg of Nutritional Mix Powder, a kg of Dates (Khajoor), three bottles of Iron Syrup, 500 grams of Ghee, and a Cup. The nutrition kit may also contain essential food items such as lentils, millets, and other nutritious food items that can help in meeting the daily nutritional requirements of pregnant women.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible to receive the nutrition kit?
content.text: Pregnant women, who belong to economically weaker sections of society and are residents of Telangana are eligible to receive the nutrition kit.

content.section_title: FAQ: How often is the nutrition kit distributed?
content.text: The nutrition kit is distributed on a monthly basis to the eligible beneficiaries.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can families with higher income also receive the nutrition kit?
content.text: The scheme is designed to provide support to the economically weaker sections of society. Families with higher incomes may not be eligible to receive the nutrition kit.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any cost associated with the scheme?
content.text: No, the scheme is free of cost for eligible beneficiaries.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to aged and needy Goan artists and their dependent family members for medical treatment expenses. It covers major or minor surgical treatment costs in local or outstation hospitals, including travelling and accommodation expenses.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The scheme "Kalakar Kritadnyata Nidhi" was launched by the Directorate of Art & Culture, Government of Goa. The "Medical Treatment" component of the scheme aims to provide financial assistance to aged and needy Goan artists and their dependent family members in indigent circumstances to meet expenses towards major or minor surgical treatment in local or outstation hospitals, including travelling and accommodation expenses outside the state for treatment. The scheme is implemented by the Directorate of Art & Culture. The applications for this scheme are accepted Offline.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
##### ***Financial Assistance***

- Financial assistance shall be sanctioned to meet the expenses towards major or minor surgical treatment in local or outstation hospitals.
- Financial assistance shall be provided to meet the expenses towards travelling and accommodation outside the state for treatment for self and or wholly dependent family members.
- The maximum financial assistance under this category shall not exceed ₹50,000/-.
- The Government may relax the ceiling in case of major chronic ailments.

##### ***Disbursement***

- The financial assistance amount shall be disbursed to the eligible applicant in the form of Electronic Clearing System mode.
- The disbursement shall be one-time financial assistance.

##### ***Conditions***

- The beneficiary shall not avail the benefit of medical reimbursement from any other Government department or under any schemes or policies for the medical treatment for which the financial assistance is granted under this scheme.
- The details of the expenditure incurred along with the vouchers shall be furnished to the department after availing the treatment.
- The unutilized amount out of the sanctioned amount if any shall be refunded to the government within 3 months from completion of the treatment.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
- The applicant must be any Goan aged needy artist, born and brought up in the state of Goa.
- The applicant must be recognized by the Directorate of Art & Culture, Government of Goa.
- The applicant must have an annual family income from all sources not exceeding ₹2,00,000/- per annum.
- The applicant must be not less than 50 years of age.

**Relaxation**
The Government may relax the age criteria for deserving cases under special circumstances.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Step 1:** Visit the office of the Directorate of Art & Culture, Sanskruti Bhavan, Patto, Panaji, Goa, and collect the prescribed application form for the scheme.
**Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
**Step 3:** Submit the completed application form along with all required documents directly to the ***Directorate of Art & Culture, Sanskruti Bhavan, Patto, Panaji, Goa.***

##### ***Post-Application Processes***
**Step 1:** The Directorate shall forward the application to the sub-committee for scrutinizing the application thoroughly. The Director shall give its recommendations to the sub-committee.
**Step 2:** The sub-committee constituted by the Government shall meet as and when required and shall consider the applications received for this purpose. The committee shall recommend the names of the artists which are considered fit by the sub-committee to avail the benefits under this scheme. The committee shall decide the quantum of assistance to be released to the needy artist.
**Step 3:** After the committee decides on the quantum of assistance, the approval from the Government shall be obtained for disbursement of the financial assistance.
**Step 4:** After obtaining Government approval, the Director shall disburse the one time financial assistance amount to the eligible applicant in the form of Electronic Clearing System mode to the bank account.
**Step 5:** After availing the treatment, the beneficiary shall furnish the details of the expenditure incurred along with the vouchers to the department within 3 months from completion of the treatment.
_*If there is any unutilized amount out of the sanctioned amount, the same shall be refunded to the government within 3 months from completion of the treatment._

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
During Application- Birth Certificate
- Marriage Certificate (if applicable for family members)
- Dependency Certificate (for dependent family members)
- Income Certificate
- Medical Certificate (issued by the authorized doctor or hospital)
- Estimate of Expenditure from the Authorized Doctor or Hospital. (However, in case of treatment availed or to be availed on emergency basis, the estimate shall be submitted subsequently)
- Recognition Certificate (from the Directorate of Art & Culture, Government of Goa)
After Treatment- Details of the Expenditure Incurred along with the Vouchers.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the maximum financial assistance I can receive for my medical treatment expenses under this component?
content.text: The maximum financial assistance available is ₹50,000/-. However, the Government may relax this ceiling in case of major chronic ailments requiring higher expenditure.

content.section_title: FAQ: If I need emergency treatment and cannot get an estimate beforehand, will my application still be considered valid?
content.text: Yes, your application will be considered valid. In case of treatment availed or to be availed on emergency basis, the estimate from the authorized doctor or hospital can be submitted subsequently.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can I apply for financial assistance to cover the medical treatment costs of my wholly dependent family members?
content.text: Yes, financial assistance is provided to meet expenses towards treatment for self and or wholly dependent family members, including travelling and accommodation expenses outside the state.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the minimum age requirement that I must fulfill to be eligible for applying for financial assistance?
content.text: The age limit for grant of benefits is not less than 50 years. However, the Government may relax the age criteria for deserving cases under special circumstances.

content.section_title: FAQ: After I receive the financial assistance and complete my treatment, what documentation do I need to submit to the department?
content.text: You must furnish the details of the expenditure incurred along with the vouchers to the department within 3 months from completion of the treatment.

content.section_title: FAQ: What should I do with any remaining amount if I do not utilize the entire sanctioned financial assistance for my treatment?
content.text: Any unutilized amount out of the sanctioned amount must be refunded to the government within 3 months from completion of the treatment.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can I claim reimbursement from another government department if I have already received financial assistance for the same medical treatment?
content.text: No, you cannot avail the benefit of medical reimbursement from any other Government department or under any schemes or policies for the medical treatment for which financial assistance is granted.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the annual family income limit that I must not exceed to qualify for this financial assistance?
content.text: Your annual family income from all sources should not exceed ₹2,00,000/- per annum to be eligible for the financial assistance under this component.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where can I obtain and submit the application form for seeking financial assistance for medical treatment expenses?
content.text: You can obtain and submit the prescribed application form directly at the Directorate of Art & Culture, Sanskruti Bhavan, Patto, Panaji, Goa during working days.

content.section_title: FAQ: Does the financial assistance cover expenses for treatment only in local hospitals or can I seek treatment outside the state?
content.text: The financial assistance covers major or minor surgical treatment in local or outstation hospitals, and also includes expenses towards travelling and accommodation outside the state for treatment.

content.section_title: FAQ: What type of recognition from authorities do I need to have to be considered eligible for this financial assistance?
content.text: You must be recognized by the Directorate of Art & Culture, Government of Goa, and you should be a Goan aged needy artist, born and brought up in the state of Goa.

content.section_title: FAQ: How will the approved financial assistance amount be transferred to me after the government approval is obtained for my application?
content.text: After obtaining Government approval, the Director of Art & Culture shall disburse the one time financial assistance amount in the form of Electronic Clearing System mode to your bank account.

content.section_title: FAQ: Besides the medical estimate, what other certificates and documents must I attach with my application to substantiate my eligibility?
content.text: You must attach birth certificate, marriage certificate (if applicable), dependency certificate (for family members), income certificate, medical certificate, and recognition certificate from the Directorate of Art & Culture, Government of Goa.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can apply on my behalf if I am unable to submit the application myself for the financial assistance?
content.text: Eligible artist or the family or co-artist or any member of the public can apply in prescribed proforma directly to the Directorate of Art & Culture on your behalf.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The "Financial Assistance for Medical" scheme by the JKBOCW Welfare Board, Labour Department, Jammu & Kashmir, provides financial support for medical expenses, including injury treatment, temporary and permanent disability, and the treatment of chronic diseases.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The scheme "Financial Assistance for Medical" is a scheme by the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Labour Department, Jammu & Kashmir. The scheme provides financial aid to registered workers' children for various medical needs, provided the worker has been engaged in building or construction work for at least 90 days in the preceding 12 months. Under this scheme, workers can receive ₹5,000/- for injury treatment, ₹10,000/- for temporary disability, ₹75,000/- for permanent disability, and up to ₹1,00,000/- for the treatment of chronic diseases.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
- Financial Assistance in the Case of Injury: ₹5,000/-
- Financial Assistance in the Case of Temporary Disability: ₹10,000/-
- Financial Assistance in the Case of Permanent Disability: ₹75,000/-
- Financial Assistancein the Case of Treatment of Chronic Diseases: ₹1,00,000/-

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
> **For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker -**

1. The applicant should be a resident of Jammu & Kashmir.
1. The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker.
1. The applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
1. The applicant should have engaged in any building or other construction work for at least 90 days during the preceding 12 months.
1. The applicant should be registration is renewed annually.

> **For Application to this Scheme -**

1. The applicant must be a registered member of the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of Jammu & Kashmir.
1. The applicant should be suffering from an injury, a disability, or from a chronic disease.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
***Registration -***
**Step 1:** Go to the J&K Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (JKBOCWWB) [official website.](https://www.jkbocw.gov.in/Index.aspx)
**Step 2:** On the homepage, find and click the "[Labour Registration"](https://portal.jkbocw.gov.in/MemberRegistration.aspx) link.
**Step 3:** If you're a new user, click on "Apply for New Registration."
**Step 4:** Complete the registration form with your details, submit the form, and send the OTP to your registered mobile number. Enter the OTP and click "Verify OTP & Submit."
**Step 5:** After the OTP is verified, a confirmation message with your Login ID and password will be sent to your registered mobile number.
**Step 6:** Use the Login ID, password, and captcha to log in (remember, the username and password are case-sensitive). filled are case-sensitive). Read all the instructions carefully and Click to continue
**Step 7:** Once logged in, choose the "Construction Worker’s Registration" option and click on "Registration Form."
**Step 8:** A dashboard will appear. Complete the form by entering all required details (marked with *).
**Step 9:** Upload all the important documents, such as ID proof and other relevant certificates.
**Step 10:** Once the form is completed and documents uploaded, click "Submit" and pay the application fee online. You will receive a registration number after successful payment and submission.

***Application -***
**Step 1:** Go to the [official website ](https://www.jkbocw.gov.in/Index.aspx)of the Jammu & Kashmir Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
**Step 2:** Click on ["Login,"](https://portal.jkbocw.gov.in/LoginGuest.aspx) enter your details, and sign in to your account.
**Step 3:** After logging in, click on "Scheme Registration" and select "Apply for Scheme."
**Step 4:** Choose the scheme you wish to apply for in order to avail the benefits.
**Step 5:** Enter all required information and upload the necessary documents as prompted.
**Step 6:** Finally, click on "Submit Application" to complete the process.

***Status Tracking -***
Applicant can track their application status online through the [official website](https://www.jkbocw.gov.in/Index.aspx) of the  Jammu & Kashmir Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board. Relevant updates will be sent to the registered mobile number.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
For Registration- Identity proof i.e. Aadhaar Card, Voter Card
- Passport-size photograph
- Attested work experience document
- Proof of residence
- Ration Card
- Bank account details
- Caste Certificate (If applicable)
- Proof of age
- Any other documents as required
For Application- Identity proof/ID Card of Worker
- Undertaking by applicant
- Certificate of Medical Officer of Government or Private Empanelled hospital
- Original Bill of expenditure on the treatment
- Any other documents as required

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the Financial Assistance for Medical scheme?
content.text: It provides financial aid for registered workers' medical expenses for injuries, disabilities, and chronic diseases.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can apply for this scheme?
content.text: Registered building or construction workers in Jammu & Kashmir.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the minimum duration of work required to register?
content.text: Workers must have been employed in building or construction for at least 90 days in the past year.

content.section_title: FAQ: How much assistance is provided for injury treatment?
content.text: The scheme offers [?]5,000 for injury treatment.

content.section_title: FAQ: What financial support is available for temporary disability?
content.text: [?]10,000 is provided for temporary disability.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the amount provided for permanent disability?
content.text: Financial aid of [?]75,000 is available for permanent disability.

content.section_title: FAQ: What assistance is given for chronic disease treatment?
content.text: Up to [?]1,00,000 is provided for chronic disease treatment.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there an age limit for registration?
content.text: Yes, applicants should be between 18 and 60 years.

content.section_title: FAQ: Do I need to be a resident of Jammu & Kashmir to apply?
content.text: Yes, only residents of Jammu & Kashmir are eligible.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can my children benefit from this scheme?
content.text: Yes, the scheme also covers medical aid for registered workers' children.

content.section_title: FAQ: What documents are required to apply?
content.text: Proof of registration, medical records, and relevant ID are typically required.

title: Financial Assistance for Miscarriage for the Construction Workers
topic: Financial Assistance for Miscarriage for the Construction Workers

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: "Financial Assistance for Miscarriage for the Construction Workers" by the Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Labour Department, Delhi, is a welfare scheme that provides an amount of ₹3,000/- as financial assistance in case of miscarriage.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The "Financial Assistance for Miscarriage for the Construction Workers" by the Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Labour Department, Delhi, is a welfare scheme for the registered workers of the board. Through this scheme, the female member or wife of a male member of the fund may be given ₹3,000/- as financial assistance in case of miscarriage. The said benefit shall be available for above beneficiaries from the date of their becoming members of the Fund.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: - An amount of ₹3,000/- as financial assistance in case of miscarriage to the female member or wife of a male member of the fund.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
> **For Registration:**

- The applicant should be a resident of Delhi.
- The applicant should be a Building/Construction Worker.
- The applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
- The applicant should have served the Board for at least 90 days in a given year.

> **For Application:**

- The applicant should be a registered women member Under the Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.

Or,
The applicant should be a wife of registered male member Under the Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
