- The applicant should have suffered a miscarriage during the delivery.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
> **Registration:**

**Step 1:** Visit the DBoCWWB [website ](https://dbocwwb.delhi.gov.in/cwapp/MyLoginPage)and click “Register Now”.
**Step 2:** Enter the following mandatory details: Aadhaar Card Number, Name as on Aadhar Card, Mobile Number, Date of Birth (as on Aadhar Card), Gender, etc. Create a password and confirm it. Click “Register”. You will be redirected to the registration page.
**Step 3:** On the registration page, fill in the mandatory details (Father Name, Mother Name, Photograph, Applicant’s Video, etc) and upload the mandatory documents in the specified filetype and filesize. Click “Save & Next”.
**Step 4:** On the next page, enter the mandatory details regarding your work/workplace (Previous/Current Employer Details, Proof of 90 Days Employment, etc) and upload the mandatory documents in the specified filetype and filesize. Click “Save & Next”.
**Step 5:** Click on “Add Family Members” to avail the benefits of various schemes.
**Step 6:** In the next window, add your Bank Account details.
**Step 7:** In the next window, upload your Aadhaar Card, Address Proof, Birth Proof, Employment Proof, Bank Account details etc.
**Step 8:** In the next window, the "Affidavit of the Applicant" will be displayed. Verify all the details, and click on the checkbox.
**Step 9:** Proceed for the online registration fee of ₹25/- through a Debit Card/ Internet Banking/ UPI.
**Step 10:** Enter the OTP received on your registered mobile number to apply. Note down the Application Number for future reference. Download the acknowledgement receipt.
> **Track Your Application:**

Visit the [homepage ](https://dbocwwb.delhi.gov.in/)of the website regularly and click on "Track Your Application" to check the status of your application.
> ***Application:***

**Step 1:** Visit the DBoCWWB [website ](https://dbocwwb.delhi.gov.in/cwapp/MyLoginPage)and click “Apply for Scheme”.
**Step 2:** Log in through Aadhaar Number/ Registration Card/ OTP.
**Step 3:** Apply for the “Miscarriage Assistance” scheme.

> **Helpline Number (24x1) : 011-41236600**

> **DBOCWWB Department Number (office hours: 9:30AM - 6:00PM) : 011-23813846**

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
In case in Hospital:- Miscarriage certificate (issued by medical Authority).
In case at Home:- Proof of visiting a Hospital/ Nursing Home in connection with the pregnancy in the pregnancy period.

content.section_title: FAQ: Which Department of the NCT of Delhi administers this scheme?
content.text: This scheme is administered by the Labour Department, of the National Capital Territory of Delhi.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where can I find the URL of the Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board?
content.text: The URL of the Official Website of the Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board is: https://bocw.delhi.gov.in/.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is self-attestation required for attaching documents with the application?
content.text: Yes, applicants need to self-attest the copies of mandatory documents while submitting the application.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the URL for the registration of this scheme?
content.text: The applicants needs to register through clicking on the "Register" button on DBoCWWB website: https://dbocwwb.delhi.gov.in/cwapp/MyLoginPage?ReturnUrl=%2Fcwapp%2F .

content.section_title: FAQ: In case of change, how does the worker update their registered Mobile number on the website?
content.text: The facility to amend a worker's registered mobile number on the new DBoCWWB Website is still under process. However, workers who are migrating their membership from E-District Website to the new DBoCWWB Website have an option to update/revise their mobile numbers in the migration form.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is a Construction Certificate/Labour Card?
content.text:
As an enrolled member with BoCW Welfare Board, the worker will receive a Construction Certificate popularly known as the Labour Card. A Construction Certificate/Labour Card is a document with the worker's personal details like DoB, Address, Registration Number, Date of Registration along with details of the next date of renewal.
The Construction Certificate/Labour Card is to be shown when applying for any of the claims under the BoCW Act. It is important for the worker to renew their Construction Certificate/Labour Card in order to remain active members. This renewal must be undertaken on a yearly basis.

content.section_title: FAQ: I am from another state but working as a construction worker in Delhi, can | get registered?
content.text: Yes, a Construction Worker native to any place in India, but working in Delhi can register with the DBoCWWB. The Construction Workers must own an Aadhar Card from any place in India and should have a Proof of Residence of staying in Delhi.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can members in my family also register if they are all registered construction workers?
content.text: Yes, if there are more than one member in the family who are engaged in Construction Work, they can all register under the DBoCWWB.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there a specific age requirement for construction workers to be eligible for registration?
content.text: Yes, construction workers must be at least 18 years old but not more than 60 years old to be eligible for registration under Section 12 of the Main Act.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the duration of engagement required for construction workers to qualify for registration?
content.text: Construction workers must have engaged in building or other construction work for a minimum of 90 days during the preceding 12 months to qualify for registration under Section 12 of the Main Act.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the Registration/Renewal fee?
content.text: Registration : [?] 25/-and Renewal : [?] 20/- and [?] 2/- per month (late fee).

content.section_title: FAQ: How can one track the status of my Application on the Website?
content.text: To track, the worker can use the track feature available on the website's landing page. Choose the "Type of Service' availed, i.e, whether the application was for Registration/Renewal/Claims/Amendment. In the white box, enter the application number mentioned in the acknowledgment receipt. Click Track Application now and a pop-up box will indicate the current status of the Application.

content.section_title: FAQ: What consequences a worker may face if he/she does not renew the card?
content.text: If a worker does not renew the card, he will not be eligible for the benefit in future, but with the permission of the authorized officer, the membership can be returned with the outstanding amount, though this facility will not be available more than 2 times.

title: Financial Assistance for Medical Treatment of Journalists
topic: Financial Assistance for Medical Treatment of Journalists

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The Tamil Development, Religious Endowments, and Information Department scheme was launched by the "Financial Assistance for Medical Treatment of Journalists". The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to four journalists for medical treatment from the Chief Minister’s Public Relief Fund.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The Tamil Development, Religious Endowments, and Information Department scheme was launched by the "Financial Assistance for Medical Treatment of Journalists".  Under this scheme, financial assistance was provided to four Journalists for medical treatment and to the daughter of a journalist for surgery. The total financial assistance extended under the scheme was ₹50,000/-.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: - Medical Assistant of ₹50,000/-.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The petitioner should be working as a full-time working journalist.
1. The petitioner should have work experience of at least 5 years.
1. The petitioner must apply within three months of undergoing medical treatment.
1. The annual income shall not be taken into account for obtaining medical assistance funds for journalists.
1. The treatment must be conducted in hospitals recognized by the Tamil Nadu State Hospital Organization.
1. The retired journalist receiving government pension are eligible to apply but they will receive 50% of the financial assistance.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Step 1:** The interested applicant can download the [application form](https://cms.tn.gov.in/cms_migrated/document/schemes/journalist_medical_fund.pdf) from the official website.
**Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
**Step 3:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the District Information and Public Relations Officer.
**Step 4:** Request a receipt or acknowledgment from the District Information and Public Relations Officer to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).
**NOTE 1:** If there is less space for writing details in the forms, write the details on a separate sheet of paper and attach it to the application.
**NOTE 2:** Candidate should send two completed application forms with all details duly filled and attached with relevant certificates.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Proof of employment / Copy of government order issued for receiving pension (only if receiving pension)
- Income Certificate
- Experience Certificate
- Residential Certificate - Copy of family card / Voter ID card
- Two photographs
- Treatment Certificate (A certificate from a recognized hospital regarding the disease from which the applicant is suffering)
- Tentative Cost Copy (the type of treatment/surgery required, and an approximate estimate of the cost of surgery, with the doctor's and hospital's round seal, should be attached to the application)

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the Journalists Medical Fund?
content.text: The Journalists Medical Fund provides financial assistance of up to [?]50,000/- for medical treatment to full-time working journalists in Tamil Nadu.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible to apply for this fund?
content.text: Full-time working journalists with at least 5 years of experience are eligible. This includes those in receipt of government pension, albeit at a reduced rate.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are there any income criteria to be eligible for the fund?
content.text: No, annual income is not considered for eligibility.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the application channels for journalists outside Chennai?
content.text: Journalists in districts excluding Chennai should apply through the District Information and Public Relations Officer.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there a specific time frame within which the application must be submitted?
content.text: Yes, applications must be submitted within three months of undergoing medical treatment.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can retired journalists receiving government pension apply?
content.text: Yes, retired journalists receiving government pensions can apply, but they will receive 50% of the financial assistance provided to full-time journalists.

content.section_title: FAQ: What type of documentation is required with the application?
content.text: Applicants must submit the medical treatment completion report and original medical bills.

content.section_title: FAQ: Which hospitals are covered under this scheme?
content.text: Treatment must be conducted in hospitals recognized by the Tamil Nadu State Hospital Organization, as per the regulations issued by the Department of Public Welfare and Family Welfare.

content.section_title: FAQ: What diseases are covered under this scheme?
content.text: Coverage is provided for diseases as per the regulations issued by the Tamil Nadu State Hospital Organization.

content.section_title: FAQ: How is the amount of financial assistance determined?
content.text: The amount is decided by the Journalists Welfare Fund Nominating Committee based on the nature of the illness and the cost of treatment.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is prior approval required before undergoing treatment?
content.text: The scheme does not specify prior approval, but the application must be submitted post-treatment within the stipulated time frame.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can journalists apply multiple times for different treatments?
content.text: The scheme does not specify limits on multiple applications; however, each application is subject to the rules and committee's decision.

title: Financial Assistance for Treatment of Chronic Diseases (HBOCWWB)
topic: Financial Assistance for Treatment of Chronic Diseases (HBOCWWB)

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: Under this scheme, financial assistance of ₹1,00,000/- is provided to registered workers for indoor treatment of chronic diseases like Cancer, TB, AIDS, etc. as per government rules.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The scheme “Financial Assistance for Treatment of Chronic Diseases” is implemented by the Haryana Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (HBOCWWB), Labour Department, Government of Haryana. Under this scheme, financial assistance is provided to registered workers for indoor treatment of chronic diseases like Cancer, TB, AIDS, etc. as per government rules.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: 1. Under this scheme, financial assistance up to ₹1,00,000/- is provided to registered workers for indoor treatment of chronic diseases like Cancer, TB, AIDS, etc.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant should be registered under the Haryana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
1. The registered worker must have a regular membership for one year.
1. The workers who are registered and require indoor treatment for chronic diseases such as cancer, tuberculosis (TB), AIDS, etc., are eligible to apply under the scheme.
1. The application frequency is limited to once.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
> **Registration of a Construction Worker Under HBOCWWB:**

**Step 01:** The construction worker should visit the [Official Website](https://hrylabour.gov.in/) of the Labour Department Haryana.
**Step 02:** On the home page, click on the “Building & Ors Const. Workers Welfare Board” link.
**Step 03:** Applicant has to read all the instructions and then “Tick” the acknowledgment, then click on the “Submit” button.
**Step 04:** Verification using Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP)- Family ID:
a) Forget/not having Family ID: If you do not have PPP ID or forget the PPP ID, then you have to select that radio button and you are redirected to the [PPP Portal](https://meraparivar.haryana.gov.in/) to get their PPP ID.
b) Having a Family ID: If you have a family ID, then only you can move to the next step for registration. To proceed further, follow the given steps:
• Select the “I have family ID” radio button
• Enter Parivar Pehchan Patra number - Family ID
• Click on “Fetch Family Details” which displays the family members registered under the given Family ID
• Select a family member for registration
• Enter the OTP that is being sent to the selected family member for verification
• Click on “Click to Verify”
**Step 05:** Aadhaar Verification before Registration: Provide your Aadhaar number and tick mark the checkbox for the declaration. Click on the “Continue” button.
**Step 06:** After verification, fill out the complete registration form and submit the form.
**Step 07:** Login into the account: After the final submission of the registration form, a worker can login into his/her account using their username and password but unable to avail of any benefit.
**Step 08:** To avail of the benefits, a worker has to pay the registration fee and add a work experience of a minimum of 90 days of the preceding year.
**Step 09:** Now, the worker needs to add work experience, to add 90 days of experience, the applicant has to fill in all the details where he/she has worked.
**Step 10:** The schemes and other benefits can be availed once the 90 days’ work experience is approved by the Officer.
> **Apply for Scheme: Registration Process on Antyodaya-SARAL Portal:**

**Step 01:** To avail of the benefits of the scheme, the applicant needs to visit the Official Portal -[Antyodaya-SARAL Portal](https://saralharyana.gov.in/)
**Step 02:** If the applicant is not registered on the portal, then he/she gets registered on the portal.
**Step 03:** For registration, click on “New User/Register Here” and fill in all the mandatory details i.e. Name, Email ID, Mobile Number & password. Click on ‘Submit’.
**Step 04:** Applicants will receive the login ID on their mobile number.
> **Login to Apply for the Scheme:**

**Step 01:** Visit the Official Portal -[Antyodaya-SARAL Portal](https://saralharyana.gov.in/)
**Step 02:** The “Sign in here” option is available on the right side of the screen. Applicant needs to fill in their credentials and click on the ‘Login’ button.
**Step 03:** In the opened window, click on “Scheme/Services list” and a list of schemes will appear on the screen.
**Step 04:** Now, select the scheme and click on “Apply for Service/Scheme”.
**Step 05:** Fill in all the details asked in the online application form and submit the application form.
[HBOCW Registration User Manual](https://hrylabour.gov.in/uploads/manuals/Manual_for_Worker_Registration_HBOCW.pdf)         [HBOCW Registration Basic Instructions & Features](https://hrylabour.gov.in/staticdocs/BOCW_English.pdf)

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
Documents required for registration under HBOCWWB:- Identity proof i.e. Aadhaar Card, Voter Card
- Passport-size photograph
- Attested work experience document
- Proof of residence
- Ration Card
- Bank account details
- Caste Certificate (If applicable)
- Proof of age
- Parivar Pehchan Patra (Family ID)
- Disability certificate (If applicable)
- Any other documents as required
Documents required for Scheme Benefits:- Identity proof/ID Card of Worker
- Undertaking by applicant
- Certificate of Medical Officer of Government or Private Empanelled hospital
- Original Bill of expenditure on the treatment
- Any other documents as required

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the benefits of the scheme?
content.text: Registered workers can receive financial assistance of up to [?]1,00,000/- for the indoor treatment of chronic diseases like Cancer, TB, AIDS, etc.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible to apply for this scheme?
content.text: Applicants must be registered under the Haryana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board and require indoor treatment for chronic diseases such as cancer, tuberculosis (TB), AIDS, etc.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the minimum membership requirement for eligibility?
content.text: The registered worker must have regular membership for one year to be eligible for the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What types of chronic diseases are covered under the scheme?
content.text: Chronic diseases such as Cancer, TB, AIDS, etc., are covered under this scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there a limit to the frequency of application?
content.text: Yes, applicants can apply for financial assistance under the scheme only once.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can a construction worker register under HBOCWWB?
content.text: Construction workers can register by visiting the Official Website of the Labour Department Haryana.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where can applicants apply for the scheme?
content.text: Applicants can apply for the scheme through the Official Portal - Antyodaya-SARAL Portal.

content.section_title: FAQ: What documents are required for application?
content.text: Applicants may need to provide a certificate of Medical Officer of Government or Private Empanelled hospital, original bill of expenditure on the treatment etc.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any registration fee required when registering under HBOCWWB?
content.text: Registration fee [?]25/- once in life-time and [?]5/- per month ([?]60/-P.A)as  subscription fee shall be paid to the Board by the beneficiary.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any provision to continue receiving benefits under the scheme after the death of the registered worker?
content.text: No, benefits will not be continued after the death of the worker.

title: Financial Assistance for the Treatment of Blind, Disabled, Deaf, Dumb, or Mentally Challenged Ex-Servicemen and their Dependents (Wife and Children)
topic: Financial Assistance for the Treatment of Blind, Disabled, Deaf, Dumb, or Mentally Challenged Ex-Servicemen and their Dependents (Wife and Children)

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to provide monthly financial assistance for the treatment of ex-servicemen and their dependents (wife & children) who have a disability of 40% or more and are suffering from serious illnesses such as cancer, leprosy, tuberculosis, kidney and cardiac ailments, paralysis, etc.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The "Financial Assistance for the Treatment of Blind, Disabled, Deaf, Dumb, or Mentally Challenged Ex-Servicemen and their Dependents (Wife and Children)" scheme was launched by the Department of Sainik Welfare, Government of Rajasthan. The scheme aims to provide monthly financial assistance for the treatment of ex-servicemen and their dependents (wife & children) who have a disability of 40% or more and are suffering from serious illnesses such as cancer, leprosy, tuberculosis, kidney and cardiac ailments, paralysis, etc. Only an ex-serviceman of the rank of Havildar or below or a member of the family of an ex-serviceman whose disability is 40% or more is eligible to apply. This scheme came into effect on 01.07.2020, and financial assistance will be granted from the first day of the month in which the application is approved.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
**Financial Assistance:**
**S. No.** **Percentage of Disability** **Financial Assistance** **Priority**180% and above₹3,000/- per monthFirst Priority260% but less than 80%₹2,000/- per monthSecond Priority340% but less than 60%₹1,000/- per monthThird Priority**Note:**

- The assistance is provided for three years. After that, the applicant will re-apply for renewal.
- The financial aid is disbursed online through the District Sainik Welfare Office.
- A maximum of two disabled family members of an ex-serviceman can avail of this benefit.
- The financial assistance will cease upon the death of the disabled ex-serviceman or the disabled family member.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant should be a native of Rajasthan.
1. The applicant should either be an ex-serviceman as defined by the Government of India or a dependent family member (excluding parents) of ex-serviceman.
1. Only an ex-serviceman holding the rank of Havildar or below, or their family members, are eligible to apply.
1. The ex-serviceman or their dependent should have a disability of 40% or more.
1. Both pensioner and non-pensioner are eligible. However, ex-serviceman who is already receiving a disability pension from the Army cannot apply for financial assistance for himself based on their disability. He may apply on behalf of a disabled family member with 40% or more disability.
1. Under this scheme, the family members do not include parents. Only wife/widow, son/daughter are included.
1. The ex-serviceman’s disability will be considered valid regardless of whether it occurred during military service or after retirement.
1. The name of the disabled wife, son, or daughter should be recorded in the discharge book or Part II Order of the ex-serviceman.
1. Assistance will not be provided to more than two members of the same family, including the ex-serviceman.
1. A valid recommendation from the District Sainik Welfare Officer (DSWO) is mandatory.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
***Application Process:***
**Step 1:** The interested applicant should visit the office of the District Sainik Welfare Office and request a hard copy of the prescribed format of the application form from the staff exclusively entrusted to issue and collect filled-in applications.
**OR**
The applicant should download the [application form](https://sainikkalyan.rajasthan.gov.in/content/dam/doitassests/sainikwelfare/pdf/Amalgamatedfundschemes/Medical%20Assistance.pdf) (Page No. 5) from the official website.
**Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest if required).
**Step 3:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the District Sainik Welfare Office.
**Step 4:** Request a receipt or acknowledgment from the District Sainik Welfare Office to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission and a unique identification number (if applicable).
***NOTE:***

- If the ex-serviceman/widow is alive, they apply; otherwise, a family member/guardian applies if the disabled applicant is unable.
- The application should be made through the same District Sainik Welfare Officer from whom the ex-serviceman/widow's identity card was issued.
- Beneficiaries must reapply every three years for renewal.

***Post-Application Process:***
**Step 1:** The District Sainik Welfare Officer will verify the documents and forward the application to the Director, Sainik Welfare Department, Rajasthan, Jaipur.
**Step 2:** A committee will review the applications every six months.
**Step 3:** Approved applicants will receive financial assistance online.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Discharge Book/Service Particulars
- Disability Certificate issued by a competent medical authority
- Ex-Serviceman/Widow/Dependent ID Card issued by the District Sainik Welfare Office
- Pension Payment Order (PPO), if applicable
- Death Certificate (in case of deceased ex-serviceman or widow)
- Aadhaar Card of the applicant
- Bank Passbook Copy
- Any other documents as required

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible for this scheme?
content.text: Ex-servicemen (Havildar or below) and their dependents with a disability of 40% or more who do not receive an Army disability pension.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the financial assistance provided?
content.text: ₹1,000 to ₹3,000 per month based on the level of disability.

content.section_title: FAQ: How long is the assistance provided?
content.text: The assistance is provided for three years, with renewal options.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can more than one dependent apply?
content.text: Yes, but only up to two dependents per family.

content.section_title: FAQ: How is the payment made?
content.text: Online transfer through the District Sainik Welfare Office.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any residency requirement?
content.text: Yes, the applicant must be a resident of Rajasthan.

content.section_title: FAQ: How is the priority determined?
content.text: Based on the level of disability: 80%+ (First), 60-80% (Second), 40-60% (Third).

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there a limit on the number of beneficiaries?
content.text: Yes, the scheme is limited to 300 beneficiaries at a time.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can one apply?
content.text: Submit an application to the District Sainik Welfare Office with the required documents.

content.section_title: FAQ: What documents are required?
content.text: Discharge book, disability certificate, ID proof, bank details, and other supporting documents.

content.section_title: FAQ: When does the scheme become effective?
content.text: The scheme was effective from July 1, 2020.

title: Financial Assistance to Old Age Pensioner Suffering From TB/ Cancer
topic: Financial Assistance to Old Age Pensioner Suffering From TB/ Cancer

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: “Financial Assistance to Old Age Pensioner Suffering From TB/ Cancer” by the Dept of Women & Child Development, Govt of Puducherry, was introduced to extend a helping hand by way of financial assistance to Old Age/Destitute Pensioners who are suffering from TB/Cancer to meet the medical expenses.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The scheme “Financial Assistance to Old Age Pensioner Suffering From TB/ Cancer” by the Department of Women & Child Development, Government of Puducherry, was introduced to extend a helping hand by way of a monthly financial assistance of ₹ 500/- to Old Age/Destitute Pensioners who are suffering from TB/Cancer to meet out the medical expenses.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: ₹500/- per month in addition to the old age pension amount.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
- The applicant should be a citizen of India.
- The applicant should be 60 years or above in age.
- The applicant should be a Old Age Pensioner or a Destitute Pensioner.
- The applicant should be suffering from T.B./Cancer.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Step 1:** The interested applicant should visit (during office hours) the [Office/ Sub-Office](https://wcd.py.gov.in/office-locations) of the Department of Women and Child Development, and request the hard copy of the prescribed format of the application form from the staff exclusively entrusted to issue and collect filled-in applications.
OR
The interested applicant should take print of the [prescribed format](https://wcd.py.gov.in/sites/default/files/oap-medical-treat-appl.pdf) of the application form.
**Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste the passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
**Step 3:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the Deputy Director (Women Development) or the Child Development Project Officer (depending on the location), at least 30 days before the proposed marriage date.
**Step 4:** Request a receipt or acknowledgment from the concerned authority to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Aadhaar Card.
- Residence Certificate from the Revenue Department.
- Income Certificate from the Revenue Department.
- Passport-sized Photograph.
- Declaration/undertaking (if Any).
- Medical Certificate From the Medical Authority of the Government Hospital or Institution.
- Photocopy of the Old Age Pension Book.

In the absence of an Aadhaar Card; the applicant is required to provide his/her Aadhaar Enrolment ID along with ANY ONE of the following documents -
1. Bank or Post Office Passbook with Photo.
2. Permanent Account Number (PAN) Card.
3. Passport.
4. Ration Card.
5. Voter Identity Card.
6. MGNREGA Card.
7. Kisan Photo Passbook.
8. Driving license issued by the Licensing Authority under the Motor Vehicles Act, 1988 (59 of 1988).
9. Certificate of identity having a photo of such person issued by a Gazetted Officer or a Tehsildar on an official letterhead.
10. Any other document as specified by the Department.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the key benefits of the scheme for Old Age/Destitute Pensioners suffering from TB/Cancer?
content.text: The primary benefit is the monthly financial assistance of [?]500, aimed at helping these pensioners meet their medical expenses.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there an age requirement for applicants to be eligible for the scheme in Puducherry?
content.text: Yes, applicants must be 60 years or above to be eligible for the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: How frequently is the financial assistance provided to beneficiaries?
content.text: Beneficiaries receive the financial assistance monthly to meet medical expenses.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can applicants use the scheme in addition to other government pension schemes?
content.text: Yes, eligible beneficiaries can receive [?]500/- per month in addition to their existing old age pension amount.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can applicants submit a medical certificate from any hospital?
content.text: No, the medical certificate must be from the Medical Authority of a Government Hospital or Institution.

content.section_title: FAQ: Which department issues the Old Age Pension Book required for application?
content.text: The Old Age Pension Book is issued by the concerned department handling pension matters.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can destitute pensioners apply for this financial assistance?
content.text: Yes, destitute pensioners are eligible to apply for the scheme.

title: Financial Assistance to Poor Scheduled Caste People Suffering From Prolonged Diseases
topic: Financial Assistance to Poor Scheduled Caste People Suffering From Prolonged Diseases

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme “Financial Assistance to Poor Scheduled Caste People Suffering From Prolonged Diseases” was introduced by the Adi Dravidar Welfare Dept, Govt of Puducherry. The objective of the scheme is to assist poor patients from Scheduled Castes suffering from prolonged illness.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The scheme “Financial Assistance to Poor Scheduled Caste People Suffering From Prolonged Diseases” was introduced by the Adi Dravidar Welfare Department, Government of Puducherry. The objective of the scheme is to provide assistance to poor patients from Scheduled Castes who are suffering from prolonged illness and unable to earn their livelihood.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: Financial Assistance of ₹500/- per month.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
- The applicant should be a citizen of India.
- The applicant should have resided continuously in the Union Territory of Puducherry for at least five years.
- The applicant should be from Scheduled Caste.
- The applicant should be at least 18 years of age.
- The annual income of the applicant should not be more than ₹2,00,000/-.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
**Step 1:** Visit the [Official Website](https://edistrict.py.gov.in/) of the e-District Portal of the Government of Puducherry.
**Step 2:** At the top right corner of the home page, click "[Login](https://edistrict.py.gov.in/Login.aspx)". In the login screen, click "[Register a New Membership](https://edistrict.py.gov.in/User/Registration.aspx)".
**Step 3:** On the Registration Page, fill in all the mandatory fields: Username, Password, Confirm Password, Personal Details (Full Name, First Name, Email Address, Address, Current Address, Gender, Date of Birth, Mobile Number). Agree to the declaration. Fill in the Captcha Code, and click "Signup".
**Step 4:** Visit the [Official Website](https://edistrict.py.gov.in/) of the e-District Portal of the Government of Puducherry.
**Step 5:** At the top right corner of the home page, click "[Login](https://edistrict.py.gov.in/Login.aspx)". In the login screen, provide your Username and Password, fill in the Captcha Code, and click "Sign In".
**Step 6:** On the next screen, a list of schemes by the Government of Puducherry will be displayed Department-wise.
**Step 7:** Click on the scheme you wish to apply for. You will be taken to its online application form.
**Step 8:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields (marked with a red asterisk) and upload all the mandatory documents (self-attest if required).
**Step 9:** Tick the declaration stating "I hereby declare that the above-mentioned details are true and correct as per the best of my Knowledge". Submit the application and note the application number for [tracking your application](https://edistrict.py.gov.in/User/TrackApplication.aspx). The application number will also be received on your Registered Mobile Number / Registered Email ID.
***Track Your Application** [here](https://edistrict.py.gov.in/User/TrackApplication.aspx)
*** Access the User Manual for Online Application** [here](https://edistrict.py.gov.in/Help.rar)

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Residence-cum-nativity Certificate.
- Aadhaar Card.
- Voter Card.
- Caste Certificate.
- Recent Passport-Size Photograph.
- Bank Passbook.
- Caste Certificate.
- Income Certificate.
- Medical Certificate.
- Ration Card.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where can one track the status of their application for financial assistance?
content.text: The status of the application can be tracked at https://edistrict.py.gov.in/User/TrackApplication.aspx.

content.section_title: FAQ: In the absence of an Aadhaar Card, what alternative documents can be submitted for application?
content.text: If an Aadhaar Card is unavailable, applicants can submit Aadhaar Enrolment ID along with documents like Bank Passbook, PAN Card, Passport, Voter Identity Card, or others specified by the Department.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there a contact point for further inquiries or assistance regarding the scheme?
content.text: For any queries or assistance, individuals can visit https://adwelfare.py.gov.in/contact-us.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can a person from a Scheduled Caste residing outside Puducherry apply for this scheme?
content.text: No, applicants must have continuous residence in the Union Territory of Puducherry for at least five years to be eligible.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the duration of financial assistance provided to eligible beneficiaries?
content.text: Eligible beneficiaries receive a monthly financial assistance of [?]500/- for the duration of their prolonged illness.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are there any specific guidelines regarding the submission of photographs for the application?
content.text: Recent Passport-Size Photographs are required to be submitted along with the application.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can a person above 60 years of age apply for financial assistance under this scheme?
content.text: Yes, as long as the applicant is at least 18 years old, there is no upper age limit specified for eligibility.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is There Any Compensation For The Delay In The Disbursal Of The Scheme Benefits?
content.text: The guidelines do not mention any provision for compensation in the event of delayed disbursement of scheme benefits. Applicants should note that compensation for delays is not part of the scheme's framework.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are there any specific provisions for Transgenders under this scheme?
content.text: No provisions for Transgenders are mentioned in the guidelines of this scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can Applicants From The Neighboring States/UTs Also Apply To This Scheme?
content.text: Unfortunately, this scheme is exclusively available to permanent residents of the Puducherry. Applicants from neighboring States/UTs, among others, are not eligible to apply.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there an application deadline for this scheme?
content.text: The scheme guidelines do not mention any opening date or closing date for applications.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are there any specific provisions for persons with disabilities under this scheme?
content.text: No provisions for persons with disabilities are mentioned in the guidelines of this scheme.

title: Financial Grants In Aid To SC/ST Patients Suffering From Cancer And Other Malignant Diseases
topic: Financial Grants In Aid To SC/ST Patients Suffering From Cancer And Other Malignant Diseases

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme “Financial Grants in Aid to SC/ST patients suffering from cancer and other malignant diseases” was launched by the Government of Assam in 2017 to provide financial assistance in the form of grants in Aid to SC/ST patients who are suffering from cancer and other malignant diseases.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
The Scheduled Castes people who constitute 7.15% and the Scheduled Tribe people who constitute 12.44% of the total population of the State of Assam as per the 2011 Census are Socio Economically backward. Special provisions have been enshrined in the Constitution for the welfare of SC / ST people in every sphere of their life. There are a number of SC/ST people suffering from cancer and other malignant diseases who are unable to carry forward the treatment of their diseases due to the economic hardships faced by them. The Government has decided to bring some succour to such patients by making available some financial assistance in the form of grants in Aid to SC /ST patients suffering from cancer and other malignant diseases.
The objective of this scheme is to provide financial assistance in the form of grants in Aid to SC/ST patients who are suffering from cancer and other malignant diseases and are unable to afford advanced treatment due to their Socio-economic condition prevalent in the community. Under the scheme, SC/ST patients will be given a one-time, fixed financial assistance of ₹ 50,000/- (Rupees Fifty thousand) only.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant must belong to a SC/ST community of Assam.
1. A person suffering from cancer or other malignant diseases may apply.
1. No person shall be eligible to claim this financial aid more than once.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Caste Certificate in prescribed Performa of applicant suffering from Cancer or other malignant diseases issued by the concerned authority.
- Medical certificate of the applicant and relevant documents.
- Passport size Photograph of the applicant.
- Bank Account No. along with IFSC of the applicant.(copy of Front Page of Bank Pass Book )
- Caste Certificate
- Bills of medical expenses.
- Income certificate

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the objective of this scheme?
content.text: The objective of this scheme is to provide financial assistance in the form of grants in Aid to SC/ST patients who are suffering from cancer and other malignant diseases and are unable to afford advanced treatment due to their Socioeconomic condition prevalent in the community.

content.section_title: FAQ: How much financial assistance should be provided under the scheme?
content.text: Under the scheme, SC/ST patients will be given a one-time, fixed financial assistance of [?] 50,000/-only.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is the beneficiary under this scheme?
content.text: The patient of SC/ST communities who are suffering from cancer and other malignant diseases and unable to afford advanced treatment due to their Socioeconomic condition is the beneficiary of the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Should the beneficiary be eligible to claim this financial aid more than once?
content.text: No person shall be eligible to claim this financial aid more than once.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can apply for this scheme?
content.text: The applicant belonging to the SC/ST community of Assam is eligible for the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any age criterion to apply under the scheme?
content.text: No, there is no age criterion to apply under the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who will constitute the Scrutiny Committee?
content.text: A Scrutiny Committee shall be constituted by the Director of Welfare of SC, Assam /Director of Welfare of Plain Tribe and Backward Classes, Assam which will scrutinize the documents submitted by the applicants and submits its findings in a report to the Selection Committee within a week.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who will finalise the list of beneficiary?
content.text: The Selection Committee will look into the report submitted by the Scrutiny Committee and finally select the list of SC/ST patients suffering from cancer and other malignant disease.

content.section_title: FAQ: From where the applicants get to know about the Application?
content.text: Advertisements shall be made in leading widely circulated newspapers.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the procedure to apply for applicants?
content.text: Applicants should apply as per the criteria sought in advertisements circulated in Newspapers. The applications, along with their bank account details, are received by the respective SDWOs.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can I apply through online mode?
content.text: Applications may be made online as and when the Official Website of the Directorate of Welfare of Scheduled Castes, Assam, Directorate of Welfare of Plain Tribe and Backward Classes, Assam starts to be fully functional.

title: Financial Incentives To Mothers Who Deliver A Girl Child (Mamta)
topic: Financial Incentives To Mothers Who Deliver A Girl Child (Mamta)

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The objective of the Mamta Scheme is aimed at improving the Female Child Sex Ratio in the State. The scheme is open to all mothers who deliver a live Girl Child (max. 02 deliveries) in a registered medical institution. The scheme shall be implemented through the ICDS.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
The scheme "Financial Incentives to Mothers who Deliver a Girl Child (Mamta)" is a scheme of Financial Assistance by the Directorate of Women And Child Development, Govt. of Goa. The objective of the Mamta Scheme is aimed at improving the Female Child Sex Ratio in the State. The scheme is open to all mothers who deliver a live Girl Child (max. 02 deliveries) in a registered medical institution.

> **Implementing Agency**

The scheme shall be implemented through the ICDS i.e. Child Development Project Officer, Mukhya Sevikas, and Anganwadi Worker at Taluka/Block Level and Viliage Level respectively.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
1. ₹ 10,000/- as a one-time financial incentive (at the birth of the child).
1. Those beneficiaries who had applied for the 1st Installment of ₹ 5,000/- (as on the date of this Amended Scheme published in the Official Gazette) shall be eligible for ₹ 10,000/- as per the amended scheme.

**NOTE 1:** Those beneficiaries who were paid the 1st Installment of ₹ 5,000/- under the existing scheme shall be eligible for only the balance amount (2nd instalment) of ₹ 5,000/- (to apply within 45 days from the date of the Amended Scheme published in the official Gazette).

> **Mode of Disbursal**

The benefits are directly credited to the declared Bank Account.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant should be a mother who has delivered a live Girl Child in a registered medical institution.
1. The applicant should be a resident of Goa for at least three years. OR
1. The applicant should be married to a resident of Goa.
1. The application for the scheme should have been made within 45 days of the delivery.

**NOTE:** The scheme is available to all mothers, irrespective of their social/economic status.

content.section_title: Exclusions
content.text: Those beneficiaries who were paid both 1st and 2nd Installments shall not be eligible for any further incentives/benefits under the amended scheme and the case shall be treated as closed.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
The eligible mother shall apply in the [prescribed format](https://dwcd.goa.gov.in/wp-content/uploads/2017/07/MAMTA-FORM-2.pdf) to the [Child Development Project Officer](https://www.goa.gov.in/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/DWCD-Citizen-Charter-1.pdf) of the ICDS Block Office of the respective Taluka at Anganwadi Centres.
**NOTE:** The application should be made within 45 days of the delivery of the Girl Child.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Certified/Self-Attested Copy of Aadhaar Card.
- Certified/Self-Attested Copy of Birth Report/Certificate from Registered Institution.
- Proof that the Applicant (the Mother) has been a Resident of Goa for at least 3 years OR Proof that the Applicant (the Mother) has been married to a resident of Goa (Marriage Certificate, and Residential Certificate of Husband).
- Pre-Receipt in duplicate (Annexure-2) (For New Beneficiaries).
- Pre-receipt in duplicate in (Annexure-3) (for those beneficiaries who were paid the 1st Installment under the old Scheme).

content.section_title: FAQ: How Are The Financial Incentives Paid To The Beneficiaries Under The "MAMTA" Scheme, And What Verification Process Is Followed?
content.text: The financial incentives are paid to the beneficiaries through various electronic systems like ECS, NEFT, APBS, or ACH. Accounts are opened in nationalized banks for the expected mothers. Verification is conducted to ensure funds availability and legitimacy of the applicants.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can You Explain The Declaration Process For Beneficiaries Who Have Received The 1st Installment Under The "MAMTA" Scheme?
content.text: Beneficiaries who have received the 1st installment of Rs. 5000 are eligible for the balance amount (2nd installment) of Rs. 5000. They need to sign a declaration stating their eligibility and consent to repay any excess amount with interest if mistakenly paid.

content.section_title: FAQ: What Are The Details Required In The Application Form For The "MAMTA" Scheme?
content.text: The application form for "MAMTA" requires information such as the mother's name, age, residential address, contact number, bank account details, caste, religion, delivery date, weight of the girl child at birth, among other particulars.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can You Elaborate On The Role Of The Anganwadi Centre In The "MAMTA" Scheme Application Process?
content.text: The application form includes the name of the Anganwadi Centre where the beneficiary's child is registered, which likely serves as a reference point for the authorities.

content.section_title: FAQ: How Does The "MAMTA" Scheme Ensure That Financial Incentives Reach The Beneficiaries Promptly?
content.text: By preparing lists of expected mothers beforehand and maintaining detailed records, the scheme facilitates swift sanction and payment of financial incentives, ensuring timely assistance to beneficiaries.

content.section_title: FAQ: What Steps Are Taken By The Government To Inform Beneficiaries About Any Amendments Or Modifications To The "MAMTA" Scheme?
content.text: The Government makes the amended "MAMTA" scheme effective from a specified date, usually communicated through official notifications or public announcements to inform beneficiaries of any changes.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can You Elaborate On The Purpose Of The Detailed Record-Keeping By Child Development Project Officers In The "MAMTA" Scheme?
content.text: Maintaining detailed records of applications, payments, and beneficiary information helps track the progress of the scheme, monitor its impact, and ensure transparency and accountability in the implementation process.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can You Provide Examples Of Other Children's Details That Applicants Need To Furnish In The "MAMTA" Scheme Application Form?
content.text: The application form may require details such as the names, ages, and genders of other children in the household to assess the family's circumstances and needs.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can You Clarify What Is Meant By A _Registered Medical Institution_ In The Eligibility Criteria For The _MAMTA_ Scheme
content.text: A "registered medical institution" refers to a medical facility that is officially recognized and authorized by the relevant health authorities or government to provide medical services and carry out deliveries.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to provide safe, timely, and free transportation for pregnant women and newborn babies (0 - 1 year) to access healthcare services, including travel from home to the hospital and from the hospital back home.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The scheme “Khushiyon Ki Sawari Sewa” has been launched by the Department of Medical Health and Family Welfare, Government of Uttarakhand. It aims to provide free and safe transport for pregnant women and newborn babies (0 -1 year) to and from the hospital, with services easily accessible by calling 102.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
Under this scheme, pregnant women are provided free transportation from the hospital to their homes along with their newborns after delivery. Additionally, pregnant are also provided free to-and-fro transportation from home to the hospital for ultrasound services.
These facilities are being provided across the state through 128 vehicles.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The beneficiary must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
1. The beneficiary must be a pregnant woman or a newborn baby aged 0 to 1 year.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text: The services can be availed free of cost by calling 102.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
1. Identity proof
2. Medical Reports, if any
3. Other supporting documents as required

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the main objective of this scheme?
content.text: The scheme aims to provide safe, timely, and free transport for pregnant women and newborn babies (0–1 year) to and from hospitals.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can benefit from this scheme?
content.text: Pregnant women and newborn babies up to 1 year of age can avail benefits under this scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any age limit for newborn babies under this scheme?
content.text: Yes, the scheme covers newborn babies from 0 to 1 year of age.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can beneficiaries access this service?
content.text: The service can be availed free of cost by calling 102.

content.section_title: FAQ: Does the scheme provide transportation for ultrasound visits?
content.text: Yes, pregnant women get free to-and-fro transport from home to hospital for ultrasound services.

content.section_title: FAQ: How many vehicles are available for this service in the state?
content.text: A total of 128 vehicles are deployed across Uttarakhand for providing these services.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are there any charges for using this service?
content.text: No, the service is completely free of cost.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The Department of Health and Family Welfare, Government of Odisha had introduced a scheme named "Khushi" which aims to provide Free sanitary napkins to all girls in Government and aided schools from class 6th to 12th.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
The Department of Health and Family Welfare, Government of Odisha had introduced a scheme named "**Khushi**" which aims to provide Free sanitary napkins to all girls in Government and aided schools from class 6th to 12th. Khushi (meaning "Happiness" in Odia) is a female health and hygiene program run by the Government of Odisha to provide free sanitary pads to girl students in Odisha. Khushi scheme of Odisha aims to provide good menstrual hygiene care to women of the state. The initiative aims to promote health and hygiene among school-going adolescent girls leading to higher retention in school and greater empowerment of women. The scheme provides free sanitary pads to girls in government and government-aided schools and also offers subsidized sanitary napkins to rural women. The scheme's goals include reducing the dropout rate among girls, improving academic performance, and empowering women. The Odisha State Medical Corporation Limited is the implementing agency of this scheme.
**Objectives**

1. **Promote Menstrual Hygiene:** The scheme seeks to increase awareness about menstrual hygiene and provide access to sanitary products.   1. **Reduce School Dropout Rates:** By ensuring that girls have access to menstrual hygiene products, the scheme aims to reduce school absenteeism and dropout rates among adolescent girls.   1. **Economic Empowerment:** The scheme also has elements designed to economically empower women and girls through the promotion of local production and distribution of sanitary products.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
The Khushi Scheme offers several key benefits aimed at improving menstrual hygiene and overall well-being for women and girls in the state.
1. **Free or Subsidized Napkins:** The scheme provides free or affordable sanitary napkins to women and girls, especially in rural and underserved areas where access to such products is limited.
2. **Education and Awareness:** Through educational campaigns, the scheme raises awareness about menstrual hygiene practices, helping women and girls maintain better health and hygiene.
3. **Support for Adolescent Girls:** By providing sanitary products and creating awareness, the scheme helps reduce school absenteeism and dropout rates among adolescent girls, ensuring they stay in school and continue their education.
4. **Employment:** The scheme often includes components that support the local production of sanitary napkins, which create employment opportunities and contribute to the local economy.
5. **Prevention of Health Issues:** By promoting the use of sanitary products and proper menstrual hygiene practices, the scheme helps in preventing health issues related to menstruation, such as infections and rashes.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Odisha.
1. All school girls from classes 6th to 12th are eligible.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
1. There is no need to apply anywhere to avail of the benefit of Odisha Khushi Scheme.
1. Girls Students of Class 6th to Class 12 will get free Sanitary Pads/ Napkins in their School.
1. The rural women will contact their area ASHA Worker to get 6 Napkins at the nominal rate of Rs. 6/-.
1. An applicant may visit the nearest local Primary Health Centers (PHCs) or community health centers where information about the Khushi Scheme and its benefits might be available.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Aadhaar Card
- Proof of Residence
- School ID Card

content.section_title: FAQ: Is this scheme only for girls?
content.text: Yes, this scheme is only for girls

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible for this scheme?
content.text: The school-going girls from class 6th to 12th are eligible under this scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the purpose of this scheme?
content.text: The purpose of this scheme is to improve the health and Hygiene of the girls with respect to their menstrual cycles, and to also retain them in the school even after they reach puberty.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any such benefit for grown up women in Odisha?
content.text: Yes, The government of Odisha is also providing sanitary napkins to rural women at subsidised rates of Rs. 6 for six napkins through ASHA workers.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any application procedure to get free sanitary napkins under this scheme?
content.text: No, the school takes care of the distribution of the sanitary pads to the girls, no application is needed for this.

content.section_title: FAQ: How do they keep track of the stocks and distribution and ensure proper monitoring of this scheme?
content.text: To address that, an app called "Khushi" has been created which helps them monitor and keep track of this scheme.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: This initiative aims to promote the physical, mental, and emotional well-being of adolescents aged 10 to 19 years by providing free health counselling and guidance.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The “Kishor Swasthya Paramarsh Clinic” is an initiative under the National Health Mission (NHM) implemented by the Department of Medical Health and Family Welfare, Government of Uttarakhand. The initiative aims to promote the physical, mental, and emotional well-being of adolescents aged 10 to 19 years by providing free health counselling and guidance.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: Free counselling services are provided at the Adolescent Health Clinics.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text: 1. The beneficiary must be an adolescent between 10 and 19 years of age from Uttarakhand.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text: Free consultation is provided upon registration at 13 district hospitals, 2 sub-district hospitals, and 45 community health centres across the state.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text: Documents are required as per the guidelines prescribed for the dedicated Adolescent Health Clinics.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible to benefit from this initiative?
content.text: All adolescents aged 10 to 19 years in the state of Uttarakhand are eligible to avail services under this initiative.

content.section_title: FAQ: What services are provided at the Clinics?
content.text: The clinics provide free counselling, health consultations, and awareness services.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are the counselling services free of cost?
content.text: Yes, all counselling and consultation services at the Adolescent Health Clinics are free of cost.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where are these clinics located in Uttarakhand?
content.text: Free consultation services are available at 13 district hospitals, 2 sub-district hospitals, and 45 community health centres across the state.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can an adolescent avail services at the clinic?
content.text: Adolescents can visit at the nearest district hospital, sub-district hospital, or community health centre offering Adolescent Health Clinic services.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to create awareness among adolescent girls aged 14 to 18 years about nutrition, health, and life cycle issues, and also provides supplementary nutrition for 300 days in a year.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The scheme "Kishori Balika Yojana" is implemented by the Department of Women Empowerment & Child Development, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to create awareness among adolescent girls aged 14 to 18 years about nutrition, health, and life cycle issues, and also provides supplementary nutrition for 300 days in a year.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: Under this scheme, awareness is created among adolescent girls about nutrition, health, and life cycle issues. They are also provided supplementary nutrition (fortified rice and wheat for 25 days each month) for 300 days in a year.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
1. The applicant must be an adolescent girl in the age group of 14 to 18 years.
1. The applicant must be residing in one of the aspirational districts of Uttarakhand- Haridwar or Udham Singh Nagar.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text: Registration of beneficiaries is carried out through the [Poshan Tracker App](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.poshantracker&pli=1), for which the Aadhaar card and birth certificate of the adolescent girl are required.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Aadhaar Card
- Birth Certificate

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible for the scheme?
content.text: Adolescent girls aged 14 to 18 years, who are residents of Uttarakhand, and live in the aspiring districts of Haridwar or Udham Singh Nagar.

content.section_title: FAQ: Which districts are covered under this scheme?
content.text: The scheme is currently implemented in the aspiring districts of Uttarakhand: Haridwar and Udham Singh Nagar.

content.section_title: FAQ: What kind of nutrition support is provided under the scheme?
content.text: Beneficiaries receive fortified rice and wheat for 25 days every month, for a total of 300 days in a year.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can one register for the scheme?
content.text: Registration of beneficiaries is done through the Poshan Tracker App.

content.section_title: FAQ: What documents are required for registration?
content.text: Aadhaar card and birth certificate of the adolescent girl are mandatory for registration.

content.section_title: FAQ: How long does the nutritional support last in a year?
content.text: Supplementary nutrition is provided for 300 days in a year.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is the scheme free of cost for beneficiaries?
content.text: Yes, all services and nutritional support under the scheme are provided free of cost.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme "Kishori Balika Yojana" by the Department of Women and Child Development, Government of Madhya Pradesh aims to provide vocational, health, and hygiene training to adolescent girls. Through this scheme, training and material assistance are provided to the girls.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The scheme "Kishori Balika Yojana" by the Department of Women and Child Development, Government of Madhya Pradesh aims to provide vocational, health, and hygiene training to adolescent girls. Through this scheme, girls from each Anganwadi center are selected and trained by master trainers, who in turn provide training at the Gram Panchayat level. The scheme is implemented by Anganwadi Workers and includes Two-day Master Trainer Training Programs provided at monthly intervals.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
- Each Anganwadi center selects 2 adolescent girls, 1 Anganwadi worker, and 1 Auxiliary Nurse Midwife (ANM) for a two-day master trainer training at monthly intervals.
- Master trainers provide vocational, health, and hygiene training at the Gram Panchayat level.
- A two-day master trainer training program is provided at monthly intervals.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Madhya Pradesh.
1. The applicant should be a girl.
1. The applicant should be aged between 11 and 18 years.
1. The applicant should be from one of the following categories: General, Other Backward Class (OBC), Scheduled Castes (SC), Scheduled Tribes (ST).

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text: The interested applicants should visit the nearest Anganwadi Center for registration, along with the necessary documents.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Identity Proof (Aadhaar Card, etc)
- Proof of Age (Birth Certificate, etc)
- Address Proof

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the Scheme for Adolescent Girls?
content.text: This scheme, under the Women and Child Development Department of Madhya Pradesh, provides vocational, health, and cleanliness training to adolescent girls through master trainers at Anganwadi Centres.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible to participate in the scheme?
content.text: Adolescent girls selected at the Gram Panchayat level are eligible for the training.

content.section_title: FAQ: How many adolescent girls are trained at each Anganwadi Centre?
content.text: Two adolescent girls are selected for training at each Anganwadi Centre.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who provides the training to the girls?
content.text: The training is provided by master trainers who conduct a 2-day training session every month.

content.section_title: FAQ: What topics are covered in the training?
content.text: The training includes vocational skills, health education, and cleanliness practices.

content.section_title: FAQ: How often does the training take place?
content.text: The training is held every month, with each session lasting 2 days.

content.section_title: FAQ: Do I need to pay any fee to participate in the scheme?
content.text: No, the scheme is free of charge for eligible adolescent girls.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can I apply for the scheme?
content.text: Eligible girls can register at the nearest Anganwadi Centre by filling out an application form.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can I participate in the scheme if I am not part of the Gram Panchayat list?
content.text: No, only adolescent girls selected at the Gram Panchayat level are eligible for the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can I attend the training if I am already receiving other support programs?
content.text: Yes, participation in other programs does not affect eligibility for this scheme.

title: Madhya Pradesh Kalakar Evam Sahityakar Kalyan Kosh Niyam- Serious Illness / Accident / Calamity Assistance
topic: Madhya Pradesh Kalakar Evam Sahityakar Kalyan Kosh Niyam- Serious Illness / Accident / Calamity Assistance

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to provide one-time financial assistance for serious illness, accident, or calamity-related medical treatment to artists and litterateurs.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The “Madhya Pradesh Kalakar Evam Sahityakar Kalyan Kosh Niyam- Serious Illness / Accident / Calamity Assistance” scheme has beenn launched by the Department of Culture, Government of Madhya Pradesh. The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to artists and litterateurs suffering from serious or long-term illnesses, accidents, or calamities. Under this scheme, one-time financial support is provided to meet medical treatment expenses that the beneficiary cannot afford.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
**Financial Assistance**

- One-time financial assistance of up to ₹50,000.
- Provided for serious or long-term illness, accident, or calamity-related treatment.

**Mode of Disbursement**

- Direct bank transfer to the beneficiary account.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant should be an artist or a litterateur.
1. The applicant should be a resident of Madhya Pradesh.
1. The applicant should be suffering from a serious or long-term illness, accident, or calamity.
1. The applicant’s (husband/wife) monthly income should not exceed ₹10,000.
1. The total family income should not exceed ₹20,000 per month.
1. The writer/artist's fully dependent spouse, fully dependent parents, dependent minor brothers and sisters, dependent minor children, dependent widowed daughter, and dependent differently-abled brothers and sisters are eligible to get assistance.

content.section_title: Exclusions
content.text:
1. The applicant should not be a government employee.
1. The applicant should not be employed in autonomous or semi-government institutions.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Step 1:** The interested applicant downloads the [application form](https://www.culturemp.in/pdfs/kalakar-kalyan-kosh-yojna-2023.pdf) (**Page No. 8**) from the official website.
**Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
**Step 3:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the Director, Directorate of Culture, Madhya Pradesh.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Passport-size Photo
- Proof of Residence
- Age Proof
- Self-Declaration Affidavit
- Aadhaar Card
- Medical Certificate (For Illness or Disability)
- Income Certificate
- Artist/Litterateur's Work & Achievements
- Disability Certificate
- Bank Account Details with IFSC Code
- Disaster or Accident Proof (If Applicable)

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the objective of this assistance?
content.text: To support artists during disability, long-term illness, accident, or natural calamity.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who launched the scheme?
content.text: The scheme was launched by the Department of Culture, Government of Madhya Pradesh.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is the assistance recurring?
content.text: No, the assistance is provided only on a one-time basis.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the maximum amount payable?
content.text:
The maximum financial assistance payable is up to ₹50,000.

Is a medical certificate mandatory?
Yes, a medical certificate is mandatory wherever applicable.

Are government employees eligible?
No, government employees are not eligible under the scheme.

How is the amount disbursed?
The assistance amount is disbursed through Direct Bank Transfer.

Can assistance be granted again?
Yes, but only in exceptional circumstances with proper justification.

Is age a criterion?
No, there is no specific age limit prescribed.

Who approves the assistance?
The assistance is approved by a state-level competent committee.

Are calamity victims eligible?
Yes, artists personally affected by a calamity are eligible.

Is residency mandatory?
Yes, the applicant must be a resident of Madhya Pradesh.

Whose decision is final?
The decision of the State Government is final.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is income eligibility applicable?
content.text: Yes, the applicant's monthly income limit is ₹10,000, and the family income does not exceed ₹20,000.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is a medical certificate mandatory?
content.text: Yes, a medical certificate is mandatory wherever applicable.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are government employees eligible?
content.text: No, government employees are not eligible under the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: How is the amount disbursed?
content.text: The assistance amount is disbursed through direct bank transfer.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who approves the assistance?
content.text: The assistance is approved by a state-level competent committee.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are calamity victims eligible?
content.text: Yes, artists personally affected by a calamity are eligible.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is residency mandatory?
content.text: Yes, the applicant must be a resident of Madhya Pradesh.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The Labor Department of Chhattisgarh started this scheme on March 4, 2010, under the "Building and Construction Workers Welfare Board" for female construction workers. Under the scheme registered female construction workers will receive benefits during pregnancy.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
The Labor Department of Chhattisgarh introduced the "Minimata Mahtari Jatan Yojana" on March 4, 2010, through the "Building and Construction Workers Welfare Board." This initiative is designed to provide benefits to registered female construction workers during pregnancy. The scheme applies to both male and female beneficiaries who are registered and possess a beneficiary identity card at least 90 days before the scheme application date.

**Note:** The name of this scheme was changed from "Bhagini Prasuti Sahayata Yojana" to  "Minimata Mahtari Jatan Yojana" on May 12, 2022.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: > A lump sum amount of ₹20,000/- will be provided to the registered female construction worker after the birth of the child.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Chhattisgarh.
1. The applicant should be a woman worker.
1. The applicant should be registered  Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
1. The benefit will only be provided for only first two children.
1. The applicant should be registered as a construction worker 90 days in advance.

**Note:** In case of the death of the beneficiary female worker during delivery, the payment of the Assistance Scheme will be payable to her husband (full amount).

content.section_title: Exclusions
content.text:
- Spouses of construction workers employed by public or government institutions.
- Construction worker beneficiaries who lack valid membership with the Board.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
> **Registration of a Construction Worker Under CBOCWWB**

Step-1: All the applicant have to visit the official website [Chhattisgarh Labour Department](https://cglabour.nic.in/).
Step-2: On the home page click on "[Apply](https://cglabour.nic.in/EngPages/Departments.aspx?id=3)" under "Chhattisgarh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board".
Step-3: Enter the following details

- Select : "Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board".
- Select Service : "Labour Registration".
- What do you want to do : "Registration Application".
- Click Proceed.

Step-4: Now enter your details :Name

- Father name.
- Aadhaar card.
- D.O.B.
- Age.

Step-5: Click on submit only after uploading all the important information and documents given in Part 1, Part 2, Part 3.
Step-6: When you click on submit, your registration ID will be sent to your mobile and email.

> **Apply**

Step-1: All the applicant have to visit the official [website](https://cglabour.nic.in) [Chhattisgarh Labour Department](https://cglabour.nic.in/).
Step-2: On the home page click on "[Apply](https://cglabour.nic.in/EngPages/Departments.aspx?id=3)" under "Chhattisgarh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
Step-3:  Enter the following details

- Select : "Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board".
- Select Service : Scheme
- What do you want to do : "Application".
- Click Proceed.

Step-4: Select your district name and provide old/new registration number and click proceed.
Step-5: When you enter the district and registration number, an OTP will be sent to your mobile for verification.
Step-6: After this, a dashboard will open in front of you, containing information about your name, address, age, and caste etc.
Step-7: After that, you select your Yojana and upload your important documents and basic information.
Step-8: Click "Submit" to register yourself for this scheme.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Certificate issued by self or Maternal and Child Card issued by ANM
- Bank passbook photocopies
- Birth certificate
- Registration Certificate
- Self-declaration certificate
- Aadhaar Card
- Self-declaration certificate in the prescribed form issued by the government in respect of the employer.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is this scheme?
content.text: The "Building and Construction Workers Welfare Board" of the Chhattisgarh Government provided financial assistance to women construction workers on March 4, 2010.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the benefits?
content.text: A lump sum assistance amount of [?] 20,000/- will be given to the eligible beneficiaries.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can get the benefits?
content.text: All registered construction worker individuals from Chhattisgarh State.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the official website to apply for the scheme?
content.text: URL : https://cglabour.nic.in/

content.section_title: FAQ: Is this scheme for women?
content.text: Yes, this scheme is for Registered female construction workers.

content.section_title: FAQ: How late can a applicant apply for the scheme?
content.text: Applicant can apply up to 90 days after the birth of the child

content.section_title: FAQ: For how many children can the applicant avail the benefit of this scheme?
content.text: Only upto 2 children.
