content.section_title: FAQ: How will the beneficiary get the benefit?
content.text: Beneficiary will get the benefit through the RTGS/NEFT in her bank account.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who will get the benefit if a woman dies during childbirth?
content.text: Benefits will be provided to her husband.

content.section_title: FAQ: How does one apply for assistance under this scheme?
content.text: Eligible mothers can apply for assistance under this scheme through online portals.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The "Miscarriage Benefit'' scheme by the BOCW Welfare Board, Labour Department,West Bengal, is a welfare scheme that provides financial benefits for miscarriage up to two times in a lifetime to the eligible female beneficiaries or the wives of male beneficiaries.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The "Miscarriage Benefit'' scheme by the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Labour Department, West Bengal, is a welfare scheme for the registered workers of the board. Under this scheme, the Board shall provide financial benefits for miscarriage to the female beneficiary or wife in case of a male beneficiary who is registered for 1 year under the Board, subject to maximum 2 times in his/her whole life time.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
- **Quantum of Assistance:** ₹6,000/-.
**Note:** The benefit shall be allowed for two times in beneficiarie's whole life time.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
> **For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:**

- The applicant should be a resident of West Bengal.
- The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker.
- The applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
- The applicant should have engaged in building and construction work for at least 90 days in a given year.

> **For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:**

- The applicant should be a registered female member or wife of a male member Under the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of West Bengal.
- The applicant should have gone through a miscarriage.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
> **Registration Process as a Building/ Construction Worker:**

**Step 1:** To register as a beneficiary under the BOCW Welfare Board, an applicant worker should request Form-27 from the Assistant Labour Commissioners/ Beneficiary Registering Officers and should attach all the mandatory documents (self-attested, if required).
**Step 2:** Application shall be submitted to the Assistant Labour Commissioners/ Beneficiary Registering Officers.
> **Application Process of the Welfare Scheme:**

**Step 1:** A registered worker should request Form-XII for the application from the Assistant Labour Commissioners/ Beneficiary Registering Officers and should attach all the mandatory documents (self-attested, if required).
**Step 2:** Application shall be submitted to the Assistant Labour Commissioners/ Beneficiary Registering Officers for this benefit.
> **Helpdesk:**

Labour Department
Department of Labour, Government of West Bengal,
12th Floor, N.S Building, Block-A,
1, Kiran Shankar Roy Road,
Kolkata-700001
Helpline of Labour Department, Govt. of West Bengal (Shramik Sathi): 1800-103-0009
> **Note:**

- Upon successful verification,  the construction worker is registered and an identity card and passbook are issued to him/ her. ₹20/- as the registration fee and ₹30/- as yearly subscription is taken. The amounts taken are noted in the passbook and a receipt is issued.
- After one year, the beneficiary's subscription is renewed upon payment of a renewal fee of ₹30/- and submission of an application in Form-27A.
- If not renewed the registration of the beneficiary is cancelled after the expiry of one year. Fresh application for renewal to be submitted to the ALC for consideration.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:- Passport size photographs of the worker (Four copies).
- Attested copy of Proof of Age.
For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:- Identity Card of the member.
- Medical Certificate.
- Any other document if required.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the age requirement for registration as a beneficiary under the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of West Bengal?
content.text: The building worker must be at least 18 years old and not older than 60 years old.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there a minimum service requirement to register as a beneficiary?
content.text: Yes, the building worker must have completed 90 days of service as a building worker in the preceding 12 months.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can apply for the welfare scheme?
content.text: Any registered worker of the Board can apply for the welfare scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is responsible for registering a worker as a beneficiary?
content.text: At present all Assistant Labour Commissioners and Inspectors posted at blocks and municipalities shall be responsible for registering a worker as a beneficiary.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible for registration as a beneficiary of the BOCW Welfare Board?
content.text: Every building worker who is between the age of 18-60 years and who has been engaged in any building or other construction work for not less than 90 days during the preceding one year is eligible for registration as a beneficiary of the BOCW Welfare Board of West Bengal.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the procedure for applying for the registration?
content.text: To register as a beneficiary under the BOCW Welfare Board, an applicant worker should collect and submit the Form-27 with all the mandatory documents (self attested, if required) and a fee of [?]20/- to the Assistant Labour Commissioners/ Beneficiary Registering Officers.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there a specific form for the registration as a beneficiary under the BOCW Welfare Board?
content.text: Yes, the application for registration must be made in Form-27. You can obtain this form from the Assistant Labour Commissioners/ Beneficiary Registering Officers in your area.

content.section_title: FAQ: What happens after a construction worker is successfully verified for registration?
content.text: Upon successful verification, the construction worker is registered, and an identity card and passbook are issued to him/ her.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the fees associated with the registration process?
content.text: The registration fee is [?]20/-, and the yearly subscription fee is [?]30/-. These amounts are noted in the passbook, and a receipt is issued.

content.section_title: FAQ: How is the yearly subscription fee documented?
content.text: The yearly subscription fee of [?]30/- is recorded in the passbook, and a receipt is issued for the payment.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the process for renewing the subscription after one year?
content.text: After the expiry of one year, the subscription of the beneficiary is renewed upon payment of a renewal fee of [?]30/- and submission of an application made in Form-27A.

content.section_title: FAQ: What happens if the subscription is not renewed after one year?
content.text: If the subscription is not renewed, the registration of the beneficiary is cancelled after the expiry of one year.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can a beneficiary reapply for renewal after cancellation?
content.text: Yes, a fresh application for renewal can be submitted to the Assistant Labour Commissioner (ALC) for consideration.

content.section_title: FAQ: What criteria must an applicant meet to be considered for this benefit?
content.text: A registered female member or wife of a male member who has gone thorough a miscarriage, shall be eligible for this benefit.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the maximum amount the Board may sanction for this benefit?
content.text: The Board may sanction an amount of [?].6,000/- to the beneficiaries under this scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: How many times can the beneficiary avail the benefits?
content.text: The benefit shall be allowed for two times in his/her whole lifetime.

content.section_title: FAQ: What form needs to be used to apply for this welfare scheme?
content.text: The application must be made in the prescribed Form-XII. The form can be collected from Assistant Labour Commissioners/ Beneficiary Registering Officers in your area.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where do I submit the application for this welfare scheme?
content.text: Submit your welfare scheme application to the Assistant Labour Commissioners/ Beneficiary Registering Officers in your area.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The "Mobile Lab Scheme'' by the BOCW Welfare Board, Department of Labour, Punjab, is a welfare scheme that provides reimbursement of expenditure incurred by the beneficiaries and their dependents for the treatment of silicosis.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The "Mobile Lab Scheme'' by the BOCW Welfare Board, Department of Labour, Punjab, is a welfare scheme for the registered workers of the board. Under this Scheme, if a beneficiary or a family member is diagnosed with silicosis, the Board will reimburse up to ₹1,50,000/- for the treatment expenses incurred by the worker.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
- **Financial Coverage:** Up to ₹1,50,000/-.
> **Note:** Any beneficiary who intends to avail the benefit under this scheme will have to first exhaust the benefit of ₹50,000/- for all his health ailments with the health card facility under the Bhagat “Puran Singh Sehat Beema Yojna” Scheme. Only then he/ she can get the remaining benefit under this scheme.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
> ***For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:***

- The applicant should be a resident of Punjab.
- The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker (Unorganised Worker).
- The applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
- The applicant should have engaged in building and construction work for at least 90 days in a given year.
- The applicant should have a monthly income of ₹15,000/- or below.
- The applicant should not be a member of any other Welfare Fund established under any law.
- The applicant should not have membership of the Employees' Provident Fund (EPF)/ National Pension System (NPS)/ Employees' State Insurance Corporation (ESIC).
- The applicant should not be an income tax payer.

> ***For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:***

- The applicant should be a registered member Under the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of Punjab or should be a family member of the worker.
- The applicant should be suffering from silicosis.
- The applicant should have exhausted the benefit of ₹50,000/- for all his/her health ailments with the health card facility under the Bhagat “Puran Singh Sehat Beema Yojna” Scheme.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online - via CSC)
content.text:
> **Registration Process as a Building/ Construction Worker:**

- For new registration of workers please approach the nearby Sewa Kendra or Registering officers/ Labour Inspector.

> ***Application Process of the Welfare Scheme:***

- To apply for benefits of the concerned welfare scheme, please approach to nearby Sewa Kendra or Registering Officers/ Labour Inspector of your area.

> ***Note:***

- **Registration with the Board:** A construction worker becomes a member of the board by filling out Application Form No 28 along with depositing registration fees of ₹ 25/- only (once in life) and ₹ 10/- per month as contribution fees. A worker at a time can get himself registered for a minimum period of 1 year & maximum period of 5 years. The registered worker is called 'Beneficiary' of the Board.

content.text:

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:- Age proof (If no age proof is available self declaration).
- Residence proof.
- Aadhaar card(if available).
- Bank Account details (Bank branch, account No. and IFSC/ RTGS number).
- Detail of dependents.
- Employer’s Certificate (Form No. 28) (90 days of construction work during the last 12 months in the State of Punjab).
- Nomination Form (Form No. 27).
For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:- Medical Certificate of the Beneficiary or Family Member.
- Proof of Expenditure on Treatment.
- Any other documents (if required).

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can register as a beneficiary under the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of Punjab?
content.text: Any construction worker whose age is between 18 to 60 years and has worked in Punjab State in any construction work for a minimum of 90 days in a year, are eligible to register as a beneficiary under the Board.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there a minimum service requirement to register as a beneficiary?
content.text: Yes, the building worker must have completed 90 days of service as a building worker in the preceding 12 months.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can apply for the welfare scheme?
content.text: Any registered worker of the Board can apply for the welfare scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: For how long a construction worker can get registered at a time?
content.text: A worker at a time can get himself registered for a minimum period of 1 year & maximum period of 5 years.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the procedure for applying for the registration?
content.text: Any construction worker who is of 18 years and below 60 years' age and has Worked in Punjab State for not less than 90 days shall apply for registration as construction worker on the prescribed Form No. 28 which is available on the Board's website or from the office of ALC/LCO and Labour Inspectors of the area.

content.section_title: FAQ: Whom to apply for the registration?
content.text: A construction worker who intends to get himself registered as a member of the Board shall apply to the Labour Inspector of his area.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the mandatory documents for the registration?
content.text: The documents required for registration are, age proof(If no age proof is available self declaration), residence proof, aadhaar card(if available), Bank Account details (Bank branch, account No. and IFSC/RTGS number), detail of dependents, and employer's certificate of working for 90 days as construction worker, and nomination form.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there a specific form for the registration through offline mode as a beneficiary under the BOCW Welfare Board?
content.text: Yes, the application for registration must be made in Form No. 28.

content.section_title: FAQ: How much is the fee associated with the registration process?
content.text: The rate of registration fee is [?]25/- at the time of registration. It is a one time fee.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the rate of contribution?
content.text: A registered worker is required to contribute [?]10/-per month as contribution fee to the Board.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who are the family members considered for the benefits?
content.text: Husband or wife, minor sons (including major sons who are insane or physically handicapped and unable to earn), unmarried daughters and the parents of the building worker, who are solely dependent on the registered worker.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible to apply for this scheme?
content.text: To be eligible, the applicant must be a registered member of the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of Punjab or a family member of the worker and should be suffering from silicosis. Additionally, the beneficiary must have exhausted the [?]50,000/- health card benefit under the Bhagat "Puran Singh Sehat Beema Yojna" Scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the conditions to avail of the benefits under this scheme?
content.text: The applicant must first use the [?]50,000/- health card benefit for all health ailments under the Bhagat "Puran Singh Sehat Beema Yojna" Scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the maximum financial coverage provided under this scheme?
content.text: The scheme provides reimbursement up to [?]1,50,000/- for the treatment expenses incurred by the worker.

content.section_title: FAQ: Whom to apply for getting benefits of welfare schemes?
content.text: For the application of the concerned welfare schemes of the Board, the construction worker has to visit the nearest Sewa Kendras or contact the Assistant Labour Commissioner/ Labour-cum-Conciliation Officer/ Labour Inspector/ Labour Enforcement Officer of the concerned area.

title: Motor Vehicle Driver-Conductor Welfare Scheme- Pradhanmantri Suraksha Bima Yojana
topic: Motor Vehicle Driver-Conductor Welfare Scheme- Pradhanmantri Suraksha Bima Yojana

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: This scheme provides accident insurance coverage to registered drivers and conductors, offering financial protection in the event of accidental death or permanent disability.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The “Motor Vehicle Driver-Conductor Welfare Scheme- Pradhanmantri Suraksha Bima Yojana” has been launched by the Transport Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh. The scheme aims to ensure social security and financial protection for registered transport vehicle drivers and conductors. Under this scheme, accidental insurance coverage is provided to the registered drivers and conductors.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
**Accident Insurance Coverage**

- ₹2,00,000 is payable in case of accidental death.
- ₹2,00,000 is payable in case of total and irreversible loss of both eyes, both hands, both feet, or loss of one eye and one limb.
- ₹1,00,000 is payable in case of total and irreversible loss of one eye or one limb.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Madhya Pradesh.
1. The applicant's age should be between 18 to 70 years.
1. The applicant should be a registered transport vehicle driver or conductor in Madhya Pradesh.
1. The applicant should possess a valid commercial driving or conductor license issued in Madhya Pradesh.
1. The applicant should be registered on the Samagra Portal.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
> **Samagra ID Registration Process:**

**Step 1:** Visit the official website: [samagra.gov.in](https://samagra.gov.in/).
**Step 2:** Click on the option “Register Family” or “Register Member”.
**Step 3:** Enter the Aadhaar number and other required information.
**Step 4:** Enter the OTP received on the registered mobile number.
**Step 5:** Carefully fill in all the required details and upload the necessary documents.
**Step 6:** Enter the captcha code and click on “Submit Application.”
> **Offline Samagra ID Registration Process:**

**Step 1:** Visit the nearest Lok Seva Kendra, MP Online Kiosk, or Common Service Center (CSC).
**Step 2:** Provide the required documents and necessary information to complete the registration process.
> **After Samagra Registration:**

**Step 1: Step 1:** The interested applicant downloads the [application form](https://transport.mp.gov.in/sites/default/files/documents/yojna_20150001.pdf) (Page No. 9) from the official website.
**Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
**Step 3:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the District or Regional Transport Office.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Passport-size Photograph
- Samagra ID
- Valid Commercial Driving License or Conductor License
- Aadhaar Card
- Proof of Age
- Accident Report (In Case of Accident)
- Medical Disability Certificate (For Permanent Disability Cases)
- Death Certificate (In Case of Accidental Death)
- Bank Account Details

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the purpose of this component scheme?
content.text: The primary purpose of this component is to provide comprehensive accident insurance coverage specifically tailored for registered drivers and conductors.

content.section_title: FAQ: Which insurance scheme is applicable?
content.text: The insurance coverage is governed by the guidelines and provisions of the Prime Minister’s Security Insurance Scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible for accident insurance?
content.text: Eligibility for this accident insurance is strictly limited to registered transport vehicle drivers and conductors.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the age limit for coverage?
content.text: Applicants must fall within the eligible age bracket of 18 to 70 years to qualify for the coverage.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the insurance amount for accidental death or permanent disability?
content.text: For cases involving accidental death or permanent total disability resulting from an accident, the insurance payout is set at ₹2,00,000.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the insurance amount for permanent partial disability?
content.text: If an accident results in permanent partial disability, the insured individual is entitled to a payout of ₹1,00,000.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is Samagra registration mandatory?
content.text: It is a mandatory requirement for all applicants to have a valid Samagra registration to avail of these benefits.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can other states people apply for this scheme?
content.text:
No, only residents of Madhya Pradesh are eligible.
﻿

content.section_title: FAQ: How can I apply for the scheme?
content.text: The applicant submits the application at the District or Regional Transport Office (RTO) for verification and further processing.

content.section_title: FAQ: What documents are needed to apply?
content.text: The following documents are required: Samagra Family Identification Number (Samagra ID), Samagra Member Identification Number, Aadhaar Card, Proof of Residence (Madhya Pradesh), Valid Commercial Driving or Conductor License, Bank Account Details, Passport-size Photograph, etc.

title: Motor Vehicle Driver-Conductor Welfare Scheme- Medical Assistance
topic: Motor Vehicle Driver-Conductor Welfare Scheme- Medical Assistance

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to provide healthcare and medical assistance to registered drivers, conductors, and their families. Medical benefits are delivered through existing state health schemes.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The “Motor Vehicle Driver-Conductor Welfare Scheme- Medical Assistance” scheme has been launched by the Transport Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh. The scheme aims to provide medical assistance benefits to registered drivers, conductors, and their eligible family members to reduce the financial burden arising from illness. It also enables beneficiaries to receive assistance for high-cost treatments in case of severe diseases.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
- Medical treatment benefits are provided under [Deendayal Antyodaya Upchar Yojana](https://www.mpedistrict.gov.in/MPL/getphoto.aspx?ViewCircular=eVTeefDvS1Q=) through the Public Health and Family Welfare Department.- Financial assistance for serious or life-threatening diseases may be provided under the State Illness Assistance Fund, subject to eligibility and approval.
- The benefit covers the registered driver or conductor and their eligible family members as defined under the scheme.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Madhya Pradesh.
1. The applicant should hold a valid commercial driver or conductor license issued in Madhya Pradesh.
1. The applicant should be registered on the Samagra Portal.
1. The applicant should be a registered transport vehicle driver or conductor under the scheme.
1. The applicant or eligible family member should require medical treatment as per scheme provisions.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
> **Samagra ID Registration Process:**

**Step 1:** Visit the official website: [samagra.gov.in](https://samagra.gov.in/).
**Step 2:** Click on the option “Register Family” or “Register Member”.
**Step 3:** Enter the Aadhaar number and other required information.
**Step 4:** Enter the OTP received on the registered mobile number.
**Step 5:** Carefully fill in all the required details and upload the necessary documents.
**Step 6:** Enter the captcha code and click on “Submit Application.”
> **Offline Samagra ID Registration Process:**

**Step 1:** Visit the nearest Lok Seva Kendra, MP Online Kiosk, or Common Service Center (CSC).
**Step 2:** Provide the required documents and necessary information to complete the registration process.
> **After Samagra Registration:**

**Step 1: Step 1:** The interested applicant downloads the [application form](https://transport.mp.gov.in/sites/default/files/documents/yojna_20150001.pdf) (Page No. 9) from the official website.
**Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
**Step 3:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the District or Regional Transport Office.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Passport-size Photograph
- Samagra ID
- Commercial Driver or Conductor License
- Proof of Residence
- Medical Certificate or Hospital Prescription (In Case of Treatment)
- Serious Illness Certificate (Only in Case of Critical Disease Assistance)
- Identity Proof
- Family Details Linked with Samagra ID

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the purpose of medical assistance?
content.text: To provide healthcare and financial medical support to registered drivers, conductors, and their families.

content.section_title: FAQ: Which department launched the scheme?
content.text: The scheme was launched by the Transport Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible for medical assistance?
content.text: Registered drivers, conductors, and their eligible family members are eligible.

content.section_title: FAQ: Which health scheme provides treatment benefits?
content.text: Treatment benefits are provided through the Deendayal Antyodaya Upchar Yojana.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is assistance available for serious diseases?
content.text: Yes, assistance may be provided under the State Illness Assistance Fund.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is Samagra registration mandatory?
content.text: Yes, Samagra registration is mandatory.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can other states people apply for this scheme?
content.text: No, only residents of Madhya Pradesh are eligible.

content.section_title: FAQ: How Can I register for Samagra ID?
content.text: The applicant visits the Samagra [official website](https://samagra.gov.in/) for the registration.

content.section_title: FAQ: What documents are needed to apply?
content.text: The following documents are required: Samagra Family Identification Number (Samagra ID), Samagra Member Identification Number, Aadhaar Card, Proof of Residence (Madhya Pradesh), Valid Commercial Driving or Conductor License, Bank Account Details, Passport-size Photograph, etc.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can I apply for the scheme?
content.text:
The applicant submitting the application at the District or Regional Transport Office (RTO) for verification and further processing.
﻿

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: "AYUSHMAN BHARAT PM-JAY MUKH MANTRI SEHAT BIMA YOJNA” is a flagship State Health Insurance Program of the Government of Punjab.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: Ayushman Bharat PM-JAY Mukhmantri Sehat Bima Yojana (AB PM-JAY MMSBY), was launched on 20 August 2019 this is a flagship State Health Insurance Scheme for the beneficiaires of State of Punjab. AB PM-JAY MMSBY will provide financial protection to 65% population of state of Punjab. It is entitlement based cashless Health Insurance cover. Under this scheme cashless and paperless treatment is available at Government and empaneled Private hospitals.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: - A cashless health insurance  cover of ₹5,00,000/- rupees per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization (in-patient services) for treatment of listed diseases and medical conditions.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. Residents of Punjab.
1. Applicants should be from the following categories

- SECC 2011 data families,
- Smart Ration Card holder families,
- J-Form holder farmers,
- Small traders registered with Excise & Taxation Department,
- Small & Marginal Farmers,
- Accredited & Yellow Card Journalist and Construction workers registered with Punjab Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
> **REGISTRATION PROCESS FOR E-CARD GENERATION**

- Now generate e-card from home without visiting anywhere: Download 'Ayushman App' on mobile or visit [https://beneficiary.nha.gov.in/](https://beneficiary.nha.gov.in/) to self-register.- Please visit any empaneled private or public hospital, lists available under '[Hospitals](https://sha.punjab.gov.in/shapb/publicPages/hospSpltyView)' on SHA website for getting e-card generation.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Aadhaar Card.
- Ration Card.
- Family Declaration. (If no Ration Card).
- Income Certificate.
- Construction Worker Card. (If belong to that Category).

content.section_title: FAQ: What is this scheme ?
content.text: "AYUSHMAN BHARAT PM-JAY MUKH MANTRI SEHAT BIMA YOJNA" is a flagship State Health Insurance Program of the Government of Punjab.

content.section_title: FAQ: What health services are available under AB PM-JAY MMSBY?
content.text: The Scheme covers 1579 treatment packages that include hospitalization expenses, daycare surgeries, 3 days pre and 15 days post-hospitalization expenses and treatment of newborn child.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the maximum limit of provided health insurance ?
content.text: Health insurance cover of 5 lakh rupees per family per year.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can get the benefits?
content.text: All the eligible resident of Punjab.

content.section_title: FAQ: How to apply for the scheme?
content.text: This is an entitlement-based scheme and no enrolment process for the families who are identified by the Government of India.

content.section_title: FAQ: What documents are required for this ?
content.text:
1. Aadhaar Card.
2. Ration Card.
3. Family Declaration. (If no Ration Card).
4. Income Certificate.
5. Construction Worker Card. (If belong to that Category).

content.section_title: FAQ: How to check e-cards of AB PM-JAY MMSBY
content.text: Follow the below URL and enter the e-card & captcha details.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can those families whose names are not on the list avail the benefits under AB PM-JAY MMSBY?
content.text:
In this phase, no additional new families can be added under AB PM-JAY MMSBY. However, names of additional family members can be added only in case of families of J-Form holder farmers, families of registered Small traders and families of registered construction workers based on the family identification document produced by the Beneficiary at the point of contact. The family is defined as under:
"Beneficiary Family Unit of Department Families will comprise of the Head of the Family/Main Member and any of his or her following relatives, irrespective of whether or not they are dependent or residing with the Main Member:

content.section_title: FAQ: Will e-card be given to the beneficiary?
content.text:
Now generate e-card from home without visiting anywhere: Download 'Ayushman App' on mobile or visit beneficiary.nha.gov.in to self-register.

Please visit any empaneled private or public hospital, lists available under 'Hospitals' on SHA website for getting e-card generation.

title: Mukhya Mantri Himachal Health Care Scheme-HIMCARE
topic: Mukhya Mantri Himachal Health Care Scheme-HIMCARE

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: Mukhya Mantri Himachal Health Care Scheme-HIMCARE is a health insurance scheme providing cashless treatment coverage up to Rs. 5.00 lakh per year per family in empanelled hospitals. It aims to cover families not included in the Ayushman Bharat scheme.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
**Mukhya Mantri Himachal Health Care Scheme-HIMCARE** is a health insurance scheme under the Department of Health & Family Welfare, Government of Himachal Pradesh to provide cashless treatment coverage up to ₹5,00,000/- per year per family provided in the empanelled hospitals. In case of more than five members, the remaining members are enrolled as a separate unit subject to the capping of five members for each such additional unit. This scheme is being implemented on a co-payment basis. Hospitals empanelled under Ayushman Bharat within the State are also empanelled for HIMCARE and are providing cashless treatment to the beneficiaries by making online entries through Transaction Management System. The claims are being reimbursed through the host-to-host payment system directly in the bank account of the concerned hospital after checking the complete claim papers submitted by these institutions through the online system.

1. For target groups covering below categories have the Zero Premium amount.

- BPL (Not covered under Ayushman Bharat),
- Registered Street Vendors (Not covered under Ayushman Bharat),
- MNREGA worker who have worked minimum 50 days under MNREGA during previous financial year or current financial year.
- Children living in Orphanages.

1. For target groups covering below categories have the premium rate ₹365/- per year.

- Ekal Naaris,
- Disabled >40%,
- Mid-Day meal workers,
- Daily Wage Workers (Govt., Autonomous Bodies, Societies, Boards & Corporations etc. under the control of State Government),
- Part Time Workers (Govt., Autonomous Bodies, Societies, Boards & Corporations etc. under the control of State Government)
- Contractual Employees (Govt., Autonomous Bodies, Societies, Boards & Corporations etc. under the control of State Government)
- Outsource Employees.

1. Beneficiaries not covered under category-I and category-II or who are not Govt. servants/pensioners or their dependent family members, having the premium amount ₹1,000/- per year.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
1. Cashless Treatment: The scheme provides cashless treatment coverage, allowing beneficiaries to receive medical services without upfront payment at empanelled hospitals.
1. Comprehensive Coverage: HIMCARE offers coverage up to ₹5,00,000/- per year per family, ensuring that families have access to quality healthcare services without financial burden.
1. Inclusion of Previously Uncovered Families: The scheme targets families who were not covered under the Ayushman Bharat scheme, extending health insurance benefits to a broader population.
1. Categorization for Differential Premium Rates: Beneficiaries are categorized into different groups based on their occupation or socioeconomic status, with corresponding premium rates to make healthcare more affordable for all eligible individuals.
1. Wide Range of Eligible Beneficiaries: The scheme covers various groups such as BPL families, registered street vendors, MNREGA workers, senior citizens above 70 years, children living in orphanages, disabled individuals, Anganwari workers, ASHA workers, and more.
1. Easy Enrollment Process: Enrollment can be done online through the official website or at Lok Mitra Kendra/Common Service Centers, making it convenient for beneficiaries to apply and upload necessary documents.
1. Empanelled Hospital Network: Beneficiaries can receive cashless treatment in empanelled hospitals within Himachal Pradesh, including prestigious institutions like PGIMER Chandigarh and GMCH Sector 32 Chandigarh, ensuring access to quality healthcare facilities.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant must be a permanent resident of Himachal Pradesh.
1. Families not covered under the Ayushman Bharat Yojana (Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana) are eligible to apply for the HIMCARE scheme.
1. Applicant should be HIMCARE card holder.
1. The scheme is available to Below Poverty Line (BPL) families, Above Poverty Line (APL) families, Senior citizens and daily wage earners, widows, single women, and persons with disabilities.

content.section_title: Exclusions
content.text:
1. Non-Coverage of Government Servants and Pensioners: The scheme does not provide coverage for government servants, pensioners, or their dependent family members. They are not eligible to enroll in HIMCARE.
1. Non-Coverage of Beneficiaries Already Covered Under Ayushman Bharat: Families that have already been selected and covered under the Ayushman Bharat scheme, based on SECC 2011 and RSBY, are not eligible for coverage under HIMCARE. The scheme targets those left out of Ayushman Bharat.
1. Non-Coverage of Beneficiaries Who Do Not Fall under Eligible Categories: Individuals who do not fall under the eligible categories defined by the scheme, such as those not belonging to BPL families, registered street vendors, MNREGA workers, senior citizens above 70 years, or other specified groups, are excluded from the coverage provided by HIMCARE.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online - via CSC)
content.text:
1. Locate the nearest Lok Mitra Kendra (LMK) or [**Common Service Center**](https://ddtg.hp.gov.in/csc/) (CSC).   1. Visit the LMK or CSC and inform them about your intention to apply for HIMCARE.
1. Provide the necessary information and documents to the staff at the center.
1. VLEs will assist you in filling out the application form and uploading the required documents online.
1. If you are applying through Lok Mitra Kendra/Common Service Centers, you may be required to pay an enrollment fee of ₹50/- per family unit of up to five members.
1. Once the application is submitted, it will be processed for verification. The concerned authorities will review the application and documents provided for authenticity and eligibility.
1. If the application is approved, an E-card will be issued to the eligible family.
1. The E-card will serve as proof of enrollment and will be used for availing cashless treatment under the scheme.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
1. Apply for HIMCARE card on official [**website**](https://app.hpsbys.in/website/rationcardregistration.aspx).    1. Enter the ration card details and upload the Ration card scanned copy in the application.
1. Fill in the required details accurately, including personal information, contact details, and family details.
1. Once applicant has confirmed the Application form ,he/she won't be able to edit the application and if you are eligible for Premium Amount , then applicant will have to make payment within 30 days.
1. Upload the scanned copies of the required documents as per the category you fall under.
1. Submit the application form along with the uploaded documents.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
The following documents are mandatory for enrollment under HIMCARE:
CategoryDocuments required for authenticationBPLCopy of BPL certificate attested by the Panchayat Secretary within previous one month.Registered Street VendorsRegistration Certificate attested by the Executive Officer, MC/NP/NAC within previous one month.MNREGA WorkerMNREGA Job Card and Online MIS Report indicating 50 days work under MNREGA in previous or current financial year duly attested by concerned Panchayat Secretary/BDO.Ekal NaarisCertificate to be issued by Child Development Program Officer (CDPO) of the concerned area and shall include Widows/Divorced/Legally Separated /Unmarried more than 40 years.Disabled >40%Medical Disability Certificate showing permanent disability.Mid-Day meal workersCertificate from Block Elementary Education Officer of the concerned area.Contractual EmployeesCertification from concerned Department.Daily Wage WorkerCertification from concerned Department.Part Time WorkersCertification from concerned Department.Outsource EmployeesCertification from concerned Department.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the coverage amount provided by HIMCARE?
content.text: HIMCARE provides cashless treatment coverage of up to Rs. 5.00 lakh per year per family in empanelled hospitals.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible to apply for HIMCARE?
content.text: Eligibility criteria vary based on categories, including BPL families, registered street vendors, MNREGA workers, senior citizens above 70 years, children in orphanages, and more. Refer to the scheme's guidelines for specific eligibility details.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can government servants and pensioners apply for HIMCARE?
content.text: No, government servants, pensioners, and their dependent family members are not eligible for HIMCARE coverage.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can I apply for HIMCARE?
content.text: You can apply for HIMCARE online through the official website (www.hpsbys.in) or visit Lok Mitra Kendra/Common Service Centers for assistance with the enrollment process.

content.section_title: FAQ: What documents are required for enrollment?
content.text: The required documents vary based on the category. They may include BPL certificates, registration certificates, job cards, age proof, disability certificates, and certificates issued by relevant authorities. Refer to the scheme's documentation requirements for specific details.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there an enrollment fee for HIMCARE?
content.text: Yes, if you apply through Lok Mitra Kendra/Common Service Centers, you may be required to pay an enrollment fee of Rs. 50 per family unit of up to five members.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can I avail treatment at hospitals outside Himachal Pradesh under HIMCARE?
content.text: Generally, the scheme covers treatment at empanelled hospitals within Himachal Pradesh. However, beneficiaries can also receive treatment at PGIMER Chandigarh and GMCH Sector 32 Chandigarh.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can I change my category or update my information after enrollment?
content.text: It is important to apply under the relevant category and provide accurate information during the enrollment process. Changes or updates after enrollment may not be allowed, so it's advisable to double-check the information before submitting the application.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The State Government has launched the 'Sahara Scheme' to provide financial assistance of Rs. 3000 per month to the patients belonging to the economically weaker sections of the society, who are suffering from specified diseases such Parkinson, Malignant Cancer, Paralysis etc.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The Government of Himachal Pradesh has launched the 'Sahara Scheme' to provide financial assistance to patients from economically weaker sections of society suffering from specified diseases such as Parkinson's disease, Malignant Cancer, Paralysis, Muscular Dystrophy, Hemophilia, and Thalassemia. The scheme also covers patients suffering from chronic renal failure or any other disease that permanently incapacitates a person. The objective of the scheme is to provide financial assistance to patients suffering from fatal diseases as a social security measure, to mitigate the hardships faced during prolonged treatment.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1.	The applicant must be a resident of Himachal Pradesh.
2.	The applicant must belong to the economically weaker section of society.
3.	The applicant must be suffering from specified diseases such as Parkinson's disease, Malignant Cancer, Paralysis, Muscular Dystrophy, Hemophilia, and Thalassemia, or chronic renal failure or any other disease that renders a person permanently incapacitated.
4.	The applicant must not be a beneficiary of any other government-sponsored pension scheme.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
1.	Residence proof - The applicant must provide a document that verifies their residence in Himachal Pradesh, such as a voter ID card, ration card, Aadhaar card, or a valid passport.
2.	Income certificate - The applicant must provide an income certificate that proves that they belong to the economically weaker section of society.
3.	Medical documents - The applicant must provide medical documents that prove they are suffering from specified diseases such as Parkinson's disease, Malignant Cancer, Paralysis, Muscular Dystrophy, Hemophilia, and Thalassemia, or chronic renal failure or any other disease that renders a person permanently incapacitated.
4.	Bank account details - The applicant must provide bank account details that will be used to transfer the financial assistance amount.
5.	Passport-size photograph - The applicant must provide a recent passport size photograph.
6.	Any other relevant documents - Depending on the case, the authorities may require additional documents.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who Is Eligible For The Sahara Scheme?
content.text: Patients belonging to economically weaker sections of the society who are suffering from specified diseases such as Parkinson's disease, Malignant Cancer, Paralysis, Muscular Dystrophy, Hemophilia, and Thalassemia, or chronic renal failure or any other disease that renders a person permanently incapacitated are eligible for the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What Is The Financial Assistance Provided Under The Scheme?
content.text: The eligible beneficiaries will receive financial assistance of Rs. 3,000 per month under the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: How Can I Apply For The Scheme?
content.text: Eligible beneficiaries can apply for the scheme through the official website of the Department of Health & Family Welfare of Himachal Pradesh https://sahara.hpsbys.in/Home/Default or by submitting the application form at the nearest block development office or panchayat.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can A Person Who Is Already Receiving Pension From Another Government Scheme Apply For The Sahara Scheme?
content.text: No, a person who is already receiving a pension from another government scheme is not eligible to apply for the Sahara Scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is There Any Fee To Apply For The Scheme?
content.text: No, there is no fee to apply for the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can The Financial Assistance Amount Be Transferred To A Joint Bank Account?
content.text: No, the financial assistance amount can only be transferred to the bank account of the eligible beneficiary.

content.section_title: FAQ: How Often Will The Financial Assistance Amount Be Credited To The Beneficiary's Bank Account?
content.text: The financial assistance amount will be credited to the beneficiary's bank account on a monthly basis.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to improve the health of children between 3 to 6 years of age by providing them fortified and flavored milk containing Vitamins A and D, four days a week, at Anganwadi Centres.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The scheme "Mukhyamantri Anchal Amrit Yojana" has been launched by the Department of Women Empowerment & Child Development, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to improve the health of children between 3 to 6 years of age by providing them fortified and flavored milk containing Vitamins A and D, four days a week, at Anganwadi Centres.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: Under this scheme, children between 3 to 6 years of age are provided fortified and flavored milk enriched with Vitamins A and D, four days a week.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The beneficiary must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
1. The beneficiary must be a child between 3 and 6 years of age.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text: Children who go to/are registered at the Anganwadi Centre receive benefits directly at the centre.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text: - Birth certificate of the child

content.section_title: FAQ: Who are the beneficiaries of this scheme?
content.text: Children between 3 to 6 years of age are eligible to receive benefits under the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What benefits are provided under the scheme?
content.text: Eligible children are provided fortified and flavoured milk containing Vitamins A and D, four days a week.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where do children receive these benefits?
content.text: Benefits are provided directly at the Anganwadi Centres.

content.section_title: FAQ: How often is milk provided to children?
content.text: Milk is provided four days a week.

content.section_title: FAQ: What types of milk are given under the scheme?
content.text: Children are provided both fortified and flavoured milk enriched with Vitamins A and D.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any cost for the beneficiaries?
content.text: No, the benefits are provided free of cost at the Anganwadi Centres.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can children receive benefits at home?
content.text: No, benefits are provided only at the Anganwadi Centres.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The "Mukhyamantri Abua Swasthya Suraksha Yojana" by the Department of Health and Family Welfare, Govt. of Jharkhand aims to provide quality healthcare services to eligible families. Beneficiaries receive ₹15,00,000/- per family per year as health insurance coverage for advanced medical treatments.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The "Mukhyamantri Abua Swasthya Suraksha Yojana" by the Department of Health and Family Welfare, Government of Jharkhand, aims to provide quality and affordable healthcare services to the people of Jharkhand. The scheme provides a yearly health insurance cover per family, ensuring access to advanced medical treatments. The Jharkhand State Arogya Society implements the scheme and operates in a hybrid mode to maximize efficiency and reach.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
1. Health insurance cover of ₹15,00,000/- per family per year.
1. Coverage includes expenses for both general treatments and critical illnesses listed under the scheme.
1. Beneficiaries can access services at empaneled public and private hospitals.
1. Includes treatment costs for diabetes, blood pressure, and dialysis, with free medicines provided at government hospitals.
1. Beneficiaries also gain access to treatment for [21 critical illnesses](https://www.jharkhand.gov.in/PDepartment/ViewDoc?id=D011DO003SD00302072024050748252) under the existing Mukhyamantri Gambhir Bimari Upchar Yojana.

**NOTE:**

1. Temporary health cards will be issued for urgent treatments based on the beneficiary’s ration card.
1. The first ₹1,00,000/- of coverage is managed by a selected insurance company, while the remaining up to ₹15,00,000/- is borne by the government in trust mode.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Jharkhand.
1. The applicant’s family should possess a ration card under the National Food Security Act (NFSA) or Jharkhand State Food Security Act.
1. The applicant's have pink, yellow, and green ration cards are eligible under the scheme.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
***Mukhyamantri Abua Swasthya Suraksha Card Apply Process:***
**Step 1: Visit the Official Portal**

- Go to the official website of Mukhyamantri Abua Swasthya Suraksha Yojana: [https://bis.jharkhand.gov.in/.](https://bis.jharkhand.gov.in/search)
**Step 2: Login Using Mobile Number**

- Enter your mobile number and click on Get OTP.

- Enter the OTP received on your mobile phone.
- Fill in the Captcha code and proceed.

**Step 3: Search Using Ration Card**

- Enter your Ration Card Number and Captcha code.

- Click on the Search button.
- The portal will display the list of family members based on the ration card details.

**Step 4: Download the Health Card**

- If the details of the applicant match the Ration Card and Aadhaar Card, you can download the card directly.
- Click on Download to save your card.

***If e-KYC is Incomplete:***
Follow these steps to complete e-KYC and apply:
**Step 1: Complete Aadhaar Verification**

- Click on the e-KYC option.
- Enter your Aadhaar Number and select the verification method:
- Aadhaar OTP (sent to the mobile number linked with Aadhaar).
- Fingerprint verification.
- Accept the Terms and Conditions (T&C).
- Enter the OTP and click on Verify.

**Step 2: Rectify Details (If Needed)**

- If the details in the Ration Card and Aadhaar Card do not match, you will not be able to download the card immediately.
- The portal will provide an option to update or correct the mismatched details.
- Submit the corrected details for verification.

**Step 3: Re-Apply and Download**

- Once the details are updated and verified, repeat the above steps.
- Download the card after successful verification.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Aadhaar Card (Linked with Ration Card)
- Ration Card (Pink, Yellow, or Green)

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible for the scheme?
content.text: Families with ration cards under NFSA or Jharkhand Food Security Act and SECC-2011 data beneficiaries are eligible.

content.section_title: FAQ: What illnesses are covered under the scheme?
content.text: Both general and critical illnesses, including 21 major diseases like cancer, heart surgery, and kidney transplants, are covered.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can I apply for the scheme?
content.text: Apply online through the state health portal using your Aadhaar and ration card details.

content.section_title: FAQ: What if I don’t have a health card yet?
content.text: Temporary health cards are issued based on ration card details for urgent treatments.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the validity of the health insurance coverage?
content.text: The coverage is valid for one year and is renewable annually.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can I avail of the scheme benefits outside Jharkhand?
content.text: Benefits are restricted to empaneled hospitals within Jharkhand.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the hybrid mode of implementation?
content.text: It means partial coverage by an insurance company and the remaining by the state in trust mode.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can I verify if my family is included in the scheme?
content.text: Check eligibility through the state health portal or at district health offices.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are private hospitals included in the scheme?
content.text: Yes, empaneled private hospitals provide services under the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can additional family members be added later?
content.text: Yes, families can update their details online for inclusion of additional members.

title: Mukhyamantri Anusuchit Janjati Gram Vikas Yojana
topic: Mukhyamantri Anusuchit Janjati Gram Vikas Yojana

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme "Mukhyamantri Anusuchit Janjati Gram Vikas Yojana" by the Welfare Department, Govt. of Jharkhand, aims to uplift Scheduled Tribe communities through education, health, & economic initiatives. Financial grants, equipment, & training programs are provided for holistic village development.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The scheme "Mukhyamantri Anusuchit Janjati Gram Vikas Yojana" by the Department of Scheduled Tribe, Scheduled Caste, Minority & Backward Class Welfare, Govt. of Jharkhand, aims to promote the social, economic, educational, and health development of Scheduled Tribe communities in tribal-dominated villages. Under this scheme, benefits such as financial grants, equipment, training programs, & healthcare services are provided to the tribal population to ensure their holistic development.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
1. Financial support of ₹1,00,000/-  to women self-help groups (SHGs) in selected villages as a grant.
1. Assistance of up to ₹2,00,000/-  for self-employment or equipment for unemployed educated youth.
1. Organization of regular health camps focusing on diseases like malaria, sickle cell anemia, and other chronic illnesses.
1. Support for tribal-specific economic activities, including purchasing lac and minor forest produce at minimum support prices.
1. Training programs for women SHGs and unemployed youth through selected NGOs and professional institutions.
1. Provision of forward marketing linkage to enhance economic activities.
1. Promotion of integrated village development plans under traditional village leaders’ supervision.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
**Eligibility For SHGs:**

1. The group must be selected by the Gram Sabha.
1. The applicant should belong to a Scheduled Tribe community.
1. The applicant’s village should have at least 80% tribal population.
1. The village should be included in the list of selected villages under the scheme.

**Eligibility For youth self-employment:**

1. The applicant should be an unemployed educated youth.
1. The applicant must have passed at least 10th grade.
1. Priority will be given to those with vocational training.
1. The applicant should belong to a Scheduled Tribe community.
1. The applicant’s village should have at least 80% tribal population.
1. The village should be included in the list of selected villages under the scheme.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Step 1:** The interested applicant should visit (during office hours) the District Welfare Office and request a hard copy of the prescribed format of the application form from the staff exclusively entrusted to issue and collect filled-in applications.
**Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
**Step 3:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the District Welfare Office.
**Step 4:** Request a receipt or acknowledgment from the District Welfare Office to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Scheduled Tribe Certificate.
- Address Proof (Aadhaar Card, Voter ID, etc.).
- Educational Certificate (minimum 10th-grade pass).
- SHG Registration Certificate (for SHGs applying for grants).
- Gram Sabha Resolution Document (for selected SHGs or villages).
- Training Certificate (if applicable).
- Bank Account Details.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible to apply for the scheme?
content.text: Scheduled Tribe individuals or SHGs from villages with at least 80% tribal population are eligible.

content.section_title: FAQ: How are villages selected under the scheme?
content.text: Villages with over 80% tribal population are identified and approved by the Welfare Department.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are health camps part of the scheme?
content.text: Yes, regular health camps are organized to address health issues like malaria and sickle cell anemia.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the role of Gram Sabha in the scheme?
content.text: The Gram Sabha selects SHGs and identifies eligible youth for the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who monitors the implementation of the scheme?
content.text: Committees at the state, district, and village levels monitor the scheme's implementation.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the role of NGOs in the scheme?
content.text: NGOs provide training and support in forward marketing linkage for SHGs and youth.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can vocational training enhance eligibility?
content.text: Yes, priority is given to applicants with vocational training.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can SHGs use the financial grant?
content.text: The grant can be used to initiate economic activities and enhance group sustainability.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any assistance for marketing tribal products?
content.text: Yes, forward marketing linkages are established to promote tribal products.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the frequency of scheme monitoring?
content.text: Committees meet quarterly or monthly to evaluate and review the scheme's progress.

title: Mahatma Jyotirao Phule Jan Arogya Yojana
topic: Mahatma Jyotirao Phule Jan Arogya Yojana

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The Mahatma Jyotirao Phule Jan Arogya Yojana, launched by the Government of Maharashtra, offers cashless treatment for identified diseases through a network of government and private healthcare providers.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The Mahatma Jyotirao Phule Jan Arogya Yojana (MJPJAY), launched by the Government of Maharashtra under  Public Health and Family Welfare Department,The scheme provides cashless medical services for secondary and tertiary treatments through empaneled hospitals. Initially targeted at low-income families, the scheme now covers all families in the state, offering up to ₹1.5 lakh under MJPJAY and ₹5 lakh under Ayushman Bharat annually. From 1st July 2024, it ensures comprehensive, cashless healthcare for the entire population of Maharashtra.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
- **Health Coverage for Categories A to E (Excluding D):** ₹5 lakhs per family per year on a floater basis under both schemes.- **Health Coverage for Category D:** ₹1 lakh per person per year.

**Medical Benefits**
• For categories A to E (Excluding D) - 1356 Health Benefits Packages under 34 specialities.
• For category D – 184 Health Benefits Packages for Road accident patients.
**Follow-up treatment**

- 262 Health Benefits Packages for both schemes (for categories A to E)

**Treatment Reserved for Govt. Hospitals**

-  119 Health Benefits Packages for both the schemes (for categories A to E)

**Benefits under Health Packages**
The health packages provide comprehensive coverage, ensuring a cashless treatment experience for patients. The benefits include:
**Hospitalization Costs**

- Bed charges in general wards.
- Nursing and boarding charges.
- Surgeons, anaesthetists, medical practitioners, and consultants' fees.

**Medical Expenses**

- Charges for oxygen, O.T., and ICU facilities.
- Cost of surgical appliances, drugs, disposables, consumables, implants, and prosthetic devices.
- Expenses for blood transfusion (as per State Government policy).

**Diagnostics and Support Services**

- X-rays and diagnostic tests.
- Food for inpatients during hospitalization.

**Transportation Costs**

- One-time transport cost by State Transport or second-class rail fare from the hospital to the patient’s residence.
- In the unfortunate event of a patient's death, the cost of transporting the deceased's body from the network hospital to their village or township.
- The package covers all costs from the date of reporting to discharge, including treatment complications, ensuring a truly cashless experience for patients.

**Scheme on Assurance Mode**
The Integrated Scheme is being implemented on an Assurance Basis. This means that the claims of the network hospitals for the expenses of the patients undergoing treatment under the scheme are being paid directly by the State Health Assurance Societies to the respective network hospitals. Accordingly, the State Health Assurance Society is paying claims up to ₹5 lakh per family per year of groups A, B, C and E and ₹1 lakh per person per year for road accident victims of group D.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
**Category A:** Families holding Yellow, Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY), Annapurna Yojna and Orange Ration Card.
**Category B**
• White ration card holder families (including Govt./Semi Gov employees).
• A family without having any kind of ration card but having domicile certificate of Maharashtra
**Category C**
• Students in Govt. and Govt. recognized Ashram School
• Children in Govt. and Govt. recognized Orphanages
• Women in Govt. and Govt. recognized Women's Ashram
• Sr. citizens in Govt. and Govt. recognized old age homes
• Journalists and their dependent family members as per criteria of Directorate General of Information and Public Relations Office.
• Construction workers and their families (who are resident of outside of Maharashtra) registered with Maharashtra Building and Other Construction Workers Board.
**Category D**

- Road traffic accident victims from outside Maharashtra and outside India who met with an accident on roads in Maharashtra

**Category E**
Families holding below mentioned ration cards from 865 villages of Belgaon, Karwar, Kalburgi and Bidar Districts of Maharashtra- Karnataka border
1) Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY)
2) Priority House Holder (PHH)
3) Annapurna Yojna

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Step 1:** The interested applicant should visit the  any Govt./Pvt. network hospital in State, and request the hard copy of the prescribed format of the application form from the concerned authority.
**Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste the passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
**Step 3:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents, within the prescribed period (if any), to the authority.
**Step 4:** Request a receipt or acknowledgement from the concerned authority to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).
**NOTE:** Ensure that the application is submitted within the prescribed period, if any.
> **Note 1:** The claim settlement module along with electronic clearance and the payment gateway will be part of the workflow in the State Health Assurance Society (SHAS) portal and will be operated by the Insurer.

> **Note 2:** The reports will be available for scrutiny on the State Health Assurance Society (SHAS) [login.](https://www.jeevandayee.gov.in/MJPJAY/FrontServlet?requestType=CommonRH&actionVal=RightFrame&page=undefined%3E%3E%3Cb%3EMJPJAY%20Login%3C/b%3E&pageName=MJPJAY_Login&mainMenu=MJPJAY_Login&subMenu=MJPJAY_Login)

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
List of valid photo ID proofs to be accepted along with document of eligible criteria:
1. Aadhaar Card or Aadhaar Registration Slip with a photo of the beneficiary. An Aadhaar Card would be insisted upon as an identification document and in the absence of an Aadhaar card/number; any document that is accepted for issuance of an Aadhaar card will also be accepted.
2. Pan Card
3. Voter ID
4. Driving License
5. School/College Id
6. Passport
7. Freedom Fighter ID card
8. Health Card of RGJAY / MJPJAY
9. Handicap Certificate
10. Nationalized Bank Passbook with Photo
11. Senior citizen card issued by the Central Government or State Government
12. Defense ex-servicemen card issued by Sainik Board
13. Marine Fishery Identity card (Issued by Ministry of Agriculture / Fisheries Department Government of Maharashtra).
14. Any photo ID proof issued by the Government of Maharashtra/ Government of India

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the full form of MJPJAY?
content.text: MJPJAY's full form is Mahatma Jyotiba Phule Jan Arogya Yojana.

content.section_title: FAQ: When was MJPJAY started?
content.text: MJPJAY was launched in 28 districts of Maharashtra on April 1st, 2020.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the objective of MJPJAY?
content.text: MJPJAY aims to provide cashless, quality medical care to the beneficiaries under the scheme for catastrophic illnesses requiring hospitalization for surgery and treatment under identified specialized services through a network of health care providers.

content.section_title: FAQ: How much assistance is available under MJPJAY Yojana?
content.text: MJPJAY Yojana 2024 provides coverage up to [?] 1,50,000/- per policy year per family to meet all expenses related to hospitalization of the beneficiary. For renal transplants, this limit has been increased to [?] 2,50,000/- per family per policy year.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible for receiving Maintenance Haemodialysis treatment under MJPJAY?
content.text: A patient suffering from chronic kidney disease requiring maintenance HD and who has a health card is eligible. If the patient does not have a health card, he or she should have an orange or yellow ration card or an Antyodaya or Annapurna card along with valid ID proof.

content.section_title: FAQ: Which services are provided under package of Maintenance Haemodialysis under MJPJAY for the patient?
content.text:
The package includes pre- and post-treatment diagnostics,consultation, hospital charges, consumables, and medicines.
The detailed package is as follows:
>> Investigations
Creatinine tests (Monthly)
Complete blood count (CBC, Monthly)
HIV/Hep.A/Hep.C tests (once every three months)
Serum electrolytes (as per advise)
Blood glucose, if required
>> Consultion
Clinical examination by Nephrologist/Medical Cordinator.
>> Consumable
-Dialyser (It will be used upto optimal capacity and replaced if needed free of charge under scheme),
-Tubing (It will be used upto optimal capacity and replaced if needed free of charge under scheme),
- Fistula Needle (It will be used upto optimal capacity and replaced if needed free of charge under scheme).
- IV fluids,
- Haemodialysis Part A/B and
o Heparin.
>> Medicines
Inj. Erythropoetin, as per the advice of Nephrologist

content.section_title: FAQ: What should be frequency of dialysis cycles under MJPJAY for each approved preauth?
content.text: The frequency of dialysis should be a minimum of 8 cycles and a maximum of 12 cycles, as recommended by the nephrologist or medical coordinator based on his or her serum creatinine level.

content.section_title: FAQ: Does patient have to pay any extra money for treatment?
content.text: No. The patient should not pay any extra money to the hospital or center for services that are covered under the approved package.

content.section_title: FAQ: Will Balance Sum Insured used for dialysis patient limit other household members to avail services under MJPJAY?
content.text: The maximum sum assured under MJPJAY is [?] 1,50,000/- per family, on a family-floater basis. If the dialysis patient has utilized a particular amount from the sum assured, other family members can still avail services under MJPJAY till the total amount of [?] 1,50,000/- gets exhausted. This amount of Sum Insured cannot get extended under any circumstances (except if patient requires a renal transplant, where BSI will be considered as [?] 2,50,000/-)

content.section_title: FAQ: Can HIV or HCV positive cases avail Maintenance Haemodialysis service under MJPJAY ?
content.text: Yes. If a patient is a beneficiary of MJPJAY and is HIV or HCV positive, then he or she can still avail services under MJPJAY. Empanelled hospitals and stand-alone dialysis centers must have dedicated machines for HIV or HCV-positive patients. Under no circumstances shall these units be used for other patients.

content.section_title: FAQ: If patient requires blood transfusion, is cost of blood transfusion covered under package?
content.text: No. Maintenance Haemodialysis package does not cover the cost of a blood transfusion.

content.section_title: FAQ: In case of a query or to register a complaint what should I do ?
content.text: In case of a query, you can contact Arogyamitra or call the toll-free number. Arogyamitra is available round the clock in the hospital or center and will facilitate and guide you if any problem arises. But if you are not satisfied, then call our toll-free numbers, 155388 or 18002332200, to register a complaint.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: "Mamta Taruni Abhiyan" was a program launched in Gujarat, India, aimed at providing healthcare facilities to adolescent girls and pregnant women in rural areas. The program was launched by the Gujarat state government in 2010.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
**Details**
Under this program, free medical check-ups, iron and folic acid supplements, and deworming medication were provided to adolescent girls between the ages of 10 and 19 years, and pregnant women. The program also aimed to create awareness about menstrual hygiene, family planning, and nutrition among adolescent girls and pregnant women.
The program was implemented through a network of health workers, including Accredited Social Health Activists (ASHAs), Auxiliary Nurse Midwives (ANMs), and Anganwadi workers. The Mamta Taruni Abhiyan was seen as a significant step towards improving maternal and child health in rural areas of Gujarat.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
**Eligibility**

1. Adolescent Girls: The scheme is open to all adolescent girls between the ages of 10 and 19 years who are residents of Gujarat.
1. Pregnant Women: The scheme is open to all pregnant women who are residents of Gujarat.
1. BPL Families: The scheme gives priority to women and girls belonging to Below Poverty Line (BPL) families.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
List of required documents
- Identification Documents: Beneficiaries must have identification documents such as a birth certificate, Aadhaar card, or any other valid government-issued identity proof.
- Medical Certificate: Pregnant women may be required to provide a valid medical certificate indicating their pregnancy status and health condition.
- Bank Account Details: Beneficiaries may need to provide their bank account details to receive financial support under the scheme.
- Income Certificate: Women and girls belonging to Below Poverty Line (BPL) families may need to provide an income certificate as proof of their eligibility.
- Address Proof: Beneficiaries may need to provide an address proof such as a utility bill or a rent agreement to establish their residence in Gujarat.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can avail the benefits of the Mamta Taruni Abhiyan scheme in Gujarat?
content.text: The scheme is open to adolescent girls and pregnant women who are residents of Gujarat. Women and girls belonging to Below Poverty Line (BPL) families are given priority.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the benefits provided under the scheme?
content.text: The scheme provides access to health and wellness services such as health check-ups, antenatal care, postnatal care, and nutritional support. It also provides health education and awareness sessions to beneficiaries and financial support to pregnant women in the form of a cash incentive

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the cash incentive provided to pregnant women under the scheme?
content.text: Pregnant women are provided a cash incentive of Rs. 6000 under the scheme to meet their medical and nutritional needs.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any age limit for availing the benefits of the scheme?
content.text: The scheme is open to all adolescent girls between the ages of 10 and 19 years and pregnant women.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can I avail the benefits of the scheme if I am not a resident of Gujarat?
content.text: No, the scheme is only for residents of Gujarat.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the necessary documents needed?
content.text:
1. Identification Documents.
2. Medical Certificate.
3. Bank Account Details.
4. Income Certificate.
5. Address Proof.

content.section_title: FAQ: How to apply for the scheme ?
content.text:
1. Visit the Nearest Health Center:
2. Fill out the Application Form.
3. Submit the Required Documents
4. Get Enrolled.
5. Receive Benefits and Services.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The "Maternity Benefit'' scheme by the BOCW Welfare Board, Labour Department,West Bengal, is a welfare scheme that provides maternity benefits up to two times in a lifetime to the eligible female beneficiaries or the wives of male beneficiaries.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The "Maternity Benefit'' scheme by the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Labour Department, West Bengal, is a welfare scheme for the registered workers of the board. Under this scheme, the Board shall provide financial benefit during the period of maternity to the female beneficiary or wife in case of a male beneficiary who is registered for 1 year under the Board, subject to a maximum 2 times in his/ her whole life time.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
- **Quantum of Assistance:** ₹6,000/-.
**Note:** The benefit shall be allowed for two times in beneficiarie's whole life time.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
> **For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:**

- The applicant should be a resident of West Bengal.
- The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker.
- The applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
- The applicant should have engaged in building and construction work for at least 90 days in a given year.

> **For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:**

- The applicant should be a registered female member or wife of a male member Under the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of West Bengal.
- The applicant should be pregnant.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
> **Registration Process as a Building/ Construction Worker:**

**Step 1:** To register as a beneficiary under the BOCW Welfare Board, an applicant worker should request Form-27 from the Assistant Labour Commissioners/ Beneficiary Registering Officers and should attach all the mandatory documents (self-attested, if required).
**Step 2:** Application shall be submitted to the Assistant Labour Commissioners/ Beneficiary Registering Officers.
> **Application Process of the Welfare Scheme:**

**Step 1:** A registered worker should request Form-XII for the application from the Assistant Labour Commissioners/ Beneficiary Registering Officers and should attach all the mandatory documents (self-attested, if required).
**Step 2:** Application shall be submitted to the Assistant Labour Commissioners/ Beneficiary Registering Officers for this be
> **Helpdesk:**

Labour Department
Department of Labour, Government of West Bengal,
12th Floor, N.S Building, Block-A,
1, Kiran Shankar Roy Road,
Kolkata-700001
Helpline of Labour Department, Govt. of West Bengal (Shramik Sathi): 1800-103-0009
> **Note:**

- Upon successful verification,  the construction worker is registered and an identity card and passbook are issued to him/ her. ₹20/- as the registration fee and ₹30/- as yearly subscription is taken. The amounts taken are noted in the passbook and a receipt is issued.
- After one year, the beneficiary's subscription is renewed upon payment of a renewal fee of ₹30/- and submission of an application in Form-27A.
- If not renewed the registration of the beneficiary is cancelled after the expiry of one year. Fresh application for renewal to be submitted to the ALC for consideration.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
