For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:- Passport size photographs of the worker (Four copies).
- Attested copy of Proof of Age.
For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:- Identity Card of the member.
- Medical Certificate.
- Any other document if required.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the age requirement for registration as a beneficiary under the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of West Bengal?
content.text: The building worker must be at least 18 years old and not older than 60 years old.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there a minimum service requirement to register as a beneficiary?
content.text: Yes, the building worker must have completed 90 days of service as a building worker in the preceding 12 months.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can apply for the welfare scheme?
content.text: Any registered worker of the Board can apply for the welfare scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is responsible for registering a worker as a beneficiary?
content.text: At present all Assistant Labour Commissioners and Inspectors posted at blocks and municipalities shall be responsible for registering a worker as a beneficiary.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible for registration as a beneficiary of the BOCW Welfare Board?
content.text: Every building worker who is between the age of 18-60 years and who has been engaged in any building or other construction work for not less than 90 days during the preceding one year is eligible for registration as a beneficiary of the BOCW Welfare Board of West Bengal.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the procedure for applying for the registration?
content.text: To register as a beneficiary under the BOCW Welfare Board, an applicant worker should collect and submit the Form-27 with all the mandatory documents (self attested, if required) and a fee of [?]20/- to the Assistant Labour Commissioners/ Beneficiary Registering Officers.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there a specific form for the registration as a beneficiary under the BOCW Welfare Board?
content.text: Yes, the application for registration must be made in Form-27. You can obtain this form from the Assistant Labour Commissioners/ Beneficiary Registering Officers in your area.

content.section_title: FAQ: What happens after a construction worker is successfully verified for registration?
content.text: Upon successful verification, the construction worker is registered, and an identity card and passbook are issued to him/her.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the fees associated with the registration process?
content.text: The registration fee is [?]20/-, and the yearly subscription fee is [?]30/-. These amounts are noted in the passbook, and a receipt is issued.

content.section_title: FAQ: How is the yearly subscription fee documented?
content.text: The yearly subscription fee of [?]30/- is recorded in the passbook, and a receipt is issued for the payment.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the process for renewing the subscription after one year?
content.text: After the expiry of one year, the subscription of the beneficiary is renewed upon payment of a renewal fee of [?]30/- and submission of an application made in Form-27A.

content.section_title: FAQ: What happens if the subscription is not renewed after one year?
content.text: If the subscription is not renewed, the registration of the beneficiary is cancelled after the expiry of one year.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can a beneficiary reapply for renewal after cancellation?
content.text: Yes, a fresh application for renewal can be submitted to the Assistant Labour Commissioner (ALC) for consideration.

content.section_title: FAQ: What criteria must an applicant meet to be considered for this benefit?
content.text: A registered female member or wife of a male member who is pregnant, shall be eligible for this benefit.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the maximum amount the Board may sanction for this benefit?
content.text: The Board may sanction an amount of [?].6,000/- to the beneficiaries under this scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: How many times can the beneficiary avail the benefits?
content.text: The benefit shall be allowed for two times in his/her whole lifetime.

content.section_title: FAQ: What form needs to be used to apply for this welfare scheme?
content.text: The application must be made in the prescribed Form-XII. The form can be collected from Assistant Labour Commissioners/ Beneficiary Registering Officers in your area.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where do I submit the application for this welfare scheme?
content.text: Submit your welfare scheme application to the Assistant Labour Commissioners/ Beneficiary Registering Officers in your area.

title: Maternity Assistance for the Construction Workers
topic: Maternity Assistance for the Construction Workers

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The "Maternity Assistance for the Construction Workers" by the Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Labour Department, Delhi, is a welfare scheme  that provides maternity benefits of ₹ 30,000/- to the registered women members and wives of male members for upto 2 children.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The "Maternity Assistance for the Construction Workers" by the Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Labour Department, Delhi, is a welfare scheme for the registered workers of the board. Through this scheme, maternity benefits of ₹ 30,000/- is provided to the registered women members and wives of male members for upto 2 children from the date of joining membership of the fund.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: - ₹30,000/- is provided to the registered women members and wives of male members for upto 2 children from the date of joining membership of the fund.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
> **For Registration**

- The applicant should be a resident of Delhi.
- The applicant should be a Building/Construction Worker.
- The applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
- The applicant should have served the Board for at least 90 days in a given year.

> **For Application**

- Registered women member Under the Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board .

Or,
Wives of registered male members Under the Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.

- The applicant shall be eligible to avail the benefits only for their first 2 children.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
> **Registration**

**Step 1:** Visit the DBoCWWB website and click “Register Now”.
**Step 2:** Enter the following mandatory details: Aadhaar Card Number, Name as on Aadhar Card, Mobile Number, Date of Birth (as on Aadhar Card), Gender, etc. Create a password and confirm it. Click “Register”. You will be redirected to the registration page.
**Step 3:** On the registration page, fill in the mandatory details (Father Name, Mother Name, Photograph, Applicant’s Video, etc) and upload the mandatory documents in the specified filetype and filesize. Click “Save & Next”.
**Step 4:** On the next page, enter the mandatory details regarding your work/workplace (Previous/Current Employer Details, Proof of 90 Days Employment, etc) and upload the mandatory documents in the specified filetype and filesize. Click “Save & Next”.
**Step 5:** Click on “Add Family Members” to avail the benefits of various schemes.
**Step 6:** In the next window, add your Bank Account details.
**Step 7:** In the next window, upload your Aadhaar Card, Address Proof, Birth Proof, Employment Proof, Bank Account details etc.
**Step 8:** In the next window, the "Affidavit of the Applicant" will be displayed. Verify all the details, and click on the checkbox.
**Step 9:** Proceed for the online registration fee of ₹25/- through a Debit Card/ Internet Banking/ UPI.
**Step 10:** Enter the OTP received on your registered mobile number to apply. Note down the Application Number for future reference. Download the acknowledgement receipt.
> **Track Your Application**

Visit the homepage of the website regularly and click on "Track Your Application" to check the status of your application.
> **Application**

**Step 1:** Visit the DBoCWWB website and click “Apply for Scheme”.
**Step 2:** Log in through Aadhaar Number/ Registration Card/ OTP.
**Step 3:** Apply for the “Maternity Assistance” scheme.

> **Helpline Number (24x1) : 011-41236600**

> **DBOCWWB Department**

> **Number (office hours: 9:30AM - 6:00PM) : 011-23813846**

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
In case of Delivery at Hospital:- Birth certificate of the Child (duly issued by any Municipal/Govt. Authority),
- Discharge summary/Medical Certificate (to be obtained from medical officer not below the rank of an Assistant Surgeon).
In case of Emergency Delivery at Home:- Birth certificate of the Child (duly issued by any Municipal/Govt. Authority),
- Proof of visiting a Hospital/Nursing Home in connection with the pregnancy in the pregnancy period,
- Photocopy of any ID proof of Aaya/Dai.

content.section_title: FAQ: Which Department of the NCT of Delhi administers this scheme?
content.text: This scheme is administered by the Labour Department, of the National Capital Territory of Delhi.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where can I find the URL of the Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board?
content.text: The URL of the Official Website of the Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board is: https://bocw.delhi.gov.in/.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is self-attestation required for attaching documents with the application?
content.text: Yes, applicants need to self-attest the copies of mandatory documents while submitting the application.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the URL for the registration of this scheme?
content.text: The applicants needs to register through clicking on the "Register" button on DBoCWWB website: https://dbocwwb.delhi.gov.in/cwapp/MyLoginPage?ReturnUrl=%2Fcwapp%2F.

content.section_title: FAQ: In case of change, how does the worker update their registered Mobile number on the website?
content.text: The facility to amend a worker's registered mobile number on the new DBoCWWB Website is still under process. However, workers who are migrating their membership from E-District Website to the new DBoCWWB Website have an option to update/revise their mobile numbers in the migration form.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is a Construction Certificate/Labour Card?
content.text:
As an enrolled member with BoCW Welfare Board, the worker will receive a Construction Certificate popularly known as the Labour Card. A Construction Certificate/Labour Card is a document with the worker's personal details like DoB, Address, Registration Number, Date of Registration along with details of the next date of renewal.
The Construction Certificate/Labour Card is to be shown when applying for any of the claims under the BoCW Act. It is important for the worker to renew their Construction Certificate/Labour Card in order to remain active members. This renewal must be undertaken on a yearly basis.

content.section_title: FAQ: I am from another state but working as a construction worker in Delhi, can I get registered?
content.text: Yes, a Construction Worker native to any place in India, but working in Delhi can register with the DBoCWWB. The Construction Workers must own an Aadhar Card from any place in India and should have a Proof of Residence of staying in Delhi.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can members in my family also register if they are all registered construction workers?
content.text: Yes, if there are more than one member in the family who are engaged in Construction Work, they can all register under the DBoCWWB.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there a specific age requirement for construction workers to be eligible for registration?
content.text: Yes, construction workers must be at least 18 years old but not more than 60 years old to be eligible for registration under Section 12 of the Main Act.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the duration of engagement required for construction workers to qualify for registration?
content.text: Construction workers must have engaged in building or other construction work for a minimum of 90 days during the preceding 12 months to qualify for registration under Section 12 of the Main Act.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the Registration/Renewal fee?
content.text: Registration : [?] 25/-and Renewal : [?] 20/- and [?] 2/- per month (late fee).

content.section_title: FAQ: How can one track the status of my Application on the Website?
content.text: To track, the worker can use the track feature available on the website's landing page. Choose the "Type of Service' availed, i.e, whether the application was for Registration/Renewal/Claims/Amendment. In the white box, enter the application number mentioned in the acknowledgment receipt. Click Track Application now and a pop-up box will indicate the current status of the Application.

content.section_title: FAQ: What consequences a worker may face if he/she does not renew the card?
content.text: If a worker does not renew the card, he will not be eligible for the benefit in future, but with the permission of the authorized officer, the membership can be returned with the outstanding amount, though this facility will not be available more than 2 times.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The "Maternity Assistance" is a Welfare Scheme by the Puducherry Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Labour Department, Union Territory of Puducherry. Through this scheme, the women employees who are beneficiary of the fund shall be given ₹5,000/- each as maternity benefit.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The "Maternity Assistance" is a Welfare Scheme by the Puducherry Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Labour Department, Union Territory of Puducherry. Through this scheme, the women employees who are beneficiary of the fund shall be given ₹5,000/- each as maternity benefit during the period of maternity. The spouse of male employee who is the beneficiary of the fund shall also be given this benefit. The benefits can only be availed twice.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
Financial Assistance of ₹5,000/- credited within 15 days through DBT.
*The spouse of male employee who is the beneficiary of the fund shall also be given this benefit.
*The benefits can only be availed twice.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
- The applicant should be a citizen of India.
- The applicant should be registered as a Construction Worker with the Puducherry Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
- The applicant should have completed the age of 18 years.
- The applicant should be a Woman.
- The applicant should be Pregnant.
- The applicant should not have completed the age of 60 years during the preceding 12 months in the construction work.
- The beneficiary should be contributing to the fund until he/she attains the age of 60 years.
- The applicant should not have defaulted in payment of contribution to the Board for a continuous period of more than one year.
- The applicant should not already have received the benefits of this scheme two times.
- The spouse of male employee who is the beneficiary of the fund is also eligible.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
> **Registration**

**Step 1:** The interested construction worker should visit the Office of the Puducherry Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (Industrial Estate, Thattanchavady, Puducherry - 605 009) and obtain the prescribed format of the registration form free of cost from the concerned authority exclusively entrusted to issue and collect filled-in applications.
**Step 2:** In the registration form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste the passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required), add a declaration stating that you are a construction worker, mentioning your worksite.
**Step 3:** The interested construction worker should submit the duly filled and signed registration form and the documents to the concerned authority.
**Step 4:** Request a receipt or acknowledgement from the concerned authority to whom the registration form has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).
**Step 5:** Upon verification by the inspectors under the Act towards genuineness, the concerned worker will be enrolled as a beneficiary with the approval of the Secretary (Board).
_*The beneficiary should pay the registration of ₹25/- at the time of enrollment and ₹100/- per year as an annual subscription._
_*The Board collects ₹300/- as a renewal fee once in three years._

> **Application**

**Step 1:** The interested construction worker should visit the Office of the Puducherry Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (Industrial Estate, Thattanchavady, Puducherry - 605 009) and obtain the prescribed format of the application form free of cost from the concerned authority exclusively entrusted to issue and collect filled-in applications.
OR
The interested construction worker should take print of the prescribed format of the application form provided in the section "Application for Welfare Benefits" on [Page No. 77](https://labour.py.gov.in/sites/default/files/citizens-charter-2020.pdf) of the Citizen's Charter.
**Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste the passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
**Step 3:** The interested construction worker should submit the duly filled and signed application form and the documents to the concerned authority.
**Step 4:** Request a receipt or acknowledgement from the concerned authority to whom the application form has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).
**NOTE:** The application should be submitted within one year from the date of delivery period

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
For Registration1. Duly Filled and Signed Application Form.
2. Birth Certificate/Transfer Certificate/Doctor Certificate for Age Verification.
3. Experience Certificate to Be Obtained From a Registered Engineer/contractor/association/trade Union, Serving as Evidence of Being a Construction Worker.
4. Details Related to Construction Work/worksite.
5. Proof of Blood Group (Medical Health Card or Laboratory Certificate).
6. Two Passport-size Photographs.
7. Ration Card.
8. Aadhaar Card.
9. Bank Details.
10. Family Particulars/status and Nomination Details of the Beneficiaries.
11. Certificate From the Revenue Department or Social Welfare Department or Anganwadi Officials of the Respective Area.

For Application- For Application
- Original Birth Certificate.
- Discharge Slip.
- Last Subscription Bill.
- Board ID Card.
- Aadhaar Card.
- Ration Card.
- Bank Details.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any provision for addressing grievances or complaints related to the Maternity Assistance scheme?
content.text: Yes, you can refer to page 131 of the Citizens' Charter document (https://labour.py.gov.in/sites/default/files/citizens-charter-2020.pdf) for guidelines on grievance redressal and the process to follow in case of any grievances related to the Maternity Assistance scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are there any penalties for defaulting in payment of contributions to the Board for more than one year?
content.text: Yes, defaulting in payment of contributions to the Board for more than one continuous year may result in disqualification from the Maternity Assistance scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What role does the Secretary (Board) play in the registration process, and how is the enrollment of beneficiaries approved?
content.text: The Secretary (Board) approves the enrollment of beneficiaries after inspectors verify the genuineness of the registration. This step ensures the authenticity of the registration process.

content.section_title: FAQ: How long does it take for the financial assistance to be credited to the beneficiary's account under the Maternity Assistance scheme?
content.text: Beneficiaries can expect the financial assistance of [?]5,000 to be credited to their accounts within 15 days through the Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) mechanism.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there an annual subscription fee for beneficiaries of the Maternity Assistance scheme?
content.text: Yes, beneficiaries are required to pay [?]100 per year as an annual subscription fee. Additionally, there is a registration fee of [?]25 at the time of enrollment, and the Board collects [?]300 as a renewal fee once in three years.

content.section_title: FAQ: How much financial assistance is provided to women employees and the spouses of male employees under the Maternity Assistance scheme?
content.text: Women employees and the spouses of male employees receive [?]5,000 each as maternity benefit within 15 days through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT). The benefit can be availed twice.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can the spouse of a male employee who is a beneficiary of the fund also avail the maternity benefit under the Maternity Assistance scheme?
content.text: Yes, the spouse of a male employee who is a beneficiary of the fund is eligible to receive the maternity benefit under the Maternity Assistance scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where can I find the guidelines for the Maternity Assistance scheme provided by the Puducherry Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board?
content.text: You can refer to page 60 of the Citizens' Charter document available on the official website (https://labour.py.gov.in/sites/default/files/citizens-charter-2020.pdf) for detailed guidelines regarding the Maternity Assistance scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can the scheme be availed by individuals in rural areas, or is it limited to specific regions?
content.text: The scheme is designed to benefit individuals across both urban and rural areas, ensuring inclusivity and reaching those in need, regardless of their location.

content.section_title: FAQ: How does the scheme adapt to changing economic conditions to continue providing adequate financial support?
content.text: The scheme is periodically reviewed and adjusted based on changing economic conditions to ensure that the financial support provided remains relevant and sufficient.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can an applicant update their personal details or make corrections after submitting the application?
content.text: Applicants should ensure accuracy while submitting the application. In case of any discrepancies, they may contact the concerned authority for guidance on making corrections or updates.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can beneficiaries avail of the maternity benefit more than twice, and what are the implications if this limit is exceeded?
content.text: No, beneficiaries are restricted to availing the maternity benefit only twice, emphasizing the scheme's intention to provide support during two distinct maternity periods. Exceeding this limit may result in ineligibility for further benefits.

title: Maternity Benefit For Female Workers- JKBOCWWB
topic: Maternity Benefit For Female Workers- JKBOCWWB

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The "Maternity Benefit for Female Workers" scheme by the JKBOCW Welfare Board, Labour Department, Jammu & Kashmir, is a welfare program that provides maternity benefits to eligible registered female workers up to two times in their lifetime.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The "Maternity Benefit for Female Workers" scheme by the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Labour Department, Jammu & Kashmir, provides financial assistance to registered female workers during maternity. Under this scheme, female beneficiaries are eligible to receive ₹5,000 (for up to two children), provided they have been engaged in building or construction work for at least 90 days in the preceding 12 months.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
Under the scheme, maternity benefits for female workers amount to ₹5,000 for up to two children.
Note: The benefit is allowed only twice in the beneficiary's lifetime.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
**For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:**

1. The applicant should be a resident of Jammu & Kashmir.
1. The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker.
1. The applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
1. The applicant should have engaged in any building or other construction work for at least 90 days during the preceding 12 months.
1. The applicant should be registration is renewed annually.

**For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:**

1. The applicant should be a registered female member Under the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of Jammu & Kashmir.
1. The applicant should be pregnant.
1. The worker's membership with the Welfare Board must be active at the time of application.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
**Registration of a Construction Worker under JKBOCWWB:**
Step 1: Go to the J&K Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (JKBOCWWB) [official website.](https://www.jkbocw.gov.in/Index.aspx)
Step 2: On the homepage, find and click the "[Labour Registration](https://portal.jkbocw.gov.in/MemberRegistration.aspx)" link.
Step 3:If you're a new user, click on "Apply for New Registration."
Step 4: Complete the registration form with your details, submit the form, and send the OTP to your registered mobile number. Enter the OTP and click "Verify OTP & Submit."
Step 5: After the OTP is verified, a confirmation message with your Login ID and password will be sent to your registered mobile number.
Step 6:Use the Login ID, password, and captcha to log in (remember, the username and password are case-sensitive). filled are case sensitive)Read all the instruction carefully and Click to continue
Step 7: Once logged in, choose the "Construction Worker’s Registration" option and click on "Registration Form."
Step 8: A dashboard will appear. Complete the form by entering all required details (marked with *).
Step 9: Upload all the important documents, such as ID proof and other relevant certificates.
Step 10: Once the form is completed and documents uploaded, click "Submit" and pay the application fee online. You will receive a registration number after successful payment and submission.

**Steps to Apply for Scheme Benefits:**
Step1: Go to the [official website](https://www.jkbocw.gov.in/Index.aspx) of the Jammu & Kashmir Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
Step2: Click on "[Login,"](https://portal.jkbocw.gov.in/LoginGuest.aspx) enter your details, and sign in to your account.
Step3: After logging in, click on "Scheme Registration" and select "Apply for Scheme."
Step4: Choose the scheme you wish to apply for in order to avail the benefits.
Step5: Enter all required information and upload the necessary documents as prompted.
Step6: Finally, click on "Submit Application" to complete the process.

**Application Tracking:**
Applicant can track their application status online through the official website of the  Jammu & Kashmir Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board. Relevant updates will be sent to the registered mobile number.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
> **The applicant should submit the completed application form along with all the required documents to the concerned JKBOCWWB office for processing.**

1. [Application Form](https://www.jkbocw.gov.in/MediaGallery/New%20Registration%20form%204%20.pdf) for Registration as a Beneficiary1. [Application Form](https://www.jkbocw.gov.in/downloads/circ.pdf) for Scheme (Page no. 17)

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
Documents Required for Registration as a Building/Construction Worker:
- Identity Proof (e.g., Aadhaar Card)
- Passport-size Photograph
- Skill-upgradation Training Certificate
- Proof of Residence (e.g., Utility bill, Voter ID, etc.)
- Ration Card (optional)
- Bank Account Details (e.g., Copy of Bank Passbook)
- Caste Certificate (if applicable)
- Undertaking/Declaration
- Age Certificate (any of the following):
- School Certificate
- Registrar of Birth Certificate
- Certificate issued by District Medical Board (if both of the above are unavailable)
- Employment Certificate
- Signature
- Any other documents as required
Documents Required for Application of a Welfare Scheme:
- Identity Card of the Member
- Medical Certificate (if applicable)
- Any Other Document as Required

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the Maternity Benefit Scheme for Female Workers?
content.text: The Maternity Benefit Scheme provides financial assistance to eligible registered female construction workers during maternity. It can be availed up to two times in a worker's lifetime.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there a minimum service requirement to register as a beneficiary?
content.text: Yes, the building worker must have completed 90 days of service as a building worker in the preceding 12 months.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can apply for the welfare scheme?
content.text: Any registered worker of the Board can apply for the welfare scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: How many times can a worker avail the Maternity Benefit?
content.text: A registered female worker can avail the Maternity Benefit up to two times in her lifetime.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the amount provided under the Maternity Benefit Scheme?
content.text: The financial benefit amount may vary based on state policies. You can check the exact benefit amount on the JKBOCWWB website or contact the board.

content.section_title: FAQ: How do I register as a construction worker under JKBOCWWB?
content.text: To register, visit the official JKBOCWWB website, apply through the "Labour Registration" section, and follow the steps to fill out your details, upload documents, and submit the form online.

content.section_title: FAQ: What documents are required for registration as a construction worker?
content.text: Documents like Aadhaar Card, proof of residence, bank passbook, passport-size photograph, age certificate, and employment certificate are needed for registration.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can I apply for multiple welfare schemes under JKBOCWWB?
content.text: Yes, registered construction workers can apply for multiple welfare schemes, including maternity benefits, medical assistance, and education assistance, as long as they meet the eligibility criteria.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can I apply for welfare schemes under JKBOCWWB?
content.text: Log in to your account on the JKBOCWWB website, click on "Scheme Registration," select the scheme you wish to apply for, fill in the required information, upload documents, and submit your application.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The “Maternity Benefit” scheme by the BOCW Welfare Board, Labour Department, Odisha, is a welfare scheme that provides maternity benefits to the female beneficiaries subject to a maximum of two children who  are registered beneficiaries for a period of more than one year under the board.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The “Maternity Benefit” scheme by the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Labour Department, Odisha, is a welfare scheme for the registered workers of the board. Under this scheme, the Board shall provide financial assistance in the form of maternity benefits to the female beneficiaries subject to a maximum of two children who  are registered beneficiaries for a period of more than one year under the Odisha Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
- **Quantum of Assistance:** ₹8,000/- to ₹10,000/- per Child.  - **For eligible beneficiaries under “Mamata” being implemented by W&CD Department:** ₹15,000/- (₹10,000/- under OB&OCWW Board and ₹5,000/- under “Mamata”).
> **Note:** Assistance shall be given for a maximum of two children.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
> **For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:**

- The applicant should be a resident of Odisha.
- The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker.
- The applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
- The applicant should have engaged in building and construction work for at least 90 days in a given year.
- The applicant should not be a member of any other Welfare Fund established under any law.

> **For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:**

- The applicant should be female.
- The applicant should be a registered member of the Odisha Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
- The applicant should be registered for a period of one year or more under the board.
- The applicant should be pregnant.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
> **Registration Process as a Building/ Construction Worker:**

**Step 1:** Visit the official [website ](https://nirmanshramik.odisha.gov.in/obocwwb/public/cwLogin)of the Odisha BOCWW Board.
**Step 2:** Click on “One Time Registration” on the top right side of the page.
**Step 3:** Click on the Declaration box and then on the “Proceed” button.
**Step 4:** In the next window fill in all the required details carefully and click on “Proceed”.
**Step 5:** Enter the received OTP and click on “Submit”.
**Step 6:** Your account will be successfully created and a Username will be displayed (Keep the Username for future references).
> **Application Process of the Welfare Scheme:**

**Step 1:** Visit the Applicant [Login page](https://nirmanshramik.odisha.gov.in/obocwwb/public/cwLogin) of the Odisha BOCWW Board.
**Step 2:** Enter your Username, OTP and fill in the captcha then click on “Sign In”.
**Step 3:** In the next window, fill in your General Details, Address Details, Family & Nominee Details, Details of the establishment(s) where the applicant worked during the last one year, Bank Details etc. and then click on “Submit”.
**Step 4:** Now in the Menu bar section, click on “Benefit”. From the list select the concerned benefit you want to apply.
**Step 5:** Fill in all the mandatory details carefully and click on “Submit” to complete the application process.
> **Check Your Application Status:** Registered workers can check their application status through visiting this [link ](https://nirmanshramik.odisha.gov.in/obocwwb/public/applicationTracking)upon entering their Application Tracking Number.

> **Pay Annual Contribution:** Registered workers can pay the Annual Contribution through visiting this [link ](https://nirmanshramik.odisha.gov.in/obocwwb/public/payAnnuealContribution)upon entering their Registration Number.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
> **Registration Process as a Building/ Construction Worker:**

**Step 1:** To register as a beneficiary under the BOCW Welfare Board, an applicant worker should download [Form-XXVII](https://bocboard.labdirodisha.gov.in/Building/homecontent/BeneficiaryForms/REGISTRATION%20FORM.pdf) and attach all the mandatory documents (self-attested, if required).
**Step 2:** Application shall be submitted to the District Labour Officers (D.L.O)/ Asst. Labour Officers/ Rural Labour Inspectors of your area.
> **Application Process of the Welfare Scheme:**

**Step 1:** A registered worker should download [Form-XLI](https://bocboard.labdirodisha.gov.in/Building/homecontent/BeneficiaryForms/MATERNITY%20BENEFIT%20FORM.pdf) and attach all the mandatory documents (self-attested, if required).
**Step 2:** Application shall be submitted to the District Labour Officers (D.L.O)/ Asst. Labour Officers/ Rural Labour Inspectors of your area.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:- Aadhaar Linked Bank Passbook Front Page Copy (Allowed file types: PNG, JPG, PDF. Allowed size: less than 500 KB).
- Passport Size Photo (Allowed type: PNG, JPG. Allowed size: less than 500 KB).
- Aadhaar Card (Allowed file types: PNG, JPG, PDF. Allowed size: less than 500 KB).
- Signature of Applicant (Allowed type: PNG, JPG. Allowed size: less than 500 KB).
- Employment certificate (Allowed file types: PNG, JPG, PDF. Allowed size: less than 500 KB).
For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:- Registration ID card of the member.
- Proof of deposit of annual contribution of the member.
- Medical Certificate.
- Any other documents if required.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the age requirement for registration as a beneficiary under the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of Odisha?
content.text: The building worker must be at least 18 years old and not older than 60 years old.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there a minimum service requirement to register as a beneficiary?
content.text: Yes, the building worker must have completed 90 days of service as a building worker in the preceding 12 months.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can apply for the welfare scheme?
content.text: Any registered worker of the Board can apply for the welfare scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is responsible for registering a worker as a beneficiary?
content.text: The District Labour Officers (D.L.O)/ Asst. Labour Officers/ Rural Labour Inspectors are acting as Registering Officers for the registration of beneficiaries under the Board.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible for registration as a beneficiary of the BOCW Welfare Board?
content.text: Every building worker who is between the age of 18-60 years and who has been engaged in any building or other construction work for not less than 90 days during the preceding one year is eligible for registration as a beneficiary of the BOCW Welfare Board of Odisha.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can Odia migrant workmen be registered as beneficiaries under the Odisha Building & Other Construction Workers’ Welfare Board?
content.text: Yes, Odia migrant workmen engaged in building and other construction works in other states can be registered as beneficiaries under the Odisha Building & Other Construction Workers' Welfare Board.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the procedure for applying for the registration?
content.text:
To register as a beneficiary under the BOCW Welfare Board, an applicant worker should download Form-XXVII and attach all the mandatory documents (self attested, if required) and a fee of [?]20/- to the District Labour Officers (D.L.O)/ Asst. Labour Officers/ Rural Labour Inspectors of your area.
Alternatively, registration can also be done through the official website of the Odisha BOCWW Board: https://nirmanshramik.odisha.gov.in/obocwwb/public/cwLogin .

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there a specific form for the registration through offline mode as a beneficiary under the BOCW Welfare Board?
content.text: Yes, the application for registration must be made in Form-XXVII. You can obtain this form from the District Labour Officers (D.L.O)/ Asst. Labour Officers/ Rural Labour Inspectors in your area.

content.section_title: FAQ: How much is the fee associated with the registration process?
content.text: The registration fee is [?]20/- which is payable during the registration.

content.section_title: FAQ: What happens after I pay the application fee?
content.text: After you pay the application fee of  [?]20/-, your application will be reviewed by the registering officer. The decision to approve or reject your application will be based on the merits of your application.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who decides whether my application will be approved or rejected?
content.text: The registering officer is responsible for deciding whether your application will be approved or rejected based on its merits.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can my application be rejected even if I have paid the application fee?
content.text: Yes, your application can be rejected even if you have paid the application fee. The final decision is made by the registering officer based on the merits of your application.

content.section_title: FAQ: How much is the fee associated with the renewal of the registration?
content.text: The renewal fee is [?]50/- which is payable during the renewal process of the registration.

content.section_title: FAQ: What criteria must an applicant meet to be considered for this benefit?
content.text: Female beneficiaries who are registered under the Odisha Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board for a period of more than one year are eligible for this benefit and for up to two children.

content.section_title: FAQ: What kind of assistance is provided under this scheme?
content.text: The financial assistance provided under this scheme ranges from [?]8,000/- to [?]10,000/- per child.

content.section_title: FAQ: How many children of a member can benefit from this scheme?
content.text: The scheme covers a maximum of two children per eligible female beneficiary.

content.section_title: FAQ: What form needs to be used to apply for this welfare scheme?
content.text: The application must be made in the prescribed Form-XLI. The form can be downloaded from https://bocboard.labdirodisha.gov.in/Building/homecontent/BeneficiaryForms/MATERNITY%20BENEFIT%20FORM.pdf or can be collected from the District Labour Officers (D.L.O)/ Asst. Labour Officers/ Rural Labour Inspectors of your area.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where do I submit the application for this welfare scheme?
content.text: Application can be submitted online on: https://nirmanshramik.odisha.gov.in/obocwwb/public/cwLogin . Alternatively , the offline application for this welfare scheme should be submitted to the District Labour Officers (D.L.O)/ Asst. Labour Officers/ Rural Labour Inspectors in your area.

content.section_title: FAQ: What happens if the application is rejected?
content.text: In case of rejection, the applicants would be intimated about such rejection, with due intimation to the Board.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the consequences of submitting fraudulent documents for assistance?
content.text: In case of receipt of assistance on the basis of fraudulent documents, the amount released towards any assistance would be recovered from the applicant under the provisions of the OPDR Act, and his/ her membership would be ceased.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The Govt. of Arunachal Pradesh has proposed and implemented the scheme “Maternity Benefit Scheme” for Registered Workers of Arunachal Pradesh Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (APB&OCWWB).

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
The scheme “Maternity Benefit Scheme” was launched by the Department of Labour and Employment, Govt. of Arunachal Pradesh for Registered Workers of Arunachal Pradesh Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (APB&OCWWB). The state government has proposed and implemented this scheme through the APB&OCWWB to its women workers who have registered their names in the Labour Welfare Board.
**Objective:**
• To mitigate suffering in the course of delivery and to supplement well upbringing of the infant.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
1. Cash assistance of ₹1,000/- during the period of maternity
1. Payable for up to two adult daughters of registered workers.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Arunachal Pradesh.
1. The applicant should be a woman.
1. The woman worker must be employed in the Building and Other Construction works.
1. The woman worker should be registered with Labour Welfare Board (APB&OCWWB).
1. The applicant's membership must be active at the time of application.

**Note:** The woman worker is not to be entitled to the benefits in case of second marriage.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Registration Process:**
**Step 01:** The applicant is to register herself as a labour/worker under the Arunachal Pradesh Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (APB&OCWWB).
**Step 02:** The applicant can obtain the application form from the Registered Office of the District/Sub-Division, APB&OCWWB which can also be downloaded from the official website of the Department of Labour and Employment.
**Step 03:** The applicant must ensure that the filled information should be correct before submitting the application form to the concerned department.
**Step 04:** After successful verification, the applicant will be registered as a worker/labour under Board and an Identity Card to be issued to her.

**Application Process:**
**Step 01:** For taking benefit of this scheme, the applicant needs to visit the nearest registered office of the District/Sub-Division, APB&OCWWB along with all the relevant documents.
**Step 02:** Application in the prescribed format may be received from the concerned office.
**Step 03:** The applicant needs to fill the application form completely and attach all the relevant documents. Thereafter, the applicant may submit the application form to the concerned office.
**Step 04:** After successful verification, the applicant can avail the benefit of the scheme.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Identity proof i.e. Aadhaar Card, Voter ID card, etc.
- Xerox copy of the registration card
- Medical Certificate
- Bank Account number/ front page of the passbook
- Complete application form
- Passport-size photographs
- Address proof

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the objective of the scheme?
content.text: The objective of this scheme is to mitigate the suffering of women in the course of delivery and to supplement well upbringing of the infant.

content.section_title: FAQ: By which department was this scheme launched?
content.text: Department of Labour and Employment, Government of Arunachal Pradesh

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the full form of APB&OCWWB?
content.text: Arunachal Pradesh Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the benefit of this scheme?
content.text: Cash assistance of [?]1000/- during the period of maternity will be paid to the registered women worker under Board.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible under this scheme?
content.text: The women workers employed in the Building and Other Construction works who are registered under APB&OCWWB are eligible to take benefit of the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is it mandatory to register under Board as a labour/worker?
content.text: Yes, the labour/worker must be registered under the Board to take benefits of the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can I also apply for the scheme if my membership has expired?
content.text: No, membership must be active at the time of application.

content.section_title: FAQ: Am I eligible for the scheme after my second marriage?
content.text: No, after the second marriage, the woman worker is not to be entitled to the benefit under this scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can I register myself under the Board?
content.text: The applicant needs to visit the nearest Registered Office of the District/Sub-Division, APB&OCWWB for registration under Board.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can I also get the application form online for registration?
content.text: Yes, an Offline Mode of application form to register as a labour/worker under the Board can also be downloaded from the official website of the Labour Welfare Board, Department of Labour and Employment.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The "Maternity Benefit Scheme'' by the BOCW Welfare Board, Department of Labour, Punjab, is a welfare scheme that provides financial assistance to the female construction workers and spouse of the male workers to support childbirth-related expenses maximum for two living children.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The "Maternity Benefit Scheme'' by the BOCW Welfare Board, Department of Labour, Punjab, is a welfare scheme for the registered workers of the board. Under this scheme, the Board shall provide financial assistance to construction workers to support childbirth-related expenses for upto two living children of the registered female construction workers and spouse of the male workers.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
**Female Construction Workers:** ₹21,000/- per child, maximum of two children.
**Male Construction Workers:** ₹5,000/- per child, maximum of two children (for children of their spouse).

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
> ***For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:***

- The applicant should be a resident of Punjab.
- The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker (Unorganised Worker).
- The applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
- The applicant should have engaged in building and construction work for at least 90 days in a given year.
- The applicant should have a monthly income of ₹15,000/- or below.
- The applicant should not be a member of any other Welfare Fund established under any law.
- The applicant should not have membership of the Employees' Provident Fund (EPF)/ National Pension System (NPS)/ Employees' State Insurance Corporation (ESIC).
- The applicant should not be an income tax payer.

> ***For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:***

- The beneficiary should have been a registered member of the Board for at least 6 months prior to the birth of the child.
- The beneficiary should have regularly depositing his contribution.
- The scheme applies upto two living children.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online - via CSC)
content.text:
> **Registration Process as a Building/ Construction Worker:**

- For new registration of workers please approach the nearby Sewa Kendra or Registering officers/ Labour Inspector.

> ***Application Process of the Welfare Scheme:***

- To apply for benefits of the concerned welfare scheme, please approach to nearby Sewa Kendra or Registering Officers/ Labour Inspector of your area.

> ***Note:***

- **Registration with the Board:** A construction worker becomes a member of the board by filling out Application Form No 28 along with depositing registration fees of ₹ 25/- only (once in life) and ₹ 10/- per month as contribution fees. A worker at a time can get himself registered for a minimum period of 1 year & maximum period of 5 years. The registered worker is called 'Beneficiary' of the Board.
> **Important Links:**

- **Check Your Registration Application Status:** Registered workers can check their registration application status through visiting this [link ](http://bocw.punjab.gov.in/Login.aspx?Id=1)upon entering their Registration Number.  - **Download Registration Card:** Registered workers can download their registration Registration Card through visiting this [link ](https://bocw.punjab.gov.in/Login.aspx?Id=1)upon entering their Registration Number.  - **Check your Scheme Application Status:** Registered workers can check their scheme application status through visiting this [link ](https://bocw.punjab.gov.in/Login.aspx?Id=1)upon entering their Registration Number.  - **CSC Locator:** Visit this [link ](https://www.apnacsconline.in/csc-locator/punjab/)to search nearest Common Service Center (CSC) of your area.  - **List of Sewa** **Kendra:** Visit this [link ](https://punjab.gov.in/documents/10191/1112286/Final+List-V1.0.pdf/6ab437f3-f04c-427d-a2b7-dd49515f075b)to see the list of Sewa Kendra.
> **Helpdesk:**

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
> ***Registration Process as a Building/ Construction Worker:***

**Step 1:** To register as a beneficiary under the BOCW Welfare Board, an applicant worker should download [Form No-28](http://bocw.punjab.gov.in/userfiles/file/Registration%20Form%2028%20updated.pdf) and attach all the mandatory documents (self attested, if required).
**Step 2:** Application shall be submitted to the Labour Inspector of your area.
> ***Application Process of the Welfare Scheme:***

> ***Note:***

- **Registration with the Board:** A construction worker becomes a member of the board by filling out Application Form No 28 along with depositing registration fees of ₹ 25/- only (once in life) and ₹ 10/- per month as contribution fees. A worker at a time can get himself registered for a minimum period of 1 year & maximum period of 5 years. The registered worker is called 'Beneficiary' of the Board.
> **Important Links:**

- **Check Your Registration Application Status:** Registered workers can check their registration application status through visiting this [link ](http://bocw.punjab.gov.in/Login.aspx?Id=1)upon entering their Registration Number.  - **Download Registration Card:** Registered workers can download their registration Registration Card through visiting this [link ](https://bocw.punjab.gov.in/Login.aspx?Id=1)upon entering their Registration Number.  - **Check your Scheme Application Status:** Registered workers can check their scheme application status through visiting this [link ](https://bocw.punjab.gov.in/Login.aspx?Id=1)upon entering their Registration Number.  - **CSC Locator:** Visit this [link ](https://www.apnacsconline.in/csc-locator/punjab/)to search nearest Common Service Center (CSC) of your area.  - **List of Sewa** **Kendra:** Visit this [link ](https://punjab.gov.in/documents/10191/1112286/Final+List-V1.0.pdf/6ab437f3-f04c-427d-a2b7-dd49515f075b)to see the list of Sewa Kendra.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:- Age proof (If no age proof is available self declaration).
- Residence proof.
- Aadhaar card(if available).
- Bank Account details (Bank branch, account No. and IFSC/ RTGS number).
- Detail of dependents.
- Employer’s Certificate (Form No. 28) (90 days of construction work during the last 12 months in the State of Punjab).
- Nomination Form (Form No. 27).
For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:- Child Photo with Parents.
- Aadhaar Card of Child.
- Date of Birth Certificate by the Registrar.
- Death Certificate (In case one of the children has died).

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can register as a beneficiary under the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of Punjab?
content.text: Any construction worker whose age is between 18 to 60 years and has worked in Punjab State in any construction work for a minimum of 90 days in a year, are eligible to register as a beneficiary under the Board.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there a minimum service requirement to register as a beneficiary?
content.text: Yes, the building worker must have completed 90 days of service as a building worker in the preceding 12 months.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can apply for the welfare scheme?
content.text: Any registered worker of the Board can apply for the welfare scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: For how long a construction worker can get registered at a time?
content.text: A worker at a time can get himself registered for a minimum period of 1 year & maximum period of 5 years.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the procedure for applying for the registration?
content.text: Any construction worker who is of 18 years and below 60 years' age and has Worked in Punjab State for not less than 90 days shall apply for registration as construction worker on the prescribed Form No. 28 which is available on the Board's website or from the office of ALC/LCO and Labour Inspectors of the area.

content.section_title: FAQ: Whom to apply for the registration?
content.text: A construction worker who intends to get himself registered as a member of the Board shall apply to the Labour Inspector of his area.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the mandatory documents for the registration?
content.text: The documents required for registration are, age proof(If no age proof is available self declaration), residence proof, adhar card(if available), Bank Account details (Bank branch, account No. and IFSC/RTGS number), detail of dependents, and employer's certificate of working for 90 days as construction worker, and nomination form.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there a specific form for the registration through offline mode as a beneficiary under the BOCW Welfare Board?
content.text: Yes, the application for registration must be made in Form No. 28.

content.section_title: FAQ: How much is the fee associated with the registration process?
content.text: The rate of registration fee is [?]25/- at the time of registration. It is a one time fee.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the rate of contribution?
content.text: A registered worker is required to contribute [?]10/-per month as contribution fee to the Board.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who are the family members considered for the benefits?
content.text: Husband or wife, minor sons (including major sons who are insane or physically handicapped and unable to earn), unmarried daughters and the parents of the building worker, who are solely dependent on the registered worker..

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the amount of financial assistance provided under the “Maternity Benefit Scheme”?
content.text: Female construction workers receive [?]21,000/- per child. Male construction workers receive [?]5,000/- per child for their spouse.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can I apply for this benefit if I have received similar benefits from another organization?
content.text: Yes, you can apply for this benefit even if you have received similar benefits from other organizations.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the timeframe to apply for the “Maternity Benefit Scheme”?
content.text: You must apply within 3 months of the child's birth.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can I apply for this benefit if I have not been a member for 6 months?
content.text: No, you must have been a registered member for at least six months before the birth of the child to qualify for this benefit.

content.section_title: FAQ: What form needs to be used to apply for this welfare scheme?
content.text: The application must be made in the prescribed Form No. 46 which can be downloaded from https://bocw.punjab.gov.in/userfiles/file/Maternity%20Benefit%20Performa.pdf or can be collected from the Labour Inspectors of the concerned area.

content.section_title: FAQ: Whom to apply for getting benefits of welfare schemes?
content.text: For the application of the concerned welfare schemes of the Board, the construction worker has to visit the nearest Sewa Kendras or contact the Assistant Labour Commissioner/ Labour-cum-Conciliation Officer/ Labour Inspector/ Labour Enforcement Officer of the concerned area.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The "Maternity Benefit/Delivery Assistance'' by the BOCW Welfare Board, Department of Labour, Karnataka, is a welfare scheme that provides financial assistance to registered women construction workers for the birth of upto two (2) children.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The "Maternity Benefit/Delivery Assistance'' by the BOCW Welfare Board, Department of Labour, Karnataka, is a welfare scheme for the registered workers of the board. Under this Scheme, financial support shall be provided to registered woman construction workers in Karnataka upon the birth of a child. This assistance is available for the first two (2) deliveries only.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: - **Financial Assistance:** ₹50,000/- for each of the first two (2) deliveries.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
> ***For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:***

- The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker (Unorganised Worker).
- The applicant should be a minimum of 18 years of age.
- The applicant should have engaged in building and construction work for at least 90 days in a given year.

> **For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:**

- The applicant should be a registered woman construction worker Under the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of Karnataka.
- The applicant should be pregnant.
- Assistance is only for the first two (2) deliveries.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
> ***Registration Process as a Building/ Construction Worker:***

**Step 1:** Visit the official [Website ](https://kbocwwb.karnataka.gov.in/login)of Karnataka Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (KBOCWWB) and click on “Register”.
**Step 2:** Select “Register as New Construction Worker” then enter your mobile number as per the Aadhaar Registered and verify with the received OTP.
**Step 3:** From the dashboard, click on “Registration”.
**Step 4:** Fill in all the mandatory details and upload the required documents. Click on “Final Submit” to complete the registration process.
> **Application Process of the Welfare Scheme:**

**Step 1:** Visit the official [Website ](https://kbocwwb.karnataka.gov.in/login)of Karnataka Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (KBOCWWB) and click on “Login”.
***Step 2:*** Enter your registered mobile number and the received OTP.
**Step 3:** From the dashboard, click on “Schemes” (After the user Registration is approved, the user can access the schemes page).
**Step 4:** Select the concerned scheme. Fill in all the mandatory details and upload the required documents.
**Step 5:**  Review the details and check the declaration box. Now click on “Submit” to complete the application process.
> **Renewal Process:**

**Step 1:** Visit the official [Website ](https://kbocwwb.karnataka.gov.in/login)of Karnataka Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (KBOCWWB) and click on “Login”.
Step 2: Enter your registered mobile number and the received OTP.
**Step 3:** From the dashboard, click on “Renewal”. Fill in mandatory details and upload the required documents.
**Step 4:** Review the details and click on “Submit” to complete the renewal process.
> **Note:**

- **Renewal:** Every construction worker must renew their registration with the Karnataka Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board every 3 years. If not renewed within this period, a 1-year grace period is granted. After that, the registration becomes invalid.
> **Helpdesk:**

Karnataka Building And Other Construction Workers Welfare Board,
**Address:** Karmika Bhavan, Dairy Circle, Bannerghatta Road, Bangalore-29.
**Office No:** 080-29753078
**E-mail:** labour.commissioner42@gmail.com
[List of District Office.](https://karbwwb.karnataka.gov.in/7/district-office/en)
[List of Taluk Offices.](https://karmikaspandana.karnataka.gov.in/page/Contact+Us/Labour+Inspector/en)

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:- Employment Certificate/90 Days Work Certificate (Form V(A)/V(B)/V(C)/V(D)).
- Aadhar Card (Self-Attested).
- Ration Card(non-mandatory).
- Age Proof (Voter ID Card/Aadhar Card).
For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:- Proof of Identity/Smart card issued by the Board (Attested).
- Affidavit for second child.
- Proof of Bank Account.
- Photo of Child.
- Employment Certificate.
- Discharge Summary.
- Birth Certificate of Child.
For the Renewal Process:- Employment Certificate/90 Days Work Certificate (Form V(A)/V(B)/V(C)/V(D)).

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can register as a beneficiary under the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of Karnataka?
content.text: Any construction worker whose age is minimum 18 years of age and has worked in any construction work for a minimum of 90 days in a year, are eligible to register as a beneficiary under the Board.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there a minimum service requirement to register as a beneficiary?
content.text: Yes, the building worker must have completed 90 days of service as a building worker in the preceding 12 months.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can apply for the welfare scheme?
content.text: Any registered worker of the Board can apply for the welfare scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the mandatory documents for the registration?
content.text: The documents required for registration are: Employment Certificate (Form V(A)/V(B)/V(C)/V(D)), Aadhar Card (Self-Attested), Ration Card(non-mandatory), Age Proof (one of them Example: Voter ID Card, Aadhar Card).

content.section_title: FAQ: How often must a construction worker renew their registration with the Karnataka Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board?
content.text: A construction worker must renew their registration every 3 years.

content.section_title: FAQ: What happens if a worker doesn't renew their registration within the 3-year period?
content.text: If a worker doesn't renew their registration within the 3-year period, they are granted a 1-year grace period to complete the renewal.

content.section_title: FAQ: What happens if a worker doesn't renew their registration during the grace period?
content.text: If the worker fails to renew their registration during the 1-year grace period, their registration will become invalid.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any application fee required for the registration under the Karnataka Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board?
content.text: No, as per the guidelines, there is no fee required for the registration.

content.section_title: FAQ: How to enroll as a beneficiary under the Karnataka Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board?
content.text: Applicants can visit the official Website of Karnataka Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (KBOCWWB) to enroll themselves as a beneficiary under the board: https://kbocwwb.karnataka.gov.in/login

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible for the “Maternity Benefit/Delivery Assistance” scheme?
content.text: Registered woman construction workers who have given birth are eligible for this scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: How much financial assistance shall be provided under this scheme?
content.text: Under this scheme, [?]50,000/- is provided for each of the first two deliveries.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is the scheme applicable for more than two children?
content.text: No, the scheme is only applicable for the first two children.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is the assistance available if there are already two living children?
content.text: No, the assistance is not available if there are already two living children.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is required to claim assistance for the second delivery?
content.text: To claim assistance for the second delivery, an affidavit stating that the claim is for the second delivery must be submitted, along with other documents.

content.section_title: FAQ: How to apply for the assistance under this scheme?
content.text: Applicants should visit the official Website of Karnataka Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (JKBOCWWB) to avail the assistance under this scheme: https://kbocwwb.karnataka.gov.in/login

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The "Maternity Benefit for Women Beneficiaries" scheme under the Nagaland Building and Other Construction Workers' Welfare Board provides financial assistance to pregnant women registered as beneficiaries and wives of registered beneficiaries. The assistance can be availed up to two times.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The "Maternity Benefit for Women Beneficiaries" scheme launched by the Nagaland Building and Other Construction Workers' Welfare Board provides financial assistance of ₹6,000/- per pregnancy to women registered as beneficiaries and wives of registered beneficiaries. The assistance can be availed up to two times.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: - **Financial assistance of ₹6,000/- per pregnancy (up to two times).**

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant should be a registered women construction worker under the Nagaland Building and Other Construction Workers' Welfare Board or the wife of a registered male worker.
1. The applicant should be Pregnant.
1. Benefit is available for the first two live births.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Step-1:** The interested applicant should visit (during office hours) the Nagaland Building and Other Construction Workers' Welfare Board and request a hard copy of the prescribed format of the application form from the staff exclusively entrusted to issue and collect filled-in applications.
**Step-2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
**Step-3:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the Assistant Labour Commissioner.
**Step-4:** Request a receipt or acknowledgement from the Assistant Labour Commissioner to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Aadhaar Card of the Applicant and Registered Worker.
- Admission and Discharge Summary (issued by the Medical Officer of the Hospital where the baby was delivered).
- Combined Certificate (issued from the Chairperson of the Village Health Committee and VCC for home delivery cases)
- Birth Certificate of the Child.
- Bank Account Passbook.
- Any Other Documents if required.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible for the maternity benefit?
content.text: Women registered beneficiaries and wives of registered building workers.

content.section_title: FAQ: How much financial assistance is provided?
content.text: [?]6,000 per pregnancy, up to a maximum of two pregnancies.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can the benefit be availed for more than two pregnancies?
content.text: No, the benefit is limited to two pregnancies.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is home delivery covered under this scheme?
content.text: Yes, home delivery is covered, but a certificate from the Village Health Committee and VCC is required.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can this benefit be availed for miscarriages?
content.text: No, this benefit is specifically for live births.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any age restriction for applying for this benefit?
content.text: No specific age restrictions are mentioned as long as the beneficiary is registered.

content.section_title: FAQ: What documents are required for the application?
content.text: Admission and discharge summary from the hospital or a combined certificate from Village Health Committee for home delivery, along with the baby's birth certificate.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where should I submit my application?
content.text: Submit your application to the Assistant Labour Commissioner (ALC) office.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is a birth certificate mandatory for the application?
content.text: Yes, a birth certificate is required for both hospital and home deliveries.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are there any charges or fees to apply for this scheme?
content.text: No, the application process is free of charge.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: "Maternity Benefits Scheme" provides an amount equivalent to 90 days of the minimum wages set by the State Govt. for unskilled workers at the time of childbirth. The applicant should be a female construction worker who has completed one year of membership with the Board.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: "Maternity Benefits Scheme" by the Bihar Building & Other Construction Workers' Welfare Board, Labour Resources Department, Government of Bihar, provides an amount equivalent to 90 days of the minimum wages set by the State Government for unskilled workers at the time of childbirth. This benefit is applicable for the first two deliveries. The applicant should be a female construction worker who has completed one year of membership with the Board.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: Financial assistance equivalent to 90 days of the minimum wages set by the State Government for unskilled workers.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
- The applicant should be female.
- The applicant should be registered with the Bihar Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (BBOCWWB).
- The applicant should have completed at least one year of membership with the Board.
- The membership must be active at the time of application.
- The benefits will be provided for the first two childbirths.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
> **Registration**

**Step 1:** Visit the [Official Website](https://bocw.bihar.gov.in/Website/LabourReg.aspx) of the Bihar Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, and click "[Apply for New Registration](https://bocw.bihar.gov.in/Registration/AddLabourRegistrationWeb.aspx)".
**Step 4:** On the next page, provide your details including your Aadhaar Number. Then click "Authenticate".
**Step 5:** An online form will appear. In the form, fill in all the mandatory fields. The fields marked with an asterisk (*) are mandatory.
**Step 6:** Click "Submit". Proceed to pay the application fee online.
**Step 7:** **** Upon successful payment, you will receive your construction worker registration number.
> **Application**

**Step 1:** **** Visit the [Official Website ](https://bocw.bihar.gov.in/Website/SchemeApply.aspx)of the Bihar Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
**Step 2:** Click on "[Scheme Application](https://bocw.bihar.gov.in/Website/SchemeApply.aspx)" and select "[Apply for Scheme](https://bocw.bihar.gov.in/Registration/SchemeTaggingWeb.aspx)".
**Step 3:** **** Enter your registration number and click "Show" to view your details.
**Step 4:** **** Select the scheme you intend to apply for, enter all mandatory information and upload the necessary documents in the specified format and size.
**Step 6:** **** Click on "Submit Application" to complete the process.
> **Application Tracking**

Applicants can track their application status online on the [official website ](https://bocw.bihar.gov.in/Registration/ApplicationStatus.aspx)of the Board by providing their registration number.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Identity Card of the Registered Worker
- Bank Account Details
- Birth Certificate
- Proof of Membership
- Proof of Age at the Time of Registration
- Any Other Documents as Required

content.section_title: FAQ: If I am a female construction worker who has been working for 8 months in Bihar, would I be eligible to receive maternity benefits from the state welfare board?
content.text: No, you would not be eligible at this time. The scheme requires female construction workers to complete at least one year of membership with the Bihar Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board before they can receive benefits.

content.section_title: FAQ: As a registered female construction worker in Bihar, what is the maximum number of times I can receive maternity benefits through this welfare scheme?
content.text: You can receive maternity benefits for your first two childbirths only. The benefit amount for each eligible childbirth will be equivalent to 90 days of the state-mandated minimum wages for unskilled workers.

content.section_title: FAQ: What steps do I need to take to register myself as a construction worker before I can apply for maternity benefits in Bihar?
content.text: You need to visit the official website of the Bihar Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, click on "Apply for New Registration", provide your Aadhaar Number for authentication, fill in all mandatory fields marked with asterisk (*), and pay the application fee online to receive your registration number.

content.section_title: FAQ: Could you explain the complete process of applying for maternity benefits after I have received my construction worker registration number?
content.text: Visit the official website, click on "Scheme Application", select "Apply for Scheme", enter your registration number to view your details, select the maternity benefits scheme, fill in all mandatory information, upload required documents in the specified format and size, and click "Submit Application".

content.section_title: FAQ: If I register as a construction worker today, when will I become eligible to apply for maternity benefits under this scheme?
content.text: You will become eligible to apply exactly one year after your registration date, provided your membership remains active throughout this period and it is either your first or second childbirth.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the exact financial assistance I will receive as maternity benefits under this welfare scheme?
content.text: You will receive an amount equivalent to 90 days of the current minimum wages that are set by the Bihar State Government for unskilled workers at the time of your childbirth.

content.section_title: FAQ: As a registered construction worker, what should I do to ensure my membership remains active so I can claim maternity benefits when needed?
content.text: Your membership must remain active at the time of application, though the provided information doesn't specify the exact requirements for maintaining active membership status. It would be best to contact the welfare board directly for these details.

content.section_title: FAQ: If I am currently pregnant and planning to register as a construction worker, will I be eligible for maternity benefits immediately after completing one year of membership?
content.text: Yes, once you complete one year of membership with the Board, you will be eligible for maternity benefits provided this is either your first or second childbirth and your membership remains active.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: "Matrutva Prasuvidha Yojana" is a Medical Assistance scheme by the Department of Labour, Employment, Training & Skill Development Govt of Jharkhand. The scheme provides financial assistance of ₹ 15,000/- for the first two deliveries of the registered female worker.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: "Matrutva Prasuvidha Yojana" is a Medical Assistance scheme by the Department of Labour, Employment, Training & Skill Development Govt of Jharkhand. This is a 100% State Sponsored Scheme. Only the residents/domicile of the state of Jharkhand can apply to this scheme. Applications for this scheme are accepted online through the official website of the department. The scheme provides financial assistance of ₹ 15,000/- for the first two deliveries of the registered female worker.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant should be a domicile in the state of Jharkhand.
1. The applicant should be a female.
1. The applicant should be pregnant.
1. The applicant should be a Worker registered with the 'Jharkhand Building and Other Construction Worker Welfare Board (JBOCWW Board)'.
1. The applicant should be between 18-60 years of age.
