content.section_title: FAQ: How can I apply for this benefit?
content.text: You need to submit an application in Form XL along with the required documents within the time specified by the Board.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can the amount be sanctioned for both the beneficiary and his dependants?
content.text: Yes, the amount can be sanctioned for the treatment of both the beneficiary and his dependants suffering from the specified ailments.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there a specific timeframe for submitting the application and documents?
content.text: Yes, the Board will specify the timeframe within which the application and documents must be submitted.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where can I obtain Form XL for the application?
content.text: Form XL can be obtained from the Board's office or their official website: https://labour.sikkim.gov.in/Uploads/Bocw/8F1D404A-3EC3-4DA9-A4D6-75880B26D191.pdf

title: Medical Expenses for Treatment of Major Ailments (O.B.O.C.W.W.B)
topic: Medical Expenses for Treatment of Major Ailments (O.B.O.C.W.W.B)

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The “Medical Expenses for Treatment of Major Ailments” scheme by the BOCW Welfare Board, Labour Department, Odisha, is a welfare scheme that provides financial assistance for the treatment of beneficiary or his/ her dependents suffering from major diseases.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The “Medical Expenses for Treatment of Major Ailments” scheme by the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Labour Department, Odisha, is a welfare scheme for the registered workers of the board. Under this scheme, the Board shall provide financial assistance as medical expenses for the treatment of beneficiary or his/ her dependents suffering from disease of Nervous system (Brain & spine related), Cardiovascular System, Kidney and Cancer. The beneficiary should be registered at least for one year in lines of the Odisha State Treatment Fund.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: - **Quantum of Assistance:** ₹3,00,000/-.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
> **For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:**

- The applicant should be a resident of Odisha.
- The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker.
- The applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
- The applicant should have engaged in building and construction work for at least 90 days in a given year.
- The applicant should not be a member of any other Welfare Fund established under any law.

> **For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:**

- The applicant should be a registered member of the Odisha Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board or the applicant should be a dependent of the member.
- The beneficiary should be registered at least for one year in line of the Odisha State Treatment Fund.
- The applicant should suffer from any major diseases.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
> **Registration Process as a Building/ Construction Worker:**

**Step 1:** Visit the official [website ](https://nirmanshramik.odisha.gov.in/obocwwb/public/cwLogin)of the Odisha BOCWW Board.
**Step 2:** Click on “One Time Registration” on the top right side of the page.
**Step 3:** Click on the Declaration box and then on the “Proceed” button.
**Step 4:** In the next window fill in all the required details carefully and click on “Proceed”.
**Step 5:** Enter the received OTP and click on “Submit”.
**Step 6:** Your account will be successfully created and a Username will be displayed (Keep the Username for future references).
> **Application Process of the Welfare Scheme:**

**Step 1:** Visit the Applicant [Login page](https://nirmanshramik.odisha.gov.in/obocwwb/public/cwLogin) of the Odisha BOCWW Board.
**Step 2:** Enter your Username, OTP and fill in the captcha then click on “Sign In”.
**Step 3:** In the next window, fill in your General Details, Address Details, Family & Nominee Details, Details of the establishment(s) where the applicant worked during the last one year, Bank Details etc. and then click on “Submit”.
**Step 4:** Now in the Menu bar section, click on “Benefit”. From the list select the concerned benefit you want to apply.
**Step 5:** Fill in all the mandatory details carefully and click on “Submit” to complete the application process.
> **Check Your Application Status:** Registered workers can check their application status through visiting this [link ](https://nirmanshramik.odisha.gov.in/obocwwb/public/applicationTracking)upon entering their Application Tracking Number.

> **Pay Annual Contribution:** Registered workers can pay the Annual Contribution through visiting this [link ](https://nirmanshramik.odisha.gov.in/obocwwb/public/payAnnuealContribution)upon entering their Registration Number.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
> **Registration Process as a Building/ Construction Worker:**

**Step 1:** To register as a beneficiary under the BOCW Welfare Board, an applicant worker should download [Form-XXVII](https://bocboard.labdirodisha.gov.in/Building/homecontent/BeneficiaryForms/REGISTRATION%20FORM.pdf) and attach all the mandatory documents (self-attested, if required).
**Step 2:** Application shall be submitted to the District Labour Officers (D.L.O)/ Asst. Labour Officers/ Rural Labour Inspectors of your area.
> **Application Process of the Welfare Scheme:**

**Step 1:** A registered worker should download [Form-XL](https://bocboard.labdirodisha.gov.in/Building/homecontent/BeneficiaryForms/MAJOR%20AILMENTS.pdf) and attach all the mandatory documents (self-attested, if required).
**Step 2:** Application shall be submitted to the District Labour Officers (D.L.O)/ Asst. Labour Officers/ Rural Labour Inspectors of your area.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:- Aadhaar Linked Bank Passbook Front Page Copy (Allowed file types: PNG, JPG, PDF. Allowed size: less than 500 KB).
- Passport Size Photo (Allowed type: PNG, JPG. Allowed size: less than 500 KB).
- Aadhaar Card (Allowed file types: PNG, JPG, PDF. Allowed size: less than 500 KB).
- Signature of Applicant (Allowed type: PNG, JPG. Allowed size: less than 500 KB).
- Employment certificate (Allowed file types: PNG, JPG, PDF. Allowed size: less than 500 KB).
For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:- Xerox copy of Registration Certificate of the beneficiary duly attested by DLO/ ALO/ RLI of the concerned district.
- Xerox copy of updated annual contribution receipt to be verified by the DLO/ ALO/ RLI of the concerned district.
- Essentiality Certificate to be filled completely and to be signed not below the rank of Assistant Surgeon of Government Hospital.
- All original Bills should be verified by the treating doctor and it should be signed on the face of the bills with a seal.
- In case of disability due to accident caused during employment and as per the OB & OCWW Board Rules, Disability Certificate by a Medical Board to be submitted stating the cause of accidents, percentage of disability with permanent/ temporary nature.
- In case of Temporary Disability, the construction worker is eligible only for medical treatment.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the age requirement for registration as a beneficiary under the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of Odisha?
content.text: The building worker must be at least 18 years old and not older than 60 years old.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there a minimum service requirement to register as a beneficiary?
content.text: Yes, the building worker must have completed 90 days of service as a building worker in the preceding 12 months.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can apply for the welfare scheme?
content.text: Any registered worker of the Board can apply for the welfare scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is responsible for registering a worker as a beneficiary?
content.text: The District Labour Officers (D.L.O)/ Asst. Labour Officers/ Rural Labour Inspectors are acting as Registering Officers for the registration of beneficiaries under the Board.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible for registration as a beneficiary of the BOCW Welfare Board?
content.text: Every building worker who is between the age of 18-60 years and who has been engaged in any building or other construction work for not less than 90 days during the preceding one year is eligible for registration as a beneficiary of the BOCW Welfare Board of Odisha.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can Odia migrant workmen be registered as beneficiaries under the Odisha Building & Other Construction Workers’ Welfare Board?
content.text: Yes, Odia migrant workmen engaged in building and other construction works in other states can be registered as beneficiaries under the Odisha Building & Other Construction Workers' Welfare Board.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the procedure for applying for the registration?
content.text:
To register as a beneficiary under the BOCW Welfare Board, an applicant worker should download Form-XXVII and attach all the mandatory documents (self attested, if required) and a fee of [?]20/- to the District Labour Officers (D.L.O)/ Asst. Labour Officers/ Rural Labour Inspectors of your area.
Alternatively, registration can also be done through the official website of the Odisha BOCWW Board: https://nirmanshramik.odisha.gov.in/obocwwb/public/cwLogin .

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there a specific form for the registration through offline mode as a beneficiary under the BOCW Welfare Board?
content.text: Yes, the application for registration must be made in Form-XXVII. You can obtain this form from the District Labour Officers (D.L.O)/ Asst. Labour Officers/ Rural Labour Inspectors in your area.

content.section_title: FAQ: How much is the fee associated with the registration process?
content.text: The registration fee is [?]20/- which is payable during the registration.

content.section_title: FAQ: What happens after I pay the application fee?
content.text: After you pay the application fee of  [?]20/-, your application will be reviewed by the registering officer. The decision to approve or reject your application will be based on the merits of your application.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who decides whether my application will be approved or rejected?
content.text: The registering officer is responsible for deciding whether your application will be approved or rejected based on its merits.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can my application be rejected even if I have paid the application fee?
content.text: Yes, your application can be rejected even if you have paid the application fee. The final decision is made by the registering officer based on the merits of your application.

content.section_title: FAQ: How much is the fee associated with the renewal of the registration?
content.text: The renewal fee is [?]50/- which is payable during the renewal process of the registration.

content.section_title: FAQ: What criteria must an applicant meet to be considered for this benefit?
content.text: A registered member of the Odisha Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board or dependents of the member who is registered at least for one year in lines of the Odisha State Treatment Fund, shall be eligible for this benefit.

content.section_title: FAQ: What kind of assistance is provided under this scheme?
content.text: Under this scheme, the Board shall provide an assistance of [?]3,00,000/-.

content.section_title: FAQ: What form needs to be used to apply for this welfare scheme?
content.text: The application must be made in the prescribed Form-XL. The form can be downloaded from https://bocboard.labdirodisha.gov.in/Building/homecontent/BeneficiaryForms/MAJOR%20AILMENTS.pdf  or can be collected from the District Labour Officers (D.L.O)/ Asst. Labour Officers/ Rural Labour Inspectors of your area.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where do I submit the application for this welfare scheme?
content.text: Application can be submitted online on: https://nirmanshramik.odisha.gov.in/obocwwb/public/cwLogin . Alternatively , the offline application for this welfare scheme should be submitted to the District Labour Officers (D.L.O)/ Asst. Labour Officers/ Rural Labour Inspectors in your area.

content.section_title: FAQ: What happens if the application is rejected?
content.text: In case of rejection, the applicants would be intimated about such rejection, with due intimation to the Board.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the consequences of submitting fraudulent documents for assistance?
content.text: In case of receipt of assistance on the basis of fraudulent documents, the amount released towards any assistance would be recovered from the applicant under the provisions of the OPDR Act, and his/ her membership would be ceased.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The "Medical Assistance to Beneficiaries" scheme under the Labour and Employment Department HP, provides financial support for healthcare. It covers ₹50,000 for outdoor treatment, ₹1,00,000 for indoor treatment, and ₹5,00,000 for critical illnesses each year, upon submission of valid medical bills.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The "Medical Assistance to Beneficiaries" scheme, launched by the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (HPBOCWWB) under the Government of Himachal Pradesh, offers registered beneficiaries and their dependents annual financial support for medical expenses, including ₹50,000 for outdoor treatment, ₹1,00,000 for indoor medical reimbursement, and ₹5,00,000 for critical illnesses, provided they submit valid medical bills from government hospitals or government-approved facilities.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
- ₹50,000 is provided annually for outdoor medical treatment.
- ₹1,00,000 is available each year for indoor medical expenses.
- ₹5,00,000 is granted yearly for critical illness treatment for the registered beneficiary and dependents, with valid medical bills.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
**For Registration**

1. The applicant should be a resident of Himachal Pradesh
1. The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker.
1. The applicant should be aged between 18 and 60 years.
1. The worker should have completed at least 90 days of building and other construction work in the last 12 months.
1. The registration of the applicant should be renewed annually.

**For Application**

1. The applicant must be engaged in building or construction work.
1. The applicant must be registered under the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (HPBOCWWB).
1. The worker’s membership with the Welfare Board must be active at the time of application.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Step 1:** The interested applicant should visit the Labor Welfare Office (during office hours) and request a hard copy of the prescribed application form from the concerned authority.
**Step 2:** **** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste a passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attested, if required).
**Step 3:** **** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with a wage slip or appointment letter from the employer, verifying 90 days of construction work. If unavailable, a certificate from a local authority (such as a councillor, executive officer, or Panchayat Secretary) can be submitted to verify employment.
**Step 4:** After submitting the documents and payment, the worker will receive an acknowledgement receipt confirming their registration.

[**Application Form**](https://bocw.hp.nic.in/pdf/formno37_A1b.pdf) **For the Scheme**
**Labour Registration** [**Application Form**](https://bocw.hp.nic.in/pdf/formno27_A1b.pdf)

**NOTE 1:** Ensure that the application is submitted within the prescribed period, if any.
**NOTE 2:** After three years, the worker must visit the office to pay a renewal fee and submit proof of 90 days of work each year to maintain eligibility for benefits.
**NOTE 3:** A one-time registration fee must be paid at the office, and it will be valid for three years.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
**For Registration**
**Step 1:** **** The applicant must visit the [official website](https://bocw.hp.nic.in/index.aspx) of the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
**Step 2:** **** If you are a new user then click worker services, click on "[New Registration](https://bocw.hp.nic.in/PublicPages/AddBeneficiary.aspx)."
**Step 3:** ****  Once the dashboard appears, locate and complete the form by entering all required details. Mandatory fields are marked with an asterisk (*), ensuring that all essential information is provided.
**Step 4:** Attach all necessary documents, including ID proof and any other relevant certificates, as specified. Ensure the documents are clear and meet the format requirements.
**Step 5:** After completing the form and uploading the documents, click on the "Submit" button. You will be directed to the payment gateway to pay the application fee online.
**Step 6:** **** Upon successful payment and submission, a registration number will be generated. This number serves as a confirmation of your application and can be used for future reference.

**For Application**
**Step 1:** The applicant must visit the [official website](https://bocw.hp.nic.in/index.aspx) of the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
**Step 2:** **** Click on the option "[Apply for Benefits.](https://bocw.hp.nic.in/PublicPages/ApplyForSchemes.aspx)"
**Step 3:** Enter your beneficiary details and date of birth, then click "Search" to view your information.
**Step 4:** Select the scheme you wish to apply for to avail of the benefits.
**Step 5:**  Enter all required information and upload the necessary documents as prompted.
**Step 6:** Finally, click on "Submit Application" to complete the process.

**Application Tracking**
Applicants can track their application status online through the[ official website](https://bocw.hp.nic.in/index.aspx) of the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board. Relevant updates will be sent to the registered mobile number.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Identity proof i.e. Aadhaar Card, Voter Card
- Passport-size photograph
- Undertaking
- A copy Work Slip
- Proof of residence
- Ration Card
- Bank account details/copy of bank passbook
- Caste Certificate
- Age proof
- Labour Card
- Medical Document
- Medical Bills
- Identity proof of the nominee/legal heir
- Any other documents as required

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the "Medical Assistance to Beneficiaries" scheme?
content.text: It provides financial support for medical expenses to registered construction workers and their dependents under the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (HPBOCWWB).

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible for the scheme?
content.text: Registered beneficiaries of the HPBOCWWB, aged between 18 and 60 years, who have completed at least 90 days of building or construction work.

content.section_title: FAQ: What benefits are provided under the scheme?
content.text:
[?]50,000 for outdoor medical treatment
[?]1,00,000 for indoor medical treatment
[?]5,00,000 for critical illness treatment

content.section_title: FAQ: How can I register for the scheme?
content.text: Registration can be done by visiting the Labour Welfare Office or through self-registration on the official portal.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any age limit for registration?
content.text: Yes, applicants must be between 18 and 60 years of age.

content.section_title: FAQ: What documents are required for registration?
content.text: A certificate confirming at least 90 days of building or construction work, along with proof of residence in Himachal Pradesh.

content.section_title: FAQ: Do I need to be a resident of Himachal Pradesh to apply?
content.text: Yes, applicants must be residents of Himachal Pradesh.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can I claim the medical benefits for my dependents?
content.text: Yes, the scheme covers the registered beneficiary and their dependents for medical expenses.

content.section_title: FAQ: What kind of medical treatment is covered?
content.text: The scheme covers outdoor treatment, indoor treatment, and critical illness treatment.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are there any restrictions on the hospitals for treatment?
content.text: Only government hospitals or government-approved hospitals can be used for medical treatment to claim the benefits.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to provide medical check-up facilities to registered construction workers through door-to-door visits, health camps, and collection centers.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The scheme “Medical Check-Up Facility” is implemented by the Uttarakhand Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (UKBOCWWB) under the Labour Department, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to provide medical check-up facilities to registered construction workers through door-to-door visits, health camps, and collection centers.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: Under this scheme, medical check-up facilities are provided to construction workers.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text: 1. The applicant must be a construction worker registered with the Uttarakhand Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text: To provide medical check-up benefits as part of social security to construction workers registered with the Uttarakhand Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, medical check-up facilities are offered through door-to-door visits, health camps, and collection centers.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text: 1. A valid identity card i.e. Labour Card

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible for this scheme?
content.text: Construction workers registered with the Uttarakhand Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board are eligible.

content.section_title: FAQ: What benefits are provided under this scheme?
content.text: Medical check-up services and other preventive health services are provided.

content.section_title: FAQ: How are the medical check-up facilities delivered?
content.text: Facilities are provided through door-to-door visits, health camps, and collection centers.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can unregistered workers avail of this facility?
content.text: No, only registered construction workers are eligible for the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can workers choose to visit collection centers instead of door-to-door services?
content.text: Yes.

content.section_title: FAQ: How does this scheme benefit construction workers?
content.text: It promotes early detection of health issues, ensures preventive care, and contributes to overall health and productivity.

title: Medical Assistance for the Treatment of Critical Illness
topic: Medical Assistance for the Treatment of Critical Illness

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: Under this scheme, medical assistance of ₹1,00,000/- for the treatment of serious ailments for registered worker and his/her family members. (the benefit can be availed only after 26th July 2014 as the benefit is covered under the Mediclaim & Personal Accident Insurance scheme).

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The "Medical Assistance for the Treatment of Critical Illness" scheme was launched by the Maharashtra Building And Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (MBOCWW), Labour Department Maharashtra. Under this scheme, medical assistance of ₹1,00,000/- for the treatment of serious ailments for registered worker and his/her family members. (the benefit can be availed only after 26th July 2014 as the benefit is covered under the Mediclaim & Personal Accident Insurance scheme).

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: - **Financial assistance of ₹1,00,000/- is provided for medical treatment.**

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The worker must be engaged in building or construction work.
1. The worker should be registered under the Maharashtra Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (MBOCWW).
1. The worker or family member must be undergoing treatment for a serious illness or critical medical condition.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Step-1:** The interested applicant downloads the [application form](https://mahabocw.in/wp-content/uploads/2019/06/SCHEME_HEALTH_FORM-1.pdf) from the MBOCWW official website.
**Step-2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
**Step-3:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the Labor Commissioner / Government Labor Officer.
**Step-4:** Request a receipt or acknowledgment from the concerned authority to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Passport-size Photo.
- Aadhaar Card.
- Identity Card of Maharashtra Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
- Bank Passbook.
- Medical Certificate (issued by competent medical authorities about serious illness).
- Medical Treatment Papers.
- Proof of Residence (One of Aadhaar Card/Passport/Driving Licence/Ration Sheet/Last Month's Electricity Bill/Gram Panchayat Certificate).

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the Medical Assistance for the Treatment of Critical Illness scheme?
content.text: It provides [?]1,00,000 for medical treatment of critical illnesses for registered construction workers and their immediate family members.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can benefit from this scheme?
content.text: Registered construction workers under the Maharashtra Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (MBOCWW) and their family members undergoing treatment for critical illnesses.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the amount of financial assistance provided?
content.text: The scheme provides up to [?]1,00,000 for medical treatment.

content.section_title: FAQ: Does this scheme cover treatment for family members?
content.text: Yes, the scheme covers both the worker and their immediate family members.

content.section_title: FAQ: How do I apply for this scheme?
content.text: Download the application form from the MBOCWW website, fill it out, attach the required documents, and submit it to the Labour Commissioner or Government Labour Officer.

content.section_title: FAQ: What documents are required to apply for the scheme?
content.text: Documents include a passport-size photo, Aadhaar card, identity card from MBOCWW, bank passbook, medical certificate, treatment papers, and proof of residence.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there a deadline for applying for the scheme?
content.text: There is no specific deadline, but it is recommended to apply promptly after the diagnosis of a critical illness.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is the scheme only available after 26th July 2014?
content.text: Yes, the benefit can be availed only after 26th July 2014, as it is covered under the Mediclaim & Personal Accident Insurance scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is this scheme available for workers across India?
content.text: No, it is available only for registered workers in Maharashtra under the MBOCWW.

content.section_title: FAQ: Does the scheme cover all types of illnesses?
content.text: The scheme covers serious ailments and critical medical conditions, as certified by a competent medical authority.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can I track my application?
content.text: Upon submission, request a receipt or acknowledgment from the concerned authority to track your application status.

title: Medical Assistance for the Construction Workers
topic: Medical Assistance for the Construction Workers

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: "Medical Assistance for the Construction Workers" was launched by the Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Labour Dept, Delhi. The registered workers are given medical assistance upto ₹ 10,000/- in case of being hospitalized for 5 or more days due to an accident or disease.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The "Medical Assistance for the Construction Workers" by the Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Labour Department, Delhi, is a welfare scheme for the registered workers of the board. Through this scheme, the eligible workers of the board are provided with medical assistance upto ₹ 10,000/- in case of being hospitalized for 05 or more days due to an accident or any disease.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
- ₹2,000/- for the first five days.
- ₹200/- each for remaining days, upto a maximum of ₹10,000/-, available from the date of registration.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
> **For Registration**

- The applicant should be a resident of Delhi.
- The applicant should be a Building/Construction Worker.
- The applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
- The applicant should have served the Board for at least 90 days in a given year.
> **For Application**

- The applicant should be registered with the "Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board".
- The applicant should have been hospitalized for 05 or more days.
- The cause of hospitalization should be an accident or any disease.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
> **Registration**

**Step 1:** Visit the DBoCWWB [website ](https://dbocwwb.delhi.gov.in/cwapp/MyLoginPage)and click “Register Now”.
**Step 2:** Enter the following mandatory details: Adhaar Card Number, Name as on Aadhar Card, Mobile Number, Date of Birth (as on Aadhar Card), Gender, etc. Create a password and confirm it. Click “Register”. You will be redirected to the registration page.
**Step 3:** On the registration page, fill in the mandatory details (Father Name, Mother Name, Photograph, Applicant’s Video, etc) and upload the mandatory documents in the specified filetype and filesize. Click “Save & Next”.
**Step 4:** On the next page, enter the mandatory details regarding your work/workplace (Previous/Current Employer Details, Proof of 90 Days Employment, etc) and upload the mandatory documents in the specified filetype and filesize. Click “Save & Next”.
**Step 5: C**lick on “Add Family Members” to avail the benefits of various schemes.
**Step 6:** In the next window, add your Bank Account details.
**Step 7:** In the next window, upload your Aadhaar Card, Address Proof, Birth Proof, Employment Proof, Bank Account details etc.
**Step 8:** In the next window, the "Affidavit of the Applicant" will be displayed. Verify all the details, and click on the checkbox.
**Step 9:** Proceed for the online registration fee of ₹25/- through a Debit Card/ Internet Banking/ UPI.
**Step 10:** Enter the OTP received on your registered mobile number to apply. Note down the Application Number for future reference. Download the acknowledgement receipt.
> **Track Your Application**

Visit the [homepage](https://dbocwwb.delhi.gov.in/) of the website regularly and click on "Track Your Application" to check the status of your application.

> **Application**

**Step 1:** Visit the DBoCWWB [website ](https://dbocwwb.delhi.gov.in/cwapp/MyLoginPage)and click “Apply for Scheme”.
**Step 2:** Log in through Aadhaar Number/ Registration Card/ OTP.
**Step 3:** Apply for the “Medical Assistance” scheme.

> **Helpline Number (24x1) : 011-41236600**

> **DBOCWWB Department**

> **Number (office hours: 9:30AM - 6:00PM) : 011-23813846**

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Discharge Certificate of the Hospital.
- Bill for Purchase of Medicine.

content.section_title: FAQ: Which Department of the NCT of Delhi administers this scheme?
content.text: This scheme is administered by the Labour Department, of the National Capital Territory of Delhi.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where can I find the URL of the Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board?
content.text: The URL of the Official Website of the Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board is: https://bocw.delhi.gov.in/.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is self-attestation required for attaching documents with the application?
content.text: Yes, applicants need to self-attest the copies of mandatory documents while submitting the application.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the URL for the registration of this scheme?
content.text: The applicants needs to register through clicking on the "Register" button on DBoCWWB website: https://dbocwwb.delhi.gov.in/cwapp/MyLoginPage?ReturnUrl=%2Fcwapp%2F.

content.section_title: FAQ: In case of change, how does the worker update their registered Mobile number on the website?
content.text: The facility to amend a worker's registered mobile number on the new DBoCWWB Website is still under process. However, workers who are migrating their membership from E-District Website to the new DBoCWWB Website have an option to update/revise their mobile numbers in the migration form.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is a Construction Certificate/Labour Card?
content.text:
As an enrolled member with BoCW Welfare Board, the worker will receive a Construction Certificate popularly known as the Labour Card. A Construction Certificate/Labour Card is a document with the worker's personal details like DoB, Address, Registration Number, Date of Registration along with details of the next date of renewal.
The Construction Certificate/Labour Card is to be shown when applying for any of the claims under the BoCW Act. It is important for the worker to renew their Construction Certificate/Labour Card in order to remain active members. This renewal must be undertaken on a yearly basis.

content.section_title: FAQ: I am from another state but working as a construction worker in Delhi, can I get registered?
content.text: Yes, a Construction Worker native to any place in India, but working in Delhi can register with the DBoCWWB. The Construction Workers must own an Aadhar Card from any place in India and should have a Proof of Residence of staying in Delhi.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can members in my family also register if they are all registered construction workers?
content.text: Yes, if there are more than one member in the family who are engaged in Construction Work, they can all register under the DBoCWWB.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there a specific age requirement for construction workers to be eligible for registration?
content.text: Yes, construction workers must be at least 18 years old but not more than 60 years old to be eligible for registration under Section 12 of the Main Act.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the duration of engagement required for construction workers to qualify for registration?
content.text: Construction workers must have engaged in building or other construction work for a minimum of 90 days during the preceding 12 months to qualify for registration under Section 12 of the Main Act.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the Registration/Renewal fee?
content.text: Registration : [?] 25/-and Renewal : [?] 20/- and [?] 2/- per month (late fee).

content.section_title: FAQ: How can one track the status of my Application on the Website?
content.text: To track, the worker can use the track feature available on the website's landing page. Choose the "Type of Service' availed, i.e, whether the application was for Registration/Renewal/Claims/Amendment. In the white box, enter the application number mentioned in the acknowledgment receipt. Click Track Application now and a pop-up box will indicate the current status of the Application.

content.section_title: FAQ: What consequences a worker may face if he/she does not renew the card?
content.text: If a worker does not renew the card, he will not be eligible for the benefit in future, but with the permission of the authorized officer, the membership can be returned with the outstanding amount, though this facility will not be available more than 2 times.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The "Medical Termination of Pregnancy Scheme (PULWS)" is a Welfare Scheme by the "Puducherry Unorganised Labourers' Welfare Society", Labour Department, Union Territory of Puducherry. The Society extends ₹250/- to the member/ spouse of the member for the Medical Termination of Pregnancy.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The "Medical Termination of Pregnancy Scheme (PULWS)" is a Welfare Scheme for the Unorganized Workers in Puducherry by the "Puducherry Unorganised Labourers' Welfare Society", Labour Department, Union Territory of Puducherry. The Society extends ₹250/- to the member/ spouse of the member for the Medical Termination of Pregnancy (not more than two occasions). The amount is credited through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) into the bank account. The applications are accepted offline.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
- Financial Assistance of ₹250/- (not more than two occasions).
- The amount is credited through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) into the bank account.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
- The applicant should be a citizen of India.
- The applicant should be a resident of the Union Territory of Puducherry.
- The applicant should be an Unorganized Worker: Taxi and Auto Drivers, Workers Employed in Shops & Catering Establishments, Carpenters, Nathaswara Vidwans, Hair Dressers, Tailors, Cycle Rickshaw Drivers, Cobblers, Washermans, Goldsmith, Blacksmith, Brass Vessel Makers, Sculptors, Loading and Unloading Workers, Tree Climbers, Pottery Workers, Coir Workers, Toddy Neera Tappers, Bullock Cart Drivers, Thatchers, Cooks, Hawkers, News Paper Vendors, Workers Engaged in Bricks and Tiles Manufacturing, One Hundred, Workers Engaged in Distribution of Liquid Petroleum Gas Cylinder, Domestic Workers, Light Music Instrument Players, Automobile Workshop Workers.
- The applicant should be enrolled with the Puducherry Unorganised Labourers' Welfare Society.
- The applicant should be between 18 to 59 years of age.
- The applicant should be taking an Annual Subscription of ₹100/- every year.
- The applicant should be paying a Registration Fee of ₹25/-.
- The applicant should be Pregnant.
- The applicant should be willing to terminate the pregnancy.
- The benefits are provided for up to two ocassions of Medical Termination of Pregnancy.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Step 1:** The interested applicant should visit the Office of the Puducherry Unorganised Labourer's Welfare Society, No.1, Rue Sufferen, Puducherry, and obtain the prescribed format of the application form free of cost from the concerned authority exclusively entrusted to issue and collect filled-in applications.
**Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste the passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
**Step 3:** The interested construction worker should submit the duly filled and signed application form and the documents to the concerned authority.
**Step 4:** Request a receipt or acknowledgement from the concerned authority to whom the application form has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Two Passport-size Photographs.
- Residential/Domicile Certificate.
- Original Birth Certificate.
- Discharge Slip.
- Last Subscription Bill.
- Society ID Card/ Proof of Registration.
- Aadhaar Card.
- Ration Card.
- Bank Details.
- Medical Certificate, issued by a registered medical practitioner confirming the need for the termination of pregnancy, based on the conditions specified under the Medical Termination of Pregnancy Act.
- Additional medical reports or documents that support the need for the termination of pregnancy.

content.section_title: FAQ: Which entity of the Labour Department of Pudcherry administers this scheme?
content.text: The scheme is administered by the Puducherry Unorganised Labourers' Welfare Society under the Labour Department, Union Territory of Puducherry.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the mode of payment for the benefit under the scheme?
content.text: The benefit is transferred through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) into the bank account.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are applications for this scheme accepted online or offline?
content.text: Applications for this scheme are accepted offline. Interested applicants need to visit the Office of the Puducherry Unorganised Labourer's Welfare Society to obtain the application form.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the common eligibility criteria to apply for the welfare schemes of the "Puducherry Unorganised Labourers' Welfare Society"?
content.text: To be eligible, the applicant should be an Indian citizen, a resident of the Union Territory of Puducherry, an unorganized worker in specified occupations, enrolled with the Welfare Society, aged between 18 to 59 years, and meeting the annual subscription and registration fee requirements.

content.section_title: FAQ: What occupations are considered as "Unorganized Workers" under the scheme?
content.text: Occupations such as taxi and auto drivers, workers employed in shops and catering establishments, carpenters, hairdressers, cobblers, and various others are considered as unorganized workers eligible for the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: How much is the Annual Subscription for the Maternity Benefit Scheme, and who is required to pay it?
content.text: The applicant needs to pay an annual subscription of [?]100 every year to avail the benefits under the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where can an interested applicant obtain the application form for the scheme?
content.text: The applicant should visit the Office of the Puducherry Unorganised Labourer's Welfare Society, No.1, Rue Sufferen, Puducherry, to obtain the prescribed format of the application form free of cost.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is self-attestation required for attaching documents with the application?
content.text: Yes, applicants need to self-attest the copies of mandatory documents while submitting the application.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can an applicant request a receipt or acknowledgement for the submitted application?
content.text: The applicant should request a receipt or acknowledgement from the concerned authority to whom the application form has been submitted. The receipt should contain essential details such as the date, time, and a unique identification number if applicable.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are there any specific locations where the application forms are available, or is it only at the main office?
content.text: The application forms can be obtained from the Office of the Puducherry Unorganised Labourer's Welfare Society, No.1, Rue Sufferen, Puducherry, where the concerned authority is exclusively entrusted to issue and collect filled-in applications.

content.section_title: FAQ: What steps should an interested construction worker follow to apply for the scheme?
content.text: Interested construction workers should visit the Office of the Puducherry Unorganised Labourer's Welfare Society, obtain the application form, fill in mandatory fields, attach required documents, and submit the completed form to the concerned authority.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can applicants track the status of their application for the scheme?
content.text: No, but the applicants can request a receipt or acknowledgement from the concerned authority as a form of confirmation upon submission.

title: Medical Expenses-Treatment for Major Ailments Including Surgery
topic: Medical Expenses-Treatment for Major Ailments Including Surgery

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: "Medical Expenses-Treatment for Major Ailments Including Surgery'' scheme by the BOCW Welfare Board, Labour Dept.,West Bengal, is a welfare scheme that provides medical expenses for the treatment of beneficiary or his/ her dependents suffering from Cancer, Heart disease, Kidney disease, T.B. etc.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The "Medical Expenses-Treatment for Major Ailments Including Surgery'' scheme by the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Labour Department, West Bengal, is a welfare scheme for the registered workers of the board. Under this scheme, the Board shall provide financial assistance as medical expenses for the treatment of beneficiary or his/ her dependents suffering from Cancer, Heart disease, Kidney disease, T.B., Eye disease, Leprosy and Nerve disease. For the operation of diseases in case of Heart, Kidney etc. the Board may sanction a higher amount.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
- **Quantum of Assistance:** Upto a maximum amount of ₹20,000/- Per Annum.
**Note:** For the operation of diseases like Heart, Kidney etc. the Board may sanction a maximum amount of ₹60,000/-.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
> **For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:**

- The applicant should be a resident of West Bengal.
- The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker.
- The applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
- The applicant should have engaged in building and construction work for at least 90 days in a given year.

> **For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:**

- The applicant should be a registered member Under the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of West Bengal.
- The member or his/ her dependents should suffer from Cancer/ Heart disease/ Kidney disease/ T.B./ Eye disease/ Leprosy/ Nerve disease.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
> **Registration Process as a Building/ Construction Worker:**

**Step 1:** To register as a beneficiary under the BOCW Welfare Board, an applicant worker should request Form-27 from the Assistant Labour Commissioners/ Beneficiary Registering Officers and should attach all the mandatory documents (self-attested, if required).
**Step 2:** Application shall be submitted to the Assistant Labour Commissioners/ Beneficiary Registering Officers.
> **Application Process of the Welfare Scheme:**

**Step 1:** A registered worker should request Form-XI for the application from the Assistant Labour Commissioners/ Beneficiary Registering Officers and should attach all the mandatory documents (self-attested, if required).
**Step 2:** Application shall be submitted to the Assistant Labour Commissioners/ Beneficiary Registering Officers for this benefit.
> **Helpdesk:**

Labour Department
Department of Labour, Government of West Bengal,
12th Floor, N.S Building, Block-A,
1, Kiran Shankar Roy Road,
Kolkata-700001
Helpline of Labour Department, Govt. of West Bengal (Shramik Sathi): 1800-103-0009
> **Note:**

- Upon successful verification,  the construction worker is registered and an identity card and passbook are issued to him/ her. ₹20/- as the registration fee and ₹30/- as yearly subscription is taken. The amounts taken are noted in the passbook and a receipt is issued.
- After one year, the beneficiary's subscription is renewed upon payment of a renewal fee of ₹30/- and submission of an application in Form-27A.
- If not renewed the registration of the beneficiary is cancelled after the expiry of one year. Fresh application for renewal to be submitted to the ALC for consideration.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:- Passport size photographs of the worker (Four copies).
- Attested copy of Proof of Age.
For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:- Identity Card of the member.
- Dependent/ Heirship Certificate (In case the applicant is spouse/ nominee of the beneficiary).
- Medical Certificate.
- Copy of bills.
- Any other document if required.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the age requirement for registration as a beneficiary under the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of West Bengal?
content.text: The building worker must be at least 18 years old and not older than 60 years old.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there a minimum service requirement to register as a beneficiary?
content.text: Yes, the building worker must have completed 90 days of service as a building worker in the preceding 12 months.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can apply for the welfare scheme?
content.text: Any registered worker of the Board can apply for the welfare scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is responsible for registering a worker as a beneficiary?
content.text: At present all Assistant Labour Commissioners and Inspectors posted at blocks and municipalities shall be responsible for registering a worker as a beneficiary.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible for registration as a beneficiary of the BOCW Welfare Board?
content.text: Every building worker who is between the age of 18-60 years and who has been engaged in any building or other construction work for not less than 90 days during the preceding one year is eligible for registration as a beneficiary of the BOCW Welfare Board of West Bengal.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the procedure for applying for the registration?
content.text: To register as a beneficiary under the BOCW Welfare Board, an applicant worker should collect and submit the Form-27 with all the mandatory documents (self-attested, if required) and a fee of [?]20/- to the Assistant Labour Commissioners/ Beneficiary Registering Officers.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there a specific form for the registration as a beneficiary under the BOCW Welfare Board?
content.text: Yes, the application for registration must be made in Form-27. You can obtain this form from the Assistant Labour Commissioners/ Beneficiary Registering Officers in your area.

content.section_title: FAQ: What happens after a construction worker is successfully verified for registration?
content.text: Upon successful verification, the construction worker is registered, and an identity card and passbook are issued to him/her.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the fees associated with the registration process?
content.text: The registration fee is [?]20/-, and the yearly subscription fee is [?]30/-. These amounts are noted in the passbook, and a receipt is issued.

content.section_title: FAQ: How is the yearly subscription fee documented?
content.text: The yearly subscription fee of [?]30/- is recorded in the passbook, and a receipt is issued for the payment.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the process for renewing the subscription after one year?
content.text: After the expiry of one year, the subscription of the beneficiary is renewed upon payment of a renewal fee of [?]30/- and submission of an application made in Form-27A.

content.section_title: FAQ: What happens if the subscription is not renewed after one year?
content.text: If the subscription is not renewed, the registration of the beneficiary is cancelled after the expiry of one year.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can a beneficiary reapply for renewal after cancellation?
content.text: Yes, a fresh application for renewal can be submitted to the Assistant Labour Commissioner (ALC) for consideration.

content.section_title: FAQ: What criteria must an applicant meet to be considered for this benefit?
content.text: A registered member of the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of the West Bengal or his/ her dependents suffering from Cancer/ Heart disease/ Kidney disease/ T.B./ Eye disease/ Leprosy/ Nerve disease, shall be eligible for this benefit.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the maximum amount the Board may sanction for medical expenses annually?
content.text: The Board may sanction up to a maximum amount of [?]20,000 per annum for the treatment of a beneficiary or their dependents suffering from specific diseases.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can the Board sanction more amount of assistance for any medical treatment?
content.text: Yes, for operations related to Heart, Kidney etc, the Board may sanction a maximum amount of [?]60,000/-.

content.section_title: FAQ: What form needs to be used to apply for this welfare scheme?
content.text: The application must be made in the prescribed Form-XI. The form can be collected from Assistant Labour Commissioners/ Beneficiary Registering Officers in your area.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where do I submit the application for this welfare scheme?
content.text: Submit your welfare scheme application to the Assistant Labour Commissioners/ Beneficiary Registering Officers in your area.

title: Medical Expenses for Treatment of Major Ailments of a Beneficiary
topic: Medical Expenses for Treatment of Major Ailments of a Beneficiary

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The Manipur Building and Other Construction Workers' Welfare Board provides financial support for the treatment of serious illnesses such as Tuberculosis, Cancer, Heart Disease, Kidney Disease, and more, for building workers and their family members.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
The Manipur Building and Other Construction Workers' Welfare Board launched a scheme "Medical Expenses for Treatment of Major Ailments of a Beneficiary". The scheme aims to provide financial support for the treatment of serious illnesses such as Tuberculosis, Cancer, Heart Disease, Kidney Disease, and more, for building workers and their family members.

***Major Ailment Cover:***

- Tuberculosis (T.B.)
- Cancer.
- Heart Disease.
- Kidney Disease.
- Eye Disease.
- Leprosy.
- HIV Disease.
- Nerve Disease.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
***Financial Assistance:***

- ₹2,000/- per year for minor treatments.

- Up to ₹45,000/- (each operation) for major surgeries.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The construction worker should be registered under the Manipur Building and Other Construction Workers' Welfare Board.
1. The construction worker's family members (spouse and children) are eligible for financial assistance under this scheme.
1. The construction worker or family members should suffer from T.B., Cancer, Heart Disease, Kidney Disease, Eye Disease, Leprosy, HIV Disease and Nerve Disease.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Step-1:** The interested applicant should visit (during office hours) the Manipur Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board and request a hard copy of the prescribed format of the application form from the staff exclusively entrusted to issue and collect filled-in applications.
**OR**
The interested applicant visits the [official website](http://manipurbcwb.in/) and downloads the [application form](http://manipurbcwb.in/files/forms/beneficiaryForm.pdf).
**Step-2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
**Step-3:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the Labour Board Inspector
**Step-4:** Request a receipt or acknowledgement from the Labour Board Inspector to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Certificate of Registration.
- Payment Receipt of the Last Contribution.
- Calculation Sheet for the medical expenses for ailment.
- Medical Certificate.
- Aadhaar Card of the Beneficiary.
- First Page of Bank Passbook.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible for the scheme?
content.text: Registered building workers and their families (spouse and children) suffering from specific major illnesses are eligible.

content.section_title: FAQ: What diseases are covered under this scheme?
content.text: The scheme covers TB, Cancer, Heart Disease, Kidney Disease, Eye Disease, Leprosy, HIV, and Nerve Disease.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can family members of the worker avail of this scheme?
content.text: Yes, the worker's spouse and children are eligible for medical assistance under the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any limitation on the number of times assistance can be availed?
content.text: Minor treatment assistance is annual, but major surgeries are covered per operation.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can retired workers avail of the scheme?
content.text: Only actively registered workers and their families can avail of this scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are private hospital treatments covered?
content.text: Treatment in both government and recognized private hospitals is eligible, provided proper documentation is submitted.

content.section_title: FAQ: What documents are needed for the application?
content.text: A copy of the labour card, medical reports, and bank account details are required.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can one apply for this scheme?
content.text: Applications can be submitted to the Manipur Building and Other Construction Workers' Welfare Board with necessary documents.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the maximum financial assistance for major surgeries?
content.text: Up to [?]45,000 is provided for major surgeries like heart or kidney operations.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is the assistance available annually?
content.text: Yes, [?]2,000 is available per year for minor treatments.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: In the scheme "Mentally Retarded Children Benefit" by the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, an amount of ₹20,000/- per annum is provided to the registered workers to take care of their mentally retarded/disabled children.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: In the scheme "Mentally Retarded Children Benefit" by the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Government of Himachal Pradesh, an amount of ₹20,000/- per annum is provided to the registered workers to take care of their mentally retarded/disabled children.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: ₹20,000/- per annum

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
***For Registration***

1. The applicant should be a resident of Himachal Pradesh.
1. The applicant should be a Building or Construction Worker.
1. The age of the applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
1. The applicant should have worked in building or construction activities for at least 90 days in the preceding 12 months.

_*The registration should be renewed annually._
***For Application***

1. The worker should be engaged in building or construction work under the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
1. The worker should be registered with the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
1. The worker's membership with the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board must be active.
1. The child of the worker should be mentally retarded/disabled and dependent on the registered worker.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Registration**
**Step 1:** Take the print of the [prescribed format](https://bocw.hp.nic.in/pdf/formno27_A1b.pdf) of the registration form.
**Step 2:** **** In the form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste a passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attested, if required).
**Step 3:** **** Submit the duly filled and signed registration form to the Labour Officer.
_* A one-time registration fee must be paid at the office, and it will be valid for three years._
_* After three years, the worker must visit the office to pay a renewal fee and submit proof of 90 days of work each year to maintain eligibility for benefits._
**Application**
**Step 1:** Take the print of the [prescribed format](https://bocw.hp.nic.in/pdf/pension3539_A1b.pdf) of the application form.
**Step 2:** **** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste a passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attested, if required).
**Step 3:** **** Submit the duly filled and signed application form to the Labour Officer.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
**Registration**
**Step 1:** **** Visit the [official website](https://bocw.hp.nic.in/index.aspx) of the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
**Step 2:** **** In the "Menu Ribbon", click "Worker Services > Registration > [New](https://bocw.hp.nic.in/PublicPages/AddBeneficiary.aspx)".
**Step 3:** **** Navigate to the registration form. In the form, fill in all the mandatory fields (marked with an asterisk *). Carefully review all the information provided. Make any necessary corrections.
**Step 5:** Click "Save" to submit your application for registration.
*The [Registration Status](https://bocw.hp.nic.in/PublicPages/checkstatus.aspx) can be checked by providing your Beneficiary ID and your Date of Birth.
**Application**
**Step 1:** **** Visit the [official website](https://bocw.hp.nic.in/index.aspx) of the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
**Step 2:** **** In the "Menu Ribbon", click "Worker Services > [Apply for Benefits](https://bocw.hp.nic.in/PublicPages/ApplyForSchemes.aspx)". On the next screen, provide your Beneficiary ID, and Date Of Birth, and click "Search". A list of schemes will be displayed. Click on the scheme you intend to apply for. You will be taken to the online application form of the scheme.
**Step 3:** **** In the form, fill in all the mandatory fields and upload all the mandatory documents in the specified format and size. Carefully review all the information provided and the documents uploaded. Make any necessary corrections.
**Step 4:** Finally, click "Submit" to complete the process.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Identity Proof I.e. Aadhaar Card, Voter Card
- Bank Account Details/ Copy of Bank Passbook
- Labour Card
- ID Proof of the Worker Issued by the Board

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the Mentally Retarded Children Benefit Scheme?
content.text: The scheme, introduced by the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (HPBOCWWB), provides annual financial assistance of [?]20,000 to support the care of mentally retarded or disabled children of registered construction workers.

content.section_title: FAQ: How much financial assistance is provided under this scheme?
content.text: A financial aid of [?]20,000 per year is provided for the care and well-being of the mentally retarded or disabled child.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can both parents apply for assistance separately?
content.text: No, the benefit is provided per eligible child and only one parent can apply for assistance under the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can a worker register with HPBOCWWB?
content.text:
A worker can register by:
Visiting the Labour Welfare Office in their jurisdiction.
Self-registering on the official portal of HPBOCWWB.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the eligibility criteria for HPBOCWWB registration?
content.text:
To register, a worker must:
Be between 18 and 60 years old.
Have completed at least 90 days of construction work.
Be a resident of Himachal Pradesh.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can a worker apply if they have more than one mentally retarded or disabled child?
content.text: Yes, if a registered worker has more than one eligible child, they can apply separately for each child.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there a deadline for applying for the benefit?
content.text: There is no specific deadline, but the worker must have an active membership at the time of application. It is advisable to check with HPBOCWWB for any updates.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can the financial assistance be used?
content.text: The financial assistance is meant for the care and well-being of the mentally retarded or disabled child, including medical, educational, and other necessary expenses.

content.section_title: FAQ: How is the amount disbursed to the beneficiary?
content.text: The financial assistance is directly transferred to the bank account of the registered worker.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can a retired worker apply for the scheme?
content.text: No, only active registered workers under HPBOCWWB are eligible for the scheme.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The "Mentally Retarded Children Benefit Scheme'' by the BOCW Welfare Board, Department of Labour, Punjab, is a welfare scheme that provides financial assistance for the care of mentally retarded children (son/daughter) of construction workers.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The "Mentally Retarded Children Benefit Scheme'' by the BOCW Welfare Board, Department of Labour, Punjab, is a welfare scheme for the registered workers of the board. Under this scheme, the Board shall provide financial assistance for the care of mentally retarded children (son/ daughter) of construction workers.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: - **Financial Coverage:** ₹20,000/- Per Year.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
> ***For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:***

- The applicant should be a resident of Punjab.
- The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker (Unorganised Worker).
- The applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
- The applicant should have engaged in building and construction work for at least 90 days in a given year.
- The applicant should have a monthly income of ₹15,000/- or below.
- The applicant should not be a member of any other Welfare Fund established under any law.
- The applicant should not have membership of the Employees' Provident Fund (EPF)/ National Pension System (NPS)/ Employees' State Insurance Corporation (ESIC).
- The applicant should not be an income tax payer.

> ***For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:***

- The applicant should be a registered member Under the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of Punjab.
- The member should be depositing his contribution to the Board regularly.
- The child of the member should be mentally retarded or disabled.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online - via CSC)
content.text:
> **Registration Process as a Building/ Construction Worker:**

- For new registration of workers please approach the nearby Sewa Kendra or Registering officers/ Labour Inspector.

> ***Application Process of the Welfare Scheme:***

- To apply for benefits of the concerned welfare scheme, please approach to nearby Sewa Kendra or Registering Officers/ Labour Inspector of your area.

> ***Note:***

- **Registration with the Board:** A construction worker becomes a member of the board by filling out Application Form No 28 along with depositing registration fees of ₹ 25/- only (once in life) and ₹ 10/- per month as contribution fees. A worker at a time can get himself registered for a minimum period of 1 year & maximum period of 5 years. The registered worker is called 'Beneficiary' of the Board.
> **Important Links:**

- **Check Your Registration Application Status:** Registered workers can check their registration application status through visiting this [link ](http://bocw.punjab.gov.in/Login.aspx?Id=1)upon entering their Registration Number.  - **Download Registration Card:** Registered workers can download their registration Registration Card through visiting this [link ](https://bocw.punjab.gov.in/Login.aspx?Id=1)upon entering their Registration Number.  - **Check your Scheme Application Status:** Registered workers can check their scheme application status through visiting this [link ](https://bocw.punjab.gov.in/Login.aspx?Id=1)upon entering their Registration Number.  - **CSC Locator:** Visit this [link ](https://www.apnacsconline.in/csc-locator/punjab/)to search nearest Common Service Center (CSC) of your area.  - **List of Sewa** **Kendra:** Visit this [link ](https://punjab.gov.in/documents/10191/1112286/Final+List-V1.0.pdf/6ab437f3-f04c-427d-a2b7-dd49515f075b)to see the list of Sewa Kendra.
> **Helpdesk:**

> Punjab Building And Other Construction Workers Welfare Board

> **Address:** Labour Bhawan Model Welfare Centre Phase 10, Sector 64, Sahibzada Ajit Singh Nagar, Punjab, 160062

> **Phone :** +91 172-2540125

> **Email :** bocwhq@gmail.com

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
> ***Registration Process as a Building/ Construction Worker:***

**Step 1:** To register as a beneficiary under the BOCW Welfare Board, an applicant worker should download [Form No-28](http://bocw.punjab.gov.in/userfiles/file/Registration%20Form%2028%20updated.pdf) and attach all the mandatory documents (self attested, if required).
**Step 2:** Application shall be submitted to the Labour Inspector of your area.
> ***Application Process of the Welfare Scheme:***

**Step 1:** A registered worker should download [Form No-48](https://bocw.punjab.gov.in/userfiles/file/Disabiled%20Children%20Scheme%20Performa.pdf) and attach all the mandatory documents (self attested, if required).
**Step 2:** Application shall be submitted to the Labour Inspectors of your area.
> ***Note:***

- **Registration with the Board:** A construction worker becomes a member of the board by filling out Application Form No 28 along with depositing registration fees of ₹ 25/- only (once in life) and ₹ 10/- per month as contribution fees. A worker at a time can get himself registered for a minimum period of 1 year & maximum period of 5 years. The registered worker is called 'Beneficiary' of the Board.
> **Important Links:**

- **Check Your Registration Application Status:** Registered workers can check their registration application status through visiting this [link ](http://bocw.punjab.gov.in/Login.aspx?Id=1)upon entering their Registration Number.  - **Download Registration Card:** Registered workers can download their registration Registration Card through visiting this [link ](https://bocw.punjab.gov.in/Login.aspx?Id=1)upon entering their Registration Number.  - **Check your Scheme Application Status:** Registered workers can check their scheme application status through visiting this [link ](https://bocw.punjab.gov.in/Login.aspx?Id=1)upon entering their Registration Number.  - **CSC Locator:** Visit this [link ](https://www.apnacsconline.in/csc-locator/punjab/)to search nearest Common Service Center (CSC) of your area.  - **List of Sewa** **Kendra:** Visit this [link ](https://punjab.gov.in/documents/10191/1112286/Final+List-V1.0.pdf/6ab437f3-f04c-427d-a2b7-dd49515f075b)to see the list of Sewa Kendra.

> (09:00 AM to 05:00 PM)

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:- Age proof (If no age proof is available self declaration).
- Residence proof.
- Aadhaar card(if available).
- Bank Account details (Bank branch, account No. and IFSC/ RTGS number).
- Detail of dependents.
- Employer’s Certificate (Form No. 28) (90 days of construction work during the last 12 months in the State of Punjab).
- Nomination Form (Form No. 27).
For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:- Aadhaar card of Mentally retarded/ disabled Child.
- Medical Certificate issued by Civil Surgeon Regarding Mentally retardness/ disability.
- Joint Photo of Mentally retarded/ disabled child with Beneficiary.
- Life Certificate of Mentally retarded/ disabled Child in the month of January Every Year. (Aadhaar based online life certificate through the Jeevan Pramaan Website: https://jeevanpramaan.gov.in/, which generates digital life certificate may be submitted).

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can register as a beneficiary under the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of Punjab?
content.text: Any construction worker whose age is between 18 to 60 years and has worked in Punjab State in any construction work for a minimum of 90 days in a year, are eligible to register as a beneficiary under the Board.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there a minimum service requirement to register as a beneficiary?
content.text: Yes, the building worker must have completed 90 days of service as a building worker in the preceding 12 months.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can apply for the welfare scheme?
content.text: Any registered worker of the Board can apply for the welfare scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: For how long a construction worker can get registered at a time?
content.text: A worker at a time can get himself registered for a minimum period of 1 year & maximum period of 5 years.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the procedure for applying for the registration?
content.text: Any construction worker who is of 18 years and below 60 years' age and has Worked in Punjab State for not less than 90 days shall apply for registration as construction worker on the prescribed Form No. 28 which is available on the Board's website or from the office of ALC/LCO and Labour Inspectors of the area.

content.section_title: FAQ: Whom to apply for the registration?
content.text: A construction worker who intends to get himself registered as a member of the Board shall apply to the Labour Inspector of his area.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the mandatory documents for the registration?
content.text: The documents required for registration are, age proof(If no age proof is available self declaration), residence proof, adhar card(if available), Bank Account details (Bank branch, account No. and IFSC/RTGS number), detail of dependents, and employer's certificate of working for 90 days as construction worker, and nomination form.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there a specific form for the registration through offline mode as a beneficiary under the BOCW Welfare Board?
content.text: Yes, the application for registration must be made in Form No. 28.

content.section_title: FAQ: How much is the fee associated with the registration process?
content.text: The rate of registration fee is [?]25/- at the time of registration. It is a one time fee.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the rate of contribution?
content.text: A registered worker is required to contribute [?]10/-per month as contribution fee to the Board.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who are the family members considered for the benefits?
content.text: Husband or wife, minor sons (including major sons who are insane or physically handicapped and unable to earn), unmarried daughters and the parents of the building worker, who are solely dependent on the registered worker.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible to apply for this scheme?
content.text: A mentally retarded or disabled child of a registered member of the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of Punjab, actively depositing contributions to the Board, shall be eligible for this benefit.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the financial assistance provided under this scheme?
content.text: The scheme offers financial assistance of [?]20,000/- per year for the care of mentally retarded children.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the conditions regarding the child’s disability?
content.text: The child must be mentally retarded or disabled to qualify for the benefits under this scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What form needs to be used to apply for this welfare scheme?
content.text: The application must be made in the prescribed Form No. 48 which can be downloaded from https://bocw.punjab.gov.in/userfiles/file/Disabiled%20Children%20Scheme%20Performa.pdf or can be collected from the Labour Inspectors of the concerned area.

content.section_title: FAQ: Whom to apply for getting benefits of welfare schemes?
content.text: For the application of the concerned welfare schemes of the Board, the construction worker has to visit the nearest Sewa Kendras or contact the Assistant Labour Commissioner/ Labour-cum-Conciliation Officer/ Labour Inspector/ Labour Enforcement Officer of the concerned area.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The purpose of the Award is to recognize and felicitate individual(s)/ organization(s) who have made a significant impact on society for a sustained period by way of promotion and development of Yoga

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
The Prime Minister’s Award for Yoga under the Ministry of Ayush, Government of India, aims to recognize and felicitate individuals and organizations that have made a significant and sustained contribution to the promotion and development of Yoga, creating a positive impact on society.
**Number of Awardees:**

- 2 Awards for Individuals (National/International)
- 2 Awards for Organizations/Institutions (National/International)

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: The winners will be felicitated by means of a Trophy, Certificate and a Cash Award. The value of each Cash Award would be ₹ 25 lakh.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant should have rich experience and a deep understanding of Yoga.
1. The applicant (individual category) must be at least 30 years of age.
1. The applicant should have a minimum of 10 years of service with an excellent track record in promoting and developing Yoga.

**Note:** The applicant can apply or be nominated for only one category (Individual or Institutional) in a particular year.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
**Registration:**
**Step 1:** Visit the o[fficial website](https://awards.gov.in/) and click on the[ Registration button.](https://awards.gov.in/Registration/SignUp)
**Step 2:** Select your category: Individual or Organization.
**Step 3:** Fill in the required details:
Individual: Name, Date of Birth, Nominator Type, Mobile, Email, Aadhaar Number.
Organization: Type, Authorized Person Name, Date of Birth, Organization Description, Designation, Mobile, Email, Aadhaar Number.
**Step 4:** Click Submit to complete registration.
**Apply:**
**Step 1:** [Log in](https://awards.gov.in/Login) to the portal using your registered credentials.
**Step 2:** Select the scheme from the list of awards and click "Nominate / Apply Now".
**Step 3:** Fill in all mandatory fields in the online application form.
**Step 4:** Click Submit to complete your application.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Completed application/nomination form
- Proof of age (for individual category)
- Documents showing experience in Yoga (minimum 10 years)
- Evidence of contributions and achievements in Yoga
- Recommendation/nomination letter (if applicable)
