content.section_title: FAQ: What is the Prime Minister’s Award for Yoga?
content.text: It is an award to recognize individuals and organizations for their significant contribution to the promotion and development of Yoga.

content.section_title: FAQ: Which ministry administers this award?
content.text: The award is administered by the Ministry of Ayush, Government of India.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible to apply for this award?
content.text: Individuals and organizations with significant experience and contribution in Yoga are eligible.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the minimum age required for individuals?
content.text: The applicant must be at least 30 years old.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can organizations apply for the award?
content.text: Yes, both individuals and institutions can apply under separate categories.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can an applicant apply for multiple categories?
content.text: No, an applicant can apply for only one category (Individual or Institutional) in a year.

content.section_title: FAQ: What does the award include?
content.text: The award includes a trophy, certificate, and a cash prize of ₹ 25 lakh.

content.section_title: FAQ: How many awards are given each year?
content.text: A total of 4 awards are given—2 for individuals and 2 for institutions.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are international applicants eligible?
content.text: Yes, both national and international applicants can be considered.

content.section_title: FAQ: What happens after screening?
content.text: The Selection Committee evaluates shortlisted candidates and makes final decisions.

content.section_title: FAQ: Does the award consider social impact?
content.text: Yes, impact on society and promotion of ethical and spiritual values are key factors.

title: Procedure for Making Medical Certificate (for Certifying Disability)
topic: Procedure for Making Medical Certificate (for Certifying Disability)

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The certificate aims to ensure that persons with disabilities can easily obtain authentic medical certification required to avail benefits under various social welfare schemes, including disability pensions, scholarships, healthcare facilities, and employment reservations.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The “Procedure for Making Medical Certificate” is implemented by the Department of Medical Health and Family Welfare, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to ensure that persons with disabilities can easily obtain authentic medical certification required to avail benefits under various social welfare schemes.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: This certificate is essential to verify a person’s disability and to enable them to avail the benefits of various social welfare schemes such as pensions, scholarships, and reservations in employment.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
1. The applicant must be a person with disabilities.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
To obtain a medical certificate, a person with disabilities must visit the district hospital on Wednesdays. They can collect a registration form there after paying a nominal fee. After registration, the individual is examined by the concerned doctors or specialists relevant to their type of disability.
The applicant must carry their Aadhaar card, mobile number, and a passport-size photograph. The Chief Medical Officer (CMO) is generally present at the district hospital every Wednesday and, based on the medical examination reports, issues the disability certificate to the concerned individual. If the certificate is not available immediately, it can later be collected from the office of the Chief Medical Officer.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Aadhaar Card
- Passport-size Photograph
- Mobile Number
- Any Previous Medical Reports or Disability-related Documents (if available)

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible to apply for the medical certificate?
content.text: A person with disabilities residing in Uttarakhand is eligible to apply for the disability medical certificate.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where can a person apply for the medical certificate?
content.text: Applicants must visit the district hospital in their respective districts to apply for the medical certificate.

content.section_title: FAQ: On which day of the week can one apply for the certificate?
content.text: The procedure is conducted every Wednesday at the district hospital under the supervision of the Chief Medical Officer (CMO).

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any fee for registration?
content.text: Yes, applicants must pay a nominal registration fee to obtain the registration form at the district hospital.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who examines the applicant for issuing the disability certificate?
content.text: The applicant is examined by a panel of doctors, including the concerned specialist and the Chief Medical Officer (CMO), who evaluates the disability and approves the certificate.

content.section_title: FAQ: How long does it take to receive the medical certificate?
content.text: In most cases, the certificate is issued on the same day after the examination. If not available immediately, it can be collected later from the CMO’s office.

title: National Livestock Mission: Establishment of State Semen Bank
topic: National Livestock Mission: Establishment of State Semen Bank

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: "Establishment of State Semen Bank (Genetic Improvement of Sheep and Goat breeds)" aims at the genetic improvement of sheep/goat breeds through selective breeding by propagation of superior male germplasm through artificial insemination.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
Launched in the Financial Year 2014-15, the scheme "National Livestock Mission" by the Department of Animal Husbandry & Dairying, Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying, aims towards employment generation, entrepreneurship development, increase in per animal productivity and thus targeting increased production of meat, goat milk, egg and wool. The excess production will help in the export earnings after meeting the domestic demands. The concept of the NLM Scheme is to develop the entrepreneur in order to create the forward and backward linkage for the produce available in the unorganized sector and to link with the organized sector.

"Establishment of State Semen Bank (Genetic Improvement of Sheep and Goat breeds)" is a component of the "Sub-mission on Breed Development of Livestock and Poultry" scheme of the National Livestock Mission. The objective of this component is the genetic improvement of sheep/goat breeds through selective breeding by propagation of superior male germplasm through artificial insemination. For propagation of Artificial Insemination Technology for goats through frozen semen, there will be a requirement of storage depot at the state level for onward supply of goat frozen semen to the AI centres. The State Semen Bank working for storing cattle and buffalo frozen semen can work as the semen bank for goats also. Therefore, one time assistance will be provided for strengthening the existing state semen bank for goat frozen semen storing by providing equipment and storing containers.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
1. One-time assistance of up to ₹ 10,00,000/- will be provided to the state for strengthening of existing cattle and buffalo semen bank to store and distribute goat frozen semen.
1. The assistance will be provided for procurement of equipment like cryo-container and other related equipment.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
**The following entities are eligible -**

- State Livestock Agencies.
- Animal Husbandry Department, State Government.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
**Step 1:** Visit the [Official Website](https://nlm.udyamimitra.in/) of "National Livestock Mission" and click "[Apply Here](https://nlm.udyamimitra.in/Login/Login)".
**Step 2:** The following methods of Logging In will be displayed:

- Login as Entrepreneur
- Login as Government / Other Agencies
- Login as Research & Innovation

Login to the website using your preferred method.
**Step 3:** On the next page, verify your Mobile Number via OTP. You will be taken to the Online Application Form.
**Step 4:** In the form, fill in all the mandatory fields and upload all the mandatory documents in the specified format and size.
**Step 5:** Agree to the "Declaration & Authorization" by ticking the checkbox. Provide the Date & Place, and finally click "Submit".
> **Post-Application Processes**

**Step 1:** **Screening of application by State Implementing Agency (SIA)**
SIA will scrutinize the submitted application and will recommend the application of the entrepreneurs/ eligible entities to avail balance financing for the project through scheduled banks or financial institutions like National Cooperative Development Corporation (NCDC) etc.
**Step 2:** **Loan sanction by Lender**
After the recommendation of the project by SIA, the same will be considered by the bank or financial institutions for financing. Lender will pick the application along with all the documents from the portal and will approve the loan after examining the project/ documents submitted by applicant and completing their internal deligent process. After sanctioning the loan, Lender will upload sanction letter on the portal.
**Step 3:** **Recommendation from State Level Executive Committee (SLEC)**
Once the bank or financial institution sanction the project, SIA shall place the same in SLEC for recommending the application to the Central Government (Department of Animal Husbandry & Dairying).
**Step 4:** **Approval of subsidy by DAHD**
DAHD will approve the subsidy and will mark the approval of the same on portal.
**Step 5:** **Disbursement and release of subsidy**
After compliance of terms and conditions mentioned in the sanction letter, lender will disburse the loan amount to the beneficiary. fund the subsidy amount for the approved projects through Small Industries Development Bank of India (SIDBI) to the lending Bank or financial institutions as the case may be to release the subsidy to Beneficiaries.
**Track Application Status**
You can check your application status from the "TRACK STATUS" tab available in the upper right corner of the portal
**Help and Support**
If you encounter any issues or have questions, visit the "[Contact Us](https://nlm.udyamimitra.in/Home/Contact)" page.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
A. Documents Related to Project
- Detailed project report (DPR)* including cost of project, means of finance, recurring cost, net income etc.
- Land document (Ownership / Lease deed/ Rent Agreement etc.)
- Photographs of project site
- Documentary proof of applicant’s share in the project
- List of farmers linked/attached with applicant comprising Name, Aadhar Number, Mobile No. and Address
B. Documents Related to Applicant
- PAN card*
- GST registration certificate
- Certificate of Incorporation (In case of company)
- Partnership Deed (In case of partnership firm)
- Address Proof* (Election Commission Photo ID card, Electricity Bill, Water Bill, Telephone Bill, Passbook, rent agreement etc.)
- Last three years audited Annual financial statements, if applicable
- Last three years income tax returns, if applicable
- Bank statement for last six months
- Canceled cheque along with bank mandate form*
C. Documents Related to Key Promoter
- PAN Card*
- Aadhar Card*
- Address Proof * (Election Commission Photo ID card, Electricity Bill, Water Bill, Telephone Bill, Passbook, rent agreement etc.)
- Photograph*
- Caste certificate, if applicable
- Education certificates
- Training certificates
- Experience letter/ certificate about any livestock farming activities done earlier

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the objective of this scheme?
content.text: The objective is to genetically improve sheep and goat breeds through selective breeding by propagating superior male germplasm via artificial insemination.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible to apply for this scheme?
content.text: Eligible entities include State Livestock Agencies and the Animal Husbandry Department of State Governments.

content.section_title: FAQ: What financial assistance is provided under this scheme?
content.text: The scheme provides one-time assistance of up to Rs. 10 lakh for strengthening existing cattle and buffalo semen banks to store and distribute goat frozen semen. This assistance is 100% centrally funded.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the key components of the project?
content.text: Key components include strengthening the existing state semen bank by providing equipment like cryocontainers and other related equipment for storing goat frozen semen.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the funding pattern for this scheme?
content.text: The funding pattern is 100% central assistance for all States and Union Territories.

content.section_title: FAQ: What documents are required to apply for this scheme?
content.text: Required documents include the application form, project proposal, identity proof, address proof, entity registration documents, bank account details, land ownership/lease documents (if applicable), technical specifications, detailed cost estimates, approval letters, environmental clearances (if applicable), a declaration form, and an Expression of Interest (EOI) from the state.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the process for disbursement of funds?
content.text: Funds are disbursed in a one-time assistance manner, released to the scheduled bank or financial institution upon approval by the State Implementing Agency.

content.section_title: FAQ: What equipment can be procured under this scheme?
content.text: Equipment that can be procured includes cryo-containers and other related equipment necessary for the storage and distribution of goat frozen semen.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can this scheme be used for setting up new semen banks?
content.text: No, this scheme is intended for strengthening existing cattle and buffalo semen banks to store and distribute goat frozen semen.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is recurring expenditure covered under this scheme?
content.text: No, the scheme does not cover recurring expenditure for running the semen bank. The bank is expected to generate its own resources for operational expenses.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the responsibilities of the State Implementing Agency?
content.text: The State Implementing Agency is responsible for verifying the submitted documents, assessing project feasibility, disbursing funds, and conducting follow-up verification after project completion.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where can I get more information about the scheme?
content.text: For more information, contact the State Implementing Agency or the Department of Animal Husbandry and Dairying (DAHD), Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry, and Dairying (MoFAHD), Government of India.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the purpose of the Expression of Interest (EOI)?
content.text: The EOI from the state indicates their intent and readiness to strengthen the existing semen bank for storing and distributing goat frozen semen.

content.section_title: FAQ: What happens after the project is completed?
content.text: After project completion, a final verification will be conducted to ensure adherence to the guidelines and successful strengthening of the state semen bank.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: This award is to motivate young persons to achieve excellence in the field of national development or social service and to develop in them a sense of responsibility to the community and thus to improve their potential as good citizens.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
The National Youth Award was sponsored by The  Ministry of Youth Affairs for the Indian youth for their tremendous and outstanding work in social service. This award is to motivate young persons to achieve excellence in the field of national development or social service and to develop in them a sense of responsibility to the community and thus to improve their own personal potential as good citizens. The award shall be given for identifiable excellent work for youth in different fields of development activities and social services such as health, research and innovation, culture, promotion of human rights, art and literature, tourism, traditional medicine, active citizenship, community service, sports and academic excellence and smart learning.

> **Objectives of the award:**

- To motivate young persons (aged between 15 – 29 years) a challenge to achieve excellence in the field of national or social service.
- To encourage young people to develop a sense of responsibility to their community and thus to improve their own personal potential as good citizens.
- To give recognition to the outstanding work done by young persons for national development and / or social service.
- To give recognition to the outstanding work done by voluntary organizations working with the youth for national development and / or social service.

> **Ceremony Details:**

- Presentation of the award shall be made at a special ceremony on a convenient date and time to be announced every year.

> **Selection Process:**

-  In the initial stage, the Screening Committee under the Chairmanship of the  Joint Secretary, Department of Youth Affairs will screen all the applications and propose the names of the awardees thrice the number of awards to be given.
- The Central Selection Committee headed by the Secretary, Department of Youth Affairs, Government of India will scrutinize all the recommendations of the Screening Committee and make the final selection of the Youth Awards. The Central Selection Committee will also have powers to consider any other case based on the merits.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
> **Individual Awards:**

- Receive a medal, certificate, and ₹1,00,000/-.

> **Voluntary Youth Organization Awards:**

- Receive a medal, certificate, and ₹3,00,000/-

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
> **For Individuals:**

1. The applicant should be a citizen of India.
1. The applicant should be Between the age of 15- 29 years as of the first January of the nominated year.
1. The applicant should have done extraordinarily identifiable social work.
1. The applicant who Persons employed in any government service i.e. with the Central/State Governments, PSUs/Universities, Colleges/schools, etc. is not eligible for the award.
1. The award will be conferred once in a lifetime.

> **For Voluntary Organizations:**

1. The Organizations Should be registered under the Registration of Societies Act, 1860 (Act XXII of 1860) for the last three years or under the corresponding State Act and have a proper constitution or Articles of Association.
1. The organization should be a non-profit organization.
1. The organization Should not have discriminated against any person or group of persons on the grounds of sex, religion, caste, or creed;
1. The organization Has rendered extra-ordinary service involving the Youth in the relevant field for at least three years;
1. The organization Has a good reputation among the local community/area
1. The organization Should not have been conferred with this award earlier.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
**Registration:**
> **Step-1:** Visit the [official website](https://awards.gov.in/).

> **Step-2:** Click on the [Registration ](https://awards.gov.in/Registration/SignUp)button.

> **Step-3:** Choose type Individual or  Organization.

> **Step-4.1:** Under Individual category provide the following details.

>                - Name as per Aadhaar/Valid Identity Proof.

>                - Date of Birth.

>                - Nominator/Applicant Type.

>                - Mobile No.

>                - Email ID.

>                - Aadhaar Number.

> **Step-4.2:** Under Organization category provide the following details.

>                - Type of Organization.

>                - Name of the Authorized Person as per Aadhaar/Valid Identity Proof

>                - Date of Birth.

>                - Brief Description about the Organization (100 Words)

>                - Designation

>                - Mobile No.

>                - Email ID.

>                - Aadhaar Number.

> **Step-5:** Submit.

**Apply:**
> **Step-1:** Visit the [official website](https://awards.gov.in/).

> **Step-2:** Click on l[ogin button on](https://awards.gov.in/Login) portal and login using the credentials.

> **Step-3:** From the list of awards, select scheme name, then click "Nominate / Apply Now".

> **Step-4:** On the next page, fill in all the mandatory fields in the online application form, and then click "Submit".

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Proof of Date of birth (DOB)
- Supporting documents for outstanding work.
- Recommendation letter.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the National Youth Award?
content.text: The National Youth Award is a prestigious honor in India, recognizing outstanding work and achievements in the field of youth development and social service.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who organizes the National Youth Award?
content.text: The award is organized by the Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports, Government of India, to acknowledge and celebrate the contributions of young individuals and organizations.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the eligibility criterion for the National Youth Award?
content.text: Eligibility criteria include being an Indian citizen aged between 15 to 29 years, having made significant contributions to community service and nation-building.

content.section_title: FAQ: In which categories are the National Youth Awards conferred?
content.text: The awards are given in various categories, including social service, art and culture, education, health, sports, and promotion of human rights.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can one apply or be nominated for the National Youth Award?
content.text: Individuals can apply or be nominated through a formal process outlined by the Ministry, usually involving submission of achievements, recommendations, and supporting documents.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any monetary reward associated with the National Youth Award?
content.text: Yes, along with the honor, recipients of the National Youth Award receive a medal, a certificate, and a cash prize as a token of appreciation for their exemplary work.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can organizations or institutions be honored with the National Youth Award?
content.text: Yes, organizations and institutions that have made substantial contributions to youth development can be recognized in specific categories of the National Youth Award.

content.section_title: FAQ: What benefits are provided to the Individual ?
content.text: Receive a medal, certificate, and [?]1,00,000/-

content.section_title: FAQ: What benefits are provided to the Voluntary Youth Organization?
content.text: Receive a medal, certificate, and [?]3,00,000/-

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: This scheme is a government initiative to provide nutrition and healthcare support to pregnant and lactating mothers in tribal districts of Gujarat.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The "Poshan Sudha" scheme launched by the Women and Child Development Department, Gujarat, aimed at providing nutritional support to pregnant and lactating mothers in Gujarat’s tribal districts. This initiative provides registered mothers with one nutritious meal daily, iron and calcium supplements, and health education to improve maternal and child health outcomes.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: - **Nutritional Support:** Provision of one full nutritious meal daily for pregnant and lactating mothers. Supply of iron and calcium supplements to address nutritional deficiencies.  - **Health Education:** Awareness sessions on health and nutrition for better maternal and child care.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
- The applicant must be a resident of Gujarat.

- The applicant must be a pregnant or lactating woman residing in one of the tribal districts of Gujarat.
- The applicant must be registered in a local Anganwadi center.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Step 1:** Visit the nearest Anganwadi center.
**Step 2:** Register yourself with the Anganwadi center.
**Step 3:** Provide the required documents.
**Step 4:** Avail of the benefits during scheduled distribution days.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Proof of identity (e.g., Aadhaar Card).
- Proof of residence.
- Medical certificate of being pregnant.
- Any other document (if required).

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the "Poshan Sudha Yojna"?
content.text: Poshan Sudha Yojna is a government scheme aimed at providing nutritional support to pregnant and lactating mothers in Gujarat's tribal districts.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible for the scheme?
content.text: Pregnant and lactating women residing in tribal districts and registered with an Anganwadi center are eligible.

content.section_title: FAQ: What benefits are provided under the scheme?
content.text: Beneficiaries receive one nutritious meal daily, iron and calcium supplements, and health education.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can one apply for the scheme?
content.text: Women can apply by visiting the nearest Anganwadi center and completing the registration process.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is the scheme free of cost?
content.text: Yes, the scheme is free of cost for eligible beneficiaries.

content.section_title: FAQ: How are the meals provided?
content.text: Meals are served at local Anganwadi centers.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are there any financial benefits provided?
content.text: No direct financial assistance is provided; the scheme focuses on nutritional and healthcare support.

content.section_title: FAQ: How often are the meals provided?
content.text: Meals are provided daily during the operational hours of Anganwadi centers.

content.section_title: FAQ: What impact has the scheme had so far?
content.text: The scheme has shown a reduction in low birth weight cases, improved maternal and child nutrition, and decreased mortality rates.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: Pradhan Mantri Bhartiya Janaushadhi Pariyojana was launched by the Department of Pharmaceuticals, in November 2008. Under the scheme, dedicated outlets known as Janaushadhi Kendras are opened to make quality generic medicines available to all at affordable prices.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
The scheme Pradhan Mantri Bhartiya Janaushadhi Pariyojana (PMBJP) was launched by the Department of Pharmaceuticals, Ministry of Chemicals & Fertilizers, in November 2008. Branded medicines are sold at significantly higher prices than their unbranded generic equivalents, though both are identical in therapeutic value. Given the widespread poverty across the country, making reasonably priced quality generic medicines available in the market benefits all sections of society.
**Objectives:**
To make quality generic medicines available at affordable prices to all, PMBJP was launched by the Government of India in November 2008. Under the scheme, dedicated outlets known as Janaushadhi Kendras are opened to provide generic medicines at affordable prices. As of March 2026, more than 19,000 Kendras are functional across the country. The product basket comprises 2,100 medicines and 300 surgical items. The scheme is implemented by the Pharmaceuticals & Medical Devices Bureau of India (PMBI) [erstwhile BPPI], registered under the Societies Registration Act.
**Objectives include:**

- Ensuring access to quality medicines for all, especially the poor and deprived.
- Creating awareness about generic medicines through education and publicity to counter the perception that quality is linked only to high prices.
- Generating employment by engaging entrepreneurs in opening Kendras.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
> **The scheme is operated by government agencies as well as private entrepreneurs.**

**1**.	**Normal Incentive:**

- PMBJKs run by entrepreneurs/Pharmacists/Trusts/Societies & Charitable Organizations linked with PMBI headquarters through PoS software receive incentives.
- Incentive @ 20% of monthly purchase from PMBI, subject to a ceiling of Rs. 20,000 per month.
- Disbursed on a 50:50 basis:
- 10% incentive on purchase (up to Rs. 10,000).
- 10% incentive for maintaining stock of 200 medicines (up to Rs. 10,000).

**2**. **Special Incentive:**

- One-time grant of Rs. 2 lakh for women entrepreneurs, SC/ST, Divyangjan, ex-servicemen, and entrepreneurs in aspirational districts, Himalayan, island, and North-Eastern regions.
- ₹1.5 lakh reimbursement for furniture/fixtures.
- ₹0.5 lakh reimbursement for computer, internet, printer, etc.

**3**.	**Affordable Medicines:**

- Prices of Janaushadhi medicines are 50%–80% lower than branded medicines.

**4.**	**Quality Assurance:**

- Medicines procured only from WHO-GMP certified suppliers.

**5**.	**Testing:**

- Each batch is tested at NABL-accredited laboratories.

**6**.	**Margin:**

- Operating agencies receive a 20% margin on MRP (excluding taxes) of each drug.

> **Janaushadhi Suvidha Sanitary Napkin:**

Launched on 27 August 2019, Janaushadhi Suvidha Oxo-biodegradable sanitary napkins are available at Rs. 1 per pad.

- Distributed through all Kendras nationwide.
- Cumulative sales crossed 100 crore pads by March 2026.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
**Individuals:**

- The applicant must hold D. Pharma/B. Pharma degree or employ qualified pharmacists.

**Organizations/NGOs:**

- The applicant must employ B. Pharma/D. Pharma degree holders.

**Government hospital premises (including medical colleges):**

- Preference to reputed NGOs/charitable organizations though individuals are also eligible.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
##### **Step 1:** **** ***Accessing the Online Application Portal***

- The applicant must navigate to the official website of the Pharmaceuticals & Medical Devices Bureau of India to initiate the application process.
- The complete URL to access the official portal is [https://janaushadhi.gov.in](https://janaushadhi.gov.in).
##### **Step 2:** **** ***Filling the Application and Selecting Categories***

- The applicant must fill out all mandatory details accurately on the digital application form.
- During this step, the applicant must ensure they select the correct category (such as woman entrepreneur, SC, ST, Divyang, etc.) to avail special benefits, as this category selection cannot be altered in the future under any circumstances.

##### **Step 3:** **** ***Payment of the Application Fee***

- The applicant must pay a non-refundable application fee of ₹5,000/- along with the application form through the integrated online payment gateway.
- However, if the applicant belongs to special categories like women entrepreneurs, Divyang, SC, ST, ex-servicemen, or specific backward geographical regions, they must submit proof of their category to be exempted from this fee.

##### **Step 4:** **** ***Signing the Operating Agreement***

- Once the application is approved, the applicant must officially enter into an agreement with the PMBI before commencing any store operations.
- This agreement legally binds the applicant to follow all operational terms and conditions set forth by the agency.

##### **Step 5:** **** ***Securing Drug Licenses and System Setup***

- The applicant is required to secure a valid drug license from the local authorities strictly in the name of the Pradhan Mantri Bhartiya Janaushadhi Kendra.
- The applicant must also adopt PMBI’s billing software and provide the required bank cheques before receiving the initial dispatch of generic medicines and surgical items.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
At the Application Stage- Aadhaar Card (mandatory for individual or proprietor applications)
- PAN Card
- Darpan ID (only for NGO)
- Registration Certificate (for institutions, NGOs, or charitable institutes)
- Details of Department Who Has Allocated the Space along with Supporting Documents or Sanction Order (for Government-nominated Agencies)
- Certificate of SC/ST or Divyang (if applying for special incentive)
- Pharmacist Registration Certification
- ITR for Last Two Years (if nominated operating agency is a private entity)
- Bank Statement for Last Six Months
- Declaration for GST Registration Once Threshold Limit is Achieved
- Undertaking of Distance Policy as per Guideline
- Self-Undertaking of PMBJP Kendra for Availing One-Time Special Incentive (if applying under the category of woman entrepreneur, Divyang, SC, ST, ex-serviceman, aspirational district, Himalayan, island territories, or North-Eastern States)
At the Setup and Final Approval Stage- Proper Lease Agreement or Space Allotment Letter
- Proof of Securing a Pharmacist with Name and Registration with the State Council
- Drug License in the Name of Pradhan Mantri Bhartiya Janaushadhi Kendra
- Three Cheques Issued from Indian Nationalized Banks
- One Cancelled Cheque Issued from an Indian Nationalized Bank

content.section_title: FAQ: What happens if I wish to open a store right next to a large private hospital with over one hundred beds, considering there is usually a strict distance rule?
content.text: You are exempt from the 1km distance restriction if the location is within 500 meters of private hospitals having 100 or more beds, district government hospitals, or medical college hospitals.

content.section_title: FAQ: I am looking to lease a commercial property to set up my retail pharmacy business, but I need to know the exact minimum physical dimensions legally required to be eligible.
content.text: You must secure either an owned or hired space of at least 120 square feet, backed by a proper lease agreement or space allotment letter.

content.section_title: FAQ: As an individual applicant without a medical background, what are the specific educational requirements I need to fulfill regarding pharmacy degrees to be considered for opening a store?
content.text: You must either hold a Diploma in Pharmacy or a Bachelor of Pharmacy degree yourself, or you must employ someone who holds one of these specific degrees.

content.section_title: FAQ: When organizing the financial documents for my application, how many active bank instruments must I issue in favor of the implementing agency, and from what type of institution?
content.text: You are required to submit three cheques issued specifically from an Indian Nationalized Bank in favor of the Pharmaceuticals & Medical Devices Bureau of India to facilitate goods payments.

content.section_title: FAQ: If I intend to establish a dispensing unit inside the premises of a government hospital or medical college, will individual applicants or specific organizational entities be prioritized?
content.text: Reputed Non-Governmental Organizations and charitable organizations are preferred for these specific institutional locations, although individual applicants remain eligible to apply.

content.section_title: FAQ: Suppose I make an error while selecting my demographic category on the application form; will I have the opportunity to rectify or update this selection at a later stage?
content.text: Once you select a specific category during the initial application to claim suitable benefits, you are strictly prohibited from changing it in the future for any reason.

content.section_title: FAQ: Once the retail space is fully operational and approved, am I permitted to lease out a portion of the shop to another vendor or business owner to cover rent?
content.text: The allotted premises must be utilized solely for operating the mandated store, and you cannot transfer or sublet any part of the space directly or indirectly.

content.section_title: FAQ: Apart from the payment instruments required for supply transactions, what other specific banking stationery must be submitted to the overarching pharmaceutical bureau during the application process?
content.text: You must provide one cancelled cheque drawn from an Indian Nationalized Bank to the Pharmaceuticals & Medical Devices Bureau of India.

content.section_title: FAQ: At what exact stage of the evaluation process am I strictly required to submit the registration details and official name of the pharmacist I have hired for the shop?
content.text: You can submit the required proof of securing a pharmacist, including their name and registration with the State Council, either during the initial application or at the time of final approval.

content.section_title: FAQ: Before commencing the sale of medications to the public, whose responsibility is it to secure the necessary statutory permissions and what exact name must be on the license?
content.text: It is your sole responsibility to obtain the required drug license and other permissions explicitly in the name of the Pradhan Mantri Bhartiya Janaushadhi Kendra.

content.section_title: FAQ: In order to prevent unnecessary competition between similar retail outlets in the same district, what is the mandatory minimum geographic gap required between two approved medical stores?
content.text: A minimum distance of 1.00 Kilometer must be strictly maintained between two separate operating units when applying for a new location anywhere in the country.

content.section_title: FAQ: If I belong to a marginalized group or reside in a specified backward geographical area and wish to claim the associated financial incentives, what documentation must I attach?
content.text: You must submit suitable certificates or proof from respective authorities along with an undertaking if applying as a woman entrepreneur, Ex-Serviceman, Person with Disability, Scheduled Caste, Scheduled Tribe, or from specific regions.

content.section_title: FAQ: If our established charitable trust or society applies to manage a medical outlet, are we mandated to have qualified pharmaceutical staff on our payroll from the beginning?
content.text: Yes, trusts, societies, and charitable organizations must officially employ Diploma in Pharmacy or Bachelor of Pharmacy degree holders and provide evidence of their employment.

content.section_title: FAQ: Will the government bureau implementing this health initiative assist me in finding, securing, or arranging the physical real estate required to set up the dispensing facility?
content.text: No, the applicant is solely responsible for arranging the necessary physical space for establishing and operating the store; the implementing bureau has no role in this process.

title: PM Janjati Adivasi Nyaya Maha Abhiyan (PM JANMAN)
topic: PM Janjati Adivasi Nyaya Maha Abhiyan (PM JANMAN)

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to improve the socio-economic status of PVTGs by saturating families and habitations with basic facilities and services. It provides 11 critical infrastructure and welfare interventions, including housing, water, electricity, and connectivity.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The scheme “PM- Janjati Adivasi Nyaya Maha Abhiyan (PM JANMAN)” was launched by the Ministry of Tribal Affairs, Government of India, aims to improve the socio-economic status of Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Groups (PVTGs) by bridging gaps in health, education, and livelihoods, and by saturating PVTG communities and families with basic facilities and services. The scheme provides 11 critical interventions through nine line Ministries/Departments, focusing on ensuring access to pucca houses, safe drinking water, road and mobile connectivity, electricity, and vocational education for individual households and habitations. A unique aspect is the inter-ministerial convergence, with the Ministry of Tribal Affairs serving as the nodal ministry while each participating ministry implements its respective Central Sector and Centrally Sponsored schemes.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
##### ***Housing***

- Provision of pucca houses to all eligible Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Group households having kaccha houses (Target: 4,90,000 households).
- The basic unit cost of the house is enhanced to ₹2,00,000/-, with additional facilities raising it to ₹2,39,000/-.

##### ***Road Connectivity***

- Construction of connecting roads (Target: 8,000 KM) linking approximately 6,500 Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Group villages.
- The construction cost per Kilometer would be ₹1,00,00,000/-.

##### ***Drinking Water***

- Provision of piped water supply/Functional Household Tap Connection (FHTC) to all Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Group households.
- Community water supply will be provided to villages/habitations with a population of less than 20 households.

##### ***Health***

- Deployment of Mobile Medical Units (MMUs) (Target: 1,000 MMUs) in Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Group habitations/villages.
- The cost is ₹33,88,000/- per MMU per annum, with Operational Expenditure (Op Ex) as per norms.

##### ***Education Hostels***

- Construction of residential hostels (Target: 500 hostels) in existing schools in Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Group areas.
- The cost is ₹2,75,00,000/- per hostel construction, subject to the actual estimate as per existing cost sharing ratio.

##### ***Anganwadi Centers (AWCs)***

- Construction of Anganwadi Centers (Target: 2,500 AWCs) in Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Group habitations/villages.
- The unit cost is ₹12,00,000/- per Anganwadi Center.

##### ***Livelihood (VDVKs)***

- Setting up of Van Dhan Vikas Kendras (VDVKs) (Target: 500 VDVKs).
- An amount of ₹15,00,000/- meant for 300 members will be released on a pro rata basis.

##### ***Multipurpose Centers (MPCs)***

- Construction of Multipurpose Centers (Target: 1,000 MPCs).
- The unit cost is ₹60,00,000/- per unit.

##### ***Solar Power/Electricity***

- Provision of solar power solutions to 1,00,000 Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Group households.
- Funding is ₹50,000/- per household or actual cost for 300 Watt supply.

##### ***Telecom Connectivity***
Installation of mobile towers (Target: 3,000 mobile towers) in all uncovered villages/habitations.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
- The applicant must belong to a Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Group (PVTG) community.
- The applicant must reside in one of the 18 States and Union Territories having a notified Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Group population.
- The applicant household must be identified as eligible through the physical survey conducted by state governments using the mobile application on the PM Gati-shakti Portal.

***Eligibility Specific to Interventions***

- The applicant household seeking a pucca house must currently reside in a kaccha house.
- The applicant household seeking solar power solutions must be an unelectrified household not covered under the Revamped Distribution Sector Scheme (RDSS).
- The applicant household seeking an on-grid electricity connection shall be an identified beneficiary household under PM-JANMAN, irrespective of standard eligibility criteria under the Revamped Distribution Sector Scheme (RDSS) for the State and Electricity distribution company (DISCOM).

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Step 1:** **** ***Initiation of Identification and Physical Survey***

- The State Governments initiates a detailed physical survey of each Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Group habitation to ascertain the gaps regarding the 11 critical interventions covered under the mission.
- The data gathered during this exhaustive survey is simultaneously updated and cross-verified on a mobile application developed on the PM Gati-shakti Portal.

**Step 2:** **** ***Facilitation of Application Filing for Eligible Beneficiaries***

- The Block Level Implementation Team, consisting of officers designated by the District Collector, will be responsible for identifying each beneficiary family who has not yet been saturated with entitlements and will facilitate the necessary application filing for the respective schemes.
- The District Level Committee is also responsible for the registration and filling of applications of eligible beneficiaries for each scheme covered under the mission.

**Step 3:** **** ***Verification and Project Approval***

- Following the application filing and preliminary eligibility checks, certain proposals for infrastructure, such as the sanctioning of Multi-Purpose Centers, must be forwarded by the Secretary/Principal Secretary, State Tribal Welfare Department to the Ministry of Tribal Affairs.
- These proposals are subsequently examined by the relevant division, approved by the Mission Director of PM-JANMAN, and granted financial concurrence by the Internal Finance Division.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- PVTG/Tribal Status Certificate.
- Household Verification Report/Survey Data.
- Location Tagging Data.
- Completion Certificates.
- Photographs Of Constructed Pucca Houses.
- Aadhar Card.
- Ayushman Bharat Card.
- Forest Right Act (FRA) Pattas.

content.section_title: FAQ: If my habitation is seeking the installation and operation of a new Anganwadi Centre facility, what is the modified minimum population requirement for its establishment?
content.text: To facilitate the construction and running of an Anganwadi Centre, the existing population norms have been changed to require a minimum habitation population of 100 and above.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the core characteristic I must possess to be considered a target beneficiary for all the infrastructure and welfare improvements offered by this major mission?
content.text: You must strictly belong to one of the 75 identified communities categorized as a Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Group (PVTG).

content.section_title: FAQ: If I am applying for construction support to improve my residential situation, what specific condition must my current dwelling meet to qualify for a pucca house?
content.text: To be eligible for the provision of a pucca (permanent) house, your Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Group household must currently be residing in a kaccha (temporary) house.

content.section_title: FAQ: What essential personal identification documents or entitlements are facilitated or required for identified beneficiaries under this mission?
content.text: The Block Level Implementation Team is responsible for facilitating entitlements like Aadhar card, Ayushman Bharat card, and Forest Right Act (FRA) Pattas for eligible beneficiaries.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the extended timeline provided for Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Group households to receive pucca houses under the Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana-Gramin component of this mission?
content.text: Specifically for Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Groups (PVTGs), the Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana-Gramin (PMAY-G) housing scheme component has been extended until March 2026.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the minimum population threshold required in my area for the modified scheme guidelines to sanction and construct a connecting road under the infrastructure plan?
content.text: To qualify for connecting road construction under the modified scheme norms, your village or habitation must have a minimum population of 100 and above.

content.section_title: FAQ: I am an individual seeking vocational skill training; what size restriction applies to the Self-Help Group I must be a member of to participate in the livelihood enhancement activities?
content.text: If participating in the Van Dhan Vikas Kendra program, the flexibility norms specify that each Van Dhan Self-Help Group (VDSHG) must consist of between 10 and 20 members.

content.section_title: FAQ: If I belong to one of the identified traditional groups, what is the geographical requirement regarding my place of residence to qualify for this mission?
content.text: The applicant must reside within one of the 18 States and Union Territories where the population of Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Groups has been officially notified.

content.section_title: FAQ: I am seeking support for income generation activities; what organizational structure must my group belong to in order to establish a Van Dhan Vikas Kendra?
content.text: Your group should be established as a Van Dhan Vikas Kendra (VDVK). This Kendra must be composed of between five and fifteen Van Dhan Self-Help Groups (VDSHG).

content.section_title: FAQ: Regarding the provision of safe drinking water facilities, what specific household limit applies to villages that will receive community water supply instead of individual household connections?
content.text: The provision for a single point community water connection is intended for Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Group villages or habitations that have a population count of less than 20 households.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are there any special considerations for securing an electricity connection through the on-grid Revamped Distribution Sector Scheme if my household does not meet the standard state eligibility requirements?
content.text: Yes, all identified beneficiary households under this mission are eligible for funding for an on-grid electricity connection under the Revamped Distribution Sector Scheme (RDSS), irrespective of the standard eligibility criteria for the State and Electricity distribution company (DISCOM).

content.section_title: FAQ: In the event that my unelectrified house is considered for an off-grid solar power solution, is there any previous government scheme coverage that would disqualify me?
content.text: Yes, if your household has already been covered under the SAUBHAGYA scheme, you would be excluded from receiving the off-grid solar power solution intervention.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to train and create a professional cadre of geriatric caregivers to meet the increasing demand for specialized care for senior citizens. It offers skill development and certification in geriatric care job roles to Indian citizens, ensuring professional services for the elderly.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The scheme "PM-Special Training of Geriatric Caregivers" was launched by the Department of Social Justice & Empowerment, Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment, Govt. of India. The scheme aims to bridge the gap in supply and increasing demand in the field of geriatric caregivers, provide more professional services to senior citizens, and create a cadre of professional caregivers in geriatrics. The scheme provides training in four specific job roles: Geriatric Caregiver (Institutional & Home Care), Elderly Caretaker (Non-Clinical), Elderly Care Companion, and Wellness Therapist for Elderly, with an emphasis on physical, mental, and overall well-being of the elderly through a multi-disciplinary approach, including on-job training for specific courses. The scheme is implemented by the Department of Social Justice & Empowerment with the help of the National Institute of Social Defence. The applications for this scheme are accepted through registration on the Skill India Digital Hub (SIDH).

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
> ***Skill Training***

The scheme provides training to candidates in four specific job roles: Geriatric Caregiver (Institutional & Home Care) (480 hours), Elderly Caretaker (Non-Clinical) (360 hours), Elderly Care Companion (450 hours), and Wellness Therapist for Elderly (570 hours).
> ***On-Job Training (OJT)***

For job roles as mandated by the National Council for Vocational Education and Training, On-Job Training is provided, with its duration guided by the approved qualification pack.
> ***Assessment and Certification***

The skill assessment is conducted by empaneled Assessment Agencies, and a certificate is made available to candidates in physical and digital form, along with corresponding 'credits' as per the National Credit Framework of MSDE. The assessment fee is provided under the scheme.
> ***Re-assessment Opportunity***

Every candidate is offered one chance of re-assessment free of cost, to be borne from the scheme.
> ***Placement Support***

Apprenticeship is promoted, and training linked to captive placements (training and placement within the same industry) will be encouraged. Trainees are also encouraged to participate in Rozgar Melas for placement, with Training Institutes having tie-ups for proper placement.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
- The applicant must be a citizen of India.
- The applicant must meet the minimum age as specified in the respective job role.
- The applicant must be less than 40 years of age.
- The applicant must have the minimum education qualification as per the required job roles.
- The applicant must not indulge in any substance abuse.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
##### **Official Website**
[https://www.skillindiadigital.gov.in](https://www.skillindiadigital.gov.in)

**Step 1:** **** ***Account Registration***
Navigate to the Skill India Digital Hub (SIDH) at https://www.skillindiadigital.gov.in, and click "Register". Provide your mobile number and verify via OTP. Provide your email ID. Set a strong password of minimum 8 characters. Complete the basic registration form and proceed to profile creation.
**Step 2:** **** ***Profile Creation and e-KYC***
Fill in your complete personal details including full name, date of birth, gender, category, and current address. Enter your Aadhaar number and click "Verify Aadhaar" for automatic e-KYC. The system will auto-populate your basic details and send OTP to your Aadhaar-linked mobile number for final authentication. Ensure all information matches exactly with your Aadhaar card. Upload the required documents in the specified file format and file size.
**Step 3:** **** ***Course and Institute Selection***
Browse through available geriatric caregiver training courses. Select your preferred training course. Choose up to three empaneled training institutes in order of preference. The system will allocate seats based on availability and your preference order during final selection process.
**Step 4:** **** ***Application Review and Submission***
Carefully review all entered information, uploaded documents, and selected preferences in the application preview section before submission. Read and accept the terms and conditions, scheme guidelines, and declaration. Click "Submit Application" to generate unique Application Reference Number (ARN) and receive confirmation SMS and email.

##### ***Post-Application Processes***
**Step 1:** **** ***Verification and Selection***
The application verification (15-30 working days) by Project Appraisal Committee under Joint Secretary of DoSJE. Selected candidates receive training institute allocation, batch schedule, and joining instructions through registered communication channels.
**Step 2:** **** ***Training Enrollment and Support***
Report to assigned training institute as per schedule provided in selection notification with original documents for physical verification. Complete the free training program. Upon successful completion, receive government-recognized certification.

##### **Grievance Redressal**
**Email:** secretary-sje@nic.in
**Phone:** +91-11-23382065
**Address:** Shastri Bhavan, Dr. Rajendra Prasad Road, New Delhi - 110001

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Aadhaar Card
- Proof of Age
- Proof of Education
- Proof of Residence

content.section_title: FAQ: What types of training courses are available for aspiring geriatric caregivers and what are their respective durations?
content.text: Four distinct courses are offered: Geriatric Caregiver (Institutional & Home Care) for 480 hours, Elderly Caretaker (Non-Clinical) for 360 hours, Elderly Care Companion for 450 hours, and Wellness Therapist for Elderly for 570 hours. All courses focus on comprehensive elderly care with varying specializations.

content.section_title: FAQ: Do I need to maintain a minimum attendance percentage during the training period to be eligible for assessment?
content.text: Yes, you must maintain more than 70% biometric attendance throughout the entire training duration to qualify for the final assessment. This attendance requirement applies to all types of training courses offered under this program.

content.section_title: FAQ: What essential documents must I possess to prove my eligibility for enrollment in the training program?
content.text: You need valid documentary proof of your age, educational qualifications, and current residence address. Additionally, possession of a valid Aadhaar card is mandatory for registration and profile verification through the Skill India Digital Hub platform.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are there any age restrictions that I need to meet to be considered eligible for this training program?
content.text: You must meet the minimum age requirement specified for your chosen job role, but your age should not exceed 40 years. The exact minimum age varies depending on the specific course you wish to pursue.

content.section_title: FAQ: What educational qualifications are required for different job roles offered in the geriatric caregiver training program?
content.text: The minimum education qualification varies according to each specific job role you choose to pursue. You must verify that you meet the educational requirements specified for your selected course before applying for enrollment.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can I participate in this training program if I am not an Indian citizen or permanent resident?
content.text: This training program is exclusively available to all citizens of India only. You must be an Indian citizen to be eligible for enrollment in any of the four geriatric caregiver training courses offered.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any restriction regarding substance abuse that could affect my eligibility for the training program?
content.text: Yes, you should not be involved in any form of substance abuse to be eligible for this program. This is a mandatory requirement that ensures you can provide professional and reliable care services to elderly individuals.

content.section_title: FAQ: How will my profile verification be conducted after registering on the Skill India Digital Hub platform?
content.text: Your profile verification will be completed through mobile-based One Time Password electronic Know Your Customer verification. This digital verification process ensures the authenticity of your registration details and facilitates secure access to the training platform.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the maximum number of trainees allowed in practical training sessions compared to theoretical classes?
content.text: Practical training classes are limited to a maximum of 25 trainees per session, while theoretical classes can accommodate up to 50 trainees. This difference ensures adequate hands-on learning opportunities and personalized attention during practical skill development.

content.section_title: FAQ: Will I receive any study materials or handbooks as part of the training program without additional cost?
content.text: Yes, you will receive a participant handbook and study materials at the start of your training program. These materials are provided as part of the training cost and do not require any additional payment from your side.

content.section_title: FAQ: How many free re-assessment opportunities will I receive if I fail the initial certification assessment?
content.text: You will be offered one free re-assessment opportunity under this program if you do not pass the initial certification assessment. This re-assessment cost will be covered by the scheme without any additional expense to you.

content.section_title: FAQ: What happens if I need to undergo On the Job Training as part of my selected course curriculum?
content.text: If your chosen course includes On the Job Training, the Training Provider will arrange suitable establishments and coordinate your placement. You must maintain proper attendance during this period, and the final assessment will occur only after completing the On the Job Training component.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is the training conducted entirely through online mode or do I need to attend physical classroom sessions?
content.text: The training follows a 100% classroom-based approach where both theoretical and practical sessions are conducted physically at the affiliated Training Centre. Online or remote learning options are not available under this program structure.

content.section_title: FAQ: Will I receive both physical and digital certificates upon successful completion of the training and assessment?
content.text: Yes, upon successful completion, you will receive certificates in both physical and digital formats. Additionally, you will receive corresponding credits as per the National Credit Framework of Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship.

title: PM POSHAN - Prime Minister's Overarching Scheme For Holistic Nourishment
topic: PM POSHAN - Prime Minister's Overarching Scheme For Holistic Nourishment

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: PM POSHAN is a centrally sponsored scheme by the Department of School Education & Literacy, Ministry of Education. Under this scheme, one hot cooked meal will be provided to the children studying in Government and Government – aided schools. The Scheme is implemented by the Ministry of Education.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
PM POSHAN is a centrally sponsored scheme by the Department of School Education & Literacy, Ministry of Education. Under this scheme, one hot cooked meal will be provided to the children studying in Government and Government – aided schools. The Scheme is implemented across the country covering all the eligible children without any discrimination of gender and social class.
> **Objective:**

The main objectives of the PM POSHAN Abhiyaan are to address two of the pressing problems for the majority of children in India, viz. hunger and education by improving the nutritional status of eligible children in Government and Government-aided schools as well as encouraging poor children, belonging to disadvantaged sections, to attend school more regularly and help them concentrate on classroom activities.

POSHAN Abhiyaan is one of the many schemes under the umbrella scheme "Integrated Child Development Services Scheme (ICDS)". ICDS also includes Anganwadi Services Scheme, Pradhan Mantri Matru Vandana Yojana, and Scheme for Adolescent Girls.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
**Nutrition norm per child per day:**

1. Primary: Calorie – 450; Protein - 12 gms
1. Upper Primary: Calorie – 700; Protein - 20 gms

**Food norms per child per day:**

1. Primary: Food grains - 100 gms; Pulses - 20 gms; Vegetables - 50 gms; Oil & fat - 5 gms, Salt & condiments - As per need.
1. Upper Primary: Food grains - 150 gms; Pulses - 30 gms; Vegetables - 75 gms; Oil & fat – 7.5 gms, Salt & condiments - As per need.

Under the Scheme, there is the provision of hot cooked meals to children of pre-schools or Bal Vatika (before class I) in primary schools also in addition to the 11.80 crore children of classes I to VIII studying in 11.20 lakh schools.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant must be a citizen of India.
1. The applicant must be studying in a Primary or Upper Primary class (Class 1st to Class 8th) in a Government or a Government-aided school.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text: There is no application process for this scheme. PM POSHAN is equally applicable to all children who are studying in Government and Government – aided schools.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text: No documents are required for availing the benefits of this scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is PM POSHAN Scheme?
content.text: **PM POSHAN** is a centrally sponsored scheme by the **Ministry of Education** that provides hot cooked meals to children studying in Government and Government-aided schools to improve their nutritional status and encourage **regular school attendance**.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible to benefit from PM POSHAN?
content.text:
**All children studying in:**

- Pre-school (**Bal Vatika**) within primary schools- **Class** **1 to Class 8**
in Government or Government-aided schools are eligible. There is no need to apply for the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What kind of meals are provided under this scheme?
content.text: The scheme provides one **hot cooked meal** per school day that meets specific calorie and protein norms. The meal includes food **grains, pulses, vegetables, oil/fat, and condiments.**

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the nutrition norms per child per day under PM POSHAN?
content.text:
- **Primary (Class 1–5):**

1. Calories: 450 kcal
1. Protein: 12 grams

- **Upper Primary (Class 6–8):**

1. Calories: 700 kcal
1. Protein: 20 grams

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the food quantity norms per child per day?
content.text:
- **Primary:**

1. Food grains: 100 g
1. Pulses: 20 g
1. Vegetables: 50 g
1. Oil & fat: 5 g

- **Upper Primary:**

1. Food grains: 150 g
1. Pulses: 30 g
1. Vegetables: 75 g
1. Oil & fat: 7.5 g

(Salt & condiments as per need)

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any discrimination based on caste, religion, or gender under PM POSHAN?
content.text: **No**. The scheme is **universal and inclusive**, and benefits are provided to **all eligible children** without any discrimination.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there an application process for this scheme?
content.text: **No application** is required. **All eligible children** automatically receive the benefits as **part of their school** program.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the objective of PM POSHAN Scheme?
content.text:
**The scheme aims to:**

- Enhance nutrition for **school children**- Encourage school **attendance**- Improve concentration and learning outcomes
- Promote **social equity** through common meals

content.section_title: FAQ: Is this scheme part of a broader initiative?
content.text:
**Yes**, PM POSHAN operates under the umbrella of the Integrated Child Development Services (**ICDS**), which includes:

- **Anganwadi** **Services Scheme**- **Pradhan Mantri Matru Vandana Yojana**
- **Scheme for Adolescent Girls**

content.section_title: FAQ: How many children and schools are covered under PM POSHAN?
content.text: The scheme benefits approximately more than **11.80 crore children** across **11.20 lakh schools** throughout India, making it one of the world’s largest school nutrition programs.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to reduce malnutrition, anemia, and early marriage among adolescent girls in Gujarat by educating them on health, nutrition, life skills, and legal rights, while promoting self-empowerment through various activities.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The "PURNA Scheme" launched by the Women and Child Development Department, Gujarat, is a comprehensive initiative designed for adolescent girls aged 15-18 in Gujarat. It focuses on improving their nutritional status, health, and life skills while empowering them through education, vocational training, and exposure to public services. With no income limitations, the scheme ensures inclusivity by offering nutritional supplements, health check-ups, and life-skill training to all adolescent girls registered at Anganwadi centres. The scheme also promotes awareness of legal rights, self-competency, and social skills through engaging activities and field visits.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: - **Nutritional Support: S**upplementary nutrition (PURNA Shakti) 4 packets (1kg/packet) on 4th Tuesday of every month which contains 9 micronutrients.  - **Health Enhancement:** Distribution of IFA tablets every Wednesday at schools and AWCs, along with health check-ups and referral services, is conducted in convergence with the Health Department.  - **Educational Encouragement:** Support out of school adolescent girls for school re-enrollment and vocational training.  - **Life Skill Development:** Knowledge of nutrition, health, and legal rights.  - **Public Exposure**: Visit at public places (Primary health centre, Bank, Post office and any government offices etc.) for their self-empowerment).  - **Awareness Programs:** Celebration of PURNA divas at Anganwadi centres on the 4th Tuesday of every month where various theme based activities are carried out based on health, nutrition and self -development of adolescent girls.  - **Empowerment Kits:** Distribution of PURNA kit at Anganwadi centres for awareness about nutrition, health and empowerment among adolescent girls.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
- The applicant must be a girl.
- The applicant must be aged between 15-18 years.
- Applicant must be enrolled at an Anganwadi center in Gujarat.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Step 1:** Visit the nearest Anganwadi centres.
**Step 2:** Submit age proof and enrollment details.
**Step 3:** Register for the scheme with the assistance of the Anganwadi worker.
> **Helpdesk:** [Contact Details.](https://wcd.gujarat.gov.in/general_contact)

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Age proof document (15-18 years).
- Address proof.
- Any other documents (if required).

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the "PURNA Scheme"?
content.text: The "PURNA Scheme" is an initiative aimed at reducing malnutrition, anemia, and early marriage among adolescent girls in Gujarat by educating them on health, nutrition, and life skills.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible to participate in the "PURNA Scheme"?
content.text: The "PURNA Scheme" is open to adolescent girls aged 15-18 years who are enrolled at an Anganwadi center in Gujarat.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any income limitation for the scheme?
content.text: No, the scheme does not have any income limitations for participation.

content.section_title: FAQ: How often are IFA tablets distributed?
content.text: IFA tablets are distributed every Wednesday at schools and Anganwadi centers in coordination with the Health Department.

content.section_title: FAQ: What health services are included in the scheme?
content.text: The scheme provides health check-ups and referral services in collaboration with the Health Department.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are PURNA Shakti packets?
content.text: PURNA Shakti packets are monthly nutritional supplements containing nine micronutrients, distributed to eligible girls on the 4th Tuesday of each month.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the purpose of PURNA Divas?
content.text: "PURNA Divas" is celebrated on the 4th Tuesday of every month at Anganwadi centers with theme-based activities focused on health, nutrition, and the empowerment of adolescent girls.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can out-of-school girls participate in the scheme?
content.text: Yes, the scheme encourages out-of-school adolescent girls to re-enroll in school or join vocational training programs.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can one apply for the PURNA Scheme?
content.text: Applicants can apply by visiting their local Anganwadi center, providing the necessary documents such as age proof and enrollment details.

content.section_title: FAQ: What kind of vocational training is supported under the scheme?
content.text: The scheme encourages enrollment in various vocational training programs such as ITI or KVK (Krishi Vigyan Kendra) to enhance self-empowerment.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are there any fees to join the PURNA Scheme?
content.text: No, there are no fees to join the PURNA Scheme, as it is a government initiative aimed at empowering adolescent girls.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the significance of visits to public places?
content.text: Visits to public places such as primary health centers, banks, post offices, and government offices are organized to empower girls and enhance their self-competency.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to ensure access to supplementary nutrition by all children, residing in hard-to-reach villages where there is no Anganwadi centers. This programme is functioning as an extension of respective Anganwadi Centers in the hard-to-reach villages.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
The “Pada Pushti Karyakram (PPK)” has been launched by the Department of Women & Child Development, Government of Odisha, to ensure access to supplementary nutrition by all children, residing in hard-to-reach villages where there is no Anganwadi centers. As part of this programme preschool children aged 3-6 years residing in hard-to-reach villages and currently not accessing the spot feeding programme that runs at the Anganwadi center shall be provided with both morning snacks and hot cook meals at their village
location. The Pada Pushti Karyakram is functioning as an extension of respective Anganwadi Centers (AWCs) in the hard-to-reach villages.
***Objectives:***
The primary objective of Pada Pushti Karyakram are as follows:

- **Improve access to entitlement:** To ensure that that all preschool children aged 3-6 years in the hard-to-reach villages have access to mandated Supplementary Nutrition entitlements.  - **Increased uptake and utilization:** To improve coverage and utilization of ICDS services among children of nutritionally vulnerable communities.  - **Community Participation:** To enhance awareness among mothers and community members on nutrition services and promote proper maternal and child nutrition practices in hard-toreach villages.
***Identification of hard-to-reach villages:***
The following criteria shall be followed for identification of villages for hard-to-reach villages for this programmе

- The pada/village that does not have its own Anganwadi Center and located more than two kilometers from the tagged center and there is geographic barrier for communication.
- Forest villages not having an Anganwadi Center.
- There should be at least 5 or more children between the age group of 3 years to 6 years to run Pada Pusti Karyakram. In case the number of children comes down below 5, the program would be discontinued in that village and may be moved to another village.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
***Entitlements for children under Pada Pusti Karyakram:***__
The menu will be part of regular Supplementary Nutrition Programme (SNP) under ICDS for Anganwadi centers in Odisha and children of aged 3-6 years will be fed as per the government norm prescribed for spot feeding at Anganwadi Centre (AWC) for all six days in a week and minimum 300 days in a year.
***Growth monitoring of children of Pada Pusti Karyakram village at Anganwadi Centre:***

- Growth Monitoring of all children shall be done at the Anganwadi Centre every month as per the usual practice.
- The age, weight and height/length will be measured and recorded through Poshan Tracker and in other registers maintained by the Anganwadi worker.
- The Anganwadi Workers in consultation with ICDS supervisor shall fix a day in a month to visit the respective pada/village with all growth monitoring devises and take the measurements of all children.

***Incentive for Pushti Sakhi:***

- Pushti Sakhi will be given a monthly lumpsum incentive of ₹1,000/-.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The beneficiary must be a resident of Odisha.
1. The beneficiary must be a child aged between 3 and 6 years.
1. The beneficiary must be residing in an identified hard-to-reach village where no Anganwadi Centre is available.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text: The beneficiaries must visit or contact their nearest Anganwadi Centres or Anganwadi Workers (Pushti Sakhi) to avail the benefits under the programme.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Identity proof (Aadhaar Card, etc.)
- Date of Birth Certificate
- Any Other Documents (as per requirement)

content.section_title: FAQ: What is Pada Pushti Karyakrama?
content.text: Pada Pushti Karyakrama is a special nutrition programme in Odisha to provide supplementary nutrition such as hot cooked meals and morning snacks to children aged 3 - 6 years living in remote and hard-to-reach villages that do not have Anganwadi Centres.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who are the beneficiaries of this programme?
content.text: Children aged 3-6 years residing in hamlets or villages that are remote/hard-to-reach and where access to regular Anganwadi services is limited or unavailable.

content.section_title: FAQ: How is the food provided to the children?
content.text: Food is provided through spot feeding at the village level, including hot cooked meals and morning snacks, prepared locally by community members or designated workers.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who prepares and distributes the meals?
content.text: The meals are typically prepared and distributed by Mother Groups or a designated Pushti Sakhi/volunteer at the hamlet level through community participation.

content.section_title: FAQ: How frequently are meals provided?
content.text: Meals are typically served six days a week, similar to the regular SNP pattern, to ensure regular nutrition intake.

content.section_title: FAQ: What types of food are included?
content.text: The meals include a combination of hot cooked foods (e.g., rice with dal/vegetables) and nutritious morning snacks appropriate for children aged 3-6 years as per the guidelines.

content.section_title: FAQ: How are beneficiaries identified?
content.text: Eligible children are identified based on their age (3-6 years) and residence in hard-to-reach villages/hamlets that lack an Anganwadi centre.

content.section_title: FAQ: What defines a “hard-to-reach” village?
content.text: Villages are defined as “hard-to-reach” if they do not have an Anganwadi Centre nearby and face geographical or access barriers that prevent children from attending regular services.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are growth measurements of children taken?
content.text: Yes, growth monitoring including height and weight measurements is carried out periodically at nearby Anganwadi Centres.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The government of Meghalaya provides rehabilitation treatment for persons with disabilities under the "Disability Rehabilitation Scheme".

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: - Financial assistance to disabled persons who cannot afford to undergo treatment inside and outside the state.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The person must be a resident of Meghalaya.
1. The person must have a disability certificate issued by a registered medical practitioner.
1. The disability percentage should be more than 40%.
