content.text: For second pregnancy, you receive ₹2,000 at registration, ₹3,000 after six months, ₹6,000 after delivery, and ₹1,000 after vaccination, totaling ₹12,000.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who will help me register for this scheme when I visit the Anganwadi Centre for my application?
content.text: The Accredited Social Health Activist or Auxiliary Nurse Midwife Worker at the Anganwadi Centre will register you in the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where exactly must I deliver my child to remain eligible for receiving all the financial benefits under this scheme?
content.text: You must deliver your child in any government or private hospital to remain eligible for all financial assistance installments.

content.section_title: FAQ: What essential documents do I need to carry with me when I first apply for this maternal assistance program?
content.text: You need to bring your Aadhaar Card, bank account details, mobile number, and MAMTA Card for the initial application process.

content.section_title: FAQ: At what specific stage of my pregnancy must I complete the registration process to be eligible for benefits?
content.text: You must register at the time of your first ante natal check up to be eligible for the scheme benefits.

content.section_title: FAQ: How will I receive the financial assistance payments, and do I need to visit any office to collect them?
content.text: The assistance amount is transferred directly into your bank account through installments, so you don't need to visit any office for collection.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the special provision regarding the gender of my newborn child for the second pregnancy financial assistance?
content.text: For second pregnancy, ₹6,000 after delivery is provided by central government if the newborn is a girl and by state government if a boy.

content.section_title: FAQ: When exactly during my second pregnancy will I receive the ₹3,000 installment, and from which government source?
content.text: You will receive ₹3,000 after six months of your second pregnancy, and this amount comes from the state government.

content.section_title: FAQ: For how many of my children can I receive this financial assistance if I meet all the eligibility requirements?
content.text: You can receive this financial assistance for your first two live children only, not for subsequent pregnancies.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The programme aims to prevent and control the spread of HIV/AIDS through awareness, testing, and treatment, while providing care and support to affected individuals.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The “National AIDS Control Programme” is implemented by the Uttarakhand State AIDS Control Society under the Department of Medical Health and Family Welfare, Government of Uttarakhand. The programme aims to prevent and control the spread of HIV/AIDS through awareness, testing, and treatment, while providing care and support to affected individuals.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: Raising public awareness about HIV/AIDS and providing free HIV testing, treatment, and medication.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text: 1. The beneficiaries should be the general public and HIV-infected individuals residing in Uttarakhand.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
- Under the National AIDS Control Programme, the general public is made aware about HIV/AIDS through various publicity campaigns, and complete information on prevention can be obtained by calling the toll-free helpline 1097.
- HIV testing is provided free of cost at ICTCs established in government hospitals.
- People living with HIV receive free anti-retroviral medication through designated Anti-Retroviral Therapy (ART) centres.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text: Documents are required in accordance with the programme guidelines.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can benefit from the programme?
content.text: Both the general public (through awareness and preventive education) and HIV-infected individuals (through treatment and care services) benefit from the programme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are HIV testing and treatment services free of cost?
content.text: Yes. All HIV testing and treatment services under the programme, including medicines, are completely free of cost at government-designated centers.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where can individuals get tested for HIV in Uttarakhand?
content.text: HIV testing is available at Integrated Counselling and Testing Centres (ICTCs) located in government hospitals and health facilities across the state.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the helpline number for HIV/AIDS information?
content.text: Individuals can call the National AIDS Helpline – 1097 to get complete information about HIV/AIDS prevention, treatment, and support services.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are ART centres and what services do they provide?
content.text: Anti-Retroviral Therapy (ART) Centres provide free anti-retroviral medicines, counselling, and regular health monitoring for people living with HIV.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme "Payment of Death Benefit" under the Labour and Employment Department, Himachal Pradesh, provides financial assistance to the nominees or dependents of registered members. In case of accidental death, ₹4,00,000 is given, while ₹2,00,000 is payable in case of natural death.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The "Payment of Death Benefit" scheme, introduced by the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (HPBOCWWB) under the Government of Himachal Pradesh, provides ₹4,00,000 (four lakhs) to the nominees or dependents of a registered member in the event of accidental death, and ₹2,00,000 (two lakhs) in case of natural death.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
- ₹4,00,000 in the event of accidental death.
- ₹2,00,000 in the event of natural death.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant should be a nominee/legal heir of the deceased worker.
1. The deceased worker should have been registered with the "Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board".
1. The membership of the deceased worker with the Welfare Board must be active.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
**Scheme Application Process:**
Step 1: The applicant must visit the official website of the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
Step 2: Click on the option "Apply for Benefits."
Step 3: Enter your beneficiary details and date of birth, then click "Search" to view your information.
Step 4: Select the scheme you wish to apply for to avail of the benefits.
Step 5: Enter all required information and upload the necessary documents.
Step 6: Click on "Submit Application" to complete the process.

Application Tracking:
Applicants can track their application status online through the official website of the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board. Relevant updates will be sent to the registered mobile number.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Step 1:** The interested applicant should visit the Labor Welfare Office (during office hours) and request a hard copy of the prescribed [application form](https://bocw.hp.nic.in/pdf/formno37_A1b.pdf) from the concerned authority.
**Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste a passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attested, if required).
**Step 3:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with a wage slip or appointment letter from the employer, verifying 90 days of construction work. If unavailable, a certificate from a local authority (such as a councilor, executive officer, or Panchayat Secretary) can be submitted to verify employment.
**Step 4:** After submitting the documents and payment, the worker will receive an acknowledgment receipt confirming their registration.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Identity proof i.e. Aadhaar Card, Voter Card
- Passport-size photograph
- Undertaking
- A copy Work Slip
- Proof of residence
- Ration Card
- Bank account details/copy of bank passbook
- Caste Certificate
- Age proof
- Labour Card
- Death Certificate
- Identity proof of the nominee/legal heir
- Any other documents as required

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the purpose of the scheme?
content.text: To provide financial assistance to nominees or dependents of registered members in case of death.

content.section_title: FAQ: How much is given for accidental death?
content.text: [?]4,00,000 is provided for accidental death.

content.section_title: FAQ: How much is given for natural death?
content.text: [?]2,00,000 is provided for natural death.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible for the benefit?
content.text: Nominees or dependents of registered members.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is the benefit limited to accidental deaths?
content.text: No, it also covers natural deaths.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the registration requirement?
content.text: The deceased must be a registered member of the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can be nominees or dependents?
content.text: Family members designated as nominees or legal dependents.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there an age limit for members?
content.text: Age criteria may apply as per scheme rules.

content.section_title: FAQ: What documents are required to claim the benefit?
content.text: ID proof, death certificate, and nominee verification documents.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can nominees apply for the benefit?
content.text: By submitting the required documents to the Labour and Employment Department.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme “Pension Scheme for Cancer Patients" was launched by the Directorate of Social Welfare and Social Education, Government of Tripura. The scheme aims to provide a monthly pension of ₹2,000/- to the person suffering from cancer.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The scheme “Pension Scheme for Cancer Patients" was launched by the Directorate of Social Welfare and Social Education, Government of Tripura. The scheme aims to provide a monthly pension of ₹2,000/- to persons suffering from cancer.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: ₹2,000/- per month.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Tripura.
1. The income of the applicant should not exceed ₹1,50,000/- per annum.
1. No member of the family (father, mother, son(s), or unmarried daughter(s)) of the applicant should be in government service.
1. The applicant should be suffering from cancer.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
_Applications would be invited in the prescribed form by giving wide publicity through print and electronic media._
**Step 01:** The applicant should take print of the [prescribed format](https://socialwelfare.tripura.gov.in/sites/default/files/Notification%20of%20Scheme%20for%20providing%20pension%20to%20the%20Cancer%20Patients_0.pdf) of the application form.
**Step 02:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste the passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
**Step 03:** Application should be submitted to the Office of the Child Development Project Officer (CDPO) of the concerned area.
**Step 04:** Request a receipt or acknowledgement from the concerned authority to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Passport-size Photograph.
- Bank Passbook.
- Aadhaar Card.
- Income Certificate (issued by the DCM/ Sub Divisional Magistrate).
- Certificate From a Gazetted Government Officer That No Person in His/her Family is a Government Servant.
- Medical Certificate from Regional Cancer Center, Agartala, or a Govt. Hospital or Any Recognized Hospital.
- Proof of Residence (Ration Card, Permanent Resident of Tripura Certificate (PRTC), Voter ID, Driving License, or Passport).

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the permissible proofs of residence?
content.text: Proof of residence can be in the form of a Ration Card, Permanent Resident of Tripura Certificate (PRTC), Voter ID, Driving License, or Passport.

content.section_title: FAQ: What medical certificate is accepted for the scheme?
content.text: A Medical Certificate from Regional Cancer Center, Agartala, or a Govt. Hospital or any recognized hospital is accepted.

content.section_title: FAQ: What role does the Directorate of Social Welfare and Social Education play in the scheme?
content.text: The Directorate is responsible for launching and overseeing the Pension Scheme for Cancer Patients.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where should the application be submitted?
content.text: Applications should be submitted to the Office of the Child Development Project Officer (CDPO) of the concerned area.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there an age limit for applicants to qualify for the scheme?
content.text: The guidelines do not specify an age limit; eligibility is primarily based on residency, income, and cancer diagnosis.

content.section_title: FAQ: How is the proof of income determined for eligibility?
content.text: The Income Certificate issued by the DCM/Sub Divisional Magistrate is used to determine income eligibility.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can the applicant submit the application to any government office?
content.text: No, the application should be submitted specifically to the Office of the Child Development Project Officer (CDPO) of the concerned area.

content.section_title: FAQ: What details should the acknowledgment receipt contain?
content.text: The receipt should include essential details such as the date, time of submission, and a unique identification number if applicable.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there a deadline for submitting applications?
content.text: The scheme guidelines do not specify a deadline; however, timely submission is recommended.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the role of the Child Development Project Officer (CDPO) in the application process?
content.text: The CDPO is the authority to whom the application should be submitted for processing.

content.section_title: FAQ: How is the publicity for inviting applications carried out?
content.text: Applications are invited through wide publicity in print and electronic media.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can applicants from outside Tripura avail of the scheme benefits?
content.text: No, applicants must be residents of Tripura to be eligible for the Pension Scheme for Cancer Patients.

title: Pension Scheme for Grade II Deformed Leprosy Patients
topic: Pension Scheme for Grade II Deformed Leprosy Patients

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme "Pension Scheme for Grade II Deformed Leprosy Patients" is a Pension Scheme by the Directorate of Social Welfare and Social Education, Government of Tripura. The scheme provides financial assistance of ₹2,000/- per month to Leprosy Patients who are residents of Tripura.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: Launched on 1st August 2015, the scheme "Pension Scheme for Grade II Deformed Leprosy Patients" is a Pension Scheme by the Directorate of Social Welfare and Social Education, Government of Tripura. The scheme provides financial assistance of ₹2,000/- per month to Leprosy Patients who are residents of Tripura. The applications are accepted offline.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: Pension of ₹2,000/- per month.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Tripura.
1. The applicant should be affected with Leprosy Grade II.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
_The applications would be invited from the eligible candidates by giving wide publicity through print media and electronic media._
**Step 1:** The interested applicant should visit (during office hours) the [Office ](https://socialwelfare.tripura.gov.in/sites/default/files/List-of-Service-Provider-Protection-Officer_0.pdf)of the Child Development Project Officer (CDPO)/ Gram Panchayat (GP)/ Village Committee (VC)/ Nagar Panchayat (NP)/AMC of the concerned area and request a hard copy of the [prescribed format](https://slsa.tripura.gov.in/sites/default/files/SW%20%26%20SE_0.pdf) of the application form from the staff exclusively entrusted to issue and collect filled-in applications.
OR
The interested applicant should take print of the [prescribed format](https://slsa.tripura.gov.in/sites/default/files/SW%20%26%20SE_0.pdf) of the application form.
**Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste the passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
**Step 3:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the concerned authority.
**Step 4:** Request a receipt or acknowledgement from the concerned authority to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Aadhaar Card.
- Income Certificate, issued by the local DCM/SDM (not applicable for BPL Family).
- Passport-size Photograph.
- Bank Passbook.
- Ration Card.
- Address Proof.
- Age Proof.
- Voter ID.
- Permanent Resident of Tripura Certificate (PRTC).
- Medical Certificate

content.section_title: FAQ: How does the Directorate of Social Welfare and Social Education ensure fair and transparent selection of beneficiaries, and what criteria are considered during the application process?
content.text: The selection process involves inviting applications through wide media coverage and consultation with local authorities to ensure fairness and transparency. Criteria such as residency and leprosy patient status are considered during the application process.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are there any provisions for a periodic review of the beneficiaries' eligibility, and what factors are taken into account during such reviews?
content.text: Yes, the Department conducts periodic reviews of beneficiaries' eligibility. Factors such as changes in health status or residency are taken into account during these reviews.

content.section_title: FAQ: Could you elaborate on the role of local authorities in the selection process and how their consultation is conducted?
content.text: Local authorities play a crucial role in the selection process by providing input during consultations. Their knowledge of the local context ensures a more informed and effective selection process.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there a grievance redressal mechanism in place for applicants who may face issues during the application or selection process?
content.text: Yes, a grievance redressal mechanism is in place to address any issues applicants may face during the application or selection process.

content.section_title: FAQ: What measures are in place to ensure that the Pension Scheme for Leprosy Patients reaches the intended beneficiaries without any delays or disruptions?
content.text: The Department monitors the progress and submits monthly reports to the government, ensuring a streamlined and efficient distribution process.

content.section_title: FAQ: Has there been any assessment or impact study conducted since the implementation of the scheme in 2015, and what were the findings, if available?
content.text: As of now, there is no information available regarding any assessment or impact study conducted since the implementation of the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are there any plans to expand the scope of the scheme to include other categories of individuals in the future, and how would such decisions be made?
content.text: The government reserves the right to make changes to the scheme as necessary, and any expansion of the scope would be decided based on the evolving needs and priorities.

content.section_title: FAQ: In case of modifications to the scheme, what channels of communication will be utilized to inform the public, and what considerations will be taken into account before implementing such changes?
content.text: Communication channels may include official announcements, government websites, and local media. Considerations for modifications would involve assessing the impact on beneficiaries and ensuring transparency in the decision-making process.

content.section_title: FAQ: Why is the Permanent Resident of Tripura Certificate (PRTC) included in the list of mandatory documents, and how does it contribute to the scheme's objectives?
content.text: The PRTC establishes the applicant's permanent residency in Tripura, aligning with the scheme's requirement that beneficiaries should be residents of the state.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The Mukhyamantri Amrutum Yojana, is a health insurance scheme launched by the Government of Gujarat on 4th September 2012. It was initially launched only to cater to people who fall under the lowest income groups of the state i.e. Below Poverty Line (BPL).

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: A large number of households are pushed into poverty as a result of the high costs of household spending on health care. The Below Poverty Line (BPL) population is especially vulnerable to catastrophic health risks. To address this key vulnerability faced by the BPL population in Gujarat, the State Government has launched a medical care scheme called Mukhyamantri Amrutum (MA) Yojana. The objective of the scheme is to improve access of BPL families to quality medical and surgical care for the treatment of identified diseases involving hospitalization, surgeries and therapies through an em panel network of health care providers.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
> **Financial Assistance**

- Each family is eligible for health insurance coverage of up to ₹5,00,000/- annually.
- Members of eligible families can avail benefits from any empanelled hospital.

> **Key medical procedures covered:**

1. Eye surgeries
1. Ear, nose, and throat treatments
1. Kidney transplants
1. Cancer treatments
1. Critical accident-related treatments
1. Cardiac surgeries and joint replacements.

> **Surgeries Covered in this Scheme**

1. Cardiovascular Surgeries (a total 153 Benefit Packages)
1. Neurosurgery (a total 49 Benefit Packages)
1. Burns (a total 12 Benefit Packages)
1. Poly Trauma (Not covered by Motor Vehicle Insurance) (a total 8 Benefit Packages)
1. Cancer (Malignancies) (Surgical Oncology, Chemotherapy & Radiation Oncology) (a total 210 Benefit Packages)
1. Renal (Kidney) (a total 21 Benefit Packages)
1. Neonatal (newborn) diseases (a total 23 Benefit Packages)

Mukhyamantri Amrutum Yojana provides quality medical and surgical care for catastrophic illnesses involving hospitalization, surgeries and therapies through an em panel network of hospitals to the BPL families.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
The individuals and families listed in the BPL list by the State Government are eligible under the MA Yojana. Furthermore, each family member should be present when enrolled under the scheme. However, families that belong marginally above the poverty line can be under the MA Vatsalya Yojana if they follow these criteria –

1. The annual income of the family is up to ₹4,00,000/-
1. Any member(s) who are Accredited Social Health Activist (ASHA) workers
1. Any member(s) who have accredited reporters
1. Class-3 and Class-4 employees employed on a fixed payroll by the State Government
1. Senior Citizens with families of a total annual income of up to ₹6,00,000/-

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
1. Visit a Registration Center:

- Register for the scheme at civil hospitals, taluka-level centers, or Community Service Centers (CSC).
- Other registration locations include E-Gram centers, UTI-ITSL centers, and nCode Agency centers.

1. Provide Required Documents:

- Proof of income (e.g., income certificate).
- Government-issued ID proof (e.g., Aadhaar card, voter ID).
- Proof of residence (e.g., ration card, utility bills).
- For specific groups (widows, orphans, etc.), provide relevant supporting documents.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Proof of income (e.g., income certificate).
- Government-issued ID proof (e.g., Aadhaar card, voter ID).
- Proof of residence (e.g., ration card, utility bills).
- For specific groups (widows, orphans, etc.), provide relevant supporting documents.

content.section_title: FAQ: How many members of a family are covered under this scheme?
content.text: A maximum of 5 members can be enrolled as per the regulations. However, a newborn child can be considered as the 6th member.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible under the MA Yojana scheme?
content.text:
1) The annual income of the family is up to [?]4 Lakhs
2)	Any member(s) who are Accredited Social Health Activist (ASHA) workers
3)	Any member(s) who have accredited reporters
4)	Class-3 and Class-4 employees employed on a fixed payroll by the State Government
5)	Senior Citizens with families of a total annual income of up to [?]6 Lakhs

content.section_title: FAQ: What documents do the beneficiaries need to provide at the time of enrolment?
content.text:
The eligible beneficiaries have to submit the following documents -
*	Address proof
*	Identity proof
*	BPL certificate
*	Income certificate

content.section_title: FAQ: How much coverage does the MA Yojana scheme provide ?
content.text: The scheme provides tertiary medical coverage of up to [?]5 lakh to each eligible family. Besides, [?]300 is paid to the beneficiary for transportation to the empanelled hospital

content.section_title: FAQ: Which Surgeries Covered in this Scheme ?
content.text:
1. Cardiovascular Surgeries (a total 153 Benefit Packages).
2. Neurosurgery (a total 49 Benefit Packages).
3. Burns (a total 12 Benefit Packages).
4. Poly Trauma (Not covered by Motor Vehicle Insurance) (a total 8 Benefit Packages).
5. Cancer (Malignancies) (Surgical Oncology, Chemotherapy & Radiation Oncology) (a total 210 Benefit Packages).
6. Renal (Kidney) (a total 21 Benefit Packages).
7. Neonatal (newborn) diseases (a total 23 Benefit Packages).

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the application process ?
content.text:
1. The eligible beneficiaries must visit
2. the Kiosks at Taluka or Civic Centres in their area. Mobile kiosks are also available at a local level.The authorities are well equipped with the needed hardware for enrolment.
3. The hardware is used to capture biometrics such as fingerprints, iris scans, and photos of the beneficiaries.
4. The beneficiaries might even have to fill out the MA Card application form in Gujarati with appropriate details.
5. Once the details are verified, the MA health card is issued to the beneficiaries and the enrolment process is complete.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The Mukhyamantri Ayushman Arogya Yojana, launched on 1 May 2021, aims to reduce medical expenses by offering free treatment for 1,798 procedures in government and empanelled private hospitals. Eligible NFSA, SECC 2011 families, contract workers, small farmers, and others can avail benefits.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: Rajasthan’s Mukhyamantri Ayushman Arogya Yojana (launched 1 May 2021) provides cashless health insurance to reduce out-of-pocket expenses and ensure quality treatment through government and empanelled private hospitals. Eligible families include NFSA, SECC 2011, contract workers, small/marginal farmers, and COVID-19 relief beneficiaries, while others may join by paying a premium.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
Under the Chief Minister Ayushman Arogya Yojana, along with the eligible families of NFSA and SECC currently benefiting from it, contractual workers, small and marginal farmer families and destitute and helpless families who received Covid-19 ex-gratia amount last year will get free insurance cover and apart from these categories, other remaining families of the state can join the scheme on payment of premium amount of Rs 850/- and the remaining 50 percent premium amount will be borne by the state government.

Free range family:

- Farmers (Small and Marginal) of the State, eligible beneficiary families under the State Food Security Act (NFSA), eligible families as per Socio Economic Census (SECC) 2011
- Contract workers - Contract workers working in all departments/boards/corporations/government companies of the state.
- Destitute and helpless families receiving COVID-19 ex-gratia payment from the state government
- Fee: Nil.

Fixed premium family

- Those other families of the state who are not government employees/pensioners, i.e. are not taking benefits under the Medical Attendance Rules, can join the scheme by paying the prescribed premium.
- Fee: ₹850/-

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. Free Category :

- Families eligible under the National Food Security Act (NFSA).
- Families identified under the Socio-Economic Caste Census (SECC) 2011.
- Contractual employees working in state departments, boards, corporations, and government companies.
- Small and marginal farmers.
- Destitute and helpless families who received COVID-19 ex-gratia relief in the previous year.

1. Premium Category :

- Families not included in the free category.
- Families who are not government employees or pensioners.
- Families who are not availing benefits under the Medical Attendance Rules.
- These families can enroll by paying ₹850 per year per family (50% of the premium), while the remaining 50% is borne by the State Government.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
- **Registration Process for Categories Receiving Free Benefits:**

1. Families eligible under the Food Security Act and eligible families as per the Socio-Economic Census 2011 are already eligible under the scheme. Therefore, they do not need to register.
1. Beneficiaries will be required to register on the scheme's registration portal, a link to which is available on the scheme's official website: https://maayojana.rajasthan.gov.in/. Beneficiaries can register online using their SSO ID or by visiting an e-Mitra center.
1. For registration, beneficiaries must have their Aadhaar card/JAN Aadhaar card number/JAN Aadhaar card registration receipt number and Aadhaar card number.
1. Before registration, e-authentication will be performed via OTP on the mobile number registered in the applicant's Aadhaar card, for which the applicant must have the Aadhaar card/Aadhaar card number.
1. Applications for registration of contract workers under the scheme will be verified online by the nodal officer of the concerned department and updated regularly.
1. Small and marginal farmers who are not linked to the Aadhaar card can have their land holdings seeded into the Aadhaar card through e-Mitra, as per the procedure prescribed on the Aadhaar portal.
1. After seeding, the family can be registered online or through e-Mitra on the above-mentioned registration portal of the scheme.
1. After successful registration, the beneficiary will be able to print the policy document.

- **Category eligible for benefits by paying ₹850/- per family per year:**

1. Beneficiaries must register themselves online on the scheme's registration portal or through e-Mitra. These beneficiaries must pay a premium of ₹850 per family per year to the respective e-Mitra center or through digital payment mode. After successful registration, beneficiaries will be able to print the policy document.
1. A detailed step-by-step process for registering under the scheme through e-Mitra or in-person is available on the scheme's website https://maayojana.rajasthan.gov.in/.
1.  Registration Fee: Beneficiaries in both categories are not required to pay any fee at the e-Mitra center. The fee for successful registration, premium payment, and printing of the policy document on pre-printed paper will be borne by the State Government.

- **Process for availing benefits under the scheme: The following steps will be followed to avail free treatment under the scheme:**

- Identification of Eligible Family: Eligible families will be identified through their Jan-Aadhaar card number/Jan-Aadhaar EID/policy document/Aadhaar card. Therefore, provide this information to the health guide present at the scheme counter at the time of admission to the hospital to ensure the family's eligibility.
- Identification of Beneficiary: Once the family's eligibility is confirmed, the patient's eligibility will be verified. For this purpose, by entering the Jan-Aadhaar card number or registration number into the software, the family category and family member details will be displayed. The patient will be identified and biometrically verified. A live photo will be taken in front of the web camera at the time of admission and discharge.

- **The patient will only be eligible for benefits under the scheme if the selected category and family member details are displayed in the scheme software.**

- Treatment of the patient will begin according to the package available under the scheme.
- Provisions for treatment of children up to one year of age: Even if the name of the eligible family is not included in the Jan-Aadhaar card details, a provision has been made to provide treatment under the scheme to children up to one year of age. For this purpose, treatment can be provided by generating the child's TID in the name of any available family member registered in the Jan-Aadhaar card.
- If the name of a child above one year of age is not included in the Jan-Aadhaar card, treatment for that child is not possible under the scheme. (In such a situation, the family should be advised that the child's name can be added to the Jan-Aadhaar card by presenting the birth documents at any e-Mitra center.) However, free treatment under the scheme will be available only after the name is added to the Jan-Aadhaar card.
- Provisions regarding treatment for children under five years of age: Biometric verification and the presentation of a photo ID are not mandatory for treatment of children under five years of age. A child's TID can be generated by biometric verification of another family member listed on the family identity card.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Jan Aadhaar Card/Jan Aadhaar Card Number/Jan Aadhaar Card Registration Receipt Number
- Aadhaar Card Number

content.section_title: FAQ: When was the scheme launched?
content.text: The scheme started on 1 May 2021, expanding the Ayushman Bharat–Mahatma Gandhi Rajasthan Health Insurance Scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the main objective of the scheme?
content.text: To reduce out-of-pocket medical expenses and provide quality treatment through government and empanelled private hospitals.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who are eligible under the free category?
content.text: Families under NFSA, SECC 2011, contractual workers in state departments/boards/corporations/government companies, small & marginal farmers, and families that received COVID-19 ex-gratia in the previous year.

content.section_title: FAQ: What premium is payable under the premium category?
content.text: ₹850 per family per year (the rest 50% is borne by the State Government).

content.section_title: FAQ: What kind of treatment is covered under the scheme?
content.text: Only IPD (In-patient Department) and identified procedures.

content.section_title: FAQ: How many medical packages are covered?
content.text: 1,798 packages, divided into 3,219 in the scheme software for ease.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are pre-existing diseases covered?
content.text: Yes, all diseases prior to the start of the scheme are included.

content.section_title: FAQ: What medical expenses are included in the package?
content.text: Registration, bed, nursing, consultation, surgery, anesthesia, medicines, diagnostics (X-ray/tests), oxygen, blood, OT, and protective equipment.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where can beneficiaries get treatment?
content.text:
At all government hospitals (CHCs, satellite hospitals, sub-divisional, district hospitals, medical colleges), central government hospitals in Rajasthan, and empanelled private hospitals.

Search : [https://maayojana.rajasthan.gov.in/#/hospital-search](https://maayojana.rajasthan.gov.in/#/hospital-search)

content.section_title: FAQ: Are infants eligible under the scheme?
content.text: Yes, infants up to one year are eligible even if not listed in the family card.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme provides financial assistance ranging from ₹25,000/- to ₹1,00,000/- for treatment, covering 07 identified heart diseases in children. Key features include covering costs for treatment at recognized government or private hospitals.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The scheme "Mukhyamantri Bal Hridaya Upchar Yojana" was launched by the Public Health and Family Welfare Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh. The scheme aims to provide free treatment and surgery to children aged 0 to 15 years who are suffering from identified heart diseases. The scheme provides financial assistance ranging from ₹25,000/- to ₹1,00,000/- for treatment, covering 07 identified heart diseases in children. Key features include covering costs for treatment at recognized government or private hospitals and ensuring the process is governed by the Madhya Pradesh Public Services Guarantee Act, 2010.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
- Financial assistance ranging from ₹25,000/- to ₹1,00,000/- per case for treatment.
- Provision of free treatment/surgery for 07 identified heart diseases in children.
- The sanctioned amount is transferred to the account of the concerned medical institution via Cheque or E-Payment (e-transfer).
- The benefit delivery period is stipulated as 20 working days.

***Conditions***

- Treatment must be confined to the approved package amount.
- Hospitals must not demand any additional fee from the patient for treatment beyond the sanctioned amount.
- The beneficiary or hospital must submit a detailed expenditure report/utilization certificate for the funds spent on the patient's treatment.
- A duplicate or photocopy of the discharge ticket issued after the patient's recovery must be provided.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
- The applicant must be a resident of Madhya Pradesh.
- The applicant must be a member of an Economically Weaker Family or a Below Poverty Line (BPL) family.
- The applicant must ensure their name is recorded in the family roster and they hold a ration card.
- The intended beneficiary (child) must be aged 0 to 15 years.
- The intended beneficiary (child) must be suffering from one of the identified heart diseases.

content.section_title: Exclusions
content.text: The beneficiary is not eligible for assistance if treatment for the sanctioned disease is sought prior to the approval date.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
##### ***Registration***
**Step 1:** Visit the Official Website of "[Madhya Pradesh Lok Sewa Guarantee Portal](https://mpedistrict.gov.in/)" and click "[नागरिक पंजीयन (Citizen Registration)](https://mpedistrict.gov.in/MPL/CitizenRegistration.aspx)".
**Step 2:** In the registration page, provide the following mandatory information: Name, Gender, Mobile No., Email ID.
**Step 3:** Create a new password and confirm the same. Fill the captcha code. Verify your Mobile Number via OTP. Finally, click "Register".
##### ***Application***
**Step 1:** Visit the Official Website of "[Madhya Pradesh Lok Sewa Guarantee Portal](https://mpedistrict.gov.in/)" and click "[लॉग इन (Login)](https://mpedistrict.gov.in/MPL/Login.aspx)".
**Step 2:** Login using your registered Email ID and the password created during registration.
**Step 3:** On the homepage, sort the list of services "By Department". Further, select the Department and the respective scheme that you intend to apply for. On the next page, detailed information about the scheme will be displayed, along with the following resources:

- [Official Guidelines](https://mpedistrict.gov.in/MPL/getphoto.aspx?ViewCircular=UcK/7Q97U9M=) of the Scheme.- [Prescribed Format](https://mpedistrict.gov.in/MPL/DisplayServiceForm.aspx?ParamDownloadForm=05RYlMoDTVA%3d) of the Application Form (For Offline Application).- Link to Apply Online (By Self or Through Lok Sewa Kendra / MP Online / CSC).

**Step 4:** In the scheme's online application form, fill in all the mandatory fields and upload all the mandatory documents in the specified format and size (fields marked with * are mandatory), and click "Submit". ***Alternatively, you may apply via your nearest Lok Sewa Kendra / MP Online / CSC by making a payment of ₹20/-.***
##### ***Grievance Redressal / Help & Support (Appeal Mechanism)***
The applicant may file an appeal in the following situations:

1. Non-receipt of the application acknowledgment receipt.
1. Approved treatment amount is less than the prescribed package/estimate.
1. Non-resolution within the specified time limit.

- **First Appeal:** Must be filed with the Collector. The prescribed time limit for the resolution of the first appeal is 15 working days.- **Second Appeal:** Must be filed with the Samagra Sanyukt Sanchalak, Swasthya Seva (Integrated Joint Director, Health Services).

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Step 1:** The interested applicant should take a printout of the [prescribed format](https://mpedistrict.gov.in/MPL/DisplayServiceForm.aspx?ParamDownloadForm=05RYlMoDTVA%3d) of the application form.
**Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
**Step 3:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents, within the prescribed period (if any), to the [Chief Medical and Health Officer (CMHO)](https://www.mpdoctors.com/doctors/cmho%20in%20mp.htm) of their district.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
During Application- Estimate From A Government Or Government-Recognized Private Hospital (Prāklan/Estimate).
- Proof Of Being Economically Weaker Section Or Below Poverty Line (Family List/Ration Card).
- Residence Proof (For Rural Area: Certificate Issued By Chief Executive Officer, Janpad Panchayat; For Urban Area: Certificate Issued By Commissioner, Nagar Palika/Nagar Parishad Official).
- Recent Color Photograph Of The Patient.
- Patient's Age Proof (Birth Certificate/Aadhar Card/Panchayat Certificate/Any Other Authorized Document).
Post-Treatment- Detailed Expenditure Report/Utilization Certificate
- Duplicate Or Photocopy Of The Discharge Ticket Issued After Treatment.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the maximum financial assistance amount that can be provided for the treatment of a child suffering from heart disease under this government program?
content.text: The financial assistance provided ranges from a minimum of ₹25,000/- to a maximum of ₹1,00,000/- per case, depending on the specific heart condition and treatment requirements.

content.section_title: FAQ: If my child is 16 years old and has been diagnosed with a heart disease covered under this scheme, will they be eligible to receive the benefits?
content.text: No, the intended beneficiary must be aged between 0 to 15 years at the time of application. Children who have crossed 15 years of age are not eligible for benefits under this program.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are there any restrictions on the type of medical institutions where my child can receive treatment under this financial assistance program for heart diseases?
content.text: Yes, the treatment must be conducted at government hospitals or government-recognized private hospitals only. The medical institution must be approved under the scheme to provide treatment for the identified heart diseases.

content.section_title: FAQ: What happens if the hospital where my child is receiving treatment demands additional payment beyond the sanctioned amount that was approved under the scheme?
content.text: Hospitals are strictly prohibited from demanding any additional fees from patients beyond the sanctioned amount approved under the scheme. Treatment costs must be confined to the approved package amount only.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is it necessary for my family to possess a ration card and have our names recorded in the family roster to qualify for this heart treatment assistance?
content.text: Yes, it is mandatory that the applicant's name is recorded in the family roster and the family holds a valid ration card as part of the eligibility verification process.

content.section_title: FAQ: How long does it typically take from the date of application approval to the actual disbursement of the financial assistance to the medical institution for treatment?
content.text: The benefit delivery period is stipulated as 20 working days from the date of approval. The sanctioned amount is transferred to the medical institution's account via Cheque or Electronic Payment.

content.section_title: FAQ: What kind of proof do I need to submit to establish that my family belongs to the Economically Weaker Section or Below Poverty Line category?
content.text: You must submit your family list or ration card as proof of belonging to the Economically Weaker Section or Below Poverty Line category at the time of application.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can I still receive financial assistance if my child has already undergone treatment and surgery for the heart disease before receiving official approval under this scheme?
content.text: No, the beneficiary is not eligible for assistance if treatment for the sanctioned disease was sought prior to the approval date. Treatment must be undertaken only after receiving official approval.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the different documents that must be submitted by the hospital or the family after the child's treatment has been completed and they have been discharged?
content.text: After treatment completion, a detailed expenditure report or utilization certificate for the funds spent must be submitted, along with a duplicate or photocopy of the discharge ticket issued after the patient's recovery.

content.section_title: FAQ: How many specific types of heart diseases are covered under this financial assistance scheme for children in Madhya Pradesh, and what is the age criterion?
content.text: The scheme provides coverage for 07 identified heart diseases in children. The intended beneficiary must be aged between 0 to 15 years and suffering from one of these identified conditions.

content.section_title: FAQ: What type of residence proof is required for families living in rural areas versus those living in urban areas when applying for this heart treatment assistance?
content.text: For rural areas, a certificate issued by the Chief Executive Officer of the Janpad Panchayat is required. For urban areas, a certificate issued by the Commissioner of Nagar Palika or Nagar Parishad is necessary.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any application fee that needs to be paid when submitting the application through Lok Sewa Kendra, MP Online, or Common Service Centre?
content.text: Yes, if you choose to apply through your nearest Lok Sewa Kendra, MP Online, or Common Service Centre, a payment of ₹20/- is required for processing the application.

content.section_title: FAQ: What documents are acceptable as valid proof of the child's age when submitting an application for financial assistance under this heart treatment program?
content.text: Acceptable age proof documents include Birth Certificate, Aadhar Card, Panchayat Certificate, or any other authorized document that officially establishes the child's date of birth and current age.

content.section_title: FAQ: To whom should I submit my offline application form along with all the required documents if I choose not to apply through the online portal?
content.text: The duly filled and signed application form, along with all mandatory documents, should be submitted to the Chief Medical and Health Officer of your district within the prescribed period.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to improve the health of pre-school children (aged 3 to 6 years) by providing nutritious food, including eggs twice a week and banana chips twice a week, at Anganwadi Centres.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The scheme "Mukhyamantri Bal Poshan Yojana" has been launched by the Department of Women Empowerment & Child Development, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to improve the health of pre-school children (aged 3 to 6 years) by providing nutritious food, including eggs twice a week and banana chips twice a week, at Anganwadi Centres.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: Under the scheme, pre-school children (aged 3 to 6 years) are provided with eggs twice a week and banana chips twice a week.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The beneficiary must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
1. Children aged 3 to 6 years are eligible to receive nutritious food under this scheme.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text: Children who go/get registered at Anganwadi Centre are provided benefits at the centre itself.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text: - Birth Certificate of child

content.section_title: FAQ: Who are the beneficiaries of this scheme?
content.text: Pre-school children aged 3 to 6 years who are registered at Anganwadi Centres are eligible.

content.section_title: FAQ: What nutritional benefits are provided under the scheme?
content.text: Children are provided eggs twice a week and banana chips twice a week at the Anganwadi Centre.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where are the benefits provided?
content.text: The benefits are provided directly at the Anganwadi Centre where the child is registered.

content.section_title: FAQ: How often are eggs provided under the scheme?
content.text: Eggs are provided twice a week.

content.section_title: FAQ: How often are banana chips provided under the scheme?
content.text: Banana chips are provided twice a week.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the age limit for children to receive benefits?
content.text: Children aged 3 to 6 years are eligible.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can parents register their child for this scheme?
content.text: Parents can register their child at the nearest Anganwadi Centre in their area.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The state government has announced the implementation of 'Universal Health Coverage' in the state in the budget announcement 2021-22, in compliance of which Chief Minister Chiranjeevi Health Insurance Scheme was started in the state from May 1, 2021.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
The Government of Rajasthan has always been in the forefront in making various innovations to provide health services to the citizens of the state. Rajasthan has also successfully operated free medicines and free tests in the state in the past, due to which the general public is getting the benefit of free medicines and tests in the government hospitals of the state.
This scheme has been brought with the commitment of providing good health to all the citizens of the state by freeing them from the huge expenditure on medical treatment so that there is no compulsion of money in the treatment of suffering and serious diseases.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
1. Cashless treatment facility up to Rs. 25,00,000/- will be provided to each family of Rajasthan.
1. National Food Security Act, the insurance premium of families eligible for Socio-Economic Census, small and marginal farmers and contract workers will be borne by the state government.
1. Other families will have to pay a premium of Rs. 850/- per year.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
Under the scheme, eligible families have been divided into two categories-

1. **Category receiving free benefits:-** 100 percent of the premium of the eligible families of such category determined by the state government is paid by the government. Presently eligible families under Food Security Act, eligible families of Socio-Economic Census (SECC) 2011, contract workers working in state government departments/boards/corporations/government companies, small marginal farmers and those who received Covid-19 ex-gratia last year destitute and helpless families are included in the free category.   1. **Category receiving benefits by paying Rs.850/- per family per year:-** The families of the state who do not come under the category of free eligible families and are not government employees/pensioners and are not taking benefits under the Medical Attendance Rules They can avail the benefits of the scheme by paying 50% of the fixed premium Rs. 850/- per family per year. The remaining 50 percent of the premium will be borne by the government.   1. **All EWS** families are also proposed to be given the benefit of Chiranjeevi Yojana free of cost.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
The [process ](https://chiranjeevi.rajasthan.gov.in/#/home)for registering category wise is as follows -
**Registration process for free benefit category-**

1. The eligible families under the Food Security Act and the eligible families of the Socio-Economic Census 2011 are already benefited under the scheme. Hence they do not require registration.
1. It will be necessary for the beneficiary to register on the [registration portal or ](https://chiranjeevi.rajasthan.gov.in)beneficiaries can register online through their SSO ID or by visiting E Mitra Kendra.1. It is necessary for the beneficiary to have Jan Aadhar card / Jan Aadhar card number / Jan Aadhar card registration receipt number and Aadhar card number.
1. OTP on the mobile number registered in the Aadhaar card of the applicant before registration.
1. The application for registration in the scheme of contract workers will be verified online by the nodal officer of the concerned department and will be updated regularly.
1. Small and marginal farmers who are not linked with Jan Aadhaar card, they will be able to get the seeding of land holding done in Jan Aadhaar card as per the prescribed procedure on Jan Aadhaar portal through E-Mitra. After seeding, the family can be registered on the above mentioned registration portal of the scheme itself through online/e-mitra.
1. After successful registration, the beneficiary will be able to print the policy document.

**Category receiving benefits by paying Rs.850/- per family per year:-**

1. It will be necessary for the beneficiary to register himself [online portal.](https://chiranjeevi.rajasthan.gov.in/.)1. These beneficiaries will have to pay Rs. 850/- per family per year as premium amount to the concerned E Mitra center or through digital payment mode.
1. After successful registration, the beneficiary will be able to print the policy document.
1. The step-wise detailed process of registration under the scheme by e-Mitra or by yourself is available on the scheme's website [https://chiranjeevi.rajasthan.gov.in/.](https://chiranjeevi.rajasthan.gov.in/.)

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Jan Aadhar Card / Jan Aadhar Card Number / Jan Aadhar Card Registration Receipt Number
-  It is necessary to have Aadhaar card number.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the " Mukhyamantri Chiranjeevi Swasthya Beema Yojana" ?
content.text: The state government has announced the implementation of 'Universal Health Coverage' in the state in the budget announcement 2021-22, in compliance of which Chief Minister Chiranjeevi Health Insurance Scheme was started in the state from May 1, 2021.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the benefits under the scheme ?
content.text: Cashless treatment facility up to Rs. 25,00,000/- will be provided to each family of Rajasthan.

content.section_title: FAQ: How much amount need to be pay by "Category Receiving Benefits By Paying Some Amount" ?
content.text: These beneficiaries will have to pay Rs. 850/- per family per year

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can join the Chiranjeevi scheme?
content.text: Every family of Rajasthan can join this scheme. The family of government employees does not need to join this scheme because Rajasthan Government Health Scheme Yojana being brought for them.

content.section_title: FAQ: How To Apply For The Scheme ?
content.text: The process for registering category wise is as follows-

content.section_title: FAQ: What Documents Are Needed ?
content.text:
1. Jan Aadhar/Bhamashah Card Copy.
2. Aadhar Card Copy.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the official web portal to apply ?
content.text: Https://chiranjeeviapp.rajasthan.gov.in/webapp/

content.section_title: FAQ: Once The Registration Is Done In Chiranjeevi Yojana, If A Beneficiary Has To Add Or Delete The Name Of A Family Member, Then What Will Be The Process?
content.text: For this, amendment in Janadhar card can be done by visiting E-Mitra center.

content.section_title: FAQ: Will a genera category have to pay the rupees during the registration itself or can he deposit the rupees later also?
content.text: Applicant have to pay a premium amount of Rs 850/- at the time of registration at the E-Mitra center only.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme "Mukhyamantri Gambhir Bimari Upchar Yojana" by the Department of Health, Medical Education & Family Welfare, Government of Jharkhand, provides medical grants of up to ₹5,00,000/- for the treatment of critical illnesses and supports acid attack victims.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The “Mukhyamantri Gambhir Bimari Upchar Yojana” is implemented by the Department of Health, Medical Education & Family Welfare, Government of Jharkhand, aims to provide financial assistance for the treatment of critical illnesses such as cancer, kidney transplant, and severe liver diseases, as well as support to acid attack victims. Through this scheme, eligible individuals receive medical grants of up to ₹5,00,000/- to cover treatment costs.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
- Financial Assistance: Up to ₹5,00,000/- per case for the treatment of critical illnesses such as cancer, kidney transplant, and severe liver diseases.
- Valid Hospitals: Treatment costs are covered only in Jharkhand's medical colleges, central medical institutions, or empaneled hospitals. (Link)
- Expense Reimbursement: Covers bed charges, medicines, surgeries, and other related costs.

**NOTE:** Disbursal: Grants are disbursed directly to hospitals via electronic transfer or bank draft.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Jharkhand.
1. The applicant’s gross annual income must be below ₹8,00,000/- for three consecutive years (not applicable for acid attack victims).
1. The applicant must suffer from one of the specified illnesses:

- All types of cancer.
- Kidney transplant.
- Severe liver diseases.

content.section_title: Exclusions
content.text: - Applicants receiving aid from similar government health schemes are excluded unless the medical condition is non-overlapping.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Step 1:** The interested applicant should visit (during office hours) the Civil Surgeon’s office and request a hard copy of the prescribed format of the application form from the staff exclusively entrusted to issue and collect filled-in applications.
**Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
**Step 3:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the District-level Health Committee.
**Step 4:** Request a receipt or acknowledgment from the District-level Health Committee to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Passport-size Photographs
- Income Certificate
- Medical Reports
- Bank Account Details

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can apply for the MGBUY?
content.text: Residents of Jharkhand with an annual income below [?]8,00,000 suffering from specific critical illnesses can apply.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are acid attack victims eligible?
content.text: Yes, they are eligible regardless of income.

content.section_title: FAQ: What illnesses are covered under this scheme?
content.text: All types of cancer, kidney transplant, and severe liver diseases are covered.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are private hospitals included?
content.text: Only empaneled private hospitals listed under the scheme are eligible.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can the assistance amount exceed ₹5,00,000?
content.text: In exceptional cases, additional amounts may be approved by the Cabinet.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is income proof mandatory for acid attack victims?
content.text: No, income proof is not required for acid attack cases.

content.section_title: FAQ: How is the grant amount disbursed?
content.text: The amount is transferred directly to the hospital via bank draft or electronic transfer.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where can I get the application form?
content.text: Application forms are available at the Civil Surgeon's office.

content.section_title: FAQ: What documents are needed to apply?
content.text: Residence proof, income certificate, medical reports, hospital registration, and bank account details.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can applicants apply for multiple treatments?
content.text: Yes, provided the illnesses fall within the scheme's scope.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the role of the district committee?
content.text: The committee verifies applications, approves grants, and monitors disbursals.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is the scheme applicable for emergency treatments?
content.text: Yes, immediate assistance is provided, but post-approval is required.

content.section_title: FAQ: How often are district committee meetings held?
content.text: Meetings are held weekly, typically every Monday.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: This initiative by the State Government aimed at reducing school dropout rates among adolescent girls by providing free sanitary napkins, promoting hygiene, and supporting their overall health.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The "Mukhyamantri Kishori Swasthya Yojna", launched by the Education Department, Bihar, addresses the issue of increased school dropout rates among girls during puberty due to a lack of access to sanitary hygiene products. Under this scheme, the State Government distributes free sanitary napkins to adolescent girls in secondary schools, encouraging them to continue their education while promoting awareness of menstrual health and hygiene.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: - Free sanitary napkins for adolescent girls in secondary schools.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
- The applicant must be a resident of Bihar.
- The applicant must be a girl.
- The applicant must be enrolled in a recognized secondary school.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text: - Contact the concerned school Administration to avail the scheme benefits.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Birth Certificate or School Admission Certificate.
- Any other document if required.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the "Mukhyamantri Kishori Swasthya Yojna"?
content.text: It is a government scheme to provide free sanitary napkins to adolescent girls in secondary schools to promote hygiene and reduce dropout rates.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can benefit from this scheme?
content.text: Adolescent girls who are enrolled in secondary schools within the state.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any fee to avail of the scheme?
content.text: No, the scheme is completely free of cost.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can one apply for this scheme?
content.text: Applicants can approach their school administration to avail of the scheme benefits.

content.section_title: FAQ: Does this scheme cover rural areas?
content.text: Yes, the scheme is implemented statewide, including rural and urban areas.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can out-of-school girls benefit from this scheme?
content.text: No, the scheme is currently limited to girls enrolled in secondary schools.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are private school students eligible?
content.text: The scheme is primarily for students of government and government-aided schools.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can a parent or guardian apply on behalf of the student?
content.text: Yes, parents or guardians can assist in the application process if needed.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to improve the health of pregnant and lactating women by providing nutritional food such as eggs twice a week and dates twice a week at Anganwadi Centres.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The scheme "Mukhyamantri Mahila Poshan Yojana" has been launched by the Department of Women Empowerment & Child Development, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to improve the health of pregnant and lactating women by providing nutritional food such as eggs twice a week and dates twice a week at Anganwadi Centres.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: Under the scheme, pregnant and lactating women are provided eggs two days a week and dates two days a week. Dates are given to those registered women who do not consume eggs.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
1. The applicant must be a pregnant or lactating woman.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text: Women who are registered at the Anganwadi Centre receive the benefits at the centre itself.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text: - Identity proof i.e. Aadhaar card, etc.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible for this scheme?
content.text: All pregnant and lactating women who are residents of Uttarakhand and registered at an Anganwadi Centre are eligible.

content.section_title: FAQ: What benefits are provided under this scheme?
content.text: Eligible women are given eggs two days a week and dates two days a week at Anganwadi Centres.

content.section_title: FAQ: What if a woman does not eat eggs?
content.text: Women who do not consume eggs are provided dates as an alternative.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where are the benefits distributed?
content.text: The benefits are provided directly at the Anganwadi Centres to registered beneficiaries.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any cost for receiving these benefits?
content.text: No, the benefits are provided free of cost to eligible women.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can women register for the scheme?
content.text: Women need to enroll at the nearest Anganwadi Centre during pregnancy or lactation to avail the benefits.

content.section_title: FAQ: How often are eggs and dates given?
content.text:
- Eggs: 2 days a week
- Dates: 2 days a week (or as a substitute for eggs if not consumed)

content.section_title: FAQ: Are these benefits available throughout pregnancy and lactation?
content.text: Yes, benefits are provided during the entire period of pregnancy and lactation to ensure maternal health.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to improve the health and well-being of both mothers and their newborn girl children by providing essential item kits after the institutional delivery of pregnant women registered at an Anganwadi Centre.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The scheme "Mukhyamantri Mahalakshmi Kit Yojana" has been launched by the Department of Women Empowerment & Child Development, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to improve the health and well-being of both mothers and their newborn girl children by providing essential item kits after the institutional delivery of pregnant women registered at an Anganwadi Centre.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
In the state, after the institutional delivery of a pregnant woman, the following benefits will be provided:

- The woman will receive one Mahalakshmi kit for the birth of each of her first two girl children.
- In the case of twins, the woman will be given one Mahalakshmi kit, and each child will receive a separate Mahalakshmi kit.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The beneficiary must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
1. The beneficiary must be a pregnant woman registered with an Anganwadi Centre and eligible to receive a Mahalakshmi Kit after delivery.
1. The beneficiary must not be an income tax payer.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
After the institutional delivery of a pregnant woman registered at an Anganwadi Centre, a Mahalakshmi Kit is provided as a benefit.
The following items are included in the kit for mothers and their newborn girl children:

- **For Mothers:** Dry fruits, socks, scarf, towel, shawl, blanket, bedsheet, sanitary pads, mustard oil, soap, and nail cutter.- **For Newborn Girl Child:** Cotton/warm cap, socks, diaper, towel, baby soap, rubber sheet, warm blanket, and vaccination card.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Identity proof of beneficiary
- Delivery related medical reports/documents

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible for the scheme?
content.text: Pregnant women registered with an Anganwadi Centre, who are not income tax payers, are eligible to receive the Mahalakshmi Kit after delivery.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where should a pregnant woman be registered to avail benefits?
content.text: She must be registered at an Anganwadi Centre in Uttarakhand.

content.section_title: FAQ: When is the Mahalakshmi Kit provided?
content.text: The kit is provided after the institutional delivery of the pregnant woman.

content.section_title: FAQ: How many kits does a woman receive?
content.text: A woman receives one kit for each of her first two girl children.

content.section_title: FAQ: What happens in the case of twins?
content.text: For twins, the woman receives one Mahalakshmi Kit, and each child is given a separate Mahalakshmi Kit.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can women who are income tax payers receive the kit?
content.text: No, only women who are not income tax payers are eligible.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is the scheme available for mothers of boy children?
content.text: No, the scheme is specifically for mothers of girl children.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is registration at the Anganwadi Centre mandatory?
content.text: Yes, only women registered at an Anganwadi Centre are eligible for the kit.

content.section_title: FAQ: Does the kit support home deliveries?
content.text: No, the kit is provided only after institutional delivery at recognized health facilities.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to enhance maternal and child health by providing nutritional support to first-time pregnant women and mothers of young children during the crucial first 1000 days of life.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The "Mukhyamantri Matrushakti Yojana (MMY)" launched in 2022 by the Women and Child Development Department, Gujarat, focuses on improving the health and nutrition of pregnant women and mothers with children under 2 years. The scheme offers a nutritional kit containing groundnut oil, tuver dal, and chana to support calorie and protein needs. The initiative addresses the critical first 1000 days of life, aiming to enhance maternal and child health, reduce infant mortality, and prevent low birth weight.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: - **Commodities:** The nutrition kit includes 1 Litre of Groundnut oil, 1 Kg Tuver Dal, & 2 Kg Chana.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Gujarat.
1. The applicant must be a woman who is conceiving for the first time.
1. The applicant must be a mother with her first child who is under the age of 2 years.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
**Step 1:** Open the [registration portal ](https://1000d.gujarat.gov.in/)and select the "Self Enrollment" option under "Services" tab.
**Step 2:** Fill in the beneficiary’s details, including Aadhaar card number, name in English, date of birth, and beneficiary's affidavit.
**Step 3:** Validate the Aadhaar by clicking on the "Validate Aadhaar" button.
**Step 4:** The beneficiary’s ration card ID (NFSA number) and name will be displayed automatically.
**Step 5:** If the Anganwadi name is not visible in the list, manually select the appropriate Anganwadi.
**Step 6:** The name of the Anganwadi worker will be displayed automatically.
**Step 7:** Enter the mobile number you wish to use for registration and click on the “SEND OTP” button.
**Step 8:** Enter the 4-digit OTP sent to the registered mobile number in the online form.
**Step 9:** After entering the OTP, click on the “Save & Next” button to proceed.
**Step 10:** A message will appear saying, “After your general information, add your health-related information and submit the form.” Click “OK.”
**Step 11:** Enter the ID number or email address provided by the health center and click the "Search" button.
**Step 12:** The beneficiary’s name, status, P.A.C. center name, last month's status, and probable start date will auto-fill.
**Step 13:** Click on the “Confirm and Submit” button to complete the registration process.
**Step 14:** After submitting, click on the “Print Application” button to take a printout of the registration.
**Step 15:** If corrections are needed, click on “Edit Enrollment,” enter the Enrollment Number, Aadhaar Card Number, or Mobile Number, and click "Edit." Enter the OTP received to make corrections.
**Step 16:** If needed, click on “Print Enrollment,” enter the registration number, Aadhaar card number, or mobile number, click “Verify,” and enter the OTP to take a printout of the registration form.
> **Registration Receipt:** Click [here ](https://1000d.gujarat.gov.in/ApplicationPrint)to check.

> **Beneficiary Information:** Click [here ](https://1000d.gujarat.gov.in/GetSalesDetailsFromAadhar)to check.

> **Beneficiary Registration Status:** Click [here ](https://1000d.gujarat.gov.in/BeneficiaryStatus)to check.

> **Help-desk:** [Click here to Contact](https://1000d.gujarat.gov.in/ContactUS/Index).

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Aadhaar number.
- Proof of pregnancy or first childbirth.
- Any other document (if required).

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the "Mukhyamantri Matrushakti Yojana"?
content.text: The Mukhyamantri Matrushakti Yojana is a government initiative designed to improve the health and nutrition of first-time pregnant women and mothers with children under two years of age in Gujarat.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible for the "Mukhyamantri Matrushakti Yojana"?
content.text: The scheme is available to women who are pregnant for the first time and mothers whose child is under two years of age.

content.section_title: FAQ: What does the scheme offers to beneficiaries?
content.text: The scheme provides a nutritional kit that includes 1 liter of groundnut oil, 1 kg of tuver dal, and 2 kg of chana to support the nutritional needs of mothers and children.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can someone apply for the Mukhyamantri Matrushakti Yojana?
content.text: Eligible beneficiaries can apply through the official online portal by registering using their Aadhaar number and other required details.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any age restriction to apply for the Mukhyamantri Matrushakti Yojana?
content.text: No specific age restriction is mentioned; however, applicants must be first-time pregnant women or mothers with children aged under two years.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can non-residents of Gujarat apply for the scheme?
content.text: No, only residents of Gujarat are eligible to benefit from the Mukhyamantri Matrushakti Yojana.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is included in the nutritional kit provided by the scheme?
content.text: The nutritional kit contains 1 liter of groundnut oil, 1 kg of tuver dal, and 2 kg of chana.

content.section_title: FAQ: What outcomes are expected from the "Mukhyamantri Matrushakti Yojana"?
content.text: The scheme is expected to improve maternal and child health, reduce preterm births, lower infant mortality rates (IMR), and reduce maternal mortality rates (MMR).

content.section_title: FAQ: How does the scheme help in improving maternal and child health?
content.text: By providing essential nutrition, the scheme helps improve the overall health, growth, and development of mothers and children, particularly during the critical first 1000 days.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can beneficiaries collect the nutritional kit from any Anganwadi center?
content.text: Yes, eligible beneficiaries can collect their nutritional kit from the nearest Anganwadi center.

content.section_title: FAQ: When was the "Mukhyamantri Matrushakti Yojana" launched?
content.text: The scheme was launched on June 18, 2022, by the Hon'ble Prime Minister of India in Vadodara, Gujarat.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can the benefits of the Mukhyamantri Matrushakti Yojana be accessed?
content.text: After registering on the official portal, beneficiaries can visit their nearest Anganwadi center to receive the benefits of the scheme.

title: Mukhyamantri Nirmaan Shramik Dirghayu Sahayata Yojana
topic: Mukhyamantri Nirmaan Shramik Dirghayu Sahayata Yojana

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: Chhattisgarh State Government started a scheme "Mukhyamantri Nirmaan Shramik Dirghayu Sahayata Yojana" for Building and other construction worker on 11 January 2012.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: Labor Department of Chhattisgarh State started this scheme "Mukhyamantri Nirmaan Shramik Dirghayu Sahayata Yojana" for Building and other construction worker on 11 January 2012. The main objective of the scheme to provides financial assistance medical treatment and medical equipment.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
- Accidental medical treatment:

1. Registered workers will be eligible for a one-time payment of up to ₹20,000/- for medical treatment due to an accident.
