The system will redirect the applicant to enter the Biodata of the Promoter, Partner, or Director, which is typically a multi-step process. The necessary KYC and supporting documents must be uploaded by clicking the "Choose File" button next to each field.
##### **Step 6:** **** ***Application Fees Payment***

- Once the entire application and bio-data submission is complete, pay the applicable application fee.

- If selecting online payment, the system automatically redirects the applicant to the payment summary page, where they verify their details and make the payment.

##### **Step 7:** **** ***Receiving and Saving Payment Receipt***

- Upon successful payment, the user is redirected back to PIPDIC website, where a receipt for the application fee paid is generated.
- The applicant can print this receipt or save it to their computer as a PDF for future reference.

##### ***Application Status Tracking and Support***
The customer can monitor the status of their submitted application (which will initially show a status like 'UNDER REVIEW') by clicking the "View My application" link on the dashboard and selecting the relevant loan application in the drop-down menu.
##### ***Application Fees***

- For loans up to ₹25,00,000/-, the application fee is ₹100/-.
- For loans above ₹25,00,000/-, the application fee is ₹200/-.

##### ***Grievance Redressal / Help & Support***

- The applicant can contact the Managing Director, Pondicherry Industrial Promotion Development and Investment Corporation Limited at No.60, Romain Rolland Street, Puducherry 605001.
- The office phone number is 0413-2334361.
- The email address for contact is info@pipdic.com.
- The General Manager (Administration) can be contacted via mobile at 9894653835.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
Documents to be Submitted at the Time of Filing Loan Application- Detailed Project Report on the Project.
- Copy of the Sale Deed for the Work Site.
- Copy of Site and Building Plan Approved by the Puducherry Planning Authority (Two Copies).
- Estimate for Building and Other Civil Works Obtained from Approved Panel Valuer of Pondicherry Industrial Promotion Development and Investment Corporation Limited (PIPDIC).
- Quotation from Selected Supplier for All the Equipment Proposed with Detailed Specifications and Catalogues (Original Quotations to be Produced).
- Two Comparative Quotations from Standard Suppliers for All the Equipment Proposed (Original Quotations to be Produced).
- Comparative Statement for Equipment with Justification for Choosing the Supplier.
- List of Equipment Proposed to be Purchased for the Project.
- Selected Supplier Client List with Detailed Postal Address and Copy of Proof for Having Registered as an Approved Supplier with Other Financial Institutions or Banks.
- Brief Write Up on the Scope of the Project.
- Copy of Partnership Deed and Acknowledgement of Registrar of Firms (In the Case of a Partnership Firm).
- Memorandum and Articles of Association (In the Case of a Private Limited Company).
- Bio-Data of All the Promoters (Duly Filled In as Per the Format Enclosed).
- Copies of Permanent Account Number Card (PAN Card) for All the Promoters.
- Copies of Voters Identity Card for All the Promoters.
- Copies of Ration Card for All the Promoters.
- Copies of Aadhaar Card for All the Promoters.
- Copies of Bank Pass Book for All the Promoters.
- Balance Sheet and Profit and Loss Account for the Last Three Years and Bankers Details for Interested Concerns or Existing Unit.
- Consent Letter to Offer Collateral Security to the Extent Required by Pondicherry Industrial Promotion Development and Investment Corporation Limited.
- Track Record, Name and Address of Concerns, and Type of Consultancy Service Rendered by the Consultant (If Any).
- Details of Effluent and Arrangement Proposed for Treatment and Disposal of Effluent (If Any During Process).
- Investigation Fee at 0.75% on the Loan Amount Applied Plus Applicable Service Tax (for Loans Above ₹25.00 Lakhs).
- Investigation Fee (50% Concession on Investigation Fee for Women, Scheduled Caste, Scheduled Tribe, or Differently Abled Entrepreneurs).
Documents to be Submitted Either During Processing Time or After Sanction of Loan- Applicable License Copies (Municipal or Panchayat Permission, Food License, etc.).
- Original Title Deed for the Site and Collateral Security Property.
- Antecedent Title Deeds for the Site and Collateral Security Property.
- Encumbrance Certificate for Thirty Years for the Site and Collateral Security Property.
- Valuation Certificate Obtained from Approved Panel Valuer of Pondicherry Industrial Promotion Development and Investment Corporation Limited for the Site and Collateral Security Property.
- Field Measurement Book Copy (FMB Copy) for the Site and Collateral Security Property.
- Patta Copy for the Site and Collateral Security Property.
- Legal Opinion Report for the Site and Collateral Security Property.

content.section_title: FAQ: How do I create an account and begin the registration process on the application portal?
content.text: To create your account, visit the PIPDIC website at https://pipdic.in/, click "Login", and then select the "New customer" link. Enter a unique username, your email address, a secure password, and the Captcha code, then click "Register" to complete the registration process.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the purpose of the One-Time Password verification during the login process?
content.text: The OTP is a security measure sent to your registered email address to verify your identity and ensure that only authorized users can access your account. You must enter this OTP and click "Login" to proceed to your customer dashboard.

content.section_title: FAQ: What information am I required to provide when updating my profile after successful login?
content.text: Upon logging in, you must access the "Edit Profile" option in the top right corner of your dashboard and complete all required profile details as requested by the system. These details are necessary for the loan application processing and verification purposes.

content.section_title: FAQ: What documents and information should I prepare before starting my loan application?
content.text: Before beginning your loan application, gather all Know Your Customer (KYC) documents and supporting documents as specified in the application checklist. Additionally, prepare detailed biodata information for the Promoter, Partner, or Director, as you will need to upload these documents during the application process.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where can I access the loan application form once I have completed my profile setup?
content.text: After completing your profile update, navigate to the "Applications" tab on your customer dashboard to access the loan application form section.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is included in the checklist that appears before I can proceed with the loan application?
content.text: The pop-up checklist displays the General Terms and Conditions, interest rate structure, investigation fees, and collateral security norms applicable to your loan. You must carefully review this information and confirm that you have gathered all necessary documents before proceeding.

content.section_title: FAQ: What declaration must I read and agree to before submitting my loan application?
content.text: You must read the comprehensive declaration within the application form, which covers confirmation of true and accurate information provided, absence of unauthorized borrowing arrangements, and consent to verification and investigation by PIPDIC officials.

content.section_title: FAQ: How many steps does the loan application form typically contain, and what should I do if I need to pause my application?
content.text: The loan application is structured as a multi-step form across several stages. While the document does not specify a save-and-resume feature, you should complete all mandatory fields across each required step before proceeding to the next stage.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the process for uploading Know Your Customer and supporting documents after submitting my application details?
content.text: After completing the main application form submission, the system will redirect you to enter your Promoter, Partner, or Director biodata information in a multi-step process. For each required document field, click the "Choose File" button, select the appropriate file from your computer in the format specified by the portal, and upload it to the system.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the applicable application fees for different loan amounts under this scheme?
content.text: For loan amounts up to ₹25,00,000/-, the application fee is ₹100/-. For loan amounts exceeding ₹25,00,000/-, the application fee is ₹200/-.

content.section_title: FAQ: How do I make the payment for my application fee, and what happens after successful payment?
content.text: Once your application and biodata submission is complete, proceed to pay the applicable application fee. If you select online payment, the system will automatically redirect you to a payment summary page where you can verify your details and complete the payment transaction.

content.section_title: FAQ: What should I do with the payment receipt I receive after paying my application fee?
content.text: Upon successful payment, a receipt for your application fee is generated automatically. You can print this receipt or save it to your computer as a PDF for future reference and record-keeping purposes.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can I track the status of my submitted loan application after submission?
content.text: You can monitor your application status by clicking the "View My Application" link on your customer dashboard and selecting your relevant loan application from the drop-down menu. Your application will initially display a status such as "UNDER REVIEW" as it progresses through processing.

content.section_title: FAQ: What contact information and support channels are available if I need assistance or wish to file a grievance regarding my application?
content.text: You can contact the Managing Director at the Pondicherry Industrial Promotion Development and Investment Corporation Limited office located at No.60, Romain Rolland Street, Puducherry 605001. The office phone number is 0413-2334361, and you can email your queries to info@pipdic.com. The General Manager (Administration) is also available via mobile contact at 9894653835. You can also submit a grievance form at https://pipdic.in/grievance_form.

title: Scheme for Providing Counseling to the Trainees of Government Industrial Training Institutes at Directorate of Skill Development & Entrepreneurship
topic: Scheme for Providing Counseling to the Trainees of Government Industrial Training Institutes at Directorate of Skill Development & Entrepreneurship

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to provide youth-friendly counseling services in Government Industrial Training Institutes, delivered by appointed Counselors, to assist trainees suffering from mental, physical, or psychological stress. The benefit is the provision of a safe, confidential space.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The "Scheme for Providing Counseling to the Trainees of Government Industrial Training Institutes at Directorate of Skill Development & Entrepreneurship" was launched by the Directorate of Skill Development & Entrepreneurship, Government of Goa. The scheme aims to spot the trainees of Industrial Training Institutes undergoing mental, physical related stress and provide solace and a safe, confidential vent to release their stress, anguish, grievances, guilt, or feeling of unrest/complain of neglect. The scheme provides youth-friendly counseling services delivered by appointed Counselors, along with dedicated supervision and data entry support staff.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
##### ***Provision of Counseling Services***

- Youth-friendly counseling services are provided to assist distressed students to think more clearly and positively and develop the skills required to find their way forward.
- Counselors discuss and assist trainees with various concerns, including Stress Management, adjusting to the Industrial Training Institute environment, life transitions and changes, difficulties in family relationships, depression, anxiety, anger, physical, sexual, and emotional abuse, thoughts of suicide, trauma, and other feelings that cause discomfort.

##### ***Monetary Remuneration (For Contract Staff)***

- Remuneration for Counselors is ₹28,000/-.
- Remuneration for the Supervisor is ₹35,000/-.
- Remuneration for the Data Entry Operator is ₹20,000/-.

##### ***Conditions***

- The appointment of the Counselor, Supervisor, and Data Entry Operator is on a contract basis for a period of 12 months.
- The renewal of the contract shall be strictly based on the performance of the staff member, which will be assessed by the constituted Committee.
- The Directorate of Skill Development & Entrepreneurship will pay the remuneration from the funds provided by the State Government or by the Government of India through any Centrally Sponsored Schemes.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
##### ***Eligibility for the Trainee (The Applicant)***

- The applicant must be a trainee studying in Government Industrial Training Institutes (ITIs) under the Directorate of Skill Development & Entrepreneurship (DSDE) in the State of Goa.

##### ***Eligibility for the Counselor Profile***

- The applicant must not exceed the age limit of 45 years.
- The applicant must possess a Regular Masters degree in Clinical/Counseling Psychology OR Masters in Social Work (MSW) with specialization in medical and psychiatric.
- OR The applicant must possess a Regular Graduation in Psychology (6 units), Social Work, or Home Science (Human Resources) from a University Grants Commission (UGC) recognized College and a Diploma in Guidance and Counseling from a recognized Institution.
- The applicant must have a minimum of 1 year experience in Counseling.
- The applicant must possess a 15 years residence certificate.
- The applicant must possess knowledge of Konkani.
- The applicant should possess knowledge of Marathi.

##### ***Eligibility for the Supervisor Profile***

- The applicant must not exceed the age limit of 45 years.
- The applicant must possess a Regular Masters Degree in Clinical/Counseling Psychology OR Masters in Social Work (MSW) with specialization in medical and psychiatric.
- The applicant must possess a Diploma in Guidance and Counseling from a recognized institution.
- The applicant must have a minimum of 3 years experience in Counselling.
- The applicant must have experience of conducting training and workshops.
- The applicant must possess a 15 years residence certificate.
- The applicant must possess knowledge of Konkani.
- The applicant should possess knowledge of Marathi.
- The applicant should possess a Master of Philosophy (M. Phil) or Doctor of Philosophy (Ph. D.) in Psychology.

##### ***Eligibility for the Data Entry Operator Profile***

- The applicant must not exceed the age limit of 45 years.
- The applicant must have passed the Higher Secondary School Certificate Examination (HSSCE) from a recognized Institution.
- The applicant must possess a certificate confirming completion of a course in Computer for a minimum period of 3 months from a recognized Institute.
- The applicant must possess a 15 years residence certificate.
- The applicant must possess knowledge of Konkani.
- The applicant should possess knowledge of Marathi.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
** **The process described below outlines the selection of contract staff (Counselors, Supervisor, Data Entry Operator) to implement the scheme for trainees.***
##### ***Step 1: Committee Constitution***
The Directorate of Skill Development & Entrepreneurship (DSDE) shall constitute a dedicated Committee for the specific purpose of selecting the Counselors, Supervisor, and Data Entry Operator.
##### ***Step 2: Conducting Selection Interviews***
The Committee constituted by the Department shall conduct walk-in interviews as the official method for selecting the eligible candidates for the positions of Counselors, Supervisor, and Data Entry Operator.
##### ***Step 3: Contract Appointment and Renewal***
Selected candidates will be appointed on a contract basis for a period of 12 months. The Directorate of Skill Development & Entrepreneurship may subsequently renew this contract strictly based upon the staff member's performance, which will be assessed by the aforementioned selection committee.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
DOCUMENTS REQUIRED BY ALL CONTRACT APPLICANTSThese documents are essential for all three roles: COUNSELOR, SUPERVISOR, and DATA ENTRY OPERATOR, based on mandatory eligibility criteria (Age, Residency, and Language).
- Proof Of Age (To Ensure Applicant Is Under The Age Limit Of 45 Years).
- 15 Years Residence Certificate.
- Proof Of Knowledge Of Konkani.
- Proof Of Knowledge Of Marathi (If Claimed As Desirable).
DOCUMENTS REQUIRED BY COUNSELOR APPLICANTS- Regular Masters Degree Certificate In Clinical/Counseling Psychology.
- OR Masters In Social Work (MSW) With Specialization In Medical And Psychiatric.
- OR Regular Graduation Certificate In Psychology (6 Units), Social Work, Or Home Science (Human Resources) From A University Grants Commission (UGC) Recognized College.
- AND Diploma In Guidance And Counseling From Recognized Institution (If Qualification Is Based On The Regular Graduation Route).
- Documentary Proof Of Minimum 1 Year Experience In Counseling.
DOCUMENTS REQUIRED BY SUPERVISOR APPLICANTS- Regular Masters Degree Certificate In Clinical/Counseling Psychology.
- OR Masters In Social Work (MSW) With Specialization In Medical And Psychiatric.
- Diploma In Guidance And Counseling From Recognized Institution.
- Documentary Proof Of Minimum 3 Years Experience In Counselling.
- Documentary Proof Of Experience Of Conducting Training And Workshops.
- Master Of Philosophy (M. Phil) Or Doctor Of Philosophy (Ph. D.) In Psychology (If Claimed As Desirable).
DOCUMENTS REQUIRED BY DATA ENTRY OPERATOR APPLICANTS- Higher Secondary School Certificate Examination (HSSCE) Passed Certificate From Recognized Institution.
- Certificate Confirming Completed Course In Computer For A Minimum Period Of 3 Months From Recognized Institute.

content.section_title: FAQ: Which specific regional language is considered an essential and required element of knowledge for successful fulfillment of the duties in this role?
content.text: The applicant must possess essential knowledge of the Konkani language to fulfill the linguistic requirements necessary for successful engagement with the scheme's beneficiaries.

content.section_title: FAQ: If I hold a Masters degree in Social Work, what precise specialization must have been pursued to meet the essential educational requirement for the Counselor role?
content.text: If possessing a Masters in Social Work (MSW), the applicant must have specialized in both medical and psychiatric fields to meet the required educational criteria for the Counselor profile.

content.section_title: FAQ: For the Supervisor role overseeing the professional counseling team, what is the minimum length of professional experience that is considered mandatory?
content.text: Applicants for the Supervisor role must possess a minimum of 3 years experience specifically in the field of Counselling to qualify for the position.

content.section_title: FAQ: How long must the computer training course certificate cover to satisfy the technical requirement for the Data Entry Operator role?
content.text: The required certificate must confirm that the applicant has completed a computer course for a minimum period of 3 months from a recognized institution.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the maximum age limit prescribed by the official guidelines for individuals seeking to apply for any of the contract positions under this initiative?
content.text: Applicants seeking employment for any of the contract positions, including Counselor, Supervisor, or Data Entry Operator, must not exceed the age limit of 45 years.

content.section_title: FAQ: What advanced academic degree specializing in psychological study is required as an essential qualification for the specific Counselor position?
content.text: Applicants for the Counselor position must possess a Regular Masters degree in Clinical Psychology or Counseling Psychology from a recognized university or institution.

content.section_title: FAQ: Regarding my prior work history, must I show specific experience in organizing and executing professional skill development sessions?
content.text: Yes, it is mandatory for the applicant to possess verifiable experience of conducting training and workshops relevant to the scope of work described under the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is it absolutely mandatory for all candidates, regardless of their position, to provide official proof of extended residency in the State?
content.text: Yes, every applicant must mandatorily possess and submit a 15 years residence certificate of the State of Goa to satisfy the essential residency requirement.

content.section_title: FAQ: Which specific certification or diploma related to guidance services is required as a mandatory supplement to the Masters degree for the Supervisor role?
content.text: The applicant must possess a Diploma in Guidance and Counseling issued by an officially recognized institution as an essential criterion for the Supervisor role.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the basic mandatory educational certificate required for an applicant applying for the supportive Data Entry Operator position?
content.text: The applicant must have successfully passed the Higher Secondary School Certificate Examination (HSSCE) from an officially recognized educational institution.

content.section_title: FAQ: Will my application receive favorable consideration if I have pursued doctoral or higher-level research degrees in the field of psychological studies?
content.text: Yes, possessing a Master of Philosophy (M. Phil) or a Doctor of Philosophy (Ph. D.) degree in Psychology is listed as a desirable qualification for the Supervisor position.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is having competency or prior knowledge of the Marathi language considered a mandatory prerequisite for securing an employment contract?
content.text: No, while knowledge of the Marathi language is considered a desirable qualification, it is not listed as an essential or mandatory prerequisite criterion for application.

content.section_title: FAQ: How much professional experience is required in the field of guidance or psychological services for me to be deemed eligible for the Counselor role?
content.text: The applicant must have a minimum of 1 year experience specifically in the field of Counseling to meet the mandatory professional qualification criteria.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are there alternative academic pathways that can be considered sufficient for meeting the educational requirements if I do not hold a Masters degree in Psychology?
content.text: Yes, a Regular Graduation degree in Psychology (6 units), Social Work, or Home Science (Human Resources) combined with a Diploma in Guidance and Counseling is also acceptable.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: Chhattisgarh State Government started a scheme "Safai Karmakar Prasuti Sahayata Yojana" for unorganized worker on 11 March 2015.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: Labor Department of Chhattisgarh State started this scheme "Safai Karmakar Prasuti Sahayata Yojana" for unorganized worker on  11 March 2015. The main objective of the scheme to empower unorganized worker by providing financial assistance for Maternity Assistance.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
- Female employees will receive a financial assistance of ₹4,200 in the third month of pregnancy and ₹2,800 in the eighth month of pregnancy as maternity benefit.
- Male employees will receive a financial assistance of ₹3,000 as paternity leave after the birth of the child.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. Applicant(Male or Female) must be registered worker.
1. Benefits will be provided only for two children.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
**Unregistered Applicant :**

1. All the applicant have to visit the official [website](https://cglabour.nic.in) Chhattisgarh Labour Department. 1. On the home page click on "[Apply](https://cglabour.nic.in/Departments.aspx?id=4)" under "Chhattisgarh Unorganized Workers State Social Security Board".1. Enter the following details

1. Select Group Name : "Asangathit Marmakaar Mandal"
1. Select Service : "Asangathit Shramik Panjikaran"
1. What do you want to do :  "Aavedan"
1. Click next.

1. Check the eligibility criteria for registration in Chhattisgarh Unorganized Workers State Social Security Board and click next.
1. Fill out the online "Registration form".
1. Submit.

**Already Registered Applicant :**

1. All the applicant have to visit the official [website](https://cglabour.nic.in) Chhattisgarh Labour Department. 1. On the home page click on "[Apply](https://cglabour.nic.in/Departments.aspx?id=4)" under "Chhattisgarh Unorganized Workers State Social Security Board".1. Enter the following details

1. Select Group Name : "Asangathit Marmakaar Mandal"
1. Select Service : "Yojana"
1. What do you want to do :  "Aavedan"
1. Click next.

1. Select your district name and provide old/new registration number and click next.
1. Select the scheme name.
1. Fill out the online with required details.
1. Submit.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Aadhar card.
- Labor registration Card.
- Permanent Residence Certificate.
- Copy of ration card.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is this scheme ?
content.text: Chhattisgarh State Government started a scheme "Safai Karmakar Prasuti Sahayata Yojana" for unorganized worker on 11 March 2015.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the objective of the scheme?
content.text: The main objective of the scheme to empower unorganized worker by providing financial assistance for Maternity Assistance.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the benefits?
content.text:
1. Female employees will receive a financial assistance of [?]4,200 in the third month of pregnancy and [?]2,800 in the eighth month of pregnancy as maternity benefit.
2. Male employees will receive a financial assistance of [?]3,000 as paternity leave after the birth of the child.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can get the benefits?
content.text: All eligible registered unorganized labor individuals and their family from Chhattisgarh State.

content.section_title: FAQ: For how many children can it be availed?
content.text: Benefits will be provided only for two children.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can either parent apply for this benefit?
content.text: Either one can apply for the benefits.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the procedure to apply for the scheme?
content.text: Applicant must be registered under "Chhattisgarh Unorganized Workers State Social Security Board". Only than applicant can apply for the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can applicant register under Chhattisgarh Unorganized Workers State Social Security Board" ?
content.text:
1. All the applicant have to visit the official website Chhattisgarh Labour Department.
2. On the home page click on "Apply" under "Chhattisgarh Unorganized Workers State Social Security Board".
3. Enter the following details,
Select Group Name : "Asangathit Marmakaar Mandal"
Select Service : "Asangathit Shramik Panjikaran"
What do you want to do :  "Aavedan"Click next.
4. Check the eligibility criteria for registration in Chhattisgarh Unorganized Workers State Social Security Board and click next.
5. Fill out the online "Registration form".
6. Submit.

content.section_title: FAQ: How to apply for the scheme?
content.text:
1. All the applicant have to visit the official website Chhattisgarh Labour Department.
2. On the home page click on "Apply" under "Chhattisgarh Unorganized Workers State Social Security Board".
3. Provide the required details.
4. Fill out the application form.
5. Submit.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the official website to apply for the scheme?
content.text: URL : https://cglabour.nic.in/

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to provide free sanitary napkins to the daughters of registered construction workers and to women construction workers, in order to promote health and hygiene.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The scheme “Sanitary Napkin Scheme” is implemented by the Uttarakhand Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (UKBOCWWB) under the Labour Department, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to provide free sanitary napkins to the daughters of registered construction workers and to women construction workers, in order to promote health and hygiene.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: 1. Under the scheme, free sanitary napkins will be provided to the daughters of registered construction workers and to women construction workers.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant must be a registered construction worker with the Uttarakhand Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
1. The benefits of the scheme will be provided to the daughters of registered construction workers and to women construction workers registered with the Board.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online - via CSC)
content.text:
- The online application can be submitted through Shramik Suvidha Kendras.
- Along with the application form, applicants must attach a self-attested photocopy of the Shramik Card, Aadhaar Card, A Self-Declaration Certificate stating that no similar benefit has been availed from any other department.
- After verification, sanitary napkins are provided by the department.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Labour Card
- Aadhaar Card
- A Self-Declaration Certificate stating that no similar benefit has been availed from any other department

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible for this scheme?
content.text: The scheme is meant for the daughters of registered construction workers and women construction workers who are registered with the Uttarakhand Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.

content.section_title: FAQ: What benefits are provided under the scheme?
content.text: Eligible beneficiaries receive free sanitary napkins from the Labour Department to support menstrual hygiene management.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can one apply for the scheme?
content.text: Applications can be submitted online through Shramik Suvidha Kendras.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can unregistered construction workers avail the benefit?
content.text: No, only registered construction workers and their daughters are eligible under this scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can a beneficiary receive this benefit from multiple departments?
content.text: No, beneficiaries must submit a certificate declaring that they are not availing similar benefits from any other department.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can beneficiaries apply offline?
content.text: Applications are primarily done online through Shramik Suvidha Kendras.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to promote menstrual hygiene and prevent reproductive tract infections among adolescent girls aged 10 to 19 years in Uttarakhand by providing sanitary napkins at a nominal cost of ₹6/-, available through all ASHA workers across the state.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The scheme “Sanitary Napkin Distribution” is an initiative under the National Health Mission (NHM) implemented by the Department of Medical Health and Family Welfare, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to promote menstrual hygiene and prevent reproductive tract infections among adolescent girls aged 10 to 19 years in Uttarakhand.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: Under this scheme, to prevent reproductive tract infections and inculcate menstrual hygiene habits, sanitary napkins are provided to adolescent girls by ASHA workers at a nominal cost of ₹6/-.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text: 1. All girls aged between 10 to 19 years residing in Uttarakhand are eligible under this scheme.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text: Sanitary napkins are available with all ASHA workers across Uttarakhand.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text: No documents are required to avail the benefits of this scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who are the beneficiaries of this scheme?
content.text: All adolescent girls aged 10 to 19 years residing in Uttarakhand are eligible beneficiaries.

content.section_title: FAQ: How are sanitary napkins distributed under this scheme?
content.text: Sanitary napkins are distributed by ASHA workers across the state.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the cost of sanitary napkins under this scheme?
content.text: Sanitary napkins are available at a nominal cost of ₹6/- per pack.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where can beneficiaries get sanitary napkins?
content.text: Sanitary napkins are available with all ASHA workers across Uttarakhand.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the role of ASHA workers in this scheme?
content.text: ASHA workers are responsible for distributing sanitary napkins.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to provide sanitary napkins to adolescent girls and women at subsidized rates through Anganwadi centres across the state. Under this scheme, sanitary napkins are made available to adolescent girls and women at a cost of ₹6.00 through Anganwadi centres.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The scheme "Sanitary Napkin Scheme" has been launched by the Department of Women Empowerment and Child Development, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to provide sanitary napkins to adolescent girls and women at subsidized rates through Anganwadi centres across the state.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: - Sanitary napkins are distributed to adolescent girls/women at a cost of ₹6/-.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text: All adolescent girls and women are eligible to purchase sanitary napkins at subsidized rates from Anganwadi centres.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text: Sanitary napkins can be purchased at subsidized rates from Anganwadi centres.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- No documents are required.
Note: Beneficiaries can directly approach their nearest Anganwadi centre to purchase sanitary napkins.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can benefit from this scheme?
content.text: All adolescent girls and women are eligible to benefit from this scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the cost of sanitary napkins under the scheme?
content.text: Sanitary napkins are available at a subsidized cost of ₹6.00.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where can I buy these sanitary napkins?
content.text: You can buy sanitary napkins from your nearest Anganwadi centre.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any income limit to avail this benefit?
content.text: No, there is no income limit; all adolescent girls and women can purchase sanitary napkins.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is the scheme available throughout the year?
content.text: Yes, sanitary napkins are available at Anganwadi centres throughout the year.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can a woman buy multiple packs at once?
content.text: Yes, subject to availability at the Anganwadi centre.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can women from both rural and urban areas benefit from this scheme?
content.text: Yes, the scheme covers all Anganwadi centres across the state, benefiting both rural and urban women.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The Savitribai Phule Kishori Samriddhi Yojana was initiated by the "Department of Women, Child Development, and Social Security, Government of Jharkhand."

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
The Savitribai Phule Kishori Samriddhi Yojana was initiated by the Department of Women, Child Development, and Social Security, Government of Jharkhand. The objective of the Savitribai Phule Kishori Samriddhi Yojana is to address negative perceptions about the birth of daughters in society, issues like female foeticide, child marriage, low female child sex ratio, and the weak educational status of girls. The scheme has been approved across the entire state of Jharkhand to address these issues. The objectives of this scheme are as follows:

Objectives of the Savitribai Phule Kishori Samriddhi Yojana:
> (a) Women's empowerment

> (b) Emphasis on girls’ education

> (c) Eradication of the child marriage practice

> (d) Support for the educational expenses of girls in higher classes to reduce school dropout rates and encourage them towards higher education.

> (e) Assistance for adolescent girls' health and personal hygiene.

> (f) Enable adolescent girls to make independent decisions regarding their lives.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
Under this scheme, girls/adolescent girls will be provided with the following financial assistance:

**S. No.** **Beneficiary Category** **Amount of Financial Assistance**1Girls enrolled in Class 8₹2,500/-2Girls enrolled in Class 9₹2,500/-3Girls enrolled in Class 10₹5,000/-4Girls enrolled in Class 11₹5,000/-5Girls enrolled in Class 12₹5,000/-6One-time grant for girls aged 18-19₹20,000/-

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The beneficiary will apply for the benefits under the scheme, and the beneficiary will be required to have the following eligibility till the date of application:

- This assistance shall be payable to the first two daughters of the mother.
- A self-declaration to this effect will be submitted by the mother of the beneficiary, which will be required to be attached to the application.

2. Under this scheme, all eligible girls studying from class 12 in the Government/Scheduled Tribes, Scheduled Castes, Minority and Backward Classes Welfare Department, Jharkhand supported/run by Women Child Development and Social Security Department, Jharkhand/run under National Child Labour Project (NCLP)/managed by Jharkhand Government/aided by the schools will be covered.
3. The beneficiaries of the scheme will be those girl children whose

- Mother/father is not permanently employed in or retired from the Central Government/State Government/Central Public Sector Undertakings/State Public Sector Undertakings and is not in receipt of pension/family pension from these employers.
- Mother/father will not be an income taxpayer.
- The mother of the applicant will submit a self-declaration form to this effect, which will be necessary to attach to the application.

4. The beneficiary should be between 18 and 19 years of age at the time of application for the last installment.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text: A beneficiary can avail of a child development facility in his area directly or through an Anganwadi worker.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Aadhaar Card.
- Residence Proof.
- Antyodaya Card.
- Certificate of Inclusion under SECC-2011.
- School Enrollment Certificate.
- Income Certificate.
- Passport-sized Photograph.
- Bank Account Details.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can apply for benefits under this scheme?
content.text: Only the first two daughters of a mother are eligible to apply for the scheme. Applicants must meet age and educational requirements and fall within the specified family financial criteria.

content.section_title: FAQ: What age group does the scheme target?
content.text: Girls aged between 18-19 years can apply for the last installment. To receive this installment, it is mandatory for them to be enrolled in the voter list of Jharkhand, updated after January 1 of that year.

content.section_title: FAQ: What documentation is required for applying?
content.text: Applicants must submit self-attested copies of their birth certificate, Aadhaar (for both the girl and her mother), and a bank/post office account passbook. A self-declaration by the mother about eligibility is also required.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where can applications be submitted?
content.text: Applications must be submitted at the Child Development Project Officer's office in the applicant's area, either directly or through an Anganwadi worker.

content.section_title: FAQ: What income restrictions apply?
content.text: The girl's parents should not be employed by or retired from central/state government positions, or receive a pension/family pension from government employment. Additionally, neither parent should be an income taxpayer.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any special consideration for orphaned girls?
content.text: Yes, orphaned girls must provide their parents' death certificates and an orphan certificate issued by the Child Welfare Committee. In this case, a legal guardian's documentation will be accepted.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the process of verification for the scheme?
content.text: Physical verification of beneficiaries is conducted by a female supervisor at the Child Development Project Office. Upon eligibility confirmation, the District Social Welfare Officer reviews and approves applications.

content.section_title: FAQ: How will the assistance amount be disbursed?
content.text: Approved funds will be directly transferred to the beneficiary's bank account via ABPS, NEFT, RTGS, or PFMS.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is the application process online or manual?
content.text: Currently, the process is manual, but plans are underway to transition to an online application system in the future.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: A support scheme by Ministry of Women & Child Development enabling self-development and empowerment of out-of-school adolescent girls, and improving their nutritional, health & hygiene  status. All out-of-school adolescent girls in the 11 to 14 years age group are eligible for this scheme.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
A support scheme by Ministry of Women & Child Development enabling self-development and empowerment of out-of-school adolescent girls, and improving their nutritional, health & hygiene  status.

The scheme aims at supporting out of school AGs to successfully transition back to formal schooling or bridge learning / skill training. Upgrade their home-based skills and life skills. Provide information/guidance about existing public services such as Primary Health Centers, Rural Hospitals/CHCs, Post Office, Bank, Police Station, etc.

Scheme for Adolescent Girls - SAG is implemented using the platform of Anganwadi Services of Umbrella ICDS Scheme through Anganwadi Centers (AWCs).

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
**Nutrition Component:**

1. Take Home Ration or Hot Cooked Meal for out of school girls - Nutrition Provision ₹ 9.50 per day (600 calories; 18‐20 gram of protein and recommended daily intake of micronutrients per day for 300 days in a year.).
1. The financial norms will be ₹ 9.5/- per beneficiary per day for 300 days in a year. This would be inclusive of the cost of micronutrient fortification.

**Non Nutrition Component:**

1. IFA supplementation.
1. Health check‐up and Referral services.
1. Nutrition & Health Education (NHE).
1. Counseling / Guidance on family welfare, ARSH, child care practices.
1. Life Skill Education and accessing public services.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text: 1. The applicant must be a **FEMALE**.1. The age of the applicant must be between **11 and 14 years**.

content.section_title: Exclusions
content.text: NA

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text: The out-of-school adolescent girls (11 - 14 years age) are identified by the State / UT through a Base Line Survey, a one-time activity carried out by the Anganwadi Workers (AWW) who should go from house to house to meet every family in the area covered by her AWC, and gather information about the details of such girls. Such girls are provided the benefits through the Anganwadi Centre (AWC).

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Aadhaar
- Proof of Age / DOB

content.section_title: FAQ: What is IFA Supplementation?
content.text: The Iron & Folic Acid (IFA) Supplements is provided to address the iron deficiency anaemia as per the National Nutrition Anaemia Control Programme (NNAPP). AGs will be given information by ANM/AWW on food fortification, dietary diversification and advantages of supplementation by IFA tablets, for combating iron deficiency anaemia.

content.section_title: FAQ: When is the general health check-up for adolescent girls organized?
content.text: Health check-up of all AGs is organized at least once in three months on a special day called the "Kishori Diwas" which may be synchronized with Village Health & Nutrition Day (VHND) where focused attention on Adolescent specific Health issues may given when immunization is done in the first half and later special sessions may be organized on various issues for AGs.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is Kishore Health Card?
content.text: Kishore Health Card provided to the adolescent girl under this scheme is used to record the information about the weight, height, Body Mass Index (BMI) of AGs along with other services provided under the scheme. These health cards for AGs are maintained at the AWCs.

content.section_title: FAQ: I am a 12 year old girl living in a rural area. Am I eligible to apply in this scheme?
content.text: Yes, you are eligible to apply.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are three adolescent girls from the same family eligible to apply in this scheme?
content.text: Yes, they are eligible to apply.

content.section_title: FAQ: How do I know if my district is covered under is scheme?
content.text: The complete list of district under the geographical coverage of SAG can be found in Annexures I and II of scheme guidelines.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any application fee?
content.text: No. The entire application process is completely free of cost.

content.section_title: FAQ: Do I need to visit an AWC to get myself identified?
content.text: The AWW of your locality should visit your home and gather the details.

content.section_title: FAQ: Will my AADHAAR Number be made public if I provide the detail to the AWW?
content.text: No. The information containing Aadhaar number will be obtained with the consent of the beneficiary and will be password protected following the SoP issued by MWCD.

content.section_title: FAQ: Do I need to submit the original copies of the required documents to apply?
content.text: No, you only need to submit photocopies of Aadhaar Card and the Proof of Age / DOB (Birth Certificate).

content.section_title: FAQ: How can one know if the data gathered by the AWW is genuine / authentic?
content.text: The Supervisor would check and authenticate 20% of the entries in the survey registers.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where can I find the format of the baseline survey form?
content.text: The form is available in the annexure of the scheme guidelines..

title: Scheme for Acquisition of Electro-Medical and Other Equipment (PIPDIC)
topic: Scheme for Acquisition of Electro-Medical and Other Equipment (PIPDIC)

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to financially assist the registered Medical practitioners for acquisition and installation of Electro Medical and other related equipment. The applications for this scheme are accepted online through the PIPDIC portal.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The scheme “Scheme for Acquisition of Electro-Medical and Other Equipment” was launched by the Pondicherry Industrial Promotion Development and Investment Corporation Limited (PIPDIC), Department of Industries & Commerce, Puducherry. The scheme aims to financially assist the registered Medical practitioners for acquisition and installation of Electro Medical and other related equipment. The applications for this scheme are accepted online through the PIPDIC portal.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
- Financial Assistance to registered Medical practitioners for acquisition and installation of Electro Medical and other related equipment.

_*Cost of Equipment must not exceed ₹60,00,000/-._

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
- Registered Medical practitioners.
- Cost of Equipment must not exceed ₹60,00,000/-.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
**User Manual:** [https://pipdic.in/uploads/PIPDIC_docs/user_manual.pdf](https://pipdic.in/uploads/PIPDIC_docs/user_manual.pdf)
**How to Apply:** [https://pipdic.in/how_to_apply](https://pipdic.in/how_to_apply)
**Applicant Login:** [https://pipdic.in/customer_login](https://pipdic.in/customer_login)
**Application Link:** [https://pipdic.in/forms](https://pipdic.in/forms)
**Grievance Form:** [https://pipdic.in/grievance_form](https://pipdic.in/grievance_form)
##### ***Registration Process***
##### **Step 1:** **** ***Registration and Profile Creation***
Visit the [PIPDIC website.](https://pipdic.in/,) Click "Login". Click the “New customer” link under the “Login” menu, enter a username, email-id, password, and captcha, and then click the "Register" button.
##### **Step 2:** **** ***Email Verification and Login***

- Use the registered email-id to log in. An OTP is sent to the registered email address.
- The applicant is then required to enter the OTP and click "Login" to access to the customer dashboard.

##### **Step 3:** **** ***Profile Update***

- Upon successful login, the customer needs to update their profile by accessing the "Edit Profile" option (top right corner of the dashboard).
- The applicant must provide all required details. Click "Submit".

##### ***Application Process (Loan Application)***
##### **Step 1:** **** ***Accessing the Loan Application Form***

- To initiate the loan application process, the applicant must navigate from the customer dashboard by clicking on the "Applications" tab.

##### **Step 2:** **** ***Reviewing Checklist and Accepting Terms***

- A pop-up window displaying a checklist of General Terms and Conditions, interest rate structure, investigation fees, and collateral security norms will appear for review.
- Carefully read this checklist, confirm that all necessary documents have been gathered as requested, and click the “I Agree” button to proceed to the application form.

##### **Step 3:** **** ***Filling the Loan Application Form***
Fill the multi-step application form, providing appropriate details in all mandatory fields across the required steps.
##### **Step 4:** **** ***Declaration and Submission***
The applicant must carefully read and agree to the comprehensive declaration provided within the application form (covering confirmation of true information, absence of unauthorized borrowing arrangements, and consent to verification) before finally ticking the "I agree to terms" checkbox and clicking the final "Submit" button for the main application form.
##### **Step 5:** **** ***Promoter Bio-Data and Document Upload***
The system will redirect the applicant to enter the Biodata of the Promoter, Partner, or Director, which is typically a multi-step process. The necessary KYC and supporting documents must be uploaded by clicking the "Choose File" button next to each field.
##### **Step 6:** **** ***Application Fees Payment***

- Once the entire application and bio-data submission is complete, pay the applicable application fee.

- If selecting online payment, the system automatically redirects the applicant to the payment summary page, where they verify their details and make the payment.

##### **Step 7:** **** ***Receiving and Saving Payment Receipt***

- Upon successful payment, the user is redirected back to PIPDIC website, where a receipt for the application fee paid is generated.
- The applicant can print this receipt or save it to their computer as a PDF for future reference.

##### ***Application Status Tracking and Support***
The customer can monitor the status of their submitted application (which will initially show a status like 'UNDER REVIEW') by clicking the "View My application" link on the dashboard and selecting the relevant loan application in the drop-down menu.
##### ***Application Fees***

- For loans up to ₹25,00,000/-, the application fee is ₹100/-.
- For loans above ₹25,00,000/-, the application fee is ₹200/-.

##### ***Grievance Redressal / Help & Support***

- The applicant can contact the Managing Director, Pondicherry Industrial Promotion Development and Investment Corporation Limited at No.60, Romain Rolland Street, Puducherry 605001.
- The office phone number is 0413-2334361.
- The email address for contact is info@pipdic.com.
- The General Manager (Administration) can be contacted via mobile at 9894653835.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
Particulars to be Submitted at the Time of Filing Loan Application- Copy of Acknowledgement of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises Registration Certificate Obtained from Industries Department.
- Copy of Registered Lease Deed for the Work Premises for a Period Not Less than Eight Years or Consent Letter to Produce Registered Lease Deed for the Work Premises.
- Copy of Sale Deed for the Work Premises in Case the Premises is Owned by the Applicant.
- Site and Factory Building Plan with Machinery Layout and Civil Estimate Obtained from Approved Panel Valuer of Pondicherry Industrial Promotion Development and Investment Corporation (Two Copies).
- Quotation from Selected Supplier for All Machinery and Equipments Proposed with Detailed Specification and Catalogue (Original to be Produced).
- Two Comparative Quotations from Standard Suppliers for All Machinery and Equipments Proposed (Original to be Produced).
- Comparative Statement for Machinery with Justification for Choosing the Supplier.
- Selected Machinery Supplier's Client List with Detailed Postal Address and Copy of Proof for Having Registered as an Approved Supplier with Other Financial Institutions/Banks.
- Proof for Sale Price of End Product from the Existing Manufacture/Dealer.
- Quotation/Bill Copies for Each Item of Raw Materials to be Used with Arrangements for Regular Supply.
- Manufacturing Process Details with Flow Chart.
- Statistics for Demand and Supply Position of Product with Proof, Existing Competitions, Major Consumers Details, and Tie-Up Letters for Marketing the Product.
- Copy of Partnership Deed and Acknowledgement of Registrar of Firms (In the Case of Partnership Firm) or Memorandum and Articles of Association (In the Case of Private Limited Company).
- Bio-Data of All the Promoters (Duly Filled In All Respect as Per the Format Enclosed).
- Copies of Permanent Account Number Card, Voters Identity, Ration Card, Aadhaar Card, and Bank Pass Book.
- Detailed Project Report on the Project.
- Details Asked for in the Application at Page Numbers 12, 13, 14, and 15 are to be Furnished Compulsorily (To Prepare Appraisal Report).
- Consent Letter from Bank for Sanction of Working Capital Loan.
- Consent Letter to Offer Collateral Security to the Extent Required by Pondicherry Industrial Promotion Development and Investment Corporation.
- Balance Sheet and Profit and Loss Account for Last Three Years and Bankers Details for Interested Concerns/Existing Unit.
- Track Record, Name and Address of Concerns, and Type of Consultancy Service Rendered by the Consultant, If Any.
- Details of Effluent and Arrangement Proposed for Treatment and Disposal of Effluent If Any During Process.
- Investigation Fee at 0.75% on the Loan Amount Applied Plus Applicable Service Tax (With 50% Concession for Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe Entrepreneurs).

Particulars to be Submitted Either During Processing Time or After Sanction of Loan- Applicable Licence Copies, Puducherry Planning Authority Approval—Inspector of Factories Approval, Municipal/Panchayat Permission, Pollution Clearance, etc.
- Original Title Deed, Antecedent Title Deeds, Encumbrance Certificate for Thirty Years, Valuation Certificate Obtained from Approved Panel Valuer of Pondicherry Industrial Promotion Development and Investment Corporation, Field Measurement Book Copy, Patta Copy, and Legal Opinion Report for Factory Site and Collateral Security Property.

content.section_title: FAQ: How do I create an account and begin the registration process on the application portal?
content.text: To create your account, visit the PIPDIC website at https://pipdic.in/, click "Login", and then select the "New customer" link. Enter a unique username, your email address, a secure password, and the Captcha code, then click "Register" to complete the registration process.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the purpose of the One-Time Password verification during the login process?
content.text: The OTP is a security measure sent to your registered email address to verify your identity and ensure that only authorized users can access your account. You must enter this OTP and click "Login" to proceed to your customer dashboard.

content.section_title: FAQ: What information am I required to provide when updating my profile after successful login?
content.text: Upon logging in, you must access the "Edit Profile" option in the top right corner of your dashboard and complete all required profile details as requested by the system. These details are necessary for the loan application processing and verification purposes.

content.section_title: FAQ: What documents and information should I prepare before starting my loan application?
content.text: Before beginning your loan application, gather all Know Your Customer (KYC) documents and supporting documents as specified in the application checklist. Additionally, prepare detailed biodata information for the Promoter, Partner, or Director, as you will need to upload these documents during the application process.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where can I access the loan application form once I have completed my profile setup?
content.text: After completing your profile update, navigate to the "Applications" tab on your customer dashboard to access the loan application form section.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is included in the checklist that appears before I can proceed with the loan application?
content.text: The pop-up checklist displays the General Terms and Conditions, interest rate structure, investigation fees, and collateral security norms applicable to your loan. You must carefully review this information and confirm that you have gathered all necessary documents before proceeding.

content.section_title: FAQ: What declaration must I read and agree to before submitting my loan application?
content.text: You must read the comprehensive declaration within the application form, which covers confirmation of true and accurate information provided, absence of unauthorized borrowing arrangements, and consent to verification and investigation by PIPDIC officials.

content.section_title: FAQ: How many steps does the loan application form typically contain, and what should I do if I need to pause my application?
content.text: The loan application is structured as a multi-step form across several stages. While the document does not specify a save-and-resume feature, you should complete all mandatory fields across each required step before proceeding to the next stage.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the process for uploading Know Your Customer and supporting documents after submitting my application details?
content.text: After completing the main application form submission, the system will redirect you to enter your Promoter, Partner, or Director biodata information in a multi-step process. For each required document field, click the "Choose File" button, select the appropriate file from your computer in the format specified by the portal, and upload it to the system.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the applicable application fees for different loan amounts under this scheme?
content.text: For loan amounts up to ₹25,00,000/-, the application fee is ₹100/-. For loan amounts exceeding ₹25,00,000/-, the application fee is ₹200/-.

content.section_title: FAQ: How do I make the payment for my application fee, and what happens after successful payment?
content.text: Once your application and biodata submission is complete, proceed to pay the applicable application fee. If you select online payment, the system will automatically redirect you to a payment summary page where you can verify your details and complete the payment transaction.

content.section_title: FAQ: What should I do with the payment receipt I receive after paying my application fee?
content.text: Upon successful payment, a receipt for your application fee is generated automatically. You can print this receipt or save it to your computer as a PDF for future reference and record-keeping purposes.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can I track the status of my submitted loan application after submission?
content.text: You can monitor your application status by clicking the "View My Application" link on your customer dashboard and selecting your relevant loan application from the drop-down menu. Your application will initially display a status such as "UNDER REVIEW" as it progresses through processing.

content.section_title: FAQ: What contact information and support channels are available if I need assistance or wish to file a grievance regarding my application?
content.text: You can contact the Managing Director at the Pondicherry Industrial Promotion Development and Investment Corporation Limited office located at No.60, Romain Rolland Street, Puducherry 605001. The office phone number is 0413-2334361, and you can email your queries to info@pipdic.com. The General Manager (Administration) is also available via mobile contact at 9894653835. You can also submit a grievance form at https://pipdic.in/grievance_form.

title: Support for Marginalized Individuals for Livelihood and Enterprise (SMILE): Composite Medical Health for Transgender Persons
topic: Support for Marginalized Individuals for Livelihood and Enterprise (SMILE): Composite Medical Health for Transgender Persons

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to provide health Insurance coverage to all Transgender persons living in India to improve their health condition through proper treatment including sex re-assignment surgeries as well as medical support. The scheme provides health insurance in the form of Ayushman Bharat Plus.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: Launched on 12th February 2022, “Composite Medical Health for Transgender Persons” is a component of the “Comprehensive Rehabilitation for Welfare of Transgender Persons” sub-scheme of the “Support for Marginalized Individuals for Livelihood and Enterprise (SMILE)” scheme. The scheme was launched by the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of India. The scheme aims to provide health Insurance coverage to all Transgender persons living in India to improve their health condition through proper treatment including sex re-assignment surgeries as well as medical support. The scheme provides health insurance in the form of Ayushman Bharat Plus, inclusive of gender reaffirmation surgery and comprehensive transition-related healthcare, covering hormone therapy and post-operation formalities, redeemable at all private and government healthcare facilities.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
- Health insurance cover of ₹5,00,000/- (Five Lakh Rupees) per year.
- Coverage is provided in the form of Ayushman Bharat Plus health insurance.
- Coverage is inclusive of gender reaffirmation surgery within the health benefit package under Ayushman Bharat Yojana.
- The comprehensive package covers all aspects of transition related healthcare for Transgender persons.
- Coverage specifically includes hormone therapy and Sex Re-assignment surgery inclusive of post operation formalities.
- Treatment for the beneficiary shall be cashless. The Government of India provides for 100% of the approved expenditure.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
- The applicant should be Transgender.
- The applicant should not be availing similar benefits from any other schemes of the Central Government or State Government.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
**Step 1:** **** ***Ensure Possession of Mandatory Identification***

- The applicant must possess the Transgender Certificate issued by the "[National Portal for Transgender Persons](https://transgender.dosje.gov.in/)", as this document is mandatory to avail the health insurance package.- Beneficiaries who have already received their certificates from the National Portal are automatically eligible to receive the insurance.

**Step 2:** **** ***Access the Benefits at an Empanelled Facility or Service Centre***

- The Transgender beneficiary or applicant possessing the Transgender Certificate should visit an empanelled hospital or a [Common Service Centre (CSE)](https://findmycsc.nic.in/csc/). - These empanelled hospitals are identified from the list of institutions already approved under the Ayushman Bharat Scheme.

**Step 3:** **** ***Verification of Certificate Details on the System***

- The applicant is required to present their registration Identification/Transgender person’s certificate serial number from the National Portal to the staff at the facility or centre.
- The beneficiary's details will then be entered and verified on a real-time basis, adhering to the current Beneficiary Identification System procedure utilized for Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana beneficiaries.

**Step 4:** **** ***Obtain the Ayushman Transgender Health Card***

- If an individual does not possess the mandatory Transgender Certificate, they shall be redirected to the National Portal website to obtain the required document.
- After successful verification of the Transgender Certificate, the eligible applicant will subsequently be issued the specific Ayushman Transgender Health card.

***Contact Us***

- **For General Queries:** 011-20893988, mahimanisd@gmail.com- **For Technical Queries:** +91 79 23268279, tghelp@mail.inflibnet.ac.in- **Working Hours:** 10:00 AM to 17:00 PM, Monday to Friday

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Transgender Certificate And Identity Card Issued By The National Portal For Transgender Persons (Mandatory Document To Avail Health Insurance Package).
- Registration Identification/Transgender Person’s Certificate Serial Number (Required From The National Portal For Transgender Persons To Access Benefits).

content.section_title: FAQ: Following the successful completion of the verification procedure, what official document is then issued to the eligible Transgender applicant?
content.text: After successful verification of the Transgender Certificate and eligibility, the applicant will subsequently be issued the specific Ayushman Transgender Health card.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are there any limitations regarding other governmental financial or healthcare support that might disqualify an applicant from receiving these services?
content.text: Yes, the beneficiary must not be currently receiving similar health or financial benefits from any other schemes provided by either the Central Government or a State Government.

content.section_title: FAQ: When receiving treatment at an authorized medical facility, will the individual typically be required to pay for the services upfront or is the process fully managed?
content.text: The treatment provided to the beneficiary under this composite medical health scheme shall be entirely cashless. The hospitals submit the bills directly to the National Health Authority.

content.section_title: FAQ: Does the insurance coverage provided specifically include procedures necessary for gender transition, such as gender affirmation surgeries?
content.text: Yes, the provided health insurance, known as Ayushman Bharat Plus, is inclusive of gender reaffirmation surgery within the health benefit package under the Ayushman Bharat Yojana.

content.section_title: FAQ: What specific certification documents must an individual hold to prove their status as a Transgender person eligible for this benefit?
content.text: The applicant must be a Transgender person as officially notified by the Government of India and must possess the mandatory Transgender Certificate and Identity Card issued by the National Portal for Transgender Persons.

content.section_title: FAQ: If a Transgender person wishes to avail of the medical health package but does not currently hold the required certificate, what action should they take?
content.text: Such Transgender persons who do not possess the necessary certificate issued by the National Portal shall be redirected to the website to first obtain the certificate so as to become eligible for the health package.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the total maximum financial coverage value provided annually to each eligible beneficiary under this specific health care offering?
content.text: Each eligible Transgender person receives an insurance cover of ₹5,00,000/- (Five Lakh Rupees) per year under the scheme, provided in the form of Ayushman Bharat Plus health insurance.

content.section_title: FAQ: What percentage of the total approved expenditure for the medical services provided to the beneficiaries is funded by the Government?
content.text: The Government of India fully funds the scheme by providing 100% of the total approved expenditure incurred for providing the Composite Medical Health services to Transgender persons.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can you detail the comprehensive scope of healthcare services covered, ensuring that transition-related needs are fully addressed?
content.text: The Comprehensive Package covers all aspects of transition-related healthcare for Transgender persons. This coverage specifically includes essential treatments like hormone therapy and Sex Re-assignment Surgery.

content.section_title: FAQ: If I have already successfully obtained the official identity documentation, am I automatically considered eligible to enroll for the associated insurance policy?
content.text: Yes, Transgender persons who have already received their official certificates from the National Portal for Transgender Persons would be automatically eligible to receive the health insurance.

content.section_title: FAQ: At which types of medical institutions, including both public and private, can an eligible beneficiary redeem the transition-related health services?
content.text: The services offered under the comprehensive package can be redeemed for treatment and care at all recognized private and government healthcare facilities.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is the Transgender Certificate issued via the National Portal an absolute prerequisite document for gaining access to the health insurance benefits package?
content.text: Yes, the Transgender person certificate issued by the National Portal for Transgender Persons is an absolute mandatory document required to successfully avail the health insurance package.

content.section_title: FAQ: Does the comprehensive package extend its coverage to include follow-up consultations and necessary procedures after a sex reassignment surgery has been performed?
content.text: Yes, the comprehensive package explicitly includes coverage for Sex Re-assignment Surgery, and this inclusion extends to covering necessary post operation formalities.

content.section_title: FAQ: Once certification is confirmed, what is the primary physical location or administrative center where an applicant can initiate the process of accessing the benefits?
content.text: The beneficiary possessing the Transgender Certificate from the National Portal can visit an empanelled hospital or a Common Service Centre (CSC) to initiate the entry and verification of their details.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The "Urban Health Kiosk Scheme'' by the BOCW Welfare Board, Department of Labour, Punjab, is a welfare scheme that aims to provide primary health facilities to the construction workers & their families in urban health kiosks run by Punjab Health Society in slum areas.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The "Urban Health Kiosk Scheme'' by the BOCW Welfare Board, Department of Labour, Punjab, is a welfare scheme for the registered workers of the board. Under this Scheme, primary health facilities are provided to the construction workers & their families in urban health kiosks run by Punjab Health Society in slum areas.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: - Health Facilities: Primary Health Facilities are provided to the construction workers & their families in Urban Health Kiosks in slum areas.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
> ***For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:***

- The applicant should be a resident of Punjab.
- The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker (Unorganised Worker).
- The applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
- The applicant should have engaged in building and construction work for at least 90 days in a given year.
- The applicant should have a monthly income of ₹15,000/- or below.
- The applicant should not be a member of any other Welfare Fund established under any law.
- The applicant should not have membership of the Employees' Provident Fund (EPF)/ National Pension System (NPS)/ Employees' State Insurance Corporation (ESIC).
- The applicant should not be an income tax payer.

> ***For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:***

- The applicant should be a registered member Under the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of Punjab or should be a family member of the worker.
- The applicant should be suffering from any primary disease.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online - via CSC)
content.text:
> **Registration Process as a Building/ Construction Worker:**

- For new registration of workers please approach the nearby Sewa Kendra or Registering officers/ Labour Inspector.

> ***Application Process of the Welfare Scheme:***

- To apply for benefits of the concerned welfare scheme, please approach to nearby Sewa Kendra or Registering Officers/ Labour Inspector of your area.

> ***Note:***

- **Registration with the Board:** A construction worker becomes a member of the board by filling out Application Form No 28 along with depositing registration fees of ₹ 25/- only (once in life) and ₹ 10/- per month as contribution fees. A worker at a time can get himself registered for a minimum period of 1 year & maximum period of 5 years. The registered worker is called 'Beneficiary' of the Board.
> **Important Links:**

- **Check Your Registration Application Status:** Registered workers can check their registration application status through visiting this [link ](http://bocw.punjab.gov.in/Login.aspx?Id=1)upon entering their Registration Number.  - **Download Registration Card:** Registered workers can download their registration Registration Card through visiting this [link ](https://bocw.punjab.gov.in/Login.aspx?Id=1)upon entering their Registration Number.  - **Check your Scheme Application Status:** Registered workers can check their scheme application status through visiting this [link ](https://bocw.punjab.gov.in/Login.aspx?Id=1)upon entering their Registration Number.  - **CSC Locator:** Visit this [link ](https://www.apnacsconline.in/csc-locator/punjab/)to search nearest Common Service Center (CSC) of your area.  - **List of Sewa** **Kendra:** Visit this [link ](https://punjab.gov.in/documents/10191/1112286/Final+List-V1.0.pdf/6ab437f3-f04c-427d-a2b7-dd49515f075b)to see the list of Sewa Kendra.
> **Helpdesk:**

> Punjab Building And Other Construction Workers Welfare Board

> **Address:** Labour Bhawan Model Welfare Centre Phase 10, Sector 64, Sahibzada Ajit Singh Nagar, Punjab, 160062

> **Phone :** +91 172-2540125

> **Email :** bocwhq@gmail.com

> **For Bocw Portal Related Queries (Technical Support):-**

> **Phone :** +91 172-2540125

> **Email :** bocwhq@gmail.com

> **Office Visiting Hours :** Monday to Friday

> (09:00 AM to 05:00 PM)

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
> ***Registration Process as a Building/ Construction Worker:***

**Step 1:** To register as a beneficiary under the BOCW Welfare Board, an applicant worker should download [Form No-28](http://bocw.punjab.gov.in/userfiles/file/Registration%20Form%2028%20updated.pdf) and attach all the mandatory documents (self attested, if required).
**Step 2:** Application shall be submitted to the Labour Inspector of your area.
> ***Application Process of the Welfare Scheme:***

**Step 1:** A registered worker needs to contact the Assistant Labour Commissioner/ Labour-cum-Conciliation Officer/ Labour Inspector/ Labour Enforcement Officer of his area for the purpose of Application.
**Step 2:** Application shall be submitted to the Labour Inspectors along with the required documents as directed by the authority.
> ***Note:***

- **Registration with the Board:** A construction worker becomes a member of the board by filling out Application Form No 28 along with depositing registration fees of ₹ 25/- only (once in life) and ₹ 10/- per month as contribution fees. A worker at a time can get himself registered for a minimum period of 1 year & maximum period of 5 years. The registered worker is called 'Beneficiary' of the Board.
> **Important Links:**

- **Check Your Registration Application Status:** Registered workers can check their registration application status through visiting this [link ](http://bocw.punjab.gov.in/Login.aspx?Id=1)upon entering their Registration Number.  - **Download Registration Card:** Registered workers can download their registration Registration Card through visiting this [link ](https://bocw.punjab.gov.in/Login.aspx?Id=1)upon entering their Registration Number.  - **Check your Scheme Application Status:** Registered workers can check their scheme application status through visiting this [link ](https://bocw.punjab.gov.in/Login.aspx?Id=1)upon entering their Registration Number.  - **CSC Locator:** Visit this [link ](https://www.apnacsconline.in/csc-locator/punjab/)to search nearest Common Service Center (CSC) of your area.  - **List of Sewa** **Kendra:** Visit this [link ](https://punjab.gov.in/documents/10191/1112286/Final+List-V1.0.pdf/6ab437f3-f04c-427d-a2b7-dd49515f075b)to see the list of Sewa Kendra.
> **Helpdesk:**

> Punjab Building And Other Construction Workers Welfare Board

> **Address:** Labour Bhawan Model Welfare Centre Phase 10, Sector 64, Sahibzada Ajit Singh Nagar, Punjab, 160062

> **Phone :** +91 172-2540125

> **Email :** bocwhq@gmail.com

> **For Bocw Portal Related Queries (Technical Support):-**

> **Phone :** +91 172-2540125
