> **Office Visiting Hours :** Monday to Friday

> (09:00 AM to 05:00 PM)

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:- Age proof (If no age proof is available self declaration).
- Residence proof.
- Aadhaar card(if available).
- Bank Account details (Bank branch, account No. and IFSC/ RTGS number).
- Detail of dependents.
- Employer’s Certificate (Form No. 28) (90 days of construction work during the last 12 months in the State of Punjab).
- Nomination Form (Form No. 27).
For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:- Registration ID of the worker.
- Any other documents (if required).

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can register as a beneficiary under the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of Punjab?
content.text: Any construction worker whose age is between 18 to 60 years and has worked in Punjab State in any construction work for a minimum of 90 days in a year, are eligible to register as a beneficiary under the Board.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there a minimum service requirement to register as a beneficiary?
content.text: Yes, the building worker must have completed 90 days of service as a building worker in the preceding 12 months.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can apply for the welfare scheme?
content.text: Any registered worker of the Board can apply for the welfare scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: For how long a construction worker can get registered at a time?
content.text: A worker at a time can get himself registered for a minimum period of 1 year & maximum period of 5 years.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the procedure for applying for the registration?
content.text: Any construction worker who is of 18 years and below 60 years' age and has Worked in Punjab State for not less than 90 days shall apply for registration as construction worker on the prescribed Form No. 28 which is available on the Board's website or from the office of ALC/ LCO and Labour Inspectors of the area.

content.section_title: FAQ: Whom to apply for the registration?
content.text: A construction worker who intends to get himself registered as a member of the Board shall apply to the Labour Inspector of his area.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the mandatory documents for the registration?
content.text: The documents required for registration are, age proof(If no age proof is available self declaration), residence proof, aadhaar card(if available), Bank Account details (Bank branch, account No. and IFSC/RTGS number), detail of dependents, and employer's certificate of working for 90 days as construction worker, and nomination form.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there a specific form for the registration through offline mode as a beneficiary under the BOCW Welfare Board?
content.text: Yes, the application for registration must be made in Form No. 28.

content.section_title: FAQ: How much is the fee associated with the registration process?
content.text: The rate of registration fee is [?]25/- at the time of registration. It is a one time fee.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the rate of contribution?
content.text: A registered worker is required to contribute [?]10/-per month as contribution fee to the Board.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who are the family members considered for the benefits?
content.text: Husband or wife, minor sons (including major sons who are insane or physically handicapped and unable to earn), unmarried daughters and the parents of the building worker, who are solely dependent on the registered worker.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can benefit from the Urban Health Kiosk Scheme?
content.text: The scheme is targeted at construction workers and their families, providing them with access to essential health services.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where are the health kiosks located?
content.text: The health kiosks are situated in urban slum areas to ensure easy access for construction workers and their families living in these regions.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who operates the health kiosks under this scheme?
content.text: The health kiosks are managed and operated by the Punjab Health Society, ensuring the delivery of quality healthcare services.

content.section_title: FAQ: What type of health services are provided at these kiosks?
content.text: The kiosks offer primary health facilities, which may include basic medical consultations, treatment of common ailments, and preventive healthcare services.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any cost involved for the workers to access these services?
content.text: The scheme is designed to provide free or highly subsidized healthcare services to construction workers and their families.

content.section_title: FAQ: Whom to apply for getting benefits of welfare schemes?
content.text: For the application of the concerned welfare schemes of the Board, the construction worker has to visit the nearest Sewa Kendras or contact the Assistant Labour Commissioner/ Labour-cum-Conciliation Officer/ Labour Inspector/ Labour Enforcement Officer of the concerned area.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to provide immediate medical assistance and financial support to road traffic accident victims. It offers comprehensive medical treatment packages for various injury types with cashless treatment at empanelled hospitals.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: Launched in 2020, the scheme "Vahan Aksmat Sahay Scheme" by the Health and Family Welfare Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to provide immediate medical assistance and financial support to victims of road traffic accidents to ensure timely treatment and reduce mortality rates. The scheme provides medical treatment packages ranging from ₹1,000/- to ₹45,000/- for various types of injuries and covers emergency medical procedures, surgeries, and hospitalization for accident victims. The applicant must be a victim of road traffic accident requiring immediate medical treatment and the treatment must be provided at empanelled hospitals under the scheme. The scheme is implemented by Health and Family Welfare Department, Government of Gujarat and applications are accepted through participating hospitals where treatment is provided.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
**Medical Treatment Packages**
Comprehensive treatment packages ranging from ₹1,000/- to ₹45,000/- covering various types of injuries:

**Package-wise Benefits**

- Simple Injury-I (Package 1): ₹1,000/- for treating simple wounds, injection Tetanus Vaccination, analgesic, antibiotics, general conditions check, cleaning, suturing and dressing
- Simple Injury-II (Package 2): ₹2,500/- including face and scalp wounds, change of soiled cloth with disposable dress, intravenous line, multiple lacerated wounds suturing
- Deglove Injury of Extremities (Package 3): ₹12,000/- for wound debridement in operation theatre under anaesthesia, intravenous line, intravenous antibiotics, remaining wounds suturing and inpatient treatment
- Simple Fracture Without Anaesthesia (Package 4): ₹1,500/- for closed reduction, cleaning, suturing, dressing of wounds, fracture stabilization with splint
- Closed Fracture Reduction Under Anaesthesia for First Fracture (Package 5): ₹4,000/- including closed fracture reduction under short general anaesthesia with plastering and inpatient treatment
- Closed Fracture Reduction Under Anaesthesia for Additional Fracture (Package 6): ₹5,500/- for additional fractures with similar treatment as Package 5
- Dislocation Reduction Under Anaesthesia (Package 7): ₹8,000/- for joint dislocation reduction under general anaesthesia and inpatient treatment
- Open Reduction Internal Fixation-Small Bone (Package 8): ₹17,250/- including internal/external fixation of fracture in compound fractures, flap cover and inpatient treatment
- Open Reduction Internal Fixation-Large Bone (Package 9): ₹40,250/- for large bone fractures with similar treatment as Package 8
- Inter Costal Drainage One Side (Package 10): ₹8,000/- including intercostal drainage and inpatient treatment
- Inter Costal Drainage Bilateral (Package 11): ₹11,000/- for bilateral intercostal drainage with inpatient treatment
- Inter Costal Drainage Bilateral with Intensive Care Unit (Package 12): ₹12,000/- with Intensive Care Unit stay
- Inter Costal Drainage Bilateral with Intensive Care Unit and Ventilator (Package 13): ₹14,000/- including ventilator support
- Blunt Injury Abdomen (Package 14): ₹10,000/- for suspected blunt injury abdomen with stabilization as inpatient
- Blunt Injury Abdomen with Intensive Care Unit Stay (Package 15): ₹12,000/- including Intensive Care Unit stay
- Supra Pubic Cystostomy (Package 16): ₹5,000/- including supra pubic cystectomy and inpatient treatment
- Immobilization for Suspected Spine Injury (Package 17): ₹5,000/- for non-operative management using spinal brace/collar
- Cervical Spine Injury-Skeletal Tong Traction (Package 17): ₹25,000/- with tong traction
- Spine Injuries with Computed Tomography/Magnetic Resonance Imaging (Package 18): ₹7,000/- including Computed Tomography scan/Magnetic Resonance Imaging
- Tracheostomy (Package 19): ₹5,000/- for tracheostomy procedure
- Head Injury with Intensive Care Unit Stay Conservative (Package 20): ₹7,000/- for non-operative management with Intensive Care Unit stay
- Head Injury with Intensive Care Unit Stay with Ventilator (Package 21): ₹15,000/- including ventilator support
- Head Injury with Intensive Care Unit Surgical Intervention (Package 22): ₹25,000/- for operative management including burr hole surgery
- Blood Transfusion (Package 23): ₹1,500/- per unit (maximum admissible 3 units)
- Road Traffic Accident Injuries Associated with Burns Conservative Management (Package 24): ₹4,000/- for conservative management of burns
- Intensive Care Unit Stay Other than Head Injury (Package 25): ₹4,000/- for non-head injury Intensive Care Unit care
- Pelvic Injury-Conservative Management (Package 26): ₹20,000/- for non-operative management with pelvic belt and traction
- Pelvic Injury-External Fixation with Implant (Package 27): ₹45,000/- for stabilization with external fixation and haemodynamic stabilization

**Mode of Disbursement**
Cashless Treatment: Treatment provided at empanelled hospitals without upfront payment.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
- The applicant must be a victim of road traffic accident.
- The applicant must require immediate medical treatment for injuries sustained in the accident.
- The applicant must receive treatment at hospitals empanelled under the Vahan Aksmat Sahay Scheme.
- The applicant must have Medico Legal Case registered for the accident.
- The applicant must have police intimation with acknowledgment for the road traffic accident.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Step 1:** _Emergency Treatment at Empanelled Hospital_
In case of a road traffic accident, the victim should be immediately taken to the nearest empanelled hospital under the Vahan Aksmat Sahay Scheme for emergency medical treatment. The hospital will provide immediate medical care as per the severity of injuries.
**Step 2:** _Medico Legal Case Registration_
The hospital authorities will ensure that a Medico Legal Case is registered with the local police station as it is mandatory for all road traffic accident cases. The hospital will coordinate with the police for proper documentation of the accident and injuries.
**Step 3:** _Treatment Package Assessment_
The treating doctor will assess the nature and severity of injuries and determine the appropriate treatment package from the 27 available packages under the scheme. The treatment will be provided as per the standardized protocols defined for each package.
**Step 4:** _Documentation Collection_
The hospital staff will collect all required documents including Medico Legal Case register details, police intimation copy, pre and post-treatment photographs, medical records, investigation reports, and hospital bills as specified for the respective treatment package.
**Step 5:** _Cashless Treatment Provision_
The empanelled hospital will provide cashless treatment to the accident victim as per the approved package amount. No upfront payment is required from the patient or family members for the covered treatment under the scheme.
**Step 6:** _Claim Processing and Settlement_
The hospital will submit the claim along with all required documents to the scheme authorities for processing and settlement. The payment will be directly settled between the government and the empanelled hospital.
**Step 7:** _Discharge and Follow-up Care_
Upon completion of treatment, the patient will be discharged with proper discharge summary and follow-up instructions. Patients are advised to retain copies of all medical records and treatment documents for future reference and any follow-up care requirements.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
At Time of Treatment
- Scanned Copy of Medico Legal Case Register Showing the Details of Injury
- Police Intimation Copy with Police Acknowledgment
- Two Photos Showing the Wound Before and After Treatment with Patient Face Clearly Along with the Treating Doctor (Includes Patient Photo for Identity)
- Out-Patient Record/Case Sheet Details Showing the Treatment Given (Including Operation Theatre Notes If Any)
- Investigation Done Report
- Hospital Bill

Package-Specific Additional Documents
- For Fracture Cases: X-Rays To Rule Out Fracture, X-Ray Showing the Fracture Before and After Stabilization
- For Chest Injuries: Chest X-Ray Before and After Intercostal Drainage
- For Abdominal Injuries: Blood Investigation, Chest X-Ray and X-Ray of Erect Abdomen, Ultrasound, Computed Tomography Scan Report (If Required), Electrocardiogram with Report (If Required)
- For Spine Injuries: X-Ray Spine Anterior Posterior/Lateral, Computed Tomography/Magnetic Resonance Imaging
- For Head Injuries: Computed Tomography Scan Report, Other Relevant Investigations

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the highest amount of financial coverage available for the most severe road accident injuries under this medical assistance program?
content.text: The maximum treatment package available is ₹45,000 for Pelvic Injury-External Fixation with Implant, which includes stabilization with external fixation and haemodynamic stabilization.

content.section_title: FAQ: Do I need to pay any upfront costs at the hospital when I receive emergency treatment for my road accident injuries?
content.text: No, the empanelled hospitals provide completely cashless treatment, so neither you nor your family members need to make any upfront payments for covered treatments.

content.section_title: FAQ: What specific legal documentation must be completed at the hospital before I can receive treatment benefits under this accident support scheme?
content.text: A Medico Legal Case must be registered with the local police station, and you need a police intimation copy with police acknowledgment for your road traffic accident.

content.section_title: FAQ: How much financial assistance is available for treating simple wounds and basic injury care following a minor road traffic accident?
content.text: Package 1 provides ₹1,000 for treating simple wounds, including Tetanus vaccination, analgesic, antibiotics, general condition check, cleaning, suturing and dressing.

content.section_title: FAQ: What additional documentation do I need to provide if my road accident resulted in fractures requiring treatment under this scheme?
content.text: For fracture cases, you need X-Rays to rule out fracture and X-Ray showing the fracture before and after stabilization, along with standard documentation requirements.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can I receive treatment at any hospital for my road accident injuries, or are there specific hospital requirements under this program?
content.text: You must receive treatment only at hospitals that are empanelled under the Vahan Aksmat Sahay Scheme to be eligible for benefits.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the coverage amount available for head injury cases that require Intensive Care Unit stay with ventilator support?
content.text: Package 21 provides ₹15,000 for head injury with Intensive Care Unit stay including ventilator support for comprehensive critical care management.

content.section_title: FAQ: How many units of blood transfusion are covered under this scheme, and what is the financial assistance provided per unit?
content.text: Blood transfusion is covered at ₹1,500 per unit with a maximum admissible limit of 3 units under Package 23 of the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What specific photographic evidence must be submitted as part of my treatment documentation for claiming benefits under this medical assistance program?
content.text: You need two photos showing the wound before and after treatment with patient face clearly visible along with the treating doctor, including patient photo for identity.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the financial coverage available for treating complex chest injuries requiring intercostal drainage on both sides with critical care?
content.text: Package 13 provides ₹14,000 for Inter Costal Drainage Bilateral with Intensive Care Unit and Ventilator support for comprehensive chest injury management.

content.section_title: FAQ: Which government department is responsible for implementing this road traffic accident medical assistance program, and when was it launched?
content.text: The scheme is implemented by Health and Family Welfare Department, Government of Gujarat and was launched in 2020 to provide immediate medical assistance.

content.section_title: FAQ: What medical investigations and reports are specifically required for abdominal injury cases to qualify for treatment benefits under this scheme?
content.text: For abdominal injuries, you need blood investigation, chest X-Ray and X-Ray of erect abdomen, ultrasound, Computed Tomography scan report if required, and Electrocardiogram with report if required.

content.section_title: FAQ: How much financial assistance is provided for treating open fractures of large bones that require surgical intervention and internal fixation?
content.text: Package 9 provides ₹40,250 for Open Reduction Internal Fixation-Large Bone including internal/external fixation of fracture in compound fractures, flap cover and inpatient treatment.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the coverage amount available for spine injury cases that require advanced imaging studies like Computed Tomography or Magnetic Resonance Imaging?
content.text: Package 18 provides ₹7,000 for Spine Injuries with Computed Tomography/Magnetic Resonance Imaging including the cost of advanced imaging studies required for diagnosis.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: This scheme is a Gujarat government initiative providing financial assistance to pregnant and lactating women from economically weaker sections to promote maternal and child health.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The "Vandan Matru Yojana (VMY)" is a maternity benefit scheme launched by the Women and Child Development Department, Gujarat, to support pregnant and lactating women from economically weaker sections. The scheme emphasizes maternal nutrition, encourages institutional deliveries, and promotes breastfeeding practices to ensure the well-being of both mother and child. Beneficiaries receive financial assistance in stages during pregnancy, delivery, and the breastfeeding period. The scheme is implemented through Anganwadi centers and government health facilities, with funds transferred directly to the beneficiaries' bank accounts.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
> **Financial Assistance:**

- **Registration Benefit:** A one-time financial assistance of ₹1000/- is provided.  - **Additional Benefit:** An additional amount of ₹1000/- is disbursed after institutional delivery.  - **Further Benefit:** ₹2000/- is given in two installments during the breastfeeding period, promoting continued maternal and child health.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
- The applicant must be a resident of Gujarat.
- The applicant must be a pregnant or lactating woman.
- The applicant must belong to a family with an annual income below ₹2,00,000/-.
- The applicant must be registered at an Anganwadi center.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Step 1:** Visit the nearest Anganwadi center or government health facility.
**Step 2:** Submit the required documents, including proof of eligibility.
**Step 3:** Complete the registration process with the assistance of Anganwadi staff.
**Step 4:** Upon approval, financial assistance will be transferred to your bank account in stages.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Proof of residence (Aadhaar Card, Voter ID, etc.).
- Income certificate (proof of family income below Rs. 2 lakhs per annum).
- Medical Certificate (of being pregnant).
- Bank account details for direct transfer of financial assistance.
- Recent passport-sized photograph.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the "Vandan Matru Yojana"?
content.text: "Vandan Matru Yojana" is a maternity benefit scheme by the Gujarat government for pregnant and lactating women from economically weaker sections.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible for the scheme?
content.text: Pregnant and lactating women residing in Gujarat with a family income below Rs. 2 lakhs per annum are eligible.

content.section_title: FAQ: What financial benefits are provided under the scheme?
content.text: Beneficiaries receive [?]1000/- at registration, [?]1000/- after institutional delivery, and [?]2000/- during the breastfeeding period.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can one apply for the scheme?
content.text: Applications can be submitted at Anganwadi centers or government health facilities with the required documents.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is the financial assistance provided in cash?
content.text: No, the financial assistance is directly transferred to the beneficiary's bank account.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can women from outside Gujarat apply for the scheme?
content.text: No, only residents of Gujarat are eligible for the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What documents are required for registration?
content.text: Documents such as proof of residence, income certificate, pregnancy proof, and bank details are required.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where can beneficiaries get more information?
content.text: Beneficiaries can visit the official website of the Gujarat government or consult Anganwadi centers.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any fee for registering under the scheme?
content.text: No, registration under the scheme is completely free.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the role of Anganwadi centers in the scheme?
content.text: Anganwadi centers facilitate registration, document verification, and information dissemination.

title: Weekly Iron Folic Acid Tablets Free Distribution - Uttarakhand
topic: Weekly Iron Folic Acid Tablets Free Distribution - Uttarakhand

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: This aims to improve the health and nutritional status of school-going children by preventing and reducing iron deficiency anemia. Under this scheme, teachers distribute free iron and folic acid tablets every Monday to all students enrolled in government & government-aided schools across the state.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The scheme “Weekly Iron Folic Acid Tablets Free Distribution” is an initiative under the National Health Mission (NHM) implemented by the Department of Medical Health and Family Welfare, Government of Uttarakhand. The initiative aims to improve the health and nutritional status of school-going children by preventing and reducing iron deficiency anemia.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: Under this scheme, teachers distribute free iron and folic acid tablets every Monday to all students enrolled in government and government-aided schools across the state.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text: 1. All students enrolled in government and government-aided schools in Uttarakhand are eligible under this scheme.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text: The benefits of this scheme are provided to students enrolled in all government and government-aided schools.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text: Documents are required as per the directions of the respective schools, if any.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who are the beneficiaries of this scheme?
content.text: All students enrolled in government and government-aided schools in Uttarakhand are beneficiaries under this scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What does the scheme provide?
content.text: The scheme provides free iron and folic acid tablets to school students on a weekly basis.

content.section_title: FAQ: When are the tablets distributed?
content.text: The tablets are distributed every Monday by school teachers.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who distributes the tablets to students?
content.text: The school teachers are responsible for distributing the tablets to students.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any cost involved for students?
content.text: No, the tablets are distributed free of cost to all eligible students.

title: West Bengal Transport Workers’ Social Security Scheme: Assistance on Hospitalization
topic: West Bengal Transport Workers’ Social Security Scheme: Assistance on Hospitalization

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme provides financial assistance to transport workers who are hospitalized for 5 or more days due to an accident.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The “West Bengal Transport Workers’ Social Security Scheme: Assistance on Hospitalization” by the Labour Department, offers assistance to transport workers who are hospitalized for five or more days due to an accident. Benefits are directly transferred to the beneficiaries' bank accounts through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT).

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: - **Assistance for Hospitalization:** ₹400/- per day for the first 5 days of hospitalization due to an accident.  - **Extended Financial Support:** ₹200/- per day for the remaining period of hospitalization after the first five days.  - **Maximum Benefit:** Up to a total of ₹10,000/- for the entire hospitalization period.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant must be between 18 and 60 years of age at the time of enrolment.
1. The applicant must be an unorganized worker employed in the operation of commercial vehicles, such as cars, taxis, luxury taxis, light commercial vehicles, vans, auto-rickshaws, tempos, lorries, trucks, or buses.
1. The applicant’s vehicle must be used for commercial purposes and not solely for personal or family use.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
**Step 1:** Visit the “[eDistrict 2.0](https://edistrict.wb.gov.in/portal/home)” portal. Click on Log In/Sign Up.
**Step 2:** In the User Login window, scroll down to the bottom and click on “Register”.
**Step 3:** Fill in all the details carefully, enter a username, and click on “Register”.
**Step 4:** Upon entering the One Time Password received on your mobile, you will be successfully registered.
**Step 5:** Now in the User Login window, enter your Username or email and captcha and click on “Sign In”.
**Step 6:** Enter the received O.T.P and click on “Submit”.
**Step 7:** Upon successful Login, You will be redirected to the “Dashboard”. From the right side menu apply for the concerned scheme.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
For New Registration:- Certification by Prescribed Authority in the Prescribed format,
- Passport Size Photo (self and for each dependent member).
- Age Proof Document.
- Driving Licence in case of Drivers.
- EPIC/ AADHAR Card.
For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:- Medical Certificate.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the purpose of the scheme?
content.text: The scheme provides financial assistance to transport workers who are hospitalized for 5 or more days due to an accident.

content.section_title: FAQ: How much financial assistance is provided under the scheme?
content.text: Beneficiaries receive [?]400/- per day for the first five days of hospitalization and [?]200/- per day for the remaining period, up to a maximum benefit of [?]10,000/-.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible for the West Bengal Transport Workers' Social Security Scheme?
content.text: The scheme is available to unorganised workers aged between 18 and 60 years who are engaged in commercial transportation, such as taxis, buses, auto-rickshaws, and similar vehicles.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the maximum benefit a worker can receive under this scheme?
content.text: The maximum financial assistance a beneficiary can receive is [?]10,000/- for the entire hospitalization period.

content.section_title: FAQ: How is the financial assistance disbursed?
content.text: The financial assistance is disbursed directly to the beneficiary's bank account through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT).

content.section_title: FAQ: What documents are required to apply for the scheme?
content.text: Applicants need to provide identity proof, age proof, proof of employment in the transport sector, a medical certificate confirming hospitalization due to an accident, and bank account details for DBT.

content.section_title: FAQ: What happens if a worker is hospitalized for less than five days?
content.text: The scheme provides assistance only if the hospitalization is for five days or more due to an accident.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can family members of the worker apply for the scheme?
content.text: No, the scheme is specifically for the transport workers themselves, not their family members.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is this scheme available to all types of transportation workers?
content.text: No, the scheme is available only to workers involved in commercial transportation, excluding vehicles used solely for personal or family purposes.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the “Assistance for Purchase of Spectacles” scheme under the “West Bengal Transport Workers’ Social Security Scheme”?
content.text: The "Assistance for Purchase of Spectacles" Scheme is a government initiative that provides financial assistance of [?]500/- for purchasing spectacles to eligible beneficiaries every 5 years.

content.section_title: FAQ: How often can a person avail of this benefit?
content.text: A beneficiary can avail of this benefit only once every five years.

content.section_title: FAQ: How much financial assistance does the scheme provide?
content.text: The scheme provides [?]500/- as financial assistance to eligible beneficiaries for purchasing spectacles.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is the scheme applicable to all age groups?

content.section_title: FAQ: Can a person apply for the scheme without a prescription?

content.section_title: FAQ: The availability of online application options varies. Applicants should contact their local authorities or visit the official website for more information.
content.text: Are there specific sellers or brands from whom the spectacles must be purchased?

content.section_title: FAQ: Are there specific sellers or brands from whom the spectacles must be purchased?

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can be contacted for more information about the scheme?
content.text: Applicants can contact their local government office or visit the official website of the scheme for further details and guidance.

title: West Bengal Transport Workers’ Social Security Scheme: Assistance on Death and Permanent Disablement
topic: West Bengal Transport Workers’ Social Security Scheme: Assistance on Death and Permanent Disablement

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme provides financial relief to the beneficiaries in case of permanent disability due to accidents or their dependents/nominees in case of accidental or natural death.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The “West Bengal Transport Workers’ Social Security Scheme: Assistance on Death and Permanent Disablement” by the Labour Department, offers financial assistance to unorganised transport workers or their nominees/dependents during times of distress caused by death or disability. The relief includes monetary compensation for accidental or natural death, as well as graded benefits for permanent disabilities.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
> **Relief on Death:**

1. **For accidental death:** ₹1,50,000/-.   1. **For natural death:** ₹50,000/-.

> **Relief on Permanent Disability:**

1. **For loss of one hand, one leg below the knee, one eye, or hearing capacity in one ear:** ₹25,000/-.   1. **For loss of a thumb:** ₹9,000/-.   1. **For loss of any other finger:** ₹4,000/-   1. **Maximum relief for total disablement:** ₹50,000/-.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant must be between 18 and 60 years of age at the time of enrolment.
1. The applicant must be an unorganized worker employed in the operation of commercial vehicles, such as cars, taxis, luxury taxis, light commercial vehicles, vans, auto-rickshaws, tempos, lorries, trucks, or buses.
1. The applicant’s vehicle must be used for commercial purposes and not solely for personal or family use.

> **Note:** A nominee/ dependent of a beneficiary shall be entitled for the benefits in case of death.

content.section_title: Exclusions
content.text:
No assistance under this provision shall be allowed if such death or permanent disablement was :

- Caused by intentional self-injury, suicide or attempted suicide, insanity or immorality or if the member was under the influence of intoxicating liquor, drug, or narcotic.
- Caused by injuries resulting from riots, civil commotions, or racing of any kind.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
**Step-1:** Visit the “[eDistrict 2.0](https://edistrict.wb.gov.in/portal/home)” portal. Click on Log In/Sign Up.
**Step-2:** In the User Login window, scroll down to the bottom and click on “Register”.
**Step-3:** Fill in all the details carefully, enter a username, and click on “Register”.
**Step-4:** Upon entering the One Time Password received on your mobile, you will be successfully registered.
**Step-5:** Now in the User Login window, enter your Username or email and captcha and click on “Sign In”.
**Step-6:** Enter the received O.T.P and click on “Submit”.
**Step-7:** Upon successful Login, You will be redirected to the “Dashboard”. From the right side menu apply for the concerned scheme.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
For New Registration:- Certification by Prescribed Authority in the Prescribed format,
- Passport Size Photo (self and for each dependent member).
- Age Proof Document.
- Driving Licence in case of Drivers.
- EPIC/ AADHAR Card.
For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:- Details of the Nominee/ Dependent.
- Death Certificate (for death claims).
- Medical Certificate/ Report (for disability claims).

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the purpose of the scheme?
content.text: The scheme provides financial relief to the beneficiaries in case of permanent disability due to accidents or their dependents/nominees in case of accidental or natural death.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can apply for this scheme?
content.text: Unorganised transport workers aged 18-60 years working with commercial vehicles are eligible.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the benefits provided under the scheme?
content.text: Financial assistance for death ([?]50,000-[?]1,50,000) and permanent disability ([?]4,000-[?]50,000) are provided under the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: How is the assistance disbursed?
content.text: Benefits are transferred directly to the beneficiary's or nominee's bank account through DBT.

content.section_title: FAQ: What documents are required to apply?
content.text: Age proof, employment proof, bank details, and relevant medical or death certificates.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are privately owned vehicles included in the scheme?
content.text: No, vehicles used solely for personal or family purposes are excluded.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any age limit for the scheme?
content.text: Yes, the beneficiary must be between 18 and 60 years of age.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are benefits provided for self-inflicted injuries or suicides?
content.text: No, such cases are excluded.

content.section_title: FAQ: What if multiple disabilities occur in one accident?
content.text: Relief is calculated based on total disablement, subject to a maximum of [?]50,000/-.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is this scheme applicable in cases of natural disasters?
content.text: Yes, provided the death or disability is not due to excluded circumstances.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can family members apply on behalf of the worker?
content.text: Yes, nominees or dependents can apply in case of death or incapacitation.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are accidental injuries under intoxication covered?
content.text: No, injuries occurring under the influence of alcohol, drugs, or narcotics are not covered.

content.section_title: FAQ: What happens if the nominee is not registered?
content.text: The beneficiary should ensure nominee details are updated during registration.

title: West Bengal Transport Workers’ Social Security Scheme: Maternity Benefit
topic: West Bengal Transport Workers’ Social Security Scheme: Maternity Benefit

content.text: This scheme provides financial assistance to a beneficiary on successful delivery of a child or miscarriage by such beneficiary or his wife.

The benefit is limited to two instances per beneficiary to promote maternal health and provide support during critical times.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the “Maternity Benefit” scheme under the “West Bengal Transport Workers’ Social Security Scheme”?
content.text: The "Maternity Benefit" Scheme is a government initiative that provides financial assistance of [?]6,000/- to beneficiaries for childbirth or miscarriage to support maternal and child health.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible to apply for the scheme?
content.text: The scheme is available to pregnant women or their spouses who meet the eligibility criteria.

content.section_title: FAQ: How many times can a beneficiary avail of the scheme?
content.text: A beneficiary can avail of the financial assistance a maximum of 2 times, either for childbirth or miscarriage.

content.section_title: FAQ: Does the scheme provide benefits for miscarriages?
content.text: Yes, the scheme provides financial support for miscarriages, subject to the submission of a valid medical certificate from a registered medical practitioner.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can a beneficiary apply for the scheme?
content.text: Beneficiaries can apply for the scheme through the online e-District portal.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there an income limit to qualify for the scheme?
content.text: No, there is no income limit to qualify for the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can both spouses claim the benefit separately?
content.text: No, the financial assistance is granted to the family and can only be claimed by one beneficiary per instance.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the process to obtain a medical certificate?
content.text: Beneficiaries must visit a registered medical practitioner who will issue the required certificate confirming childbirth or miscarriage.

title: West Bengal Transport Workers’ Social Security Scheme: Medical Benefit for Major Ailments
topic: West Bengal Transport Workers’ Social Security Scheme: Medical Benefit for Major Ailments

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: This scheme provides financial assistance for the treatment of beneficiaries or their family members suffering from major ailments like cancer, TB, brain stroke, cardiac problems, and others.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The “West Bengal Transport Workers’ Social Security Scheme: Medical Benefit for Major Ailments” by the Labour Department, West Bengal, is designed to alleviate the financial burden of medical expenses for individuals and families dealing with severe health conditions. Under this scheme, financial assistance up-to ₹20,000/- is provided for the treatment of beneficiaries or their family members suffering from major ailments Cancer, Leprosy, T.B, brain stroke, cardiac problems, malfunctioning of kidney(s), AIDS, Eye Ailments, Vericosis of Vein, or Thalassemia requiring hospitalisation and treatment. In cases of major surgery, additional relief up to ₹60,000/-may be allowed.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: - **Assistance for Major Ailments:** Up-to ₹20,000/-.  - **Assistance for Surgery:** Up-to ₹60,000/-.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
- The applicant must be between 18 and 60 years of age at the time of enrolment.
- The applicant must be an unorganized worker employed in the operation of commercial vehicles, such as cars, taxis, luxury taxis, light commercial vehicles, vans, auto-rickshaws, tempos, lorries, trucks, or buses.
- The applicant’s vehicle must be used for commercial purposes and not solely for personal or family use.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
**Step 1:** Visit the “[eDistrict 2.0](https://edistrict.wb.gov.in/portal/home)” portal. Click on Log In/Sign Up.
**Step 2:** In the User Login window, scroll down to the bottom and click on “Register”.
**Step 3:** Fill in all the details carefully, enter a username, and click on “Register”.
**Step 4:** Upon entering the One Time Password received on your mobile, you will be successfully registered.
**Step 5:** Now in the User Login window, enter your Username or email and captcha and click on “Sign In”.
**Step 6:** Enter the received O.T.P and click on “Submit”.
**Step 7:** Upon successful Login, You will be redirected to the “Dashboard”. From the right side menu apply for the concerned scheme.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
For New Registration:- Certification by Prescribed Authority in the Prescribed format,
- Passport Size Photo (self and for each dependent member).
- Age Proof Document.
- Driving Licence in case of Drivers.
- EPIC/ AADHAR Card.
For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:- Medical Certificate.
- Hospital Bills,
- Any other documents (if required).

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the purpose of the scheme?
content.text: This scheme provides financial assistance for the treatment of beneficiaries or their family members suffering from major ailments like cancer, TB, brain stroke, cardiac problems, and others.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the maximum financial aid provided under this scheme?
content.text: The scheme offers financial assistance of up to [?]20,000/-. Beside this, In cases of major surgery additional relief up to [?]60,000/- may be allowed.

content.section_title: FAQ: Which illnesses are covered under the scheme?
content.text: Ailments like cancer, leprosy, TB, brain stroke, cardiac problems, kidney malfunction, AIDS, eye ailments, varicosis of veins, and thalassemia.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can this benefit be availed for outpatient treatments?
content.text: No, the scheme is applicable only for hospitalization and treatment.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can multiple family members avail of this benefit?
content.text: Yes, if multiple family members of the beneficiary are affected, they can avail this scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there an age limit for applicants?
content.text: No specific age limit is mentioned under the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What happens if the treatment cost exceeds ₹20,000/-?
content.text: The scheme only covers up to [?]20,000/-; additional expenses must be borne by the beneficiary.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is this scheme applicable to non-residents of the state?
content.text: No, the applicant must be a resident of West Bengal state.

content.section_title: FAQ: What proof of treatment is required for the application?
content.text: Hospital bills, discharge summaries, and medical certificates must be provided.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any annual income limit for eligibility?
content.text: No, there is no specific income criteria to be eligible for this scheme.

title: Workers In Case Of Permanent Disablement-JKBOCWWB
topic: Workers In Case Of Permanent Disablement-JKBOCWWB

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The "Workers In Case of Partial Permanent Disablement" scheme by the JKBOCW Welfare Board, Labour Department, Jammu & Kashmir, provides financial assistance to registered Building and Other Construction (BOC) workers in the case of permanent disablement.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The "Workers In Case of Partial Permanent Disablement" scheme by the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Labour Department, Jammu & Kashmir, under the MUHAFIZ Scheme (PMJJBY: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana, PMSBY: Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana) from 01.01.2018, provides ₹2,00,000 to registered BOC workers in the case of permanent disablement.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: > Under the scheme, ₹2,00,000 is provided to registered BOC workers in case of partial permanent disablement.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
> **For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:**

1. The applicant should be a resident of Jammu & Kashmir.
1. The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker.
1. The applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
1. The applicant should have engaged in any building or other construction work for at least 90 days during the preceding 12 months.
1. The applicant should be registration is renewed annually.

> **For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:**

1. The worker must be a registered member of the Building and Other Construction Workers (BOC) Welfare Board.
1. The scheme applies to workers who suffer from permanent disablement.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
**Registration of a Construction Worker under JKBOCWWB:**
Step 1: Go to the J&K Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (JKBOCWWB) [official website.](https://www.jkbocw.gov.in/Index.aspx)
Step 2: On the homepage, find and click the "[Labour Registration"](https://portal.jkbocw.gov.in/MemberRegistration.aspx) link.
Step 3:If you're a new user, click on "Apply for New Registration."
Step 4: Complete the registration form with your details, submit the form, and send the OTP to your registered mobile number. Enter the OTP and click "Verify OTP & Submit."
Step 5: After the OTP is verified, a confirmation message with your Login ID and password will be sent to your registered mobile number.
Step 6:Use the Login ID, password, and captcha to log in (remember, the username and password are case-sensitive). filled are case sensitive)Read all the instruction carefully and Click to continue
Step 7: Once logged in, choose the "Construction Worker’s Registration" option and click on "Registration Form."
Step 8: A dashboard will appear. Complete the form by entering all required details (marked with *).
Step 9: Upload all the important documents, such as ID proof and other relevant certificates.
Step 10: Once the form is completed and documents uploaded, click "Submit" and pay the application fee online. You will receive a registration number after successful payment and submission.

**Steps to Apply for Scheme Benefits:**
Step1: Go to the [official website ](https://www.jkbocw.gov.in/Index.aspx)of the Jammu & Kashmir Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
Step2: Click on ["Login,"](https://portal.jkbocw.gov.in/LoginGuest.aspx) enter your details, and sign in to your account.
Step3: After logging in, click on "Scheme Registration" and select "Apply for Scheme."
Step4: Choose the scheme you wish to apply for in order to avail the benefits.
Step5: Enter all required information and upload the necessary documents as prompted.
Step6: Finally, click on "Submit Application" to complete the process.

**Application Tracking:**
Applicant can track their application status online through the [official website](https://www.jkbocw.gov.in/Index.aspx) of the  Jammu & Kashmir Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board. Relevant updates will be sent to the registered mobile number.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
> **The applicant should submit the completed application form along with all the required documents to the concerned JKBOCWWB office for processing.**

1. [Application Form](https://www.jkbocw.gov.in/MediaGallery/New%20Registration%20form%204%20.pdf) for Registration as a Beneficiary1. [Application Form](https://www.jkbocw.gov.in/MediaGallery/Disability%20Assistance.pdf) for Scheme

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Original Medical Certificate issued by the Medical Board or competent authority.
- Attested Age Proof of the registered worker.
- Duly attested photocopy of Bank Passbook of the worker or nominee/legal heir, or a cancelled cheque bearing the name of the worker/nominee/legal heir and IFSC code of the bank branch.
- Photocopy of Aadhaar Card/Biometric Card of the registered worker.
- Registration Certificate of the worker under the BOC Welfare Board.
- Proof of employment in the building/construction sector (work records or pay slips).
- Disability Certificate from a recognized medical authority confirming the partial permanent disablement.
- Proof of residence (Voter ID, Ration Card, Utility Bill, etc.) for address verification.
- Photograph of the worker (passport size).
- Income Certificate to establish eligibility under the scheme (if applicable).

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the purpose of the "Workers In Case of Permanent Disablement" scheme?
content.text: The scheme provides financial assistance to registered Building and Other Construction (BOC) workers in case of permanent disablement.

content.section_title: FAQ: How much financial assistance is provided under this scheme?
content.text: A financial assistance of [?]2,00,000 is provided to the worker in case of partial permanent disablement.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is the worker required to be registered with the Welfare Board?
content.text: Yes, the worker must be a registered member of the Building and Other Construction Workers (BOC) Welfare Board.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can a worker register with the BOC Welfare Board?
content.text: The worker should be a resident of Jammu & Kashmir, engaged in construction work for at least 90 days in the preceding 12 months, and renew their registration annually.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the age requirement for registering as a BOC worker?
content.text: The worker must be between 18 and 60 years of age.

content.section_title: FAQ: What documents are required to apply for the scheme?
content.text: Original Medical Certificate from the Medical Board, attested age proof, bank passbook or cancelled cheque, and Aadhar card/biometric card of the worker.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there a minimum period of work required for the worker to qualify?
content.text: Yes, the worker must have worked in construction for at least 90 days in the last 12 months.

content.section_title: FAQ: Does the worker need to provide proof of the disability?
content.text: Yes, the worker must submit an Original Medical Certificate issued by a competent authority to confirm the partial permanent disablement.

content.section_title: FAQ: How will the funds be disbursed to the worker?
content.text: The funds are disbursed to the registered worker's bank account after submission of all required documents.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are there any other conditions for applying under the scheme?
content.text: The worker must be actively engaged in construction work, and the disablement should be partial and permanent, as confirmed by the Medical Board.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to promote holistic health and wellness among people of all age groups through yoga, naturopathy, and personalized counseling.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The “Yoga Wellness Center” is implemented by the Ayush Department, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to promote holistic health and wellness among people of all age groups through yoga, naturopathy, and personalized counseling.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: Under this scheme, Yoga, naturopathy, and counseling services are provided based on the individual’s health condition. For this, the OPD fee is ₹1.00 per person in rural areas and ₹2.00 per person in urban areas.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The beneficiary must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
1. The beneficiary can belong to any age group.
1. The beneficiary must be registered at an identified Government Ayurvedic Centre or Hospital.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text: The beneficiaries may visit and register at an identified Government Ayurvedic Centre or Hospital to avail the benefits under the scheme.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Identity Proof
- Any other document as required

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can benefit from this scheme?
content.text: Any resident of Uttarakhand, irrespective of age, can avail the benefits of the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What services are provided under the Yoga Wellness Center?
content.text: The scheme provides yoga therapy, naturopathy treatment, and personalized counseling based on the individual’s health condition.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the OPD fee for availing services?
content.text: The OPD fee is ₹1.00 per person in rural areas and ₹2.00 per person in urban areas.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where can beneficiaries avail these services?
content.text: Beneficiaries can visit and register at identified Government Ayurvedic Centres or Hospitals in Uttarakhand offering Yoga Wellness Center facilities.

content.section_title: FAQ: Do beneficiaries need to register to avail services?
content.text: Yes, beneficiaries must be registered at an identified Government Ayurvedic Centre or Hospital to avail the benefits.

title: “Home for the Aged" under Grant-in-Aid to Pondicherry Society for the Care of the Aged (PONCARE)
topic: “Home for the Aged" under Grant-in-Aid to Pondicherry Society for the Care of the Aged (PONCARE)

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: Through the scheme “Home for the Aged", the senior citizens are provided with stay at subsidiary rates, food (both vegetarian and non-vegetarian), routine health check up by doctors thrice in a week, providing news papers and television for entertainment.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The sub-scheme “Home for the Aged" under the umbrella scheme "Grant-in-Aid to Pondicherry Society for the Care of the Aged (PONCARE)" was launched by the Department of Social Welfare, Government of Puducherry. Through this scheme, the senior citizens are provided with stay at subsidiary rates, food (both vegetarian and non-vegetarian), routine health check up by doctors thrice in a week, providing news papers and television for entertainment.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
- Stay at Subsidiary Rate of ₹1200/- for the Inmates.
- Food (Both Vegetarian and Non-vegetarian).
- Routine Health Check Up by Doctors Thrice in a Week.
- Newspapers and Television for Entertainment.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
- The applicant should be 60 years or above in age.
- The applicant should be a Native/Resident of the Union Territory of Puducherry for at least 5 years.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Step 1:** The interested applicant should visit (during office hours) the **Pondicherry Society for the care of the Aged (PONCARE), Puducherry, 4th Cross - Sithankudi, 6th Cross Extension, Brindavanam, West, Puducherry - 605 013, +91-413-2245317, +91-413-2246318, +91-9443405499**, and request the hard copy of the prescribed format of the application form from the staff exclusively entrusted to issue and collect filled-in applications.
**Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste the passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
**Step 3:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to The Secretary, PONCARE.
**Step 4:** Request a receipt or acknowledgement from the concerned authority to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Aadhaar Card.
- Birth Certificate / Age Proof.
- Residence-cum-Nativity Certificate, issued by the competent authority.
- Recent Passport-Size Photograph.Bank Passbook.

content.text: Inmates enjoy a stay at a subsidiary rate of [?]1200 per month.

content.section_title: FAQ: What services are provided to senior citizens under this scheme?
content.text: Senior citizens receive food, routine health check-ups, newspapers, and television for entertainment.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can you specify the age requirement for applicants?
content.text: Applicants must be 60 years or above to be eligible for the scheme.

content.text: For more information, contact the Secretary, Pondicherry Society for the care of the Aged (PONCARE).

content.section_title: FAQ: What contact details are available for inquiries about the scheme?
FAQ: What health services are part of the scheme?
content.text: Inmates receive routine health check-ups by doctors three times a week.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is the stay rate inclusive of meals for the inmates?
content.text: Yes, the stay rate of [?]1200 includes both vegetarian and non-vegetarian meals.

content.section_title: FAQ: What entertainment facilities are provided to the senior citizens?
content.text: Senior citizens have access to newspapers and television for entertainment.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the geographical scope of this scheme?
content.text: The scheme is for residents of the Union Territory of Puducherry.

content.section_title: FAQ: How often do doctors conduct health check-ups for the inmates?
content.text: Doctors conduct routine health check-ups for the inmates three times a week.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can applicants from outside Puducherry apply for this scheme?
content.text: No, applicants must be residents of the Union Territory of Puducherry for at least 5 years.

title: “Family Counseling" under Grant-in-Aid to Pondicherry State Social Welfare Board
topic: “Family Counseling" under Grant-in-Aid to Pondicherry State Social Welfare Board

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: “Family Counseling" under the umbrella scheme "Grant-in-Aid to Pondicherry State Social Welfare Board" was launched by the Department of Social Welfare, Government of Puducherry. Through this scheme, the residents of Puducherry are provided with counselling, rehabilitative and referral services.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The sub-scheme “Family Counseling" under the umbrella scheme "Grant-in-Aid to Pondicherry State Social Welfare Board" was launched by the Department of Social Welfare, Government of Puducherry. Through this scheme, the residents of Puducherry are provided with counselling, rehabilitative and referral services.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
- Counselling, rehabilitative and referral services are provided in the centres for the victims of social problems.
- Psychological, Social, Educational and Economic issues of the individuals are also dealt with.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text: The applicant should be a Native/Resident of the Union Territory of Puducherry.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Step 1:** The interested applicant should visit (during office hours) the **Secretary, State Social Welfare Board,17, Deivasigamani Nagar, Sanjay Gandhi Nagar, Ilango Nagar, Puducherry, 605013**, and request the hard copy of the prescribed format of the application form from the staff exclusively entrusted to issue and collect filled-in applications.
**Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste the passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
**Step 3:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form and the documents to The Secretary, State Social Welfare Board.
**Step 4:** Request a receipt or acknowledgement from the concerned authority to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).

content.section_title: Documents Required

content.section_title: FAQ: Are the counselling services limited to specific social issues?

content.section_title: FAQ: Where can one find the State Social Welfare Board's office for inquiries?
content.text: The office is located at 17, Deivasigamani Nagar, Sanjay Gandhi Nagar, Ilango Nagar, Puducherry, 605013.

content.section_title: FAQ: What issues are addressed as part of the counselling services?
content.text: Psychological, social, educational, and economic issues of individuals are comprehensively addressed.

content.section_title: FAQ: What services are offered to individuals facing social problems under this scheme?
content.text: Centres provide counselling, rehabilitation, and referral services for victims of social problems.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who oversees the "Family Counseling" sub-scheme in Puducherry?
content.text: The oversight is managed by the Secretary, State Social Welfare Board, Puducherry.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can individuals address grievances related to the scheme?
content.text: Grievances can be addressed through the Grievance Redressal portal at https://pgportal.gov.in/.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is There Any Application Fee?
content.text: No, applicants can rest assured that the entire application process is entirely free of cost. There are no fees associated with applying for this scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is There Any Compensation For The Delay In The Disbursal Of The Scheme Benefits?
content.text: The guidelines do not mention any provision for compensation in the event of delayed disbursement of scheme benefits. Applicants should note that compensation for delays is not part of the scheme's framework.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can Applicants From The Neighboring States/UTs Also Apply To This Scheme?
content.text: This scheme is exclusively available to permanent residents of the Puducherry. Applicants from neighbouring States/UTs, among others, are not eligible to apply.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there an application deadline for this scheme?
content.text: The scheme guidelines do not mention any opening date or closing date for applications.

title: Silicosis (Bimari) Se Pidit Shramiko Ke Liye Aarthik Evam Punarvas Sahayata Yojana
topic: Silicosis (Bimari) Se Pidit Shramiko Ke Liye Aarthik Evam Punarvas Sahayata Yojana

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: Chhattisgarh state Government started a scheme "Silicosis (Bimari) Se Pidit Shramiko Ke Liye Aarthik Evam Punarvas Sahayata Yojana" for unorganized worker on 16 January 2018.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: Labor Department of Chhattisgarh State started this scheme "Silicosis (Bimari) Se Pidit Shramiko Ke Liye Aarthik Evam Punarvas Sahayata Yojana" for unorganized worker on 16 April 2018. The main objective of the scheme to provide the financial assistance to the person who are suffering from Silicosis disease.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
- Unorganized workers with silicosis will get ₹ 300,000.
- If the worker dies, their family will get ₹100,000 and the rest will be paid as a fixed deposit with monthly interest.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. Workers registered with the Chhattisgarh Unorganized Workers State Social Security Board.
1. Workers who claim to be working in the organized sector but cannot prove it, or construction workers who are not eligible for registration with the Chhattisgarh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, will also get the benefits.
1. Competent doctors must confirm the Silicosis disease  to the worker.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
1. Visit the office of the Assistant Labor Commissioner/Labor Officer of the district.
1. Submit the application and the required documents.
1. The officer will review the application and send it to the divisional office for further action.
1. Applicant can be submitted by mother, father, wife, husband, minor son, or minor daughter.
1. The application should include the following information:

1. Name and address of the deceased worker
1. Name and address of the dependent
1. Occupation of the deceased worker
1. Date of death of the deceased worker
1. Medical report confirming that the deceased worker had silicosis

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Labor registration Card.
- Resident prove.
- Name and address of the deceased worker
- Name and address of the dependent
- Occupation of the deceased worker
- Date of death of the deceased worker
- Medical report confirming that the deceased worker had silicosis

content.section_title: FAQ: What is this scheme ?
content.text: Chhattisgarh state Government started a scheme "Silicosis (Bimari) Se Pidit Shramiko Ke Liye Aarthik Evam Punarvas Sahayata Yojana" for unorganized worker on 16 January 2018.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the objective of the scheme?
content.text: The main objective of the scheme to provide the financial assistance to the person/Family who are suffering from Silicosis disease.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the benefits?
content.text: Provide the Financial Assistance of [?] 3,00,000/-

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can get the benefits?
content.text: All eligible registered unorganized labor individuals from Chhattisgarh State.

content.section_title: FAQ: Will the benefit amount be paid in one go to the family?
content.text: If the worker dies, their family will get [?]100,000 and the rest will be paid as a fixed deposit with monthly interest.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can unregistered workers also get benefits?
content.text: Yes, but they have to fist register them self.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the procedure to apply for the scheme?
content.text:
1. Visit the office of the Assistant Labor Commissioner/Labor Officer of the district.
2. Submit the application and the required documents.
3. The officer will review the application and send it to the divisional office for further action.
4. Applicant can be submitted by mother, father, wife, husband, minor son, or minor daughter.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can applicant register under Chhattisgarh Unorganized Workers State Social Security Board" ?
content.text:
1. All the applicant have to visit the official website Chhattisgarh Labour Department.
2. On the home page click on "Apply" under "Chhattisgarh Unorganized Workers State Social Security Board".
3. Enter the following details,
Select Group Name : "Asangathit Marmakaar Mandal"
Select Service : "Asangathit Shramik Panjikaran"
What do you want to do :  "Aavedan"Click next.
4. Check the eligibility criteria for registration in Chhattisgarh Unorganized Workers State Social Security Board and click next.
5. Fill out the online "Registration form".
6. Submit.

content.section_title: FAQ: What documents are required for the scheme?
