1. Labor registration Card.
2. Resident Documents.
3. Name and address of the deceased worker
4. Name and address of the dependent
5. Occupation of the deceased worker
6. Date of death of the deceased worker
7. Medical report confirming that the deceased worker had silicosis.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the official website for registration?
content.text: URL : https://cglabour.nic.in/

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to heirs of workers engaged in Akik occupation who died due to silicosis disease. It benefits the families of deceased workers with monetary compensation of ₹1,00,000/- as support for occupational disease-related deaths.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: Launched in 2006, the scheme "Silicosis Financial Assistance Scheme" by the Gujarat Rural Workers Welfare Board, Labour and Employment Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to provide financial assistance to the heirs of self-reliant persons carrying on occupation of Akik who suffer from the serious disease of silicosis in the state, addressing the occupational health hazards faced by workers in the Akik industry. The scheme provides financial assistance of ₹1,00,000/- to the heirs of affected workers as a security measure and compensation for the occupational disease. The applicant must be the heir of a self-reliant person who was engaged in Akik occupation and died due to silicosis disease in Gujarat state. The scheme is implemented by Gujarat Rural Workers Welfare Board under Labour and Employment Department. The applications for this scheme are accepted through the prescribed process.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
**Financial Assistance:** ₹1,00,000/- (one lakh rupees) per case.
**Purpose:** Financial assistance to heirs of deceased workers who died due to silicosis.
**Nature:** One-time payment as compensation.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
- The applicant must be the legal heir of a deceased person who was engaged in Akik occupation.
- The applicant must be the heir of a self-reliant person who carried on occupation of Akik in Gujarat state.
- The applicant must be the heir of a person who died due to serious disease of silicosis.
- The applicant must be the heir of a worker whose death occurred due to occupational silicosis while engaged in Akik work.
- The applicant must establish the occupational nature of the silicosis disease in relation to Akik work.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Step 1:** The interested applicant should visit the Office of the Government Labour Officer, and request the hard copy of the prescribed format of the application form from the staff exclusively entrusted to issue and collect filled-in applications.
**Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste the passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
**Step 3:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents, within the prescribed period (if any), to the authority given below -
Office of the Government Labour Officer (Rural) in the case of worker living in rural area.
Office of the Government Labour Officer (Industry) in the case of worker living in urban area.
**Step 4:** Request a receipt or acknowledgement from the concerned authority to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).
**Step 5:** The concerned authorities will verify the submitted documents and establish the eligibility of the applicant as legal heir and confirm the occupational nature of silicosis disease.
**Step 6:** The board will assess the case to confirm that the deceased was engaged in Akik occupation and that the death was due to silicosis disease as per medical evidence.
**Step 7:** Upon successful verification and assessment, the case will be processed for approval and financial sanction by the competent authority.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Death Certificate of The Worker
- Medical Certificate Confirming Silicosis as Cause of Death
- Legal Heir Certificate
- Proof of Employment in Akik Occupation
- Identity Proof of The Applicant (Heir)
- Address Proof of The Applicant
- Medical Records Establishing Silicosis Disease
- Employment Records or Proof of Akik Occupation of The Deceased

content.section_title: FAQ: What documents do I need to provide to prove that my deceased family member worked in the Akik occupation?
content.text: You must submit employment records or proof of Akik occupation of the deceased, along with medical records establishing silicosis disease and proof of employment in Akik occupation.

content.section_title: FAQ: How much financial compensation will I receive if my application for assistance is approved by the authorities?
content.text: The scheme provides a one-time financial assistance of ₹1,00,000/- (one lakh rupees) per case to the heirs of deceased workers.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can I apply for this financial assistance if my family member died from silicosis but worked in a different industry?
content.text: No, the deceased person must have been specifically engaged in Akik occupation and died due to occupational silicosis while working in this field.

content.section_title: FAQ: What type of medical documentation is required to establish that silicosis was the cause of my relative's death?
content.text: You need to submit a medical certificate confirming silicosis as the cause of death and medical records establishing the silicosis disease.

content.section_title: FAQ: Do I need to prove that I am the legal heir of the deceased worker to qualify for this assistance?
content.text: Yes, you must provide a legal heir certificate as part of the required documentation to establish your relationship with the deceased worker.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can family members of workers who died from silicosis in other states apply for this financial assistance program?
content.text: No, the applicant must be the heir of a person who died due to silicosis while engaged in Akik work specifically in Gujarat state.

content.section_title: FAQ: What personal identification documents do I need to submit along with my application for this compensation scheme?
content.text: You must provide identity proof of the applicant (heir) and address proof of the applicant as part of the required documentation.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is this financial assistance provided multiple times or is it a single payment to the family of the deceased?
content.text: This is a one-time payment of ₹1,00,000/- provided as compensation upon death of worker due to silicosis disease.

content.section_title: FAQ: What specific occupational connection must I establish between the deceased worker's silicosis and their work to qualify for assistance?
content.text: You must establish the occupational nature of the silicosis disease in relation to Akik work and prove the worker died due to occupational silicosis.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can I apply for this assistance if my family member was employed by a company in Akik industry rather than being self-employed?
content.text: No, the scheme specifically covers heirs of self-reliant persons who carried on the occupation of Akik, not employees of companies.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the primary purpose of this financial assistance scheme and what type of workers does it aim to help?
content.text: The scheme aims to provide compensation for occupational disease-related deaths, specifically helping families of workers who died from silicosis contracted during Akik occupation.

content.section_title: FAQ: How long has this financial assistance program been available to help families affected by occupational silicosis in the Akik industry?
content.text: The scheme was launched in 2006 by the Gujarat Rural Workers Welfare Board under the Labour and Employment Department, Government of Gujarat.

title: Silicosis Pidit Hita-dhikariyon Hetu Sahayata Yojana
topic: Silicosis Pidit Hita-dhikariyon Hetu Sahayata Yojana

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The Silicosis Affected Workers Welfare Scheme is an initiative launched by the Government of Rajasthan to provide financial assistance to workers suffering from silicosis and their families.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: "Silicosis Pidit Hita-dhikariyon Hetu Sahayata Yojana" has been started for the laborers working in stone mines in Rajasthan. An assistance scheme has been started for the beneficiaries suffering from silicosis. In which financial assistance will be provided to laborers and workers in case of silicosis or death. Applicants can apply for the assistance scheme to silicosis victims through both online and offline modes.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
1. In case of suffering from silicosis : ₹1,00,000/-
1. In case of death of a victim of silicosis : ₹3,00,000/-

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. Those construction workers who are registered with the Board as beneficiaries and are depositing contributions
1. The beneficiary is suffering from silicosis as certified by the Pneumoconiosis Medical Board constituted under the Rajasthan Workmen's Compensation (Occupational Diseases) Rules, 1965.
1. The beneficiary cannot receive silicosis assistance amount from the Rajasthan Environment and Health Care Fund (Rehab).

**Note - A person suffering from silicosis will be eligible to get assistance only under any one of the schemes of Rajasthan Environment and Health Cess Fund or the Board.**

content.section_title: Exclusions
content.text: > Those workers who work in mines and quarries and on whom The provisions of the Mines Act, 1952 apply and buildings or other construction works not included in the definition of building or other construction work in Section 2 (d) of the Act, will not be eligible to receive assistance from the Board.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
> **Registration**

**Step-1:** Applicant have to visit the [official portal](https://sso.rajasthan.gov.in/signin).
**Step-2:** Click on the option “[Register](https://sso.rajasthan.gov.in/register)”.
**Step-3:** Then you will be redirected to the SSO registration page. The registration page will appear with the following options.

-   Citizen

**Step-4:** Choose the either one option from  the Jan Aadhaar Or Google to process further.

- Jan Aadhaar : Enter the Jan Aadhaar number, click on the ‘Next’ button,

- Select your name, the name of the head of the family and all the other members and
- Click on the ‘Send OTP’ button. Enter the ‘OTP’ and
- Click on the ‘Verify OTP’ button to Complete the registration.

- Google : Enter the Gmail ID, click on the ‘Next’ button,

- Enter the password.
- A new link appear on screen, now click on new SSO link.
- SSO id will appear on screen, now create the password.
- Enter Mobile number, click on registration.

> **Apply**

**Step-1:** Applicant have to visit the [official portal](https://sso.rajasthan.gov.in/signin).
**Step-2:** After login, dashboard will open.
**Step-3:** Click on “LDMS” option.
**Step-4:** In LDMS, select "Welfare Schemes" from the side menu and select BOCW Welfare Board.
**Step-5:** Now click on "Apply for Scheme" on screen.
**Step-6:** In this new page, will get a list of names of all the schemes of Rajasthan Shramik Card, out of which click on the name of " Yojana"
**Step-7:** Fill out the all relevant detail.
**Step-8:** Submit.

>  **Periodd for submission of application: Application for financial assistance can be made for a period of 6 months from the date of issuance ofther  certificate by the Pneumoconiosis Medical Board and in case of death, for 6 months from the date of death. In special circumstances, the time limit can be relaxed by the Divisional Secretary after a satisfactory and appropriate reason for the delay is explained.**

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
-  Copy of beneficiary identity card/booklet.
-  Certificate regarding silicosis from Pneumoconiosis Medical Board.
-  Bank account details.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the objective of the scheme?
content.text: The Silicosis Affected Workers Welfare Scheme is an initiative launched by the Government of Rajasthan to provide financial assistance to workers suffering from silicosis and their families.

content.section_title: FAQ: What benefits are provided under the scheme?
content.text: Financial assistance will be provided.

content.section_title: FAQ: What happens in silicosis?
content.text: Permanent scarring on the lungs caused by inhaling silica (quart) dust is called silicosis. People have difficulty breathing during exercise, which sometimes turns into shortness of breath even at rest.

content.section_title: FAQ: How much financial assistance will be provided in the case of death?
content.text: In case of death of a victim of silicosis will get the [?]3,00,000/-

content.section_title: FAQ: What amount of financial assistance is available to individuals diagnosed with silicosis?
content.text: In case of suffering from silicosis [?]1,00,000/-

content.section_title: FAQ: What documents are required while applying?
content.text:
1. Copy of beneficiary identity card/booklet.
2. Certificate regarding silicosis from Pneumoconiosis Medical Board.
3. Bank account details

content.section_title: FAQ: Can applicant get the same benefits from other schemes also?
content.text: The beneficiary cannot receive silicosis assistance amount from the Rajasthan Environment and Health Care Fund (Rehab).

content.section_title: FAQ: Is this only applicable in Rajasthan?
content.text: Yes, the scheme is currently limited to Rajasthan. Similar schemes, with varied objectives and components, may be implemented in other states.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can applicant apply online ?
content.text: Applicants have to visit the official portal. https://sso.rajasthan.gov.in/ and follow the steps.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: “Sishu Saathi”, a scheme of the Health and Family Welfare Department, Government of West Bengal as part of the Rastriya Bal Swasthya Karyakram (RBSK) under National Health Mission is meant to treat Indian children of 0-18 years age group with zero out of pocket expenditure.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The 'Sishu Saathi' scheme is a healthcare initiative launched by the Health and Family Welfare Department, Government of West Bengal, that aims to provide free treatment to children needing heart surgeries. Partnering with both government and private hospitals, the scheme ensures that children up to the age of 12, diagnosed with heart conditions, receive free surgery and related care.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: - **Health Screening in Schools:** Free health check-ups in government schools to detect heart ailments at an early stage among children.  - **Free Cardiac Surgeries:** Completely free cardiac surgeries for children up to 12 years of age who are diagnosed with heart ailments, ensuring no financial burden on families.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
- All children from 0 to 18 years are eligible.
- Applicable to children residing in both rural and urban areas of West Bengal.
- There is no income criterion; the scheme is open to all children regardless of their family's financial capacity.
- The child must be diagnosed with a cardiac ailment requiring surgical intervention.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Step 1:** Children diagnosed through Rashtriya Bal Swasthya Karyakram or other health check-ups are referred for further treatment.
**Step 2:** Parents or guardians can contact government hospitals or partner private hospitals for free surgery under the scheme.
**Step 3:** Hospitals handle the registration and approval process for eligible children.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Child’s birth certificate or age proof (below 18 years).
- Medical records or diagnostic reports showing the heart ailment.
- Identity proof of parents or guardians (Aadhaar card, Voter ID, etc.).
- Any other documents as required.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the "Sishu Saathi" Scheme?
content.text: The "Sishu Saathi" Scheme is a health initiative launched by the Government of West Bengal to provide free cardiac surgeries and treatment to children aged 0 to 18 years, irrespective of their financial background.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the main objective of the "Sishu Saathi" Scheme?
content.text: The main objective of the scheme is to offer free treatment to children with cardiac and congenital conditions, ensuring that no child is denied surgery due to financial constraints.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible for the "Sishu Saathi" Scheme?
content.text: All children between 0 to 18 years of age, living in rural or urban areas of West Bengal, are eligible for the scheme regardless of their family income.

content.section_title: FAQ: Which medical conditions are covered under the Scheme?
content.text: The scheme primarily covers paediatric cardiac conditions and is being expanded to include surgeries for congenital cataract, cleft lips, and cleft palates.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are there any income criteria to avail benefits under this scheme?
content.text: No, the scheme does not have any income criteria. Children from all economic backgrounds are eligible.

content.section_title: FAQ: Which hospitals provide treatment under the "Sishu Saathi" Scheme?
content.text: Treatment is provided at both government and empanelled private hospitals including BM Birla Heart Research Centre, RN Tagore Institute of Cardiac Sciences, Mission Hospital (Durgapur), B.C. Roy Memorial Hospital for Children, and others.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can a child be identified for treatment under the scheme?
content.text: Children are identified through school health screenings under the Rashtriya Bal Swasthya Karyakram or referred by medical professionals from government hospitals.

content.section_title: FAQ: Does the scheme cover the cost of surgery and post-operative care?
content.text: Yes, the West Bengal Government bears all expenses related to the surgery, hospital stay, and post-operative care.

content.section_title: FAQ: What steps has the government taken to identify children in need of treatment?
content.text: The government conducts regular health screenings in schools to identify children with cardiac or congenital issues and refers them for further treatment.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where can parents apply or seek help for enrolling their child in the scheme?
content.text: Parents or guardians can contact government hospitals or partner private hospitals for free surgery under the scheme.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: "Sneha Sparsha" is a healthcare scheme by the Dept. of Health & Family Welfare, Govt. of Assam. In this scheme, financial assistance shall be provided for specialized treatment of children below 12 years of age affected with some serious ailments.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: Launched on 30th December 2021, the "Sneha Sparsha" is a healthcare scheme by the Dept. of Health & Family Welfare, Govt. of Assam. In this scheme, financial assistance shall be provided for specialized treatment of children below 12 years of age affected with some serious ailments. Families with annual income less than ₹ 2,50,000 shall be eligible for availing benefits under the scheme, while priority shall be given to Below Poverty Line (BPL) families.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
> **Permitted procedure and the maximum amount admissible:**

**Bone Marrow Transplant:** Leukemia (Blood Cancer) and other conditions like Thalassemia, in which Bone Marrow Transplant may be considered as a mode of treatment, financial assistance up to ₹ 10,00,000 per case for referral of such cases to 'leading Hospitals outside the state equipped with facilities for such treatment.

**Cochlear Implant:** The scheme will offer financial assistance of up to ₹ 5,35,000 per case to BPL beneficiaries and ₹ 3,00,000 per case to APL beneficiaries for referral of such cases to a Hospital outside the state, if necessary.

**Liver Transplant:** The scheme will offer financial assistance of up to ₹ 16,00,000 per case to BPL beneficiaries and ₹ 10,00,000 per case to APL beneficiaries. Necessary arrangements shall be made for referral of such cases to leading Hospitals outside the state with state-of-the-art facilities for such treatment.

**Kidney Transplant:** The scheme will offer financial assistance of up to ₹ 3,00,000 per case to BPL beneficiaries and ₹ 2,00,000 per case to APL beneficiaries for referral of such cases to Hospitals outside the state, if necessary.

**Artificial Limb:** The scheme will offer financial assistance of up to ₹ 1,00,000 for Cosmetic Limbs and up to ₹ 2,50,000 if a Motorized Artificial Limb is considered necessary.

**Blood Cancer:** For treatment of Blood Cancer in children with Chemotherapy, financial assistance of up to ₹ 1,00,000 per case will be given.

**Specialized Eye Surgery:** like Retinal Surgery or Posterior Segment Procedures, an amount of ₹ 15,000 will be given as assistance.

**Tumors:** Solid tumors needing costly investigations and treatment with Chemotherapy and Radiotherapy will be given financial assistance of up to ₹ 25,000. Children in need of cancer-related surgery will be given financial assistance of up to ₹ 50,000.

**Neurological Anomalies:** Hydrocephalus and Spina Bifida will be given assistance up to ₹ 50,000 for investigation, surgical treatment, and rehabilitation.

**Thalassemia:** Financial assistance of ₹ 1,00,000 will be provided to children suffering from Thalassemia, who are not fit for Bone Marrow Transplant but require specialized treatment like splenectomy, blood transfusion, and associated medicines.

**NOTE 1:** The rates notified are indicative and the actual admissible amount shall be fixed through a transparent process.
**NOTE 2:** Out of these, the first five points shall be considered for referral outside the State, and the rest of the procedures (6 to 10) treatment is available in the Medical College Hospitals and other Hospitals inside the state.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The beneficiary should be a child below 12 years of age.
1. The beneficiary should be a resident of the state of Assam.
1. The beneficiary should be affected by some serious ailment(s).
1. The annual income of the family of the beneficiary should be less than ₹ 5,00,000, while priority shall be given to Below Poverty Line (BPL) families.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
1. The applications from the parents of the children in need of financial assistance under this scheme shall be submitted to the Office of the Mission Director and/or to the Sneha Sparsha Committee, Sneha Sparsha C/o Gauhati Medical College.
1. The applicants shall have to appear before the Sneha Sparsha Committee along with the patient on an appointed date to be notified by the Member Secretary, and submit all necessary documents for verification. The final selection shall be done by Sneha Sparsha Committee on the basis of the documents received.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Aaadhaar Card
- Birth Certificate of the Patient.
- Residential Proof  / Domicile Certificate of Assam
- Income Certificate (issued by a competent authority such as the Deputy Commissioner, Sub-Divisional Circle Officer, circle officer, Block Development officer, or any other authority notified by Govt.)
- Photograph of the Patient (Attested by Doctor)
- OPD Advice Slip / Discharge Summary / Prescription

content.section_title: FAQ: When Was The "Sneha Sparsha" Scheme Announced?
content.text: The "Sneha Sparsha" was announced on 30th December 2021.

content.section_title: FAQ: Which Department Launched The "Sneha Sparsha" Scheme?
content.text: The "Sneha Sparsha" scheme was launched by the Dept. of Health & Family Welfare, Govt. of Assam.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who Are The Target Beneficiaries Of This Scheme?
content.text: In this scheme, financial assistance shall be provided for specialized treatment of children below 12 years of age affected with some serious ailments.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is There Any Reservation/Preference/Priority/Earmarking In The Selection Of The Beneficiary?
content.text: Families with annual income less than [?] 5,00,000 shall be eligible for availing benefits under the scheme, while priority shall be given to Below Poverty Line (BPL) families.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who Should Be Issuing The Income Certificate?
content.text: The Income Certificate should be issued by a competent authority such as the Deputy Commissioner, Sub-Divisional Circle Officer, circle officer, Block Development officer, or any other authority notified by Govt.

content.section_title: FAQ: What Is The Maximum Amount Of Financial Assistance In The Case Of A Bone Marrow Transplant?
content.text: For children with Leukemia (Blood Cancer) and other conditions like Thalassemia, in which Bone Marrow Transplant may be considered as a mode of treatment, financial assistance up to [?] 10,00,000 per case for referral of such cases to 'leading Hospitals outside the state equipped with facilities for such treatment.

content.section_title: FAQ: What Is The Maximum Amount Of Financial Assistance In The Case Of Blood Cancer?
content.text: For treatment of Blood Cancer in children with Chemotherapy, financial assistance of up to [?] 1,00,000 per case will be given.

content.section_title: FAQ: Will The Cost Of Travel And Stay For Obtaining Treatment Be Borne By The Scheme?
content.text: No, the cost of travel and stay for obtaining treatment estimate from a Referral Hospital, shall not be borne by the Scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What Is The Maximum Amount Of Financial Assistance In The Case Of A Kidney Transplant?
content.text: Children in need of a Renal Transplant will be given financial assistance up to [?] 3,00,000 per case to BPL beneficiaries and [?] 2,00,000 per case to APL beneficiaries for referral of such cases to Hospitals outside the state, if necessary.

content.section_title: FAQ: To Whom Should The Application For The "Sneha Sparsha" Scheme Be Submitted?
content.text: The applications from the parents of the children in need of financial assistance under this scheme shall be submitted to the Office of the Mission Director and/or to the Sneha Sparsha Committee, Sneha Sparsha C/o Gauhati Medical College.

content.section_title: FAQ: What Is The Full Form Of "OPD" In The OPD Advice Slip?
content.text: The full form of OPD is Outpatient Department.

title: Sparsh Abhiyan: Surgery to Prevent Disability of Poor and Destitute Disabled People of Madhya Pradesh
topic: Sparsh Abhiyan: Surgery to Prevent Disability of Poor and Destitute Disabled People of Madhya Pradesh

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to provide surgical treatment to prevent disability among poor and destitute disabled persons. Benefits include financial assistance/reimbursement for various surgeries related to visual, hearing, bone impairment, and leprosy, available to eligible residents of Madhya Pradesh.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The scheme “Sparsh Abhiyan: Surgery to Prevent Disability of Poor and Destitute Disabled People of Madhya Pradesh” is launched by the Department of Social Justice and Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities, Government of Madhya Pradesh. The scheme aims to provide financial assistance for the surgery of destitute and poor people, specifically to prevent disability in such individuals in Madhya Pradesh. The scheme provides reimbursement of expenses incurred on the surgery of eligible disabled persons. The scheme is implemented by the District Administration, with application submission handled by the Joint Director/Deputy Director, Panchayat and Social Justice at the district level. The applications for this scheme are accepted at the District Office of Panchayat and Social Justice.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
- Provision of financial assistance for surgery of destitute and poor people.
- Reimbursement of expenses incurred on surgery of disabled persons identified by the District Administration from the destitute funds collected at the district level under Madhya Pradesh Nirashrit Evam Nirdhan Vyakti Sahayata Niyam 1999 (as per rules amended from time to time).
- Reimbursement of expenses incurred on surgery of visually impaired/low vision disabled persons from the funds received by the Public Health and Family Welfare Department under the National Blindness Prevention Program.
- Reimbursement of expenses may be sourced from the funds of District Rogi Kalyan Samiti, Treasury of District Red Cross Society, or the amount collected from social workers and donors.

##### _Intended Use (Specific Surgeries Covered)_

- **Surgery for Visually Impaired/Low Vision Persons:** **** Intraocular lens implant, Corneoplasty, Corneal repair- **Surgery for Hearing Impaired Persons:** **** Cochlear Implant, Tympanic Membrane Repair, Mastwired Surgery- **Surgery of Leprosy-affected Persons:** **** Reconstructive surgery Head, Reconstructive surgery Foot- **Surgery of Bone-Impaired Persons:** **** SP Nail Operation, Orthosis, External Fixation, Replacement Implant, Surgery for Knee Hip and Ankle Correction, Shoulder Elbow and Wrist Correction, Post-Polio Correction Surgery, Construction Repair, Ligament Repair, Soft Tissue Release Surgery, Construction Correction Surgery, Elizarobe Lengthening and Corrective Surgery

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
- The applicant must be a resident of Madhya Pradesh.

- The applicant must be a disabled person.
- The applicant must have a disability as defined in the "Persons with Disabilities (Equal Opportunities, Protection of Rights and Full Participation) Act 1995".
- The applicant’s guardians/guardians' annual income must not exceed ₹96,000/-.
- The applicant must have surgery recommended by a subject specialist doctor.
- The applicant’s surgery must be recommended by a subject specialist surgeon.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
##### **Step 1:** **** ***Application Submission to District Authorities***

- Such eligible disabled persons must obtain and fill out the required [application form](https://sparsh.mp.gov.in/Common/SJ/PDFFiles/form-sgry.PDF), which includes providing personal details, residency information, citizenship status, family details, disability nature and percentage, annual income proof, and details of the recommended surgery. - The applicant must submit their complete application along with all required documents to the Joint Director or Deputy Director, Panchayat and Social Justice at the district level.

##### **Step 2:** **** ***Submission to Medical Authorities for Authentication***

- The applications that have been received from each side by the District Magistrate will subsequently be submitted to the nearest Medical College or District Hospital for official authentication and recommendation.
- The hospital or institution must also provide a recommendation that includes the estimated expenditure on the surgical treatment.

##### **Step 3:** **** ***Approval by District Collector***

- Based on the necessary recommendation provided by the Medical College or District Hospital, the proposed surgery will proceed only upon receiving the final approval from the District Collector.

##### **Step 4:** **** ***Reimbursement/Financial Assistance Disbursement***

- Financial assistance is provided through the reimbursement of expenses incurred on the surgery of eligible disabled persons.
- The funds for reimbursement are drawn from various sources, including destitute funds collected at the district level, Public Health and Family Welfare Department funds, District Rogi Kalyan Samiti funds, District Red Cross Society Treasury funds, or amounts collected from social workers and donors.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Disability Certificate, Issued by the District Medical Board.
- Residence Certificate, Issued by the Municipal Body or Gram Panchayat.
- Income Certificate (Must be provided by a person or officer authorized by the Social Justice Department, showing annual income from all sources).
- Recommendation For Surgery by a Medical Expert.
- Two Photographs Of The Disabled Person (Latest certified photo must be pasted).
- Proof Of Age
- Identity Proof
- Details Of Surgery.
- Recommendation Of The Hospital/Institution (Including estimated expenditure on surgery, signed and sealed by the Superintendent, Incharge, or Director of the institution).

content.section_title: FAQ: I have completed my application form and gathered all the necessary documents; where exactly should I submit these materials at the district level?
content.text: Applications must be submitted to the Joint Director or the Deputy Director, Panchayat and Social Justice, at the appropriate district level office.

content.section_title: FAQ: Which specific governmental act or official definition must my disability align with in order for me to be considered eligible for surgical assistance?
content.text: The applicant must possess a disability as defined specifically under Section-2 of the Persons with Disabilities (Equal Opportunities, Protection of Rights and Full Participation) Act 1995.

content.section_title: FAQ: After the medical college or district hospital authenticates my application and provides a recommendation, who is responsible for granting the final approval for my surgery?
content.text: Based on the authentication and recommendation received from the medical institution, the surgery requires final approval from the District Collector.

content.section_title: FAQ: I need to prove my residence in the state of Madhya Pradesh; what types of official certificates are acceptable for this purpose?
content.text: You must submit a Residence Certificate that has been issued by either the local Municipal Body or the relevant Gram Panchayat.

content.section_title: FAQ: My guardians' combined annual earnings are slightly high; what is the maximum permissible income limit for a family to qualify for this essential surgical assistance?
content.text: To be eligible for this assistance, the annual income of the applicant’s guardians or guardian must not exceed ₹96,000/- (ninety-six thousand Indian Rupees).

content.section_title: FAQ: If an applicant has hearing impairment, what are the specific types of surgery that are covered under the scope of this financial assistance provision?
content.text: Surgical options for hearing impaired persons include Cochlear Implant procedures, Tympanic Membrane Repair, and necessary Mastwired Surgery.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are some examples of the types of surgical procedures available for applicants who are visually impaired or suffering from low vision conditions?
content.text: Covered procedures for visual impairment include the Intraocular lens implant, Corneo plasticity procedures, and necessary Corneal repair.

content.section_title: FAQ: To prove the existence and nature of my impairment, what is the mandatory document I need to obtain and include with my application submission?
content.text: You must submit the official Disability Certificate that has been duly issued by the authorized District Medical Board.

content.section_title: FAQ: How do I ensure that the specific surgery recommended by my doctor is covered by the provisions outlined in the scheme rules and guidelines?
content.text: The surgery recommended for the eligible disabled person must specifically be mentioned under clause-3 of the scheme rules.

content.section_title: FAQ: I have decided on a specific surgical procedure; is it mandatory that the surgery must be officially recommended by a specialized medical practitioner?
content.text: Yes, the required surgery must be recommended by a subject specialist doctor or surgeon who has been defined in clause-2 of the scheme rules.

content.section_title: FAQ: If I currently reside in another state but occasionally visit Madhya Pradesh, can I still apply for this surgical reimbursement program?
content.text: No, the applicant must strictly demonstrate that they are a resident of the state of Madhya Pradesh.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is the specific authority or officer permitted to issue the income certificate that I must provide as proof of my financial status during the application process?
content.text: The income certificate must be provided by a person or officer who is officially authorized by the Social Justice Department.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is it necessary for the hospital or institution where the surgery will take place to provide any specific recommendation along with the application form?
content.text: Yes, the institution must provide a recommendation, certified by the Superintendent, Incharge, or Director, detailing the estimated expenditure on the surgical treatment.

content.section_title: FAQ: The application form requires a formal declaration; what specific information am I confirming regarding my legal history or submitted details?
content.text: You certify that you have not been convicted in any criminal case and that all information presented in the application is true, acknowledging that false information leads to rejection.

content.section_title: FAQ: Could you list a few specific corrective surgeries covered under this assistance program for individuals who are suffering from bone impairment or orthopaedic issues?
content.text: Covered surgeries include Replacement Implants, Ligament Repair, Post Polio Correction Surgery, and specialized Surgery for Knee, Hip and Ankle Correction.

title: Special Health Scheme for Primitive Tribes
topic: Special Health Scheme for Primitive Tribes

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The "Special Health Scheme for Primitive Tribes" scheme by the Welfare Department, Government of Jharkhand, aims to improve healthcare services for primitive tribal communities. Financial assistance of ₹38,41,632 is provided for targeted healthcare benefits.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The "Special Health Scheme for Primitive Tribes" scheme by the Welfare Department, Government of Jharkhand, aims to improve healthcare access and outcomes for primitive tribal communities. Through this scheme, financial assistance of ₹38,41,632 is allocated for health-related services and programs to ensure better medical support and infrastructure for the targeted groups.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
- Focused healthcare services for primitive tribal communities.
- Distribution of funds to districts such as Dumka, Sahebganj, Pakur, and Godda.
- Utilization for better medical facilities, treatments, and preventive care programs.
- Priority to regions with high tribal populations and pressing health needs.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant should belong to a primitive tribal community.
1. The applicant should reside in districts covered under the scheme, including Dumka, Sahebganj, Pakur, and Godda.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Step 01:** Visit the district welfare office in your region and obtain the application form for the Special Health Scheme for Primitive Tribes.
**Step 02:** Fill in the required details, including personal, income, and community information.
**Step 03:** Attach the necessary documents.
**Step 04:** Submit the completed form to the welfare officer and keep the acknowledgment receipt for future reference.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Proof of Identity (Aadhaar Card/Voter ID/Driving License)
- Proof of Residence (Ration Card/Utility Bill)
- Community Certificate
- Medical Reports
- Any Other Document If Required

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the objective of the Special Health Scheme for Primitive Tribes?
content.text: The scheme aims to improve healthcare facilities and provide financial support for health-related services to primitive tribal communities in Jharkhand.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can apply for this scheme?
content.text: Applicants from primitive tribal communities residing in Dumka, Sahebganj, Pakur, and Godda districts are eligible.

content.section_title: FAQ: What benefits are provided under this scheme?
content.text: The scheme provides financial support for better healthcare facilities, treatments, and preventive health programs, totaling [?]38,41,632.

content.section_title: FAQ: How are the benefits disbursed?
content.text: Benefits are disbursed through the district welfare offices via direct transfer to approved applicants.

content.section_title: FAQ: What documents are needed to apply for the scheme?
content.text: Documents include proof of identity, residence, community certificate, medical reports, bank account details, and photographs.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the process for application?
content.text: Applicants must visit the district welfare office, fill out the application form, attach necessary documents, and submit it for approval.

content.section_title: FAQ: What happens after the application is submitted?
content.text: The welfare office verifies the application and documents. Upon approval, benefits are provided to the applicant.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the role of district welfare officers in this scheme?
content.text: They manage applications, verify eligibility, disburse funds, and ensure implementation as per guidelines.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can I apply online for the scheme?
content.text: No, the application process is offline through the district welfare offices.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where can I get more information about this scheme?
content.text: Visit the district welfare office or contact the Tribal Welfare Commissioner's office for detailed information

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: "Special Livestock Insurance Scheme" is a welfare initiative targeting SC families engaged in livestock rearing. The scheme provides free insurance for livestock, enhancing the economic security of resource-poor SC families. The scheme covers milch animals, sheep, goats, pigs, camels, etc;

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: "Special Livestock Insurance Scheme" by the Animal Husbandry & Dairying Department, Government of Haryana is a continuation of a welfare initiative targeting scheduled caste families engaged in livestock rearing. The scheme is designed to provide free insurance for livestock, enhancing the economic security of resource-poor Scheduled Caste families. It has been allocated a budget of ₹3,00,00,000/- by the State Government and aims to cover various livestock species, including milch animals, sheep, goats, pigs, pack animals, and camels.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: 1. **Free Insurance Coverage:** For livestock owned by Scheduled Caste families, ensuring financial protection in case of animal loss.1. **Employment Support:** Covers livestock units established under the “Scheme for Providing Employment Opportunities to Scheduled Castes (SCSP).”1. **Wide Coverage:** Includes dairy, piggery, sheep, goat units, milch buffalo, cattle, pack animals, and camels.1. **Publicity and Awareness:** ₹5,50,000/- allocated for widespread publicity to ensure maximum participation.1. **Veterinary Support:** Honorarium to Veterinary Surgeons for health and post-mortem certificates.1. **Cost-Free to Beneficiaries:** Entire costs related to insurance premiums, publicity, and veterinary support are borne by the State Government.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant should be from a Scheduled Caste family.
1. The livestock of the applicant should include milch buffaloes, milch cattle, sheep, goats, pigs, pack animals, or camels.
1. The animals must be owned by the applicant.
1. The animals should be reared within the State.

_*Livestock units established under the “Scheme for Providing Employment Opportunities to Scheduled Castes” are automatically eligible._

content.section_title: Exclusions
content.text:
- Livestock not owned by Scheduled Caste families.
- Animals without a valid health certificate issued by Veterinary Surgeons.
- Losses not covered under the insurance policy terms (e.g., negligence, willful harm).
- Livestock not registered under the scheme or insured during the eligibility period.
- Claims without proper documentation or post-mortem reports.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
> **Registration**

**Step 1:** The eligible applicant may apply online through the Official Portal- Antyodaya-SARAL Portal. [https://saralharyana.gov.in/](https://saralharyana.gov.in/)
**Step 2:** If the applicant is not registered on the portal, he/she gets registered there.
**Step 3:** For registration, click “[New User](https://saralharyana.gov.in/citizenRegistration.html)” under ‘Sign In Here’ and fill in all the mandatory details i.e. Name, Email ID, Mobile Number, Password & State. Click on ‘Submit’.
**Step 04:** Verify your mobile number and email ID through the received OTP and complete the registration process.
> **Application**

**Step 1:** To avail the benefits of the scheme, visit the Official [Antyodaya-SARAL Portal](https://saralharyana.gov.in/) and log in through the email ID used while registering.
**Step 2:** Enter Password & Captcha and click on ‘Login’.
**Step 3:** After successful login, click on ‘Apply for Services’ and then ‘View all Available Services’
**Step 4:** Now, you can search for the scheme and click on the scheme to go ahead and fill out the application form.
**Step 5:** Enter your Parivar Pehchan Patra number - Family ID and click on “Click here to Fetch Family Data” which displays the family members registered under the given Family ID.
**Step 6:** Select the beneficiary/applicant name and enter the OTP that is being sent to the selected family member for verification. Click on “Click to Verify”
**Step 7:** Fill in all the mandatory details and upload all the mandatory documents.
**Step 8:** Preview the application form and click on ‘Submit’.
> **Tracking of Application:**

Applicants can track his/her application through the Official [Antyodaya-SARAL Portal](https://status.saralharyana.nic.in/) by entering their Department Name, Scheme Name, and Application Reference ID.
_*In case of loss, file a claim with supporting documents, including the post-mortem report._

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Proof of Scheduled Caste status.
- Livestock ownership documents (e.g., purchase receipt or ownership affidavit).
- Health certificate issued by a designated Veterinary Surgeon.
- Identification proof of the applicant.
- Address proof.
- Post-mortem report in case of claim filing.
- Application form duly filled and signed.
- Recent passport-sized photograph of the applicant.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the Special Livestock Insurance Scheme?
content.text: It is a State Government initiative providing free livestock insurance to Scheduled Caste families for the year 2018-19.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible for this scheme?
content.text: Scheduled Caste families owning livestock such as milch animals, sheep, goats, pigs, pack animals, or camels within the State are eligible.

content.section_title: FAQ: What costs are covered under the scheme?
content.text: The entire insurance premium, publicity expenses, and Veterinary Surgeon honorariums are covered by the State Government.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can I apply for this scheme?
content.text: Applications can be submitted at the nearest Deputy Director office for Animal Husbandry & Dairying along with the required documents.

content.section_title: FAQ: What documents are needed for application?
content.text: Proof of Scheduled Caste status, livestock ownership documents, and health certificates issued by Veterinary Surgeons.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the role of Veterinary Surgeons in this scheme?
content.text: Veterinary Surgeons issue health and post-mortem certificates required for insurance and claim processes.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are all types of livestock covered under this scheme?
content.text: Yes, milch animals, sheep, goats, pigs, pack animals, and camels are covered.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any cost to the beneficiaries?
content.text: No, the scheme is completely free for Scheduled Caste families.

content.section_title: FAQ: What happens in case of livestock loss?
content.text: File a claim with necessary documentation, including a post-mortem certificate, to receive insurance benefits.

content.section_title: FAQ: How is the scheme publicized?
content.text: [?]25,000 per district is allocated for publicity, ensuring that beneficiaries are well-informed about the scheme.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The Puducherry Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board provides up upto ₹2,00,000/- for specialized medical treatment in Hospitals having tie-ups with Government General Hospitals. The amount for the medical treatment is credited through DBT into the bank account.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The "Specialized Treatment & Medical Assistance Scheme" is a Welfare Scheme by the Puducherry Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Labour Department, Union Territory of Puducherry. The Board provides for upto ₹2,00,000/- for taking specialized medical treatment in Hospitals having tie-up with Government General Hospitals. The amount for the medical treatment is credited through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) into the bank account of the registered construction worker. The applications are accepted offline.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: Financial Assistance of upto ₹2,00,000/- for taking specialized medical treatment in Hospitals having tie-ups with Government General Hospitals.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
- The applicant should be a citizen of India.
- The applicant should be registered as a Construction Worker with the Puducherry Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
- The applicant should have completed the age of 18 years.
- The applicant should not have completed the age of 60 years during the preceding 12 months in the construction work.
- The beneficiary should be contributing to the Board's Fund.
- The applicant should not have defaulted in payment of contribution to the Board for a continuous period of more than one year.
- The applicant should have either met an accident or should be suffering from a disease such as Tuberculosis, Lung Issues, Neurological Disorders, etc.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Step 1:** The interested construction worker should visit the Office of the Puducherry Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (Industrial Estate, Thattanchavady, Puducherry - 605 009) and obtain the prescribed format of the application form free of cost from the concerned authority exclusively entrusted to issue and collect filled-in applications.
OR
The interested construction worker should take print of the prescribed format of the application form provided in the section "Application for Welfare Benefits" on [Page No. 83](https://labour.py.gov.in/sites/default/files/citizens-charter-2020.pdf) of the Citizen's Charter.
**Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste the passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
**Step 3:** The interested construction worker should submit the duly filled and signed application form and the documents to the concerned authority.
**Step 4:** Request a receipt or acknowledgement from the concerned authority to whom the application form has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Last Subscription Bill.
- Board ID Card.
- Aadhaar Card.
- Ration Card.
- Accident Report (if applicable).
- Bank Pass Book.
- Referral Letter, obtained from the Health Department, Government of Puducherry.
- Medical Certificate issued by the competent authority (RMO).

content.section_title: FAQ: Is it mandatory to submit an Accident Report, and under what circumstances is it required?
content.text: The Accident Report is mandatory only for those who have met with an accident.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the purpose of submitting a Referral Letter for the scheme?
content.text: The Referral Letter obtained from the Health Department serves as part of the documentation required for the scheme and may be necessary for validating the applicant's medical condition.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any relaxation in the age criteria for eligibility under certain circumstances?
content.text: There is no relaxation in the age criteria. Eligibility requires the applicant to be between 18 and 60 years of age.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can an applicant be eligible for the scheme if they have defaulted in payment of contribution to the Board?
content.text: The eligibility criteria state that an applicant should not have defaulted in payment of contribution to the Board for a continuous period of more than one year to be eligible for the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What financial assistance does the "Specialized Treatment & Medical Assistance Scheme" offer?
content.text: The scheme provides financial assistance of up to [?]2,00,000/- for taking specialized medical treatment in hospitals having tie-ups with Government General Hospitals.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the role of the Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) in the disbursement of financial assistance under the scheme?
content.text: The amount for medical treatment is credited through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) into the bank account of the registered construction worker.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can you provide more details about the health conditions that make an applicant eligible for the scheme?
content.text: The document mentions that an applicant should have either met with an accident or be suffering from specific diseases such as Tuberculosis, Lung Issues, Neurological Disorders, etc., to be eligible for the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the age limit for eligibility to register as a Construction Worker under the scheme?
content.text: To be eligible for registration, an applicant should have completed the age of 18 years and should not have completed the age of 60 years during the preceding 12 months in construction work.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can I file a grievance online, or is it limited to offline methods?
content.text: The grievance can only be submitted offline. Detail about the method is outlined on page 131 of the document at https://labour.py.gov.in/sites/default/files/citizens-charter-2020.pdf.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any specific information about the format or submission process of the application form?
content.text: Details about the application form, including its format and submission process, are available on page 87 of the document at https://labour.py.gov.in/sites/default/files/citizens-charter-2020.pdf.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the registration process for the scheme, and where can I find details about the required documents?
content.text: Information about the registration process and required documents is available on page 57 of the document at https://labour.py.gov.in/sites/default/files/citizens-charter-2020.pdf.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the source document for the guidelines of the mentioned scheme?
content.text: The source document for the guidelines is available at https://labour.py.gov.in/sites/default/files/citizens-charter-2020.pdf, specifically on page 73.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The Government of Kerala launched the “Thalolam Scheme” under Kerala Social Security Mission to provide free treatment to children affected with chronic diseases such as haemophilia, cerebral palsy, etc.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
The Government of Kerala launched the “Thalolam Scheme” on 1st January 2010 under Kerala Social Security Mission by the Social Justice Department, Kerala. The aim of the scheme is to provide free treatment to the children below age of 18, who are suffering from Kidney diseases, Cardiovascular diseases, Cerebral Palsy, Brittle Bone Disease, Hemophilia, Thalassemia, Sickle Cell Anemia, Orthopedic deformities, and other Neuro-Developmental Disabilities, Congenital anomalies (Endosulfan victims) and accident cases which needs surgery.
**Hospitals receiving free treatment:**

1. Govt. Medical College Hospital, Trivandrum
1. Govt. Medical College Hospital, Thrissur
1. Govt. Medical College Hospital, Alappuzha
1. S. A. T Hospital, Trivandrum
1. Govt. Medical College Hospital, Kottayam
1. IM C. H, Kozhikode
1. Govt. Medical College, Kozhikode
1. I.C.H.M.C. Kottayam
1. Co-operative Medical College, Kannur
1. Regional Cancer Center, Thiruvananthapuram
1. District Hospital, Aluva, Ernakulam
1. Sree Chithira Thirunal Hospital, Thiruvananthapuram

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
1. A patient will be eligible for initial assistance of ₹50,000/- only under this scheme.
1. Under the scheme, free treatment will be provided to the children below age of 18, who are suffering from Kidney diseases, Cardiovascular diseases, Cerebral Palsy, Brittle Bone Disease, Hemophilia, Thalassemia, Sickle Cell Anemia, Orthopedic deformities and other Neuro-Developmental Disabilities, Congenital anomalies (Endosulfan victims) and accident cases which needs surgery.

**Note:** Additional financial assistance can be met based on the decision of the Superintendent, Head of the concerned Department of Specialty, and Head of the Radiotherapy Department.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant should be a resident of Kerala State and the scheme is applicable only to patients from Kerala.
1. The patient should be below 18 years of age.
1. The scheme is applicable to patients belonging to families too poor to afford treatment of the Below Poverty Line (BPL) category.
1. Under this scheme, assistance will be provided to the patient for diseases other than cancer which are lethal or fatal.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
To avail of the benefits of the scheme the applicant may contact the following authorities:

1. Scheme counselor of the Safety Mission assigned to the respective hospitals, contacts hospitals covered under this scheme.
1. Concerned District Social Justice Officer, Kerala

**Note:** No special application is required. Based on the economic and social analysis done by the Counselors of the Safety Mission assigned to the respective hospitals, the benefit of the scheme will be applicable to the children of families who are unable to bear the cost of treatment.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
1.	Copy of Aadhaar/ Proof of Identity of patient
2.	Proof of age of the patient
3. Income certificate
4.	Copy of BPL card (if applicable)
5.	Copy of Ration card (if applicable)
6.	Residence certificate
7.	Passport-size photograph
8.	Patient’s medical reports

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the aim of the scheme?
content.text: The Thalolam scheme aims to provide free treatment to the children below age of 18, who are suffering from Kidney diseases, Cardiovascular diseases, Cerebral Palsy, Brittle Bone Disease, Hemophilia, Thalassemia, Sickle Cell Anemia, Orthopedic deformities, and other Neuro-Developmental Disabilities, Congenital anomalies (Endosulfan victims) and accident cases which needs surgery.

content.section_title: FAQ: When was this scheme launched?
content.text: The Government of Kerala launched this scheme on 1st January 2010 under Kerala Social Security Mission.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible to get the benefits under the scheme?
content.text: The scheme is applicable to patients who are suffering from diseases other than cancer which are lethal or fatal.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is this scheme only for a resident of Kerala?
content.text: Yes, the applicant should be a resident of Kerala State and the scheme is applicable only to patients from Kerala.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the age limit to apply under the scheme?
content.text: The age limit of the applicant has to be 18 years or below.

content.section_title: FAQ: How much initial assistance will be provided to the patient under the scheme?
content.text: A patient will be eligible for initial assistance of [?]50,000/- only under this scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can additional financial assistance also be provided to the patient?
content.text: Yes, additional financial assistance can be met based on the decision of the Superintendent, Head of the concerned Department of Specialty, and Head of the Radiotherapy Department.

content.section_title: FAQ: Which type patients can avail of the benefits under the scheme?
content.text: Under the scheme, free treatment will be provided to the children below age of 18, who are suffering from Kidney diseases, Cardiovascular diseases, Cerebral Palsy, Brittle Bone Disease, Hemophilia, Thalassemia, Sickle Cell Anemia, Orthopedic deformities and other Neuro-Developmental Disabilities, Congenital anomalies (Endosulfan victims) and accident cases which needs surgery.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can an applicant avail of the benefits of the scheme?
content.text: To avail of the benefits of the scheme the applicant may contact the scheme counselor of the Safety Mission assigned to the respective hospitals or the concerned District Social Justice Officer, Kerala.

content.section_title: FAQ: How will the beneficiary be selected under the scheme?
content.text: Based on the economic and social analysis done by the Counselors of the Safety Mission assigned to the respective hospitals, the benefit of the scheme will be applicable to the children of families who are unable to bear the cost of treatment.

title: Theka Shramik Evan Hamaal Shramik Baahy Rogi Chikitsa Sahaayata Yojana
topic: Theka Shramik Evan Hamaal Shramik Baahy Rogi Chikitsa Sahaayata Yojana

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: Chhattisgarh State Government started a scheme "Theka Shramik Evan Hamaal Shramik Baahy Rogi Chikitsa Sahaayata Yojana" for unorganized worker on 11 March 2015.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: Labor Department of Chhattisgarh State started this scheme "Theka Shramik Evan Hamaal Shramik Baahy Rogi Chikitsa Sahaayata Yojana" for unorganized worker on 27 August 2015. The main objective of the scheme to provide financial assistance for the medical treatment.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: Financial Assistance : ₹ 2,000/-

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. Applicant should be registered in any district of the state.
1. If applicant received from any other parallel scheme of the State Government, then applicant is not eligible for this scheme.
1. Beneficiaries aged between 18 years to 60 years will be eligible under the scheme.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
**Unregistered Applicant :**

1. All the applicant have to visit the official [website](https://cglabour.nic.in) Chhattisgarh Labour Department. 1. On the home page click on "[Apply](https://cglabour.nic.in/Departments.aspx?id=4)" under "Chhattisgarh Unorganized Workers State Social Security Board".1. Enter the following details

1. Select Service : "Asangathit Shramik Panjikaran"
1. What do you want to do :  "Aavedan"
1. Click next.

1. Fill out the online "Registration form".
1. Submit.

**Already Registered Applicant :**

1. All the applicant have to visit the official [website](https://cglabour.nic.in) Chhattisgarh Labour Department. 1. On the home page click on "[Apply](https://cglabour.nic.in/Departments.aspx?id=4)" under "Chhattisgarh Unorganized Workers State Social Security Board".1. Enter the following details

1. Select Group Name : "Asangathit Marmakaar Mandal"
1. Select Service : "Yojana"
1. What do you want to do :  "Aavedan"
1. Click next.

1. Select your district name and provide old/new registration number and click next.
1. Select the scheme name.
1. Fill out the online with required details.
1. Submit.

- Registration Card.
- Disease details by Vikas khand Adhikari / Surgeon / Chief medical officer/ Heath Officer.

topic: Theka Shramik, Hamaal Shramik Evan Gharelu Mahila Kamgar Gambhir Bimaari Chikitsa Sahayata Yojana

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: Chhattisgarh State Government started a scheme "Theka Shramik, Hamaal Shramik Evan Gharelu Mahila Kamgar Gambhir Bimaari Chikitsa Sahayata Yojana" for unorganized worker on 05 June 2018.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: Labor Department of Chhattisgarh State started this scheme "Theka Shramik, Hamaal Shramik Evan Gharelu Mahila Kamgar Gambhir Bimaari Chikitsa Sahayata Yojana" for unorganized worker on 05 June 2018. The main objective of the scheme to provides financial assistance for serious illness.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
1. Medical aid of ₹50,000/- will be provided for the treatment of registered contract workers, Hamal workers and female domestic workers who are suffering from serious illness.
1. Serious diseases under the scheme will include kidney, cancer, sickle cell anemia, heart disease, AIDS and paralysis.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. Applicant must be registered with the State Social Security Board at least 90 days in advance.
1. Applicant must be in the age group of 18 to 60 years will be eligible for this scheme.
1. Applicant can be only received benefits at once.
1. If applicant received from any other parallel scheme of the State Government, then he/she is not eligible for this scheme.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: This scheme facilitates a complete physical examination of the construction workers, which can diagnose potential illnesses and early treatment can prevent major illnesses. Hence, major illness can be avoided and the construction worker can lead a healthy life.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The “Total Health Check-up Scheme” is implemented by the Gujarat Building and Other Construction Worker’s Welfare Board (GBOCWWB), Labour, Skill Development & Employment Department, Government of Gujarat. This scheme facilitates a complete physical examination of the construction workers, which can diagnose potential illnesses and early treatment can prevent major illnesses. Hence, major illness can be avoided and the construction worker can lead a healthy life.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
1. Under the scheme, preventive health check-ups for workers, with a view to initiate proper treatment/health restoration measures.
1. This health check-up will be recommended regularly to every individual for diagnosis of any disease at its initial stage.
1. This health check-up includes routine doctors, pathology, radiology and cardiology check-ups. In which a limit of ₹1950/- per beneficiary will be paid to the empanelled hospital/institution.

> **Terms and Conditions:**

1. The benefits of this scheme will be given to the state employees. In which per beneficiary has to spend within the limit of ₹1950/-.
1. Ayushman Bharat Yojana empanelled hospitals from which workers can go for check-ups and treatment in district-wise hospitals decided by the Government/Board through MOU.

> **Where to Benefit from:**

- Actions are taken by empanelled institutions through camps in Kadianaka/construction sites/colonies.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text: 1. The construction workers who are registered with the Gujarat Building and Other Construction Worker’s Welfare Board will be eligible to get the benefit of this scheme.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
> **Application Process for ‘E-Nirman Card’ under the GBOCWWB:**

**Step 01:** The applicant may visit the e-Nirman Portal: [https://enirmanbocw.gujarat.gov.in/](https://enirmanbocw.gujarat.gov.in/)
**Step 02:** On the home page, under the tab ‘Login to Portal’, click on ‘[Please Register Here](Registration: https://enirmanbocw.gujarat.gov.in/Registration.aspx)’.
**Step 03:** Enter all the mandatory information and then click on ‘Register’.
**Step 04:** After successful registration, the applicants can login through their User ID and Password.
**Step 05:** Now, update your profile by entering all the necessary details as required and click on ‘Update’.
**Step 06:** Select the ‘Construction Works Form’ and fill out all the mandatory information in the form.
**Step 07:** Upload all the relevant documents, agree with the terms & conditions and submit the form.
**Step 08:** Once the application has been submitted, an application Number is generated which can be kept for future reference.
> **Process to track Application Status:**

**Step 01:** The applicant may visit the e-Nirman Portal: [https://enirmanbocw.gujarat.gov.in/](https://enirmanbocw.gujarat.gov.in/)
**Step 02:** On the home page, click on ‘View Citizen Application Status’.
**Step 03:** Enter the Application Number and Date of Birth.
**Step 04:** Now click on ‘View Status’.
> **Application Process for Scheme Benefit:**

**Step 01:** The applicant may visit the Sanman Portal: [https://sanman.gujarat.gov.in/](https://sanman.gujarat.gov.in/)
**Step 02:** On the home page, under the tab ‘Citizen Login’, click on ‘[Please Register Here](https://sanman.gujarat.gov.in/Home/Registration)’.
**Step 03:** Enter your Aadhaar Card Number, select user type, and then enter your E-Nirman Card Number.
**Step 04:** Now, click on ‘Fetch’ & submit the details.
**Step 05:** After successful registration, the applicants can login through their User ID and Password.
**Step 06:** Now, select the scheme and read the instructions carefully for the selected scheme.
**Step 07:** Fill out the application form and upload all the relevant documents.
**Step 08:** Agree with the Rules & Regulations and submit the application form. A confirmation email with the application Number will be sent on the registered email ID.
> **Post Application Process:**

**Step 01:** After filling out the form, the construction worker will be allotted time for the test by logging on to Sanman portal by North Star Institute.
**Step 02:** After that, the medical officer of the North Star Institute will check the list of health check-up tests and upload it on the report portal, and apply for the monthly joint bill through North Star at the Head Office, the bill will go to the Government Labour Officer of the Head Office.
**Step 03:** The Government Labour Officer of the Head Office will verify the bill and send it to the Hon'ble Member Secretary for approval.
**Step 04:** After checking the bill by the accounting officer, North Star Diagnostic Pvt. Ltd. Ahmedabad will pay the bill online into their account

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
Documents required for ‘E-Nirman Card’ under the GBOCWWB:- Photograph of the worker
- Aadhaar Card
- Ration Card
- Caste Category certificate, if applicable
- Proof of age (Birth certificate/Driving Licence/PAN Card/Voter ID Card)
- Self-declaration certificate
- Employer Certificate
- Bank Passbook/cancelled Cheque
- Any other documents as required
Documents required for Scheme Benefit:- e-Nirman Card of the worker
- Aadhaar Card
- Passport-size photograph
- Any other documents as required

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the aim of this scheme?
content.text: The scheme aims to facilitate early detection and treatment of illnesses among construction workers.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the benefits of this scheme?
content.text: The benefits include preventive health check-ups for workers, early diagnosis of diseases, and initiation of proper treatment or health restoration measures.

content.section_title: FAQ: How often are health check-ups recommended under this scheme?
content.text: Health check-ups are recommended regularly for every individual to diagnose any potential diseases at their initial stages.

content.section_title: FAQ: What does the health check-up include?
content.text: The health check-up includes routine doctor consultations, pathology tests, radiology examinations, and cardiology check-ups.

content.section_title: FAQ: How much does the scheme cover for each beneficiary?
content.text: The scheme covers up to [?]1950/- per beneficiary for the health check-up expenses at empanelled hospitals or institutions.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible for the Scheme?
content.text: Construction workers registered with the Gujarat Building and Other Construction Worker's Welfare Board are eligible for this scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are there any specific terms and conditions for availing the benefits?
content.text: Yes, beneficiaries must adhere to the spending limit of [?]1950/- and utilize Ayushman Bharat Yojana empanelled hospitals for check-ups and treatments.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where can beneficiaries avail themselves of the scheme's benefits?
content.text: Beneficiaries can benefit from the scheme at empanelled institutions through camps held in various locations like construction sites or colonies.

content.section_title: FAQ: How can an applicant apply for the scheme?
content.text: The applicant may visit and apply through the Sanman Portal: https://sanman.gujarat.gov.in/

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The Govt. of Kerala launched the “Sruthitharangam (Cochlear Implantation Scheme)” under Kerala Social Security Mission by the Social Justice Department, Kerala. This scheme envisioned providing free cochlear implantation surgery for children in the age group of 0-5 years, who are hearing impaired.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
The Government of Kerala launched the “Sruthitharangam (Cochlear Implantation Scheme)” under Kerala Social Security Mission by the Social Justice Department, Kerala. This scheme envisioned providing free cochlear implantation surgery for children in the age group of 0-5 years, who are hearing impaired. The early identification and intervention procedures initiated within six months of age should be the golden standard for the holistic development of a child with hearing loss. Cochlear Implant is a proven option for the treatment of profound hearing loss. The child from poor families whose annual family income is below ₹2 lakhs are eligible to get the benefit of this scheme. This scheme is implemented through Government as well as selected empaneled private hospitals in this field.
The objective of this scheme is to provide a cochlear implant to children selected by Regional and State level technical committees for cochlear implantation and to provide financial support for Auditory Verbal Habilitation (AVH) to operate children through empaneled hospitals/centers.
**Hospitals Covered Under the Scheme:**

1. Govt. Medical College Hospital, Trivandrum.
1. Govt. Medical College Hospital, Kozhikode.
1. Govt. Medical College Hospital, Kottayam.
1. KIMS Hospital, Trivandrum.
1. Medical Trust Hospital, Kochi.
1. Dr. Noushad’s ENT Institute and Research Centre, Ernakulam.
1. West Fort Hi-Tech Hospital, Thrissur.
1. Dr. Manoj’s ENT Super Speciality Institute & Research Centre, Kozhikode.
1. MIMS Hospital, Kozhikode.
1. ASCENT ENT Hospital, Perinthalmanna

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
1. Under the scheme, children who are hearing impaired will be provided free cochlear implantation surgery.

**Note:** The children will be selected by Regional and State level technical committees for cochlear implantation.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1.	The applicant should be a resident of Kerala State.
2.	The child who is hearing impaired is eligible under the scheme.
3.	The child should be in the age group of 0-3 years.
4.	The annual family income of the applicant should be below ₹2 Lakhs.
**Note:** The Screening Committee would be empowered to make exceptions beyond 3 years and up to 5 years of age on a case-to-case basis; where the committee feels that a high possibility of success exists or if there are other compelling technical reasons warranting or making an exception.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
To avail of the benefits of the scheme, the [application form](https://socialsecuritymission.gov.in/vmcatalogue/notifications/ea56ddc8ce0461b846d58f30a101fdaf.pdf) is duly filled and the necessary documents are to be submitted directly to Executive Director, Kerala Social Security Mission.
**Address for Communication:**
Executive Director,
Kerala Social Security Mission,
Poojappura, Thiruvananthapuram, Kerala-695012
Phone No – 0471 2341200
