content.text:
1.	Proof of Identity
2.	Proof of age of the child
3.	Income certificate
4.	Copy of BPL card (if applicable)
5.	Copy of Ration card (if applicable)
6.	Residence certificate
7.	Passport-size photograph
8.	Child’s medical reports

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the “Sruthitharangam Scheme”?
content.text: This scheme envisioned providing free cochlear implantation surgery for children in the age group of 0-5 years, who are hearing impaired. The early identification and intervention procedures initiated within six months of age should be the golden standard for the holistic development of a child with hearing loss. Cochlear Implant is a proven option for the treatment of profound hearing loss. The child from poor families whose annual family income is below [?]2 lakhs are eligible to get the benefit of this scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the objective of the scheme?
content.text: The objective of this scheme is to provide a cochlear implant to children selected by Regional and State level technical committees for cochlear implantation and to provide financial support for Auditory Verbal Habilitation (AVH) to operate children through empaneled hospitals/centers.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the benefit of the scheme?
content.text: Under the scheme, children who are hearing impaired will be provided free cochlear implantation surgery.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible to get the benefits under the scheme?
content.text: Children who are hearing impaired are eligible under the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can all the hearing-impaired children avail of the benefits under the scheme?
content.text: No, The children will be selected by Regional and State level technical committees for cochlear implantation.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is this scheme only for hearing-impaired patients?
content.text: Yes, this scheme is only for hearing-impaired patients.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the income criterion to avail of the benefits of the scheme?
content.text: The annual family income of the applicant should be below [?]2 Lakhs.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is this scheme only for a resident of Kerala?
content.text: Yes, the applicant should be a resident of Kerala State.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the age limit to apply under the scheme?
content.text: The applicant/child should be in the age group of 0-3 years.

content.text: Yes, the Screening Committee would be empowered to make exceptions beyond 3 years and up to 5 years of age on a case-to-case basis; where the committee feels that a high possibility of success exists or if there are other compelling technical reasons warranting or making an exception.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is this scheme implemented only in Government Hospitals?
content.text: No, this scheme is implemented through Government as well as selected empaneled private hospitals in this field.

content.text: The application form can be downloaded from the official website of Kerala Social Security Mission or can be collected from the office of the Executive Director, Kerala Social Security Mission.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to provide quality healthcare facilities to all government employees, pensioners, and their dependents in Uttarakhand. Under this scheme, prescribed medical treatment is provided free of cost.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The scheme “State Government Health Scheme (Golden Card)” has been implemented since January 1, 2021, by the Uttarakhand State Health Authority under the Department of Medical Health and Family Welfare, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to provide quality healthcare facilities to all government employees, pensioners, and their dependents in the state.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: The State Government Health Scheme has been implemented with the aim of providing quality healthcare facilities to all government employees, pensioners, and their dependents in Uttarakhand. Under this scheme, prescribed medical treatment is provided free of cost.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text: 1. All government employees and pensioners of Uttarakhand who have obtained Golden Cards and make monthly contributions are eligible under the scheme.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
Golden Cards for government employees, pensioners, their family members, and employees of autonomous bodies, corporations, authorities, universities, and aided institutions are issued at Jan Seva Kendras, the Unit Trust of India (UTI), or identified hospitals. The definition of ‘family’ and ‘dependents’ under the State Government Health Scheme shall be the same as those specified in the [Uttarakhand Retirement Benefits Act, 2018](https://www.indiacode.nic.in/bitstream/123456789/4884/1/retirement_benefit_.pdf).
**Dependent’s Income Limit:** The monthly income limit for a dependent will be determined as per the Central Government Health Scheme (CGHS) guidelines.
**Note:** Disability refers to a minimum of 40% disability, which must be certified by a Medical Board through an official Disability Certificate.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text: Documents are required as per the guidelines of the Uttarakhand State Health Authority for this scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible to avail benefits under the scheme?
content.text: All government employees and pensioners of Uttarakhand who have obtained Golden Cards and make monthly contributions are eligible under the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are dependents of employees and pensioners covered under this scheme?
content.text: Yes, the dependents and family members of government employees and pensioners are covered under the scheme, as per the eligibility criteria defined in the Uttarakhand Retirement Benefits Act, 2018.

content.section_title: FAQ: Where can Golden Cards be obtained from?
content.text: Golden Cards can be obtained from Jan Seva Kendras, the Unit Trust of India (UTI), or identified hospitals authorized under the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What are the benefits provided under the scheme?
content.text: Under the scheme, prescribed medical treatment is provided free of cost to all eligible beneficiaries at empanelled hospitals.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the income limit for dependents under the scheme?
content.text: The monthly income limit for dependents is determined as per the Central Government Health Scheme (CGHS) guidelines.

content.section_title: FAQ: How is disability defined under the scheme?
content.text: Disability refers to a minimum of 40% disability, which must be certified by a Medical Board through a valid Disability Certificate.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any contribution required from the beneficiaries?
content.text: Yes, beneficiaries (employees and pensioners) are required to contribute a fixed amount monthly.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: "Su-Swastha Yojna" provides comprehensive and affordable healthcare coverage to the employees of the Government of Sikkim and their families. Cashless hospitalization at top-tier hospitals across India is provided to eligible beneficiaries with the best-in-class healthcare.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: Launched in April 2021, the scheme "Su-Swastha Yojna" by the Department of Health & Family Welfare, Government of Sikkim aims to provide comprehensive and affordable healthcare coverage to the employees of the Government of Sikkim and their families. Through this scheme, cashless hospitalization at top-tier hospitals across India is provided to the eligible beneficiaries. The scheme covers regular employees in service of the Government of Sikkim and dependent family members up to a maximum of 5 dependents in a scheme that provides 10 lacs of cover to the family per year.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
**Cashless Convenience:** Cashless admission and discharge across network hospitals eliminate the burden of arranging funds during hospitalization.
**Access to Top-Tier Healthcare:** Beneficiaries gain access to best-in-class healthcare by leveraging a network of top-tier hospitals and institutes across India.
**Quality Assurance:** The Su-Swastha Facilitation Center ensures quality of care by overseeing medical services provided to beneficiaries.
**Transparent Billing:** Standardized tariffs with hospitals based on infrastructure and quality of care promote transparency in billing.
**Patient-Centric Approach:** Communication with patients and their families is maintained throughout the care journey.
**Wide Network Coverage:** Beneficiaries can avail of the scheme's benefits at over 400+ empanelled hospitals within the Su-Swastha Yojna network.
**Comprehensive Coverage:**

- Base Cover - 10 lacs base cover.
- Critical Care Coverage - 10 lacs additional critical care coverage for the entire family, regardless of the number of dependents.

**In-Patient Treatment:** Covers hospitalization expenses exceeding 24 hours.
**Road Ambulance Assistance:** Covers road ambulance expenses up to ₹3500 per hospitalization, with a maximum of 3 claims per year.
**Pre-existing Disease Coverage:** Covers all pre-existing diseases from the first day of enrollment.
**Post-Hospitalization Care:** Covers medical expenses incurred within 30 days of hospital discharge.
**Specialized Coverage:** Includes coverage for special conditions, treatments, implants, vehicular accidents, and trauma.
**Maternity Benefits:** Covers delivery expenses up to ₹50,000, including newborn baby cover.
**Dental and Ophthalmic Care:** Covers daycare dental and ophthalmic treatments, including implants.
**Organ Donor Expenses:** Covers expenses incurred for organ transplantation from a donor to a Su-Swastha beneficiary, subject to claim eligibility criteria.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text: The applicant should be a regular employee in service of the Government of Sikkim or the dependent family members of such employee (spouse; brother and/or sister; children (own or legally adopted); parents of self or spouse) (up to a maximum of 5 dependents).

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
**Step 1:** Visit the Official Website of "[Su-Swastha Sikkim](https://suswasthasikkim.com/)" and click "[Enrol Now](https://suswasthasikkim.com/login/employee)".
**Step 2:** Login to the website using your registered Mobile Number. Verify your Mobile Number via OTP. You will be taken to the online enrolment form.
**Step 3:** There are two parts of the application. First, the employee must fill and submit part 1 of the form which is to enrol himself/herself in the scheme. Then he/she should fill and submit part 2 of the form. Fill in all the mandatory fields and if required, upload all the mandatory documents in the specified format and size.
**Step 4:** Agree to the self-declaration and submit the application, you will receive confirmation message on your phone (SMS) as well as an email confirmation. Please note that the application will go to your respective [nodal officers](https://suswasthasikkim.com/Nodal%20Officers%20List.pdf) for approval.
Once submitted successfully, the application is evaluated and processed. Once approved, your application you will receive a link to download your member e-card on SMS and email. This card is required for availing any benefits of Su-Swastha later.
***In case of any query or assistance please reach us at info@suswasthasikkim.com or call us at 9800347944***

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text:
**Step 1:** The interested applicant should take print of the <prescribed format> of the application form.
**Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste the passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
**Step 3:** Each department has [nodal officers](https://suswasthasikkim.com/Nodal%20Officers%20List.pdf) made in charge of enrolment of employees of their respective departments. Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents, within the prescribed period (if any), to the Department Nodal Officer.
***The duly filled and signed application form can also be email to support@suswasthasikkim.com along with all documents and photos attached.***

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Passport Size Photo
- One of the following ID Proofs that shows the identity of the employee and the dependents: Voter’s Card, Aaadhaar Card, Driver’s license, Passport, or Any other government ID
- One of the following Proofs of the Relationship between employee and dependent: Aadhaar Card, Voter’s ID, PAN Card, Passport, Driver’s License, Hospital Discharge Card (attested by a gazette government official for newborn babies), Birth Certificate, Immunization Certificate, School Certificate (attested by school authority and gazette government official), Marriage Certificate, Affidavit (attested by gazette government official in lieu of no government issue proof)

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the amount of contribution for the dependents for availing the scheme?
content.text: The amount is Rs 200/- per month per dependent. For example, if you have 5 dependents, you will be contributing (Rs 200 x 5= Rs 1000/-) per month for the 5 dependents. This amount is contributed to a healthcare fund to which the Government of Sikkim contributes funds as well to cover the total healthcare expenses incurred in Su-Swastha Yojna.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the amount I am covered for? Can I increase this amount?
content.text: Total Cover is 10 lacs (ten lacs) for the family- irrespective of number of dependents in the family. This amount is fixed and for the current year it cannot be increased by employee.

content.section_title: FAQ: What am I covered for in the Su-Swastha cashless benefit scheme?
content.text:
1. In-patient Treatment - Covers hospitalization expenses for period more than 24 hrs
2.Day care Dental and Ophthalmic treatment inclusive of implants (However, for cosmetic purpose, dental implants are excluded)
3. Maternity and child healthcare - e.g. hospitalizations related to delivery, caesarean and neonatal care
4.	Road Ambulance Expenses-Expenses incurred for transportation of the Beneficiary by ambulance service from home//one hospital to another hospital.
5.	Post-Hospitalization- Medical Expenses incurred up to 30 days from the date of discharge from the hospital.
6.	Organ Donor Expenses-Expenses incurred for organ transplantation from the Donor to the recipient Su   Swastha beneficiary person are payable provided the claim for transplantation is payable
7.	Cancer chemotherapy and dialysis
8.	Vehicular Accident and Trauma

content.section_title: FAQ: Where can I avail the cashless treatment?
content.text: Cashless benefits scheme can be availed from any of the hospitals in India which have joined on the panel or network of Su-Swastha Yojna. This list is available on our website and continuously updated.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can two or more people be admitted in the hospital under Su-Swastha Yojna at the same time to avail medical care benefits under the yojna?
content.text: Yes, provided you are within the coverage limit and in the empanelled hospital.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who is eligible for enrolling in Su-Swastha Yojna?
content.text: All employees of the Sikkim government appointed against the regular Post from the date of joining regular post. A central government servant or a government servant of any other state while on deputation to the Government of Sikkim. Dependents of the eligible employees- a maximum of 5 eligible dependents can be added for each employee

content.section_title: FAQ: Are contractual/MR employees eligible for enrolment in Su-Swastha Yojna?
content.text: No, not in this phase. Contractual, MR employees and ad hoc employees are not eligible in the current year of launch; there are plans to include them in subsequent phases- please check our website for notifications on same periodically.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are retired employees eligible?
content.text: No, retired employees are not eligible in the current year of policy period; there are plans to include them in subsequent phases- please check our website for notifications on same

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the maximum number of dependents I can add?
content.text: You can add any number of dependents between zero (0) and five (5); a maximum of five (5) dependents can be added for a primary member or employee; in no case can more than 5 dependents be added to a primary member.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the amount of contribution for availing the scheme?
content.text: The employee contribution is Rs 200/- per employee per month. The rest of the healthcare fund is created by government contributions.

content.section_title: FAQ: My spouse  is also a government employee. Will both of us be entitled to enrol as a primary insured?
content.text: When both spouses are government regular employees they can choose to enrol separately as primary member of Su-Swastha Yojna OR as dependent on the other spouse. However, an employee cannot be enrolled as a primary and dependent at the same time.

content.section_title: FAQ: Form where I can enrol for Su-swastha Yojana ?
content.text: Go to https://suswasthasikkim.com/login/employee and apply

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: A Health Insurance Scheme by the Directorate of Information & Culture Affairs, for Journalists who are permanent residents of the state of Tripura. The applicant must be an accredited journalist by the state government or PIB. The age must be in between 21-65 years.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: A Health Insurance Scheme by the Directorate of Information & Culture Affairs, for Journalists who are permanent residents of the state of Tripura. The applicant must be an accredited journalist by the state government or PIB. The age must be in between 21-65 years. The applicant must not have been covered under the Ayushman Bharat Scheme or any other Health Insurance Scheme of the Central or State Government. The beneficiary will receive medical care facilities for all the diseases as listed by the State government after recommendation by the committee. The maximum coverage of benefits from all sources will be ₹3,00,000. The application shall be submitted to the Director, the Information and Cultural Affairs Department. The basic objective of the scheme is to financially protect the Journalists from monetary burdens and liabilities that are a result of unfortunate health-related situations and emergencies.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant must be a permanent resident of the state of Tripura.
1. The applicant must be an accredited working journalist by the state government or by the Press Information Bureau (PIB).
1. The age of the applicant must be in between 21 years and 65 years.
1. The applicant must not have been covered under the Ayushman Bharat Scheme or any other Health Insurance Scheme of the Central or State Government.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Proof of Age / DOB
- Proof of Residence
- Bank Details (Refer Source)
- Income Certificate
- Proof reg the health-related situations

content.section_title: FAQ: Who are excluded from applying to this scheme?

With the aim of providing health care protection to the working Journalists of the State, the Government of Tripura has launched the Tripura Journalist Health Insurance Scheme. The basic objective of the scheme is to financially protect the Journalists from monetary burden and liabilities

that are a result of unfortunate health-related situations and emergencies. Under this Scheme, the beneficiaries are eligible to receive coverage benefits upto Rs. 3 lakhs for a list of diseases that need hospitalization and others.

content.text: Under the Tripura Journalist Health Insurance Scheme, beneficiaries will get medical care facilities for all the disease as listed by the State government after recommendation by the committee. The maximum coverage of benefit from all sources will be Rs.3.0 Lakh.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who shall be the Implementing agencies for this scheme?
content.text: For the implementation of the Scheme, State Government shall invite EOI (Expression of Interest) from reputed Health insurance companies of India. (see Clause10). For a successful EOI process, separate criteria or eligibilities shall be fixed by the health experts of the State.

List of disease covers under the Scheme: The empanelled Insurance Company shall be bound to provide cashless health facilities for all the disease as listed by the state government or as directed by the competent authority after approval of the recommendation of the committee under the scheme.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: Madhya Pradesh State Government started "Udita Yojana" for women health promotions.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text: The Project Udita program was started on November 1, 2016, under the Women and Child Development Projects of Madhya Pradesh Government. According to the concept of this scheme, Anganwadi workers and assistants will inform adolescent girls about the importance of proper hygiene during menstruation and sexually transmitted diseases due to negligence and provide them with sanitary napkins. If girls become aware of their health in their teenage years, then they all can remain healthy in future also.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: - Distribution of sanitary napkins, nutrition and health education, training on menstrual hygiene and nutrition and health issues in adolescent girls are done through Udita Corner established in Anganwadi centers.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text: - All adolescent girls and women aged 18 to 49 will be beneficiaries.

content.text: Madhya Pradesh State Government started the "Udita Yojana" for women's health promotions.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: UDID Card or Unique Disability Identity card is an initiative by Government of India with a view of creating a National Database for Persons with Disabilities (PwDs). Under the UDID project Disability Certificate and Unique Disability Identity Card is issued to each PwD through online platform.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
The “Unique Disability ID (UDID)” has been launched by the Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities (DEPwD), Government of India, aiming to create a national database of Persons with Disabilities (PwDs) and issue a Unique Disability Identity Card to each individual to ensure transparency, efficiency, and ease in delivering government benefits. Through this scheme, Disability Certificates and Unique Disability Identity Cards are provided to Persons with Disabilities across the country. The scheme is implemented by competent medical authorities notified by the respective State Governments and Union Territories through the centralised UDID portal. The project supports online application submission, assessment of disability percentage by hospitals or Medical Boards, online renewal and updating of information, and integrated management of government schemes and benefits for PwDs. It also eliminates duplicate records and enables tracking of the physical and financial progress of beneficiaries at the village, block, district, state, and national levels. District Welfare Officers assist in registration camps and application management, while District Collectors monitor implementation through reporting features available on the portal.
**Objectives:**

- To create a national database of Persons with Disabilities (PwDs).
- To issue a Unique Disability Identity Card (UDID) to each individual.
- To ensure transparency, efficiency, and ease in delivering government benefits to PwDs.
- To maintain uniformity across regions in disability certification and identification.
- To eliminate duplicate records of Persons with Disabilities.
- To enable online renewal and updating of disability-related information.
- To support integrated management of government benefits and schemes for PwDs.
- To provide nationwide availability of PwD data through a centralized web application.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text: The Unique Disability Identity (UDID) card, also known as the Swavlamban Card, is a mandatory document for persons with disabilities (PwDs) in the Union Territory of Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu to access various central and state government welfare schemes. The UDID card ensures a uniform, digitized record that eliminates the need for carrying multiple documents and provides a single, nation-wide proof of disability.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
1. The applicant should be suffering from any of the 21 specified disabilities listed under the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016.
1. The applicant should apply for issuance of a Disability Certificate and Unique Disability Identity (UDID) card through the UDID portal.
1. The applicant should undergo disability assessment by the concerned hospital, Medical Board, or competent medical authority for the determination of disability percentage.
1. The applicant should get the disability verified and certified by medical authorities notified by the respective State Government or Union Territory Administration.
1. The applicant should provide the required personal, medical, and disability-related details during the application process.

content.section_title: Application Process (Online)
content.text:
**Step 1:** Visit the official [UDID Portal](https://swavlambancard.gov.in/).
**Step 2:** Click on “Apply For UDID” on the homepage.
**Step 3:** Register as a Person with Disability (PwD) on the portal.
**Step 4:** Log in using the registered credentials.
**Step 5:** Fill in the online application form for the Disability Certificate and UDID card with the required details.
**Step 6:** Upload the required documents, including medical and identity-related documents.
**Step 7:** Submit the application through the portal.
**Step 8:** The concerned Medical Authority/Medical Board verifies the application and conducts disability assessment for the calculation of disability percentage.
**Step 9:** The Medical Authority issues the Disability Certificate and UDID card electronically through the portal upon approval.
**Step 10:** Track the application status using the “Track Your Application” facility available on the portal.
**Step 11:** Download and print the Disability Certificate and UDID card after approval.
**Step 12:** Use the portal for renewal, replacement, or updating of information whenever required.

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
1. Aadhaar Card
2. Passport Size photo

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the objective of the Unique Disability ID (UDID) project?
content.text: The UDID project aims to create a national database of Persons with Disabilities (PwDs) and issue a Unique Disability Identity Card for ensuring transparency, efficiency, and ease in delivering government benefits.

content.section_title: FAQ: Which department implements the UDID project?
content.text: The project is implemented by the Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities (DEPwD), Government of India.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can apply for a UDID card?
content.text: Persons suffering from any of the 21 specified disabilities listed under the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016 can apply for a UDID card.

content.section_title: FAQ: What services are available through the UDID portal?
content.text: The portal provides services such as online application for Disability Certificate and UDID card, application tracking, renewal, updating information, downloading certificates, and replacement requests.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is online application available for the UDID card?
content.text: Yes. Applicants can apply online through the official UDID portal.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who issues the Disability Certificate and UDID card?
content.text: The Disability Certificate and UDID card are issued by competent medical authorities notified by the respective State Governments and Union Territories.

content.section_title: FAQ: How is the disability percentage determined?
content.text: The disability percentage is assessed by hospitals or Medical Boards during the medical assessment process.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can the Disability Certificate and UDID card be downloaded online?
content.text: Yes. Approved applicants can download and print their Disability Certificate and UDID card through the portal.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can information in the UDID card be updated or renewed?
content.text: Yes. The portal provides facilities for renewal, updating information, and replacement requests.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the benefit of creating a national database of PwDs?
content.text: The national database helps in eliminating duplicate records, improving transparency, and enabling better implementation and monitoring of government schemes and benefits for Persons with Disabilities.

content.section_title: Overview
content.text: The scheme aims to provide support, rehabilitation, and empowerment to persons with disabilities through financial assistance for healthcare, education, vocational training, and assistive devices.

content.section_title: Detailed Description
content.text:
The "Welfare Services for Disabled Persons" under the Mizoram Grant-in-Aid framework focuses on improving the quality of life of persons with disabilities by supporting voluntary organizations in delivering specialized services. Beneficiaries include persons with locomotor, visual, hearing, and mental disabilities, and multiple disabilities such as cerebral palsy and autism.
The scheme supports activities like vocational training, early detection, medical treatment, rehabilitation, and the provision of assistive devices. It also promotes awareness regarding the rights of disabled persons and supports the establishment of special schools and institutions.

content.section_title: Benefits
content.text:
> **Rehabilitation & Training:**

- Vocational training for self-employment.
- Early detection and intervention services.

> **Medical Support:**

- Treatment and rehabilitation services.
- HIV/AIDS care for disabled persons.

> **Assistive Devices:**

- Prosthetics, mobility aids, hearing aids, Braille equipment.
- Education Support.
- Special schools and vocational centres.

> **Awareness:**

- Awareness programmes on disability rights.

content.section_title: Eligibility Criteria
content.text:
> **For Individuals (Beneficiaries):**

- The applicant must be a person with disability having at least 40% disability, duly certified by a competent medical authority.
- The applicant must belong to any category such as locomotor, visual, hearing, mental, or multiple disabilities.
- The applicant must require support for rehabilitation, medical treatment, education, or livelihood.

> **For Organizations:**

- The organization must be a registered voluntary organization/NGO/institution.
- The organization must have experience in implementing disability welfare programmes.
- The organization must have adequate infrastructure and trained personnel to provide services.
- The organization must ensure that services are provided without discrimination.

content.section_title: Application Process (Offline)
content.text: - Apply through Social Welfare Department via NGOs

content.section_title: Documents Required
content.text:
- Age proof.
- Income certificate.
- Identity proof.
- Any other documents as required.

content.section_title: FAQ: What is the objective of the scheme?
content.text: The objective of the scheme is to provide rehabilitation, support, and empowerment services to persons with disabilities.

content.section_title: FAQ: Who can benefit from this scheme?
content.text: Persons with disabilities having at least 40% certified disability can benefit from this scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: What types of disabilities are covered under the scheme?
content.text: The scheme covers locomotor, visual, hearing, mental, and multiple disabilities including cerebral palsy and autism.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is a disability certificate mandatory to apply?
content.text: Yes, the applicant must possess a valid disability certificate issued by a competent medical authority.

content.section_title: FAQ: Does the scheme provide financial assistance?
content.text: Yes, the scheme provides financial assistance for rehabilitation, treatment, and support services.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are assistive devices provided under this scheme?
content.text: Yes, assistive devices such as prosthetics, hearing aids, and mobility aids are provided to eligible beneficiaries.

content.section_title: FAQ: Does the scheme support vocational training?
content.text: Yes, the scheme provides vocational training to help persons with disabilities become self-employed.

content.section_title: FAQ: Can organizations apply under this scheme?
content.text: Yes, registered voluntary organizations and institutions can apply to implement welfare services.

content.section_title: FAQ: What kind of services can organizations provide under the scheme?
content.text: Organizations can provide services such as rehabilitation, medical care, special education, and awareness programmes.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is medical treatment covered under the scheme?
content.text: Yes, the scheme includes support for medical treatment and rehabilitation services.

content.section_title: FAQ: Are there any awareness programmes under this scheme?
content.text: Yes, awareness programmes on disability rights and inclusion are supported under the scheme.

content.section_title: FAQ: Does the scheme support special schools for disabled persons?
content.text: Yes, the scheme supports the establishment and running of special schools and vocational centres.

content.section_title: FAQ: Is there any age limit for beneficiaries?
content.text: No, there is no specific age limit, but eligibility depends on disability certification and need.
